delicate

"you can only run so far from a past this dark."

01:00 | best friend

"Fuck, fuck, fuck!" She was whispering to herself in exasperation, running down the halls of YG entertainment. Her footsteps were quick and loud as her converse clicked against the wooden floors where she was located on the fifth, her green eyes darting in every which direction.

It wasn't unusual for her to be late to work, at least not recently. The truth she was a little ashamed to admit was the fact she had gotten a bit more — distracted — from her career than she'd liked. Although she wouldn't say the fact she got highly intoxicated and ended up doing some uncalled for things with another idol was her fault, it was also definitely her fault.

Such an unplanned event that happened all those nights ago in the cold month of October had ended up becoming a regular thing — almost too much of a regular thing. It was the solemn reason she kept ending up late to her recording sessions because she would end up sleeping in due to the 'rough' night she'd had, then she'd end up late to her recording session all over again. 

No matter how many times she asked him to wake her when he woke up to come to the studios, he never did. Of course, whenever she confronted him about it he always said the same thing, "I try, but you're seriously a heavy sleeper."

Stressed from the fact she was in deep shit, the brunette runs a hand through her disaster of hair. The staff gave her worried looks as she passed them, definitely noticing the usual angel of YG was absolutely out of whack.

They weren't exactly wrong for thinking so, either. All the stress of her job was building up on her, and what she was doing with him was one of the only ways she knew to try to get rid of it.

But the fact that doing that kept ending up with her being late absolutely was not helping her case.

The small girl was exhausted by the time she finally reached the studio, due to the fact she had ran like a mad man the entire way. Looking at her reflection in one of the windows, she tried to at least barely fix the mop atop her head. After a second, however, she'd deemed it was hopeless.

Knowing there was no way she would end up making herself presentable, she'd shrugged to herself as she grabbed the handle. It was cold against her fingertips, and she forced a smile onto her face as she prepared to face her manager on the other side.

Who she hadn't expected to meet — was them of all people.

The seven boys eyes widened at the sight of her. Her green eyes bulged as she saw how their eyes went from her hair, to her dead expression, all the way to how she probably looked like a zombie. It was when Bobby turned his head to try to hide his snicker that the others joined in, and she felt herself having a hard time resisting the urge to kill them all on sight — including him.

"Seems like you and Jinhwan almost had too much fun," Junhoe had hummed as he stifled the laugh he so badly wanted to let out at the poor girls state

The maknae had sighed as he looked to the boy, "no shit, you could hear it through out the entire apartment," he winced as he spoke the words, "it was fucking traumatizing."

Clenching her fists at her sides, she didn't even get a chance to yell at the boys who were making her life more of a pain than it already was. It was clear her staff that were going to help her record had left the studio as it must've been time for iKON to use it. The guys were either lazy, or just taking a break from the difficulty that recording was.

Either way, she didn't sign up to deal with their bullshit. Not when she had only been awake for a maximum of two hours.

As they began laughing and making fun of the noises they heard from her, she found her cheeks growing bright red and her veins flowing with fire. Just as she opened her mouth to scream at them, she heard a voice that was strong enough to cut through all the others. It was one that sounded like pure bliss, that seemed to sink deep into her eardrums and soak them with her favorite scent of cherry blossoms. Such a sound was enough for her to close her mouth, her eyes jolting as he grabbed her full attention just like that.

"Soomi, just ignore them," Jinhwan sent her that grin that she loved so desperately, opening his arms towards her as he cooed, "come here."

Just like that, was the reason she could never be mad at him even if she wanted to be. Stepping forward a few steps, she fell into the arms of the place she'd grown so accustomed to since October. It was a place she felt safe in, and one she didn't want to leave no matter how dangerous it really was.

They knew it was bad. You weren't supposed to be with other idols or other people romantically period. It was dire to your career, and if anyone like the fans and especially the CEO found out — it's over.

It was why the two were usually so secretive, but then, they had had too hard of a year, too hard of a day. They didn't have the energy to worry about anything else than the warmth of each other.

Jinhwan's arms felt secure around her waist as she buried her face in the crook of his neck. Her lips left a short, lingering kiss there as if to tell him how she was thankful for him. It was a pleasant moment that was once again, broke by the idiots laughing in the background.

In the fits of their giggles, Donghyuk had worriedly questioned, "aren't you both worried about getting caught?"

He wasn't trying to ruin their moment at all. Donghyuk (and beloved Yunhyeong) were the ones Soomi could actually really get along with out of the members of iKon that didn't consist of Nani. She could tell he was just thinking of the reality, and the fact that them being so clearly affectionate with each other — especially in the studios — was dangerous.

It was true, at any moment a staff even as simple as a janitor could walk in. From there, both of their cherished careers would practically be dead for. If anyone found out, it would be the end of everything they knew so well.

However, with all the weight on her shoulders recently, Soomi wasn't quite sure if she even cared in the first place.

The laughter and making fun of their hyung had eventually died down, as everyone was beginning to think of the truth that there was to Donghyuk's question.

Soomi didn't have the energy to speak from where she was hidden in the safety of his hoodie. Jinhwan had sighed as he bit his lip nervously, unsure of what to say to the matter. Of course, he knew the obvious well. Everyone knew the harsh rules of the industry.

"It's okay," he was careful with his words as they slowly left his lips, "we aren't technically together."

Bobby had snorted humorously from where he was leaning back in the leather couch that was outside of the recording room, "bullshit."

Annoyed with the attitude, Soomi had sat up from where she was getting rather comfy. The frustration radiated off of her, making everyone aware especially due to the expression on her face as she turned to face the six boys. Their eyes turned fearful as they watched her remove one of her converse. With a swift motion, she winged it straight at Bobby's head.

The boy had been the only one who hadn't been paying attention, which was why it had hit the top of his head harshly. Such a sudden impact caused a yelp to leave his lips as he jumped in the leather seat, looking down to where the shoe had fallen.

"Fuck, alright, I'm sorry!" He was crying in defense, because as soon as he looked up, the same girl was standing right before him with flaming eyes, "I get it, you're not really together."

Soomi only sent him a salty, close lipped smile as she picked up her shoe. Walking over to fall onto the seat beside Jinhwan instead of on his lap, she bent over to put it back on.

"Jinhwan is just — my best friend," She hummed as if it was no big deal as she was sitting back up.

The others eyes were confused as they stared at her, before looking to their hyung. Jinhwan wasn't paying attention to anyone, his brown eyes were heavily fixated on his feet. Not a word left his lips, and they worried that he might not have the same thoughts about their relationship that Soomi did.

Until finally, he cleared his throat, "yeah, we're just best friends."

It was one of the dumbest things any of them had ever heard. Jinhwan practically only spoke about the poor girl whenever they had free time, that was, if she was actually there with them. Soomi lived in the same apartment complex as the boys of iKon, but she spent most of her time in their apartment instead of her own.

They knew how their hyung really felt for the female idol, even though he hadn't said it himself. There was no way they couldn't know with how obvious he made it.

But the words she spoke made it clear she must not feel the same. It was sad to see they were just 'using' each other for stress relief, or at least that's what Soomi thought.

They felt bad for Jinhwan, as they watched him shift uncomfortably in his seat.

A long, awkward silence filled the recording waiting room. It was one that made everyone want to sink into the ground to never be seen again, until someone finally broke it.

"So, I'm guessing you were late for your recording session again?"

The still tired girls green eyes had met Hanbin's, and she let out a deep sigh at his question.

"You guessed it."






02:00 | freedom

"Soomi-ah," the CEO of one of the largest entertainment companies in the entirety of South Korea's eyes were stern but sincere as he peered into the green irises of her own, "you can't keep messing up your schedule like this and ignoring the staffs calls. If there is something going on, tell them, and we can arrange for you to take some time off, but you cannot keep horsing around. It's letting down not only yourself, but your fans." His words were more heartfelt than she expected, due to the known fact that CEO YG wasn't always the nicest person around. Especially with the way she'd been recently regarding her job, she was surprised that he was being so genuine. 

Then again, it didn't take her long to understand why. For the last three years, all she'd been doing was working non-stop. After all, being an idol had been her dream ever since she'd watch them on TV growing up. The moment she saw Big Bang on television, she knew exactly where she saw herself. And to the halls of YG Entertainment she'd went, after years and years of practicing to make the perfect audition, and even more endless hours of training to then make the perfect idol.

It'd been a long, sweaty road she'd embarked on. Sure, she painted this image of where she wished for herself to be, but never did she think of everything she would have to do to get there.

After practically starving herself to lose weight to be fit enough so she could fit the correct standards, and making sure she was in good enough shape to be able to with-hold her stable vocals while still doing the correct dance, she'd made it. It'd taken countless music banks, promotion shows, and TV appearances, but she was here. She was where she always wanted to be.

As one of the largest female solo artists in all of Korea, everyone envisioned her as some sort of goddess. With the fame brought stress, and she had never once stopped to think about how it would affect her and her mental health.

Soomi had always thought that once you made it to the top, you have it easy from then on. It'd been the largest wake up call she could've dreamed of once she actually got there, only to find out you had to work even harder than you did to get there to stay there. The demand for her music and appearances only rose as her popularity did, so much that she felt as if the entirety of the country was on her shoulders. 

It was why she looked to other things to try to weigh out how empty she was feeling. Her schedule was only getting tighter and tighter with the fame she was gaining, to the point where she was losing her sanity. It was why she started missing so many schedules, delaying her rehearsals, ignoring the staffs calls - but most importantly - why she was hooking up with Kim Jinhwan.

She didn't really even know where to begin explaining that one.

Feeling awkward and nervous under the harsh gaze of her elder, Soomi shifts uncomfortably in the leather chair which she sat across from him. The sunlight peeking through the large windows in the large office seemed to be mocking her as it beamed into her eyes, and she coughs a bit, trying to come up with the correct words to say.

"I'm sorry, there's just been some personal issues happening in my life, I haven't been feeling well mentally recently," she finally confesses, her eyes too afraid to meet those of her boss, "there's been a lot going on the last few years, and I just feel exhausted."

There's a notable, long silence that comes over the office of YG. Soomi only stares at her feet, far too worried of the expression that must be on his face. Was she about to get fired?

Until the sound of him clearing his throat is heard, and his deep voice cuts through the once eery silence, "You've been very busy since your debut, I understand you just need some time off. I'll cancel your current schedule and arrange for you to have some time to yourself." His words were thoughtful despite his voice still being as stern as ever. Either way, hearing such a thing caused her eyes to nearly bulge out of their sockets as she whipped her head back up to the CEO.

Surprise evident in her voice, she couldn't contain the overwhelming joy. "Really? Thank you so much!"

Though a rare occurrence, a small smile spreads over the lips of YG. "All you had to do was communicate, you're an important asset to this company, Soomi, take care of yourself."

Soomi rises to her feet, bowing a few times before she exits out of the room. A giddy expression was on her face, and she jumpily walks through the hallways of the studios. Many workers that had most likely seen her this morning - or just recently in usual - grew shocked at the sight of her so bright for once. Their eyes follow her as she bouncily jogs through the company.

Time to herself was all she had been wishing for what felt like a century now. At least, that was what she thought as she stepped out of the large doors of YG Entertainment, but now that she had rethought it, she was thinking all she'd really wanted was free time. Time to do whatever she wanted, free without having to worry about work, or what anyone around her was thinking about her. For once, Soomi felt like she was free to be herself as she inhales the outside air deeply, extending her arms beside her.

In that moment, as she pulls her car keys out of her bag and made her way to her Toyota, she had the perfect idea of how to celebrate.


+


"Dude, you fucking suck," Chanwoo snorts in humor as he beats Yunhyeong in yet another video game. None of the boys knew why someone like Yunhyeong was attempting to do something like play video games - especially with someone like Chan. But ever since the episode on the reality show, he'd seemed determined to learn the ways of - as he put it - gaming.

Groaning in defeat, the loser of the game yet again throws his game controller aside on the couch. He shakes his head as he leans back into the soft mattress, scolding the youngest, "Shut up, you shouldn't talk to your elders like that."

Chanwoo had rose to his feet to shut off the game console. He's just falling back onto the couch when Yunhyeong had said this, and a smirk spreads across his face, "sorry, ahjussi."

An offended look had never spread across Yunhyeong's face quicker. Gasping, he reaches out a hand to slap Chanwoo's arm, but the boy squirms away from him. "I swear - you have no manners."

"Probably from growing up around you retards."

"The disrespect!" The bickering with the two never seemed to end, and it was giving Donghyuk a headache as he was just trying to enjoy some Dancing High on the TV now that they were finally done playing games. However, it was rather difficult to do anything when there was always fighting and bickering going on - whether it was playful or not, it was still extremely annoying.

Not even a second had passed when the two had finally quit, only for someone new to be walking into the living area where the three were located. 

"Yo, what are we eating?" Bobby hums to the three as if they'd know, but they only shrug. He frowns at their blank expressions, "No one cooked? I'm starving."

Chanwoo, who was blankly scrolling through his phone, only mumbles, "Just order take out."

"I already did!" Junhoe's voice breaks through the halls, as he must've heard what was going on since Bobby always spoke so loud.

A pleased smile forms on Bobby's lips, "convenient." He spins on his heel to head back to his bedroom, probably to play some video games himself, when the doorbell had let out a sharp ding. At this signal that someone was there, everyone's head whips to look at the door.

Of course, their immediate assumption was the hope it was the take out guy. This is why not only Bobby - but Junhoe and Chanwoo - run towards the door like mental patients.

"Thank God, I'm fucking starving," Chanwoo is saying as he waits for one of them to open it.

"Who said I even ordered any for you?" Junhoe grins wickedly, being the joker he always was. Chanwoo's dark glare causes him to chuckle, "alright, chill."

Bobby undoes the lock on their door, hurriedly pulling it open. The three of them waited for the scent of sweet food to fill their nostrils, where they would then be filling their stomachs only minutes later. It was like a sweet symphony, just the mere thought of eating food, that was driving them all mental. 

This was why it was like someone had repeatedly hit them all with a grand piano when it isn't the take out worker - but Kim Soomi. 

Groans of death leave their lips as they all turn away from the brightly shining girl, who's mouth falls agape in offense at their reactions to her. "Alright, I can see I'm not wanted," she deadpans as she watches them walk away, their shoulders slumping over in depression. Nonetheless, they still left the door open for her.

Chanwoo and Bobby has disappeared into the kitchen, leaving Junhoe to walk slightly ahead of her into the apartment. "It's not that, we were hoping you were the take out guy," he explained to her, and she only hummed in understanding behind him, about to speak up when he had walked into the halls that led to Jinhwan's room. His voice suddenly turned into that of a booming yell, "JINHWAN-HYUNG, YOUR GIRLFRIEND IS HERE!"

at the sound of the name, soomi's eyes bulge out of their sockets. she steps forward to start punching his arm, "stop calling me his fucking girlfriend!"

june flinches and scurries out of her reach, even though her punches probably weren't hurting him in the slightest due to his muscle. "that's such a lie!"

he's running down the hall to his own bedroom as he screeches these truthful words, a rather ticked off soomi chasing close after him. she didn't know what it was going to take to finally get it through their heads - she wasn't dating kim jinhwan. it was like no matter how many times she said this, they all continuously insisted - like it was just to spite her.

only just as her small figure is quickly speeding past jinhwan's bedroom door to catch junhoe, his door had been opened. this revealed a tired jinhwan, his hair an unkempt mess on his head. he was in his boxers, and wearing nothing more than a white tank top - the material being rather thin, meaning his toned, baby soft skin was on display.

soomi had screeched at the sight of him suddenly appearing when she'd least expected him to, running straight into his chest as he had turned into the hallway, not seeing her there. her eyes bulge as she looks up to him, her heart dropping out of her chest and onto the floor. with wide eyes, her previous confidence that she always had with her seemed to melt into the tiled floor as she stepped back to look up at him.

his face was bare, not a singular droplet of makeup on him, but somehow he still managed to have not one flaw. it blew her mind, and she felt herself gawking subconsciously as she met his confused eyes.

"soomi?" he blinks a few times, "what are you doing here?" his voice was raspy, and with the way he rubbed his eyes, it was clear he had just awoken from slumber.

she felt herself getting lost in just staring at him, so much that he had raised a hand, snapping his fingers in front of her face. at the sight of her like this, jinhwan seemed to finally have a good idea of what she was doing here. although they never did things like this this early, he had a feeling she was feeling rather different about it today.

a smirk spreads across his lips as he crosses his toned arms over his chest, his muscles popping out and causing her heart to pop in her chest once again, "you okay, sweetheart?"

his tone was mocking, and it caused her courage to be abruptly chunked back at her. snapping out of her gaze, she rolls her eyes, not another thought crossing her mind as she places both of her hands on his chest, feeling his chest muscles under her palms through the thin material. and even though it would've been easy for him to stop her, he only grinned deviously down at her as she pushed him back into his bedroom, closing the door with her foot.



03:00 | rude awakening

"award show?!" soomi repeats the words she had just heard echo from the kitchen as she walks in with jinhwan, popping out from behind his figure to peer at the six of them in confusion.

the six, who had been casually eating the waffles yunhyeong had made for everyone, heads all whip up to the sound of the female voice. chanwoo stares at his hyung's who look confused at the fact she was still here, "why do you all look so lost? did you not hear them the entire night?"

his words cause jinhwan to spit out his water that he had been drinking from a glass into the sink, and soomi buries her face in her hands to try to hide the annoyance that rid up her spine. she swore she was going to end up hurting one of them one day. however, she knew they only continued to tease just to purposely try to anger her, so she forced a smile onto her face as she slides into the booth that was on one side of the kitchen table next to junhoe. 

"i'm just gonna pretend like you didn't say that," yunhyeong says as he eats more of his waffles, "you bully our poor soomi too much."

thankful for his existence, she offers him a smile, and he only returns it with a wink. "i'm used to it honestly, i've gotten to the point where i wear the noise cancelling ear muffs when i sleep," hanbin is sharing at he eats his own food.

getting his own food, jinhwan takes the last seat next to soomi, his cheeks a tinted shade of red, "can you guys please stop talking about this?"

donghyuk looks to the two embarrassed humans, an empathetic looks in his eyes. immediately, he tries to change the subject back to something more pleasant, "anyway, as we were saying earlier, soomi, the end of the year award shows are coming up, remember?"

she has to pause and think for a moment. suddenly, a look of realization spreads over her face, and she covers her mouth with her hands, "shit, yeah, like MAMA and Gaon and all that - fuck, I forgot!"

"didn't your manager tell you - or have you been ditching work that bad?" bobby asks from the other side of the table, still clearly hearing the conversation.

pursing her lips nervously, she shrugs hesitantly, "i've been ditching it pretty bad, i talked to ceo yesterday and he said i could halt any activities for a while."

at this news they had yet to hear, everyone looks up with wide eyes - including jinhwan. "really? so are you not attending them?"

"i'm pretty sure you're up for a lot of awards, it'd be a shame not to go."

a frown spreads over her lips as she thinks about this. her fans would probably be very sad if she didn't attend, and along with the fact she was supposed to of been releasing new music within the next few months. she'd been slacking too much, and the guilt was finally starting to climb up her spine.

"i don't know, i guess i'll have to talk to them about it," she only mumbles, reaching out to grab jinhwan's fork from his hands and take a bite of his waffle. yunhyeong's cooking was always really good, and she hummed in satisfaction as she chewed,  ignoring the surprised jinhwan at the fact she stole his fork and instead pointing a finger at yun, "you're so good at cooking."

he grins confidently, "oh, i know.”

+

"oh, soomi, i was actually going to talk to you about that when i saw you at the company the next day, but then i heard about your break, so i wasn't sure if you still wanted to attend or perform," her manager was saying over the phone. soomi grips it a bit harder as she listens to his words, looking to the ceiling of jinhwan's bedroom in thought.

however, one certain word had caught her attention. "perform? was i invited to perform at anything?"

he was quickly to pipe up in reply, "yeah! practically every award show is eager for you. you're up for the album of the year, solo artist of the year, female artist of the year, and a lot more. since you're feeling exhausted, i understand if you would like to halt any appearance, but it'd be disappointing to your fans."

a sigh leaves soomi's lips as she ponders. it would be awful for her not to go, and as for the performance, she would be dumb not to do them. everyone always responded well to her music, and no matter how tired soomi felt, how overworked her body grew, she would always love the adrenaline performing gave her. to see her fans wave the lightsticks, to hear their fanchants, and the way they would sing along to the lyrics with their hearts, it made her life worth living.

even though she felt drained from working so hard the last few years, even though soomi knew she needed actual time to herself. something inside of her pushed her to say, "no, manager-nim, book me for all the performances, i'll be appearing at all of the shows."

her manager was thrilled to hear these words. "i'm glad you've had a change in heart, i'll let everyone know!"


[smut warning, read at your own risk or skip until you see this message again]




the sudden sound of the door to jinhwan's bathroom was heard opening. out came a bare jinhwan, only a towel covering his bottom half. her eyes nearly bulge out of her sockets and she has to direct them away from that area.

turning away from him, she tries her hardest to still focus on what her manager was telling her, when there was a sudden feeling of arms snaking their way around her waist from behind. his chest was still damp from the shower, and she felt the warmth of his body against her back as he leaned his head into her neck. 

her legs grew weak under her with the feeling of his lips leaving soft kisses along her neck, up to her jawline. it was when they went from gentle kisses to his tongue swiping over her skin, sucking on certain spots that he knew killed her, that she had to cover her mouth to hold in a whimper.

"get off the phone," his voice was low, but she didn't know if it was quiet enough for her manager to not hear.

she places her hands over his that are roaming her body under her shirt, trying to tell him to stop. "y-yes manager-nim, that sounds great, i think it would be good to present a new song at one of them, i just haven't gotten to record," she kept having to pause, trying to hold any noises that her manager shouldn't hear inside of her. clearing her throat as she listened the best she could manage, she stumbles over her next words, "i-i know, yeah, i know i said i needed a break, but after hearing about the award shows, i've reminded myself of the fans that are waiting for me and my new music."

"oh, i see, so are you interested in coming to record and cancel your break?"

"i suppose so, i'll try my best to get back on my feet and come record as soon as possible!"

"that sounds good, soomi!"

suddenly, there was a feeling of him slipping his hand into her shorts. a small squeal leaves her lips when his hand hovers over her heat, and she didn't mean to let out a hushed curse of his name, "jinhwan!"

only she didn't realize she was still on the phone with her manager.

"jinhwan?" her manager grew confused, "jinhwan of ikon?"

with wide eyes, she tries to think quickly of how to regain herself. as far as the company rules went, she wasn't even supposed to be around the boys of ikon. if her manager found out she was doing more than just hanging out with jinhwan, there was no telling what would happen.

"u-uh, yeah, i just had an idea! it would be fun to do a collab with jinhwan from ikon!" she laughs nervously at the end of her statement, praying to god he wouldn't question her sudden thought.

there was a sound of confusion evident in her manager's voice, "oh, i didn't know you were fond of him. that might be something that could be discussed, we would have to talk to ceo yg - but you know how he is about the male and female idols being even in contact with each other."

a breath of relief leaves her lips, and she tries to ignore the fact jinhwan was rubbing her there. "yeah, i know, i just think it would be responded to well by the fans."

"i'll talk to him about it, soomi."

"alright, good talking to you. see you soon, manager-nim!" she ended the call before another unholy noise could leave her lips. 

"asshole!" she cries as jinhwan had immediately stopped everything he was doing. his hands go back to his side, and he smirks at her as he walks over to his bed, falling onto the soft mattress.

a giggle leaves his parted lips as he raises a brow, "a collab, huh?"

"oh, shut up," she whines, walking over to fall onto his bed as well. sitting beside him, she swings her legs back and forth off the edge of the bed, mumbling in disbelief, "i can't believe you just did that when i was on the phone with my manager!"

"it was funny," is all jinhwan has to say about the matter, "and cute how you got all flustered and nervous to make any noise."



[end, continue freely]




she threw him a glare, "obviously, something bad could've happened, jinhwan!"

he notices she was growing genuinely upset by his actions. usually, he wasn't the type to perform these sorts of mean actions, but with how he was feeling recently - how his heart was feeling - he did it, in a way just to spite soomi. jinhwan felt for her, more than he could even explain to himself, and it broke his heart that she didn't feel the same - at least he didn't think.

but then again, everyone knew kim jinhwan was maybe even too egotistical. it was hard for him to believe that after all his life, when he finally liked a girl sincerely, that she didn't like him back. the guys could tease him about his deep crush on his best friend all they wanted, saying it was never going to happen because of how closed off soomi truly was, but he felt like she was just lying to herself. there was no way she didn't feel the sparks when they touched, the acceleration of her heartbeat, the elephants thumping against the bottom of her stomach. there was no way.

it was as simple as the fact she didn't want anyone to know, not even jinhwan - yet.

and even though he didn't know if there was truth to these thoughts, he was willing to wait.

"soomi-ah~," he sing songs her name, his face growing into an expression that of a soft puppy. it was one he did when he was trying to get her to forgive him, or just get something from someone in general no matter who it may be. rarely did it not work, even on soomi. it was hard for people to resist him, he supposed, as he pushed out his bottom lip and grabbed onto the sleeves of her sweater, pulling her towards him. 

her back was too him as she had turned the other way, clearly being upset with him for his sick joke. there'd been a blatant, pissed look on her face when he'd pulled her to face him, and she seemed nervous to look up at him.

"hey, i'm sorry, okay? it wasn't funny," he made his voice a lower tone, sounding more pleading with each word that left his lips. with the way she shifted uncomfortably beside him, her eyes still on her hands that laid in her lap, he could tell she was having a hard time not giving in.

after he'd whined her name like a desperate child a few more times, an exaggerated exhale had left her lips. she finally looks up to meet his soft eyes, and he grows shocked at the sight of her own green orbs glassy. 

"what's wrong?" he's quick to throw the questions in immediate concern, the fun and jokes evaporating in the air as soon as he saw tears even welling in her eyes, "did i make you cry?! please don't cry."

she's quick to shake her head as his hand reached up, and jinhwan uses the pads of his thumbs to wipe away a singular tear that threatened to fall from one of her eyes. soomi raises her own hand to grab his that was on her face, pulling it off and holding it in her own. there was a short silence before she speaks almost inaudible, "i don't know what made me get this way for a second, i just - it just made me think about how dangerous this is."

having had been looking at their hands that were in each other's, soomi's eyes trail back up to see jinhwan's reaction. she wasn't sure what she had expected, but he looked rather confused as he blinked down at her. "what are you talking about?"

at the fact he wasn't immediately understanding, she heaves out a sigh. letting go of his hands, soomi rises to her feet, clearing her throat so she could normally explain, "what's going on," she starts, before motioning between the two of them, "this, it's so dangerous. think about it, jinhwan, our companies rules state we aren't even supposed to speak to each other, but here we are - doing this," her hands go to the sides of her head, balling her own hair up in fists out of stress, "talking about the award shows just made it all hit me, i've been slacking so much ever since we started doing this."

the fairy of ikon was still extremely lost as he furrowed his brows. or perhaps it was more of, he got what she was saying, but he didn't know what it meant. "what are you trying to tell me?" he slowly voices his concern.

soomi paces back and forth around the room before she stops in front of him again. with dull eyes, she hesitantly opens her mouth, "jinhwan, i don't think we can keep doing this. it's gotten to far, i'm getting to distracted from my career - i'm letting my fans down. and what if someone finds out? the both of us could be hated, or kicked out of the company, we'd be left for dead," her words were only getting more out of wack as she covered her face with her hands. obviously, the sudden talk of all the end of year award shows had triggered soomi's hard working side, completely obliterating the bum they'd all grown to know for so long.

it was then that he'd rose to his feet, his caramel hair falling in his eyes as he walks up to her. she hadn't noticed due to how much she was still freaking out from the thoughts in her head, when jinhwan reaches up to pull her hands from her face, using his own to cup her cheeks. he wasn't going to lose her now, not after all this time.

"soomi, you're overreacting," he bent down only slightly so he was level with her eyes, "stop thinking so hard, you were thrilled about being able to finally be lazy only a couple of hours ago."

she frowns, "i know, that's the thing! i completely forgot about all the shows, and my fans," her voice grows nearly silent again as she looks to the ground, away from him, "i feel horrible about it."

seeing her so down about something made jinhwan feel the same thing. it was almost like he was connected to her, so when she felt something - he felt it as well. just seeing her expression grow that of a pained one, feeling like she had disappointed everyone, was enough for him to sternly inform, "hey, you didn't do anything wrong. you've been working hard for the last years since your debut, there's nothing wrong with letting yourself rest," he wittily tells her, her green eyes finally making contact with his again, "and we aren't going to get caught, soomi. i know it sounds silly because we only started doing this to clear our heads but, you make me feel content. i'm a better person when you're around," he offers her a smile, and her eyes widen from hearing each word he's speaking, "i'm really glad you're in my life, soomi, so i don't want this to end. call me selfish, but you mean too much to me to let you go."

04:00 | the g-word

his words felt like a euphoria to her. it was something she had never expected to hear, especially from jinhwan. just seeing the way he was looking so passionately down at her, his eyes soft as they looked into hers. it was enough to make her heartbeat vigorously pump against her chest, nearly making her sweat from the overwhelming emotions that bundled up inside her.

emotions that she didn't want to believe existed. they aren't real, right?

"you mean a lot to me too, jinhwan. you're my closest friend. you're right about everything you said," she says after a long while, "i'm sorry i freaked out, i trust we aren't stupid enough to get caught, right? i don't think i could go without you, anyway."

a smile had found its way to her lips again, and it took him a moment to return it. although he knew he should be happy about her words, there was a certain something that had nearly left him a crying mess on the floor (not literally,) but just the sound of it - the sound of hearing her say such an unfortunate thing. it broke him inside.

when he doesn't say anything for a while, a bummed look on his face as he seemed to be in some sort of daze, soomi reaches out her pointer finger and pokes his cheek. "what's with you? now you're the one looking glum."

he'd snapped out of it at the sound of her sweet voice, filling his ears and throwing him into a place of peace. jinhwan has to push his negative thoughts away, he had to focus on the positive. maybe they were just doing this with no feelings attached (or at least that's what she thought) and he would just have to live with that. because at least he got her, one way or another.

even if it's not the way he wanted.

a best friend with benefits wasn't so bad.

"just feeling a little jaded," he hums, before bending down. soomi is confused at what he's doing when he'd suddenly wrapped both his arms around her legs, picking her up and causing her to lean against his back as a squeal leaves her lips. "but i know what can make us both feel better," he'd matter-of-factly stated as he threw her back onto the feather like mattress, soomi in a fit of giggles as he'd climbed over her.

"kim jinhwan, you're something else," she mutters as her arms go around his neck, but she only opens her mouth to allow him access as he dips down to connect their lips.



+



it was growing near sunset already that day, and soomi found herself yawning as she walks out into the living area of the ikon apartment. she'd just taken a short nap in jinhwan's room after their rendezvous, and when she walked, there was an obvious limp in her step due to how rough he tended to be - intentional or not. she reaches her hands up to rub her eyes, probably looking like the exact definition of a train wreck since she hadn't even bothered to try to fix her hair or appearance in general before wandering into the main room, oh well, it wasn't like she cared. the seven boys were too used to not only hearing them, but seeing her afterwards as well.

the sight of them all crowded in the room, some sitting on the large sofa and some on the floor since it could only fit so many people. her eyes trail to the tv, where they were watching what most definitely looked like a horror movie she didn't know the name of. wincing at the sight, she thought about going to sit next to jinhwan because it was clear he left a seat open for her on the leather sofa, but soomi hated horror movies.

however, despite her being a complete asshole, the boys all looked rather engaged in the movie that she didn't want to disturb their experience. it was why she tried to get jinhwan's attention mentally, waving her hands in the air and mouthing his name over and over from the side of the room.

only a second later had he noticed her rapid, ridiculous looking gestures. his brows had furrowed, and a humored look spread over his face. when he doesn't move a muscle, she narrows her eyes at him, but he only sends her an innocent look, patting the still empty seat beside him and junhoe.

soomi only shakes her head no, pointing to the front door where she was about to walk out of. that's what she had been trying to tell him the entire time. she needed to go home and wash up anyway, she didn't have anymore time to mess around with the boys and jinhwan, even if she really wanted to.

sure, she wasn't ending her stress relief fling with jinani, but that didn't mean she was going to keep slacking. she knew she didn't have a real choice, her fans would grow impatient and angry when she wasn't releasing the new music that was announced. it would only grow worse the longer she slacked off.

she knew that now. it was why her try hard mode had been activated once again, and she was going to make sure she attended to recording session she had tomorrow. which meant that she had to actually go back to her apartment for once.

a defeated look spreads over jinhwan's face when she waves, before spinning on her heel to leave. the other boys hadn't even noticed her presence due to how captivated they were by the movie, but at the sound of their eldest suddenly shuffling out of his seat and running after her, they'd glanced up to see what was going on. when they see it had to do with soomi, they'd immediately went back to the movie, thinking the same old nothing new.

they all knew their poor hyung was oddly obsessed with her.

she's already standing out in the hall when he'd stepped out after her. desperately, his hand reaches out and grabs her wrist, spinning her around so she comes flailing onto his chest.

shocked, soomi blinked a few times before stepping back. her eyes meet his as she sighs, "what is it? i told you i was leaving."

a part of him wanted to make a joke, but another part was too eager to tell her, "don't leave, you haven't even been here very long," his voice was like a whining kid as his hand went from her wrist to her own hand, where he intertwined their fingers.

the feeling of his hand engulfing hers was enough to possess her for a moment, and as she thought about how she enjoyed spending time with him, she realized she really didn't want to leave. but soomi knew if she stayed with jinhwan, she'd be late again and only grow lazier.

knowing this fact well is the reason she wrinkles her nose as she gazed up to him, "nani, you're too clingy."

his once pouty faced formed into a smile at this, as if he was glad to hear her say what she thought would of been an insult. gently, he pushes her back into the wall in the hallway of their apartment floor, his hands grabbing hers. his face grows daringly close to her own, and he leans over to her side to whisper in her ear, his lips grazing it, "what's wrong with that?"

she felt her stomach doing flips, and it was becoming more difficult for her to snap herself out of the trance he always put her in. soomi knew just what he was doing. he was making her want him so bad she couldn't help but stay.

groaning, she ignores her question, her hands hurriedly going up to push him off of her. "you're so difficult," she complains (even though she really loved it,) walking away without another word. the thing was, her hand was still inside of jinhwan's, but he didn't move as she tried to make her way to the elevator. this caused her to turn back to him with a look of annoyance on her face, as she had expected him to already get her gist, "come on, jinhwan, i'm tired."

chuckling at her assertive tone despite the both of them knowing who really had all the control, he happily jogged a bit to walk side by side with her. the entire time their hands comfortably fit with one another, and soomi felt herself yawning again as she pushed the button to call the elevator.

noticing how she seriously was exhausted, he raises a brow, "do i ware you out that much? you're limping too, aren't you?" his tone was highly mocking as he smirked, and she resisted the urge to punch him in the face right then and there.

"shut up," she snaps, "you're lucky you're so cute."

he only giggles in response, when the elevator had dung indicating it was there. the two of them turn, watching as the doors open so they could enter to head to her apartment floor.

however, the sight of somebody in the elevator caused their eyes to bulge. instantly, their hands left each others and they stepped away from their once close proximity, acting as if they were mere acquaintances.

"oh, hi ms. lee," jinhwan had offered a kind smile to the boys neighbor, bowing politely. soomi hurriedly did the same, stepping aside out of the old woman's way which meant she was next to jinhwan again. their shoulders were touching as they both innocently peered at the woman who exited the elevator.

"jinhwan, nice to see you," ms. lee, who soomi now knew, had returned the kind gesture. she was about to walk past when she noticed the two of them, causing her to stop and raise a brow, "is this your girlfriend? she's so beautiful, how cute you are together!"

girlfriend, the sound of the word soomi hadn't heard in so long was enough to cause a tint to spread through her cheeks. at the realization she never blushed and actually was, her hands go to cover her cheeks as she lets out a nervous laugh, opening her mouth to quickly deny the nice woman's assumption.

"yes, she is," jinhwan had other plans though as he'd grinned from ear to ear, "thank you, ms. lee."

her eyes were confused and frustrated as they peered up at the boy, but he was only ignoring her death glare. 

"very sweet, say, you seem familiar," she'd hummed as she looked to soomi again.

trying to dismiss the fact jinhwan had called her his girlfriend, she nodded curtly, clearing her throat to speak casually, "oh, i'm actually an idol like jinhwan. you might've heard my songs," she introduces, "i'm kim soomi."

at the sound of the name, it was like a light bulb went off above her head. ms lee's eyes brighten, and she nods as if she already knew that, "oh yes, i do know you! my grand daughter loves you, i can't believe you two are together, that's so lovely! i'm sure she'll be thrilled to hear that."

and this was why she didn't want jinhwan to unnecessarily run his mouth - even to his elder neighbor.

"oh thank you," she bows again, "but please don't tell her about jinhwan and i."

"we're trying to keep our relationship private for the now being," jinhwan had cut in, keeping the same grin on his face despite how he was freaking out inside.

ms lee, thankfully, seemed to understand, "ah, i see, no problem, kids. you both take care."

the two had bowed again one last time before bidding goodbye to the woman. soomi waited until they were in the closed elevator before she started hitting jinhwan. although her punches didn't effect him in the slightest, she still felt better by venting out her anger towards his actions.

"what's wrong with you?! i just told you how i was scared about us getting caught and then you tell someone i'm your girlfriend?!" she was crying in exasperation, hitting his arm like her life depended on it, "where did that even come from?"

growing bothered by the feeling of her tiny fists on his arm, he reached out to grab them mid punch. soomi tried to struggle out of his grip, but he was obviously stronger than her, and it left her to stand there in defeat, a pout on her lips.

"stop freaking out again, it was just our old neighbor, she doesn't even remember 90% of the things i say to her," he deadpans as if it was no big deal.

sighing, she diverts her eyes and he finally let her go. her hands fall back to her sides as they ride the elevator, and she leans against one of the walls, "you still didn't even answer my other question."

but jinhwan had no intention of doing so as he leaned beside her, humming, "yup, and i'm not going to."















05:00 | gentlemen

soomi yawns as she actually woke up the next morning due to her - for once - setting alarms on her phone. reaching her hands up to rub her eyes after she silences her cellular device, she was about to throw her legs over the side of the bed where she'd then begin to prepare for a day of work. since yesterday, she had been reminded of how important it was for her to do her job, and it had motivated her to stop sitting on her ass.

however, suddenly the feeling of someone pulling her back against their chest was preventing her from moving a muscle. she squirms a bit, before realizing jinhwan didn't have any intention of letting her free. 

"jinhwan," she lets his name leave her lips as she turns in his grip to face his 'sleeping' expression. his eyes were still closed, but it was clear to her he had to be awake. when he doesn't respond to her calling him, soomi heaves out a sigh, her hands reaching up to graze her fingers across his baby soft skin, pushing strands of his messy caramel locks out of his face. 

suddenly, his eyes open, and he raises a brow as they peer into hers, "take a photo, soomi, it'll last longer."

she rolls her eyes at the overused remark, "oh shut up, let me go, i have to get ready for work."

jinhwan frowns, "what? you're actually going today?" he whines, "the one day i'm actually off."

"that's not my fault, plus it's good you won't be there, it means i'll actually be able to focus without thinking about you."

he perks up at this, and only then did she realize what words just came out of her mouth. "so you think about me when i'm there, huh?"

she hurriedly manages to scurry out of his grip due to how he wasn't paying as much attention, rushing to her feet so he wouldn't see how red her cheeks were turning. "no, you suck," is all she says as she practically runs towards her bathroom.

jinhwan only chuckles at her obviously embarrassed reaction. when she acted this way in times like this, it was why he knew for a fact she was just denying her feelings towards him. how could she not feel the same? 



+




"what are you doing?" the girl, who was perfectly dressed from head to toe in attire that didn't look like she just rolled out of bed, blinks at jinhwan in confusion. when she walked out of the restroom finally ready for work, she was met with the sight of kim jinhwan, standing at the front door and twirling her car keys around her fingers. 

he looks up from his phone that he'd been holding with his other hand, speaking casually, "i'm gonna drive you to the company."

her reaction was the definition of priceless, as her eyes nearly fell out of their sockets and she furrowed her brows at him. clearly, she misunderstood what he was suggesting, seeing him as some sort of complete sociopath. "are you retarded?" she asks, speaking each word slowly as if he didn't understand what she was saying.

"soomi," he exhales deeply, knowing exactly what she was about to start rambling about all over again, "i'm just driving you into the parking lot, not going inside. no one is going to see me. i'll even drop you off at the back," he said reassuringly, using the voice he always used when he wanted something. it was one that seemed to sink deeply into her heart and pull at all the strings, playing with her emotions in more ways than she could explain.

this was why she purses her lips in thought, before shrugging, "alright, fine, let's go."

figuring he was still going to wait for her to get her things, she spins around to grab her bag off the couch. flinging the blue bag over her shoulder, soomi spins on her heel to walk back towards she door where she presumed jinhwan was still waiting. this meant she jumped in surprise at the sight of him instead suddenly behind her.

in a demanding tone, he holds out a hand, "give me your bag."

not processing just what he was trying to do, she only blinks at him blankly, "what?"

his eyes peer into hers, and he couldn't believe how clueless she really was sometimes. "i'm going to carry your bag for you," he says, and a wave of understanding spreads over the small girls face.

for once, he thought the stubborn soomi he'd always known was actually going to give in for once, but instead she shrugs, "it's not a big deal, you don't have to carry my bag for me."

she tries to walk past him casually, thinking he would just let it go and not care. but he seemed to have other thoughts in mind when he reached his hand to the side to stop her again, and she looks up to him in shock. "if it's not a big deal, let me carry it," he persists.

completely lost as to what he was up to, soomi shakes her head as she deadpans, "nani, i get the whole gentlemen look you try to go for, but it's not going to work on me, we both know how you really are - especially in bed."

his face grows paler for a second at her sudden words, but being the cool guy, he immediately hides it as he fully turns to face her in the living area of her apartment. raising a brow, he gives her those puppy eyes he was so good at as he pleads, "that's just in bed though, i am a gentlemen, so let me have my satisfaction."

at his audacity that never disassembled even for a second, she scoffs in disbelief, shaking her head as if she was upset. in reality, she had no issue with his proposition in the first place, she was just stubborn. she'd always been this way, she liked to set her foot down and never change her mind no matter what, but he seemed to be changing that about her.

"okay, fuck, fine," she mutters, chunking her blue bag at him because she couldn't take the way his sweet eyes kept staring down at her. despite how everyone bullied jinhwan for being 'short,' he'd been growing over the years since their debut. the boy who was once the exact same height as her was now a whopping few inches above her, if not more. he was still short, to say the least, but not as short - meaning she couldn't bully him about it anymore because she was tinier. his height advantage, which was something that he never got to have much, was something he loved and she hated, because it made him only that much more intimidating. this was currently taking it's effect as she hurriedly rushed towards the door, knowing a certain urge would break through her if she met his eyes again.

he'd slung the bag on his shoulders, chuckling at her obvious flustered reaction as he makes his way out the door after her. jingling the keys she'd given him, he locks the door himself before rushing to catch up with soomi, who was walking like a mad woman down the hall.

"yah, sweetheart, slow down," he whines out to her, "you look like you're about to go kill someone."

stopping by the elevator, she pushes the button as she sasses, "i wonder who."

knowing exactly what she was suggesting, jinhwan rolls his eyes as he stands beside her. reaching up his arm, he wraps it around her shoulders and pulls her into his chest before she could object, "oh please, you would never lay a hand on me, unless it's -," she raised a hand and put it over his mouth, having a feeling as to what he was about to say, and for once - not wanting to hear the dirty talk nor jokes.

"kim jinhwan, i'd advise you to shut up," she only warned as she took her hand off his mouth, but jinhwan was just grinning like a love struck kid. as she took the hand off, he took that as his chance to grab it with his own, interlocking their fingers instead of saying anything in reply.

this action caused her to look up at him with a look of annoyance, but he could see her trying to hide a smile. with that, the elevator before them let out a ding, and they both stepped in to head towards the first floor of the apartment building.

standing there in the elevator, jinhwan was preoccupied by humming a song in the quiet air. due to her hearing all of the songs so often, she recognized it as beautiful. hearing the way he hummed it made her think of his voice, in so many ways. she knew the other day on the phone with her manager, it was panic words that made her suggest a collab with him. but truthfully, she really wouldn't mind it. jinhwan could sing scarily well, he had talent that was rare to come by, and it was why she found herself almost gawking up at his figure, getting lost in the way he sounded.

not to mention looked, as he had obviously gotten ready in a hurry. with not a spec of makeup on his face, his skin still managed to glow. she always wondered how he did that, how he could look so flawless with no effort. a grey hoodie and jeans was all he was wearing, with his caramel hair parted in the middle and untouched on his head. but he still looked perfect to her.


he suddenly looks to her, and she hurriedly had diverted her gaze in hopes he didn't notice her creepily admiring him for that entire time. something was getting into her, she curses to herself mentally, tearing her hand away from his as the elevator opened and revealed the first floor to them.

jinhwan, on the other hand, only smirked as she walked ahead of him, quickening her pace with each step all over again. sure enough, he'd saw the way she had been staring at him, and he knew exactly what it meant.

throwing the hood of his hoodie over his head, he slips sunglasses on over his nose before rushing to catch up with the girl. as he does so, she looks up to him, noticing his change in appearance, and raising a brow, "you look like a spy."

he snorts humorously, nodding his head, "yeah, i'm from dispatch."

at the rather spooky joke, she hits his arm lightly, "shut up, don't say that, they're scary!"

jinhwan only laughs as they exit the doors. the real reason he was wearing the hoodie and glasses was just so no one would recognize him. although it was unlikely anyone that was in the apartment lobby actually would - or care enough to in the first place, for soomi (and himself) he felt it necessary to take the extra precautions.

however, that didn't mean he wasn't going to hold her hand in the car.





06:00 | recording

"perfect, you did well, soomi-ah!" the producer's voice explodes in cheer, and everyone that had been in the studio working on the new songs rise to their feet. claps arise in the air as the girl steps out of her room, feeling no doubt exhausted after the long day of work, but as she sees the proud looks on all of the staffs faces, she couldn't help but grin from ear to ear. 

"we did it!" she yells in unison with them, and everyone gathered around for a group hug. after hours and hours, she had finally finished recording some new songs that would be released on her new album coming out. with the recording mainly finished, this meant all that was left was the choreo. but either way, she always sought really getting the songs done was the most important part, so it was as if a huge weight had been lifted off her shoulders as she thanks the staffs who had mixed and helped with the music. 

"we're proud of you, soomi," many of them had assured her, and she couldn't help but bow numerous times to show her undeniable thanks to them. 

soon enough, she was falling onto one of the sofas as the room had emptied out. she had been working non-stop since she got there this morning, meaning she hadn't spoken a word to jinhwan or any of the guys in general. she was just pulling out her phone to text him when someone in the room cleared their throat, causing her to look up in confusion.

her manager sits beside her without a warning, and she hurriedly turns off her phone so he didn't see the name that was on her screen. "so, i spoke to yg-nim," he was saying, and a look of confusion spread over her face when she didn't remember at first. he notices this, and explains, "i told him your interest in working with ikon, and he said it would be a possible collaboration he'd look into."

nearly coughing on air, her manager looks at her like she's gone crazy when it takes her a moment to regain her breathe. surprised, she hurriedly contains her emotions and sits back up straight, "really?" hearing something like that from ceo yg was the last thing she had ever expected. she knew she said she wouldn't mind collaborating with jinhwan, but now that it might actually happened, she wasn't sure.

it wasn't that she didn't want to, it was that she was scared. the two of them on stage together? it'd be fun, but dangerous. with fans these days, what if they noticed something unusual about the two of them? the rumors would spread like wildfire, they might grow suspicious and follow them everywhere which would lead to bad things.

despite these thoughts, she forces a smile on her lips, "that's great, did he say when?"

"well, actually," the manager clears his throat as if he was about to tell her some ground breaking news, "since both you and the boys of ikon are very involved with the song writing process, he suggested you both have a writing session together. you can discuss your interests in music and see if you could make a song together, does that make sense?"

with wide eyes, she nods vigorously even though she was having a hard time processing this. "this is a golden opportunity, the session is scheduled for tomorrow. if you both come up with a solution and the song is created quickly enough, it can even be released during one of the shows."





+



"i'm done recording, can you pick me up now?" she's humming into the phone, rubbing her arms with the hand that isn't holding the phone to her ear. this morning, the weather had been rather pleasant, to the point where she didn't think it was necessary to bring a coat. but soomi hadn't thought ahead to the fact it would be cold by the time she was leaving the company, meaning she was standing out in the back where jinhwan had dropped her off this morning, freezing her ass off.

"i'm on the way to my car now," his voice breaks through the sound of her teeth chattering together from the cold, "i'll be there soon, okay?"

he sounded as if he was about to end the call. before he could, she hurriedly speaks up, "jinani-ah, wait, did you guys get a call from ceo-sunbaenim?" 

there was a moment of silence, and the sound of a closing car door. she presumed he was getting situated, before he replies, "oh, yeah we did, sounds like your manager was serious about asking him about us working together." he didn't sound all too surprised as she was about it.

"i know," she hums, "that's not surprising to you - or even scary?"

"i mean, it's surprising, but i'm glad he's open to us doing this," he casually mutters, seeming in focus as the car engine was heard roaring to life through the phone, "and scary? i'm not going to grope you on stage, soomi."

she was glad he couldn't see her cheeks heat up through the phone. "yah, who said that?! i swear, you're so inappropriate, kim jinhwan, gentlemen my ass."

he's doing that cute little giggle as he whines, "i was just joking!"

rolling her eyes even though he couldn't see her, she only mumbles, "if you say so, now hurry up~, it's cold."

"pabo, you didn't bring a jacket?" he cries in disappointment.

"i forgot."

"soomi, it's like negative degrees outside, you're going to get sick."

"i know, that's why i'm telling you to hurry!"

"i'm trying," he only heaves out a sigh. soomi mumbles an okay before ending the call.

looking back up towards the darkening sky, she runs a hand through her hair after putting her phone back in her bag. the stars were beginning to become visible despite the city lights, but they were only a mere speck in the sky. in order to see them, you had to visually focus as hard as possible and squint your eyes. 

this is what she was doing, when she heard the sound of a rustling bush. it was louder than what she thought the wind could do, causing her head to immediately whip that way. 

blinking a few times, she didn't see a sign of anyone or thing. this was confusing to her since she swore the wind hadn't been blowing that hard, but she found herself shrugging it off. even though it was low key creepy.

"jinhwan will be here soon," she says to herself, trembling in the cold and now wanting nothing more than to be in his arms.

what felt like a millennia later when in reality, it was only a mere ten or so minutes, the headlights of his camaro became visible. the vehicle had pulled into the lot of yg entertainment, swiftly pulling around the back until it came to a halt in front of her.

her eyes widening in joy that he was finally here, she practically ran like a lunatic to the passenger side. swinging open the door, she chunks her bag to the back seat before climbing inside the car.

"jinhwan-ah!" she's squealing like that of a kid as she closes the door. jinhwan had looked at her in question as to the sudden change in attitude, when she'd climbed across the seats to go onto his lap.

his eyes widened due to the fact they were still in the parking lot of their work and she was suddenly sitting in his lap like a lost puppy who had just found its home. this was shocking because she was always trying to act cool, like she didn't want him as bad as she did. soomi was stubborn, and wanted to seem strong and independent, even though she was the complete opposite. 

so, when she was all of a sudden showing the true side of herself, he didn't know how to react.

"are you okay?"

a pout forms on her lips, "no, i missed you."

chuckling, he raises a brow, "i can see that," he teases, "it's like you can't stand being away from me for a day." before she can fight back from his joke, he reaches out to touch her arms, only to notice she was freezing. "holy shit," he mumbles, leaning away from her to pull his hoodie over his shoulders.

her face turns shocked at his action, "oh, i didn't mean in that way exactly, but i'm not complaining."

at the fact she'd thought the complete wrong thing, he couldn't help but start laughing, "i'm not fucking you in the parking lot of the company, soomi."

her cheeks turn a light shade of pink, and she realizes what he had actually been doing when he begins to pull the hoodie over her head. wanting to quickly hide the blush, she grabs it from him and finishes pulling it down.

"boring," is all she says as she climbs off his lap and onto the middle seat since the cup holder was set back. after putting her seat belt on, she'd laid her head on his shoulder, yawning as she mutters, "let's go home then."

his bare shoulder was warm against her cheek. jinhwan taking off his hoodie left him in just his white tank top, much like the other day. this meant his tattoos were on full display, and she closed her eyes so she wasn't distracted by looking at them. because god knew it only made her crave him.

"cutie," she hears him mumble as he'd sped to the exit of the parking lot, chuckling to himself at how whipped he was for her.














07:00 | whipped cream

The end of the year award shows were coming up quicker than Soomi was expecting. With the days approaching at a scary speed, she only forced herself to work harder, even when she grew exhausted.

Weeks had passed, and with her waking up at 7 AM everyday and heading down to the studio to practice with her choreographer, she felt prepared to showcase her new song at MAMA, Melon, and the other shows that were scheduled. Although she had worked hard enough, and even begun the filming for the music video for her new single, this meant she had barely even spoken to Jinhwan. The boys were working on new music as well, as they were releasing a new song called I'm Ok. This meant they were also usually at the studios practicing, and their heavy schedules clashing against each other meant they hadn't said anything more than a mere 'hello' in those two weeks.

With all the blood, sweat, and tears she had spilt, she felt just like she had the last couple of years. Ever since her debut, Soomi had never even thought about a break. After all, being at the top was her dream, so she always wanted to constantly work towards it.

This in mind, you'd assume it would be easy for her to go back to the way she had always been. Hardworking was why the CEO always loved her so much along with her screaming talent. Never had she been distracted by anyone or anything, which was why it was always so easy for her to stay on task.

However, now that she genuinely thought about it, her slacking began when she met Jinhwan.

In a way, she didn't see that as a bad thing. Soomi had an undeniable liking towards him, one she couldn't seem to quite explain or put her finger on, or maybe she was just refusing to. But Jinhwan had pulled her out of a dark place. Even though she was successful, she'd been stressed with all the weights thrown on her shoulders. Without him, she would've only gotten worse. 

But she always grew to like being around him more than she should've. She liked the feeling of just being normal. The feeling that they were just normal idiots in their early twenties, making stupid decisions together without having to worry about what the next day brought. It was one she had never felt until she was with him, and the more she had experienced it, the more she craved it, she craved him.

Jinhwan ignited a flame inside of her. A flame of acceptance, of admiration, of love. There were so many things she liked about him. Other than loving her fans, she had never been sweet to anyone else. Soomi was always cold, she was always careless if it didn't have to do with her career.

But he changed that about her.

In that sense, she was thankful for Jinhwan. However, she knew that being around him for a mere second would only distract her from the tasks at hand more. She needed to make sure she had the choreo perfect, that she had the facial expressions perfect, and the live vocals. If everything wasn't precise, she feared in horror, she wouldn't be the number one anymore. 

Because deep down, even though she always acted as if she was strong, Soomi was scared and weak. She was vulnerable to the worries that formed in her head, and there were a lot of them.

Extending her arms to the air, a yawn leaves Soomi's lips as she tilts her head back, stretching her exhausted muscles. Yet another night of tireless practicing had passed, and she finally felt confident in the dance for her new song. 

Walking to the exit of her practice room, she grabs that same blue bag she always carried, slinging it over her shoulders. Running a hand through her damp hair from sweat, she flicks the light off as she leaves the room. For once, every thought in her head seemed to clear out, until she looked down at the bag she was carrying.

A vivid memory of Jinhwan shoots into her brain like a sleep dart. At once, it was as if someone had hypnotized her into such a sight, as she saw it again so clearly in her head. The way he was, the gentlemen act he claimed to go for. Of course, she was only joking with him when she said he wasn't. In reality, Jinhwan was probably the sweetest person she had ever met. Never had she seen someone be so selfless and thoughtful towards those around them. It was a strong quality and one of the many things she admired about him.

Just the thought of him and the magnificence he was made her want to text him, but she hurriedly beats the thought out of her head. I can't, she tells herself, no distractions. After all, the rehearsals for Melon started yesterday. I'll be seeing the boys there, anyway, she remembers, shaking her head in worry as she left the company that night.




+




The sound of her phone rapidly going off, not one but many times, was what woke Soomi up the next day. Her head was practically pounding from the annoying sound of the default ringtone, and she scurried out of her comfy duvet in search for the device, eager to stop the horrible tone. 

"I swear to god, I will murder whoever is calling me," she's mumbling when she looks to the alarm clock on her end table. It was five in the morning, and she was enjoying her beloved beauty sleep.

Due to the fact that her eyes were still half closed, she didn't even see who was calling when she slid her thumb across the screen to answer it. She also didn't notice it was a FaceTime call, meaning she nearly fell off her bed from shock when she saw a newly-awoke Jinhwan on her screen.

"Yah, Soomi-ah, you look like a mess, did you just wake up?" his raspy morning voice breaks through her skull like an avalanche, crushing all of her emotions and turning her into a hungry dog. 

Shaking the want away, she holds her phone with one hand as she falls back onto her comfy mattress, rubbing her eyes with the other. "Nani, it's five in the morning, of course I did," she rolls her eyes, not wasting a second to sass him.

Pouting from her rude tone, he wasn't too phased by it as he hums, "Mianhae, I just wanted to tell you I heard the teaser for your new song, it sounds so good! I can tell you've been working hard the last couple of weeks since we haven't gotten to see each other."

Still not fully awake, her head falls onto the soft pillow, making her words come out barely audible, "oh, thanks."

As soon as her face felt the featherlike fabric, she could feel the dreamland calling her once again. Jinhwan sees this, and begins clapping into the phone speaker as he calls, "Kim Soomi-ah, don't fall asleep!"

A whine of annoyance leaves her lips as she rolls onto her back, holding the phone above her. "I'm tired, I can't believe you woke me up for this."

"Why not? I missed you and I wanted you to know you're doing well."

"I'm literally going to see you in like five hours."

"Or you could see me right now when I come over."

"No," she's quick to snap at him, her eyes suddenly wide open as if she just heard something mind-blowing. 

He wasn't bothered by her stern voice, though. Through his phone camera, she could already see him getting out of bed. Soomi knew exactly what that meant, and her suspicion was confirmed further when he hums, "I'm on my way now."

Knowing there was no way he was going to listen to her telling him no for once, she groans, "ugh, wait, I'll just come to yours instead."

That seemed to of grabbed his attention, as he had stopped from digging through his closet to find some clothing to wear. Raising a brow, he asks, "why?"

Already climbing out of her own bed, she sighs, "because if I have to deal with your annoying ass, I at least want to eat Yunhyeong's cooking for breakfast."






+




It was an early morning in the iKON household. The boys mostly hardworking and only occasionally lazy, and today was one of those days where the determined, passionate side of them and their love for their job would come out. At around 7, the seven boys were awake and sitting in the kitchen while Yunhyeong was cooking when Soomi arrived.

The one who answered the door was Junhoe, who usually was never very thrilled to see her since she was around so often. If she was being honest, he was probably the one she got along the best with out of all the boys in the group. The both of them had the same sort of humor, and a knack for just messing around with everyone (then again so did the others,) but her and June had always connected in another way. The two of them showed their appreciation for each other through their negative comments and jokes.

However, the aura around him was different that day when he grinned at the sight of the small girl on their doorstep, "Soomi, you're alive."

"June-ya!" she screams like that of an obsessed fan girl, already beginning her child like attitude as she jumps forward to hug him, "it's been so long, you've already gotten taller!"

Although he reluctantly accepts her hug, he's chuckling as he shakes his head at her, "it's too early for this."

Frowning since he didn't play along like usual, she nods, "yeah, you're right," the rest of the boys had looked out into the living room to see what was going on. At the sight of her, they all seemed cheery, especially Jinhwan.

"Hi, Soomi, I didn't know you were coming over!" Donghyuck cries brightly as she walks over to hug him and the rest of the boys. 

They were all walking into the kitchen and taking their seats after she'd greeted everyone. Casually, she falls onto the booth, scooting in next to Jinhwan so there was enough room for Junhoe beside her as well. As she does so, she scooted so close to him her entire body was nearly one with his. It was an extremely unusual act out of her, and it had all the boys taking a double take.

Their minds wander back to nearly a month ago. We're just best friends, the words of the two of them repeated in their heads. Everyone at the table - except for possibly Soomi - knew Jinhwan had strong feelings for her. He never shut up about her, and that day when those words so normally slipped from her lips, they could tell his heart was demolished by hearing such.

But now that they were seeing what was right before them, the six of them exchange knowing glances. There was definitely a spark there.

"You got whipped cream on the corner of your mouth," Jinhwan isn't paying attention to the fact that everyone in the kitchen of the iKON dorm was watching the two of them like it was an intense drama as he giggles down at the girl beside him. 

Not really listening to his words, Soomi glances up to meet his humor tinted eyes, "huh?" However, she jumps when his face was abruptly right in front of hers, the proximity creating a tornado within her heart. She could practically feel the sweat forming on her forehead and the way she clenched her hands together under the table as he'd leaned it, kissing the corner of her mouth before ever so gently running his tongue across the corner, getting rid of the substance that had been there.

With her eyes bulging out of her sockets, she can only peer up at him, completely dumbstruck. The other members had witnessed the entire thing, and it was rather difficult for them to hold in their laughter at her blatant expression. Usually, Soomi was hard headed and stubborn. She refused to admit to her true feelings, and always liked to seem like nothing in the world could taint her untouchable attitude. But with the way Jihwan triumphantly grins down at her as if he had done the most wonderful deed, it was clear the table's were turning.

"Why are you gawking at me like that? Hurry and eat, rehearsals start soon," he hums to her as he goes back to eating his pancakes, snapping the shocked girl out of her daze.

Rubbing her temples, she shakes her head to try to rid herself of the tickling feeling creeping through her veins. This boy is something else.

Jinhwan tries not to show the obvious smirk on his face as he chews. For now, he had her right where he wanted her.




08:00 | bittersweet memories

Award show rehearsals were always hectic. Usually even more than the actual show itself, since it was the first time for some of the artists to be there after a year or at all. Nobody knew when they would be on, the arrangements, where to go at the right given second, and most importantly, who else would be there.

With the fact she had been one of the top winning artists for the last few years, Soomi was well known at every show. In fact, she was the top winning solo artist. With selling millions of albums globally, everyone - especially idol wise - knew who she was when she entered a room. She was never friends with many idols, as a lot of them didn't like her because of her success. As usual, she always shrugged it off, not her fault she was so talented.

At the new show tomorrow, she would be show casing her new single, How's This.
