It was a song that showed her carefree attitude she was so well known for, talking about how she likes to breathe and let go, live her life differently than the stereotypical idol. With an upbeat dance, she was sure her fans would react positively to her upcoming comeback.

Along with that, she was prepared for all the awards that were going to be announced that she was nominated for. She was even going to announce an award herself, one that was for boy groups. Actually, I'm pretty sure iKON are in the nominations as well.

She bows to the security guards that had escorted her to the doors of the backstage entrance of the enormous arena where Melon was held. There, they'd opened the door for her, and she hurries inside to the hectic area. Immediately, her eyes were met with the crowds and crowds of workers. Everyone, along with many recognizable idols, were in a rush to try to understand when they were coming on stage and what queue to look for.

With a sigh as she directs her eyes towards the stage, she recognizes one of the only friends she had practicing on stage for their performance. A smile finds it's way to her lips as she watches the girls of Blackpink. With their debut having had come a couple years ago, they were rising to fame quickly, about as fast as she had. The five of them got along well, and she was actually allowed to be around them.

Watching their rehearsal, she grows distracted for a while. She knew hers wasn't going to be around until towards the end of the show. They always saved the most shocking performances for that time. Then, at the end of the show, the grand prize would be announced, and the winning artist would give an encore stage.

Starting to bop her head along to the music the girls were performing, she saw a few idols she barely knew waving at her. Offering a grin and a wave back at them, she was pulled out of her trance when her phone furiously vibrates in the back pocket of her jeans.

Taking it out of her pocket, she has to hold her hand over the top of the phone to block out the lights so she could clearly read the text.

jinani:
come to our dressing room 

soomi:
y

jinani:
bc i wanna see you

soomi:
we saw each other like 2 hours ago

soomi:
jinhwan, you're so clingy

jinani:
if you knew how beautiful you were you would be too :)

jinani:
you're a little clingy too recently~
don't make me talk about the other day when I picked you up

soomi:
omg

soomi:
shut up, don't

soomi:
give me a bit, i have to try to slip back there
 without anyone noticing

soomi:
you know it's dangerous :(

jinani:
we're dangerous. that's ok.

jinani:
be careful, baby <3


Her cheeks heat up just reading the name, and she quickly shoved her phone back in her pocket. Using her hands, she covers her face so people around her didn't see how angrily her cheeks were. Without another thought, she tries to think back to where the dressing rooms were, but how was she supposed to know where iKON's specifically was? It felt like there were over a million artists here.

Although she knew where the dressing room halls were, she didn't know which one they'd be down. However, thankfully she'd pulled out her phone just to ask him that question, only to see he'd already texted it to her.

Second hall, next to IU's and IZ*ONE's.

Blinking a few times as she mentally pictures the route, she types an okay, be there soon, before hitting the send button. She still had a while until it was her turn to rehearse, anyway. There was enough time to spare.

Deeply exhaling as if to prepare herself to go stealth mode, she puts her phone back in it's spot before scurrying through the crowds of people. As she does so, she keeps her head low in hopes no one pays much attention to her. If so, she was just going to act like she was going to her own dressing room anyway, since it was down the same hall.

"Excuse me, excuse me, mianhae," she's repeating the same words nearly every few seconds as she keeps accidentally bumping into people. In response, they'd brighten their eyes before bowing to her, offering her a hello. She noticed there were actually a lot of artists she didn't know, figuring they must be newbies to the business.

"Wah~, did you see that? Kim Soomi talked to me," one had whispered to another one of her members in exasperation, squealing like that of a child.

"She won top female artist every year since her debut, right?" another one had asked curiously.

"Yeah, she's so cool!" they'd all gushed to each other, and Soomi wanted to turn around and thank them, but she figured that'd be a little weird - and one of them might faint.

So, she only continued on her way. Finally, she was met with the many halls that each led down for what seemed like miles, lined with many dressing rooms for every artist there. Her eyes peered down each one, finding the second one, and she smiles victoriously to herself.

Just going to my own dressing room, she reminds herself, stepping out one foot to begin on her way. That was, until a boy who seemed to come out of the ceiling had stepped in front of her, preventing her from actually taking the step forward and scaring the living shit out of her.

"Yah!" she squeaks, a hand flying to her heart from the shock she had just been thrown into. Slowly, her green eyes make their way up the figure, and it takes her a moment to actually process who was standing in front of her.

The sight of the all too familiar face was enough for her to nearly belch on sight. Not because something was wrong with this particular person, but the astonishment of seeing them that brought upon her this certain feeling. In complete disbelief, she has to turn away, holding a hand to her head to try to calm her screaming emotions.

"Soomi-ah, you look like you just saw a ghost? Am I that scary?" the boy voices out, his now much taller height than her almost over bearing as he stepped a little closer, bending down to meet her eye level so he could peer at her.

His eyes were full of questions, full of things she knew he wanted to ask her. If she stayed around him any longer, if she spoke to him, she knew people would see. She knew he would see, he would come talk to her as well. 

She didn't have the strength to do that.

"Uh, I-," she can't seem to find words as she slowly backs away, the nerves she usually never had suddenly rising up inside of her like a wildfire. The memories began to shoot into her brain, the ones she had forced herself to push away and burn. They had come back to her as she felt her breathing grow irregular, everything was coming back to her. 

"I'll see you around, Jimin," she manages to voice out to stop herself from growing too emotional. I'm not like this anymore, she reminds herself harshly, I'm not that weak anymore.

Before he could say another word to convince her otherwise, she pushes past the old friend. Her feet are quick against the tiled floor, and she could hear the stunned boy call back to her in hope, "Soomi, wait, this isn't about-," but she didn't let herself hear the name.

She couldn't, not after all this time. How silly of her to think something like this wouldn't happen, of course they would be here, after all. What did she expect? 

For them to not approach her, she answered the question for herself mentally. Just meeting the eyes of him had brought it all back, all the glistening memories that turned bitter. Something so sweet had grown so sour, and it hurt her, even then, even now.

Pushing her hair out of her eyes as she continues running down the hall, she has to fight back the tears. I don't cry anymore, but sometimes, even with someone as strong-willed as Soomi, she couldn't hold it in.

Desperately, her eyes search for the familiar sign on one of the doors. Monsta X, Seventeen, Pentagon, she kept reading the names, growing more hopeful with each one. 

Until finally, there it was. 

iKON.

A breath of relief leaves her lips, and she rushes towards the door. Looking either sides of her, there was no sight of anyone around the halls. With one last exhale, she turns the door handle and runs into the room.

The seven boys were lounging around on the sofas set in the room. Their heads whip up in surprise to her, but unlike usual, it didn't disappear once they saw it was just her. No, they kept looking at her with wide eyes.

"Soomi, are you okay?" the concerned words were thrown at her from practically all of them at once, "what happened?!"

Blinking at them in wonder as to why they were asking, she raises a hand to feel under her eyes. She was crying, without even knowing.

"fuck," she mumbles under her breathe as she sniffles the pain away, dipping her head so her hair covered her face and using the back of her hand to wipe the tears away. She didn't want to be weak again.

Before she knew it, a certain boy was by her side. His hands make their way under her chin, where he forces to her look up so he could rid her of her tears himself. As he did so, she notices Jinhwan is already dressed, his makeup on as well, for his rehearsal. His light colored locks were styled to perfection, his dreamy eyes soft with worry as they stare down at her.

For a while, she wanted nothing more than to stare at him. He was so gorgeous to her, he was caring and loving. He was everything, and she had to tell herself that many times. Just looking up at his delicate expression, she felt safe. She felt okay again, just in his presence.

Until he finally uttered the words she had a feeling she was going to hear, "what's wrong, Soomi-ah?"

Letting out a deep breath, she tries to regain her composure. Forcing her courage to come back, she coughs a bit so her voice is back to normal, "nothing, it's really nothing, just an uh, an anxiety thing. I get a little nervous sometimes."

Every one of the boys was staring at her in distrust. A few of them exchange glances, a nervous look on their faces. It was clear they were all thinking the same thing, even Jinhwan had a small idea. After all, it was the only thing that would ever make someone so headstrong like Kim Soomi shed a tear. 

There wasn't a single person in the arena that didn't know about it.

And knowing who was there, they knew it made perfect since. 

A sigh leaves Jinhwan's lips as he feels nothing more than a bundle of mixed emotions piling up inside him. Shaking his head, he doesn't know what else to do as he wraps his arms around her, pulling her fragile body into his chest.

You'll be okay, Soomi.







09:00 | the scandal

"iKON, it's time for your rehearsals," a voice suddenly sounds from the other side of the door, followed by a series of gentle knocks.

Soomi's eyes widen as she realizes she was still there, and there was a chance the worker would come inside. The boys notice this as well, and Jinhwan is quick to think as he scurries around the room. Hanbin calls in reply to the staff member, saying they will be out there shortly and they just have to finish up something.

In a hectic manner, the short fairy-like boy bolts across the room. He grabs his previous outfit that he'd worn to the arena in the first place. It was a hoodie, followed by a cap and a mask that would cover the lower part of her face. She only watches with curious eyes as he helps her slip it over her head, putting the cap atop her hair and then hiding it behind the hood of the hoodie that he flips on top of the hat. Lastly, she gets the gist of what he was trying to do, taking the mask from his hands and slipping the sides over her ears so it was over her mouth.

The other boys gather round to see the progress, humming in satisfaction. "I can barely recognize you now," Donghyuk is humming with a grin.

Nodding in agreement, Junhoe motions to the way the hoodie is too long for her and rather baggy, "look, somehow Jinhwan's midget clothes are too big for her."

Narrowing her eyes at him, she walks forward to slap his arm when Jinhwan does it for her, earning a curse from the giant boy. Soomi only giggles, when Bobby had slipped something onto her face, throwing her into a state of surprise once again.

"What's this?" she's asking even though she already knew from the way it turned her vision dark.

He shrugs, "Just in case someone sees your eyes. You know, green eyes aren't common in Korea."

A look of understanding spreads over her face, he was right. "Nice thinking," she compliments, and he only offers her a wink.

"You should be able to get out safe now, people will just think you're a staff member," Jinhwan tells her reassuringly as the boys all gather around the exit, preparing to head to their rehearsals. He leans down to leave a lingering kiss on her cheek, before planting one on each corner of her mouth. She frowns at the fact he hadn't actually kissed her when he did this, however.

In frustration, she wraps her hand around the nape of his neck, pulling his face forward so she could do it herself. Although shocked at first from her bold action, their lips melted into each other for a few seconds until someone clears their throat.

"I'd love to let you guys suck each other's faces off but, we have a stage to do," Hanbin informs, crossing his arms over his chest and giving them an unimpressed look.

Chuckling at his leader-like attitude, Soomi pulls away from the boy before her. A tint of rose was left on her cheeks as she waves to him, "Good luck, I'll be watching~!" she calls this out to all the members, not just Jinhwan.

They all offer her their bright smiles, showing their thanks for her words. Waving in return to her, she only watches as they all exit the dressing room.

A sigh leaves her lips as she watches them disappear into the hall. Now she just has to get out of here.

Running a hand through her hair, she waits a few minutes after they had left. The staff would most likely be coming back to their dressing room soon, so she knew she had to leave while it was still safe.

Walking to the door they had exited out of previously, she slowly turns the knob. Sticking her head out into the hall, she looks either way, seeing no one. This gives her the courage to casually walk out, a breath of relief leaving her lips as she breaks into a jog towards her own dressing room.

Pulling out the key she was given to it, she sticks it in the keyhole to unlock the door to her safe place. The only thing was, it was already unlocked.

Her eyes fall out of their sockets as she looks down at the handle, her mouth agape, completely dumbfounded. Who would've gotten into her room? She wonders in astonishment, backing up so she can double check the sign to make sure it was actually hers.

Sure enough, there was her name planted on the paper taped to the door. It was hers.

This only led her to wonder even more. Perhaps it was one of the staffs, but they don't usually leave the door unlocked. Also, there was no point for them to be here yet since there was still a while until her own rehearsal, so she didn't need to get into dressing yet.

Feeling a nervous pit form at the bottom of her stomach, she tried to think of what she was walking into as she turns the handle. Her feet feel heavy against the tiled floor, and slowly, she closes the door behind her. Pulling the hoodie off her head, she lets her locks fall down her back freely as she fully enters her dressing room.

Someone immediately rose from one of the sofas at the sight of her entrance. "Soomi," they speak her name in one breathe, their voice booming through her skull and throwing her into an instant trance. Her heart seems to come up her throat, and she feels her breathe hitch as she slowly turns, daring to meet their eyes.

No, her insides are screaming as she stares at the boy through the darkness of the sunglasses Bobby gave her, no, tell me this isn't happening.

Pulling off the sunglasses and the mask, she has to squint her eyes to make sure she wasn't seeing things.

He was really there.

Feeling like all of her emotions were bound to pour out of her at once, she felt her bottom lip quiver as she breathes out, "J-Jungkook?"









+









"I'm breaking up with you," he states again coldly, his eyes not meeting hers. Instead, they're staring ahead at a painting on the wall as he then warns, "so get the fuck out of my house."

The girl can only look up at him, gawking as a sign of disbelief and complete bewilderment. Her green eyes are glassy from the tears threatening to spill, and her jumbled mind tries to make up any sort of excuse for what was happening.

"Why? Is this just because of that?" she voices out her thoughts, and she knew he was aware of what she was talking about. How could he not be? It only happened hours ago. The events that occurred were so clear in her mind even though she tried to erase them, she thought everything would still be okay. 

Finally, when she still doesn't budge from where she's standing firmly on the carpeted floor, his doe like eyes find a way to hers. When they make eye contact, she searches for any sort of emotion on his face, scanning every inch of it. But there was nothing, he looked like a robot, only staring back at her emotionless.

"Leave," he repeats again, raising his voice and making it harsher.

Mouth agape, tears rush out of her eyes and begin to stream down her face just as quick as they fell. Everything in her heart, all the emotions she felt, were breaking out of her and falling around her like shattered glass. For once, after so long she'd laid her heart and soul out to someone, only for it to be violently torn apart so suddenly. She didn't understand, she couldn't understand.

"But why - just because of the anonymous letter sent to Dispatch?!" she cries out through her sobs, unable to control everything coming out of her as she falls to her knees.

Scoffing, the boy shakes his head in incredulity as he snaps, "you really don't get it do you? I sent the letter."

If she was stunned before, she definitely was now. Her eyes are wide as she looks back up to him from her spot on the ground, her voice cracking as she asks in bafflement, "what?!"

"I sent the letter to get rid of you," he explains fully, his face still showing no sign of emotion.

She couldn't believe it, not a single bit. With the way he'd previously looked at her, the way they kissed, the way they did everything together. It'd always been like their lives depend on each other, despite living the harsh idol life. As long as they had each other, they were happy. That's what she always thought because it was what he always told her, until this.

The entire time he was tired of her?

Each word flying from his lips was like a million daggers to her fragile heart. The tears are spilling in an eruption as she looks down at her hands, her green eyes falling onto a certain finger.

In whispers barely audible to the human ear, she whispers, "but you asked me to marry you..." her voice trails off as she stares down at the diamond ring on her left ring finger. It shone so brightly despite the dull atmosphere. The place she once grew so accustomed to because of the love it was covered in no longer felt like home as she watched the wet droplets fall onto the palms of her hand.

"You're a mistake, Soomi, get out of my house," he repeated his angered words once again. One last time, she looked up at him, only to see his frustrated eyes already narrowed down at her.

When she doesn't move still, he'd picked up a photo frame that was on the coffee table. In the photo, it was a picture of the two of them, smiling like there was nothing wrong with their lives. Back then, she realized as she watched him raise it high in the air, she realized there wasn't.

But now it was all gone, and that was only further confirmed as he'd slung the photo frame. Her eyes bulge out of shock as she watches it flail through the air, crashing into the wall with a sharp thud. The glass from the frame explodes, flying across the living room of the apartment.

With that, she knew it was time to go, to let go.

Scurrying to her feet, she turns towards the door, hearing another crack of breaking glass following behind her. Under her feet lay the broken pieces of the glass as it had scattered once again like the last photo frame, along with one of the many pictures he had thrown.

Her eyes linger upon it for a while, and with a deep breathe, she picks it up. Her hand on the door knob as she clutches it tightly under her grasp, she turns one last time to look at him.

"You really don't love me?" she felt the need to ask him, wanting to know for closure. She didn't get any of it still, and she was afraid she never would. But either way, she still needed to know the answer to this one last thing.

Out of energy from throwing and breaking glass all over the apartment, polaroids and pictures scattered everywhere, his breathing is rapid as he slowly turned to look at her again. For a second, she saw a spark of sadness in his chocolate eyes, that he quickly wiped away by replacing with - nothing.

"No."

That was all she needed to hear. Coughing up her own sobs, she didn't want to look at him anymore. Twisting the knob, she rushes out of the apartment, closing the door harshly behind her.

With that, another screaming sound of breaking glass echoes from the room she just left.

"Why?" she asks herself aloud, as if the air was going to give her some sort of answer. Her mind felt like it was melting just trying to figure out the events.

Wiping her tears away, she pulls out her phone as she walked to the elevator. The amount of notifications she had were uncountable, nearly giving her a heart attack.

Most of them were articles written about her, text messages, and a lot of SNS notifications. She was scared to see what they were since she already read a lot of them, the hate messages was enough for her to want to just never use any of it again.

But her curiosity still gets the best of her as she clicks on one of the articles.

BREAKING: Top Female Solo Artist SOOMI's Career Over? Mind-blowing Scandal is Confirmed to be True by Dispatch After They Receive an Anonymous Letter

She didn't even have to read anymore because she already knew what it was going to say. BTS' Jungkook caught to have been dating solo artist Soomi. She should've known something like this was never going to be easy. She should've known she shouldn't go against the rules, God only knew what was going to happen to her and her career now. With the rising fame they had, she felt like everyone in the industry was going to hate her forever. How was she going to recover from this?

A heavy heart was on her shoulders as she walked out of the exit of the apartment building. Her once perfected makeup was smeared by the tears, and she kept her head down, not paying attention to where she was walking as she mentally prepared herself from everything to come. There was nothing she could do now, nowhere she could hide, she had to face everything that was coming to her.

After all, she had already received the text from the CEO asking her to meet him immediately in the morning. It was over for her.

Sudden loud flashes from cameras were heard all around her, causing her head to whip up. At the sight of her state, the reporters only grew more intrigued as they shoved mics and cameras in her face.

"Soomi, why are you crying? Did something go wrong with Jungkook after the scandal was released? Do you know who sent the letter?" They throw the questions at her frantically, desperate to get the answers the media was craving.

"Please leave me alone," she only pleads, pushing through their sweating bodies.

"Soomi, are you still together?" one of them yells, forcing themselves in front of her walking path, before their eyes land on her finger, "is that an engagement ring?!"

Oh no.

"Hey, quit!" she screams in horror as they shoved their camera to take pictures of the ring on her finger. Hurriedly, she rips it off before breaking into a run towards a cap.

The calls of the reporters only seem to follow her as she climbs inside, breathless from the anxiety creeping through her veins.

Informing the driver of her dorm's location, she leans back into the seat. Sniffling her pain away, she tries to tell herself everything was fine. But she knew once she set foot in that dorm it would only get worse for her as the emotions would pour out again.

With teary eyes, only around fifteen minutes later had she entered the building of her own dorm after running away from yet more reporters. It felt like her life was becoming some sort of cruel movie, and she wasn't for it at all. Thankfully, the guards wouldn't let any of them in the building since it was a dorm specifically for many artists under her company.

Running a hand through her hair, she presses the button on the elevator. Using the back of her sleeves to wipe away the tears that were still angrily falling from her eyes, she opens the palm of her hand to look down at the ring one last time.

I should get rid of it, she tells herself the obvious. But deep down, she didn't want to.

When she looked down at it, she was reminded of all the happy times she'd had the last half a year. Everything came flooding back to her, in a complete bittersweet taste.

Still looking down at it, she stepped into the elevator when it dinged. Since it was so late, she didn't expect anyone to be in it, until she feels a warm body collide against hers.

Cursing under her breath, she didn't look up at them as she mumbled a sorry, stepping out of the way so there was enough space.

The person didn't reply, and she didn't care for them to. Still keeping her head down, she manages to press the button to her floor.

An awkward silence overcame the elevator, and she found herself sniffling again. Taking out the picture that was in the pocket of her jeans, she holds it below her, tears falling onto the photo as she peers down at it.

And she still couldn't help but ask herself again - why? Was she really that bad? Why would he fake all of it?

"Are you okay?" the sudden voice breaks her out of her racked emotions.

Surprised by the question, she slowly looked up at him, feeling like she had heard his voice from somewhere. At the sight of his face that was looking down at her, she nearly fell over in the elevator. If she hadn't been stunned then, she now felt like she was witnessing the coming of God from the sky.

"I-," she clears her throat when her voice cracks, "I-I'm fine."

He purses his lips in a thin line, clearly not believing her words. His black fringe falls in his eyes from the way it's parted in the middle, and with the way he's looking at her, she has a feeling he was definitely an idol.

She expected him to recognize her, and when he did, she had a feeling he'd ask about the scandal. After all, that was all she was probably going to hear about for the rest of eternity.

"I'm Jinhwan," is what he says instead, offering a smile, "you're really beautiful, whoever made you cry doesn't deserve you."


She can only stare back at him in consternation. Did he not know who she was? Better yet, had he not heard about it? It was literally everywhere, all over every SNS and news page. Does this guy not own a cellphone?

Realizing she had been staring at him like he was an alien, she hurriedly speaks, although her voice was that of a mouse, "Soomi, nice to meet you - and uh, thank you."

Speaking as if they were already friends, he pouts, "Cheer up, Soomi-ah, following every day is a better tomorrow."

The more she stared at him, the more she felt like he was familiar. Then it seemed to slap her across the face as she remembered. His group had just released a new song called Bling Bling, right?

iKON.

She wasn't able to speak when the elevator dings, signaling she was at her floor. Bowing to him, she forces a grin on her face, "thanks, Jinhwan, I'll try."

Before he could say another word, she left, rushing down the hall. Once she reached her dorm, she unlocked it in a rush.

The sight of the dark, empty place was enough for her emotions to come crashing down on her again. Her back slides against the door as she falls onto the ground, burying her face in her knees.
Wrong, she thought, so wrong.
How could it all end like this?



10:00 | apology

She gawks at him like she'd seen a ghost. In fact, it took her a good five minutes to fully process the boy that was standing in her dressing room.

When she did, it wasn't pretty.

"How did you get in here?!" she snaps, everything rushing back to her and her once surprised soft emotion was replaced with those of anger. "Get the fuck out!"

His face wasn't shocked by her outburst, as if he had expected it. After what he'd done, who wouldn't? Putting his hands up in defense, he slowly approaches her with small steps.

As he does so, Soomi only takes a step back, pointing a finger to the exit, "there's the door."

Frowning, his eyes are pleading as they make contact with hers, "Soomi, just please let me talk first," he desperately begs.

Rolling her eyes, she only coldly shoots the words at her like knives, "go fuck yourself, Jungkook."

Stopping in his steps due to the cold stare she's giving him, his doe eyes are pleading as they peer down at her. She won't meet his eyes any longer as she looks away, because despite everything he had done to her, he still made her feel some type of way. 

Of course, she was never going to admit this. But even after years of mourning your first love and moving on, the smallest amount of adoration would remain in your heart. This was especially true for her, and she no longer had the strength to even look at him period as she turns away.

"I just wanna explain," his voice trails off in a barely audible whisper, "I want you to know I'm sorry."

Her back now to him, she stares at her reflection on the large mirror on the wall she faced. Through it, however, she could also see Jungkook that was standing feet away from her. His face looked torn, he looked shattered. It was as if she had been the one who broke his heart.

And even though she knew she should hate him, even though she believed she did, a part of her heart still aches seeing him that way. It took all of her courage to muster the words, "save it, I don't want to hear it."

"Soomi," he calls out her name again, sounding like a lost animal. When she looks up at the mirror again, her eyes meet his through the reflection. Jungkook's peering at her through the glass like his life depends on it, his brown eyes shining from the tears welling in them, "please."

Don't, she tells herself such a word. But sometimes her heart disagreed with her mind.

"What?" she finally snaps, spinning on her heel to face him with a bold expression. She was trying to show that she was okay, that she had been okay all this time and moved on with her life, because she had. But after that spark had been thrown at her, the candle had been lit again, and the memories she had worked so hard to bury had resurfaced to haunt her. 

Everything she forced herself to lock away had flooded onshore, and it was causing her to break all over again.

When he doesn't say anything for a while, she speaks again in a low tone to try to hide the fact she was nearly breaking into tears, "explain then, tell me why you said you loved me when you didn't, tell me why you asked me to marry you when you didn't want to be with me," her voice cracks as she looks to her feet, feeling weak under his saddened gaze, "tell me why you sent the letter and exposed us to the media, why?!"

From the looks of it, it seemed as if Jungkook hadn't thought out this far into the conversation. He guessed that she would've immediately kicked him out, called security, started throwing things. He'd presumed for her to do a lot of things, but none of them had been actually listening to him.

Either way, he was getting the chance he wanted after all this time. A chance to explain how it all went wrong, how he had no choice but to do such awful things he didn't mean. 

And even though the last year, he'd rehearsed it so many times in his head, now that she was really right before him it was like he was awestruck. The sight of her breaking down in front of him like it was just yesterday, the realization of the toll it had truly taken on her heart. He didn't have the strength to say any words anymore, he didn't want to. He didn't want to do anything that wasn't involved with making her feel better.

Which is why he stepped forward to wrap his arms around her shoulders, pulling her trembling body into his chest. Soomi was shocked, she wanted to push him away, and she knew she should. But a part of her wasn't allowing her to, a part of her wanted to stand there in his arms, even if it was for a mere moment.

Even then, she still didn't know the truth. As far as she knew, he'd carelessly broke her heart and was just now trying to make up for it. However, one thing she did know was that he was the love of her life. Jungkook was the first boy she ever gave herself to, hell, she was going to marry him for god sakes.

No matter what sort of terms they had ended on, she'd always miss the way he made her feel. Which meant that the sudden feeling of his familiar cologne, and the remembrance of how he'd kiss her hair repeatedly while rubbing her back to make her feel better. It was enough to make her not want to move a muscle.

"Jungkook," she weeps, her small hands coming in front of her to ball his tee shirt in her fists. The boy could only rub her back like he always used to as she cried into him, feeling endless guilt for knowing he was the source of her pain.

"I'm sorry," he whispers, "I'm so sorry. I loved you, I loved you so much."

Her crying abruptly stops, as if someone had turned a switch off. There's a long silence, and he feels nerves shoot through his veins as she slowly pulls away. 

"Then why?" is the only words she can ask him, her eyes full of hurt but yet curiosity.

He knew it was now or never.

"I-I was getting threats," he stutters out after a while, his voice shaking with each word, "they started from random texts of photos of us together, to messages saying 'I know your secret, you aren't safe.' And then - they started sending me death threats, saying if I didn't end it they'd release everything they had."

She can't take her eyes off of the shaking boy, completely mortified as he went on, "they had uncountable pictures, ones that would've definitely gotten us both fired. So, I sent the letter and told them I'd do it, they said they'd kill you, they sent me a picture of your bedroom and everything and I - I didn't know what to do," his voice cracks, tears beginning to pour from his eyes as he forces himself to keep talking, getting every bit of the story out, "I didn't care about them for a long time, but it was the night we got engaged they - they sent me a photo of them in your bedroom with a knife, and I - I was so angry and scared so I just..."

His voice is cut off when she places a hand over his mouth to stop him from talking. Soomi's breathing is irregular, and once he gets the gist she didn't need to hear anymore, she releases her hand from his mouth. 

It instead goes to cover her face as she buries her face in her palms. It didn't take long to use his explanation to piece everything together. That night, her apartment did feel different, she had noticed it countless amounts of times. It was as if there was someone there, a different aura floating in the air.

Now she knew why, as each piece fit into the puzzle. She knew why it never made any sense.

"Sorry, I know this is a lot to take in so suddenly," he nervously clears his throat, sniffling as he wipes under his eyes to rid anymore tears, "I just - I felt like you needed to know."

Although she had been trying not to cry again, there was a certain feeling that forms in the pit of her stomach when she looks back up to him again. It was like the strong, everlasting love she always felt for him had never really died, she just threw it away because of how she believed him to be a liar. 

But he wasn't, she realized then, he never was. Someone had blackmailed him, they had been behind all of it because they didn't like their relationship.

It was terrifying, to think someone had been watching them so closely. Not just some paparazzi like a Dispatch worker, but a murderer. Someone who was so ticked off by their relationship that they were willing to kill her.

And she hadn't known it the entire time. The boy who she believed to be a monster had only done it all to protect her.

This, to her, was good and also very bad, in many ways. 

Everything that had built up inside of her, everything seemed to be pouring out of her at once. Finally, her heart was speaking for her when she steps forward, not thinking twice about her rather irrational actions. Instead, for the first time after a long time, Soomi was living in the moment as her hand goes to the nape of Jungkook's neck, pushing his face forward so she could tilt her head to perfectly meet her lips with his.

Bliss, she was reminded of the feeling, the ecstasy that he sent through her body. She thought when she kissed him, that she would be reminded of how it all used to be, as if she hadn't felt it in a long time. 

But the euphoria of his lips against hers, going up into glistening fireworks above them, wasn't foreign to her. Not because she had been with Jungkook previously, but because she felt it with someone else.

That was the last thing on her mind as his lips move in sync against her, his hands going up to cup her cheeks so he could deepen it. Everything was okay.

The two of them grew so lost in each other, no one noticed the sound of an opening door followed by the calling of her name. An abrupt screech is suddenly sounded against the tiled floor, and by the time the two of them pull away from each other to see what was going on, it was too late.

"What the fuck?"







11:00 | how's this?

There he was, standing there with a look of bewilderment on his face. Still dressed in his stage outfit, he looked as if someone had ripped his heart straight out of his chest before throwing it across the world. She noticed then there was a bouquet of roses in his hands, from wherever he may of gotten them, and they fall to the ground as not another word leaves his lips. Not wanting anyone to see the heart broken look on his face, the boy spins on his heel, heading for the door.

Fuck, she mentally curses, immediately breaking into a run towards him. "Jinhwan!" she pleads out his name, her small hand reaching out just as he was grabbing the handle and managing to get the edge of the sleeve of his red jacket.

Deeply exhaling in frustration, he's unimpressed as he looks back to her, deadpanning, "what?"

Frowning at the cold attitude which she had never received from him, she uses her other hand to grab his wrist so she had a better grip on him, "don't go, let me explain."

"There's nothing to explain," he blatantly replies, refusing to meet her eyes as he mumbled weakly, "just let me go, Soomi."

Sudden footsteps sound from behind them, and a confused voice slowly breaks through their moment. "Uh, am I missing something?" Jungkook asks from behind Soomi, motioning in between the two of them as a shocked look forms on his face, "are you two..." he trailed off, not needing to finish the sentence because they all knew what he was suggesting.

At the thought, she thought back to around a month ago when he met Jinhwan's neighbor. He'd called her his girlfriend.

Because of that, she wasn't sure what to say, until Jinhwan speaks up emotionless, "No."

For some reason, she was surprised - and hurt, as she looks back to him from Jungkook.

Before she can say another word, he rips his wrist out of her grip, leaving in a rush. "Yah, Kim Jinhwan!" she calls after him desperately, not caring that it was in the halls, "I'm sorry - you don't understand!" She prayed that he would turn around as he walked at such a quick pace further away from her, but he didn't.

He left, just like that.

Feeling defeated, she turns back into her dressing room, closing the door behind her. Jungkook is watching her with wondrous eyes as she picks up the roses from the ground, holding them close to her as she falls back onto the sofa.

The scent of not Jungkook's, but Jinhwan's, cologne fights it's way into her nostrils when she inhales. Only then was she reminded she was wearing his hoodie still.

"What was that about?"

Her head jerks up to look at the curious boy sitting beside her now. Thinking about it for a moment, she wasn't quite sure how to even answer that.

"It's - it's complicated," she replies in an utter.

With dull eyes, for the next few minutes they sit in silence, Soomi staring at her feet. She didn't know what got into her, what had pushed her to kiss him. It was a heat of the moment thing, it was almost as if she couldn't control herself.

But she didn't really still love Jungkook, did she? 

Did that mean she loved Jinhwan?

It was making her head hurt, and for the thousandth time, she felt like she was going to cry. Too much was happening in one day, this was why she always hated award shows.

The boy beside her seems to notice the way she was acting. Due to how long they had known each other, he knew her too well for her to hide her emotions. 

"You love him, don't you?" she's thrown out of her trance from his sudden words.

Her eyes had widened slightly as she thinks about what he'd just asked her. Jinhwan? Sure, he always made her feel some sort of way. She found herself always longing to be around him, to hear his laugh, to see his smile. The small habits he had she always found adorable, and the flaws he claimed he had - even though they didn't exist - were always her favorite things about him. 

Jinhwan created a tornado of emotions in the pit of her stomach. They'd go through out her body into a field of utopia, and it was like she was floating upon the clouds when she was with him. Although she never thought about something like love when it came to him, could that really be what it was?

"You don't have to tell me, Soomi, I can tell," he says after a long while, the atmosphere growing awkward when he hesitantly asks, "so why'd you kiss me then?"

"I don't know."

She didn't have to look at him to know of the hurt look that spread over his face.

"Soomi-ah, are you in there? It's time for your hair and makeup, your rehearsal starts soon," the sound of one of the staffs voices stuns her. With wide eyes, her and Jungkook exchange a horrified glance, the previous conversation disintegrating.

Thinking quickly, she calls out loudly despite her weak voice, "uh, yes, please allow me a few minutes of privacy, I'm finishing up a quick phone call!"

Mentally pleading they wouldn't question it, there's a short spring of silence. "Oh, okay, we'll return soon," the woman replied casually, and she listens to the footsteps walk away back down the hall.

Sighing, Soomi runs a hand through her hair, pushing it out of her face. Rubbing her temples as she rises to her feet, she tries to forget about all the events of today. I can't believe I kissed Jungkook, she shames herself, and Jinhwan saw. How could she get so caught up in her bundled emotions? 

Sure, this cleared up her heart break that she had felt for so long. What happened back then, it was an undeniable nightmare she thought she would never recover from.

But she had, all thanks to Jinhwan. He'd helped her more than she was letting on, and she realized that now.

I have feelings for him.

Heaving out a sigh, Jungkook gets the method as he rises to his feet. There's a tension in the room as they both made their way to the door, when he turns to look down at her dully.

"I'm sorry about everything, Soomi," he offers one last time, "I hope we can at least, uh, be friends now."

Pursing her lips in a thin line, she shrugs after a few moments, "Sure, Jungkook."




+




"Hyung, where did you go? You're missing Soomi's rehearsal," the six boys are crowded around one of the two entrances to the stage. The curtains that were usually closed during the show so you couldn't see what went on backstage were opened since it wasn't officially the day, allowing all the artists to watch whoever was practicing at the given time. It'd been going on all day, but now finally it was nearing the end of the show, when the most anticipated, popular top-charting artists would perform. One of them happened to be Soomi and her much awaited comeback, and it was hard for the six boys of iKON to get a decent view of her due to all the newbie artists jumping around like obsessed fans.

Donghyuk was the one on the phone with their eldest, who had disappeared rather randomly. Last they knew, after rehearsals he'd come back to their dressing room where they had all been given flowers from the staffs. Jinhwan being the hopeless romantic he was, he was going to joke around and act like he'd gotten them for the girl, and had headed off to her room.

That was the last they saw of him. He never came back, and they figured he was still with her. Although they hadn't gotten to ask Soomi herself since she had been rushed onto the stage, they hadn't seen a trace of him anywhere.

When there isn't a reply from Jinhwan after a long while, Donghyuk grows confused, calling out his name again. However, he's only met with the distant sound of - a sniffle?

Blinking a few times in confusion, the others had been too distracted with watching Soomi's stage to pay attention to his words, "hyung, are you crying?"

Jinhwan curses under his breath, clearing his throat a few times, "No - I just went home because I was feeling a little under the weather."

Through all the years he had known him, Dong knew he was lying. "I know you're lying, seriously, what hap-," he stops talking when he hears a sudden click, indicating he'd been hung up on.

Mumbling to himself in disbelief as he pulls his phone away from his ear, he shakes his head. What the hell was that about?

He wanted to tell the others, but they were too hypnotized by the girl on stage.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f-jCYBRoc7w&feature=emb_title
(Soomi’s performance. Stan Hyuna.)

  
"Yah~, she's so good," Yunhyeong is mumbling to the others, lost in a trance.

I have nothing to hide
I have nothing to avoid
Don't try to fit me inside your useless judgments, it's not fun
Don't try to trap me in your gross standards, it's not interesting

The lyrics seemed to show everyone just how careless and free of a soul she was. With the quirky, bouncy dance movements and the way she smiled on stage, Soomi looked like a girl who had the perfect life. Most would assume such a big idol did, as she would perfectly nail her vocals and do each dance successfully, looking like she was some sort of machine.

When the song ends, she'd left the stage in a rush, completely oblivious to the fact that everyone in the arena had started clapping for her - or more like backstage. All the artists were squealing about how good her new song was and how cool she was, and the boys turn to each other with grins.

But they fade once they notice the missing member.

"Where's Jinhwan?" some of them ask in unison.

"He's at the dorms," Donghyuk fills them in quickly, "I think something happened with him and Soomi."










12:00 | drunk words are sober thoughts

"aren't you performing another new song?" her manager is looking at her confused as she leaves the stage, breathing hard from the choreography heavy song.

Desperate to go home, she only nods, not in the mood for performing or even thinking of that song and what it was about, "I don't need to rehearse it, please trust me," she bows apologetically before leaving her clueless manager.

I have to do something, her mind is yelling at her as she walks through the crowds of artists and staff members. A lot of them are calling to her, saying how they liked her song or she was a good dancer, but she could do nothing more than crack a small smile.

Once she was safely in the halls where the dressing rooms were, she pulls her phone out of her back pocket. her breathe still rigid, she leans against one of the walls, dialing the number quickly before shoving it to her ear.

Please, she prays mentally, diverting her eyes to the ceiling as she listens to it ring. It rang once, twice, three times, and she hoped that soon, she would hear his voice. Soomi knew he was upset, she knew why as well, and she knew that everything wasn't going to instantly be okay. But all she wanted, all she wanted was to talk to him.

The entire time, she supposed, they were more than just stress relief sessions. It was always more than that, wasn't it?

She knew that now.

"God dammit, Jinhwan," she curses, feeling her heart ache in her chest as her fifth call was once again declined. Putting her phone away, she faces the fact that he wasn't going to talk to her, at least not over the phone. The tears rush to her eyes before she can stop them, and she hurriedly uses her hands to wipe them away. I've cried too much today.

She wasn't supposed to be this weak, not anymore.

"Hey, Soomi-ah, what's going on with you two?!"

Her eyes bulge as her head jerked up, only to see six boys running at her like mad men. Right then, they didn't seem to be thinking about the fact they weren't even supposed to be spotted together since they were under the same company, but she guessed it wasn't a big deal since the halls were empty.

And it was definitely the last thing on all of their minds. The boys were tired of being stranded in the dark. As far as they had always known, Soomi and Jinhwan were the perfect match. They never fought, they never got mad at each other, it was a couple made in heaven, destined to be with each other. So what was it that made them this way so suddenly?

Remembering how she was crying earlier, they were a bit worried to hear. But they thought surely there was no way it had to do with that. How could it?

For once, she didn't look thrilled to see them. Her makeup was getting ruined from the tears that flew down her cheeks, and even though a part of her was telling herself it was dangerous to even go towards them, her emotions were telling her otherwise.

"I messed up, guys," she breaks into more tears for the hundredth time that day, stepping forward to accept the group hug they were offering.

"Soomi, hajima, don't cry!" Donghyuk is insisting as he repeatedly wipes her tears away with the pads of his thumbs.

Feeling warmth under their embraces, Chanwoo tries to brighten the mood, "I'm sure it can't be that bad. Jinhwan has anger issues, but he usually gets over it quickly, especially if it's you."

Although thankful for his encouragement, she knew they just didn't know what she'd done yet. "Anio, you guys don't understand."

The hug is broken, and they all stand in a circle. All eyes were on her as Bobby voices, "So inform us."

So, letting out a deep breathe and preparing herself for the lectures, she did.












+






"Yah, Soomi, are you okay?" she nearly stumbles over and falls on the ground when she ends up running straight into the boy who had stopped in front of her. Coughing, she trips over her baby steps, about to flail to the ground when he reached out both of his arms, catching her and steadying her so he could meet her bloodshot eyes and goofy expression. This sight causes him to pause for a moment, narrowing his eyes as he looks at her, "are you drunk?!"

A hiccup left her lips, and as her breathe fanned his skin he smelt nothing more than alcohol. Waving his hand in his face, he shakes his head in disappointment as he stands back up straight, "Seriously, you can't keep doing this to yourself."

She rolls her eyes, trying to push past him but he catches her arm, "Jinhwan, I'm fine! I'm not drunk."

Scoffing, he turns to face her again, "right, you think I'll believe that?"

When she doesn't say anything, he sighs, holding her wrist and helping her to the elevator. The girl could seem to barely walk, and he hated to see her like this. Although he never mentioned it to her, he felt like maybe it was time to. Someone needed to inform her, to make her understand that that useless excuse of a guy wasn't worth her time. It was unhealthy for her to keep sitting around and ruining her life. She hadn't gotten fired from the company, but she sure was going to if she kept going on like this.

However, he just couldn't seem to form the right words in his head. Jinhwan knew her well, she was readable that way. Soomi was strong on the outside, but a feather on the inside. She was practically sensitive to everything, and he knew one wrong word would break her heart all over again.

If he did that, he'd never be able to live with himself.

"Jinani~, you're pretty cute, you know," her slurred words cut him off from his deep thoughts, and he looks down only to see her standing directly under him. Her green eyes are still visible through the darkness of the small space in the elevator, and although bloodshot, he still found them beautiful.

And even though kissing her would be a dream to him, he knew he couldn't. Soomi was healing, she was getting over a boy she loved. He wasn't going to take advantage of her or rush her through that process. Unlike Jungkook, he had respect for her well being. He just wanted her to be happy, he didn't care about himself.

She kept coming closer and closer to his face, the proximity decreasing as time passed. When their noses were nearly touching, their lips only centimeters apart, he knew it was time to stop her before he couldn't stop himself.

Using his pointer finger, he puts it on the tip of her nose and pushes her back. "You're drunk," he blatantly states.

Soomi pouts, "No, I'm Soomi."

Cute.

"Pabo," he mutters under his breath as the elevator dings. He decided to just take her to his apartment since he didn't know where hers even was, and he also didn't have the energy to try to get it out of her since she was so intoxicated. Turning back to the frowning girl, he offers his hand, "come on."

At the sight of this, she perks up like she was being offered candy. Together, they walked down the halls of the YG apartment complex, Jinhwan jingling his keys in the other hand.

"Jinani~, I think you're so special," she kept saying silly things as they went, "you make me forget about him, you're so much better~."

He felt his cheeks heating up, but it wasn't like she was even meaning these things. Shaking it off, he acted as if he wasn't listening to her musical words, stopping before the door of the iKON dorm.

The two of them enter, and he prayed for the boys to all be in their rooms asleep. Soomi is still humming from behind him as they walk inside, and she turns to close the door before looking around to the apartment.

On every wall was something about iKON. There were posters, albums, everything you could imagine was decorated in every corner. As if on queue as soon as she sees it, she's reminded of the newest song they had released.

He hadn't expected her to do anything once she saw them, though, until her yells fills the apartment as she screams, "BLING BLING~!"

"Yah!" he curses in a whisper, covering her mouth with his hands, "Be quiet, the others are asleep."

He also didn't want any of them to know she was here, but it was too late for that.

His nightmares were only further confirmed when a head peeks around from the kitchen, "Jinan brought a girl home-," Junhoe had been humorously making fun of this occurrence, but his mouth falls open when he sees her, "holy shit, is that Kim Soomi?!"

"Oh my god!" Soomi gasps as she gets out of Jinhwan's grip, walking towards the giant boy, "you're the super handsome one!" she points a finger at him knowingly, a satisfied smirk on her face.

Junhoe grins at this, not even noticing how drunk she was due to the compliment. Suddenly, someone else steps into view, "I'm afraid you're mistaken, that would be me."

She gawks at the sight of Yunhyeong. "Fuck~, I'm in a house of cute guys!"

Laughing at her, Junhoe took it into consideration as he looks to the dead inside Jinhwan, "Alright, so why is she shit faced out of her mind?"

The eldest only heaves out a sigh, mumbling, "Don't fucking ask," as he walks by, grabbing her arm on the way and dragging her towards his room.

"No, wait, I want to talk to him," she's whining in desperation, "he's cuter than you!"

"Soomi," Jinhwan pleads as they enter his bedroom, "please - shut up."

Pouting from his words, she falls onto his mattress, uttering an okay.

Silence engulfs the bedroom, and Soomi yawns as she lays her head onto the pillow. It seemed the alcohol was finally throwing her body into a desire for rest as she rubbed her eyes with her fists.

He had been rummaging in his closet to find her something to wear, when he hears a barely audible mumble from his mattress.

"I was just joking, I don't think anyone compares to you~," her voice seems to trail off into the atmosphere as her eyes are closed, but she still managed to utter her last words, "I really like you, Kim Jinhwan."



13:00 | souvenirs

"You guys," her voice had cracked at the end of the long waited explanation, tears rapidly streaming down her face and making her makeup look like a splattered disaster. The six boys are still standing in the hallway, having had been listening to her words intently, and once they all processed what happened, they were gawking. All their faces were written in shock, electrified in astonishment, from each thing they had learned in such a short amount of time.

That first night, when Jinhwan brought her home drunk, was the first time they ever met the famous solo artist, Kim Soomi. They'd heard about the scandal because it was everywhere, always being the talk of each morning. To be truthful, the boys never thought she did anything wrong. All she was doing was trying to live her life aside from her career, trying to be happy.

And as they got to know her more, they grew a special liking for her. Soomi wasn't only their hyung's obvious love interest, she was their closest friend other than each other. They felt the need to protect her after seeing how torn she was after what happened with the member of BTS. With each second they got to know her, they learnt how soft of a soul she was, and how she didn't deserve any bit of what he had done to her.

So to now hear that Jungkook really didn't mean any of it, that someone was blackmailing him. It blew their minds from one end of the world to another. Everyone on the face of the planet seemed to know how in love she had been with him. 

Since what happened between them was so tragic, Soomi had no other choice than to move on from her first love. The boys figured it was for the better, since her and Jinhwan were such a strong match.

However, they now feared that since she was learning this - she might forget about their hyung. It made sense as to why he was so torn over the phone, this was why. He felt like he had lost the girl of his dreams after all this time.

This was why the six boys of iKON felt nauseous as they listened to the rest of her story. The next words they expected to hear was that she had dug up her unrequited love for Jungkook, realizing Jinhwan was truly just her stress relief and that she didn't really love him like he loved her. They were all so worried to hear this, that the six almost fell over from surprise when she finishes her statement.

"I really love him.”

Or did she?




+



"Hyung, you can't stay in bed for the rest of your life," Bobby is insisting as they pull the duvet from over his head for what seemed like the 90th time. In response, like every other time, the eldest of their group would only snatch it right back, covering his weakened, puffy face back up with the fabric.

A heaved sigh is heard from under the sheets of his bed where he was still retreating, "Get the fuck out."

Hanbin, Bobby, and Donghyuk all exhale deeply, exchanging looks in hope one of the others knew what to do. Upon realizing they all had the same clueless expression on their face, they felt hopeless.

How were they supposed to cheer him up? After hearing Soomi's confession, they wanted to tell Jinhwan about it even though she had asked them not to, telling them to just give him time. But the boys hated seeing the two of them like this, as even she was completely destroyed since they saw her earlier. It was unnecessary to them. They had a show to perform tomorrow, and with the two of them looking like they just fought a war, how were they supposed to pull that off?

The thing was, since they had gotten home, whenever one of them would even utter the beginning of her name, Jinhwan would get scarily angry. It was like he suddenly had a bipolar disorder and her name was his trigger word. Even saying the syllable Soo, would cause him to get up and practically start beating you up - Chan was the one who unfortunately discovered this.

So, they supposed after trying countless times to explain the girl made a mistake and was hopelessly in love with him, they faced the fact she was right. Jinhwan needed time. He needed time to think about it, and so did Soomi. 

She needed to process the abrupt news that her first love had been threatened into breaking her heart, which was the only reason he did. She needed time to realize, however, that although Jungkook had been her world, she had a galaxy now.

"Alright, hyung, tell us if you need anything," they all looked at each other one last time, mentally agreeing that this was the best thing to do.

Their words don't gain a reply from Jinhwan, who didn't even move a muscle from his hidden position. Dully staring at the state he was in, they shake their heads as they gradually left his bedroom, giving him the space he wished for.



+



It's weird to think about, how if you exclude a minor thing from the past, your entire life could be drastically changed up until now. Even something as tiny as not saying hello to someone, or not picking something up, could be the mix between life and death. With the scary ways the world worked, this is what Soomi found herself thinking as she lets out a shaky sigh, her chin in the palm of her hand as her eyes fixate on the wall of her bedroom.

If whoever had blackmailed Jungkook hadn't, she would be married right now. She might not even still be an idol, fuck, she could've had a kid already. There were so many things that would've happened if such an obstacle hadn't occurred. Then, Jungkook wouldn't of done that to her, the confusion wouldn't of been created, she would still be with him.

But then, she wouldn't have met the boys, or Jinhwan.

All her life, she had heard the saying everything happens for a reason, and only now was she wondering if that was true.

"Jinhwan-hyung will come around, Soomi," Yunhyeong had been saying as he sits on her bed, along with the other boy who had been staying around at her apartment with her since after the rehearsals. The two were worried about her, and they wanted to make sure she was okay through everything going on.

They understood how it was all one big misunderstanding. Her emotions had gotten the best of her, and no doubt there was now a lot of things on her mind.

"He loves you," Yun had finished his statement with those three words.

Hearing that causes her to shift in her position, suddenly uncomfortable. He loves me, she repeats it in her head, and it sounded almost foreign the more she thought about it.

Noticing her distraught face, Junhoe wraps an arm around her shoulders in comfort. Thankful for him, a close lipped grin spreads over her face as she leans on his chest, deeply exhaling.

"I guess I just didn't want to be true to it," she finally admits, "I was scared of giving myself over to anyone else after that - but now I know all of it was forced, I just don't know how to feel."

"That's normal, it's a lot for you to comprehend," Junhoe rubs her forearm reassuringly, "seriously, Soomi, all jokes aside, we'll be right here with you to get you through this."

In that moment, nothing could compare to how much she adored them. Nearly crying all over again this time out of gratitude to the two of them, she pulls Yunhyeong towards the two that were already hugging by his sleeve, forcing him to join in.

"Thank you both," she offers them as they all sit there on the edge of her bed, "nothing can explain how much I love you guys."

"We love you too, and so does Jinhwan, he'll come to his senses eventually and then you can tell him everything," Yun pats her back, and she nods in agreement, "I know every puzzle piece will fall into place, don't worry!"

She could only pray he was right.

With in the next couple of hours, Soomi and the two boys had spent their time in the living room of her dorm. Although it wasn't as big as the iKON one (since she was the only one living there versus seven grown boys,) it was still spacious and homey, so much that they found themselves liking it there. It was nice not having the noisy guys around, even though Junhoe was one of them, and they could just chill on the sofa, watching Soomi's new favorite TV show Gossip Girl.

"Soomi, aren't you part American?" Yunhyeong is casually starting a conversation as another episode ends. 

It was a question she hadn't heard in a long time, causing her to look up from her hand that was in the bowl of popcorn they were all sharing. Pursing her lips for a second as if in thought, she shifts in her seat, answering reluctantly, "uh, yeah."

She figured most people around already knew that. Most of her life she'd grown up there not knowing much of culture in Korea. She knew the language from speaking it in the house, but even then, they rarely spoke of formalities. 

This meant her family was still in the states, and the thought makes her nearly physically wince. She tried hard not to think of them, but it was hard not to when being asked such a question.

"Wah, so you understand what they're saying without the subtitles? I swear english is too hard," Junhoe is gasping as he looks completely flabbergasted from this information.

Nodding, she shrugs her shoulders as if it wasn't a big deal, "it's not as hard as you guys think, it's easier to learn than Korean."

"Is your family still there?"

She only nods, and a sympathetic look takes over Yun's expression. Wrapping an arm around her to give her a side hug, he soothes her obvious tainted emotions, "I'm sure they miss you, I hope you can see them soon."

"Me too," she hums, offering him a grin to show she was alright, "but also I don't want them to know about all this guy drama - I think my parents would murder me."

As if they would even care about her at this point, but Soomi keeps that smile on her face as if she had no problems with her family.

Yunhyeong starts giggling, and she eventually joins in, the two of them enjoying themselves in their own world. They'd been cracking jokes and having a good time, so much that they didn't notice Junhoe rising from his seat and wandering off to a shelf that was located near the corner of the living room. 

It was dark outside, and there were only a few lights in the room that illuminated it. None of them, however, were located in the corner where this shelf was. He has to use his phone to see what was on it, figuring it couldn't be anything that big of a deal to Soomi.

That is, until he sees a bright diamond ring on it, tucked into a velvet box next to a bundle of polaroids. At first, he's lost, thinking it was some sort of ring given to her by a family member. But when he notices the photos of her and Jungkook scattered around it, his mouth makes an 'o.'

Back when the scandal was released and then revealed to be true thanks to Dispatch, nobody ever really found out that they had been engaged. As far as anyone ever really knew, they just had a long relationship behind everyone's backs. That night when the reporter saw her ring, she had played it off like it was something else other than what it was.

And later on, Jungkook had denied it as well. Soomi had assumed he got rid of his own ring, and never spoke of it again to anyone.

She'd thought earlier, when telling the boys the truth about what went on to cause the scandal, that it would probably be a dire detail to tell them she was going to marry him. But a part of her was still scared to let that get out. Nobody knew, no one other than her and Jeon Jungkook himself.

So she hadn't told them. She just explained what everyone knew, how they had been together for so long and then filled them in on the shocking info she had just found out. The boys had been dumbfounded enough just hearing that part of the story, she could've only imagined what they would do if they knew they were engaged.

This was why a look of horror spreads over her face when she sees Junhoe holding the ring up in the air. Truthfully, there was no telling how much such a ring cost, it was why Yunhyeong had gawked when he saw the boy holding it as well.

"Put that down!" she snaps, as if a demon had jumped out of her. 

Surprised, he looks at her in confusion, "what is this?" 

Although Junhoe had already put most of the pieces together, he just didn't know if it was true or not. Yunhyeong seemed to get it right away as Soomi had rushed across the room to snatch the ring out of Junhoe's fingers, "is that an engagement ring?"

Feeling the anxiety creep up her spine, she frowns as she faces the both of them, the ring still clenched in her hand. Knowing she had no choice but to tell them now, she sighs, "yeah, uh, we got engaged the night before the scandal."

As soon as these words leave her lips, her eyes go to her feet, not wanting to look at them. Meanwhile, the two boys look as if they just got hit by an airplane as they could only stand there, their mouths wide open. It seemed to make even more sense to them why she had been so torn when it all went down, and even earlier.

They were engaged.

"Oh, I had no idea," Junhoe mumbles nervously, noticing her dull face, "I'm sorry," he bows apologetically.

After a moment, she turns on her heel to set the ring back down on the shelf. "It's okay, I honestly don't know why I still have it," she shares, walking back to the couch with him, "if I was smart I would've gotten rid of it by now."

"There's nothing wrong with still having it, Soomi," Yun shakes his head, putting a hand on her shoulder so she'll glance to him, "it reminds you of when you were happy, it's like a souvenir," he explains, before pointing a finger in acknowledgement, "plus, it sure looks like it's worth a hell of a lot of money."





14:00 | wrong confrontations

The 10th annual Melon Music Awards, also the first award show of the many that go on during the end of year. It was astonishing to believe it was already December, as it seemed like almost yesterday it'd just been a fall day in October. But the leaves had settled fully off the trees, and the weather was only getting colder in Seoul. 

Every attendee, and especially artists, were more hectic than ever during the month of December. After all, it was when all the award shows took place, it was when they had their chance to truly shine and be rewarded for their efforts. With three award shows happening in one month, with more in January, the artists who were performing for each show were especially nerve wrecked.

One of these happened to be Soomi. She had always worked hard for her career. The last couple of years since she hit it big, she'd been performing at every award show yearly. At this point, it was almost like a tradition. 

She understood there was a lot demanded of her with her being the top solo artist in the country. But with all the pressure, just like when she practically lost her mind with laziness from being so overworked, she felt dead inside.

"I think I'm worn for the day, gotta still have energy for the show, you know," she's saying to her back up dancers and the staffs with a fake smile. Thankfully, they nod in understanding. A breath of relief leaves her lips as she offers a goodbye, bowing politely before grabbing her bag.

It was early in the morning, and she had been scheduled to come practice the songs she would be performing tonight at the show. This was done every year by every artist who was scheduled to perform, so it was expected that the boys of iKON would also be at the company.

For once since the fight, though, Soomi wasn't thinking about Jinhwan. With everything that went on at the rehearsals, she didn't have the energy to think about any boys, not since she hadn't even ate breakfast and it was way too early in the morning for her to start stressing.

She had to be in tip top shape for the show tonight. If anything was wrong, her face might show it, and it would cause her fans to notice. She swore if she even didn't crack a smile at the right second, her fans practically jumped on her back. It was why she had to be careful and erase the thoughts from her mind.

This was the solemn reason she didn't go straight to the iKON practice room from hers. Plus, she still felt like Jinhwan needed time, and so did she. 

However, that didn't mean she didn't miss him. She'd forgotten what being lonely even felt like.

Feeling exhausted, she inhales the air around her, a familiar cologne peeking it's way into her nostrils. Her sneakers click against the tiled floor of YG, and it was then she reminded herself of the fact she was wearing one of his many hoodies he either gave or she stole. It wasn't her fault Jinhwan had such comfortable clothing, and a great sense of style. At this point, she felt like half of her closet was his clothes.

Frowning, she looks down at the sleeves of the hoodie. I miss him, she shakes the thought away immediately, I'll tell him when the time's right.

"Shit!"

The accidental curse leaves her lips when she feels herself collide into someone's chest. With wide eyes, she hadn't looked up to see who she bumped into when that same cologne enters her nose, but with a much more prominent scent. Her breath hitched in her throat, and she realized in that moment she didn't have the strength to look up at him.

Still looking down at it, she stepped into the elevator when it dinged. Since it was so late, she didn't expect anyone to be in it, until she feels a warm body collide against hers.

Cursing under her breath, she didn't look up at them as she mumbled a sorry, stepping out of the way so there was enough space.

Time, the word she had been using so much lately shoots back into her head, he needs time.

But something inside of her was longing desperately to just hear his voice, to just look into his eyes. So much that she couldn't help herself as her head jerks up, only to see him not looking at her, but instead something behind her.

"Jinhwan..." she voices out, not caring about the fact that there might be someone watching them. YG artists that are of opposite sexes aren't supposed to associate with each other. But Soomi didn't care, not right then, not right now. 

At the sound of his name leaving her lips so softly, his eyes trail back to her - but they're cold.

She'd never seen him look so emotionless. It reminded her of the night Jungkook had called off the engagement. The way his eyes seemed to grow darker, and how he looked like such an empty soul, like he had never felt an emotion in his life.

Jinhwan was never that kind of person. He was thoughtful, caring, passionate. 

Are you okay?" the sudden voice breaks her out of her racked emotions.

Surprised by the question, she slowly looked up at him, feeling like she had heard his voice from somewhere. At the sight of his face that was looking down at her, she nearly fell over in the elevator. If she hadn't been stunned then, she now felt like she was witnessing the coming of God from the sky.

"I-," she clears her throat when her voice cracks, "I-I'm fine."

He purses his lips in a thin line, clearly not believing her words. His black fringe falls in his eyes from the way it's parted in the middle, and with the way he's looking at her, she has a feeling he was definitely an idol.

It breaks her heart to see him look at her that way.

In hope, she keeps looking at him, too scared to utter a word. However, he had no intention of saying one as he pushed past her, his shoulder rubbing against hers.

Hearing the sound of his converse against the floor as he left, she couldn't take the way it seemed to take her heart right out of her chest. "Jinani!" she cries out the nickname she'd given to him when they first met in desperation, turning around to where he had walked down the hall. Breaking into a run, she manages to catch the sleeve of the hoodie he was wearing today, that same grey one when he took her to the company after sleeping over.

"I'm Jinhwan," is what he says instead, offering a smile, "you're really beautiful, whoever made you cry doesn't deserve you."

An annoyed exhale leaves his lips when he stops as she does this. Feeling nerves shoot up her spine as he turns around to glare at her, he finally speaks in frustration, "what do you want, Soomi?"

She'd never felt so speechless under someone's gaze before. Despite the millions of people she had been in front of the thousands of acceptance speeches she'd given, nothing compared to the nauseous feeling that came up her stomach as she met his gaze.

It was then that she saw it. She saw the hurt he was hiding so deeply in his eyes. I did that to him.

Realizing she had been staring at him like he was an alien, she hurriedly speaks, although her voice was that of a mouse, "Soomi, nice to meet you - and uh, thank you."

"I'm sorry," she breathes out, feeling like someone had stolen her brain since she suddenly couldn't seem to process any words, "I-I just want you to talk to me, please, don't shut me out." He doesn't say anything, so she hesitantly goes on to admit, "I-I thought it would be best to give us both time, that's what I keep telling myself, but it's fate that brought us to run into each other, I know it. And - I can't stop thinking about you," she coughs a bit, feeling embarrassed about everything that was pouring out of her, "so please just talk to me, I miss you, Nani."

She expected him to come to his senses after her confession. She expected him to admit he was too harsh and that she at least deserved to have a right to explain to him what was going on. Soomi expected that soft sparkle to appear in his irresistible eyes, for that cute angel-like smile to spread over his kissable lips, and for his eyes to crinkle from the happiness he felt when he tells her I miss you too.

It envisioned in her mind so perfectly, she could see it happening in her head like it was playing out already before her on a television show.

Speaking as if they were already friends, he pouts, "Cheer up, Soomi-ah, following every day is a better tomorrow."

But it didn't happen.

In fact, Jinhwan didn't say a single word to her. In one breathe, he only ripped his hand out of her grip before spinning on his heel and walking the other way. He didn't even spare her another glance.

"Oh, Jinhwan-hyung, we were looking for you!" some of the boys appear around the corner, their eyes brightening once they find the boy they had been searching for.

Then their happy eyes travel to the defeated girl standing only feet behind him. Only then did they notice how distressed Jinhwan looked.

"Soomi!" Junhoe calls out her name in excitement, waving his hands in a jazzy way, "Did you and uh, did you and Jinhwan talk?"

The other boys give him an angered look at the fact he was the only one who didn't get the obvious gist. Yunhyeong slaps his arm, and he gasps, offended, rubbing it. "Idiot!" the boy mouths to him, and he only shrugs dramatically as if to say how was I supposed to know?
