If he hadn't then, he knew by the shattered look on Soomi's face. The boys all frown, looking back to Jinhwan, but he didn't show a single sign of caring as he pushed passed his member, heading further down the hall and straight for the exit.

Knowing there was no way any of them were gonna get to him, the boys run up to her, having a feeling of how she was probably not doing to well after such a harsh confrontation. 

"It's okay, Soo, he'll come around," Donghyuk gives her a quick hug. The others mumbled noises and hums of agreement, hugging her after he had.

She forces a small smile, shaking her head dismissively, "I just never thought he'd treat me like that, he didn't even say anything."

"He's sensitive, that's for sure," Bobby hums.

"Just think about how big of a dick he's gonna feel like when he finds out everything."

Everyone slaps the maknae's arm in unison, as if it had been planned. He whines in fake pain as he flinches away from them, and Soomi couldn't help but giggle at their antics. 

She didn't know what she would do without all of them. Even though she hadn't done a very good thing to their hyung, they still were understanding to her. They still stood by her and treated her like their own family, and with the way they are now, they were her family.















15:00 | pinkpunk

The sight of a droplet falling onto the paper that lays beneath her seems to snap her out of a daze. Confused, she watches the way the water splattered the ink on the paper, reaching up a finger to feel under her eyes, putting her pen down in the process.

She was crying, once again, all without even knowing. To think she was putting so much emotion into one thing, one song, that it riled up what was inside of her without her even having any idea. It wasn't the first time this had happened recently since it happened. 

The award show was starting in only around four hours. In thirty or so minutes, she had to be at the company. There, they would go over everything to make sure the performance would be perfect. After practicing the songs one last time, they'd head over to the arena and prepare for the show. All the artists that didn't have early performances or any announcing to do would head to their assigned seats just before the large stage where it all took place. The fans were further behind them and on the balcony, there'd be over thousands of people there.

She knew she should've left already, and only then did she notice the time. Soomi had been buried in song writing recently. With all she had bundled up inside, she had never known a better time to write. It reminded her of the feeling after the scandal, she wrote what felt like thousands of songs. Some of which she had never released until this album. Something inside of her was telling her it was time to let those songs out into the world, now that she had learned the truth of Jungkook. 

Still, she hadn't spoken to her ex fiance. She wasn't sure if he even had her number anymore, or if she even had his. Either way, neither of them really knew what to say to each other. Obviously, Jungkook had had built up guilt for everything that happened. He loved her to the moon and back, and he hated what he had to do. All he ever wanted was to protect her, even if it meant losing her.

But after his confession, it was clear he missed her. However, it was also clear she had moved on.

He hated that.

Soomi, on the other hand, could never feel more stressed. A part of her would always love Jungkook, truly. But only now was she realizing the true hero in her story had been Jinhwan all along. God only knew what kind of hole she would of thrown herself in without him.

He saved her.

Scribbling the last few words down, she shuts her journal. Grabbing her blue bag she always carried, Soomi shoves it inside before grabbing her keys. 

Rising from the sofa, she finds her green eyes trailing around the living room. The memory appears so vividly in her mind, causing her to clench the keys in her hands inside of her fists. 

"what are you doing?" the girl, who was perfectly dressed from head to toe in attire that didn't look like she just rolled out of bed, blinks at jinhwan in confusion. when she walked out of the restroom finally ready for work, she was met with the sight of kim jinhwan, standing at the front door and twirling her car keys around her fingers.

he looks up from his phone that he'd been holding with his other hand, speaking casually, "i'm gonna drive you to the company."

she finds a melancholy feeling creeping inside of her veins as she slings her bag over her shoulders. he insisted on carrying it for her that day, and she had always been so confused by his sudden actions. In reality, he was just being a sweetheart, which was exactly what he was.

Jinhwan made her heart flutter in her chest. Even now as she thought about it, she had to hold a hand to her aching heart as she left her dorm, locking the door in the process.



+




"These are the songs you are set to perform tonight, yes?" her manager asks as he points down at a list, his fingers hovering over two of her new songs. Having had been out of breath due to the practice she just went through, she holds a hand to her forehead, looking to the list. 

Not having the energy to say any words, she nods with a forced smile. 

"I still feel like it would have been good to rehearse this one at least once," he's mumbling, shaking his head in obvious misunderstanding. She glances back down at the list, knowing exactly which one he was talking about.

She waves a hand in the air dismissively, "I don't need to, it's a very special track."

Although he still didn't seem to get it, he nodded. "If you say so, I trust in you, Soomi-ah."

Thankful for her manager's sweet words and understanding, Soomi bows in appreciation. "Thank you, Manager-nim."

"Of course," he holds his fist directly in front of his chest, doing a fighting sign before patting her shoulder, "so are you ready to win all the awards?" he hums enthusiastically, half joking and half pretty honestly.

Chuckling, she shrugs, pushing her hair out of her face. It was sticking to her because of the sweat, and finally, during their conversation the staffs had come back into the room to dab it away with cloths. She offered a smile when they also gave her water, immediately twisting the cap off like a mental patient and chugging it.

Her manager laughs as she does so since she looked absolutely ridiculous. Once the bottle was empty, she hands it back to the staff, who bow before excusing themselves once again. Looking back to her manager, she finally clears her throat, "I suppose so, but I don't think I'll win as much this year."

A gasp leaves his lips as if he was massively offended, "nonsense, your new song is going to be a hit, I can already tell!"

Shaking her head, the smile doesn't leave her lips for a second as she hesitantly agrees, "I just hope you're right."

"I am, don't worry so much," he reassures her. The conversation dies down after this, and only then did she notice most of the staffs were leaving the room. Her backup dancers seemed to already headed out, and some of the workers were standing at the entrance of the dance practice studio they both stood in.

One of them waved their hands to grab their attention, "it's time to head to the arena, Ms. Soomi-ah!"

She bows hurriedly, calling a thank you and informing them she would be there shortly. Her manager gives her a thumbs up, still having some things to do before he went there himself, signaling for her to just go ahead and leave.

"See you there, manager-nim!" she yells as she runs out of the room. 

The halls of YG Entertainment were crowded. Lots of artists were walking, along with all their staffs and workers that were carrying things like makeup bags and outfit changes. It was hectic as everyone was chattering loudly about the events that were bound to happen, such as who was performing what and who was winning what award.

Her green eyes trail along the artists and staff, searching for the familiar boys she wished to speak to. At this point, they barely cared for the company rule that forbid female artists to interact with male. As far as Soomi knew anyway, it was groups with groups, and she wasn't in a girl group - she was a solo artist.

So she didn't care. They were her friends and that was that.

While her eyes search, it isn't long until they land on a certain group of boys. It was easy to spot them because of the way they dressed, and plus she saw Junhoe and Chan thanks to their outstanding height. A giggle leaves her lips when she looks at the height difference, her eyes landing on Jinhwan.

However, just seeing the way he was dressed, the way he was that gorgeous, was enough to cause her to frown.

Just when she's about to start pushing past people like a crazy woman, screaming their names, someone puts a hand on her shoulder.

This scared the shit out of her as she had been preparing herself. Jumping, she holds a hand to her heart as she tries to think of who would be bothering her. It wasn't like many people liked her really, or they were too scared to talk to her in the first place.

But everything seemed to make sense when she sees the four girls standing there beside her, walking at the same pace. Bright smiles are planted over their faces when they see she noticed them, and they all squeal in excitement, "Soomi-ah~!"

"Oh my god!" Soomi screeches so loud a lot of people in the hall turned around to look at her in confusion, including Jinhwan himself, "I haven't seen you guys in so long!" 

The five of them gather in a large hug, nearly crying tears of joy because it really has been that long. It was an emotional time for them as they pull away, still walking together out the doors of the company. 

"Yeah, you're so busy being successful," Rose jokes as she slaps her shoulder in a playful manner.

Soomi wrinkles her nose, shaking her head vigorously in disagreement, "oh please, and you guys aren't?!"

They shrug, knowing they couldn't dismiss it. In reality, Blackpink had exploded practically everywhere. They were now the top girl group in the world. 

Lisa wraps an arm around her shoulder as they walk, "maybe, but just think how unstoppable we'd be if you debuted with us."

Soomi chuckles, "I think it'd be too much for people to handle."

Back in 2015, when the original plan for Blackpink's first name - PinkPunk - was created, it involved Soomi in the mix. Unlike the other trainees who trained with the four girls who left early on, Soomi trained with them throughout their days. The five of them had been an inseparable team, but the CEO had taken her out of the project, saying that she would be better off as a solo artist. She never really got why, and the girls had been bummed out since they all were so close. But she never got to debut with them, and eventually she came out as a female artist alone, and the girls debuted as Blackpink.

Although she didn't mind how her career itself turned out, she couldn't help but wonder what it could be like if she was with them.

Jennie points a finger in agreement, "now that is true," she wraps an arm around Soomi too, and the other girls join in. Together, they walk with their arms wrapped around each other. Lisa kept making jokes about how Soomi would probably be the main rapper, and Soomi kept denying it, fake rapping and purposely sounding horrible.

It had everyone of them in fits of laughter as they board the vehicles. For once, Soomi found herself smiling not because of a boy, but because she had friends. She had real friends who she had missed through out everything in her life. 

She felt like she was part of Blackpink then. Like she had debuted with the girls. It was a pleasant feeling - one she wished she had always. 






16:00 | serene

Soomi had never felt so awkward in her life. Well, truthfully she had never felt awkward period - until now. She wasn't someone who got nervous often, especially not in public. 

This was why she was having a hard time dealing with the fact Jungkook was sitting directly behind her along with the other members of BTS. And as if that wasn't bad enough, iKON was directly in front of her. She felt like her heart was going to combust from the stress she was feeling any moment now.

The beginning of the award show had been hard to get through particularly because of this. She could feel Jungkook's eyes on her even though she wasn't facing him. It was like he was trying to tell her something, but she knew she couldn't turn around. Not with all the screaming fans video taping their every move.

Even the boys of iKON obviously wanted to say hello with the way they would shift in their seats and act like they were looking at the crowd just to glance at her. She didn't make any eye contact, knowing it would be too simple for the fans to figure out something was up. 

Feeling a nauseous pit forming in the bottom of her stomach, she distracted herself as artists performed and the first few awards for rookies were announced by waving at her fans and sending them hearts. She saw a lot of them holding up signs that said things like we've missed you Soomi! or you deserve the world Kim Soomi, and knowing that she had these amazing people that always supported her through it all warmed her heart. With all the did, she wanted to make them happy in anyway she could, always.

I'm never slacking off again, she harshly tells herself as she waves at more fans, doing heart hands. 

Time passed and passed, and it was finally starting to get to decent awards. She and everyone else watched intently as the name of the award was announced via the robot sounding woman through the speakers. 

"Here are the nominees for: Best Dance, Female," it sounded, and a queue of screams echoed loudly through out the area. She swore she was going to be deaf afterwards, but she loved it.

Soon after, the large screens began to show snippets of the artists and their songs that were up for the award. She watched as artists like Momoland, Blackpink, Sunmi, appeared on the screen. As each one came, claps and yells were heard from their specific fans.

And then there was hers. 

"Soomi, Lip and Hip," the robot like woman says from the speakers. It was like a tornado had come through the area, and a hand goes to cover her mouth in surprise from the screeches and screams she hears. They were so much louder than ever before, and she couldn't believe it as she grins from ear to ear.

As they died down, everyone leaned forward, eager to hear the answer. A chorus of fans across the arena were chanting her name, and she felt that familiar adrenaline pump into her veins as the announcer clears their throat.

"The winner of the Best Dance for Female Artists is..." their voice trails off, they open the card with the answer increasingly slow, creating an etching feeling of anxiety inside of her.

A knowing look takes over their face, and they let out a deep breathe before leaning into the mic again, "Soomi, Lip and Hip!"

Screams, and more screams.

It takes her a few seconds to process it, even though she had won this award before. Even though she should be used to winning, she still got the same shocked feeling as she looked up to all the crying people holding up light sticks and signs, screaming her name. 

The truth was, it was a feeling you couldn't get used to. Because it got better every time.

Rising to her feet, she makes her way up to the stage, covering her hand with her mouth the entire time. Bowing many times to the seniors on stage, she takes the award before standing behind the mic.

There's a chorus of giggles from her fans when she has to lower it due to her tiny height. Clearing her throat, she still felt anxious when her eyes land on Jungkook, who was brightly smiling and looking at her with glistening eyes. But then they fall on Jinhwan, who still seemed like a robot himself. He's not happy for me, even now?

However, then she looks to her fans. The thousands of people that love her and are always there for her, and that seems to make the old Soomi jump straight out of her.

"Neverlands~!" she yells into the mic, her fandom name, and a chorus of yells echoes through the room. A grin appears on her lips as she clears her throat again from the screaming, "when I was a trainee, dancing was actually my weakest suit," she explains into the microphone, and in the corner of her eyes she sees the girls of Blackpink dramatically nodding and making a cutting motion over their throats as if to say yeah, she sucked. Rolling her eyes, a lot of people laugh when it shows them doing this on the large screen.

"Anyway, throughout my hard practice, I have always tried the most on my performance. It means the world to me to show you all my passion for being here today, so knowing that you all send me this appreciation is the reason I will only work harder," she bows a few times towards the crowds, "thank you so much everyone!"

With that, the crowd erupted into more cheers and claps. "We love you Soomi!" they call as she walks back stage to give her staff the award before heading back to her seat. With a light heart, she waves to them as she leaves.




+




"Thank you so much everyone! Shortly tonight I will be showcasing my new songs, I've worked so hard on them in thought that everyone is anticipating my comeback, I hope you enjoy them as much as I do!" she calls for the third time that night, saying yet another thank you speech before heading backstage again. Soomi was grateful for winning another award, but she was already feeling all the energy draining out of her, and she was performing next.

Thankfully, with this, however, meant that the show was coming closer and closer to an end.

"Soomi, Soomi, this way!" one of her staff workers ushers her down the hall in a rush, while another one takes her award to put it with the rest. She didn't even have time to process where she was going or how to even walk as she was pushed down the hall by many workers, shoved into her dressing room.

On queue, she's guided to a seat in a hurry. In front of a mirror, her green eyes peer back at her while she fixes her makeup quickly. Soomi tried her best to keep a straight face while they worked so nothing was messed up.

Afterwards, it was time for wardrobe. During award shows, you were on a tight clock. Everything was timed, and if you weren't there, everyone went haywire. It was why she was hurriedly forced into her stage outfit, nearly falling over as they harshly put the heels on her feet. I feel like a doll, she's thinking as they then gather around to set up her mic.

However, after that traumatizing part is over, you get to the fun part. It was the feeling of adrenaline, of excitement. It's the sound of hearing those thousands of people screaming your name, yelling about how much they've missed you, how much they adore you. Soomi loved the sound of knowing that her dream had come true, that all of her hard work was paying off right then and there.

This was how she knew she was ready to show what she had been working on.

Standing behind the curtain with her dancers around her, she lets out a deep breathe. All or nothing, she repeats the same words in her mind as she hears the announcers talking about who was next, but it seemed like they already knew as the crowd kept yelling, "Kim Soomi!"

"Now, now, it's the anticipated performance we've all been waiting for, the top female artist of three years, please welcome - Soomi!"

   https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Gkj-ZgFsOiU&feature=emb_title
(ok why did I add so many videos? Dear God)


The song ended, the lights abruptly going out. Soomi felt her body crying for air as her breath was ragged, and she hurriedly calmed herself down as she knew the next song was coming up. Thankfully, this one didn't have any heavy choreography, she just needed to stabilize her voice.

I've got this, she tells herself as she hears the footsteps of her dancers leave. The crowd had previously erupted into claps and screams. "THAT WAS SO GOOD!" they'd yell, along with things like, "I'M SO GLAD SHE'S BACK!" From the sounds of it, everyone seemed to be taking the new song well.

Maybe it really would make it big.

Standing up onto the podium that was set out like she practiced at the rehearsal, she lets out a deep breathe as her breathing returned to normal thanks to her quick stamina. This song, was a song she wrote completely by herself. The producers had liked it so much they said they didn't think the lyrics needed anymore work. "It's clear you've put your heart into this."

And she had.

This song, she didn't understand what it was about at first when she started writing it. But now that she had come to par with her feelings, come to understand what was truly inside of her. She understood exactly what it was about.

Which is why when the lights come on, showing her, and everyone in the arena started yelling for her once again. Everything seemed to tune out of her mind as her eyes travel across all the artists sitting, eyeing her. The yells, screams, none of it existed anymore as the music began to play from the speakers, and she brought the mic up to her face, her eyes only focused on one person.

In the entire arena, his eyes still seemed to be the brightest thing. He still seemed to captivate her, a hundred times over again.

Right there, on the stage of the Melon Music Awards, she began to pour out her heart. The thing that nobody else knew was it was all meant for him, the boy she loved.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vK-JEUDj6HY&feature=emb_title
(ok but fr stan hyuna)

   

When can I see you?
Why are you so busy?
I hope today passes by quick
When will we be able to have our secret time
Among all these people?

Her heart ached as she remembers it all. That fluttery feeling you give me, she speaks mentally as she stares at him, not caring if anyone figured out what she was so fixated on. But he wouldn't look at her, even when she was standing right there on the stage before him.

When I'm with you, I'm at peace
When I'm hugging you, I feel dreamy
Right now, it's just you and me
Make us our own little secret
It's the same feeling every day
Let me open your eyes
For you tomorrow
So I can get to know you better
I wanna go to a peaceful world with you
I wanna sleep next to you
Make a secret with me

She nearly messed up on a note when his eyes abruptly whip upwards, his gaze locked onto hers. When it does, she feels her heartbeat speed up, and it felt like there was no one else other than him in the arena. It was just the two of them.

If you wanna do something, tell me
Do it with me
If I'm with you, whatever it is, I'll be happy

She wondered then, if he saw the tears that welled up in her eyes.

The moon and the stars shine on us,
We lock eyes, we hold hands, into your dream

She was crying when the song ends. Everything seemed to snap her back into reality as she wipes under her eyes, the music cutting off, only to hear the arena clapping and screeching for her.

But her eyes still find a way back to him. He was the only one in the entire building not clapping, and when the boys notice, Yunhyeong hits his arm. Jinhwan shakes his head surprised, as if he himself had also been lost in his own world, before his two hands form together in a clapping manner.

Still, nothing felt quite right to her. With tears in her eyes, she bows to the crowd, holding back up her mic, and shakily calls, "mianhae," before she leaves the stage.

It was a sorry only meant for one person.



17:00 | home

The award show was almost over. Thankfully, after what felt like so long of just sitting there and watching artists and awards be given away, Soomi would be able to go home soon. The thought of laying on her bed was the only thing keeping her alive right then. She'd won five awards in total that night, and she was beyond joyous to her beloved fans. Her new songs were received well, and overall, it was a successful night for the Melon Music Awards.

But even then, a part of her was missing. She didn't know what that eye contact had meant between her and Jinhwan, or if it really meant anything. He seemed so determined to not show her any sign of emotion, and she had finally given up when he didn't react much to the song that was obviously written about him. Did he just suddenly not care?

She couldn't help but feel like he was overreacting, just in the slightest. The two of them weren't even together officially, but yet he was acting like she had done some sort of drastic sin when all it was was a heat of the moment kiss.

Did he really not care about her now because of that? Could she really be blamed for her actions? The two of them had never truly been open about their real feelings for each other, but was that her fault?

As the night went on and she kept thinking about his lack of reaction, these are the sorts of questions that pounded their way into her skull. She was so distraught about it all, that she didn't even pay attention to the boys of iKON the rest of the night. So much that she didn't notice the certain boy on her mind that was finally trying to get her attention.

Sighing, she tries to look as content as possible as she watches the announcer walk up on stage. It was the final award that would be given out, otherwise known as the grand prize. Every year, boy groups were usually given it, since they were where most of the popularity was. This is why she showed basically no interest in announcer, instead distracting herself by entertaining fans that were waving and filming her by making funny faces.

"The winner of the final daesang, and the artist of the year, I present to you all," the announcer clears their throat, slowly opening the envelope. With wide eyes, they gasp, "the winner of the artist of the year is - BTS!" 

With BTS being at the top of the list of the most popular artists in the last couple of years, it was no surprise to pretty much everyone in the room. Still, the crowd erupted into cheers as the seven boys sitting behind her rise to their feet. Soomi couldn't help but let her eyes linger on a certain boy as he passes her. However, for the first time that night, Jungkook hadn't been looking at her.

The cheering still doesn't stop as they're all standing on stage. Bright grins on their faces, the leader of the group, RM, is the one who gives the speech first. 

Of course, like every other artists acceptance speech, it was the casual 'this means a lot, thank you everyone' speech that she'd heard a million times that night. It wasn't a big deal to her, she didn't expect everyone to have some sort of mind blowing words to say when they won - she was just bored of hearing the same summary of words every ten seconds.

It was why she found herself tuning out the words of the Bangtan boys, her eyes wandering every which way in the building. Soomi just wanted to go to sleep, and it felt like the night was never going to end.

Finally, the other boys were giving short thank you's. Just when she thought they were about to finish so that the ending ceremony could begin, Jungkook looks to his hyung's as if asking permission. Everyone nods at him, and he hesitantly stepped up to the mic, straightening it with his hand. 

She assumed he was going to say the same words. 'Thanks everyone, this means a lot,' and then it would be done. Then, the music would go off, and play a BTS song since they were the grand prize winners. All the artists that were there tonight would head to the stage, where they would bow and say goodbye to the fans before leaving backstage. 

Finally, she would be able to go home. She could never be more thankful.

However, due to her lack of attention on the boy, she nearly fell out of her seat due to shock upon hearing his next words, "I'd like to say thanks to all the ARMY's around the world, and to my dear friend's that have helped me through the process, especially Soomi - thank you everyone!"

Soomi's gawking at the stage, and Jungkook catches her dumbfounded look, shooting nothing more than a wink her way. She couldn't process it, what was he trying to do?!

She expected fans to shout angrily when he thanked her. It was why she was stuck in a daze for so long as the music went off. 

But there weren't any angry shouts, there were excited screams. 

"YES, JUNGMI FOR THE WIN!"

"ARE THEY BACK TOGETHER?!"

"THAT'S SO CUTE!"

Covering her face with her hands, she didn't know which she would prefer. For everyone to hate her because of what he said, or for everyone to start screaming dating rumors. They were both nightmares that she didn't need to add to her stress level.

Her hands stay over her face as she walks behind iKON up to the stage. How was she even gonna look at Jinhwan now? She couldn't believe Jungkook would do that. He must really think he's funny.

Heaving out a sigh, she forces a smile on her face as she stands beside everyone on stage for the goodbye. This is fine, she tells herself, trying to ignore the fact iKON was standing directly beside her. It wasn't a huge issue, until she looks to her right where they were - only to see Jinhwan was right there.

Soomi's breathe hitches in her throat. What were the chances of all the artists here? she thought in exasperation.

She wondered if she should talk to him.

"Yah, Jinhwan," she whispered yells to him, not making eye contact or even moving a muscle to indicate he is who she's speaking to.

No reply.

"Kim Jinhwan!" she tries again, growing frustrated because it wasn't like she was speaking quietly at all. There was no way he wasn't hearing her, even over the yells in the arena and the music.

He was still ignoring her, wasn't he? She frowns at the thought, even after that song. 

And Jungkook probably made it worse just then. She swore she was going to hurt him.

Sadly, she stares at her feet, speaking aloud to herself and imagining she was talking to him and that he was actually listening. "I miss you, Jinhwan-ah," her voice nearly cracks at the end as she thinks about the drastic effect he really did have on her life. Everything seemed so out of place when he wasn't with her. It was like a part of her had been stolen.

Now she may never get it back.

"Kim Soomi~!" 

Her eyes bulge when she feels someone grab her arm, and before she could process what was happening she was being dragged somewhere else on the stage. With wide eyes, she looks up to see who dared to do such a thing, and they only grow angrier once they see Jungkook standing before her.

It took a huge part of her not to scream at him right there in front of all those people. Just when she's about to lose it when he yells, "sing!" before holding the mic out to her, she hears the screams of the fans in the crowd.

"Soomi and Jungkook, so cute~!"

"I can't believe this is really happening, I'm all for it!"

"WHY AREN'T YOU MARRIED ALREADY?!"

Wincing at the things she's hearing, she heaves out a sigh. Shaking her head, she knew there wasn't anything she could do to get out of this. So with a heavy heart, she takes the mic, focusing on the song that was playing.

Your love, your love, your love, I miss that
Your love, your love, your love, I want that
Your touch, your touch, your touch, I need that

There was a certain grin on Jungkook's lips when he takes the mic back from her, raising it to his lips as the next verse blasts from the loud speakers. His doe eyes seem to fight a way into her heart as they delicately meet hers, tickling her emotions and twisting her thoughts towards him. Soomi finds herself growing lost and confused for a second, the screams of the people in the arena growing softer the longer she focuses on his eyes.

One day,
Will you open the door for me
When the doorbell rings three times
So that I could give the parting words I didn't finish
I'll tell you then

He was staring at her so softly. It was like, it was only a song they had already released long before the two of them had met again, long before he had confessed the truth to her. But it seemed as if everything he was singing was meant for her, like he was trying to tell her something.

It's comfortable even as you say nothing
It became my home because you were there

When the song ends, everyone is screaming and clapping. Soomi snaps back to reality, a hand going to her head as she slowly backs away from Jungkook. 

Everyone began to leave the stage with it being over, and she shakes her head as he calls out to her. Something didn't feel right inside of her, and she lets out a shaky breath, spinning on her heel and rushing backstage.

That's when it hit her.

A cough leaves her lips as she abruptly falls to the ground on her knees, a hand still on her head. She was unaware of where the boys of iKON were, and as far as she knew BTS were still on stage thanking everyone and saying goodbye.

Nothing felt right to her. Thankfully, she was backstage so no one outside could see her and know what happened, but that didn't make it okay.

"Soomi, oh my god!" she hears screams, but she couldn't make out who they were due to the spinning going on in her head. Her vision was beginning to get hazy, and she felt like everything was crashing down before her.

All she knew was someone was holding her. They smelled like scented rose candles, and it was enough for her to feel comfortable even for a second. 

"What happened?!" another voice cries.

"She's fainting, someone call an ambulance!" the female voice yells, whoever's holding her, "Hurry, please!"

Wanting to tell the girls she was okay, that they didn't have to worry about her. She just needed some rest for a mere second. Helplessly, despite her bodies efforts to fight it, Soomi tries to reach her hand up to get the girl's attention.

But her vision goes blank.













18:00 | euphoria

"Who keeps calling your phone every three seconds?" Hanbin had finally called out in annoyance at the sound of Junhoe's phone going off yet again. It seemed like whoever was trying to reach him really needed to, and the boys were annoyed he was just ignoring it as he was leaned back in the seat of the car, his eyes closed.

When Junhoe doesn't move a muscle, appearing to be in dreamland, Yunhyeong reaches out to hit his arm, "yah, answer your phone, June!" he scolds him, and the boy blinks a few times in confusion, not noticing it going off yet again.

"huh?" he mumbled weakly, rubbing her eyes as he reaches in his pocket to grab it. Everyone is intrigued, curious as to who wanted to talk to him so bad. A look of confusion slowly spreads over the boy's face, only egging on their desire to know more, "it's Soomi."

They all exchange looks, waiting for him to answer it, but for some reason he doesn't.

"Answer it, pabo!" Donghyuk snaps, and Junhoe just looks at him blankly.

"I don't know if Jinhwan is okay with that," he explains, and all eyes fixate on the boy sitting in the back corner of the van. Their eldest was leaning his head against the window, staring out at the night sky in his own little world.

At the sound of his name, he looks up, only mumbling, "go ahead, I don't care."

They felt bad about his obvious glum attitude. All the boys had saw whatever Jungkook was trying to do. They knew the whole story now, so they had a feeling he was just trying to get Soomi back with his charms, to make her fall for him again. It was a frustrating sight for them to see, and they could only imagine how Jinhwan was feeling.

It was why after the show they tried to explain tid bits to him about how Soomi didn't like him anymore so he was just trying to bother her. But he never seemed very interested in listening.

Junhoe nods, before sliding his finger across the screen and putting it to his ear. "Yah, Soomi, what is it?" he's humming casually into the phone.

But what he heard was most definitely not casual.

"Koo Junhoe - yah, from iKON right? It's Rose, I'm Soomi's friend, I thought you might need to know she passed out at the show. She's been taken to the hospital," he recognized the voice of Rose from Blackpink nearly instantly. He didn't know they were so close with Soomi, and it was confusing to him when he realized something else.

"What? Why would that happen? How did you even know to call me?!" his mouth fell agape in surprise, as he tried to piece together the puzzle. With his hectic, worried words, the other members kept asking him what was wrong, even Jinhwan was paying attention in concern.

There's a moment of silence, before she sighs, "You're the second person she has on speed dial after Jinhwan, I didn't know she was so close with you guys, especially because she sort of hates everyone - so I felt like you must be important to her," she reasons, a dull tone of voice evident as she talks, "the doctors say she's alright, that she just fainted due to stress."

"Oh my god," he's mumbling, although flattered by being one of the top contacts in her phone, it wasn't what he should focus on now, "I feel so bad, we'll be on our way as soon as possible. Is she doing okay now?"

"Yeah, yeah, she's okay, just a little shaken up still. Poor Soomi-ah, despite her careless attitude she hates bringing people trouble," she sounded so torn up by the events as she exhales deeply, "look, me and the girls are dear friends of her, we'll protect her at all costs. I don't know what's been going on with her, but I can tell from the way she looks when I mention your names that it has to do with you guys and her ex boyfriend," his eyes widen at how fast she was catching on, "I haven't gotten her to tell me yet, but whoever is causing her this needs to fix it fast, or they're going to have a much larger problem on their hands, yes?"

He couldn't help but feel even the slightest of intimidated.

"Uh, yeah, of course - thank you, Rose," he ends the call, and all the boys immediately start throwing questions at him.

"Did you say Rose - as in Rose of Blackpink?!"

"I thought Soomi called you."

"Since when did you even know Rose?"

In annoyance and stress, Junhoe ignores them, instead giving their driver directions to the hospital. This only makes everyone even more confused, and they only parade him more.

"She was calling me from Soomi's phone," he explains, getting them to finally close their mouths, "she passed out at the show, she's in the hospital."

"WHAT?!" 

"Oh my god, I can't believe this," Donghyuk covers his face in his hands, probably bound to break into tears.

An awestruck look was on Jinhwan's face, he couldn't seem to process it. Shifting in his seat to face them, he worriedly speaks for the first time since they left, "is she alright?"

Junhoe purses his lips for a second as his eyes meet his hyung's. It's a long while before he finds the words he wishes to say.

"She's fine, hyung, but you need to sort this out with her soon," he shakes his head, "I don't think you realize how big of a toll it's taking on her and her health."

Getting the gist, the other boys nod in agreement.

But the eldest was dumbstruck, not wanting to believe it as he stutters, "are you saying this - this is because of me?"

Rolling his eyes, he accidentally says rather harshly, "yeah, that's exactly what I'm saying!"

Any other day, Junhoe would've gotten beat for his rude words towards his elder. But on that day, in that moment, it was the last thing Jinhwan cared about.

He couldn't believe this was his fault, but it was. It was enough for him to bury his face in his hands as they drive, tears welling in his eyes for the first time. He'd told himself he wasn't going to cry, but he couldn't help it. This was because of him.

She needed him, and he wasn't there. He wouldn't even let her speak despite all the times she tried.

The boys had been trying to tell him all along ever since it happened, they had been trying to tell him that it was a misunderstanding. That she realized she loved him, but he never believed them.

And now look what happened because of his own selfishness.

Thinking back to the way she sang that song, her eyes never leaving his for a moment. The way tears welled in her green orbs, and she'd apologized on that stage in front of all those people at the end - just for him.

It was enough to have him silently sobbing in the backseat of the van. "I'm sorry, Soomi-ah," he whispers, "I'm sorry."




+




"Thanks for everything, you guys," Soomi's voice is weak but yet sincere as she hugs the four girls one last time. They all nearly squeeze her to death, and although she was feeling under the weather, she accepted it because it was out of love.

Smiling comfortingly, Rose pats her shoulder in a calming manner, "we're family, Soo, we'll always be here. We have to get back because we have rehearsals early but keep in touch, okay?" 

All of them are looking at her expectantly, and Soomi nods vigorously. She really should start hanging out with them more, after all of this, something like that was just what she needed.

"And no more stress, you hear me?" Jennie raises a brow, pointing a finger at her in a joking manner.

Nodding again, she chuckles lightheartedly, "Alright, I'll try my best. See you guys at the company!" 

The four of them wave as they leave the room. Soomi had just been discharged only minutes ago, which was why the girls were leaving since they had to go back to their own dorm and it was dangerous for them to be seen together yet again. Rumors were bound to be spread by anyone, so they had to be on the lookout at all times. 

Plus, not wanting to cause them any more trouble than she already had, Soomi insisted she could take a cab or call one of her staffs. 

Although thankful nobody really heard of the accident. When she passed out, it had been in one of the empty halls where the dressing rooms were located. The girls were pretty sneaky, and she was endlessly grateful they got her here quietly.

The last thing she needed was anyone breathing down her neck.

Gathering her cell phone that had been sitting on the end table besides the hospital bed, she rubbed her temples to calm herself. I really passed out because of stress, she thinks about what the doctor had sternly told her. She guessed he was right, maybe there were too many things piling up inside of her, and it was starting to eat her alive.

It wasn't like her mental health had been very great recently. All the stuff with work plus the Jungkook truth of the scandal coming out, and then her realizing she was in love with Kim Jinhwan? It could provide a lot of stress for one girl.

Shaking her head, she grabs her coat and slings it over her shoulders. Just as Soomi's frail body is about to turn around and face the door, she hears a sound of shuffling footsteps against the tile.

Unable to react in time, voices practically explode from the entrance of the hospital room. "Soomi-ah, oh my god!"

"You're okay, thank Jesus!"

With wide eyes, she turns towards the boys standing in her room, having had closed the door. All of them look like they'd just lived through a nightmare, with their hair all messed up and their breathes ragged. In confusion at how they even found out, she blinks a few times at them, "Y-Yeah, I'm fine but - how are you guys even here?"

"Rose called June from your phone," Hanbin explains, and she slowly nods as each word leaves his lips in understanding.

Yunhyeong steps forward to hug her, "she said the doctor's claim you passed out from stress, I was so worried about you!" 

Accepting his warm hug, she wraps her arms around him as she mumbles, "I know, but I'm okay, everything was just a lot."

Suddenly, the boys of iKON grow unusually silent. Soomi's brows furrow when Yun pulls away, turning to look into the crowd of his friends with an impatient look on their face. It was like they were all waiting for something as everyone turned to land their eyes upon it - or someone.

Not understanding what they were up to, finally the six boys seem to part like water, and she only got more lost until she saw someone walking towards her. Her breath hitched in her throat at the sight of him, she couldn't believe she didn't see him there. 

Jinhwan had been hiding at the back, afraid to face her. Since his members were so much taller than him, Soomi had been completely clueless - until now.

"Jinhwan-," she starts, but he holds up a hand to cut her off.

Only until he's standing directly in front of her did he speak, their faces only inches apart and his eyes looking down on her with passion, "Don't," he whispers, "you don't have to say anything, I already know, the guys told me."

Astonished, her eyes snap to the six boys. They all put their hands up in defense, wide grins on their faces as they watch everything turn out.

Pursing her lips, she felt at a loss of words with the way his eyes were looking at her. They sparkled under the ceiling lights of the hospital room, glistening with something she hadn't seen anyone look at her with in a long time - love.

And for the first time in a long time herself, she could return it.

"Jinhwan," she mutters, breaking their eye contact from the nerves creeping into her, "I'm still sorry."

A hand of his reaches up to caress her cheek, the pad of his thumb leaving tingles against her skin as he runs it across her jawline. Her eyes flutter as they look back to him, and his were only half open as if he was preparing himself for a certain something. Somehow, he still finds the words he wanted to say in that moment, "Soomi, I love you."

Her mouth nearly fell open in shock, as if she hadn't already heard Yunhyeong and the others tell her this a million times. They said to her that he loved her, but that didn't mean she always believed it. That didn't mean it wouldn't still play with her heart and not create fireworks above them when she actually heard such words fall from his plumped, kissable lips.

Because nothing, nothing would ever prepare her for hearing someone like Kim Jinhwan confess his love for her. It created an undeniable bliss in her heart and chunked her into a field of euphoria. Right then, as she nearly tears up from the joyous bundle that forms inside of her, she realized she had never felt so free with anyone in her life.

With Jinhwan, she was in her own utopia, one made just for the two of them. And as she leans in impeccably quickly, cupping his face with her hands and kissing him like there was no tomorrow, she knew she never wanted to leave. 

She found her home in him. 

"I love you, too, Jinani."










19:00 | chemistry

Refreshing - that was the word Soomi would use to describe the feeling of falling asleep next to the boy she loved. In the morning, as she pulled her sleeping mask from over her eyes, she thought it would feel the same when she saw him next to her - but he wasn't there.

Confused, she rubs her eyes as she slips out of bed. Her feet soft against the carpet of her bedroom, she peeked into her bathroom connected to her room only to see he wasn't there either.

"Kim Jinhwan!" she's yelling as she walks into the hall of her apartment, only for her nose to be met with the scent of - food?

Speeding up her pace to figure out what it was, she just stepped into the living room when he steps out of the kitchen connected to it. There's an apron tied over his sweatshirt, and he seemed already dressed in his ripped jeans as he beams down at her, spatula in hand, "Good morning, cutie."

Wincing at the nickname, she walks towards him to kiss his cheek before the curiosity pours out of her, "so, you can cook?" She says nonchalantly, fully entering the kitchen only for her eyes to fall on the two plates set at the table. Pancakes and bacon were set upon them, drinks beside them already and everything.

The sweet gesture answered her question, and she's unable to hide the wide grin that plasters onto her lips. "This is too cute," she gushes, turning back to him to rush into his arms, hugging him tightly.

Surprised by the reaction, Jinhwan giggles as he wraps his arm around her, "I thought you deserved it, since we've got a long day ahead of us."

She raises a brow in question as the two of them pull away, taking their seats in the kitchen together to eat breakfast, "what do you mean? We have schedules together?"

Chuckling at the fact she didn't remember, he swallows a bite of bacon before explaining, "you don't remember? We have a writing session scheduled together."

Her lips form an 'o' shape at the remembrance. The session with her and the boys was actually supposed to be earlier, but because of all the recent complications they had it rescheduled.

"Right!" she cries, not minding the thought of working with the boys anymore. Soomi realized that after everything, she'd gotten a lot closer with the guys of iKON and Jinhwan. She guessed in a way, everything that happened was almost positive.

However, her and Jinhwan had yet to talk about what really happened. She didn't know if they ever would, as it was a rather sore subject for the both of them. But it seemed like there was so much bottled up inside of her just wondering what he thought.

"Nani," she clears her throat, slowly setting her fork against the plate. At the sound of his name and the clink of silverware against the glass, his eyes trail up to meet hers in curiosity. Nervously, she starts hesitantly, "um, I feel like we should talk about Jungkook."

He raises a brow at the name, not seeming pleased to hear it, "your ex fiance?"

Seems like he already knew, causing her face to grow pale. I didn't know they told him everything - as in THAT everything.

At the sight of her nerves rising, he sets his own silverware down. Jinhwan's hand reaches across the table to grab hers, and he entangles his fingers with hers as he stares into her eyes, "they told me everything, many times. You said you loved me, right? And I believe you."

Pursing her lips, she vigorously nods, "of course, yeah, I just want to make sure you know there's nothing going on there," she explains, shaking her head in frustration, "I don't know what he was trying when he said that at the award show, god knows what's already being said on the media."

Rubbing his thumb on the back of her hand to calm her anxiety, his voice soothes through her anger, "It's okay, Soomi-ah, he's doing it on purpose to try and get in your head. It's clear he's not over you - and he's upset you moved on."

Her eyes wander to the table where their hands were still perfectly one with each other. In that moment, she found herself wondering.

Jungkook, the scandal - it wasn't truly what she thought it was. He was doing it to protect her. Someone threatened her life, they blackmailed him. She'd taken so long to get over him.

Had she done it because of what happened, or was it because of Jinhwan?

Now that she knew of the truth, was that love still inside of her?

"Yah," Jinhwan pulls her out of her clouds of thoughts, his eyes concerned, "you okay?"

A smile finds it's way on her face, "Yeah, I'm great."




+





"Soomi!" there's a chorus of yells as she enters the studio alone, much later than Jinhwan since they couldn't have been seen together entering. Giggles leave her lips as she greets them all, everyone getting up and hugging her, screeching about how glad they were she was okay after yesterday.

A nostalgic feeling creeps up her spine as she turns to glance around the recording studio. To think all those months ago she'd rushed in here, late once again for work, a complete mess and nearly barfed at the sight of the seven boys. Now she couldn't be happier to see them.

"Alright," she hums with a grin as she takes her seat in the booth next to Jinhwan, shifting to get comfortable with how close they were sitting together. Jinhwan's arm goes around her shoulder, and she raises her legs to go over his thighs as she looks to the others, "any song ideas?"

Some of them shrug, not knowing what they could possibly do. Meanwhile, Yunhyeong and Donghyuk were smirking at the two of them like the sight of her and Jinhwan together was the most magnificent thing they'd ever laid eyes on. Soomi caught sight of this and rose a brow, narrowing her eyes as she asks suspiciously, "what?!"

Donghyuk is putting his hands up in defense, but the brave Yun seemed to have a lot to say. "I'm just so glad you two got over it and confessed your feelings, it's so cute," he gushes, setting his chin in the palm of his hand as he reminisces like a grandmother, "my favorite couple."

Practically on queue, they both speak up immediately to inform him, "we aren't together."

Realizing what they just did, Soomi's cheeks go pink and she has to look away. Yunhyeong snorts, finding this humorous as he shakes his head, "right, whatever you say."

"At least they aren't doing it in our apartment anymore-" Junhoe didn't even get to finish his remark when Soomi seemed to teleport across the room, shoe already in hand. "Joking, joking!" he whines pleadingly with his hands in the air.

Rolling her eyes, she returns to her seat. In that moment, Hanbin claps his hands together to grab everyone's attention, "Guys, if we don't start now we're never going to get anything done, and I'm pretty sure CEO-nim is expecting results if this collab is gonna happen."

Snapping her fingers, everyone looks to Soomi as she sits up straight to reach for her bag she slipped under the seat, saying in the meantime, "You're right, one second." 

All watching her curiously, she plops her blue bag atop her lap. Unzipping it, only seconds later she was pulling out a crumpled piece of paper. Opening it from how it was in a ball, she holds it out to them, a proud look forming on her face, "check this out, it's something I've been working on."

Hanbin is the one who takes the sheet of notebook paper first. Bobby and Donghyuk, who are sitting on either side of him, leaning over his shoulder to read what's on it as well. 

Slightly worried about the feedback, she crosses her own leg over the other and purses her lips. The sudden feeling of someone's hand on her thigh is what causes her to look up, seeing Jinhwan already peering down at her with those deep eyes of his.

"what's the song about?" he leaned in to whisper.

A small, playful grin finds its way on her lips as she shakes her head, "you'll see."

Only seconds after, the three boys who read it first look up to her with enlarged eyes. "This is really good, Soomi!" Donghyuk is crying, being the angel she'd always known him as.

Hanbin and Bobby both nod in agreement. "We could definitely do something with this, I think it'll be good," Hanbin says, the smallest bit of a smile creeping on his lips.

Soomi only shrugs as if she knew they would say these things, "called it."

Junhoe shakes his head in disappointment across from her, "wow, self absorbent much?"

Meeting his gaze, she already knew the joke session between them was starting. Raising her hands to her lips, she blows air kisses at him as she winks, "I learned it from you."

Pretending to catch them, he acts as if he has a wig on as he pretends to flip hair over his shoulder, "oh, you don't say. The difference is I'm actually gorgeous."

Her mouth falls open at his comment, the previous joking tone in her voice disappearing as she snaps, "yah, Koo Junhoe, I swear to God!"

But the boy was only in a fit of snickers.

A sudden knock at the door of the recording room the eight of them stood in caused the laughter of everyone to stop. Snapping back into reality, Jinhwan hurriedly removes his arm from her shoulders and she sits back up in a straight position. 

They just manage to scoot a normal distance away from each other the moment not one, but two people enter.

Shifting uncomfortably in her seat in hope they saw nothing, her eyes fall onto one of the faces. Hurriedly, everyone of the eight had stood up to bow politely greeting who entered. "Manager-nim, nice to see you!" she obviously knew her own manager, and since the boys had greeted the other, she assumed theirs was here as well.

"Soomi, iKON," her manager nods as a greeting, "we came to check up on how you were doing, any progress or ideas for your supposive collaboration?"

"Actually, yes," Soomi's first to speak up before anyone else, her bold attitude always cutting through, "I actually have been working on something that could work well, and I think we could come up with a few more ideas, too."

Both of them looked surprised at her words. A woman, probably in her early thirties who Soomi presumed to be their manager, offers a small smile to her, "Nice to see you're all working well together."

"So, do you mean you think you could make more than one song together?"

Soomi shrugs, not really knowing the answer so she looks to the others. Exchanging short looks, the seven boys all nod in unison. "Soomi's a talented rapper and singer, we could do a lot of new songs together."

"I think this is a splendid idea," her manager claps a few times enthusiastically, "I'll deliver the good news to the CEO immediately, and schedule more sessions for the both of you."

Everyone rises to their feet to bow as the two of them leave. Soomi couldn't contain her excitement once they had closed the door behind them once again.

"This is so daebak!" she's squealing, "I never thought I'd be able to make music with you guys!"

Pulling them into a victory group hug, Chanwoo is the one who chuckles as he remarks, "to think only months ago you hated our guts."

He wasn't wrong.











20:00 | lover boy

Hanbin:
soomi, can u meet me in my studio in like 10?

Soomi:
ur studio? i've never even been in it

Soomi:
what's the sudden occasion 

Hanbin:
to work on music, idiot

Hanbin:
i have an idea for that song you were working on

Hanbin:
and something else 

Soomi:
oh!! already?

Soomi:
you sure are a music mastermind, huh

Hanbin:
duh

Soomi:
 ok ! mr sass ! 

Hanbin:
r u coming or not? i don't have time to wait

Soomi:
ok u impatient fuck

Soomi:
 i told jinhwan i had to get some sleep but i guess i can

Hanbin:
no wonder y he came home looking so lonely

Soomi:
srsly? omg i feel bad now 

Hanbin:
 he won't know ur here

Soomi:
well can't i just go by his room real quick

Hanbin:
no bec then i won't see u 4 five hrs

Soomi:
 point taken


Only around thirty minutes later had Soomi successfully sneaked into Hanbin's studio. It was more homey than she expected it to be, with stuffed animals she didn't imagine him having set on shelves. A leather sofa sat on one side, looking onto his desk and music set up. He had a lot of expensive equipment, costing all well over thousands and thousands of dollars.

Despite all that, that wasn't what made Soomi nearly pass out when she entered.

It was the sight of the polaroid's that hung on his wall. There were a lot, many of whom she recognized as his family and with just him and Hanbyul. It was when she sees another girl she had never seen before, who she presumed might be one of his friends. That was, until her eyes landed on the ones were they were kissing and holding hands like in those aesthetic Tumblr posts. Although the photos had her cringing and awestruck from how cute they were at the same time, she suddenly understood why he never let anyone in his studio.

Luckily, she was the first one, and she was also about to make him regret that.

"Oh my god, who is she?!" She's immediately screaming just as Hanbin turns around, motioning to something about the music on his screen. However, as soon as the question leaves her lips, his entire face falls like he had heard something awful. 

Slowly, his eyes glance up to the many polaroids of him and the mystery girl. Realizing now what Soomi was freaking out over, Hanbin face-palms, a groan of frustration leaving his lips. This was the last thing he needed right now, especially from fucking Kim Soomi.

When he looks back to her, there's a devious smirk on her face.

Heaving out a sigh, he spins back around in his chair, "Please don't. I didn't invite you to talk about my personal life, I invited you so we can work on this song."

Pouting from his boring words, she couldn't help but smile again when she sees the hint of pink spreading over his cheeks. Soomi falls into the leather chair beside him, snapping her fingers in front of his concentrated face, "Hanbin-ah, if you have been having this secret girlfriend and no one has yet to find out, I demand to know."

His eyes are unimpressed. 

"Seriously," she throws her hands in the air out of exasperation, "she's so cute, how could you not tell me?!"

Hanbin is in a daze of confusion, blinking at her like she was a lunatic as he deadpans, "Soomi, it's not like we're exactly close," she was having trouble understanding what he meant until he made hand motions between the two of them.

Frowning, she realized something else from his words other than the obvious, "wait, but you didn't deny that she's your secret girlfriend-," he holds up a hand to stop her.

"Don't."

Raising a hand abruptly, she slams it against the back of his head lightly, but yet hard enough to get her message across. A sharp ow is heard from Hanbin, and he rubs the back of his head as he narrows his eyes at her in annoyance.

"Be honest, I'm the only female friend you have other than her, and I know you've never dated anyone before," she rolls her eyes, leaning back and crossing her arms over her chest, "and we are close, you're like my weirdly smart little brother," she hums with a mischievous grin, "so, who's the girl? Do the guys know?"

Exhaling deeply as he realizes she wasn't giving up any time soon, Hanbin buries his face in his hands. To think he actually thought Kim Soomi was going to actually focus on writing for once. What'd he really have to lose, anyway?

A smile creeps on his lips as he looks back up to the pictures above the both of them on his wall. Whenever he felt frustrated or stressed from working, he'd just look at them, and it was like everything was suddenly perfect. Like any negative thoughts drain from his brain because he remembers how blessed he really was, as long as he had her.

It wasn't easy to hide a healthy relationship from the public. Plus, it was Hanbin's first girlfriend. He himself had a lot to learn, he had a lot to improve and grow on. Never did he think throughout his life as an idol and work that he'd be lucky enough to meet someone willing to work through all those difficulties with him, willing to love him for who he is and who he wants to be. 

None of the guys knew about her yet, and he didn't know when the right time was going to be to tell them. He hadn't been with her long enough to really talk about confidently bringing her around this life he had. In his time off, he spent it all with her, and he didn't know if she would really be willing to take the stress it was up here.

But he knew now that Soomi knew, there was no turning back. There was only one way to go - and that way was forward.




+



"She's my whole world, Soomi-ah, there's nothing more refreshing than just being able to be myself. No matter what, when, where - whenever I'm having a hard time she's there, she understands. It's like I can do anything and she'll love me the same," he gushes, the blush staying on his cheeks the entire time he speaks. Soomi only listens intently, nodding after each sentence, motioning for him to go on. Hesitantly, he does, "I never believed in love at first sight, or really love in general. I always thought it was a silly made up figment the people before us made up, to try to make sense of the way the human brain works, to make it seem like there's something to live for throughout the darkness. That's why I've always been so good at writing these pitiful lyrics, because I didn't think it could be real."

With a sigh, he pauses, looking to his feet, "but then I met her, and it was like everything I ever dreamed of was set right before my eyes. It was like seeing the sunset for the first time, like my eyes had finally been opened, and that day I told myself I was in love."

She found herself gawking at him unintentionally, and he blinked at her a few times, "what - why are you looking at me like that?" he buries his face in his hands, mumbling in frustration, "yah - jinja, this is why I don't share my emotions-," she cuts him off by ripping his hand off his face harshly.

"Shut up, I think this is fucking adorable, keep talking about her," she snaps, and Hanbin stares at her like she was ill for a second.

Finally, he shrugs, in deep thought, "I don't know, Soomi-ah, I can so confidently say I love her. I don't think it's really love until you get that feeling in the pit of your stomach every time you see them, until you feel that lightheaded feeling and the pounding of your heart in your own ears when they smile at you. It's not love unless you feel it, that feeling of freedom. We can go anywhere together, and it's like I'm not some stupid idol from television or the media, but I'm just - me. I'm Kim Hanbin, I'm hers, and that's all I could ask for. It's like I'm normal, what I've always wanted, normal and in love."

Hanbin's words had made her think more than she intended. All she really wanted was to hear about who the mystery girl was, how they met, and what they had been up to. She wondered how Hanbin and her managed to keep their relationship private somehow, as whenever she had one it seemed like everyone was constantly breathing down her neck.

But hearing him talk about it, it made her think. Maybe that was the problem. Maybe she was thinking too much about the media, about her work, about her life and the people around her. She should just be thinking of him, and their life. Soomi should focus on their present and not their future.

She should live in the moment, her moment.

This is why only hours later after they successfully worked on a special song, meant for her and Jinhwan, she found herself tip toe-ing to his bedroom. The door was closed but luckily not locked, and she let out a deep breathe as she slowly twisted the knob, peeking inside into the darkness.

Although she couldn't see in the dark, she could only imagine how cute he must look right now (which sounded a little creepy.) Either way, she brushed her hair out of her face, a plotting look on her face as she quietly shuts the door behind her.

Still walking as slow as possible so she didn't make any noise, Jinhwan's soft snores fill the room. Due to how well she knows the whereabouts of his bedroom, she used her remembrance of everything to make her way to his bed.

Her hands out in front of her so she was careful not to walk into anything, finally she feels the soft fabric of his sheets against her palms. Gradually, she figures he was on the far side of the bed closer to the wall like usual. 

Letting out another silent breathe, she figured it was now or never. It was time to stop doubting and start living.

With such a thought, she climbs onto the bed, continuously making her way towards where she thought he would be until she felt his arm. Her hands trail up his arm until she finds his face in the darkness, and a smile spreads over her lips. Running the pad of her thumb against her soft skin, Soomi sits there, criss-crossed, wondering if she really had the heart to wake him up from his beauty sleep.

"Soomi-ah?" his tired whisper fills the room, startling her for a second.

"Hm, how'd you know it was me?" she teases.

A chuckle sounds from his lips, "I can smell your perfume, and if it was anyone else touching me I'd kill them."

"Good thing I'm not anyone else then," she giggled, "now, I'm glad you're awake, come on," without another second thought, she's grabbing his arm in the dark and pulling him towards the floor as she climbed off her own bed.

"Yah, yah," Jinhwan is saying in obvious misunderstanding as she flicks on the lamp beside his bed. Using his hand she wasn't holding onto to rub his eye, he stares at her in confusion, "what are we doing?"

She couldn't help but smile again. He said we.

"We're going out," she informs cheerily, but Jinhwan just kept staring at her blankly.

"Seriously?" he didn't sound against it, but just didn't get it. Last he checked, she was the one who was all about being careful.

She nods, "Yes, so come on, you know I'm not a patient person, Jinhwan."

Shaking his head, he keeps holding her hand as he climbs out of bed. Pulling her towards him, he lands a kiss on her forehead as he hums, "oh, I know."
















21:00 | 3 AM walks

"My family?" Soomi's eyes widen, but she tries to hide the surprise and nerve that spreads over her expression as she averts her gaze from his eyes, looking elsewhere towards the sky as they walk. The wind blew through her brunette locks, blowing them every which way, and she pulls the hems of her coat tighter against her, listening to the click of her shoes against the sidewalk pavement.

However, despite her trying to hide that look on her face with her best efforts, Jinhwan knew how to read her emotions well enough to know that it was a sore subject. Clearing his throat, he hurriedly addresses, "Sorry if it's not something you like to talk about, I just felt like we don't really know as much about each other as you know - we should."

Comprehending what he was trying to say, it didn't take her long to understand. Pursing her lips, Soomi hesitantly nods, waving her hand in the air in front of them in dismissal. With the night sky peering down on them as they make their way along the quiet trail in the park at 3 AM, she lets out a deep exhale, preparing herself to begin such a story.

"Well, my family's in the States, I'm uh - part American," she hums as a beginning, and Jinhwan only nods, not looking at her but watching the path ahead which was merely lit by the small street lights above them. Swallowing her worry, she tries to tell herself there was nothing wrong with her past, but she knew she still couldn't tell him, not things like that. "I had really strict parents, they basically held a gun to me and my siblings heads with everything we did," she looks to him again to see he was looking at her weirdly from her words, so she hurries to explain further, "I mean they were controlling. it was like, sure, you always want the best for your kids - but they never let us make any decisions on our own. They were rich, well known business folk in the area, so if anyone in the family ever made a wrong move it was death for you. You'd be all over the media, and it would blow up right in your face and your parents faces."

There's a look of astonishment on Jinhwan's face as he can only seem to mumble, "I never thought you'd come from a family like that."

She shrugs, looking up to the stars as she goes on, "Well, yeah, it's not something I like to share with anyone in Korea. Long story short, we'll just say I did something that didn't only blow up in my parents' faces, it pretty much exploded. They breathed down my neck so hard after that, so when I turned 18 I just decided to leave, I just couldn't take it anymore," she sighs, shaking her head at the memory, "I called up my old family here in Korea, and I bought a cheap ticket and just - left," her eyes grow dull at the thought as she admits all her mistakes, "changed my number, got an ID with a Korean name instead, and asked my relatives to never give them any information about me."

"What?" Jinhwan gawks, "but you're - you don't live with your relatives anymore. You never spoke to your family back in the States? Don't you want to see them, don't you think they want to see you?!" he wasn't speaking in anyway rudely, but just out of complete concern. 

A bitter laugh leaves the lips of the idol star, and she takes the hood of her jacket off her head to fix her hair, "No, trust me, if my parents wanted to find me, they could very easily."

Sympathetically, his face falls as he mutters, "what about your siblings?"

"I don't know," she shrugs, "I'm sure they're fine. It's not like I don't miss them, but it's not my business to barge into their lives, and it's not like I can now, anyway."

His mouth slightly agape as he tries to find the words to say, something to convince her she should try to see them. Jinhwan loved his family, he kept them so close to his dear heart that it broke it just hearing about this, about how Soomi's life really was. 

It was then, that he really got it. He understood her trust issues didn't come from the scandal, they came from a lifetime of betrayal and hurt. She was treated like a dog by her own parents, and as he listened to her go on to explain how they neglected and treated her in such ways for such silly things, like when she wouldn't get an A on a school assignment, he couldn't understand it any better.

Because where someone comes from has an impact on who they become.

With a sigh, Jinhwan wraps an arm around her shoulder, startling her at first. Pulling her into him, her head lands on his chest as they walk, their footsteps in sync. The wind blows against them, creating goosebumps up their skin and leaving a chill against their spines. But either way, in that moment as he assured her he would always be there, that he loved her, they still felt warm. They felt warm with the presence for each other, and the love between them that was spiraling through the air from their hearts.






+






"Oh, I almost forgot to tell you!" she suddenly gasps after popping another fry in her mouth, shifting in her seat as Jinhwan looks up to her with curious eyes, "It's about the song."

He raises a brow in question, "the song, as in the one we're making together?"

Soomi knew he was referring to her and not only him but all the boys of iKON, it was why she couldn't hide the devious grin that spreads over her lips as she boasts, "no, this song is just going to be us!"

Blinking a few times, he stared at her in disbelief. "What, really?!"

Nodding vigorously, she continues eating her McDonald's fries, "Yeah, me and Hanbin are already working on the song, and we were thinking it would be perfect for just the two of us, and then we can all just do another song together," she explains what her and Hanbin had spoken about regarding the song only hours ago, "course, we still have to run it by the CEO but, surely he won't mind!"

Chuckling in astonishment, Jinhwan reaches over the table to grab her hand. Intertwining their fingers, he squeezes it as he gushes, "that's great, I'm so excited."

"Me too, you think we could finish it in time for MAMA?"

Jinhwan furrows his brows as he grasps her words, "huh? Are you suggested we could perform it there?"

Without a second thought, Soomi shrugs casually, "yeah, why not?"

When she looks back up to him from her food again, he's staring down at her like she was a mental patient. Feeling a bit nervous under his gaze, she opens her mouth to speak, when he beat her to it. With words dripping of concern, he sighs, "Soomi-ah, are you alright?"

"Better than ever," she only hums, wondering what he seemed so bothered about, "why?"

"I don't know," his voice is a mumble as he thinks hard about it, still holding onto her hand, "it's just - you used to be so worried about getting caught, and now it's like you want to seize the world together," he laughs from his choice of words, a smile hinting it's way on his lips as he confesses, "I'm just really happy we're like this, I don't want it to change, I don't want you to change."

Before she can say anything, he raises the back of her hand to his lips, leaving a lingering kiss there. Soomi's eyes remain on his brown orbs as it falls back to the table, and she purses her lips for a second.

"Well, we still have to be careful but I'm not as focused on it as I used to be," she offers an explanation hesitantly, "It was all I used to think about, was 'what if someone sees,' or 'what if someone finds out,' and it kept me from being as happy as I could be. In reality, I just want to be with you, and it's not that I don't care about my career, but I love you, and I'm not afraid to show that."

Jinhwan was smiling from ear to ear, his heart pumping out of his chest as he rises to his feet. Walking over to her side of the table, he pulled her up, causing her to gently hit his chest as he returns the bright grin. 

Nothing could explain the bliss he felt exploding within him as he leaned down to connect his lips with hers. He kissed her not once, but twice, pulling away for only a millisecond in between each one. With lips that were so soft, so sweet, he never wanted to stop. He never wanted to stop showing her how much she really meant to him.

That was, until he realized they were still standing in a fast food restaurant. 

"Let's go," he whispers as they finally pulled away, his hand finding hers again. 

Soomi only nods in agreement, smiling giddily to herself as they make their way out of the nearly empty restaurant. 

The cold night air fans their skin as they leave, and her eyes wander to the skies. The stars were still out, mockingly shining down upon them, and she couldn't help but gawk up at the wonders of the dark sky above. Even the moon was such a sight, and it had always been one of her favorite activities just to stare at it.

With the only sound being the slight blowing of the wind through the trees, Soomi and Jinhwan began their walk home. After walking through the park for hours, getting food and ice cream from a cheap fast food place, and going into WalMart to buy useless things they didn't need, she felt normal.

She felt like she was a teenager again, doing whatever she wanted and whenever she pleased. Like this was just something she did so casually with the boy she loved. For once, it didn't feel like she was a celebrity. It didn't feel like she had to go to the company in the morning, or sing in front of millions of people.

It didn't feel like she had to live her life according to pointless rules in a book, no dating, no clubbing, no freedom. She just felt normal, like there was nothing to hide in her life.

And even though it wasn't true, it felt like it was. 

Soomi wished that feeling would never leave. 





22:00 | new rules

"Hanbin-ah!" she boasts in excitement, extending her arms out to show her enthusiasm to the boy. Usually she would have greeted him with a hug, and he knew this well, but instead she keeps her distance feet away from him, only bowing politely and forming a kind smile on her lips. 

Staring at her weirdly, he bows in return hesitantly, "Soomi," he hums out her name in a greeting, "what's up with the attitude?"

Soomi peered up at him from her small height like he was insane, shaking her head in disappointment that he didn't get it. When he only continues to look at her in wonder, she scoffs, "Silly, we're at the company, I can't seem all friendly with you."

Indeed, the two of them were standing in front of YG Entertainment at exactly 10 in the morning. With only getting a few hours of sleep, she felt like her body was bound to shut down any minute, but she remembers her and Hanbin had plans to meet up at the studio - but she thought she meant his studio. This was why it was rather difficult for her to comprehend when he called her to inform he meant the company studio, meaning they would be out in view for anyone to see. 

At the fact that anyone could do something as simple as snap a photo when she got too close to the idol and practically end her career, to say she was horrified was an understatement.

But when she meets his gaze, there's a humorous glint in his eyes. In disbelief, she realizes he must know something she didn't, otherwise he would be acting the same way. Through his child like chuckles, he offers an explanation, "it's okay, the CEO actually scheduled for us to be in the studio, so we can work on that song."

Her eyes nearly fell out of her sockets, "Whoa, really? When did this even happen?"
