Shrugging, she lets her nerves calm themselves as she walks closer so they can make their way towards the entrance since it was freezing outside. "I got a call this morning, apparently he's open to us working together, dropping the whole 'we can't be seen with each other' rule if there even was one."

She could hardly believe it.

"No way!" she screams accidentally, unable to contain her joy. Moving her hands and body in spastic ways as they walk in the building, she whisper yells to Hanbin, gaining them some weird looks from her behavior, "I can finally be around you guys without getting in trouble - this is a miracle!"

Laughing at her reaction, he nods in agreement, "Yeah, CEO-nim really believes we can make something great together, so I'm hoping to live up to those expectations."

Jumpily, Soomi makes her way to the elevator with the bright boy. Pushing the button, it wasn't long until you'd find them standing in it, and only then did she sigh dreamily, "this is great, that rule always worried me so much," but suddenly another question popped into her mind, "did he say anything about me and Nani's song together?"

Hanbin only shakes his head sympathetically, "No but I don't think he would say no, he seems to be in a good mood recently."

Letting out a breathe, she hums as the elevator dings and they exit towards the studio, "Yeah, it's a relief. Guess we can only hope for the best."

The next few hours, you'd find the two of them working their asses off. Hanbin had previously been under an impression Soomi was a bit lazy, and she liked to talk more than work. However, that was only because he had never seen her when she was working. The truth was, if Kim Soomi set her mind on something, it always got done. And this song was something she definitely had her mind set on, she wasn't planning to rest until it was done.

And being that Hanbin was a hard worker himself, especially when it came to making songs, you'd imagine they would be the ultimate team. But the unfortunate part was that both of them were a little too used to working by themselves, so much that Soomi got distracted with in her own thoughts and ideas and forgot to even tell him what she was doing.

The clock had struck 1 PM, and they had most of the song lyrics for her and Jinhwan's song finished - but most of them had been written by her. Hanbin was the one working on the music itself, and the two were having a rather hard time agreeing on certain melodies and rhythms, when it was as if a light bulb had popped above her head.

Diverting her eyes from the computer screen he was focusing on, mixing different beats together to try to form something, she put a hand to her forehead and stared down at the paper where the lyrics were. Maybe if I did that, and then that, she formatted things in her mind, like she always did when she was producing and writing. 

Pursing her lips, it came to her. As she spins in the roll-y chair back towards Hanbin, his phone suddenly went off in his back pocket, snapping him out of his serious daze.

She watches as he pulls it out in confusion, and his eyes seemed to brighten like the sun when he set sight on whoever it was. Soomi wasn't dumb, and she rose a brow as he looked back up to her with pleading eyes, "Answer it, I need to use the computer anyway."

He mouths a thank you to her, before walking out of the recording and writing studio. Soomi only watches him leave, offering him a grin and a thumbs up. 

Once he's gone, a breath of relief leaves her lips. In only a second, she fell into the chair, scooting forward so she was close enough to see the computer screen.

"Time to make a song," she mutters to herself, leaning in to begin. This was getting done today if it was the last thing she did.






+





As if it's coming out of a movie
You're my life's drama climax version roller coaster
My boring life's up and down slowly getting bigger
Sometimes I hate you so much it kills me
But without you it would kill me too
In my life you are the only light


"SOOMI!" her heart nearly falls out of her chest as the sound of screams echoing into the sound proof room. She'd dropped her pen from the yells, having only had one of the earphones connected to the computer in as she was mixing the music for the song. 

Still in a state of shock from the sight of the boys running into the room, she takes off the headset and sets it down, exhaling deeply as she spins around in the chair. Donghyuk, Yunhyeong, Junhoe, and lastly, Jinhwan, all grin down at her, and only then did her eyes land on the bags of food they held in their hands.

"Hanbin-hyung said you were probably starving when you sent him home, so we brought you lunch!" Donghyuk says cheerily, handing her a bag.

Practically drooling from the scent of food, she could only nod vigorously, melting from their efforts, "I am really hungry, thank you guys!" she gushes brightly, and they all shrug as if it was nothing.

Jinhwan greets her with that sweet voice of his, dipping down to leave a short kiss on her lips. There's whistles heard from their friends, followed by them catching her famous glare, but everyone just laughs it off as they all take their seats.

Unpacking the food, Soomi was quick to grab her chopsticks and start desperately shoving noodles in her mouth. The others watch her with wide eyes, and Junhoe raises a brow. She was expecting some sort of witty, rude joke from him so she could prepare to stab him with one of the chopsticks, but he didn't say anything. That was, until a small humorous grin spread over his lips, and he tried to mimic the sound of a cow.

"Yah, Koo Junhoe!" she snapped in annoyance like every other day, forming a fist and hitting him on the arm a few times. Although he - like every other day - wasn't phased, he still winced and attempting to dodge her hits, in fits of hysterical laughter as his childish acts.

They eat in silence for a while, making small talk every now and then. Finally, Yunhyeong clears his throat, swallowing his food before he hums curiously, "so, how is the song going? Is it the one for you and Jinhwan?"

She nods at his second question, swallowing her last rice cake before replying, "Yeah, it's actually pretty much done except for the vocals and rap, obviously, but the lyrics are finished and I have the music mixed."

Jinhwan perks up at this, clearly interested in what kind of song she wrote for them to sing together. "Really?" he cries, and she nods to him, but a look of sadness spreads over his expression, "I wanted to help you write it since it's our duet."

Surprised, she's speechless for a second. Her body nearly malfunctions from the puppy eyes he was showing her, and she offers a reassuring smile, "Oh, well there are still some last minute touches I need to make to the lyrics, you can pitch in some ideas!"

There was that smile again, "I'd love to."

Soomi giggles at how irresistible he was, leaning in to close their proximity since he was sitting beside her on the booths set around the room so she could kiss his cheek. Just as her lips touched his baby soft skin, a gagging sound echoed through the room.

Her eyes immediately set on Junhoe, who was holding his hand to his throat like there was something stuck in it. "My eyes are burning," he pleaded.

Yunhyeong rolls his own eyes, shaking his head as he sasses, "then why are you choking?"

Realizing he was caught in an idiotic act, he stops and shrugs in defeat. "I don't know, but can you guys not suck each other off in front of me? Seriously, I just turned five yesterday, it's honestly fucking distu-," his words were cut off when Yunhyeong started beating him with the empty box from his lunch.

"Okay, okay, I was fucking kidding!" you would find the tall boy pleading desperately only a minute later, as the two of them were running around like mad men. Yunhyeong didn't seem to have much of a desire to stop though, as he seemed rather caught up with June's antics as well. Dong was only laughing at the two of them, and Soomi couldn't say she didn't find it satisfying, honestly.

"Ew, you got soy sauce all over me!"

"Sounds like a you problem," Yun sighs carelessly as he falls back into his seat. Meanwhile, Junhoe was wincing, looking helpless as he grabbed a napkin to try to wipe the sauce off of him.

"Anyway," Soomi clears her throat, looking away from the incident and back to the others, "I didn't get the approval for us to do the duet yet exactly, but I was thinking that if the song is as good as I'm hoping, he'll end up loving it too much to say no!"

They all exchange looks, nodding in agreement after a moment. Donghyuk is the first one to speak as he finally finishes his own lunch, "I think that's a great idea, I'm sure the song will be amazing with as talented as you and Jinhwan-hyung are."

"Not like CEO-nim wouldn't love something by you, either way," Yunhyeong chimes in with his soft smile, "it'll be perfect, Soomi-ah."

She could only hope so.



23:00 | number one

"Good job today, you two!" her manager was clapping, a proud look plastered on his expression as she leaves the recording room. Soomi felt like her soul had left her body from working literally from morning to night on the song, not taking more than a couple minutes of a break, but now that it was finished - she felt good, more than good. That feeling only increased when she sees the wide smile on Jinhwan's lips, as he rises from one of the booth seats in the room and walked closer to her.

From the way their eyes met, his irises burning into her green orbs, she could feel the tension in the air. She wanted to hug him, and do even more than that when her lips momentarily flicker to his, they looked so soft. 

"Thank you, but most of it was really Soomi," she jumped in surprise from the feeling of a hand on her shoulder. The familiar cologne filled her nostrils only a second later, and she felt a lump form in her throat when she looked to her side, noticing the close proximity suddenly between them. 

However, Jinhwan wasn't looking at her, but to the staff that sat around the computer, and her manager that was standing before them. She swore behind the angelic smile on his lips that there was a smirk, was he aware of how she was feeling right now? How was he having such an easy time? Soomi wanted nothing more than to release all the stress she'd been feeling after working all day, he could tell, couldn't he?

"You both worked hard, you're a better team than any of us expected," her manager hums casually, "I'm sure the CEO will be pleased to hear what you've created, would you like to hear the finished piece?"

Swallowing the lump in her throat, she glances over to the boy beside her only to see him already looking at her. She knew it was now or never, and Jinhwan only made that more clear when he nodded in her direction as if to say let's do it.

"Sure, we'd love to," she clears her throat after letting these words into the air, still feeling awkward with Jinhwan's hand on her shoulder. Her clearing her throat so abruptly, thankfully only to his notice, she thought was enough to get him to take it off.

And a breath of relief left her lips when she felt the weight leave her shoulder, until she felt an arm go around both of them. He just put his arm around me, the realization sunk into her brain, and in a frenzy she looked to Jinhwan with annoyed and confused eyes, burning with fear.

But he just looked back at her as if he had no worries in the world, and flashed that awful smile of his.

"Alright, here we go," the producer had announced as he clicks something on the computer. Everyone was focused on whatever he was doing, and gratefully not paying attention to the two idols.

Looking back to Jinhwan, she nearly yelped when he immensely pulls her towards him. Her heart is already on the floor when her face hits his chest, his scent throwing her emotions into a battlefield and the feeling of his no doubt toned chest underneath the thin fabric of his tee shirt was enough to nearly break her.

Scurrying out of the position, she stands only an inch apart from him, his arms still around her. Her eyes are angry as they meet his mischievous glint, and she mouths in frustration, "what are you doing?!"

He only shrugs, and she was about to take off her shoe and start beating him with it, when a certain melody fills the air. The sound cuts her off from whatever she had been thinking, seeming to erase any thoughts in her brain. 

With wide eyes, her head jerks to watch the staff that are still gathered around the computer. That's our song? she asked herself mentally, not seeming to believe that the perfect sound that was filling the room was really theirs.

But soon, the gorgeous voice of Kim Jinhwan had filled her ears, and it was enough for her to realize it. She wrote those lyrics, this really was their song. Soomi hadn't expected it to sound so good, they really did work wonders.

Your fingertips, your fragrant lotion
You touch my dry heart
Your stares like the blue color of the ocean
Throw your body into the refreshing waves

As the lyrics filled her ears, she watched as the staff all make noises of astonishment, sounding pleased. They bop their heads to the beat, clapping when they think something sounds good. The sight made her purse her lips, trying not to grin from everything she was feeling right then.

It was perfect, she thought, pleased that her hard work had felt as if it was already paying off. Suddenly, she feels the grazing of someone's fingertips against her arm, causing her eyes to snap towards the boy that was still standing beside her. 

Jinhwan's soft eyes were enough to make her knees grow weak, and she found herself grinning when he only started mouthing the words to the song playing in the air.

On a day with blinding sunlight
A night laced with romantic stars
Wanna be with you, only you
Only you~

Her rap filled the air a second later, and the sound of ooh's filled the air from the staff at the flow of it. Giggling to herself, she looks back to them, feeling like everything had gone right in her life for once. What more could she really ask for that wouldn't be unreasonable? This was it.

But Soomi should've known that even when it feels like everything is perfect, there's always going to be something just dying to catch up. The eyes of a certain soul were burning holes into her body, as if they were scheming something within their mind just staring at her. With the way Jinhwan's arm was around her, his hand rubbing her forearm every so often as their song continued to play, the woman had a certain equation formed in her head, one that she knew made perfect sense. 

If only poor Soomi and Jinhwan knew to watch out.



+




"SOOMI!"

"SOOMI, DID YOU HEAR?!"

The loud, ear piercing screeches of the boys only got more excruciating as her and Jinhwan entered the apartment building. Soomi's nose immediately sensed the smell of Yunhyeong's cooking, and as desperately as she wanted something to eat after her and Jinani's long day, it seemed like it was the last thing she was going to get. As only seconds later, six boys were running at her from the kitchen, their arms flailing in the air and screaming like children.

"I CAN'T BELIEVE THIS, CONGRATULATIONS!" they continued to cheer, and she stared at them blankly as they all crowded around her like fan boys.

Her eyes look to Jinhwan, who was now pushed far away from her and was still standing by the door with her blue bag in hand. He sets it down before meeting her gaze, knowing she was asking him if he had any idea what they were on about. Clueless as she was, he shrugs.

"What's up with you guys?" she dares to ask hesitantly.

And the moment the words left her lips, she'd never regretted anything more. It was like she set off a time bomb as they all imploded with yells.

"YOU REALLY HAVEN'T HEARD?!"

They only continued on, but had yet to tell her what it was about. She was honestly getting annoyed when Jinhwan had started fussing at them to calm down. He was just pushing them all away from crowding around her, when Donghyuk holds his phone out to her.

Still lost, she takes it, turning around the screen so it was visible to her.

Global Charts
#1: SOOMI - How's This?

"Wah~," she stands there for a second as she stares down at it, refreshing the page to try and see if it was some sort of sick joke. But every time, it only loaded the same result. Mouth hanging open, her jaw practically on the floor from shock, she could only mutter in surprise, "No fucking way."

"Congrats, Soomi-ah!" everyone cheers in unison, thankfully not screaming at the top of their lungs anymore. 

When she hands Dong his phone back, Jinhwan snatches it to see what all the fuss is about. Once he realizes, his eyes brighten, and he has the same look on his face that was on Soomi's only seconds ago. 

Shaking his head as he smiles from ear to ear, he couldn't help but gush, "I knew you could do it." 

"Thanks, Jinan," she grins up at him, and he only waved a hand dismissively. A yelp leaves her lips when he grabs both of her hands, pulling her towards him and once again into his chest.

But this time, when her face hits the soft fabric of that tee, inhaling the scent of that cologne he always wore, she snuggled into it. Never had anyone been so comfortable, but her moment of peace only lasted so long when he lifted her face by putting a finger under her chin.

"You're amazing, Soomi," his voice is a whisper as his eyes dreamily looked down at hers. She feels her heartbeat pace increase to a scary speed, and her knees grow weak under her from the way his eyes peered into hers. He was too much for her to take at times like this.

And even though the other boys were watching them like it was a cinema movie, she didn't pay any attention. The thing was, when she was with Jinhwan, it didn't feel like anyone else was in the room, the building, or even the world. He made her feel like it was only them, as if the whole world was theirs to explore.

Soomi cherished that feeling, which was what inclined her to lean forward, tilting her head ever so slightly to kiss him. Those soft lips of his moved in sync with hers, and she never wanted to leave his arms then. 

As whistles from the boys filled the room, she knew that at least then - she never had to.





24:00 | apartment rendezvous

Exhaling deeply, she clicks the post button, watching as the app loads and prepares the photo. Only a second later had it been thrown out into the world, and she purses her lips, tapping on the notifications bar and refreshing. Her green eyes are met with already thousands of likes and comments, and her heart felt full of joy as she scrolled through them.

This was the basis of her morning. Soomi and Jinhwan's song had been sent to the CEO, who would check over the song and report back to her manager and staff, that would report back to her and Jinhwan. If he ended up liking it, they'd hopefully be able to perform it at the next award show coming up with in the next couple of weeks, right before Christmas. 

However, it was obviously going to take more than just a couple of hours for their boss to run over the new song and decipher how he felt about it. This meant all she felt like doing was waiting, as her mind was too full to even think about writing another song.

Although to her dismay, there was still work to do. Even though her and Jinhwan's song was done, there was still an expected collaboration with her and all the boys of iKON together. They were going to the studio to write today, and they'd even come into Jinhwan's room - where she spent the night - asking if she was coming along. The boys had been confused when she declined, but they reluctantly left without her.

Even Jinhwan had gone, as he had been talking about wanting to really get into songwriting himself lately. She admired his desire, he was already talented enough as it was, but he always kept striving to better himself. It was one of the many things she adored about Kim Jinhwan.

On the other hand, since they were all gone to the company, this left her in the dorm of iKON by herself. She was wearing one of Jinhwan's hoodies, and the only thing keeping her sane as she sat on the edge of his bed, dangling her legs back and forth, was the scent of that cologne she loved radiating off the material. 

A dull sigh leaves her lips as she replies to more comments from her fans. It seemed that people really were liking her new single and even the other side track she performed that was meant for Jinhwan (though thankfully no one knew that.) Everyone was very hyped about her new EP, and she was thrilled for the support. 

Even with what Jungkook had pulled at Melon, people weren't giving her any hate. Sure, she always got a few rude comments here and there, but she expected for them to be as bad as they were when the scandal happened.

But they weren't. As she scrolled, she even saw a few comments talking about how great her and Jungkook would look together. There ended up being more than she planned for regarding her ex fiance, and it was enough to make her stop reading them. I don't think I can see anymore of that.

The last thing she wanted to think about was him.

Sighing, she's about to logout of Instagram itself, when her phone refreshes her feed, and a new post appears.

        

gnani___: recording. big surprise coming to u soon ikonics!

She never knew it was possible for someone's heart to clench so much from seeing one person. Holding a hand to her temples that seemed to be exploding, she could only gawk down at it in disbelief. He was something else.

There were thousands and thousands of comments under the post only minutes later as she was still staring at it. She chuckled as she read some of them, people really were passionate about their love for him - and she found it completely understandable.

As she stared at it, she realized she missed him, even though it had only been around four hours since the guys left. Although she had been planning to just wait at the guys' dorm all day, she was starting to get a bit bored.

I'll just go to my apartment and watch Gossip Girl, she thinks to herself. It was as if a giant light bulb appeared above her head as she climbs out of Jinhwan's bed, gathering her things. Since there weren't many things of hers around - other than her clothes on the floor from last night - it wasn't a huge task to throw it all in that blue bag of hers.

Which was why only moments later, she was heading out the door. Using the key Jinhwan left for her in case she locked it behind her.

Humming to herself as she was walking to the elevator, Soomi pushed the button. Of course, she was feeling anxious about the CEO's opinion on their song, but she felt like other than that - she was in a good place right now. Jungkook had stopped bothering her since that day at Melon, and she and Jinhwan were finally healthy.

Even though they weren't dating, she didn't think there was anything wrong with how they were. It was perfect in her eyes, she didn't need the label of his girlfriend.

The elevator dings, and her head jerks up to see that same old woman from all those months ago. At the sight of her, Soomi immediately bows, offering a smile. 

When she notices the petite young girl standing there, she returned it, waving as she steps out of the elevator herself. "Oh, Soomi, isn't it? I haven't seen you in a while, dear!"

"Hi, Ms. Kim," she was shocked when the woman pulls her in for a hug, but she politely accepts it, "I've just been very busy with work."

"I thought so, my granddaughter has been playing that song of yours for weeks now," she chuckles.

Her eyes widen slightly in surprise, and she couldn't help but gush, "Really? I'm so glad she likes it!"

The woman only nods, saying that it wasn't bad at all. When she's about to leave to her own apartment, Soomi thinks back to how she told her not to inform her granddaughter of ever seeing her because of Jinhwan. 

But what could it really hurt?

"Wait!" she calls as she had taken her first step towards her own apartment. The woman turns around in wonder, only to see the girl bent over and furiously rustling through her bag. She finally finds one of the postcards with her face plastered on it - it was in her bag meant for the new album. The staff gave her samples of everything that would be put in it so she could see if she liked them, and obviously they allowed her to keep them. Soomi wasn't self centered enough to see much value to the photo card, which was why she was grinning to herself as she hoped the woman's granddaughter would. 

Taking out a pen, she leans against the wall to write on the back, after asking the woman for her granddaughter's name.

Yujin, I'm so happy you like my new song <33 I hope that you'll accept this photo card from my new album, thank you for all the love!! I love you lots, I hope to see you some day!

xoxo - Soomi <33

She hands out the photo card to the woman, who had an idea of what she was doing as she grins from ear to ear. "Oh, Yujin-ah is going to love this," she boasts in excitement, "thank you!"

Waving a hand in dismissal, Soomi shakes her head, "no, thank her. I'll see you soon, Ms. Kim!" she bows one last time, and the woman waves in return before heading on her own way.

Sighing, Soomi runs a hand through her hair as she steps into the elevator finally. She was thankful she didn't ask anything about the fact that she was wearing a hoodie three sizes too big for her or that she looked like a complete train wreck. Jinhwan seriously just had that effect on her, and she didn't really know what to do about it at this point.

After pressing the button to her floor, she leans against the side of the elevator. Humming a song to herself, she wondered how the writing at the company was going. The boys did produce and write all of their songs, so she had high expectations for whatever they may create. I can't wait to hear it, she thought as she stared up at the ceiling of the enclosed space, especially if Jinhwan writes it.

A ding sounds, and she heaved her bag further up her shoulder. Making her way into the hall, it seemed like she had only taken a singular step when her phone went off in the pocket of Jinhwan's hoodie.

Pausing her movement, she pulls it out, peering down at the FaceTime call she was receiving from that certain someone.

She'd never answered a call faster.

"Jinhwan-ah!" she squeaks like a child, holding the phone up in front of her to he could see her clearly.

The boy of her dreams had leaned in to stare at his phone, as if he was inspecting where she was. "Soomi, I miss you~," he whined, his voice irregularly small. It sent her heart into a frenzy and she had to divert her eyes to try and hide how much he was playing with her emotions. "Are you in the hall - and is that my hoodie?"

A giggle leaves her lips as she continues her walk, still holding the phone. "Yes, and yes," she hums in reply, and he grins as he jokingly shakes his head in fake disappointment. She catches him doing this and raises a brow, explaining, "your clothes are comfortable, and they smell like you."

Jinhwan wrinkles his nose, "don't be so cute, jagiya, or I'm going to come to that apartment right now."

Feeling devious from his words and how much she missed him, she couldn't help but ask, "and do what?"

"PLEASE DON'T ANSWER THAT."

"I think I'd be better off not knowing."

The words of the boys in the background were enough to have her in fits of laughter as she walks down the hall towards her own apartment. Jinhwan had turned in the camera's view to most likely look in annoyance to the boys, and she could only hear them snorting in laughter somewhere behind him.

Finally, he had turned back towards his phone to reply to her question. But Soomi wasn't looking down at him anymore.

"Soomi-ah?" he calls out her name in confusion.

But the girl seemed lost, in complete mortifying shock as she gawked at the sight. She felt beads of sweat forming on her forehead, and her palms grew moist as she stepped only a foot closer, leaning in to inspect what she was seeing. She wanted to believe that this was a figment of her imagination, that someone was playing a sick joke for Halloween.

However, Halloween was already over, and the note attached to the knife jammed into the front door of her apartment said it all too well that it wasn't a joke.

I guess the lesson wasn't learned the first time. Time to play, Soomi.


25:00 | old friends

"Nani, I have to go," she can barely utter these words. Jinhwan calls out to her in confusion, looking distressed from her sudden insistence, but she didn't even look down at him as she hurriedly ended the call.

Her entire body was trembling as she stood before the door. Why, she wondered in astonishment, why would they do this now? Everything had been going so right for her recently. She was finally letting go of Jungkook after learning about the truth, she was finally strong enough to move forward. It was absolutely horrifying that someone had made such threats about her, that they found a way into her own home, but Jungkook had said they were supposed to quit when he sent the letter, when they broke up for good. This is what gave her the closure she needed, she thought it was over. 

She'd thought that whatever crazy, inhumane person would make those sorts of threats, only did it for Jungkook. Surely, they didn't believe all the rumors going around online? There wasn't any real proof anymore of the two of them being together again because they weren't.

But the note stabbed into the wood of her front door seemed to say that this mentally inept person believed different. The thought that they must be reckless, more than she could imagine, mortified Soomi. So much that she felt her breathe going ragged as she pulled the knife out of the door, her hands shaking as the note fell with it.

"It's fine, it's fine," she tells herself as she takes her keys out. However, just when she's about to unlock her front door, more worries form in her brain. It was like an abrupt rainstorm as the clouds were everywhere inside of her, and she swallowed the huge lump in her throat. The anxiety was rising like a wildfire up her spine, and she realized that she was too afraid to enter her own apartment alone.

Not after hearing the stories Jungkook told her. If this person had gained access to her apartment before without her even knowing it, they could be in there right now - since they didn't seem too fond of her once again. 

Even though she had made it clear she wanted nothing to do with Jungkook, she was too freaked out to process anything. The guys wouldn't understand, they didn't know enough to realize how bad this was. 

There was only one person who would.

She didn't realize there were tears streaming down her cheeks until she felt the wet droplets against her hand as she held the phone to her ear. Sniffling, she uses the other to rub away the tears, her back sliding against the wall until she hit the ground in a seating position.

"Jungkook?" 

There was a small gasp heard, and she had a feeling that it wasn't the boy she was looking to hear. "Jungkook-ah, it's Soomi!" the yells of Jimin is heard, and she sniffled again, listening to the sound of shuffling through the speaker. Despite it, Jimin must've heard her clearing her nose, "wait - are you crying? Yah, what's wrong?"

Although Park Jimin was the definition of an angel, she didn't know how much he knew about the situation. Jungkook never exactly specified if he ever told any of the guys, and she didn't have the energy to explain it all if he didn't.

"Nothing, I just - I just need to talk to Jungkook, please," she breathes out, her words feeling heavy and weak as she leaned her head back against the wall.

"But Soomi-," Jimin's concerned words are heard further away when she presumed the phone had been taken from him. There's sounds of protest, as with him being the caring soul he was - even after so long of them being apart - he still cared for Soomi. It warmed her heart to hear him whining to Jungkook about wanting to comfort her. Jimin was her closest friend out of all the boys, back when she was with Jungkook. They were closer than she was with even Junhoe, practically inseparable. As she thought about all those times they had together, she realized she honestly missed him.

The sound of a worried Jungkook breaks her out of her troubled thoughts, "Hey, hey, what's wrong? Are you alright?"

Just hearing his voice brought back a lot. She actually had a lot to say to him, especially regarding what he pulled at Melon. But as she looked down to the knife and the note in her hand, something as silly as that was the least of her worries.

When she doesn't reply for a while, he keeps calling out to her. Soomi didn't exactly even know what to say, she didn't know if she could say it.

She was scared, and she guessed in a way, she just wanted to be comforted.

But why by Jungkook?

"Jungkook," her voice trails off, being barely audible through her weeps, "I'm so scared."

"Are you okay?" he didn't wait to ask, and she coughed a bit to try and calm her nerves.

Breathing in and out slowly as an attempt to steady her breathing pattern, she only mutters weakly, "No, I'm not okay."

"Are you at your apartment?" was his next question.

She only replies with a mhm, and there wasn't an immediate reply like the other two times. Instead, her ears are met with the rustling sound through the speaker, as if he was frantically moving around in a hurry.

The jingling of keys rang through the phone, and a sigh is sounded from the boy on the other line as he instructed, "Don't move, okay? I'm on my way."

Any other time, she would of easily objected. The risk was too high, the thought of something happening was too great - on any other day, Soomi would be smart enough to know she shouldn't be around Jungkook.

But right then, she was too scared. She felt like all hope in her world had been lost, with the realization someone was threatening her life again - and she had no idea what they were going to do. They could be in her own house as she sat there, trembling in fear, they could be watching her right now.

Maybe she should've called Jinhwan. Maybe she should've told him everything and he would've comforted her instead. But there was something inside her that made her dial Jungkook's number, and there was no turning back now.

Exhaling deeply, she could only whisper, "hurry, please."




+




"Yah, Soomi-ah, we're done with the song-writing!" Bobby had yelled as the boys walked into the room where Jinhwan had previously been FaceTiming her. Since he left the recording session to check up on her and had yet to come back, they all assumed he was still talking to her.

Which is why they're surprised when their hyung pouts as he looks up to them, informing in a dull tone, "I'm not on call with her anymore."

"That's unusual," Donghyuk states the obvious, falling onto the seat beside his hyung.

"Weird, I didn't think it was possible for you two to not speak for so long," Junhoe hums.

Yun had noticed his weird attitude though, and he immediately questioned Jinhwan, "is she alright, though?"

"I don't know," he mumbles, shifting in the leather booth and pulling his knees to his chest. Everyone's eyes fall on the eldest in confusion, wondering what could be wrong with Soomi since she was fine when they saw her earlier. "She was going back to her apartment, then it was like she saw a ghost," he explains, and their brows furrow.

The boys exchange glances, some of them completely clueless and some of them having a weird expression. Chan is the one who clears his throat, a nervous feeling arising inside of him as he voices his thoughts, "maybe it's Jungkook."

Jinhwan's glare was deadly as it was shot straight at the maknae. Chan puts his hands up in defense, shrugging helplessly, "I'm just saying, what else would it be?"

Annoyed from the fact he was putting more worries in all their heads, Yunhyeong slaps his arm, "don't say that, she would've kicked him to the curb if it was! I'm sure she's fine."

Even despite his reassuring tone, Jinhwan didn't feel any better. "But she's not replying to my texts," he shakes his head, too many scenarios popping into his brain and making him feel nauseous.

"We're almost done and then we can head back to the apartments," Hanbin chimes in, trying to soften the mood as he pats Jinhwan's shoulder comfortingly, "don't worry about it, hyung, like Yunhyeong said - she's fine."

Although nobody actually knew if this was true or not, they knew it would be bad news if Jinhwan got any more worried for her. They still had some recording to do for the new song and arranging, so it wasn't time to go back home yet. There was only a certain amount of time they had to complete their work, and there was none that could be wasted. 

It took a good while for them to convince Jinhwan was okay. Even then, he looked completely soulless as they all stood up to go back to the recording studio.

They all looked at each other, mentally asking what to do. Junhoe heaves out a sigh, grabbing everyone's attention as he pulls his phone out of his pocket. "Here, I'll spam FaceTime her, she'll have to pick up eventually," he says thoughtlessly. 

Jinhwan's eyes widen as he watches the tall boy rapidly pressing buttons. He walks forward, about to tell him not to, but it was too late when the ring sound of a FaceTime call was heard.

No one else could see the screen since Junhoe was so tall, and was keeping it directly in front of his face as if he was blind. The boys just stood around, pursing their lips and awaiting to see if she would answer. A nervous aura spread around the room, and no one knew what to think. Surely, their hyung was just over reacting. He was in love with her, it was natural for him to be concerned for her even too much. Soomi was fine.

But she wasn't answering. That was, until Junhoe was calling her for around the fifth time.

"June-ya, what is it?!" her voice came out stern, but you could tell she was distressed. At the sudden sound of her voice, Jinhwan had jumped forward like a hungry dog.

Junhoe had peered down at the phone screen, trying to see her clearly because there was practically no lighting wherever she was. "Jinhwan is worried about you, what happened?" he doesn't hesitate to ask, still trying to make out her surroundings.

Before she could answer, Jinhwan had called out to her from behind Junhoe. The sound of his voice caused a lump to form in her throat, and Junhoe knitted his brows as he watched a nervous expression spread over her face. That's not normal.

Jinhwan still couldn't see the screen, which was no doubt angering him because of how bad he missed her. "Uh, hi, Jinani! I'm okay, really, something just came up," Junhoe noticed the shakiness in her tone that she was so desperately trying to hide, covering it with a forced smile.

Although the camera was so dark, he could make out she looked rough. Her makeup seemed messy, like she just walked out in the rain - but it wasn't raining. This only meant a singular thing.

"Are you sure? You don't-," Junhoe cuts himself off when in the background, his eyes land on a certain something - a figure entering the room. 

Completely dumbstruck at first, he has to squint to make it out. He knew that figure.

She was with Jeon Jungkook.

Panic rose up his spine, and he had absolutely no idea what was going on. Either way, one thing he did know was that the reason she must've been so nervous is because he was there with her. If Jinhwan knew, he'd go mental and there was no telling what would happen.

Everyone was aware of the fact they weren't dating yet. Junhoe wasn't exactly an angel, so he didn't see it as completely wrong if she wanted to see someone else - but then again it was his beloved hyung. 

However, Soomi was his best friend. If there was something going on there, it was between her and Jinhwan. The last thing he wanted was to be in the middle of one of their wars.

"Uh, alright, see you later then, Soomi!" he called out in a rush, before abruptly ending the call. Just as Jinhwan was about to see the screen.

A breath of relief left his lips, and the others are staring at him weirdly, noticing his act. Jinhwan, on the other hand, was irritated he hadn't even let him speak to him. 

"Sorry, hyung, she's tired," is all he says, before leaving towards the recording room to avoid any further conversation. 

Rubbing his temple, he could only imagine what could be going on. Surely, she wouldn't be talking to him if it wasn't for a good reason. Thinking back to the story she had told him, maybe there was one. 

Just be careful, Soomi.



26:00 | desirable lust

"I'm sorry, this is all my fault - I kept bothering you at Melon and now look what's happened because I'm so inconsiderate, I'm so sorry," his sympathetic words softened her heart, and Soomi's eyes beamed up towards the boy sitting next to her on the mattress. Jungkook's legs were crossed, his chin resting in the palm of his hand as he stared blankly ahead, looking dull and empty inside. She hadn't seen him so torn in a long time, and it was enough to urge her to reach a hand out, gently touching his forearm.

The sudden contact caused him to look to her, his delicate doe eyes gazing straight into her heart when he did so. Jungkook had always had a way of making her swoon, even after everything. Soomi never really got rid of her feelings for him, how could she? He was everywhere all the time for the last year with the intense growth of BTS' popularity around the world. 

Which was why she was having a hard time thinking straight with the way he kept biting his bottom lip, pulling it in between his teeth. His tongue would swipe across it when he'd release his grip with his teeth, and the sensual action was enough to make her feel some sort of way. 

Shifting in her seating position, she breaks their heavy eye contact to mumble sincerely, "it's okay, Jungkook, I'm not really going to blame you for some lunatic being obsessed with you."

A chuckle leaves his lips, and he deeply exhales, seeming to be thinking hard. "I didn't think this would happen, I mean, we aren't even together anymore," his voice grows quieter when he says those last words. It was as if it hurt him to say them, like he wanted them to be wrong.

Clearing her throat, she couldn't help but feel a bit awkward, "I know."

"I won't let anything happen to you though, Soomi-ah," he says with a reassuring grin, that same one she always adored, "as long as you trust me, I'll always protect you, just don't push me away." 

When she turns to look at him again, he was already staring straight at her. His eyes were glistening from the street lights peeking through the curtains, and she got so lost in them that it took her a moment to realize his face was inching closer to hers. 

His lips, she noted as her eyes flicker to them, I wonder how they taste now.

Mentally hitting herself, she knew she should stop whatever was happening. "I trust you, Kookie," she used the old nickname she'd given him all that time ago, "I'm glad you're here," she forces a smile, trying to hide the effect he still had on her.

She didn't want him to know how badly he was playing with her emotions. Soomi wasn't sure if anything that was going on was okay. I love Jinhwan, she reminded herself sternly, and of course she did. Jinhwan was the angel that came from the heavens, saving her from all the darkness she once subsided after in the scandal.

But when her brain put it that way, it meant Jungkook was the demon sent from down below. He was devious, mischievous, but his heart was sincere. His words were genuine, and his actions were for the best despite their appearances. He was the boy who broke her heart in two, left her alone, but yet still had that same affect on her. 

And now that she knew why it all happened, why he did what he did. It wasn't helping any of it.

She still had feelings for him.

"Soomi-ah~," he sing-songs her name, and when she looks back to his eyes that are so close to her own, he raises a brow inquisitively, "how close are you to Kim Jinhwan?"

His unexpected question startled her, and she swore she nearly fell sideways off the bed. He only peered back at her, expression completely serious, awaiting an answer. Although she had just been thinking about that, she wasn't sure how to answer. 

"How close am I?" she repeats his question, not moving an inch from their proximity. Once again, her eyes fall on those lips of his, and she could feel her body aching from the way he bit them.

When he speaks next, his voice was so raspy she could melt right under him, his eyes daring as he leaned in even closer, "I mean, would it be okay for me to kiss you right now?"

With his seductive tone, it was like all her strength had disintegrated. Not a breathe leaves her lips as she reaches forward, her hand grabbing the nape of his neck to push him forward. Jungkook's lips met hers in a blissful moment, immediately kissing her with more desire than she'd ever felt from a man in a long time.


[SLIGHT(just slight) smut warning, skip if uncomfortable or w/e]



He kissed her hungrily, lustfully, but most importantly of all - longingly. It was like this was something he had been waiting to do for months, like everything he'd ever done had led up to such a moment.

Although she'd kissed him more times than she could count, nothing compared to this kiss.

A yelp leaves her lips as he pushes her back against the mattress. His toned body was hovering over her own only seconds later. Holding himself up with one arm, he dips down to connect their lips again. She kissed him back just as desperately, her hands going around his neck, fingers tangling in his hair.

When his lips move to her neck, he seemed to remember exactly where her sweet spot was. It was a place she hadn't been kissed by anyone other than Jinhwan in a long time, and the feeling of his tongue swiping over the skin was enough for her to tug at his locks, biting her lip to suppress any noise.

You'd think that when she received a note from a killer threatening her relationship with a certain boy (that she no longer even had one with,) she wouldn't proceed to do things like this with him. There was no telling who this person was, where they were, or what they had on the two of them. She had no idea what lit the flame inside of her and made her suddenly so eager to taste him, but it was one she couldn't see to burn out as she lifted her hips to hit his. Although he was still leaving marks on her neck, she could feel the hardness of his member against her front as she bucked her hips upward against him again, he'd groaned.

"Soomi," he growled from above her, "you're really testing me."

A smirk only spread across her lips, and she momentarily seemed to forget about everything else in the world as he pulled away from kissing her neck to peer down at her, raising a brow in question to the expression on her face.

"Give me my punishment, then."



[end, continue freely]



+




What did I just do?

She found herself cursing at least a trillion times. Soomi's heartbeat is irregularly quick as she hears the sound echoed through the house, the nerve that it caused to spiral through her body was enough to make her nauseous. It was so much she nearly puked as she scurried out of the bed, everything that she had just partook in her rushing back to her brain rapidly. 

An ache shoots through her thighs, catching her in surprise. The pain had been so extreme she hadn't expected it, and a squeal left her lips as she accidentally tripped over a piece of clothing while looking for something to throw over her own bare body.

A rustling sound is heard from the bed she just climbed out of, followed by a yawn. Only seconds later, the lamp beside her bed was flicked on, and a tired Jungkook looked at where she was sat on the carpet helplessly in confusion, "Soomi, are you okay?"

Although she wanted to say yes, it would be a complete lie. Before she could even make up her mind, though, the same ding sound echoed through the apartment once again. This time, however, Jungkook heard it, but he didn't look as freaked out as she did.

Fuck, what if it was Jinhwan? she felt herself malfunctioning as she whines out to the boy who had yet to move a muscle, "Yah, help me up!"

Jungkook chuckles as he rises from the bed, walking around to grab her hands that she was holding out to him. "Sorry, I didn't mean to hurt you, I just wanted you to know how much I missed you," he winked to her as she was finally back on her feet. The words coming out of his mouth along with that sleepy deep tone of his was enough for her cheeks to grow rosy, and she turns her back to him in a rush so he didn't notice.

Grabbing her clothes, she pulled them over her body in a rush. Once she's dressed, the doorbell rung once again, and she was reminded of the horrible situation she was in.

"Hide," she instructs the boy standing in her bedroom. Jungkook, who was only in his boxers, stared at her completely clueless. 

A cocky smile forms on his lips as he raises a brow playfully, crossing his arms over his chest, "why? You don't want him to see me?"

Giving him an unimpressed look, she rushed over to him, pulling his arm and attempting to drag him towards the bathroom. But Jungkook was strong, and he didn't seem to have any intention of moving until she answered him.

Heaving out a sigh, she throws her hands in the air in exasperation, sassily snapping, "No, I would really prefer if he didn't."

Rolling his eyes, Jungkook still didn't seem interested in moving as he reaches out to pull her towards him instead. Her eyes bulge out of their sockets as she hits his chest, the warmth of his uncovered skin surging a spark through her body. 

"But he's not your boyfriend," he states, running his fingers through her messy hair.

She could only exhale impatiently, "I know."

But Jungkook still didn't seem to get it, "I don't see the big deal then," he pauses for a second, before pulling away from her so he can make eye contact. "Do you love him? I know I said you did back at the rehearsals, but - I didn't think you really did."

Her eyes find their way to meet his. The teasing was over as all humor seemed to drain from Jungkook's expression, instead being replaced with complete sincerity and sadness. He looked torn from the thought she loved someone else.

Pursing her lips for a moment, she shrugs, "I-I don't know, Jungkook."

If she was saying this to make him feel better or because it was true, she couldn't answer.

"Well," he sighs, running a hand through his hair and breaking their eye contact to nervously mutter the question, "what about me?"

The doorbell sounds again.

"Jungkook..." her voice trails off, and she felt her heart grow heavy when he looked to her again. Never had she seen him so shattered.

"I'll always love you, you know," although it was barely audible, she knew he heard her from the way a huge grin formed on his face.

"Wah~," he squeals like a child, rushing forward to kiss her forehead, "so I have a chance." She watched as he jumped around like a lunatic, before walking into her bathroom, humming, "I'll hide now, go answer the door."

Completely dumbfounded and awestruck from his sudden change, she gawked at him in disbelief. Nothing could seem to make sense of how he was, and it was making her head hurt as she watched him give her that goofy smile before shutting and locking the door.

Shaking her head, she was about to say something else, when the door was rung again. Realizing that if it was Jinhwan, he was probably rather worried right now. With a sigh, she spins on her heel, rushing out of the room and ignoring the pain in between her legs.

Only moments later, she was standing in front of her door. Her mind replays the incident earlier, that note. Thinking back to the conversation she and Jungkook just had, she wondered how serious this person was. How far would they really go? If she wanted to, could she even be with Jungkook in the first place? 

Pushing the thoughts out of her head, she unlocks the door, heaving out a deep sigh to prepare herself to face Jinhwan. After all, who else would be at her door at this hour? She hadn't really spoken to him since the call earlier, and the boys were probably working late at the studio and just got back. 

Act normal, she tells herself, everything is fine. 

But why did a part of her feel guilty?

"SOOMI!" 

She was used to hearing a certain group of boys scream her name all the time, practically everyday. It was perks of being friends with mental patients, they yelled a lot.

"WE MISSED YOU!"

But these weren't the boys of iKON, and she wasn't met with Jinhwan's worried face. Instead, she was paraded by six boys, who attacked her with giant hugs all at once. A squeal of surprise leaves her lips as she didn't even have a second to process what was going on or who was in front of her when they were all pulling her into a huge group hug.

"It's been too long!" they cry in excitement, everyone finally pulling away after noticing she had yet to utter a word.

At the sight of the six of them, she could only gawk. They were all really here, she didn't even think they remembered her apartment number, or cared about her at all. But sure enough, the six boys of Bangtan Sonyeondan were standing in the living room of her home.













27:00 | tension

"You guys," her voice seems to get stuck as she kept making noises of disbelief, holding a hand to her head, "holy fuck."

"Guys, I think she's going to pass out," a concerned Hoseok whispers.

It takes longer than she expected to come to her senses. All of her old friends were really there, standing right before her, with bright grins on their faces. Out of all the people that could have been on the other side of her front door, ranging from the crazy murderer or Jinhwan and the boys - she would've never guessed it to be them. 

Although she had seen them at the award show, it had never really sunk in that they had missed her. The last year, honestly, whenever they ran into each other at events, she would look the other way. Soomi always figured the boys did the same, she never thought someone like her actually had any sort of impact in their lives. They were some of the biggest stars on the planet now, it blew her mind that they still deeply cared for her after all this time.

Because of what Jungkook had done back then, and how she had never understood fully why, she was brought to think he used her. The boys told each other mostly everything, and she felt like they must've known. This brought her to hate them, she thought they betrayed her as her supposive close friends.

Only until now, had she fully realized that hadn't been the case for them at all - which was why tears of joy well in her eyes as she jumps forward to hug them all one by one, starting from Hoseok. "Hobi-ah!" she squeaks like a kid, hugging the living out of the boy who was shocked at first from the unexpected action, before that sunshine grin formed on his face and he hugged her back just as tight.

"Soo~," he sing songs out equally as childish, giggling from the intensity of their embrace, "I'm glad you didn't just pass out!"

Nearly breathless as she pulls away, she playfully hits him before hugging the rest of the boys. All of them had told her how long it'd been and how much they missed her. They said they'd always check up on her media and listen to her new songs, it warmed her heart to hear how much they missed her.

"Jungkook told us only parts of why he did it," Namjoon was explaining after they pulled away from their own hug. Talk of the scandal had rose among them, and she was learning that they hadn't known the entire story after all as he finished, "we never really understood - it didn't make any sense - all we knew was that he was getting blackmailed."

Pursing her lips, she curtly nods, "Yeah, it's really messed up, I just wish people would mind their own business." They all make sounds of agreement, and she lets out a shaky sigh as she turns to the last person, her mind wandering around on the fact that person was after her again. She didn't even have any idea of what they meant by play, how she ‘hadn't learned her lesson the first time,' it was all devious and nauseating. How could someone be like this?

Finally, her eyes jolt up to the last person she had yet to embrace due to the fact she got lost in her own mind once again. Park Jimin peered down at her expectantly, his irises twinkling under the moonlight that was peeking in from the windows in the living room. When they make eye contact, his lips part in that angelic smile he was always known for. The corner of Soomi's lips twitch upward into a grin as she shakes her head, unable to hold it in anymore.

"Chim, I missed you," her voice is barely audible as she steps forward into her old best friend's arms. The closest she had ever been with anyone, her first true friend, was right before her - she was finally hugging him again after so long. 

Such a thing she had previously done so often felt almost immediately normal as she laid her head against his chest, breathing in the same stupid cologne he still wore to this day. Jimin's chin was atop her head as he held her there, muttering so only she could hear, "you have no idea how much I missed you."

The other boys had been watching the reunion, gushing at the sight. "What a sweet moment," Jin had shook his head in admiration.

"Yah, Soomi, do you still buy those ice cream desserts?!" Taehyung had gasped in sudden realization as her and Jimin's hug was ending. 

Pulling away, Jimin's arm stays around her shoulders as she turns to the smiley boy known as Taehyung. He was looking at her with hopeful eyes, and she chuckles as she nods, "I sure do, Tae."

He pumps his fists in the air out of victory, before bolting towards the kitchen like a track runner. Everyone was watching him, shaking their heads at the old habit the boy never broke, but smiling because - already - it felt like those old times. 

"Wait!" Hoseok had called after a minute, following after him into her kitchen, "I want one!"

"Children," Yoongi mutters as the two are in the kitchen, squealing over the treats she had in her freezer. 

Giggling at the sound of them fighting over the last chocolate one are heard through the entire apartment, she looks to Yoongi, stepping closer to him so she could grab his wrist. The boy is confused when she pulls him into another group hug with Jimin, her arms around both of their necks as she squeezes the two of them to near death.

Jimin is laughing, but Yoongi wasn't too amused himself, "Yah, yah - jinja, is this really necessary?" 

Pulling away, she pokes Yoongi's nose with her pointer finger, "Yes, I know you missed me, Yoongles."

The moment her finger falls from his nose, he wrinkles it. Wincing in fake disgust, he holds a hand to his head as he sighs, "one thing I didn't miss was that treacherous nickname."

Before she could start pestering him even more like she used to, just to earn a reaction, Jin had clapped his hands together as he hums, "So, Soomi-ah, have you eaten?"

Her eyes widen a bit at his question, seeing him already walking towards the kitchen. She knew exactly why he was asking. Jin was the cook of the boys, much like Yunhyeong of iKON. In the old days, he was always the one who cooked the meals, practically making her kitchen his own. Any time they'd come over, he'd use whatever ingredients she had to make a practical feast. 

Being he was asking such, she had a feeling that was what he was about to do. Even though she missed them all, Jin's cooking, and their movie marathons, she didn't want him to have to stress himself over it the first day they were all together again. 

She holds up a hand as a signal to stop him, as he was staring at her expectantly for an answer. However, before she could utter the first word, someone clears their throat from the hall, and everyone's eyes jerk to them in surprise.

Well, it wasn't the fact Jungkook was there that shocked the boys - after all, it was part of the reason they even came to the apartment in the first place. It was the fact he was standing there shirtless, his jeans pulled up just above his hips, his hair a mess atop his head. Their eyes nearly fell out of their sockets at the sight of him that suggested a certain something no one had expected, and it only got worse when he crosses his arms over his chest to cockily state, "Yeah, hyung, she already ate."

Knowing what he was saying particularly, Soomi coughs on her own saliva, having to turn away from everyone to bury her face in her hands. Why did he do these things? She asked herself mentally as she could feel her cheeks burning under her palms.

"Holy fucking shit," Yoongi snorts, "I see you two didn't waste any time."

She pulls her hands away from her face to hit his arm, "can you please not?!"

He only giggles, shrugging his shoulders before walking to fall onto one of the sofas. 

The others seemed to be in some sort of state of shock, their brows knitted together as they thought deeply. She didn't want to know what was on their minds as she looks to Jungkook, narrowing her eyes to show him she wasn't pleased with his actions. But the boy only grins innocently to her, winking.

I swear, she heaved out a sigh, feeling awkward under the current circumstances. 

"Well," Namjoon clears his throat, "We're going to pick a movie." He hurriedly grabs Jin's arm, dragging him away to the couch next to Yoongi.

Soomi watched them leave, pursing her lips as she finally diverts her attention back to Jimin and Jungkook. Only then, she noticed they weren't paying any to her. Jungkook's eyes were tightly locked on the boy that still stood beside her, and as she glanced to Jimin, his fists were clenched at his sides. There was a tint of red on his cheeks, and his brows were creased downward as if he was annoyed with something as he squinted those sparkly brown eyes of his at the maknae.

There was an undeniably cocky smirk on Jungkook's lips, and it only seemed to grow when he stood up straight from leaning against the wall. His feet click against the wooden floor, and only seconds later he was standing in front of Soomi. 

Just when she looks up to him, he grabbed her hand, pulling her forward into his chest. Before her slow thoughts could even gather what was happening, he'd lifted her chin with his finger to leave a lingering kiss upon her lips. 

She hadn't kissed him back from shock, and he pulled away as if it was the most normal thing he'd ever done. Meanwhile, Jimin looked like he was about to be sick as he shook his head, not sparing another glance to her as he spun on his heel to go sit with the others.

Watching the two leave, still glaring into each other's souls, she could only blink in confusion. It'd only been around an hour and a half since they got back, and there was already something fishy going on. 

Holding a hand to her head, she tried to think of what this could possibly be about.

"Soomi-ah~, come watch Avengers with us!" Taehyung is calling as he's running out of the kitchen with not one but multiple ice creams, jumping around like the most energetic human on the face of the planet. 

Hoseok was trailing behind him, only slightly less energetic as he calls out in astonishment, "yah, he ate like all of the ice creams!"

The movie was already starting, and the girl tries to erase everything that could stress her out even slightly from her mind as she walks over to flick off the lights and make sure the door was locked. An unimpressed sigh was heard from the couch, followed by the blatant words of Yoongi, "Hoseok, that's called being fat."

"Hyung, I'm not fat!" 

"Tell that to your stomach rolls."

"Wha - I don't have any," you could tell there was a pout on Taehyung's lips as he scoffed, "whatever, at least these taste good."

She was about to go join them when her phone vibrates in her back pocket. Walking into the kitchen so her texting didn't disturb the others, she pulls it out, turning on the screen to read the message.

Hanbin:
You and Jinhwan's song was approved!! CEO-nim loved it!

Soomi:
NO WAY

Hanbin:
No joke :) Congratulations, it turned out great!

Soomi:
Thank you, Binnie, I can't believe this :D

Soomi:
What about the one you guys are working on? How's it going?

Hanbin:
We're still at the studio. Song writing is done, producing in progress, recording vocals and rap now. After tonite probably just gonna need you to record your part and it's done!

Soomi:
That was fast

Hanbin:
credit goes to my godly writing skills

Hanbin:
jk but you're gonna love your part

Soomi:
I'm intrigued ???

Soomi:
How's Jinhwan? I haven't talked to him in a while

Hanbin:
He's fine, missing you and won't shut about it but fine

Hanbin:
Which reminds me, what was that call about earlier? Why did Junhoe shit himself so suddenly?

Soomi:
because he's Junhoe

Hanbin:
Soomi...

Soomi:
Hanbin, it's nothing I'd like to talk about now, okay?

Hanbin:
Alright, fine, but I'm not letting this go.

Hanbin:
We'll be back in a couple hours.












28:00 | rooftop secrets

"Yah, Chim," she calls out his name through the wind, reaching out a hand to touch his shoulder to grab his attention fully. Under the darkening sky at the night grew closer, his eyes find a way to meet hers. Throughout all her naivety, she grins up at him, her green irises glistening under the small amount of sunlight that was peeking through the clouds, "thank you, this was such a great day."

As she says this, she stands and grins up at him for a minute. When he doesn't say anything immediately, her eyes trail away, and her small body turns towards the view they were surrounded by. In awe, they rake over every detail of New York City, taking in everything that was truly before her after so long. 

This was her home, she thought, completely astonished. The city didn't look the same in her eyes as it once had. It was full of so many memories, so many terrible occurrences, and all of her stupid decisions she would never get over. But yet, as her eyes shine at all the city lights that glow in so many different colors, she saw hope in such a city, a city she was born it.

"It was the greatest day," her best friend's words finally cut through the wind. It blew so angrily every couple of minutes, as if it was screaming at someone, at them. What it was trying to say? She'd never know.

Her eyes merely glance up to the boy who was now standing beside her, resting his hands on the railing around the rooftop of the building they stood upon. Jimin's eyes watch over the entire city they were surrounded by, until they find a way to look back to her. 

They make eye contact, but it felt different when his brown eyes peer into hers. It was like there was an emotion within them she had never seen before, or was just noticing. Either way, she didn't know what to make of it. 

"We have to meet Jungkook soon, right?" she asks blankly, breaking their eye contact out of anxiousness. There were many feelings inside of her right then, so many that she couldn't sort out. She didn't know who they were exactly for, but like always, she presumed they were all meant for her boyfriend. After all, she had a feeling he was planning something big. All the guys had refused to utter even a word about what it was, and she'd been freaking out about it for days. Jimin had offered to take her out on a fun night in her home city, since they had to leave and head back for Korea tomorrow. 

However, whatever Jungkook was planning was meant to be soon, on that exact night. The poor girl didn't know what to expect. Everything had been so perfect, she didn't think it could get any better than it was.

A sigh leaves Jimin's lips, but it sounded dull. He didn't seem to thrilled to bring her back to her boyfriend, and he wasn't. Jimin, and the rest of the boys, knew what he was doing, what he was planning for her. He knew how happy she was going to be, and as her best friend, he didn't want to get in the way of something as precious as that.

But he had to face it, he was jealous.

No matter how jealous he got, though, he couldn't do anything about it. And that fact angered Jimin more than words could explain. He hated living this way, although it was nice to see his best friend so happy, he wished it was with him instead.

Pursing his lips as he tries to force those bitter thoughts out of his brain, he only mutters, "Yeah, we do."

The girl beside him let out a deep breath, trying to take a mental picture of everything around her. Such a blissful moment, she wished she could live in it forever as she leaned her head onto her best friend's shoulder, still watching the world around them from that rooftop.

"Chim," she hums out his name, and he only utters what, so she went on to slowly voice out her wonders, "do you think my family's out there, that they still wonder about me?"

He had been glancing down to her as she was still laying on his shoulder. Hearing her ask this, his eyes wander to the city they were in, running across every building, every road. Thinking back to the stories of her family here, how she came to Korea, he frowned as he exhaled, "of course they are, and I know they think about you all the time."

She only makes a hmph sound in reply, and he pouts as he glances down to her gloomy expression, "I told you we could go visit them before we leave."

Sighing, she lifts her head to stand up straight, shaking her head, "I know, I know, I just - I don't think I'm ready to see them," she admits in a small voice, turning away.

He nods in understanding as he walks to where she was, feet away from the railing now, "I get it, it's okay," he reassures her with a grin, "but whenever you are, they'll be happy to see you," he informs her sternly, before adding, "and I'll be right here with you."

"Thank you," is all she can find to say to him. 

Words could never add up to how much she adored her best friend. If she hadn't ran into him that day, if she hadn't ever met him, she wouldn't even be where she was standing right now. 

Jimin only waves a hand in dismissal, turning away from her to pull out his cell phone. She watched curiously as he ignored the texts that were on his screen to slide it over to the camera app, switching it to the front view. 

"Smile, so we can remember this moment forever," he instructs as he holds up the phone in front of both of them.

Giddily, she tried to push her hair out of her face as she prepared to take the photo. With glistening eyes, she peered up at the camera that he was holding, and then, even without the photo, she knew she'd remember that moment forever.


"Alright," he sighs as he exits out of the app, a small grin on his face. She didn't notice him making the certain photo his wallpaper as he checks all the texts he had from the boys, trying to hide his distaste as he replied to them before sliding it back into his jeans pocket, "guess we have to meet your boyfriend now."

Soomi only nods, turning to look at the view one last time. She didn't know then when she would be back here, and she squinted her eyes to peer out in the direction her family lived. To think they were only around half an hour away, just a short drive. But she wasn't ready to see them, she still felt like they weren't happy with her. As far as she knew, they forgot she even existed.

Thinking back to her best friend's reassuring words, she abruptly spins on her heel and steps forward without looking. A squeak leaves her lips when she runs into Jimin, who had been casually waiting for her. 

His eyes widen in shock when he looks down to her, only to see she was already looking up at him. Their eyes meet for a while, faces only centimeters apart. A lump forms in his throat as he tried to fight the urge that was desperately eating his insides.

What surprised him was the state of shock Soomi seemed to be in as she opened her mouth and then closed it again, not seeming to know what words to say or if she should say anything. Her eyes look in every which way that isn't at him, and she bit her lip, not understanding why this sudden proximity was suddenly so awkward between them.

Jimin, on the other hand, knew exactly why. The tension he was feeling between them was almost over bearing, and he didn't know what to do about it.

After this night, she was going to be engaged to Jungkook. They were going to get married. He'd never get that chance he wished for all this time.

"Jimin," she breathes out, suddenly lost for words as his face was inching closer to her own.

But before she could say anything else, there was a slight push against the nape of her neck. Her eyes bulge out of surprise when she sees his eyes flutter shut, feeling the softest lips against her own. 

He kissed her, and the tingling feeling of his pillow lips remained on her own as he pulled away after a second. Jimin's eyes were widened themselves as he seemed to process what he just did, stuttering out in a nervous mess, "S-Shit, Soomi, I'm sorry, I wasn't thinking-," she only looked at him, thinking about everything that just happened.

A part of her wasn't thinking either, to tell the truth.

"I know that was wrong of me, I'm sorry," he kept ranting on, apologizing more times than she could manage to count.

Just when he opens his mouth to speak again, she steps forward to enclose the space between them again. Cupping his cheeks, she abruptly kisses him again, and he didn't waste any time to return her efforts. Their lips moved in a melody with each other, shooting a euphoric spark through the veins and pumping off of their adrenaline.

He kissed her like he was running out of air and she was his oxygen source. As if everything fell into place, her hands go behind him to tangle in his soft hair, and he bit her bottom lip through the kiss, gaining entrance to her mouth. 

Never did she think her best friend was such a good kisser.

And never did she think she'd be kissing him in the first place.

"Fuck," she curses loudly as they pull away, her hand going to cover her mouth as she realizes what she just did.

Jimin tried not to smirk, partly being satisfied and feeling like at least he had to live with. But the part of him that cared for her as his best friend felt horrible. Running a hand through his hair, he tries to calm her, "Soomi, Soomi, calm down, it's okay."

"It's not okay!" she squeaks, her hands on the sides of her head, fingers tangling in her brown locks as she paces around the rooftop frantically, "I'm a horrible person!"

Sighing, Jimin reaches out to grab her wrist, stopping her from walking around like a crazy person. Reluctantly, she meets his stern eyes with a pout. 

"Stop, this is between us, okay? I won't tell anyone," he assures her, "you love Jungkook, and that's all that matters right now."

There was more meaning to his words than Soomi was aware of. Due to the stressed thoughts in her mind though, she hadn't noticed it as she nods hesitantly, agreeing, "okay, you're right. I love Jungkook," she raises her voice confidently, forcing a grin on her lips.

Soomi turns way to walk towards the exit since it was time to go meet him wherever he may be. Her best friend only watched her go as she took a piece of him with her, thinking back to those three words she just said that broke his heart in two.

She loves Jungkook, not you.





29:00 | clingy

Despite the weird tension between the two boys, Soomi hadn't expected the night to turn out in the way it did. Truthfully, even if she would have known beforehand, she still wouldn't of been prepared, especially not for the brat that Jeon Jungkook was.

"Soomi, sit here~!" Jimin had called out in a hopeful tone, waving his hands above his head to her. She'd just walked out of the kitchen after talking to Jinhwan and making sure he was alright - and also Hanbin, the boys were excited for the new songs they had coming out together, and she would be too if she wasn't worried about the seven boys she had in her living room. 

The movie was already well over started, and everyone was watching intensely. Jin had informed everyone he ordered pizza and take out for them to eat during the night, much to her thanks since she was starving. 

There were only two sofas in her living room, so some of the boys were sitting on the floor atop a heap of blankets and pillows. Since the only empty seat on a sofa was next to Jimin, she quietly made her way towards him. This required her stepping over Taehyung and Hoseok that were on the floor, carefully trying not to trip over anyone's feet.

It was when she walked past Jungkook, not paying much attention to him since she just wanted to get to her desired spot. A sudden tug is felt on her wrist, and she squeaks a mere sound out like a mice as she's delve backwards, her life flashing before her until she felt the feeling of not a cushion of her sofa, but Jungkook's thighs under her.

Gawking at what had just happened, it took her a moment to process. She was sitting in his lap. 

In exasperation, she turns to glare at him in frustration, "what's wrong with you?!" she's whining out as he wraps his arms protectively around her waist, pulling her as far against his body as physically possible. The thought of what she was sitting on was enough to make her nerves shoot through out her body, and she shifts around in his lap as an attempt to get him to let go.

However, Jungkook had no intention of doing so as he only set his chin on her shoulder. That devious grin was on his lips as he kissed her cheek, giggling, "Yah, stop moving so much."

Heaving out a sigh, she halts her movements in defeat. Sending an apologetic look to Jimin, who had been looking like he was ready to murder Jungkook on the spot, only until the girl met his eyes, he changed his expression in an instant. Returning her smile, he hoped she didn't notice how forced his own was, as he mouths the words it's okay.

A part of her still felt bad, though. It'd been too long, and she had forgotten the issue that used to be so common between the two boys.


Back then, it was hard for her to ever spend much time with the both of them equally. Jimin being her best friend, he was always so understanding whenever she had to cancel their plans for Jungkook. The truth was, her boyfriend was always so needy, but she'd always loved him for that. He wasn't controlling, as he never minded when she was around Jimin - at least not back then - he just wanted to be around her. She'd always found it cute.

But now, they weren't together, and this wasn't back then. She wasn't too amused with his actions, and she pouted in defeat as she leaned back against his chest after a moment, knowing there wasn't anyway to get out of this.

When Jungkook was distracted by the movie, she'd sneakily pulled out her phone.

Soo:
Sorry, Chim, I really was gonna sit with you

Clicking send, she watched Jimin in the corner of his eye as he felt his phone vibrate in his pocket. The boy had slowly pulled it out, checking the message. Soomi could feel his eyes on her for a split moment, before he diverted them to type something on his own screen.

Chim:
It's okay, I didn't think Jungkook was
still THAT obsessed with you

Chim:
But it seems like you guys are on pretty
good terms already. Is everything sorted out
with you guys?

Soo:
I guess so. He never really told you guys the full story but,
 it's a lot more serious than I ever thought. He
 was just trying to protect me, and I know that now.

Chim:
Well, what exactly is the full story?

Soo:
