He was blackmailed like he told you guys, but whoever
 it was wasn't playing around. They made serious threats
 like that they'd hurt me and do all these bad things

Soo:
They even said they'd kill me

Soo:
And sent him these pictures of them
in my apartment and stuff

Chim:
What the hell?

Soo:
I know..

Chim:
Soo, I'm so sorry

Chim:
That's not fucking right.. who the fuck would do that?

Soo:
I don't know. But it's ok. I get it all now, even though
 it really hurt back then. The past is the past.

Chim:
Yeah, you're right! :)

Chim:
But Soomi?

Soo:
Yeah?

Chim:
Do you still love him?

Chim:
I heard Jungkook saying something
about you being close with some other
members of a group, I can't remember which
 one though.

Soo:
I think a part of me always will, Jungkook was the
 love of my life for a long time. Now he's back, and well,
 I don't think that feeling I had for him ever really left.

Soo:
iKON. They're under the same company as me
so we see each other a lot and we're all good friends.
 I'm actually making a song with them.

Chim:
Well, I see the logic in the entire situation.
 If anything happens again now that you're
around us again, you should go to the police, Soomi.
I don't think this is something to play around with.

Chim:
Jungkook's really missed you a
lot the last year, but I have too. So
has everyone else. I think if you're going
to be with him again you should really make
sure those feelings you have are still there. I know
you're friends with those guys but, he said something
about you being a bit more with one of them, is
that true?

Soo:
Yeah. Yeah it's true.

Soo:
We're pretty serious, I think. I love him, he's really great.
 But then there's Jungkook, too.

Chim:
Soo, you're gonna have to make a decision

Chim:
I know Jungkook might have other ideas
because he's Jungkook but

Soo:
I know, no worries.. I'll figure it out

Chim:
Okay, Soo, just tell me if anything happens.
I've always been here for you and I always will be,
you're still my best friend :))

Smiling down at the message, she exhaled deeply after turning off her phone. There was a lot she had to think about regarding those two boys and her feelings. Never did she expect to get caught in this situation, yet here she was. In love with two people at the same time.

Where do I go from here? she asks herself, her eyes raking over the boys that sat in her living room. Her old friends, that she grew so accustomed to for years, spending every waking moment with them. She practically grew up with the boys, they taught her so many things and helped her through everything. They were the people who saved her when she came to Korea, a complete lost soul, feeling neglected and lost. 

But after the scandal, she felt they no longer wished to see her anymore. Soomi felt like she did something horribly wrong she just didn't know what, and she never spoke to any of them because she was too busy trying to figure out what it was. Her mind had deemed they must all hate her, but she never actually tried to confront them about it. And when they'd try to talk to her, she'd immediately ignore them or run away like she was mental. 

Maybe she could've figured this out earlier, way before it got to this point, if she would've just listened to them when they tried to talk to her.

Now there was someone after her, all for the relationship she had with Jungkook that wasn't technically in existence anymore.

Then she had Jinhwan. Her beloved Jinhwan, the angel that seemed to come from the heavens when she was in her worst state. Just being dumped after being engaged, she was on the verge of insanity when she met him. But somehow, through it all, he saw good in her, he helped her through it all and picked her up from the dirt. He was her stress reliever, and he let her fall on him whenever she needed a breather. 

Jinhwan and the rest of the boys had always been there for her. They never treated her like an attention seeker like the media deemed her to be because of the scandal, or mentioned any of those things. Never did they make her feel like she didn't belong. 

They made her feel free, like they were just normal, idiotic kids who could do whatever they wanted to. Although she had only known them for a year, she felt as accustomed to them as she did her old friends.

All the bonds that had formed, she couldn't decide which one she cared about more. Could they both exist at the same time? Soomi wasn't a two timer, but was she technically doing that? She wasn't dating either of them, after all.

The lights being turned on had shocked her so much she nearly jumped a foot in the air. Beaming into her eyes, she blinked many times, covering them with her hands as they seemed to scream at her from the ceiling. 

Looking to the TV, she realized then the movie had ended, and she hadn't paid the slightest amount of attention to any of it. 

"I'm starving, when is the food gonna get here?" Taehyung is whining out as he rises from the heap of blankets, stretching his arms out.

"Seriously, they must not know how to drive," Jin is shaking his head as he muttered bitterly, walking back over to fall onto the couch again next to Namjoon.

Soomi once again, tuned out the small talk and bickering that was going on in the room. After being around them for years, she was more than used to it even though she hadn't seen them in so long. 

The feeling of someone playing with her hair caused her to glance behind her, remembering she was still on Jungkook's lap. He rose a brow at her when he noticed he'd grabbed her attention, still running his fingers through the brunette locks as he hummed, "Are you okay?"

She only nods, breaking their short moment of eye contact. Although she hadn't noticed, he pouts when she does so. Jungkook was getting a feeling he'd done something wrong. Maybe he was being too much by trying to show off to Jimin.

"Soomi-ah," her eyes are on him again as he frowns, "I'm sorry if I'm being a bit much, I just really missed us being like this-," she cuts him off by placing her small hand over his mouth, causing his eyes to widen slightly in confusion.

Giggling a bit at his reaction, she shakes her head, "It's okay, I missed you too, Kookie."

Releasing her hand from his mouth, her words made a smile spread over his lips. Nothing made his heart flutter more than the thought she still had feelings for him. There were so many things that were almost perfect again, but it was like they couldn't be still because of the obstacles.

And Jungkook was willing to run over every single one of them.

"Wah, is this what I think it is?" 

Her head jerks up to the sound of everyone gasping. An uneasy feeling forms in the pit of her stomach when her eyes land on the object in Hoseok's hand, suddenly feeling sick from the embarrassment.

"Yah, that's Jungkook and Soomi's engagement ring," Taehyung states the obvious, earning an unimpressed look from Yoongi.

Trying to hide the fact her cheeks were bright red, Jungkook had been caught off guard due to the shock at the sight of the familiar object in his hyung's hand. She'd taken it as the time to rush out of his grasp, hurrying across the room to rip it from his hands and push him back towards the couch area.

"Jinjja, do you not know how to mind your own business? Jesus," she's scolding the boy as he turns to look back to her, noticing how nervous she was from the mention of the object. All the boys are staring at her, and under their gazes she felt as if she was about to pass out as she cowers, "what?! Stop looking at me like that - what did you think I was going to do with it? Throw it away?!"

"No, of course not, it's cute that you still have it!" Namjoon insists with a grin, but she didn't feel much better from their sudden discovery.

Shaking her head in disbelief, she turns away to set it back on the shelf. Just as she's on her tiptoes, about to place it in it's spot, she feels a hand cover her own that's holding the ring.

Immediately knowing who it was when the scent of that cologne hits her nose, her breath hitches in her throat when he slips it out of her own fingers. Taking her hand in his own, she turns back to Jungkook in wonder, as he holds it as if it was glass. And ever so slowly, he'd slipped the ring back onto her finger, that bright grin never leaving his lips once.

"You really kept it," he's mumbling in surprise, "it still looks beautiful on you, you're beautiful." He was gushing as he admired the sight of her. It was like Jungkook sought her to be some sort of ethereal being, something that was so precious and had to be protected. 

Her heart is beating out of her chest as she glances from him, to the ring that was on her finger. It still fit like a charm, the diamond shining under the ceiling lights. 

"Jungkook," her voice trails off into the air as she whispers his name. Her tone was one of blatancy and warning. She knew how he must feel so strongly for her still, and although she still liked him after all this time, she had Jinhwan. A part of her belonged to him, too, and she couldn't just suddenly devote herself back to him out of nowhere. She couldn't break Jinhwan's heart like that, because she loved him, in the same way she loved Jungkook.

Not to mention how dangerous it was for them to be together now, especially. Soomi was already in danger, more than she knew.

When she's about to take it off, he hurriedly raises a hand to grab her own, stopping her. Looking back up to him, their eyes meet once again, and they seem to melt into each other as he pleads, "Wait, keep it on, just for tonight - for me." 


30:00 | yoonmi

"No, seriously, like 80% of our Twitter mentions are about it!" Jin is insisting, and the others are shaking their heads in bewilderment. There was a cute, pleased grin on Jungkook's lips, but meanwhile, Soomi was feeling like she was dead inside as she buried her face in her hands.

Shifting in his seat to pull his cellphone out of his pocket, only seconds later the eldest was shoving it to the middle of the table for everyone to look. No one spoke a word as they leaned over the kitchen table of Soomi's home, propping themselves up on their elbows to peer at what he was talking about. When they see he was indeed not joking, a fit of giggles spreads throughout them all.

Except for Jimin, however. The best friend of the petite girl who couldn't be more humiliated and nerve wrecked only felt tense. He bit down on his bottom lip in thought, thinking about everything that had happened.

He felt angry about whoever was doing this to Soomi. Since she had yet to inform him they were after her again, as the only one who knew was Jungkook, he was worried about what would happen if they found out the two of them were close after so long. Now that he understood the full story, he was angry at the maknae for doing what he did at Melon. Did he not think about how dangerous it was? Or was he really just that big of a cocky show off? He scoffed as he thought about it, shaking his head and tuning out the conversations the others were involved in.

"That's cute though, the fans gave you guys a ship name and everything," Hoseok was saying as he scrolls through the tweets with his pointer finger, the phone still on the table for everyone to see.

Taehyung giggles as he reads it off one of them, "Jungmi — oh my god, it's perfect."

Namjoon had glanced up from the commotion, seeing how helpless Soomi was feeling. There was a rather neutral look on Jungkook's face, as not a word left his lips and he was in deep thought himself. He truly did feel guilty about making it known to the world he was close with her, or at least he was now, once again. It was a stupid decision, one of the many he made, because he just wanted to show off in front of not only Jimin — but the other guy she was so 'intimately close' with now.

He hated seeing the girl he still so desperately longed for with anyone else. After all, he was so set on marrying her, and as his eyes wander to the diamond ring that was on her finger, he realized he still would with all his heart.

Soomi practically owned him. He gave her his heart all those years ago, when they were teenagers, and he didn't regret it.

Either way, he knew he shouldn't have said that at Melon. He couldn't believe how ignorant he'd been as he mentally hits himself. Look what you did now, now whoever had done that to the poor couple only a year ago — was out on the prowl again.

And he was scared to know what they'd do now. The last thing he ever wanted was for Soomi to get hurt. She'd already been hurt more than an angelic being like her deserved, he wouldn't be able to live with himself if this person tried anything again.

With that thought, he wondered if maybe it would've been better if he would've just stayed away from her forever. At least then she'd be safe, right?

No, he pushes the thought away, he loved her. Jungkook shouldn't have to stay away from the girl he loved because some anonymous psycho wasn't a fan of their relationship. He knew she needed to know the truth, she needed to know he still loved her and he always would.

"I don't think Soomi is a big Jungmi fan," Joon notes, causing the girl to finally take her head out of her hands.

He wanted to ask if she was okay as she looked rather stressed, but Soomi forced a smile on her face, commenting deviously, "you know, I'm really not, I'm more of a Yoomi fan."

Everyone bursts into fits of laughter that wasn't Jungkook, Jinin, or Yoongi himself. She was sitting beside him, so when she said such a thing she'd playfully laid her head on his shoulder, grabbing onto his arm jokingly for dear life. The boy had grimaced at the touch, "That's gonna have to be a no from me."

Rolling her eyes from how boring he always was, she sits up straight again and gently punches his arm. "Shut up, Yoongles, we both know how much you love me."

Although he was trying to keep that same unamused expression, everyone saw the small grin that was fighting its way on his face.

"Guilty!" Taehyung had pointed a finger at his smiling hyung, who only put his hands up in defense.

Soomi couldn't help but burst into fits of giggles herself when Yoongi sticks his tongue out at Tae. The facial expression he'd made was one that was foreign to her, and she couldn't contain the joyous sound that poured from her lips, the others soon joining in when Taehyung had done the same thing back, putting his hands on the side of his head and moving his fingers back and forth mockingly.

The jokes only went on as the two went on and on like this for a good five minutes. Their faces were only getting more traumatizing, and the poor girl was having a hard time breathing at that point.

Holding her hands on her aching stomach, she shook her head, wiping a fake tear from under her eye. "I missed you guys," she confesses once the laughter had died down in the group and everyone was silent for a moment.

All seven pairs of eyes laid on her when she admitted this, and she felt almost nauseous under their gazes. It was when they all grinned, and she felt an arm go over her shoulders, pulling her into them. The scent of Jungkook was as obvious as ever, but that time she didn't pull away from him as she leaned her head against his chest.

"Words can't even explain how much we missed you," Hoseok was the one to say this, and everyone made noises of agreement.

"Not to mention Jungkook and Jimin mentioned you every single day," Jin shares, snorting humorously when the two boys glare at him from this. But he had no intention of stopping as he went on, "literally, they had your social media on notifications. When you'd post, they'd both rush to click on it like 'oh my god, oh my god, she posted'!" He mimicked their voices in a high pitched tone, acting as if he was some sort of young fan girl.

She nearly falls out of her seat from the laugh that erupted from her mouth, holding a hand over it so she didn't look as ridiculous as she imagined. "Seriously? That's sort of creepy."

For once, Jungkook's cheeks were a shade of red as he hurriedly defends himself, "I just wanted to know how you were doing!"

But the eldest wasn't having it, "yeah, okay, stalker."

The youngest pouts, clearly offended. Soomi was about to share with him that it was okay and she was kidding, when something else cuts that off from happening.

A ding sounds throughout the house, echoing through each room and bouncing off every wall. Everyone's eyes shot up at the sound, a few of the boys moving to get out of their seats as an offer they would answer it.

"Food, finally!" Taehyung is cheering, waving his arms in the air like a newborn child.

Soomi pursed her lips, an uneasy feeling forming in her stomach. "Wait," she calls when Hoseok is about to walk into the entryway to get the door himself. He stops, turning and raising a brow in question, to which she insists in a small tone, "let me get it."

They'd all noticed the change in her attitude, but the boy didn't object. "Oh, okay," he nods, before making his way back to his seat.

A deep breathe leaves her lips as she walks out of the room after releasing herself from Jungkook's embrace. She could only pray to God none of the boys followed her as she walked down the hall, standing before her front door only seconds later.

Although it truly might be the take out guy, she had a feeling otherwise. Even though it had been a while since they ordered the food, it had also been a while since she last spoke to the boys. They had already been so close to finishing recording, it couldn't take them that long.

Due to her rising nerves, she didn't notice the boy that was standing feet behind her. Jimin had just wanted to make sure she was okay. A part of him was over protective of his long time best friend, and he was too anxious at the thought that it could be them at the door.

Leaning against the wall, he made sure to be as quiet as a mouse.

Finally clicking the lock, she pulls open her front door. And even though she had been expecting it out of the two things, she still wasn't prepared to see him.

Then, it was like a race car had been started in her heart. Just the sight of such a boy was enough to lift her off her feet, make her feel like she was floating. She didn't know how it was possible to feel so strongly for two boys at the same time, but she knew that what she was feeling was real as a grin forms on her face.

She was happy to see him, really, but she was also terrified of how he would react if he found out who was inside her house right now. Was it really that big of a deal? Would he be angry?

The last thing she could deal with right now was a fight with Jinhwan — again — over the same thing.

So, trying not to show her anxiety, she throws a smile on her face as she cries, "Jinani~!"

He was grinning back at her, until his eyes seemed to trail behind her. Jinhwan's face turned that of no emotion as he processed who that was standing behind her only feet away, and he felt his veins begin to boil — why would one of them be there?!

Heaving out a sigh, he reaches inside, stepping forward to grab the girls wrist, gently pulling her out into the hall. Closing the door, she had been peering up at him in astonishment, when he whisper yelled, "who was that?!"

"Huh?" Soomi breathes out, completely bewildered. Was one of them standing there without her noticing? "Who are you talking about?"

It mustn't of been Jungkook, because he would've recognized him. She was thankful for that, as only God knows what would've happened then.

An impatient look on his face, Jinhwan pulls at his hair out of stress. "I don't know — he had silver hair, sorta short — I know he's one of them."

The boy appears in her mind almost instantly, and her mouth forms an 'o' as she realizes. "Oh, oh, that's Jimin. And yeah, he's from BTS."

She cut herself off because she didn't know what else to say. Honestly, she was afraid to tell him. But Jinhwan knew there was more than she was letting on, as he only kept staring down at her with that knowing look on his face.

A sigh of defeat leaves her lips, pouting as she admits helplessly, "Listen, they're my friends, okay? They're here, we were hanging out like old times."

"And what about Jungkook — what about everything he's pulled? He's clearly not over you, Soomi."

"I know, don't worry about him," she lied through her teeth, feeling her heart shattering with each word, "it's complicated, Jinhwan, but you know I love you, right?"

He sighs as he meets her pleading eyes. With the puppy look she was giving him, he would have to be a monster to not believe her.

Jinhwan and Soomi had already spoken about everything. They talked about the scandal that morning at breakfast, and how she found out it was a misunderstanding. But she told him that even though she knew that now, it wouldn't change her love for him. And that wasn't a lie.

He knew he couldn't get mad at her. They weren't officially together, as he had yet to have the guts to really ask her. It wasn't necessarily his business, and he wasn't going to try and control her life. After all, she knew them before him, they were her oldest friends.

So, he finally nodded in understanding, "okay, it's fine, sorry I seemed mad."

A breath of relief leaves her lips, and she stands on her tiptoes to kiss his cheek. "Thank you, Jinhwan-ah, I'm so glad you're such an angel."

Chuckling at her words, he grabs her hands and intertwined their fingers. Leaning down, his lips met hers for a split moment, and she basks in the feeling of his sweet sensation. Everything that he was was bliss, and it sent sparks through her entire body.

Such a feeling was the same but yet so different from the way she felt with Jungkook. With him, it was familiar, but Jinhwan, she always felt like she was trying something new, flying to new heights.

They pull away, and one of his hands falls out of her grip as she turns towards the door. He was confused when she tugged on his hand that she was still holding, looking back to him, "come on."

Unsure of what she was trying to do, he blankly asks, "why?"

"I want you to meet them."

Maybe this wasn't a very good idea. Who knew what would happen if Jinhwan and Jungkook were in the same room, under the same roof? She was just hoping that Jungkook, who was aware of their relations, would chill out with whatever fling they had sprung anew. She was hoping he would respect that she had something with Jinhwan, and she wasn't sure who she truly wanted to be with.

"I don't think that's necessary," he's shaking his head, but then he sees the pleading look she was giving him again. Unable to say no to her innocent face, he groaned in defeat, "alright, fine!"

"Yay!" She cheerily voices out, still holding onto his hand as she opens the door with her other. But she hadn't expected for someone to be standing only feet away in the middle of the entryway, arms crossed over their chest and lips in a straight line as they seemed to be in deep thought.

"Oh, perfect!" Soomi hadn't even thought to ask why he was standing there as she stops her movement, "Jinhwan-ah, this is my best friend, Jimin," she says formally, "and Jimin, this is the Jinhwan I told you about."

Snapping out of his daze, his eyes find a way to the boy that was standing in front of him. He was surprised at his height, but didn't show it as he took in the boys' appearance. So this was the apparent new love interest of hers?

Trying not to show his jealousy, he grins as he shakes the boys hand, "Hi, nice to meet you!"

He took in great note of the way they were holding hands and how she was clinging onto him for dear life. She really wasn't joking when she said they were close and pretty serious, he hoped Jungkook had an idea of what sort of competition he was up against — it wasn't just the two of them anymore.

However, his eyes land on the ring that was still on Soomi's finger. Had Jinhwan not noticed? It probably wouldn't be very good if he did.

That seemed to be the last thing on Soomi's mind as she finally pulls away from the close proximity with Jinhwan, pulling him aside so she could whisper, "can you go tell Jungkook to not do anything stupid?"

Nodding, he assures her he would, before leaving to find the boy.

Satisfied, she turns back to Jinhwan, "well, time for you to meet the rest," she hums, "they're pretty close to the guys, you know, equally as bat shit crazy."

Raising a brow, the short boy hums in understanding, "well this outta be interesting."














31:00 | awkward

"Yah, Jungkook, come here," Jimin is instructing as he practically zooms into the kitchen, alarming the six boys that were casually sitting around the table. They look up in surprise at the frantic look over the usually calm angel's face as he's standing beside where the maknae is seated, looking down at him expectantly.

Jungkook only looks up at him, confusion clearly wrote over his face as he's unsure of what he needs, and doesn't seem very interested either. Meanwhile, the others look at the two boys blankly, Jin being the first one to blatantly ask, "So, where's the food?"

A stressed sigh leaves Jimin's lips, as he was trying to do what Soomi had asked of him, but Jungkook was always so troublesome - more than usual - when it came to her. Any time the boy was in the same room or in any sort of proximity with in her, it was as if he became one of the snottiest brats on the face of the Earth. He wasn't a fan of the fact Soomi was so close with this guy either, but the last thing he wanted to do was create trouble in her life by making a scene. But with the things Jungkook had been causing, it was likely he'd do just that without thinking of the consequences.

"It's not here yet," is all he says, giving Jungkook a certain glare as if to tell him they needed to speak now. The boy finally seems to get the message, and heaves out a groan as he allows him to pull him up. Ripping his wrist out of Jimin's grip, he hesitantly follows him out of the room and down the hall, where they wouldn't be seen by Soomi or Jinhwan. 

This left the other five boys in a state of confusion. Shifting in his seat, Hoseok sets his elbows on the table, leaning his chin in the palm of his hand as he mutters, "what was that about?"

It wasn't like any of them knew. Taehyung only shrugs, shaking his head as he shares, "They've been weird ever since we got here."

Namjoon had been pursing his lips as he stares down at something on his phone. There seemed to be an anxious look on his face, like he was getting perplexed just by reading whatever happened to be on the screen. Luckily, no one noticed it to ask as Yoongi had deadpanned to the two clueless boys in the room, sitting across from him, "are you two really that slow?"

Not understanding, they only sent him confused looks. 

Exhaling in disappointment, he crosses his arms over his chest, not wasting a second to inform them of everything he claimed was so obvious, "we all know how much Jungkook missed Soomi-ah, clearly since he never shut the fuck up about it. Whatever happened between them when the scandal got out seems to be solved, and he's trying to win her back," he'd explained, a bored tone dripping from his words.

Taehyung was starting to gather where he was going with his story, but Hoseok still looked like a deer in headlights. Brows knitting together, he scratches his head as he mumbles, "but what's that have to do with Jimin?"

In that moment, Yoongi had to resist the urge to slam his head on the kitchen table. Sighing, he only continues, "Jimin has always been Soomi's best friend. He's always liked her, have you never noticed?"

Hoseok's mouth forms an o shape as he says this, and he gasps like it was the craziest thing he'd ever heard, "Wah, Jimin likes Soomi?!"

Being that pretty much everyone knew that by now, Yoongi wasn't the only one who looked to the boy in disappointment.

"So I'm pretty sure Jimin wasn't expecting the two to have made up so quickly, and Jungkook seems to be aware he likes her," he hums, "but there's that other guy she mentioned too - what was his name?" his voice got lower by the time he said the last words, as he diverted his eyes to the ceiling. Rubbing his chin, he seems to be in deep thought, trying to remember the name of the boy Soomi had briefly mentioned to the others when they were talking. 

She never said much, at least not to all of them. Just something along the lines of how she'd gotten rather close with him since she stopped hanging around the boys after the scandal. He could only imagine how Jimin and Jungkook felt about that. 

"I think it was-," Jin is cut off when the sound of the girl they'd just been speaking abouts voice was heard, as if on queue. Their heads all jerk up to see her, expecting her to enter the room with their long awaited food, but she walked in with something else.

There was a nervous grin on Soomi's face, and she looked like she was about to pass out from anxiety when her eyes scanned the room, looking for Jungkook. When she notices he isn't there, and neither was Jimin, she'd put two and two together that they were having that conversation. 

Slowly but surely, the boys tried to hide the fact they were astonished at the sight of someone entering behind her. This was the guy she was talking about, was the first thing that popped into their minds simultaneously. After all, who else would it be? 

And at the sight of him, they suddenly had a feeling what Jimin wanted to talk to Jungkook about.

"Guys, uh, this is Jinhwan," she motions to the boy, and they took in the fact he was a lot shorter than all of them. With baby soft, glowing skin, and caramel hair that was perfectly parted in the middle, it also didn't take long for them to recognize who he was, even before she'd introduced him. 

A soft smile spreads over his lips as he bowed politely, to which they all rise to their feet to do the same. "Nice to meet you," although they couldn't tell just how sincere his tone was. Deep down, he didn't really seem too pleased to see them.

Meanwhile, Jimin thought Jungkook was going to burn down the entire apartment with the way his eyes flamed up. The short boy had stood in front of the door to the room they stood in, worried that if he didn't, Jungkook would run through it and do something he'd regret later on. But to his surprise, the youngest wasn't moving a muscle. He only stood there, staring off into nothingness, and letting out a deep breathe, as if trying to calm his frustration from what he just heard.


"Jungkook-," but he's cut off when he abruptly spins on his heel to face him, a burning madness evident in his facial expression. 

"Please for your sake, tell me this is a joke," he sighs out, running a hand through his dark hair and pulling at the ends to calm himself. The sympathetic look on Jimin's face tells him otherwise though, and he could only mentally prepare himself as he diverts his eyes to the ceiling once again. "God fucking dammit."

Jimin could relate to his anger, but he didn't want to share that with him. God only knew what he'd do, and Jungkook was probably already aware of it anyway, since he seemed to be fond of his feelings for her (and was not very happy about them.) That seemed to be the last thing on his mind, though, as he was too focused on this guy that was here in Soomi's apartment with them, this guy that was taking his Soomi away from him. 

How was he supposed to tolerate that? He just liked to show of to Jimin, but Jimin was his close hyung at the end of the day. They had their disagreements and fights, but they were brothers whether he liked the fact he had feelings for Soomi or not. He just wanted to get it through Jimin's head that she was his. But never would he hurt his hyung, not unless it was something treacherously terrible.

But this Jinhwan guy? He didn't know if he could hold himself back. Jungkook couldn't help the fact he was jealous and determined, determined to get the love of his life back. When he was going to marry her a year ago, he'd done it for a reason - because he saw himself destined to be with her forever. She was the light of his life, and ever since the scandal he'd felt like a piece of him had been stolen. It was why he decided he couldn't take it anymore, why he had to talk to her and tell her the truth.

Although he was torn that this awful person who ruined them a year ago was back, was threatening her to her face now, he swore to himself and Soomi that he would never let anything happen. Jungkook would never leave her side, and would protect her with his life if he had to.

But how could he do that when someone else was in the way? It broke him in two to think she had begun to move on without him, because he'd never moved on from her, he didn't think he could. Now she had feelings for someone else other than him, and it angered him, more than words could explain.

"Just don't start anything alright?" Jimin's calming tone breaks through his head, and he exhaled deeply as he met his empathetic eyes, "Soomi's in a tough place right now, you know. She's confused and scared, she told me about what really happened."

Blinking at the boy in astonishment, he utters in surprise, "She did?" 

Jimin only nods.

"Well, I'm not going to let anything happen to her," he snaps sternly, "I love her, hyung, I always have."

I love her, too.

But Jimin didn't say anything as Jungkook had gently pushed him aside so he could exit the door, only uttering the words to the boy behind him now, "I won't start anything, promise."

Only watching him leave, he bites his lip in contemplation. I hope you mean that, Jungkook.




***




"So you're releasing music together?" Namjoon had been casually asking Jinhwan and Soomi, who were sitting next to each other in the booths against the wall at her kitchen table. Jungkook and Jimin had yet to return, and she was starting to feel more anxious than usual. Jinhwan was always good at reading her emotions, and she could only hope he didn't notice what was going on with her as she nervously kept biting her lip, messing with her hands that lay in her lap.

"Ne," Jinhwan had glanced over to her a few times, no doubt noticing what was going on with her, before he looks back to one of the guys he had just met, "we are, the songs are to be released during MAMA this week."

A lot of them perked up at this, eyes wide with excitement. "Oh, really? That's great!" Hoseok had congratulated the both of them, and Soomi looked up from her lap to send him a thankful smile.

"Yeah, Soomi wrote most of them," Jinhwan had gone on and on about how good she was when it came to songwriting and producing. The guys had gushed in agreement, talking about how they used to write songs together as well. She'd piped up a bit, forgetting about the worries in her head from hearing so much talk about something she loved to do.

"I learned from the best," she'd spoke out after they'd all finished complimenting her and talking about how they were proud of how far she'd come. It took them all a minute to realize who she was talking about, but eventually Yoongi notices her bright smile that's directed to him, and a shy smile spreads on his lips. 

When they notice the eye contact happening between the two old friends, Taehyung had leaned over to whisper a certain name in Hoseok's ear, "Yoonmi!" but the thing was, it hadn't been much of a whisper at all despite his attempts.

Having heard it, Soomi giggles as she reaches over to poke Yoongi's cheek, "I'm Yoongles' apprentice, he taught me everything I know when I was a trainee."

Yoongi had winced at the nickname, playfully swatting her hand off his cheek. Shaking his head, he waves his hand dismissively, "Yeah, too bad you're still not as good as me."

His jokes caused a pout to spread over her lips as some of the boys start laughing at the satisfied look that spreads over his face. Picking up a chopstick, she chunks it at him and he lets out some sort of childish sound, putting his hands up to block it hitting his face. The chopstick makes a clink as it hits the tile floor, and she narrowed her eyes at him, "Disgusting, how you won't just accept my love, to think I ever shipped Yoonmi."

He was in fits of laughter from the dead serious expression she had, and the others couldn't help but join in. "I've never seen a ship sink faster," Jin had been mumbling.

Meanwhile, Jinhwan had been feeling a bit awkward as he didn't really know how to join in on their conversations. He could tell he didn't really fit in here, not with her and her old friends. They knew more about her than he did, having had known her ever since she'd pretty much moved here. The short year he'd known her for was practically nothing next to the years and years they had. With the obvious chemistry that had sprung once again throughout the old friends, he could only watch it happen, shifting in his seat and pursing his lips in thought.

But where was Jungkook? He was the only one he was worried about. Jinhwan had had eyes for Soomi ever since that day he saw her, and even before that. That day, he knew who she was, and he also knew about the scandal that had just occurred. How could he not? 

Any guy would be lucky to have someone like her. And he wanted to be that guy.

Knowing now that what Jungkook had done to her was a huge misunderstanding, made him feel threatened. He was worried that she was falling for him all over again now that he was back in her life. It was like the chance he finally had with her was disappearing, and it was killing him.

A doorbell suddenly sounds, and everyone in the room seemed to move to get up. However, a sudden sound of loud footsteps was heard throughout the house. Only a second later had a smiley Jimin peeked his head through the doorway of the kitchen, "Yah, I'll get it, don't worry!" before he'd rushed away towards the front door.

At the thought their food was finally here, everyone inhaled in satisfaction. "Finally, I thought the guy crashed or something," Hoseok was saying as they all sat up eagerly.

Seconds later after Jimin had walked to get the door, there were slow footsteps clattering against the tiled floor. A knowing lump formed in Soomi's throat, and as she looked up to meet his eyes, her worries were only confirmed.

Jungkook looked sad, and it tore a piece of her with the way it poured into her green irises. It was like he was trying to tell her something, and she got the message that seeing her with Jinhwan was hurting him - but it was hurting a part of her, too.

She was hurting more than he could understand, because both of them had such a special place in her heart. Jungkook was the love of her life, her fiance at one point even though it was for a short time, she had loved him with her entire being. When she said yes to him that night, she'd meant it with her heart. 

But the scandal, despite it being a giant misunderstanding which she knew now, had still left a dent in her. It'd taken her so long to get over, gave her a temporary alcohol problem, made her think she was worthless because she didn't get it. She could never understand what she had done, for she loved him so much that it felt like her body was malfunctioning at the thought it wasn't mutual.

And Jinhwan, the angel that had seemed to save her from her demons. Without him, she would've never found that peaceful place, she would've never gotten over the darkness that she'd been submerged in. He saved her life more than once when he picked her up and saved her from being drunk, from doing stupid things, from getting caught. Jinhwan was the light she didn't deserve, shining on her in her worst times. He made her herself again, and nothing she could do would ever repay him for that.

With two people that were suddenly here, who felt for her, how was she supposed to know which one was right for her? How did she know who was truly meant for her in her heart?

She didn't, and as Jungkook cleared his throat, hesitantly scooting into the booth beside her, she could feel her heartbeat accelerate. They were sitting on either side of her, and only God knew how she was going to get through the rest of this night. Perhaps it wasn't such a good idea to invite Jinhwan in, after all.










32:00 | the ex fiance

Jungkook shifted uncomfortably in his seat as an attempt to get comfortable. There was an uninterested, dull expression plastered on his face as his eyes wander right by Soomi, falling on the new presence that was beside her. Carelessly, he deadpans to the boy, "Hi, I'm Jungkook."


Jinhwan's eyes rake over the ex fiance, taking in his appearance. It looked as if he'd just woken up or something else, with the way his hair messily fell in his eyes, his eyes tired. The way his eyes twinkled under the lights in the kitchen made him feel even more threatened, especially when he ran his tongue over his bottom lip as he looked to the girl that was in between them.


 The way his eyes twinkled under the lights in the kitchen made him feel even more threatened, especially when he ran his tongue over his bottom lip as he looked to the girl that was in between them

"Jinhwan," is all he offers him, not really feeling the energy to fake a smile. It wasn't like he was putting in the effort either, anyway. The two boys were clearly not fond of each other for the obvious relations they both had with her.


Meanwhile, Soomi watched the extremely awkward interaction, sinking back in her seat in utter worry. Nothing would be greater than just sinking into the ground below her, and she nervously fiddles with her fingers in her lap, trying to think of any way to lighten such a traumatizing situation.


As if on queue, she could never have been more thankful for Park Jimin. The aroma of food enters the nostrils of everyone at the table, their eyes lighting up when the boy's walking in with boxes of pizza and bags of take out.


"Thank God," she mumbled under her breathe as everyone had cheered in excitement, screeching about how long it had seemed to take. A kind grin is on Jimin's lips as he sets everything in the middle of the table, and the boys of Bangtan lean forward, eagerly unpacking the contents that contained their food.


She could only hope it was enough to feed eight of them. 


Clearing her throat, she tries to ease the tension in her body as she reaches out, her fingers lightly grazing Jungkook's forearm to get his attention. His eyes jerk to her, and she didn't waste a second to gently request, "Jungkook-ah, can you get the plates and chopsticks?" 


Just when her hand was leaving his arm, to her surprise, he'd reached out to grab it. His large hand engulfs her much smaller one, and her eyes nearly fell out of her sockets as he'd brought it to his lips. Soomi resisted the urge to hit him when they left a lingering kiss on her knuckle, a devious grin on his face as he'd released it from his grip. Then casually, as if he hadn't done a thing in the world that could be seen as wrong, he'd slid out of the seat, humming, "anything for you, Soomi-ah."


She could feel her cheeks going rosy, and her hands quickly go to cover her face as she leans against the table. "Tell me this isn't happening," she mutters to herself, too afraid to even see the look on Jinhwan's face.


This was for good reason, as Jinhwan couldn't be more upset. He knew exactly why Jungkook was doing what he was doing, as soon as he'd walked across the kitchen, opening the cabinets and grabbing plates, his eyes had found a way to him. They make eye contact, and Jinhwan's death glare didn't seem to be scary to him at all, he'd only mischievously smirked, shaking his head as he brings them back to the table.


Feeling more stressed than ever, he wondered what to do in this sort of situation. Jinhwan knew that Soomi wasn't completely over her ex fiance, he wasn't that slow. He didn't need to confront her to know this, he saw the way her cheeks would tint, the way she'd get nervous. It was enough to know those feelings she had for him were strong enough to always remain, and now that he was back, they were resurfacing.


This hurt, more than he ever knew it could. But he wasn't going to be mad at her for it, he was done with that, and it wasn't exactly his business to get angry at her, not again. She was going through too much, and he didn't want to worsen that.


Soomi wasn't his, Jinhwan knew that, but he wanted her to be. If she loved him, a part of her loved Jungkook, too. 


Everyone was already desperately digging into their food, shoving it down their throats like starving pigs. It was a quiet dinner table for the most part, mainly filled with the sound of them chewing their food. Every once in a while, Hoseok would crack a joke and lighten the mood, or Taehyung would do something silly with his food and make them laugh. She was thankful for their light hearts, always making everything better despite the tension in the atmosphere.


Soomi was feeling better as she ate her food. Jinhwan and Jungkook weren't associating with each other, which was as much as she expected, and that was okay. She didn't invite him in to meet him, she brought him in so he could meet the others. After all, they were her old friends and they had a special place in her heart, but so did Jinhwan - and the other boys of iKON - it was only right for them to meet.


"Yah, Jinhwan," she suddenly was reminded of them, causing her to look to him with curious eyes.


He was just shoving a piece of orange chicken in his mouth, but still hums, "Hm?"


"Did the others finish recording yet, are they at home?" she's asking, maybe if they joined them the awkward atmosphere would be demolished. Since then Jinhwan would be around people he was comfortable with instead of just surrounded by new acquaintances.


Swallowing the piece of meat, he shrugs, "I'm not sure, I left early so I could see you, and they were still pretty busy."


Nodding in understanding, she tried not to show the effect his sweet words had on her. He left early to see me, she repeats it in her head, thinking back to what her and Jungkook had been doing most likely around that time while he was working in the studio on their song. She felt guilty, so guilty that she had to turn away and quickly eat her food so he didn't notice the look on her face.


Was it wrong? She wasn't sure. Technically, she didn't do anything that was bad, as it wasn't like they were actually together. 


But a part of her still felt bad.


Suddenly, Jinhwan's phone starts vigorously vibrating in his pocket, the ring tone making her deaf as she was sitting right beside him. As he takes it out of his pocket, she leans over his shoulder to peer at the screen.


At the sight of the name, her eyes light up, and the others weren't paying attention due to the conversation they were having about their performance at MAMA. In excitement, she hurriedly snatches the phone out of Jinhwan's hand, much to his surprise, answering the FaceTime call for him.


"June-ya~!" she screeches that of a kid, holding out the phone in front of the two of them. 


It'd taken it a minute to load, but once it did, a grinning Junhoe was shown on the screen, along with five other boys crowded around him. He looked confused at the sight of her, but after a minute, everyone of them was screaming equally as childish as she just had.


"Yah, Soomi!"


"She's alive!"


"Oh my god, Soomi, I miss you~!"


Junhoe had wrinkled his nose as Donghyuk's last comment, shaking his head as he deadpans, "who is this ugly being answering? I wanted to talk to hyung."


Faking a gasp, she scoffs, "I don't know what you're talking about, I am Jinhwan," she says, dramatically flipping her hair over her shoulder, "but then again, he is pretty ugly."


Having had been casually eating his own food while listening in on the conversation, the eldest of iKON had looked up from her words, clearly offended as he snaps, "Yah!"


Laughing, it wasn't long until the others had called from the phone, "are you at Soomi's? Is that food?!" Bobby is calling as he sees the chopsticks in their hands.


"Yeah," Soomi had nodded as she shoved a piece of meat in her mouth, chewing and swallowing before speaking up again, "but there's probably not enough for you guys, are you coming home now?"


They're about to answer, when someone that was not their hyung had leaned their head on Soomi's shoulder to peek at the screen. Never had she seen more shocked looks spread over their faces, and only then did she notice the scent of the cologne and the boy that was suddenly right beside her. 


"Is that Jungkook? What's he doing there?" 


"He can hear you, you know."


She's about to speak up and explain that he wasn't the only one here, when Junhoe had started screaming. 


"YOU CAN'T STOP ME LOVING MYSELF-," he was cut off when the other simultaneously hit his arm. Soomi shook her head in disappointment, facepalming as she tries to hide her laughter.


What she hadn't expected, was a wide smile on Jungkook's lips as he giggles at what Junhoe had done. His bunny teeth were out in the open, making a dent in her emotions when he waves to the phone screen, "Hi, I'm Jungkook, nice to meet you!" unlike when he met Jinhwan, his tone was cheery and welcoming. 


The others had called out greetings as well, waving back at him in return. She could see the worry in their eyes, as they were probably wondering what in the world was going on and why she was sitting in between her two love interests. Soomi looks to her side where Jungkook was, about to curiously ask him where the sudden change in attitude was from, but she seemed to lose her words when his face was only a mere inch from her own.


His lips, that always seemed to make a spark ignite within her, were right there, and she swallowed the lump that had formed in her throat. With an accelerating heartbeat, Jungkook raises a brow at her, humming in fake worry because they both knew what her issue was, "Soomi-ah, are you okay?"


Deeply exhaling, she refrains her emotions as she raises her pointer finger, placing it on his nose and pushing him back. When she looks back to the boys on her phone screen, and the boys at the table, they were all staring. No doubt had they seen the moment.


Trying to ease out of the awkward aura that was coming back, she offers a grin to the iKON boys on the phone, "You guys can come over if you'd like."


They exchange some looks, unsure expressions on their faces, "we'd love too, but we have an early schedule in the morning," Hanbin shares, "so hyung, you should probably come home."


Jinhwan looks up from his food that he had been picking at, a close lipped, forced smile on his lips as he hums, "Yeah, I know, I'll be there shortly."


However, she hadn't expected shortly to mean right now. He slid out of the seat, taking his phone out of his hand. She yelled goodbye to them as he ended the call, and his eyes pour into hers once again.


Motioning for her to follow, he walks to the entryway. The boys call farewells to him, which he returns with a small grin and wave, and Soomi hurriedly excuses herself to follow him like he requested. 


As her feet click against the tiled floor, she felt Jungkook's eyes burning holes into her back. Everything is fine, she reminds herself as she turns into the hall, following Jinhwan out the door and into the apartment hallway.


Once they're both alone and able to speak privately, he spins on his heel to face her. His caramel hair is falling in his face, his brown eyes glistening despite the dim lighting. Even then, his lips looked softer than ever as he sighed, "Soomi, listen, I don't know what's really going on with you and Jungkook."


She was going to cut him off, but she knew that what she would tell him would be a lie. So she kept her mouth shut, allowing him to continue, "but I know that he still loves you, and I know that you still love him."


Her heart was on the ground, but she knew she couldn't deny it. 


Clearing his throat, he diverts his eyes, his voice growing softer, "but even then, I love you, I always have. So I'm going to wait for you, until you make the decision that your heart desires," his eyes find a way back to hers, and only then did she see the tears in them as he whispered, "I'll be here, okay?"


Frowning, she nods immensely, stepping forward to wrap her arms around him. He didn't hesitate to accept her embrace, and she felt her heart shattering as she cries in his chest, "Jinani, don't cry. I'm sorry this is happening, I can't deny what you said because, you're right, but you have a place in my heart too," she explains as they pull away a mere second later, "you've helped me so much, you saved my life. I adore you," she pouts, twiddling her thumbs under her as she sadly admits, "this feels like we're breaking up even though we're not together."


A bitter laugh leaves his lips as he shakes his head. Jinhwan reaches his hand up to push hair out of her eyes, a sad smile on his lips, "no, you're not mine, but I wish you were. And it's okay, Soomi-ah, nothing is wrong. We can stay the same as always, I just wanted to tell you, alright? I'm not mad at you."


She only nods, "thank you, Jinhwan."


"Of course," he says, already walking towards the elevator, "I'll see you tomorrow at the studio, Soo."











33:00 | doubt

Even though she should be happy about the conversation she'd had with Jinhwan, it felt like a part of her was lost as she closed the door behind her. The time was around 3 AM, and she found herself having an urge to cry as she stares at her feet.

It was when she nearly ran into someone if they hadn't reached out a hand to stop her, that she saw some of the boys standing before her, bags in hand. "Hey, Mi, we should probably head back home, we've got an early schedule tomorrow, as well," Namjoon explains what they were doing.

"Oh," she hums, not knowing what time it actually was herself, "alright, no problem, is Jungkook going?" she had to ask when she doesn't see him with them.

As if on queue, the boy had spun the corner from the living room, his bag over his shoulder. There was a pouty look when he did so, as he walks to the front of the group. She squeaked in shock when he wraps his arms around her shoulders, but she relaxed into his embrace, thinking back to Jinhwan and her talk. She had to make a decision, but in the meantime, it didn't mean she couldn't enjoy being around him.

Seeing him ready to leave was enough to answer her question, and she frowns as they pull away, his hands finding a way to hers. She opens her mouth to speak, when the maknae beats her to it, turning to his hyungs, "can't she just come with us?"

Her eyes widen at his question, as it wasn't what she expected to hear. It'd been a while since she went to their house, and she didn't even know where they lived anymore. The boys used to live in rather large dorms, a lot more luxurious than the YG ones. But when she and Jungkook's relationship got more serious, they moved into an apartment together, and shortly after that the boys had moved into a new house as well.

It'd only been a year, but with the success they were having, she imagined they already moved again into something even larger.

Although it was dangerous, there also wasn't a big chance for anyone like Dispatch to be out and about at an hour like this. She'd just have to keep a low head when leaving tomorrow, but it wasn't like they knew where they lived. The YG dorms were in the middle of the city, and were a well known area since so many artists lived in them, which was why the paparazzi knew of their location. But if the boys had a house, there was no way it was known to the public.

Thinking back to the note she received, she realized she didn't want to be alone anyway.

Jin is the one who finally shrugs out of them, "I don't think it'd be a problem."

A bright smile spreads over Jungkook's lips, and Namjoon nods after a minute, "Yeah, sure."

"Yah, thanks guys!" she squeals, jumping up and down a few times out of joy. The others chuckle at her actions, and she pulls out of Jungkook's grip as she walks down the hall towards her room, calling back to them, "let me just get my things!"





~




"That Jinhwan guy was pretty nice, I can see why you guys are close," the light haired boy hums these words, but in a completely bright manner she didn't expect, not in that moment. The chirp of Jimin's words causes her to pause her focus on the show they were watching on the television. Instead, Soomi shifts her seating position so that she was instead facing him, legs crossed in a childlike manner. When their eyes meet, the girl raises a brow inquisitively, and it didn't take her old best friend long to get the gist. 

Waving his hands in the air in defense, he vigorously shakes his head, "Really, I can see why you're into him," he insists, before divulging, "he's not as pushy and bratty as Jungkook - he's the complete opposite, actually."

Upon the mention of the other love interest in her life, a sigh leaves her lips. Holding a hand to her head because it honestly hurt her mentally to think about it all, she groused, "I can't believe he was still trying to start drama even after you talked to him," her voice trails off, the annoyance still dripping from every word. 

Truly, she was unhappy with Jungkook's actions, but she knew he'd really always been like this. What was she really expecting from him now that he was back in her life? Ever since day one, he'd been protective and childish. It was no surprise he still messed with Jinhwan like that. She just thought he would've matured.

"Are you and Jinhwan okay?" her best friend queries, his eyes full of sympathy she missed seeing. Never had someone been as concerned for her as Jimin, always making sure she was alright in every waking moment. She missed him.

However, Soomi wasn't sure she had an answer to that question. Just thinking about the conversation made her happy, yet she still felt sort of empty. Jinhwan really was a part of her, she meant it when she said she was planning to take it to the next step with him, despite how dangerous it was. He was her savior, and for the last year she'd only fallen for him harder with each day that went by.

The two of them, Soomi and Jinhwan, they just got each other. With such unspeakable chemistry, how could they not fall in love? The thing was, there were just so many obstacles in the way, so many things keeping them from just being together.

And Jungkook was one of them, right along with the fact that they weren't allowed to date because of their job. The last thing she ever wanted was to go through something like she went through with Jungkook, and was still going through.

It ruined not only almost all her life, but her.

"I guess so," she could only mutter minutes later, fiddling with her hands that lay in her lap. Jimin notices the uncertainty in her tone, and he frowns a bit at how distressed she seemed to be. "They just both have a special place in my heart," she admits dully, "I just don't know which one fills it the most, and now with this scary person threatening me, what do I do from here?"

She looks back up to him when she finishes her rant, but he looks just as lost as she feels. Jimin exhales deeply, seeming to be in deep thought. He wished there was something he could tell her that would make it all better. If only it were truth in the words it'll be okay or it'll get better, but the reality was, nobody ever really knows if it will, especially not in something as drastic as this.

So, what could he really say?

"They both love you, Soomi," he'd finally affirmed, pursing his lips in thought again after he says so. Just hearing the L word causes her green eyes to stare into his soul, and Jimin had to look away to finish his conclusion, "both of them will wait for you, and even if it doesn't seem like it now, Jungkook will learn to respect your decision, whichever one it is you make."

His words hit a special place in her heart. Just thinking about the fact he was right caused her to grin, and she nods after a few moments. 

"Yeah, you're right," she drawls, "it'll be fine - thank you, Jiminnie."

"What are best friends for, Soo?"






~





"Hey, hyung, is Soomi okay?" Never had he been ran at by the likes of Koo Junhoe so quickly, his words flying out in a rushed manner of immense concern. It looked like he had just gotten up from a nap, with his hair sticking out in different directions, and his brown eyes were wide with question as they peer down at the eldest from his outstanding height.

Jinhwan's eyes are tired, and he didn't really feel like talking in general. Kicking off his shoes at the entryway, he hangs his jacket on the coat hanger before pushing past June and heading towards his room, not saying a word.

This wasn't what he usually did. Jinhwan always tried to be a good hyung to the others. But he didn't have the energy anymore, he felt like his heart was throbbing, bound to fall right out of his chest and onto the floor he walked on. Even though he told Soomi he was fine and acted like there was no problem, like it was no big deal, it was a huge deal to him.

He loved her more than anything, and now realizing she still had some sort of feelings for Jungkook, he didn't get it. Had the boy not done something horrible to her? He thought she hated him, but he guessed after he caught them kissing, she didn't. 

How had she just forgave him like that? Jinhwan felt like he had just been pushed aside like trash.

Wanting nothing more than to just go to bed and stare into darkness for hours, he knew this wasn't going to happen when the padding foot steps of Junhoe are heard following him. "Yah, are you not going to answer me? I'm worried about her," the boy is whining. It was the first time he ever heard him plead, sounding genuinely sad about someone other than himself. Not that June was self absorbed (okay, but he is,) but more of the fact that he just didn't show his emotions freely, he didn't let those around him know what was going on in that brain of his. Junhoe was the type to just want everyone to be happy around him, even if he didn't act like it, all he wanted was to make people laugh.

Jinhwan halts in his tracks, running a hand through his dirty blonde hair as he spins to face him. The boy is looking down at him with hopeful eyes, and he couldn't muster much of a voice as he mumbled out, "she's been hanging out with Jungkook."

The confusion washes over his face like a hurricane. Blinking a few times, June takes a minute to process the information he was given. "Jungkook?" he hums, "well yeah, but that can't be it."

With brown eyes piercing through his skull, Jinhwan raises a brow, "what do you mean? That's why she's been distant, is that not what you're talking about?" he monotones, but Junhoe only shakes his head.

"I thought you were supposed to know her better than me," the disappointment causes Jinhwan to grow even more annoyed at him, "there's something more than that on her mind, I can tell. You really haven't noticed how unusually jumpy she is?"

At this, Jinhwan has to stop for a second to ponder. He was always paying attention to Soomi, but he never thought too deep into it. Since today, he just figured she hung up on that call earlier and had been - different - because of Jungkook. But really, it had been more and more off as time went on. What else could it be, though?

"Fuck," he curses, feeling bad for the fact he was just assuming it was all because of Jungkook. Why would that make the girl so on edge?

He jumps from how deep in thought he had been when Junhoe pats his shoulder. "Don't worry, hyung," he soothes with a slight grin of reassurement, "we'll see her tomorrow at the studio, you better check on her then."

"Yeah, I will."


34:00 | i'm ok

Soomi's eyes are tired that night as she shifts in her position on the couch of the boys living room. She had fallen asleep with Chim watching her favorite show Gossip Girl and just catching up on everything they had missed in each other's lives last year. She had fallen asleep first due to her stress and lack of sleep recently, and Jimin was happy to see she was finally resting.

To his dismay, he figured Jungkook would come try to bother her though. It was exactly what he did when he came trotting out of his room late at night, probably having had just finished playing some of that game he was always on. His eyes were looking for only one person, and as soon as they landed on the sound asleep girl, his brown irises glowed as if he found a treat.

"Don't," Jimin had snapped as soon as he saw Jungkook reaching out his arms, about to have scooped her right up. His hyung's harsh tone caused him to pause, looking up to him in confusion, and almost as if he was offended.

He takes a moment to process before he blinks at him a few times, "what? I'm sure she isn't comfortable here."

The boy only rolls his eyes, not buying his poor attempt, "she's sleeping just fine, leave her alone."

At his insistent words, a pout forms on the youngest's lips. He crosses his arms over his chest, seeming to be in deep thought. His eyes peered down at the peacefully sleeping beauty, and after a moment, he heaves out a sigh, seeming to realize it really would be rude for him to just pick her up and take her to his room. Even though they were 'close,' he didn't want to do that without Soomi agreeing to sleep in his room, of course. The last thing he was trying to do was freak her out.

Plus, she looked so oddly cozy on their couch, with a fluffy pillow under her head and a BT21 blanket drenched over her small body. Soft breathes leave her slightly parted lips, and he felt his heart falling out of his chest at the remembrance of how gorgeous she was when asleep.

"You look like a creep," Jimin's words are mocking, causing Jungkook to snap out of his trance, only to send a sharp glare his way.

Scoffing, he's spinning on his heel, retorting, "says you," before beginning his way back to his room. "Tell me if she wakes up."

If Jimin would have agreed to this demand, he would've been a liar.

Thank God he didn't.

Fast forward only a couple of hours later, Soomi had awoken from her sleep feeling odd and out of place. She felt like there was something egging at her insides, and she knew exactly what it was.

After a long late night talk with her best friend, they both ended up in his car. It didn't take them long to arrive, and she felt a ping in her heart just looking up at the buildings.

Gathering her things, Soomi leans over the cup holder in the middle to wrap her arms around Chim's neck. He was glad to accept the hug, taking in the petite girl's warmth. Inhaling that familiar perfume he missed, he muttered the words, "good luck, Soo."

Pulling away from the embrace, she lets out a deep sigh. Knowing he was talking about the entire decision making thing, she sends him a thankful grin, "thank you for everything."

With words full of so much grace, he couldn't help but feel his heart flutter.

"Anything for you, Soo."

She's already waving as she's climbing out of the car, "bye Chim, tell Jungkook I'll come by soon!"

He had only called back a simple 'no problem,' and with that, she turns to face the large apartment building. Her heart beating out of her chest, she wasn't sure why she felt such a way. It wasn't unusual for her to be heading to their apartment, it was a daily thing she did for the last year after all.

No, it was because she had bigger plans for visiting, especially at such a late time. A part of her wasn't even sure if anyone would be awake to let her in.

What exactly would she tell Jinhwan? Where would she start? She didn't know, but it had to be somewhere.

She didn't know if she was talking about her feelings for him, or the deep dark problem she had currently in life - other than her choosing between Jinhwan and Jungkook. The thing was, she had been trying to hide the fact she was scared out of her mind, and she didn't think anyone was really noticing the fact she wasn't doing well.

Other than Jimin, but that was just because he knew her so well. She figured this meant she was masking it well enough.

It wasn't that she wanted to hide anything from the boys of iKON. They were her family now, they had been for a while. Never did she want to lie to them, but it was for the best. She couldn't have them being worried sick about her, especially Jinhwan.

But she was too scared, so scared that it was beginning to eat her insides. Although she had Jungkook and the others, she felt like there was a certain comfort her heart was missing.

And that was the exact comfort she was going to find whether it included spilling her guts or not.

The winter air is chilly against her skin, and she finds herself rubbing her arms to warm herself as she types in the code for the entrance. It was a miracle when the lobby doors slide open, and she wasted no time to rush inside, heading straight for the elevator.

After calling it, she pulled out her phone to check the time - 3:00 AM.

"Jeez," she mutters, wondering where all the time went.

The doors open soon enough, and she could hardly wait. Seeming to smash the floor button a million times, she was a bouncy child when the doors closed, and it began to go up.

As if the time would never go by, the doors are opening before her. Exhaling deeply, she shoves her hands in the pockets of her hoodie, walking down those familiar halls. Her hair flies behind her from the speed of which she was going, her bag flailing back and forth against her side as it hung off her shoulder.

The sight of the apartment number was something she thought she would never see. Raising her first, she knocked about four times, also ringing the doorbell just to be extra annoying.

Although it might have awoken all seven of them, she didn't really care then. Her heart was on her sleeve, and she was more tired than anything from the fact she only slept for a couple of hours.

When there's no response for a minute or two, she held in the urge to just break the door down. Ringing the doorbell one last time, there was finally the sound of it being unlocked.

She already knew who it would be behind it.

"Jinani~!" She screams like a child, probably waking the neighbors. Soomi lunges forward, practically jumping into the bewildered boy before her.

Jinhwan was beyond lost at why she was suddenly at his door at three in the morning. It took him a minute to really process it. He thought everything was ruined after that talk they had. It'd been hours and he still hadn't slept because of his emotions. He'd just laid in his bed staring at the ceiling, wondering if she was still with Jungkook and what they were doing.

But she wasn't with him, because she came to see him. It caused his heart to spiral into a frenzy, a goofy smile taking over his lips as he accepts her embrace.

It was heaven when she breathed in that cologne of his. Jinhwan kicks the door closed with his foot, using one hand to lock it back while the other arm was wrapped around her. She wasn't even paying attention as her head was buried so far in his chest, making her words a mumble that was barely audible.

"I missed you, I missed you, I missed you," she was muttering repeatedly, so much one would think she was possessed. If she wasn't so weak, she would've been hugging the life out of him.

After listening to her go on about how much she missed him for about five minutes, he finally speaks, "Soomi-ah, it's three in the morning."

She didn't move. "Yeah, and?"

"You just showed up on my doorstep with a giant bag in your pajamas and a hoodie."

"Yeah."

Her lack of reply was weird to him. Just hearing the way her voice wasn't as high as usual even made him wonder. Thinking back to what Junhoe had said, he was starting to feel like he really had been onto something.

But Jinhwan was too exhausted to ask and get into the entire conversation then. It was definitely a topic for tomorrow, plus they would be spending most of the day together since the schedule.

"Let's go to bed," he hums, pulling out of the embrace to grab her hand.

This was something she was very happy to hear. She only skips along with him to his bedroom, feeling as if she had no cares in the world even though she had so many.

That was just how Jinhwan made her feel, and it was something she never wanted to stop feeling.

She was already yawning as they're climbing into his cozy bed. The mattress was as soft as she remembered, and she was already passing out as her body sinks into the plush.

Two arms wrap themselves around her, and Jinhwan leaves a lingering kiss on her forehead. "We'll talk tomorrow," he tells her, sounding tired himself.

But by that point, she was already fast asleep in her favorite place.






~




"Yah, hyung, Yunhyeong made breakfast and we have to leave in a few minutes, are you driving yourself or coming with-," Junhoe's calls to the eldest were cut short, he had just about to waltz into his bedroom like he owned the place. His head was just barely through the doorway, when the short boy appeared seemingly out of thin air, quickly pushing him back with all his force into the hall. He hoped he hadn't seen the sights of his best friend sleeping soundly still in his cozy bed, covers all the way over her face so much you could barely see her, and slight, barely audible snores leaving her parted lips. 

However, it was far too late to hide the girl from him, and Jinhwan's attempt at ridding her of anymore stress was already beginning to fail drastically. To the caramel haired boy's dismay, Junhoe always tended to use his height to his greatest advantage, particularly in times like this when his curiosity couldn't be denied. He manages to peek over Jinhwan's head, enough to catch a glimpse of another body in his hyung's bed, just as the boy had managed to successfully push him out of his room and into the hall.

Heaving out a sigh, Jinhwan's face only falls in defeat when Junhoe is already persisting, "was that a girl? Yah, are you seeing someone else?!" He was immediately jumping to conclusions, as Junhoe assumed Soomi was still with Jungkook or just at her apartment. She hadn't been here all of last night when he was awake, and he didn't see her around much with Jinhwan like usual. So he had a bad feeling that maybe Jinhwan was rebounding to other girls, and to be honest, it angered him. 

Although it wasn't like Soomi was innocent when it came to being with other people, but Junhoe didn't actually know how involved she'd gotten with her ex fiance. But he had hope for her to not hurt Jinhwan.

When Jinhwan doesn't answer and only glares holes into his soul, the tall, stubborn boy tries to open the door again to see whoever it was. His frantic attempts surprised Jinhwan, who hurriedly grabbed his wrist, pulling him back and preventing him from touching the handle.

"Who's in there?!" Junhoe snapped in annoyance at the fact his hyung wasn't telling him anything. To think they were supposed to be close friends.

Running a hand down his face in frustration, it takes Jinhwan a few minutes to find any words. He was having a serious debate on if he wanted to tell him it was Soomi or not. Maybe it wasn't his place to be keeping it from Junhoe, after all, she was his best friend, and he'd shown that he cared for her a lot. Never had he seen someone like June care for another person other than himself so much, and he knew he was worried about her.

But Soomi had dealt with a lot, even more than he knew, and he could tell when she showed up last night she was in a lot of distress. 

"I don't have a girl over," the eldest finally deadpans, pushing locks out caramel blonde out of his face and exhaling deeply as he meets June's peering irises.

His eyes, which were filled to the rim with disbelief, and he scoffs, "hyung, do you think I'm an idiot? I saw someone else in your bed."

Resisting the urge to turn and start slamming his head into the wall beside him, Jinhwan groans, using a hand to pull at his hair, a habit he has of doing when he's frustrated, "Yes, it's fucking Soomi," he concedes in a bitter tone.

Junhoe's mouth forms an o as the realization washes over his face in a wave. Pursing his lips in thought for a moment, he's quick to ask, "well, why didn't you let me see her? Is she okay?"

"That thing you said, about her being different and clearly bothered about something, I can see it now," he explains, "I didn't want you to start yelling or something and cause her even more stress."

He meets his friend's eyes once again, only for him to be staring right back at him in complete confusion. "That's rude, I don't give my best friend any stress," he denies, shaking his head. Jinhwan had a bad feeling of what he was going to do with the information he was just given, and he made sure to protectively guard the door of his room just in case he had some devious plan up his sleeve.

This is why he couldn't hide the shock that flooded over his face when June spins on his heel, turning away from him and simply walking back down the hall towards the kitchen, all without another word leaving his lips.

Not having any idea what he was planning, he only calls to him in a stern tone, "Yah, keep it in mind, June-ya!"

But the boy hadn't even turned to look back at him, or even acknowledge his words at all. Jinhwan felt a worried feeling pile in the pit of his stomach, but he forces himself to push it away, turning and going back into his bedroom. He hadn't looked to his bed as he tries to quietly shut his door behind him, before making his way back to the safety of his warm and plush mattress.

Only then did his brown eyes meet the small girl that was sitting on her side of the bed, legs crossed over each other as she peers up at the boy approaching. "Oh, did I wake you up?" he asks in sympathy, a pout on his lips as he figures he did. 

Soomi didn't say anything, she only watched as he slowly climbed onto the bed beside her. Once he was sitting only half a foot away, he took that as her chance to move. Climbing across the mattress, she stopped only when the two weren't even a mere centimeter apart. Their bodies against each other because of how close she was now sitting, she's leaning forward to his side, and he was shocked at the sight and feeling of her small body as she wrapped both her arms around his neck, resting her head on his shoulder.

And still, she didn't say a word as she sat there and cuddled up to him.

In complete confusion, Jinhwan breathes out, "Soomi-ah, are you okay?"

There was no words of reply for a good minute. Not knowing what to do, he remembered how she didn't seem to be feeling well as of recent. With that in mind, he wraps an arm around her, pulling her closer into his body as he rubs her forearm comfortingly.

The reason Soomi was being so quiet was for many reasons. She was busy pondering a lot of things since she woke up to Junhoe's yelling. It was true she'd actually heard the poor attempt of a hushed conversation Junhwan had outside the door in the hall, and she was going to ask Jinhwan about why he wanted to keep Junhoe away. But then she'd heard him tell him, about what he said about her being different recently. 

She guessed they really did notice she wasn't doing her best. 

Although this wasn't a bad thing necessarily, it still made her worried. This did mean that they did know her well enough to read through her faking, but it also meant they were probably going to ask what was going on. Jinhwan already knew nearly the full gist with Jungkook, and so did the other guys - and what they didn't she presumed their eldest filled them in on - but they didn't know what was really happening deep down.
