Not about the psycho that was threatening her life because of her relations with Jungkook.

This meant that when they came to her, she had to tell them. How could she lie to them? She already tried doing it before just so she didn't worry them, but it was becoming too hard for her. It was weighing down her mentality, so much she felt like her soul was bound to cave in. Soomi was terrified, and she didn't know what to do to help it. If this person found out about Jinhwan and the boys of iKON, it could get worse. Not only was she always putting Jungkook in danger, but she could be putting Jinhwan in it, too. 

He was her savior, her bright light she'd always needed to go on, the last thing she ever wanted was that. She didn't know what she would do if she ever ended up threatening Jinhwan's life. Soomi wouldn't be able to live with herself if she got him put in danger. He didn't deserve anything like that, she'd already gave him enough trouble over the last year.

Just thinking about it all, it was enough for her heart to nearly break. Tears formed in her eyes, but she hurriedly buried her face in Jinhwan's chest before he could notice. Her small arms squeeze him tight, basically clinging onto him for life. 

He knew, he knew she was hurting.

But he also knew there was no need for him to pry, at least not then, as her tiny voice mutters in a muffle tone from where she's nuzzled her face in his chest, "I'm okay, Jinani."


35:00 | sickening messages

Not a word.

That was exactly all Soomi had said that entire morning and afternoon, nothing. It was like someone had zipped her lips shut and thrown away the key. They weren't sure if she had just decided to begin a newfound life of being mute, or if she just really wasn't feeling well. Either way, it was bothering more than just Jinhwan at that point, all of them felt like they were bound to go insane.

Even the attempts of that morning hadn't worked. With all the time they had spent together, Junhoe had thought that bringing her breakfast and playing one of her favorite songs would at least get her to smile. It was why he left so quietly, ignoring everything Jinhwan had said to him to retrieve her a large plate of food. And off he'd went back to his hyung's room, bringing the other boys with him after informing them Soomi was here. They'd played music, jumping around like complete idiots. If there had been camera's, it would've completely ruined their idol image, that was for sure.

On the bright side, that morning attempt - despite making the eldest very angry - did work for a while. It didn't bring her back to her usual sarcastic, bossy self, but she did pipe up at least for a bit. Junhoe managed to get a few laughs out of her with his usual stupid jokes on the way to the studio.

But it seemed to all falter again when they got there. Soomi had shoved her hands in the pockets of her jacket, seeming to keep a distance from the seven boys even after they entered the company. Everyone knew it wasn't necessary for her to be so many feet away from them anymore since they were allowed to now hang around each other, but she still looked so visibly uncomfortable.

And she was, even though she was trying so hard to hide it. She knew that they knew something was up, all seven of them, and she didn't know how much longer it was until she was going to have to spill.

Just this thought was enough to drive Soomi insane. Her head felt like it was about to cave in from the stress, and she didn't know how she was gonna keep from just banging her head on the table she sat at, listening to the boys recording their parts for the new song they were making together.

She hadn't gotten much of a chance to help with the song her and the boys were doing. Although she wrote about 90% of her and Nani's song, Hanbin seemed to make the majority of this one himself. 

Shifting in her seat, she tried her best to pay attention to Junhoe, who was recording his vocals for the song. She always loved to listen to him sing, his voice was so unique and had it's own special, raspy charm to it. For a long time now, she felt like he was one of the best vocalists in the whole industry. Just hearing him sing was usually enough to just send her into a happy place.

And just like she'd said, that's exactly what it was doing. The catchy beat of the song only made it better, as Hanbin was busy mixing different sounds to try to make it sound better. She would usually be helping him any other day, and trying to say what would be better where in her opinion, but she didn't have the energy to move a muscle. It was why she just sat, with her chin in her hands, fiddling with her fingers every so often as the voice of Koo Junhoe poured into her ears.




It was the sound of her phone suddenly going off that took her out of her safe place, a small cloud in her head that wasn't gray. A place she had finally gone to where she could just listen to her best friend sing his part for his new song and feel like she didn't actually have as many cares in the world as she really did.

Shifting in her seat, Soomi leans over to pull the phone out of her bag. There were a lot of different people she wouldn't of been surprised to see show up on her screen. Hell, it could've been Jungkook - even though she spoke to him only hours ago when he asked where she went, Jimin, one of the other guys. She wouldn't of even been shocked if it ended up being Rose or one of the girls asking if she wanted to hang out with them, or maybe just one of her other idol friends she didn't see often and needed to catch up with. 

Out of anything and everything, she would've been glad to see any name on her screen, no matter who it had been - anything that wasn't what she saw.

Unknown Number:
Sweet Soomi-ah, I didn't think you would
be a two-timer, looks like you're dirtier than I thought.

Unknown Number:
So, you do like to play, huh? Good to know.
I hope you're in for a real dirty game then.

She chokes on the lump that forms in her throat. This causes her to start coughing up a storm, causing the other boys who had been focusing on the music to pause to look at her in worry. Trying to stop her mess of a coughing fit, she covers her mouth with one hand, using the other to dismissively wave to them, trying to insist she was okay.

And so none of them thought that it was because of her phone, she makes sure to turn it off and tuck it away back in her bag. The best thing to do was ignore it, right? She didn't know what she should do, but it was at least what she thought was right.

Junhoe had been done with his part for a while now, and the one who had been in the middle of recording was Jinhwan. Only then did she notice his absence beside her, when he'd stopped singing mid-verse, seeing she was having trouble. Without a second thought, he rips the headphones off his head even though he had been recording so smoothly.

No one really cared he ruined his perfect part, though, as the eldest rushed out of the recording room and back into the booth where they all sat. Junhoe was to Soomi's left, patting her back to try to help her, and in another second, Jinhwan was to her right.

"Yah, one of you get her water, now!" he demands in a booming tone to the others, and Donghyuk is the first who processes, before rushing out of the room.

Trying to contain herself, her coughs were beginning to get better. She wasn't sure how such shock had prompted her to get such a way, but it was no doubt painful as she rubs her throat with one hand. The coughs were still there, but it wasn't a vigorous fit anymore.

Blinking down at her from his usual outstanding height, Junhoe rubs her back as he queries, "are you good now?"

Jinhwan seemed like he was about to explode from worry as he wraps both his arms around her, causing Junhoe's arm to return to his side as the boy pulls the girl into his embrace. Soomi didn't deny his movement, only sinking into it as she coughs a few more times.

"If you're getting sick, I can take you back to the dorm," Jinhwan was lecturing as his grip tightens around her like a protective parent, "you shouldn't be working when you're sick."

By then, Donghyuk was walking back into the room with water, to which Soomi gratefully takes. She'd practically chugged the entire bottle, before letting out deep heaves of breaths to make sure the coughing was gone. 

Once she felt like she was alright, she demurs in a small voice, "no, no, I'm okay, I feel fine."

Seeing right through it, Jinhwan frowns, "you do not feel fine," his stern tone takes her off guard for a second. She remembered then the conversation she overheard between him and June.

Sitting up straight again, Jinhwan's grip on her loosens. "I'm fine, Nani, we need to finish recording by tomorrow," she reminds everyone in hope they would let her whole coughing mess go. 

The others mutter noises of surprise at her words, seeming to have forgotten about it. They really did have to have their song finished by tomorrow, especially if they wanted to perform it at the next award show.

"You sure you're alright?" Hanbin confirms, raising a brow at her. When Soomi only nods, sending him a small smile of reassurement, he sighs, spinning back around in his chair, "alright, then let's finish this song, come on hyung."

He'd motioned for Jinhwan to go finish recording his part. The caramel boy didn't look happy about it at all, as he was the only one who hadn't wanted to continue the song and believed that she was okay. Although Junhoe and even the others knew something was up, they all didn't want to force her to tell them if it seemed like she didn't want to. Jinhwan had been that way as well, but he was beginning to grow so worried it was making him nauseous. Whatever it was, he wanted to help even if it just meant comforting her, so why wouldn't she tell him?

Knowing now wasn't the time, he could only exhale deeply as he rises to his feet, making his way back into the recording booth.





~





After a long four hours, the song was finally finished. This meant that Soomi and Jinhwan's duet and the featuring song with iKON was done. After checking in with their managers and sending it off to be reviewed by the CEO, they felt relieved. It was a job, but they finally finished at least that part.

All that was left was the choreo for the song to be made. That required the CEO to like the song first, though, so at least for the time being they had some breathing room.

Well, all of them except for Soomi.

In fact, she was beginning to feel like she was breathing too hard. That text she got was stuck in her mind, and every time her phone went off, she got anxious that she was getting another from the creepy stranger. 

The boys were busy laughing and dancing around to music to really pay much attention. Jinhwan was even dancing around with them, as it seemed like they were filming some sort of vlogging video for their channel. It meant they had to keep Soomi out of the frame, which was why they hadn't acknowledged her much.

That wasn't much of a problem to her, really. It meant she could keep from them asking her anything she wasn't sure she wanted to answer. The thing was, she felt like her thoughts were about to swallow her whole.

It was when a certain song started playing that she was snapped once again out of her overwhelming mind.

Put your hands up, here and there
Everyone come here and shake it
Let's all dance together
Now you shake it too

Even though it wasn't actually that long ago, the song sent a feeling of nostalgia up her spine. She looked up from where she was playing with her hands in her lap again to see the boys jumping around to the song, the camera nowhere in sight. They were singing out the words, and Junhoe was trying to mock the choreography, that was genuinely pretty difficult. She found herself managing to giggle as she sees her face appear on the computer screen it played from, and she shook her head as she recalled shooting that video.

Lay everything down here
Let it go, hurry, come and follow me

The song continues, and Jinhwan is a bundle of joy as he walks over to grab her hands, forcing her up from the seat she sat so comfortably on. He's singing the words as he spins her around, all while she's laughing at how he's trying to match her tone of voice in the song.

I have nothing to hide
I have nothing more to avoid
I don't think about anything else
This moment right here is what's important

She could feel her own song speaking directly to her. And it caused her to momentarily feel a bit lost. If only she could go back to when she really meant those lyrics she sang.

Maybe she could.

"Seriously, how do you do that thing with your stomach? I always feel like you're having a seizure," Junhoe is saying as the chorus plays, and he does the hand movement before trying to do the dance. Due to the fact he was completely clueless on how it was actually done, he ended up just looking like he was trying to thrust the air.

Everyone was in fits of snickers at how stupid he looked. It caused him to stop the dance, throwing his hands in the air out of exasperation, "fuck all y'all, I dance it better than Soomi."

"You weren't even doing it right," Yunhyeong is shaking his head, clear disappointment written all over his face.

Being that Soomi had now joined the crowd of boys jumping around and dancing to music, she clears her throat to get their attention. Jinhwan's warm hand was still in hers, calming her nerves for the time being. "June, you have to use your legs," she informs him casually, and his eyes snap to her, wide with misunderstanding.

Resuming the pose she did for the dance, he tries to figure out what it is she meant. "My legs? How the fuck do I use my legs to do that?!" he looked like he was trying to do rocket science as he looks down at his body, trying as hard as he could to mirror what Soomi was doing in the video. 

But he was still completely off. It looked like he was trying to hump a cow, and she covered her hands with one face to shield from the nightmare, waving the other vigorously in the air, telling him to stop.

"It's definitely not like that," she giggles when Junhoe had finally stopped whatever idiotic thing he was doing. She didn't know if he was being dumb that way on purpose to make her feel better, or if he was just really that lost in how she did that dance move.

Crossing his arms over his chest, her question would never be answered as he scoffs in a fake bitter tone, "well, why don't you do it then? Since you're so high and mighty."

"Why would she not be? It's literally her song," Donghyuk was correcting blatantly, earning a glare from Junhoe.

So, for the next fifteen minutes, you would find the eight of them watching Soomi as she tried to teach Junhoe the complicated dance move from her hit single. It was a technique of dance that took her long to learn, requiring you to have a scary amount of control over your muscles, meaning that she wasn't really surprised he couldn't do it. Especially being that the boy was a whopping six feet tall, and even if he did do the dance correctly, it would probably still look silly because of his height.

"That's close enough!" she's crying over her laughter, bending over and hugging her stomach that was aching from all of the laughing. Junhoe was sweating at this point, his hair a bit messier than usual, and he had some weird look on his face like he was possessed as he tried to mimic the dance she showed him one last time. 

Hanbin had his face in his hands from the spinning chair he still sat in, the cringe too serious for him to handle anymore. He knew Junhoe was the equivalent of a clown, but sometimes it still blew his mind. 

The others were basically having the same reaction. There was clear shame on their faces thanks to their fellow brother's embarrassing performance, but they couldn't help but laugh at him because of it. It left their stomachs hurting from them being out of breath, as they all take seats again on the booth against the walls of the recording studio.

With that escapade finally ending, Soomi is honestly a little tired from having to show Junhoe the entire dance. The presence of Jinhwan next to her is enough to cause a small smile to form on her lips, even through everything she was glad he was next to her. She shifts her seating position to lay her head on his shoulder, closing her eyes for a mere second to just take the moment when his arm goes around her shoulders.

"Are you feeling okay now?" his concerned tone knocks her back into the real world. Her euphoria seemed to melt as she was reminded of what he was talking about, and it causes that same lump to form in her throat again.

Clearing her throat, she's quick to assure him, "Yeah, I'm good, Jinani."

This was a lie, but she tried her best to hide that fact. I can't worry him more, she thought, knowing the fact that her odd demeanor recently already was.

Her phone dings in her pocket, cutting off the conversation the two had been having. Soomi's attention diverts from Jinhwan as she sits up straight to pull her cellular device out of her back pocket. Pushing her hair out of her face, there's an anxious pit that forms in her stomach as she dares herself to turn on the screen.

Surely, they won't text me again, not today, she tries to assure herself. She just wanted to believe that everything would be okay, somehow, even if it was impossible.

Unknown Number:

        


Unknown Number:
You're so cute when you sleep,
but I think you'd be even cuter dead.














36:00 | coming clean

No, no, no, her mind is chanting the word in a rapid patten at ludicrous speeds. Her heart flails right out of her chest from beating so fast, falling onto the floor she stood on right along with her sanity.

Suddenly, she didn't remember if she was even talking to Jinhwan only seconds ago. It was like everything in her brain had been erased from the fear and stress that piled up in her mind. Her hands become sweaty, her entire body began to tremble with petrification, and she could feel tears welling up in her eyes.

That picture, she couldn't recall when it was from. It had to be one of the nights she'd been there herself or without Jinhwan or even Jungkook. It had been one of the nights she was alone, except for the fact this insane human had been in her apartment right there with her — all without her knowing. They could've done whatever they wanted to, they'd been breathing right down her neck and she had no idea.

Due to the bile she could feel coming up her throat and all the other feelings, her hand grows limp. With a sharp crack, her phone had fell to the wooden floor below her, but Soomi didn't even process the fact it fell. She was too busy holding in the urge to puke, trying to stop her abnormal sweating as he holds both hands to her forehead.

Say it's not real, someone tell me it's a sick joke, some sort of prank. But it wasn't, this person was serious, and they could get into her own home whenever they pleased.

They wanted to kill her, and they knew about Jinhwan.

She couldn't take it. How could she live with herself if she was going to live even at all? How could she know she's putting the boy she loved, both of them, in such immense danger?

Every waking second, they could make a move.

The sudden feeling of someone grabbing her wrist harshly nearly causes her to scream. Only then did she feel the wet tears streaming down her face, ruining her makeup in the process for sure. And she hurriedly uses her free hand to wipe them away, trying to stop the obvious anxiety attack she was beginning to have as she tried to calm her body.

But she was trembling with fear, her heart was beating at an irregular pace, and she felt like everything around her was about to collapse. Soomi wasn't someone with known anxiety at all, this was the first time she had ever felt anything like it in her life.

It had to be one of the worst things she'd ever felt.

"J-Jinhwan," she breathes out his name, her voice coming out barely audible. She was breathing so hard it sounded like she was just running a marathon, and she only felt a lump form in her throat at the sight of the boy that was dragging her somewhere unknown. Her wrist was beginning to hurt from his grip, but he didn't seem to be paying attention to that factor as they make their way through the empty halls.

Just with the way he was walking, you could tell he was angry. Jinhwan was angered by his concern for the girl he loved. He couldn't watch her hurt anymore and not do anything about it. It was time for her to come clean.

What would it do for Soomi? She was just going to worry him even more. After this, she didn't even know how she would begin to tell him.

But she realizes she won't have a choice either way once her eyes land on the caramel boy's other hand that wasn't dragging her. No doubt, he was holding her phone with the incriminating texts in it.

Not a word leaves his lips as they reach the iKON dance practice room, somewhere they could speak in private. Jinhwan had practically shoved Soomi inside, slamming and locking the door behind them.

"Jina—," she tries to voice out again, but his name is cut off from leaving her lips when her back is suddenly pushed against the wall.

Eyes wide, she still wasn't genuinely calm from the panic attack she had only moments ago. Her entire body was still a shaking mess, and Jinhwan knew this as he narrows his eyes at her, bending down a bit to meet her eye level. Those brown irises peer so softly into hers, and she could see the hurt in his eyes as he stared into her own green orbs.

With his arms on either side of her, trapping her in his grasp, she had nowhere left to go. There was nothing she could hide now.

"Soomi, please tell me what's going on," despite his apparent anger and frustration, he didn't yell or even raise his voice. In fact, his tone was that of a mice when he called out pleadingly. It made her eyes divert to the ground from how sincerely concerned he sounded, she couldn't face him.

When she says saying for a minute or two, instead just staring at her feet, he cries out again, "please — I can't see you like this. I don't want to look through your phone without your permission, but I will if I have to, if you won't talk to me."

Still, she said nothing, she didn't know what to say. Soomi didn't know where to begin. She felt like she suddenly could no longer form human sentences, like someone had taken her speaking capabilities. She could only stand there, head down as tears streamed down her face, dripping onto the wood floor below them.

Jinhwan was beyond upset. She knew that. This was made especially clear when he stands up straight from having been leaning forward, trapping her in his grasp. His arm falls back to his side as he uses his other to pull her phone out of his back pocket. He had no other choice now.

At the sight of him opening her phone, Soomi's eyes fall out of their sockets. Lunging forward, she flew into his chest as her arm reaches out at an attempt to snatch her phone back.

Jinhwan manages to move and hold it out of her grasp. All the while, his eyes carefully stare at the screen, taking in everything he was seeing.

And when they did, he couldn't believe it. He didn't want to believe it.

"Oh my god," his mouth is open in shock. A part of him wanted to cry, to cry from the fact he couldn't believe someone would threaten Soomi like this, his Soomi like this. How could someone be so sick to sneak into her own home and take pictures of her like that? But another part of him, was fueled with so much anger, he wasn't afraid to commit murder himself.

And he would. Jinhwan was going to find whoever had been putting her through this, and he'd kill them.

"Soomi, I'm so fucking sorry," his words crack towards the end, and he shoves her phone away in his pocket again, not wanting her to even touch it anymore. Stepping forward, he engulfs the small girl in his embrace, pulling her shaking body into him. "I'll find them, I fucking promise you, I won't let anything happen to you ever."

Just hearing his words was enough to calm her, even if it was a little. Her entire body was still beyond afraid, however, so much so all she knew to do was wrap her arms around his waist, burying her face in his t-shirt as she sobs. She didn't care that her cries were loud, or ugly, or even annoying, she'd been bottling up emotions for too long. It had gotten too far, it wasn't just a distant issue anymore, it was a living nightmare she couldn't live any longer. No sex would make it better this time, nothing could make her ignore the fact this person had access to her own home, all of her things, at their own will. There was no telling how many times they'd come into her house when she was by herself.

Just the thought makes her cry harder. Jinhwan hugs her so tight, she could almost suffocate from his grip on her. But then, it was just what she needed to slightly make it all better, even if it was momentarily.

He kept apologizing and saying everything would be okay. Really, she wished she could believe him. Jinhwan was such a sweetheart, she loved him more than she could begin to explain. This happening was only making her realize that more. But for once, his words felt rather empty to her.

What could either of them really do? How could they find them?

"I'm so scared, Jinani," she whined into his chest, voice a muffled mess of emotion. She knew he didn't really know the full story, he didn't know why.

"Soo-," he begins to say, but she only lets the first syllable leave his mouth before she speaks again.

Standing up straight so he could hear her clearly, his arms don't leave her as she sighs, "i-it's been going on ever since I was engaged, that's when it started," Jinhwan seems to have a knowing look on his face. She remembered then, the boys and him knew this much from the fight they had that time. "Jungkook had been the one getting the threats, it's why he broke it off, and I didn't receive anything since then," her voice trembles every few words, but she forces herself to go on, to tell the truth, "the first one was when I was FaceTiming you, and I told you I had to go so suddenly and we didn't talk for a while. The night I invited you over and Jungkook was there, that's why. He was the only one who knew about it, I didn't want to worry anyone else so I called him because he seemed like the only one I could talk to."

He opens his mouth to say something again, but she doesn't let him. "Then just not long ago, I started getting texts. The first thing was a note stabbed into my door with a knife, but now they have my number, and they've been in my apartment. I'm trying to focus on the shows coming up and our music but I can't when this person is threatening my life," she's in tears again, barely being able to speak as they wash down her cheeks, "t-they could've killed me already if they wanted to, I-I could be dead-," unable to hear anymore, Jinhwan abruptly shushes her, pulling her into his chest again.

"Stop," he demands harshly, "don't ever say that."

Balling his shirt in her fists, she pulls him closer as if it was even possible. Her nostrils inhale his scent what seems like a million times, afraid it was the last time she would be able to.

"I'm sorry," she muttered after a few seconds, "I just - I don't know what to do."

"Firstly," Jinhwan sighs, "you aren't living in that apartment anymore."

Soomi stands up again to peer up at Jinhwan in confusion. "What?" she breathes out in question, even though it wasn't hard to comprehend what he was hinting at.

"I'm taking you back with the guys, and we're packing your bags," he had no intention of giving her any sort of choice as he informs her of this, "you're moving in with us, and I will never let you leave my sight," his stern tone pings her heart as he stares down at her with glowing eyes, "and if you get another text, tell me immediately, alright?"

She was happy with his demands. The last thing Soomi wanted was to ever be alone anymore.

"Okay," she mumbled, not having the strength to say anything else.

Jinhwan doesn't either for a long while, he only hugs her as tight as he could manage. His chin rests atop her head from the fact he still managed to be taller than her, and he pauses the action to leave a lingering kiss on her forehead.

"We don't have to go back there tonight," he says after a while, "we can go tomorrow or whenever you're ready."

She only nods in his chest, not wanting to speak. All she wanted in that moment was him, not sexually, but just him. Soomi just wanted his presence, to feel the up and down heaving of his chest as he breathes against her, to know he was there with her and not going anywhere. The feeling that Jinhwan was holding her was enough to make her feel stronger.

"We can go tonight."

He hadn't expected to hear her say such. Jinhwan thought it would be a while before she felt up to going back to the apartment.

"Are you sure?"

"Yeah."

Pursing his lips, there was no point in questioning her, "okay, if you're ready."

"Yeah," she repeats, finally pulling out of his everlasting embrace. His hand finds it's way back to hers, and she feels calmed by the warmth of it in her own. Facing the door to the iKON dance studio, she uses her free hand to fix her messy hair that was falling in her face, "let's go."

Jinhwan only mutters an okay, leading the way with her behind him. Neither of them had any intention of letting their hands go, even though they were about to walk openly in the hallway of YG studios. He meant it when he said he was never letting her leave his sight, especially now that he knew what was going on.

"Soomi-ah," he suddenly halts his movement just as he's about to open the door. Soomi nearly crashes into his chest from not expecting him to do such, and her green eyes inquisitively meet his own.

"I love you, don't forget that, okay?"












~



Junhoe:
Hyung is Soomi ok? I think I have an idea
of what could be wrong

Junhoe:
I completely forgot about what she told us
during that fight you had…


The boys are stunned to see the both of them entering the room with such calm expressions. Especially Soomi, since last they checked she seemed to be having some problems recently. Everyone was worried about her and her seemingly depleting mental state. With so many shows still ahead of them, they feared she wouldn't be able to do everything she was expected of.

They'd noticed her horrible state when the eldest had dragged her out of the room. Junhoe had desperately wanted to follow them and see what was going on, but the others held him back. "It's for them to handle right now, June-ya!" Yunhyeong had snapped at him like the knowing grandfather he acted like.

But they were all beyond curious too. The last thing any of the boys of iKON could think about was music. It was why the sound of the two entering the recording studio once again caused all of them to immediately jump to their feet from surprise.

"Soo, are you alright?!" Donghyuk is the first one who rushes forward, and Soomi was confused at how worried they all looked. She didn't think they had noticed her, but she guessed the boys paid more attention than she gave them credit for.

"You seem so stressed recently, I think you need to take some time off," Yunhyeong lectures, stepping forward to hold a hand to her forehead as if to check her temperature.

The small girl only allows him to do so, a blank look on her face. She wasn't sick, she was just having some serious life issues she couldn't mentally handle. The truth was, sometimes things get too overwhelming, even for an idol star who's seemed to already take on so much.

But never did she imagine she would have to deal with something like this.

"I'm oka—," her reassuring words are cut off when a certain someone steps forward in the crowd of seven boys. Soomi nearly faints from shock when Junhoe casually wraps his arms around her, pulling her into one of the warmest, most comforting hugs she'd ever experienced.

It warmed her heart.

"You're my best friend, Soo," he'd whispered in her ear, but the boys could probably still hear his sappy words since they were so close in proximity, "I know something is bothering you, please don't hesitate to come to me."

It was obvious June was trying to make it look like he was just telling her a joke or something of the sort. He wasn't the type of guy who liked to casually out his feelings, especially not around the guys. It would give them something to tease and make fun of him for, which would ruin the fact he did that to everyone else.

However, it was clear most of them still heard what he'd said to her, as grins spread over their lips and they start snickering to each other.

But Junhoe wasn't paying attention to them as he pulls away. "I will, June-ya," she pats his shoulder, which was actually quite the reach for her, "I actually have to talk to all of you," her voice grows louder by this statement. Everyone's ears pipe up from hearing these words, and Soomi could already feel the lump forming in her throat as she recalls in her head what she's bound to tell them.

But they had to know, especially if she was moving in with them. They had to know they needed to be cautious and watch out for any signs of someone breaking and entering in their home.

Most importantly, she needed to make sure they were even okay with it. Sure, they were all basically family at this point in time, and everyone knows family is made to stand by each other. But at the same time, Soomi was putting their lives in serious danger. They might not want that.

As she meets the eyes of the six boys still in the dark, Jinhwan's hand comfortingly slips back into hers. A feeling of safety creeps it's way up her spine, one she hadn't felt in a while since all of this. It gives her the strength to clear her throat, "s-so you guys remember that night I told you about my engagement?"















37:00 | ignorance is bliss

On such a once terrifying, eery night, there was one thing Soomi could admit by the end of it; she was more than extremely thankful for friends like the boys of iKON. They'd sit and listened to each and every word she spoke intently, allowing her to finish and not interrupting, even when she stumbled over her words because of the anxiety it gave her. Recalling what was going on and explaining it in such depth for them wasn't easy for her, she was horrified to think about it, nonetheless openly talk about it. 


But they listened, and comforted her when she was done. They'd all gathered around in a circle in the recording room of the studio, practically suffocating her with the enormous group hug.


All the while, Jinhwan never left her side, his hand remained in hers the entire time. 


"You can stay with us as long as you like, Soomi," Hanbin had been saying, there was an evident look of frustration in his voice. One might get confused that he was mad at Soomi, but such an assumption was very far from the truth. In reality, Hanbin was pissed at whoever it was doing this to her. Soomi had always been such a strong, sweet girl, and he couldn't believe someone could have the audacity to spend their life making such disgusting threats. "We'll be by your side always, alright?" he pats her shoulder reassuringly, and she only offered him a curt nod and a small smile of thanks.


The guys were all gathering their things to prepare to head to her apartment so she could gather her things. It'd been a long day of hard work, and an even longer one after the news they'd just heard. Despite how the threats were getting scarier and more vulgar, Soomi felt the tiniest weight being lifted off her shoulders now that the boys weren't in the dark anymore. She was afraid they were going to call her crazy, or even just tell her they didn't want to be involved in something like that. She didn't know what she would've done, then.


Well, she always had Jungkook and the others. But they weren't able to be by her side constantly with their massively busy schedule. Not to mention, the boys of iKON could be with her at the company since they were both under YG.


The thought of Jungkook spikes a feeling in her heart. She realized she hadn't talked to him in a while after she'd informed him that she was getting more texts. I hope I didn't worry him.


Suddenly, Soomi's thoughts are cut short by the feeling of someone wrapping their arms around her. Naturally, her first instinct told her it was Jinhwan. That was, until she realized how much taller the said person was than her.


"Yah," her voice is a mere mutter as she calls out in confusion, trying to figure out what in the world this boy was doing now.


Nearly squeezing her to death, her best friend was a different person from his usual obnoxious state, "Soomi-ah, I'm going to kill whoever is doing this to you," Junhoe informs confidently, his arms firmly around her tiny body. She thought she was going to explode from how tight his grip was on her, as she tries to wiggle around ever so slightly for comfort.


"Well, that's a sight you don't see everyday," Bobby blatantly states as soon as everyone else noticed what was going on.


After a moment, Soomi accepts her best friend's hug in hopes he would stop squeezing her petite figure so hard. 


"I think Junhoe's really taking this all to heart," Yunhyeong opines a second later, throwing his bag over his shoulder as he joins the others in the middle of the room, "as he should, this is a serious matter. We should all be watching Soomi like hawks."


Soomi tried to talk to tell Yun that it wasn't necessary for them to be that protective of her. Jinhwan was already going to be breathing down her neck, which was a blessing but also a curse in a way. It wasn't that this entire thing wasn't serious to her, it was more than serious, but she didn't want to lose every ounce of privacy she already barely had.


However, the poor girl was unable to speak at this point. She was astonished and grateful for her best friend's sudden desire to show affection, but she didn't want to die from it. 


Seeming to either grow jealous of the sight or realizing she was dying from his grip, Jinhwan had stepped forward to snap, "yah, let her go already, you're going to pop her lungs at this rate."


It was a relief to be out of his mad hug, and it took her a moment to catch her breathe. Jinhwan had glared at Junhoe, giving him a look of complete confusion as to his change in demeanor but yet annoyance from his actions. "What? I'm just worried about Soomi," he'd shrugged as if he wasn't any different than usual, turning around to pick up his own bag.


Shaking his head, Jinhwan gathered his own bag and Soomi's, before taking her hand. "Pabo," he'd been mumbling moments later as they were all leaving the studio.


They'd all stayed so late that there weren't really any staffs left. Therefore, it seemed okay for the two of them to be holding hands, at least that's what they thought. 


Even then, with the events that had taken place that day, neither of them really cared anyway. Soomi was craving the comfort of the boy she adored, and Jinhwan was desperate to give her it.




~

jungkook :/

Kookie:
yeah, what is it?


Jungkook had been staring down at his phone screen for minutes. Those said minutes turned into many hours, and he was still there, checking his phone every once in a while while he tried to distract himself with video games or other activities. She still wasn't replying, and seeing that she had yet to even read his text was beginning to make him feel sick.


What else could they have told her? This mystery sick piece of shit had yet to text him. He wished they would petrify him instead, even if it leads him to do what he did the first time, even though it lead to him hurting her emotionally. At least that way, she was safe.


She didn't deserve it, and knowing it was all his fault was enough to make Jungkook go overboard.


Kookie:

Kookie:
what did they say? 


Kookie:
where'd you go?


Kookie:
yah, this isn't funny. don't make me
come to your apartment.


Kookie:
alright, that's it


Kookie:
i'm on my way now


He felt like he was bound to be sick. Truthfully, it would be a blessing if it all ended up being some sort of sick joke. Jungkook wasn't sure if he even wanted to know what it was they said along with the picture. It was probably something that would definitely send him further than over the edge, because just the thought of what it could be had him clenching his fists.


Due to the fact it wasn't legal in their country, he didn't own any weapons like guns. The only thing Jungkook ever had to protect himself was a simple pocket knife. It was what he shoved in his pocket as he grabbed his jacket, putting it over his shoulders as quickly as he could manage.


If she wasn't replying, it had to be for a good reason, right? How could she just leave in the middle of something so serious?


Like always since this whole escapade of nightmares, poor Jungkook could only ever think of the worst. His heart was already breaking at the thought of anyone touching Soomi, his Soomi. 


He wouldn't let it happen.


Leaving his bedroom, he's practically running to the entrance of the boys dorm. The maknae was hoping to slip out and lock the door quietly without a trace. He didn't feel like explaining to the others what he was even doing in case they would see through his lies due to how nervous he probably looked. The undeniable worry was eating him alive, and he needed to get to her apartment as fast as possible. 


Fuck, he's cursing mentally as he's opening the front door, I said I wouldn't leave her alone, I said I'd protect her. And it was true that he tried. Hell, he didn't think Soomi would get up and leave the night she stayed over. It made him think he really should've just carried her to his room, then he would've been able to stop her, to keep her safe. Jungkook wanted to keep an eye on her at all times, he didn't want that piece of shit to ever get with in mere miles of her.


But when she left, he knew where she went. Jimin had confirmed the fact that she went to see her other friends, the boys of iKON(he didn't know what else to call them.) The thing was, they could leave her alone when she shouldn't be, and it didn't mean she was even staying with them. What if Soomi went somewhere by herself and underestimated whoever this stranger really was? It wasn't like her friends understood the danger, they didn't have any clue like she said. 


Even then, Jimin had said that Jinhwan guy was apparently so great, that he would keep her safe and he shouldn't be so worried. But after this, Jimin's words were as good as garbage. That was complete bullshit.


Just when he's closing the door, that exact voice he wasn't looking to hear then was exactly what cut through the silence. A light is turned on in the empty living room, illuminating the youngest that was just leaving the dorm.


There's a knowing look in Jimin's eyes as he sees who it was leaving, but he still felt the need to voice the question, "Jungkook, where are you going?"


Halting his action, he stands with the door half open, "do you really need to know?" he retorts back with a question, his tone dripping of the frustration and impatience he was currently feeling.


Crossing his arms over his chest, his hyung was closer to where he was just outside the entryway. "Seriously, Jungkook? I told you, she was with that Jinhwan guy, you shouldn't be intruding and stressing her out anymore. She's safe with him."


There was no word to describe how angry hearing him say that made Jungkook. Clenching a fist at his side, the other that was holding the door knob gripped it so hard he thought it was going to fall off of the door. With flaming eyes, there's venom in his next fume, "bullshit, she's not fucking fine, that dumb ass has no idea what she's even going through, and you think she's safe?"


Despite his loud, pissed-off tone, Jimin remained completely calm. If anything, the only emotion that laid apparent in his voice was pure disappointment, "can you give it up already? If you keep acting like this, she's not going to choose you."


He could only gawk at his hyung in awe.


"Really, you think that's what this is about?" the bitter laugh flows out his mouth, and he scoffs, "I don't give a fuck about that right now, at least I know I'm a candidate, you can't say the same, can you?"


That struck a nerve, Jimin couldn't lie and say otherwise. But he had to remain normal in front of the maknae. If anything, seeing that it bothered him was only what Jungkook fueled off of. And he had another mission right now.


Sighing, he shakes his head, deciding it was best to try to be as civil as he could since Jungkook already seemed so angry, "well, is there a reason you're running over there? Did she call you?"


Even though his approach was polite, Jungkook wasn't in the mood or falling for it. She could be in trouble as they spoke, and he still wasn't there.


"I don't have time for this," is all he snaps, spinning on his heel and slamming the front door shut, all before Jimin could utter another word to stop him.


He listened to his footsteps down the hall outside, and could only shake his head in disappointment. Sometimes he really felt like Jungkook had a temper problem, but only when it came to Soomi. His infatuation with her was honestly scary.


But it wasn't like he had much room to talk.


Still, he knew there had to be some deeper reason for him to waltz out like that, especially so mad. If he just wanted to see her, he wouldn't of been so pissed off about it. There was something going on.


Pulling his phone out of his back pocket, he rushes to his best friend's contact.


Chim:
Are you alright? Jungkook rushed out of
the dorm to go see you. If something happened,
please tell me, I worry about you, Mi.


Knowing that if she was busy or something of the sort, it would be a while for her to reply. Even then, Soomi wasn't the fastest replying person. It usually took her a while, and he was okay with that. Unlike Jungkook, Jimin didn't have the patience of a child.


Falling onto the plushy couch in the living room, he could only stare down at his phone screen where he'd just sent such a text. A part of him was overly worried about her just by seeing the way Kook was. But knowing him, he overreacted a lot and got protective. For all he knew, it could be nothing, Soomi could simply just be safe in her bed asleep right now.


But he did seem rather on edge. The only thing it reminded him of was what Soomi had told him about the person who was threatening her. However, she'd said nothing much else had happened with that last time they talked.


Maybe that changed, but he could only hope such wasn't the case.


Jimin wasn't going to go barge into her apartment like the youngest, though. He wasn't that type of person, and it was bad enough he feared being a nuisance. It was why all he knew to do in such a situation was wait.










~








"Yah, Kim Soomi, are you in there? Open the door!" his fist slams against the white wood of the apartment door once again. It had to be the fifth or sixth time he was knocking and ringing the door bell. From the hole in the door, you could see that there were lights on. Knowing her, she didn't leave them on if she wasn't home, which only meant that she was.


And from all his commotion, there was no way she wouldn't know it was him. So why wasn't she opening the door?


"Soomi-ah, I drove all the way here for you!" he calls again, knocking once more in hope she would just let him in. Jungkook didn't feel like driving all the way back to his dorm, especially without making sure she was okay first.


When there's still no reply, he couldn't help but pout. What was up with her? She hadn't done this to him, not since they became close again. Soomi wasn't the type to just ignore you like this. She especially wouldn't ignore Jungkook.


Unless she was in there with that Jinhwan guy. The thought made him wince. He hoped to God that wasn't the case, even though it would mean she was 'safe,' he hated the thought of them together despite him acting like it was okay.


Closing one eye, he peeks into the hole in the door again. It didn't really provide you with any sight since he wasn't on the inside. However, there was the smallest amount you could make out if you squinted hard enough.


It was exactly what he did, he squinted until his eye was bound to roll back in his head. 


And he swore he saw someone walking around, possibly going into the kitchen. It was only confirmed when he pressed his ear to the door, hearing a bunch of rustling sounds of stuff being moved around.


Now he knew for sure she was there, and ignoring him. The only excuse she had now was that she had air pods in.


"Soomi, I'm going to break this door if you don't open it," he whined like a puppy. It was always Soomi's weak spot, there was no way she could ignore it if she was actually listening.


He got no reply of words from the other side of the door, though. All he was met with was more rustling sounds.


Jungkook couldn't help but frown. Now he was really feeling a bit hurt - then again, unless that wasn't her.


He hadn't really thought about it until then. She had said that this person somehow managed to get access to her home, but why would they bother going there when she wasn't there? After all, Soomi was what they were after, was she not? 


Spinning on his heel away from the door, he pulls out his phone. Inputting the pass code, his fingers were quick to dig through his contacts and find her name. If she wasn't here and that was the person inside her apartment, then he needed to know what they were doing in there. Better yet, it was his singular chance to find out if this was someone either of them knew, or simply just some psychopath. 


Either way, he made it his mission to get this person so they could be locked up, unable to hurt people he loved.


Mayhaps this would've been possible for Jungkook, he was quite smart when he wanted to be, and he tended to be able to take care of himself.


If only he wouldn't of turned away from the front door.









38:00 | how to relieve stress

"This building is starting to feel a lot creepier now," Chan shares as they're walking down the hall that Soomi's apartment was located on. Soomi, Jinhwan, Donghyuk, and Junhoe all walked in the front row, with the other four behind. Everyone was practically crowded around Soo like she was some sort of prized possession, as they really meant it when they said they were never taking an eye off her.

She'd be more mortified than she already was if she didn't have the seven of them by her side, not to mention Jinhwan's arm around her shoulder. The scent of his cologne was the reason for her sanity, but she still couldn't help but shudder from Chan's words, "no kidding, I hate this already."

"Don't worry, if this psycho is there, we'll catch them," Donghyuk assures her with that warm smile he was known for, "then they can be put away for good."

"Put away? I'm going to fucking kill them," Jinhwan's bitter correction didn't go unnoticed by the squad. He was definitely upset about all of it, but so were the others. Either way, they couldn't help but feel bad for the state it was putting their poor eldest in.

Step by step, they draw closer to the apartment she called home. After this, now that the thought that this complete mental patient had access to her place freely at their will, that they had been watching her all without her knowing — it wasn't feeling much like home anymore. On the other hand, such a place still held so many special memories for her. She hated that she couldn't feel safe in it anymore.

The anxiety was crawling up her spine like ants, and not only Jinhwan, but the others seem to catch onto this. Junhoe links his arm with Soomi, and Jinhwan removes his arm from her shoulder to hold her hand instead. With either of them on her side, she told herself everything would be fine.

After all, she didn't only have them, she had all seven of the boys with her. They were practically invincible together. Everything would be fine as long as she had them.

As they walk down the hall, it felt longer than it'd ever been. It was like the length had multiplied, and the closer they got, the heavier her legs began to feel.

Only then, in the time where she had space to think in her head from lack of commotion, did she recall the fact she didn't text Jungkook back since Jinhwan had taken her phone. She wondered how he was doing. It was odd that the mystery psycho had yet to contact him and was only messing with her. But she guessed they did it to him the first time, so it must be her turn.

Her body shivers at the thought. How could someone be so sick?

"It feels like we've been walking for years," Bobby was saying as he's humming one of their songs. His humming was actually rather calming, so much that it left a feeling of relaxation in her rather shaky veins.

No one said anything to his comment. Instead, some of the others tried to make small talk to pass time. Maybe they were just walking incredibly slow because they were all actually pretty worried, or mayhaps it was the fact the hall was always long and they were just now acknowledging such.

Eventually, it felt like a millennia until Soomi's front door was coming into view, located at the very end of the hall.

"Finally!" Chanwoo had cried out like he just hiked miles.

However, his triumphant cry was cut short. In fact, everyone stopped in their steps, all their eyes bulging out of their sockets as they try to make out what they were seeing.

The only one who didn't notice the fact was the girl herself.

"S-Soomi—," Donghyuk's voice was barely audible, and she wouldn't have paid much attention to it if he hadn't held out his hand in front of her. The blonde extends his finger, pointing straight to the ground before the front door of her apartment.

Blinking a few times, she stares at the shocked expressions on all her friends faces. "What's up with you-," she starts to say, only then seeing Hyuk's motion.

Completely naive, her green eyes wander to the carpeted floor on the apartment where her friend was motioning. What could it be? Why did they look so horrified?

There were many bad things she could be expected, especially on such an awful day. But out of all of them, like usual, what she was truly greeted with was not one of them.

Nauseous — was immediately how she felt. It immensely implodes in her stomach, the utter feeling of emptying everything inside of it. She has to hold a hand to her mouth, both from astonishment and to resist the urge to get sick.

"What the hell?" Junhoe had mumbled in dismay. Nobody knew what to think.

Even Jinhwan was at an absolute loss for words.

"Oh my god," reality punched her in the face eighty times and over as the words rush out of her lips. She jumps forward before June or Jinhwan could hold her back, with such force that they lost their grip on her. Her heart was on the floor, right along with the boy before her as she falls to her knees.

"Jungkook?!" She's yelling out in insanity, her hands gently slapping his cheek. It wasn't like he was dead, but the fact he was unconscious, with bruises, was enough to worry her to the core. She didn't think the day could go any worse, and now look what happened.

It was her fault, wasn't it? The thought was deafening, and the tears were already fighting their way to her eyes. She was the reason this happened to him. He always tends to worry too much about her, she should've texted him back.

"Jeon Jungkook, wake up," she's pleading out, her hands leaving his cheeks and instead going to his own hands. She holds his hand, that's usually so warm, and feels herself shattering all over again. No doubt, they did this to him. He got hurt because of her, the words kept repeating in her head as she leans forward to lay her head against his chest.

All she knew to do was cry. Everything was becoming too much for her to handle. Never had she felt so weighted with so many things, even when she debuted, even when she had so many things to do. She was always fine, she pushed through it even if it was through weird remedies. Never did she feel like her entire mental state was breaking, but yet here she was, for the first time — she lost herself.

"Soomi-ah, it's okay," Yunhyeong's voice is the first one she hears. As usual, he's the first to process what's going on and try to calmly take control of the situation, "he'll be okay, you're okay," his voice is a mere whisper as he kneels beside her. Wrapping an arm around her shoulder, she leans into his chest, crying her life out as her hands grip onto the unconscious boy before her.

Her poor Jungkook.

"We should go check inside the apartment," Hanbin is walking up a few seconds later, "that piece of shit might still be inside."

The others follow him, agreeing to go check together. Jinhwan is the only one who stays behind other than Yun, who was busy comforting the crying girl. He takes the key out of her pocket for her, handing it to their friends.

She couldn't even watch as they cautiously open her apartment door, disappearing inside to inspect if they took anything or messed with something.

After a few long minutes, she seems to regain some sort of mentality. Sitting up straight again, she rubs her eyes with the hand that wasn't holding Jungkook's, demurring, "s-shouldn't we take him somewhere? He looks hurt, what could've happened?" there was no end to her questions, they just kept flowing out, "what if he doesn't wake up? What if he has memory loss? What if they drug—," she's cut off, but not by the boy sitting beside her.

"Soomi, stop being silly," Jinhwan had sighed as he kneels on the opposite side of her from Yun, "he'll be alright, we can take him to the empty room at the dorm."

Sniffling, she looked to Nani, who was offering her a kind smile. She realized how sweet he was being in such a situation. Everyone knew he didn't like Jungkook, and the feeling was definitely mutual between the two.

"A-Are you sure that's okay?" She couldn't help but ask.

He didn't meet her eyes as he was inspecting the state of the passed out figure in front of them. After a second, he nods, "yeah, we can't just leave him here."

She lets go of Jungkook's hand momentarily to lean over, wrapping both her arms around Jinhwan and pulling him in for a tight hug.

"Thank you, Jinhwan, really."

"Anything for you, Soomi-ah."








~






"Soo," Jinhwan's voice cuts her out of her everlasting cloudy thoughts for the 90th time that night. She didn't even look up to see his head peeking in the crack he opened in the door. This meant she didn't see the hurt that was always in his eyes every time he saw her there, sitting next to Jungkook on the guest bed and holding his hand with one hand while she preoccupied herself with the other.

He couldn't help it, even though it made him selfish and that was the last thing he ever wanted to be. Jinhwan despised seeing them together because deep down, he was selfish. He was as selfish as he could be when it came to the girl he loved.

But he wouldn't show it. The poor guy was hurt, and he knew that she was just worried for his safety, and especially what happened. It was at her apartment, and the only thing that would make sense to be the culprit was whoever had been traumatizing Soomi.

With such in mind, he wouldn't throw a fit or act like he was upset. A part of her still loved Jungkook, and he just had to live with it. They do say you never get over your first love, and like Soomi was his, Jungkook was hers.

"You should really come eat, he'll be fine for the time being," he says after a while of them sitting in silence. Walking deeper into the room, he makes his way to sit in the chair that was pulled up next to the bed. It was where she had been sitting for the first while, before she decided she would rather sit on the bed instead, after all, it was more comfortable there.

She doesn't reply for a while. Jinhwan was desperately wanting to reach out, to touch her because he hadn't in too long already. It felt like a part of him was missing without the contact of her skin.

"I can't, I have to be here if he wakes up," she insists the same thing she'd been insisting for hours. It was well over 4 AM by this point, and she still hadn't ate all day. Jinhwan knew she had to be starving even if she was claiming she was fine, and that was only confirmed when he hears her stomach rumble from where he sat inches away.

Heaving out a sigh, he shakes his head in dismay as he rises to his feet again, "I'm going to get you a plate."

A dull exhale leaves her lips as she watches him leave, shutting the door behind him. Once again, she was left in the grey lighting of the guest bedroom of the iKON dorm with her ex fiance, a scenario she would've never imagined even in her wildest dreams. It was bad enough she was having such internal conflict having to do with exactly these two boys, and now the fact Jungkook was in Jinhwan's home - what was she going to do now?


It was clear Jinhwan didn't like it, he didn't like him. How could he? And she knew it was the same for Jungkook and that he wouldn't be very thrilled to find his current whereabouts when he awoke, but on the other hand, there were bigger matters at hand. 

This was why Soomi pushed any emotions of trouble having to do with Jungkook and Jinhwan aside, at least for the time being. Right now, she needed to focus on him waking up and find out what exactly happened. If Jungkook saw anything about whoever this person was, they were going to have to use that new information to catch this person.

Just the mere thought of this nightmare ending was bliss to her. Thinking about it was her fuel from going crazy as she crosses her legs over the other on the bed next to the unconscious boy, picking up her phone that had been charging on the end table beside them. She'd checked it a while back, having had seen all of Jungkook's texts from earlier. It really wasn't right of her to just leave the conversation they'd been having, obviously it would worry anyone with what was going on, but it wasn't fully her fault. Jinhwan had stole her phone, what was she supposed to do about that?

Either way, she clearly should've done something, because just a simple reply could've prevented this from happening. The last thing Soomi would wish on anyone she loved was for them to be hurt, physically or mentally.

Chim:

no, i'm starting to think we should've
taken him to a hospital:(

had some bruises, didn't you?

plus you guys would've gotten recognized easily

that would cause in the media

ik but i didn't think murderous psychopath's
would be down this path

this weirdo.

alright jiminie, i'll let u kno

Chim:

Mi:
way ahead of u


"Here you go~," the intoning voice of Jinani fills her ears, and her head jerks up from her phone to see him entering with a plate of food. It was beef and rice, topped with Yunhyeong's special sauce that was genuinely the definition of heaven, and the scent of it as he walked closer to her was enough to nearly knock Soomi off the bed. She'd been telling herself she was fine without eating that day, but now that she was seeing food - especially food made by Yun - she craved it more than anything. 

Closing the door with his foot, only seconds later was the caramel haired boy before her. There was a giddy grin on his lips, one that he was using to try to make her feel better, as he holds out the plate and chopsticks. 

Her green eyes wander down to the food, and at the sight, her stomach lets out a low rumble. It was begging for it, and Jinhwan even heard it as he giggles a bit.

Ignoring the fact she could feel her cheeks grow pink from the sound her body emitted, she rushes to snatch the plate. Within seconds, she was digging into the meal, probably looking like the equivalent of a pig as she downed piece after piece.

Jinhwan wasn't judging though, as he sits next to her on the bed. Thankfully, it was a king size, so there was lots of space between the two of them and Jungkook, who was still passed out on the opposite side. The plush, white sheets melt under his body as he scoots closer to her. Soo was confused on his actions, but she didn't stop eating to watch whatever he was doing. Only until his chin was on her shoulder, and he was just watching her, did she pause due to how nervous it made her.

"Yah,"she's saying harshly with food in her mouth, mid chewing a piece of beef, "you know it's rude to watch people eat, right?"

Jinhwan pouts from her tone, "but you're so cute, Soomi-ah."

Wrinkling her nose, the Soomi he always knew was still the same even with everything happening to her. "Shush, your sappiness is showing," she tells him in fake disgust, lifting a piece of beef with the chopsticks and practically shoving it in his mouth before he could say anything else.

Not that he minded, especially being fed by her, but it took him off guard. Jinhwan scoots even closer until their bodies are practically one, his arms snaking around her waist so she couldn't get up if she wanted to. "That's not very nice of you," he'd lowly mumbled, his lips just below her ear. She could feel her heart beat increasing with just the deep tone of his voice, one he always used when he wanted to bother her.

"Yah," she snaps in a hushed voice as if telling him to watch whatever he was planning to do, "Kim Jinhwan!"

But she should've known that he never listened to her in times like the current. His lips find their destined way to her neck, where he trails those soft kisses all the way up to her jawline, sucking ever so slightly on her skin in a way that made it crawl. Soomi's brain was melting right under him as his hands go under her shirt to roam her body, and even if she wasn't okay with what he was doing (which she really was either way,) there was no way she could ever manage to utter any words with the frenzy he was sending her body into.

"I've missed you, Soo," he whispers in between those kisses, and she knew exactly what he meant when he said it.

She'd said that no sex would make everything better this time, and it wouldn't. What was going on was a serious matter that she wasn't going to ignore, but she couldn't help the fact he was tempting her so bad.

It took all of her strength to put her hands over his hands, that were just wandering to her breast area, forcing him to halt his actions. Although it was clear he was saddened by this, she sighs as she mutters out, "not here, Jungkook is right there."

A smirk takes over his lips when she rises to her feet, taking his hand and pulling him up right with her. Out the door of the guest bedroom they went, heading straight down the long hall where his bedroom was located.

"One of you guys watch Jungkook for me!" Soomi had yelled down the hall where the boys were still residing in the kitchen. 


[slight warning, skip if bothered]



There were sounds of okay's heard, and that was all she needed as the two reach his bedroom. Her hands find their way around his neck naturally like all the other times, and their lips connect in an instant. Jinhwan's hands grip her hips as he pushes her into his room, not disconnecting that kiss they were engrossed in for a millisecond.

Kicking the door shut with his foot, there was nothing compared to that euphoric feelings they were sent into. He made her feel like everything was okay just by his touch, and it was why she could never get rid of him, she could never say she didn't adore Kim Jinhwan.

Because as his hands roam her body and she feels her emotions spiking, that talk they'd had a couple of days ago outside her apartment was history. It'd made it sound like they were taking some sort of break from each other so Soomi could truly decide which one out of the two boys she really loved, but the thing was, they couldn't stay away, even if they wanted to. Soomi and Jinhwan always migrated back to each other, it was impossible otherwise.

Breaks were overrated anyway.





39:00 | confusing feelings

Soomi's chest was heaving, she felt like her body was giving up on something such as breathing air and actually functioning. Over the last hour, it seemed like she'd become one with the emotion known as pleasure, but she'd felt so much of it that she immensely required sleep, not to mention the fact it was 5 AM.

And to think they still had schedules in the afternoon. Would it be too late to take off? After this, she could really use a day without work.

"Go to sleep, babe," Jinhwan's voice is full of rasp as he'd whispered it in her ear, finally climbing off from atop of her. There's a thump against the mattress beside her as he falls onto it, and her eyes were already beginning to close when his arms find their way around her waist.

But yet, there was something inside of her urging to let him know.

"I love you, Jinhwan-ah."

Jinhwan's cheeks went pink, he was thankful she didn't have her eyes open to notice. Hearing her say such a thing was like someone had suddenly thrown him into a new utopia for the first time. He could feel his heart implode with glee, and he had to bite his lip to suppress the huge smile that was fighting it's way onto his face.

"I love you too, Soo."

She was probably already asleep at that point. This was confirmed further when slight breathes left her parted lips, and he chuckles at how innocent she looked while asleep — despite how she was the opposite. Soomi was still so gorgeous to him, and he found himself staring down at her, trying to truly intake her beauty.

A sigh leaves his lips as he finally lays down fully into the plush of the pillow. His room was dark for the most part, with only a slight dim light entering from the window that wasn't fully covered by the wine curtains.

He felt like he should be overwhelming with joy right then, he should be able to sleep like a baby. But Jinhwan couldn't sleep. His brown irises only trail around his darkly colored walls until they land on the window, narrowing in concentration.

The thought of this person, this person that was threatening Soomi's life. It appalled him in so many ways he couldn't explain. But as he thought about it, they lived in the same building as Soo, after all, it was how the two of them met in the first place.

Would it be that hard for that person to find their dorm if they didn't already? He felt his emotions heighten from the thought.

Never would he let anything happen to her. He didn't care if it hurt him in the process, he would keep Soomi safe — always.

"SOOMI-AH!"

"SOOMI-AH, WE NEED YOU IN THE LIVING ROOM SPLAT!"

Jinhwan jumps at the abrupt yelling, hurriedly pulling the covers over both of their naked bodies. You could still guess that they were bare, however, just by the simple fact their shoulders were still visible.

It didn't take Junhoe long as he opens the door, his eyes instantly filled with not surprise, but more disappointment. "Yah, seriously? You fuck now of all times?"

His jokes didn't ever have a mute button. It made Jinhwan want to throw a car at him.

"Close your mouth, what do you want?"

Rolling his eyes, he walks a bit further into the room as he retorts, "well if I do recall, I did say Soomi, not Jinan-hyung."

"She's sleeping," he deadpans in a millisecond, and Junhoe purses his lips as his eyes fall on the girl beside him.

He's shaking his head in disbelief, "for real, out of all the times you could ware her out—," a pillow being thrown at him was what caused him to shut up that time.

"Tell me what you want or leave," the eldest's words were harsh and demanding as his brown irises narrow at the playful boy.

It takes Junhoe a moment to process, but he soon realizes his hyung was clearly not in the mood for any of his games or jokes. With a sigh, he finally hums, "I just thought Soomi would want to know Jungkook's awake."







~




Sure, she was more than exhausted, it was no lie to say Kim Soomi was more than overdue for a long night's rest. But there was no room for her to be doing such a thing despite her trying, and she knew this well when she was awoken only around twenty minutes after falling asleep in the first place. Her body ached, especially her legs, and she felt like she was going to collapse any minute when she looked into the eyes of Jinhwan.

There'd been a look of sympathy in them when they met her green orbs. He knew she needed to rest, but Junhoe wasn't going to stop bothering him to wake him up, saying that Jungkook wouldn't stop asking for her. As much as he wanted to go straight into the living room and give Jungkook a piece of his mind, that wouldn't be the civil Jinhwan everyone knew and loved.

Therefore, the only way to avoid such conflict was awaking the sleeping beauty. It didn't look like she was interested in moving a muscle, that was, until he'd told her Jungkook was awake.

And before you knew it, the tiny girl was running out of the door like a sociopath. It was like that pain in between her legs had suddenly disintegrated. She rushes into the bathroom next to the bedroom's in the hall, hurriedly running a brush through her hair. The first thing she saw to make sure she didn't smell bad was Jinhwan's cologne, and she was quick to put some on. One last glance in the mirror to make sure she didn't look like she just had sex, and she was rushing back down the hall into the main room.

The eyes of Jeon Jungkook don't take long to find her. He was sitting one of the many sofa's that lined the living room, biting his lip in seemingly deep contemplation as he stares off into the distance. Some of the other boys were in the room, as well, making small talk with either him or each other. They actually got along with Jungkook rather well (even though they were aware of him wanting to be with Soomi and not really caring much for her and Jinhwan's relations,) but there was a tension in the room so high that it didn't matter.

At the sound of her footsteps, everyone's eyes jerk to see who had entered. At the mere sight of Soomi, the boys seemed to let out a sigh of relief. As if on queue, Jungkook had rose to his feet. Soomi blinks in confusion, wondering what he was up to, due to the fact he was walking full speed at her. Something inside of her was keeping her from moving a muscle as she watches him, maybe it was shock, maybe it was something else. 

"Soomi-ah," her name fell fresh off his lips in a graceful manner, a slight gasp leaves her lips when he abruptly wraps both his arms around her. One secures itself around her waist, and the other goes around her neck as he holds her tightly, his head leaning against the top of hers since he was much taller than her. 

The other boys were gawking at what they were seeing. He hadn't really said much to any of them, especially about what happened. Jungkook, oddly enough, hadn't been rude to any of them, he just didn't seem like he was feeling well. But the moment his eyes found Soomi, his entire mood was suddenly uplifted.

All of them were feeling a bit nervous at the sight of the two embracing each other like that, though. After she processes what was really going on, she slowly wraps her frail arms around his torso, hugging him back. He was okay, she tells herself, trying not to remember how lifeless he looked when she found him. Jungkook was right before her, breathing and living, all was okay.

That still wasn't true despite her reassuring herself. There was so much wrong still, so much they had left to solve. She still needed to really know what happened, and most importantly, if he saw anything.

"I thought you were in there," he's muttering into her hair a moment later, "I thought something happened to you."

Soomi could only scoff in disbelief. After all that, and he was worried about her? Sometimes, Jeon Jungkook really was something else. Even with how bratty and obnoxious he could get, he still was an angel deep down because of that soft spot he had, even if it was only for her.

"Pabo," she's chuckling lightly, shaking her head as her grip on him tightened, "I wasn't home, I found you outside my apartment unconscious," she explains to him her side, sighing afterwards, "yah, how could you worry me like that?"

"I'm fine, it's just a bruise," he waves off her nagging.

Before she could ask anything else, the sound of someone clearing their throat from the hall cuts off their conversation. The exact thing all the guys were worried about was there in all his glory, and they all have to force themselves to swallow the lump that forms in their throat's.

Soomi turns her head in the embrace to see what the sudden tension was about. At the sight of Jinhwan, a part of her snaps back into reality. Awkwardly, she gently pushes Jungkook back so she could step out of the hug. 

When she does so, his eyes fill with confusion - only until they land on the boy standing at the entrance of the hallway. Although he was trying to hide it with the emotionless look on his face, you could see Jinhwan wasn't happy at the sight of Jungkook. There was a clear jealousy trait the two boys each had, and it made everything that was happening that much harder for Soomi as she watches the intense glare session between the two.

"Jungkook," she blurts his name out, grabbing his arm and already dragging him towards the hall Jinhwan came from, "why don't we talk in the guest room?"

"Okay," he only mumbled the reply, allowing her to pull him away and out of the living room. Just before he disappears down the hall, though, he'd looked to Jinhwan one last time, narrowing his eyes a bit.

The seven boys in the living room let out deep exhales they'd been holding in once the two are gone. Nothing felt better than the tension finally leaving, and they all comfortably fall onto the sofa's, already beginning to talk about what was going on.

"This is absurd," Donghyuk had been saying in a dull tone, "I never thought all of this would be happening to poor Soomi."

"It's the person she told us about, right?" Chanwoo hums, looking for confirmation, "they're bothering her again?"

"Yeah, apparently they have access to her apartment."

"Talk about fucking creepy, I'm not letting her go there ever again," Junhoe bitterly sighs, crossing his arms over his chest as he leans back into the couch, "I can't wait until we catch this fucking weirdo."

A silence falls over them all again, until Bobby hesitantly clears his throat, "yo, but did you guys notice the tension between Jinan-hyung and Jungkook?"

Some of them start snickering as they recall it. "They're both in love with Soomi, what do you expect them to be like to each other?" Yun had been saying, and they don't take note of the annoyed expression on their eldest's face.

"I don't know," Bobby shrugs after a second of thought, "I just think it's funny."

"They're like hungry dogs waiting to attack each other."

"Yah!" the sudden shout of the short boy causes them all to halt any words. They really should have noticed how not in the mood he was, because the sight of his angry expression caused them all to shiver. Even with all the teasing, Jinhwan was scary when he got mad, and right then, he seemed pretty frustrated with the world. "Can you stop talking about it? Obviously I'm not going to like the guy who broke Soomi's heart," he scoffs, and the six boys purse their lips, afraid to say anything more.

With that, the silence remains over the group. No one wanted to push Jinan's buttons anymore than they already had.
