Meanwhile, Soomi lets out a sigh as she sits on the edge of the bed. Crossing and uncrossing her legs multiple times, she tries to find words to say in reply to everything she just heard. It was difficult to hide the disappointment on her face, she really hoped there would be something to go off of.

"I'm sorry," Jungkook could see how sad she suddenly was, and the bed sinks beside her seconds later, "I wish I had better news."

Pushing through her emotions, she tried to look on the bright side. Sending a grin to Jungkook, she manages to meet his eyes once again, "don't apologize, at least you're okay. Even if you didn't see anything, you still did something good."

There was confusion in those doe eyes of his, "what's that?"

"You went there for me, because you were worried," she admits, looking down to her hands that lay in her lap again, "I was at the studio working, that's why I didn't reply. But you still went to make sure I was okay."

He found himself smiling from her heartfelt words. "Of course I did," he chortles, "I always worry about you, Soomi-ah."

Just hearing him say that, made her think of the boy in the other room, that felt the same way. Jinhwan, the boy she sought giving her heart to. She really planned to take it to the next step with him, never did she think Jungkook would magically come back into her life. 

Once you love someone, that love doesn't ever really go away. There was truth to that statement. It was proven to her every time she looked into those eyes of his. The same eyes she was staring into then, that were begging her, taunting her to grow closer to him. A part of her wanted to, wanted to kiss him and tell him she was glad he was okay. She'd already told him she was sorry because she really felt like it was all her fault, but there was still something more that should be said, wasn't there?

Either way, she couldn't say whatever it was. And she didn't move a muscle from where she sat.

"Thank you, Kookie."




40:00 | peace at MAMA

a few days later



Recording, rehearsals, and more rehearsals all became a daily thing once again for Soomi and the boys of iKON. It would've been exhausting if they weren't used to their idol lives being so demanding, but for the eight, it was just the norm they'd grown accustomed to. After the rehearsals for MAMA started, the show was only a day away.

Once it'd come upon them, they all felt prepared to showcase the new songs they'd made. Soomi and Jinhwan's duet was called Only You, and the song she did with the boys all together was known as Just For You, which was a pretty song she wished she could say she took more credit in writing. When they'd recorded it that day, she hadn't truly paid attention to the lyrics. But after recording, when it came time to rehearse and practice any necessary choreo (although it was a slow song and none was really required,) she took the time to actually listen to what it was about.

Like every other time, you could never not be blown away by the talent of Kim Hanbin. She was always astonished by the masterpieces he created, and they only seemed to get better as time passed.

"How do you do it?" she'd asked him the night before the final show of MAMA took place. It was late at night, and the two were the only one's awake since they both tended to be night owl's, especially on special night's like that one. Their nerves always got the best of them and prevented them from sleeping. It was why Soomi had found herself wandering into his studio, figuring he was there, working on something like he always was.

Her intuition was correct, as Hanbin had been leaning in that leather chair of his like always. He'd looked up to the sudden question as she fell into the seat beside him, looking to him with wide curious green eyes.

"Do what?" He asked only a second later, setting down the pen he'd been holding atop the notebook before him. 

Soomi shakes her head, knowing he probably knew what she was talking about. What else could she be referring to? Her eyes rake across the walls of the studio he was so confidential about, and she felt honored that he allowed her in here now so freely. It meant that he really trusted her, even over the boys, with this sort of information. For that, she couldn't be more grateful.

Although, as her eyes fall onto those same polaroids he had scattered over the walls, she suddenly seemed to answer her own question.

Still, she felt the need to ask anyway, "this," she motions to his computer screen, where he had the same music app pulled up to mix beats, "you sit and make song after song, write lyric after lyric, in the blink of an eye," she gawks in disbelief, "and they always come out good, how do you do it?" she repeats again. Hanbin really was some sort of alien to Soomi. Being that she was the only one who truly also produced and wrote music, she couldn't come to grasp how he did it so easily. Even though she wrote her and Jinhwan's song quickly, it was because she wrote it thinking about him. She wrote it with his gorgeous smile in mind, his little giggle, and the way he made her stomach do flips when he would utter her name. That was the only reason she finished it so fast, otherwise she would've been sitting at that same desk with her pen, completely thoughtless for hours.

The thought causes her to nearly fall out of her chair from how silly she was. That was why, it fully sinks into her skull. She was able to finish it so quick because she'd been thinking about someone she loved, which was what the song was about. Recalling most of Hanbin's songs, the majority of them told a story about just that - love.

Her green eyes look to the pictures on the wall again, and it all fell into place. No wonder why they're scattered all over the walls.

Hanbin didn't even need to say anything as he notices her looking at them. When that certain realization washes over her face, he chuckles, knowing she finally figured it out herself, "she's my muse, all of what I do is possible because of her," he admits, and that giddy smile appears on his lips right after he finishes stating such a thing.

Soomi couldn't help but melt at what he said. It was just like the first time she came in here and heard him talk about this mysterious girl. Never did she think someone could be so immensely head over heels for anyone, never had she heard anyone gush so much about someone so genuinely. But there was something about the way he'd smile when he mentioned her, something about that tint in his brown eyes. He really loved this girl.

She admired that, more than anything.

"That's so sweet," her voice is a whisper as she's busy still looking at all their photos. It seemed that there were new one's since the last time she'd been in there, and she found herself wondering how he managed to always sneak out and go see her in private. She had never been good at doing that, apparently. 

"Are you not the same?" her head jerks to look at him with confused eyes. At the sight of her having no idea what he meant, he clears his throat, rephrasing his question, "With Jinan-hyung, are you not the same?"

She breaks their eye contact at the new question. 

"I suppose so," she muttered after a moment of thought. Thankfully, Hanbin was a patient person and didn't mind her taking a while to answer. "Thinking of Jinhwan-ah was how I finished Only You so fast, but I guess prior to that I never really tried anything like that," she explains, and he purses his lips as he listens, nodding in understanding.

"Well," he hums, "that's the secret, try it from now on."

Soomi couldn't help but giggle, "You think I can write a sensational hit like Love Scenario just thinking of Jinhwan?"

"Probably not, I don't think Jinan is that good of a muse, honestly."

She burst into laughter at his blatantly honest response. Holding a hand over her mouth, it takes her a few minutes to falter her hysterics. Hanbin starts laughing, too, after a moment. "What, am I wrong? He's too bossy."

"Yah," she's still laughing every few seconds, "I'm gonna tell him you said that."

Hanbin shrugs it off, "Hey, go ahead."

That heartfelt conversation she had with Hanbin had been her fuel for waking up the next morning. The last few days until MAMA had been irregularly quiet for her regarding the psycho, and it was nice to just be able to focus on work. She hadn't even really heard from Jungkook.

This was why she thought MAMA would go smoothly. For once, she could have a nice, peaceful award show where she gets to have fun showcasing new songs and just enjoy spending time with her fans.

And crazily enough, it did.

Her and Jinhwan's performance went extremely great. Everyone received it well, all the fans couldn't stop screaming during every second from how excited they were to see the idols together. The fact they were 'friends' was now out in the open, and the song they sang had everyone jumping around. 

Not to mention the choreography, that was simple and cute. There were fans that already seemed to have it down just from seeing it one time. The happy response made her excited to perform it on shows. After all, there was no better feeling than people enjoying something you wrote and produced. It was the solemn reason she liked doing what she did.

Winning countless awards for top female artist, top solo artist, and top choreography, was something she was used to. Soomi usually won these awards every single year. It was no different this time, especially after she released her newest song. Like per usual with her music, it was well received by the public.

And like everyone expected, despite her scandals, she remained on top. 

The song she did with the boys of iKON had the crowd in even more screams. It was bad enough when they said her and Jinhwan did a song, but seeing her come on stage with all seven of them, she didn't think she'd ever heard louder screaming in her life thus far.

Although she was exhausted as she walked backstage, she could finally say that she was happy. It was a word she hadn't gotten to use in a long time because of all the stress she had been feeling. But Soomi felt great, the award show was going great, and it was already almost over. What more could go wrong now?

She's just turning down the hall where her dressing room was, when the sound of fast footsteps cause her to turn. It wasn't fast enough, though, because soon, someone appears right before her.

"Soomi-ah!" the screaming voices didn't stop there, and before she knew it, there were four screeching girls surrounding her.

"I can't believe you got to do a song with iKON!" Lisa had been crying.

"It was so good," Jisoo is reassuring her as she hugs her from the side, "you're so talented!"

"Seriously, that duet with Jinhwan, I got chills," Rose is gushing as she holds a hand to her heart, acting as if she was in some sort of pain.

She couldn't help but smile from ear to ear as all the girls offer her a hug, boasting about how much they loved her performances. They were both under the same company, meaning they knew how strict their CEO usually was. The girls were seriously shocked that he really let her collab with them.

"I remember he doesn't even let us near them most of the time," they're saying in disbelief at how insane the rules tended to be at the company. Soomi knew well of them, and as she thought about it, they really were right - it was nuts how he was allowing her to be friends with iKON so casually.

"Yah, it's cause CEO-nim really loves Soomi," Jennie informs the others, and she looks to the girl who was trying to insist that that wasn't true, "then again, who doesn't?" she gushes, wrapping an arm around her shoulders and pulling her in for another hug.

The rookie idols around them were looking at the five girls like they were witnessing some sort of Gods. It must've been quite a sight to them, as they probably didn't imagine some of the biggest stars in Korea acted this childish.

"Hey," Soomi is whining as Jennie is practically hugging the life out of her. The girls are all in fits of laughter at the sight of the unconditional love the girl was giving her. Despite her cries, Jennie didn't seem to have much of an intention to stop squeezing her. "Jennie Kim, you make me think you're into me or something."

Even that comment didn't make her let go. She only hugged her tighter. "I don't know, you're pretty cute," the girls all burst into more hysterics when she says this. Soomi's mouth falls open out of surprise, it was the last thing she thought she would hear out of her.

Finally, she lets go of her, and she couldn't help but stand in astonishment. "I really never thought I'd hear those words," she's telling them, still shaking her head as she tries to process if that was what Jennie said or not.

"ooh, that would really shock the world," Rose is humming, "imagine Dispatch releasing news of you two being together instead of something like Soomi and Jungkook."

There's a tint of pink on her cheeks from the thought. Soomi raises her hands to cover her face when Jennie gently elbows her side, "hey, I'm down if your down."

Their jokes were driving her crazy.

"Hajima~," she's whining like a child, still covering her face with her hands so her words were just barely audible.

"Alright, alright," Lisa sighs after they all have one last good laugh, "we're done."

The three girls look to Jennie, who was still standing beside Soomi with her lips pursed. She seemed to be thinking seriously about something. At the sight, Jisoo raises a brow, "Not so sure about Jen, though, I think she's really considering hitting on you, Soo."

"Yah!" Jennie cries out upon hearing what she said, "cut it out~."

Just when another joking session is bound to begin, there's a presence coming up behind Soomi that causes all four of the girls to pause. Their eyes widen a bit in surprise at seeing them, and they exchange looks. They guessed they really shouldn't be surprised anymore. It should've been obvious that Soomi had close relations with those boys ever since that day at the hospital, after all, Soomi had told them that herself. But still, it was a crazy story to think about.

They couldn't help but wonder if she was something more with one of them. Soomi would never quite tell them that part.

Confused at why they all went dead silent, Soomi herself is about to turn to see what was going on, when someone suddenly grabs onto her shoulders, yelling boo directly in her ear. It was something she definitely hadn't been expecting, as she jumps, holding a hand to her heart.

Spinning to face the culprit, there's an unimpressed look on her face despite the grin on theirs. "Idiot, what's wrong with you?!" she pouts, reaching out to slap his arm like it would even slightly affect him.

June is giggling like a little kid, flinching away from her hits. "Nothing, I just wanted to find you, our set just ended and everyone is celebrating back in the dressing room," he explains to her, that bright smile never leaving his lips once. Seeing Junhoe's smile was contagious, it always had been. It was big and lively and just made you want to - well - smile. 

The girls are all whispering to each other behind her, and she purses her lips as she turns to them. Junhoe notices they were there, as he seemed to of forgot, and offers a quick bow and a wave to his fellow company-mates, "hi, nice to see you~"

They offer greetings and bows in return since they weren't really well acquainted with each other like they were with Soomi. She sighs as she looks between the two friends of hers, "I guess I'll come, you guys don't mind do you?" she asks the girls, who quickly wave it off.

"It's all good, we have to go on stage soon," Jisoo reassures her with a kind grin, giving her one last hug to congratulate her on everything.

"See you later, Soo, don't do anything scandalous in the dressing rooms," Lisa is telling her as she hugs her like Jisoo previously had.

Her face grows red at the sound of what she'd said. "Scandalous," she repeats the word blankly, "with who?"

The girl shakes her head is dismay, "you know who," she tries to nonchalantly motion to Junhoe, who was busy looking at something else that he actually didn't notice.

Realizing what exactly she was thinking, Soomi nearly falls on the ground from bewilderment. "Wait, no, it's not like that-," she's insisting, but the girl just shakes her head, already turning to walk away.

"Bye Soomi, you better call me so we can go on a date sometime," Jennie was still up with her jokes - or maybe they weren't jokes - as she hugs her like the others had.

They all wave goodbye to each other until the girls disappear down the halls to their own room. With a sigh, she turns back to Junhoe, who was giving her a weird look she couldn't decipher.

"What?" she asks him in confusion.

"Are you two-timing Jinhwan with Jennie?"

She couldn't help but laugh at what he said. Junhoe was obviously joking, as he starts laughing himself seconds later.

"Shut up," is all she says, grabbing his arm and dragging him down the hall. "I need to change and get my stuff out of my dressing room real quick, then we can go to you guys'," she informs June of her plan as they go down the hall where her's was.

He skips a bit to catch up to her quick walking pace, "mkay, no problem."

Soomi let's out a breath of relief as they walk down the seemingly quiet hallway. The show was practically over, all that was left was the closing stage. Nothing bad had happened, no drama had gone on, everything was fine. It was a normal, perfect award show.

Finally, something was going right. And with that, she couldn't help but smile.











41:00 | ancient pictures


"It won't take me long," she assures Junhoe, who only nods, distracted by something on his phone. He leans against the wall to wait for her, and she takes out her key to unlock the door to her dressing room. 

Twisting the key in the lock, the click sounds, and she turns the knob, pushing the door open. The outfit she wore for her stage was rather tight, and she was more than ready to get out of it, even if it meant she had to get back into the short dress she was wearing for the award show itself. Honestly, the dress was more comfortable.

Plus, it was pretty fun to wear heels. They made her taller than Jinhwan usually, and then she got to tease him with the others.

She was already unzipping the back of the outfit, which was some sort of one piece. Just when it's halfway down, does she walk into the main room of her dressing room. Like one would assume, it was completely empty, not a soul in sight. All that was located inside of it was things the staff left behind and her things like her purse and her phone.

A yawn leaves her lips as she unzips the rest of the outfit, just when she's about to take it off, her head looks up to the large mirror that covered the entire wall of the room. It was a big mirror, one that you could see your entire body in and showed every flaw if you had any. There were giant, circular bulbs that surrounded the outside lines of it, and it was a fancy, extremely expensive mirror she always liked to look into.

But not when it was cracked. 

Pieces of the mirror cover the ground she stood on. The wall was so big, that it was cracked on either side, like someone had thrown something at it or hit it with something. There's fear written all over Soomi's face, and she was in so much petrification she didn't know if she should scream or run - or both.

The worst part - was the sight of the word written on the mirror in some sort of red substance. 

Whore.

That sick feeling, the nauseating feeling that she was going to puke up everything wrong she had ever done, one she hadn't felt in the last few days. The peace that had finally come back to her that she missed so dearly, it was gone in the blink of an eye.

Tears rush to her eyes, and she didn't even care about the fact her outfit was completely unzipped in the back. She ran out of the room like a mad man, trying to wipe away the tears before they messed up her makeup. There were too many emotions in her head, so many that she didn't even know what to say when she swings open the door in a rush.

The slam of the door against the wall causes Junhoe to look up, a curious look on his face. Once his eyes land on the freaked out, crying girl, concern builds inside of those brown irises. He's stepping forward quickly, immediately assuming the worst and grabbing her like a protective mother.

"Yah, yah, what's wrong? Are you okay?" he's demanding as Soomi only grabs onto the jacket he was wearing, burying her face in his chest.

Worried someone might see them, he gently moves with her back into her dressing room hall. Closing the door, he clicks the lock before turning back to her. She was a mess of trembles, holding her arms and breathing heavily, like something traumatizing had happened to her.

He frowns at the sight. The one time everything seemed to be going well for her. All he knew to do was pull her into another hug.

They stood there, embracing, until she finally felt her breathing go back to normal. 

"June-ya," she mutters, her voice shaking with each word, "l-look inside the dressing room."

He didn't get what could possibly be wrong with her room, but there was only one way for him to find out. Pulling out of the hug, Soomi grabs onto his hand and follows him as he walked deeper into her room. He never really went into anyone else's dressing room before, but it was similar to how their's looked. 

His brown eyes rake across everything. It all seemed fine at first, and he wasn't sure what could have caused her to have some sort of breakdown - that was, until he saw the mirror.

"What the fuck?" he's gasping in awe. His hand falls from hers due to the fact he lets go so he could walk up to the mess that was created on the floor. Junhoe narrows his eyes at the sight, feeling the anger build up inside of him at the fact this awful person was still at it. How could they do things like this?

"I c-can't believe they can even get into my dressing room," she's mumbling from where she still stands, holding a hand to her aching head from what was going on, "how? How can no one ever see them?!"

Junhoe didn't know what to tell her. He was too busy leaning forward, inspecting whatever the word was written with on the mirror. A part of him wanted to touch it to try to figure it out, but from the fact it was red, he had a bad feeling about it.

"I'm sorry, Soomi-ah," he sighs as he turns back to her, "we should probably call your manager and tell him about this, I think it's getting too far."

"I can't tell him everything, though, he'll ask where I'm living and it's not like I can say I'm staying with you guys," she shook her head, "I'll just say someone broke in and trashed my room."

There's still a dull look on his face from realizing she was right, "alright, tell him that."





///



Soomi tried her best not to really freak out about the whole dressing room thing. The word they wrote on the mirror or the fact they broke it wasn't what had her nearly on the verge of a heart attack. It was more of the fact this person somehow got into her dressing room in the first place, they had access to her things, somehow even at shows like MAMA. No matter how many times she wracked her brain about it, looking for some sort of explanation to how they could manage it, nothing made sense. How could someone manage to have access to her dressing room, all without anyone thinking it was odd? Not to mention her apartment she was horrified to live in now. Something was weird about it, she just couldn't find any pieces to the puzzle.

"It's okay, Soo," Junhoe had been trying to comfort her as they walk in the boys dressing room minutes later, "at least they don't know where you're staying at now, and we're all here to protect you."

All she had the energy to do in response was send him a smile of thanks. Really, she was beyond grateful for Junhoe. Even though in the beginning she found him annoying, he grew on her over time. Him and his dumb sense of humor was comforting in the weirdest times, and he always knew how to make her laugh when she needed something to cheer her up. But even with his playful aura, when time came to be, he could be as serious as anyone when he needed to be. No one paid attention, not even the two of them themselves, but Soomi and Junhoe had become close over the last year, and especially the last several months.

Although Jimin was back in her life, she still saw him as one of her best friends.

The sound of the two entering the main part of the dressing room causes all the boys to look up. They'd been engaged in a serious talk about what kind of alcohol they wanted to buy to drink back at the dorm to celebrate their awards. There was a sense of joy in the atmosphere, and she didn't want to ruin it.

Soomi tries to force a smile on her face, but it was far too late when their eyes all land on her glassy eyes and distressed expression. 

As if he had some sort of teleportation skill, Jinhwan was suddenly before her. "What's wrong? What happened?" when he notices she doesn't look much for talking, he'd immediately looked to the tall boy that stood beside her.

He made sure to exchange a glance with Soo first to make sure it was okay to tell them. When she only nods to him, he clears his throat to explain, "someone broke into Soomi's dressing room."

All of the boys seemed to rise to their feet from the outrage they felt. "What?!" a few of them say in unison, shock written all over their faces.

Just like Soomi, it made zero sense to them. 

"No fucking way," Hanbin mutters, "how the hell is that even possible?"

"This is getting too far," Donghyuk shakes his head in worry for his dear friend, repeating the same phrase Junhoe had said back in her room.

Meanwhile, Jinhwan couldn't be more angry. Soomi had stepped forward to wrap her arms around his torso, and he allowed her to bury her face in his chest as he held her head with one hand, messing with her hair. The other was secured around the small of her back, and he was too busy trying to comfort her while attempting to figure out how this person could manage something like that. He was beyond angry at that point, the more he thought about it. It really was getting too out of hand, so much he was considering contacting the authorities and hiring extra security around their building or something.

He didn't know what to do. What could he do to catch this person? Whatever it required, he'd figure it out and wouldn't hesitate to deliver. He'd kill them when he finds them.

As if he couldn't get any more upset upon the news, Junhoe finally adds the rest of the story, "yeah, they broke her mirror and wrote uh - a not so very nice insult on it in some sort of red liquid."

Everyone got goosebumps from the words. "Was it blood?" Bobby is the first to ask, but Junhoe only shrugs.

"I think we should really tell someone," Yun was saying, "this is too much, I'm worried about Soomi's safety."

"We can ask the security here, maybe there's camera footage of whoever went into her room," Chanwoo suggests, and everyone perks up at the idea.

After a talk about it, they all agreed to head to the security team of MAMA after the final stage. It would be happening soon, and all they had to do was go stand out on the stage for one final goodbye to everyone who came tonight. Soomi couldn't help but recall the last goodbye stage she was on, which was at Melon when Jinhwan was mad at her. 

Jeez, she thought, how times had changed. At least she had him on her side now for good, even if she wasn't sure about her decision just yet. 

Jinhwan and Jungkook, the two that surprisingly hadn't been on her mind as much as usual. With the entire thing going on, she really hadn't had time to worry about who she really fell for.

Snuggling her face further into his chest, the warmth of Jinhwan was something she craved, she could confirm that for sure. The feeling of being in the same room as him was enough to make her heart leap, and she lets out a deep breathe, inhaling that cologne he always wore. 

We'll find them, she tries to tell herself, this will be over and I'll be able to live normally.

It wasn't like one could be so sure, but what more could she say to cheer herself up?

"iKON, we need you on stage in five," one of the staffs knock on the door, calling to them. 

They'd all rose to their feet, and Hanbin assured the staff they would be there in time for the final stage. 

"Guess we better get going," he'd hummed, being the first to walk to the door.

The rest follow him into the narrow hall, everyone making sure their wardrobe was okay even though their stylists just did that previously. Soomi actually hadn't seen her stylist since they were located in her dressing room. She figured she should probably head back there to check with them, especially because they probably saw what happened to the mirror. It was now or never for her to tell her manager not about the entire situation, but just that someone broke into her room. He would probably go check the camera's himself, which would save them the effort after the stage.

"I have to go back to my room," she's whispers to Jinhwan as they're walking into the main hall, and he'd nodded down to her.

Sending him a smile, she figured he would let go of her hand and go with his own members to the stage. Soomi was going to offer to meet him on the outside so they could go on by each other.

But he didn't let go, he only walked next to her as she turns to head the way her own room was.

Although it surprised her, she decided not to say anything. The feeling of his hand in hers sent butterflies throughout her entire body, so much that she didn't care who saw. 

Except for the staffs of YG, she did care if they saw. It was why once they neared her room, their hands fell out of each other's.

"I'll wait out here," he'd told her, and she only responds with a nod.

Just as she's walking in the hall of her own room, closing the door behind her, her phone buzzes in her pocket.

It's probably one of the guys asking if Jinhwan went with me, she assumes, pulling it out quickly to tell them he did.


Unknown Number:


Unknown Number:
I guess you were never really innocent, huh?

Unknown Number:
Sure would be a shame for the whole world to know.
I hope you liked my present, I just wanted you to be
reminded of what you really are, Soomi:)












42:00 | gallant boys



"Thanks to everyone for attending the 2019 MAMA, we hope to see you all next year!" the hosts had been cheerily yelling into the mics over the screaming of the fans in the crowds. There were so many lights and bright faces, and the sight of the signs they held up with her name made Soomi feel better momentarily. It warmed her heart to be in the presence of her beloved fans, they were the only reason any of her dreams had ever come true, after all.

All the artists are waving to their fans, shouting goodbyes to them. It was finally the end of the show, and she lets out a deep breathe as she rushes off the stage, making sure to wave before she does so. Her head was beginning to feel rather light again, and she was worried she might faint like she did at Melon.

I can't, she tells herself harshly. Even though there were so many people here for her, she didn't want to cause anyone anymore trouble. This was why she hadn't told Jinhwan about the new text message from the psycho.

Well, and the fact it would require her explaining what happened on that rooftop. If she had to do that, it would mean she had to admit to Jinhwan everything about her and Jimin. Did her best friend have feelings for her? She'd wondered it ever since that day, and never did she really know the answer. But then again, why else would they have kissed on that rooftop? 

Everyone got caught up in the moment sometimes. That was what she'd been telling herself was the reason for years. Even then, she never truly knew, and she never asked either since they grew apart. 

Now that the two were on talking terms again, she just didn't have the guts to bring that up. As far as she knew, Jimin probably didn't even remember. So she pushed it to the back of her mind despite the memory being so clear, it didn't matter. She didn't think she would have any reason to ever bring it up again.

Until now, now she had no choice but to talk to Jimin. The only person who had that photo was him, so how could this person ever had managed to get it? How did they know about what happened? It only caused her fear to grow more, and she holds a hand to her head as she pushes through the crowds of staffs and idols.

It might not have been the best time to have the talk with him then, but she didn't know when would be a good time. Soomi didn't have the energy to visit their dorm right now, and she needed to inform him about this now. 

Desperately, her green eyes rake over all the crowds of people in search for her best friend. No sign of Jimin was seen anywhere - or any member of BTS - and the hopeless feeling was surfacing in her veins again. Her only lead then was the fact there was a small chance her manager would find something on the camera footage he said he would search after she told him about her room incident. 

Somehow, she would catch this person that was bringing all of this stress and pain upon her. One way or another, she would find a way to find them.

Finally, after what felt like years of searching for them, she sees the seven boys walking off stage in a group. They all looked rather upbeat, probably because they got to see their fans and won some awards that night. Not that she was one to enjoy bursting bubbles, but she didn't have a choice.

The only one out of the seven that wasn't looking great was Jungkook. He kept running a hand through his hair, a habit he did when he was in deep contemplation, and he would stare off into space as the others chatted amongst themselves. She wondered how he'd been the last few days, as she really meant it when she said she hadn't heard from him much. Something about him looked different, it seemed like he was growing his hair out a lot more than usual. It was a good look on him, no doubt.

But she didn't have time to stand around and admire Jungkook, even if she wanted to. Soomi was only there to talk to Jimin about the text. Even though both Jungkook and Jinhwan told her to tell them when she got another text, she just couldn't let either of them know about this one.

Jinhwan couldn't know what happened on that rooftop because then he would suspect her best friend having feelings for Jimin. And she didn't want him to dislike him since they seemed to get along fine unlike him and Jungkook. Not to mention, it could make him feel distrustworthy towards her since she'd done that while she was with someone. She felt like she had already tainted with that enough.

And God knew Jungkook couldn't know. She'd cheated on him the night he asked her to marry him. They hadn't been engaged when her and Jimin were on that rooftop, yet, and it was just a kiss, but either way, cheating was cheating. With how competitive and protective he was, if he found out Jimin had kissed his girlfriend at the time that he was still in love with, something bad would go down that she couldn't bare to be responsible for.

Just when Soomi opens her mouth to call to Jimin, who was too distracted talking to Hoseok to notice her presence since she was still rather far from them, a force on her wrist causes her to halt in her jogging towards the group.

"Yah!" she's crying out in annoyance, spinning around to face whoever dared to do such a thing.

The eyes of Kim Jinhwan pierce into her own, and the upset expression was wiped off her face the minute she met them. He stared into her soul for a second, before trailing towards where she seemed to be in such a rush to get to.

"where you going to see Jungkook?" the blatant question leaves his lips rather monotone. 

She hadn't expected Jinhwan to come out nowhere. For once, he wasn't actually on her mind since she was so determined to inform Jimin about the text messages. Meeting those eyes of his, any words she had been planning to say seconds ago seemed to disappear. Even though it wasn't long ago that they had that talk about how he understood she still had some sort of feelings for Jungkook, and that he would have to live with it and wouldn't get mad, Jinhwan looked mad. Maybe a part of him forgot about that talk overall, and she guessed he wasn't wrong for that. They were supposed to of taken a break from each other after that sort of conversation, but Soomi ran right back to him like she always did. 

So, could she really blame him for seeming a little upset? No.

"I'm not going to see him," his grip on her wrist seemed to release from how tight it had been. Realizing others might be watching, he lets go of her fully as a breathe leaves his lips and he comes back to his senses. "I was going to go talk to Jimin about what happened," she explains before jealous Jinhwan got any other ideas.

Although she was worried he was going to ask more questions, he only lets out a sigh, "oh, alright, do you want me to come with you?"

Soomi couldn't help but giggle at his question. Jinhwan really was cute, you couldn't deny it, but he was scary when he wanted to be as well. His duality was something she adored deeply about him, so much she could melt into the ground as she met those chocolate eyes of his. 

"No, I'm fine," she assures him, "I'll meet you in the dressing room after I do this and then go see my manager and we can leave, okay?"

The caramel haired boy only nods, and that bright grin of his finds it's way back onto his lips. His eyes were flickering from meeting her own green irises, to her lips, in a cycle that was driving her insane as she shifts her weight from foot to foot in the middle of the backstage for MAMA. 

"I wish I could kiss you right now," he'd blurted with a pout, and Soomi felt her cheeks heating up from the sound of those words leaving his mouth around so many people.

Thankfully, people were too busy rushing past them and trying to get their stuff together to head home after the show, that no one had the time to pay attention to the two idols. Still, she felt the need to raise her hands over her tinted cheeks so Jinhwan couldn't make fun of her.

"Shut up, go to your room," she whines, turning away from the giggling boy who still noticed the effect he had on her.

Jinhwan hesitantly made his way back to the boys dressing room, turning back and glancing at Soomi every now and then. He wasn't trying to be creepy, or clingy, he just meant it when he said he'd watch her like a hawk. The thought that that stalker could still be somewhere around here, watching them, had his senses on their best behavior. How could he take his eyes off her in such a time?

At this thought, he abruptly turns and heads down a different hallway.

"Chim!" she's calling, breaking into a jog to catch up with the seven boys that were all walking together towards their dressing room, "Jimin-ah!"

Since it wasn't as loud in the hallways, her shouts were loud enough for them to hear her. All of the boys turn to see the petite girl running up to them, eyes wide with worry as she looks to her best friend.

"Soo, nice to see you!" Namjoon greets, and the others followed suit as well.

"Your new songs were so good," Hoseok was telling her as she accepted his side hug, "you and Jinhwan are no joke."

She offers them a small smile, "thanks, guys."

Jungkook's eyes were piercing holes into her soul, so much that she was too afraid to meet his eyes. Instead, she clears her throat as she looks to Jimin, "I really need to talk to you."

The other boys were wondrous about what it could possibly be about. They knew about small parts of what was going on, but she didn't really know if either Jungkook or Jimin ever told them the full story. One thing they did know, however, was the tension that was always between the two members anytime it came to Soomi. 

It was obvious Jungkook wasn't happy she was just asking to talk to him. He'd already been on edge because of that competitive personality of his. Jungkook hated seeing her perform with the Jinhwan guy, after all he couldn't stand him. What he hated even more was how happy she looked with him, how bright her eyes would shine, and how big she'd smile. 

Sure, he was glad she found happiness. But he loved her, so strongly it was beginning to tear him apart. Seeing her happy with someone else was a nightmare to Jungkook.

This was why when she asked for Jimin, he wanted to punch a wall. 

"Oh, alright," Jimin nods, and the two head off together the opposite way they'd been going.





///




"I don't know how they could've gotten that," Jimin looked more concerned than Soomi was about it. His nerves were on edge, and he kept messing with his hair and tapping his foot as they stood at the end of one of the hallways where nobody could really see them.

Exhaling deeply, she averts her eyes to her phone screen where the text was pulled up, "I don't know either, it's only getting more creepy. I guess it makes sense for them to be following me then, that's when they were harassing Jungkook instead."

He'd shaken his head in confusion, not understanding the antics of this person, "but yet they never told him."

"Who knows," she deadpans, crossing her arms over her chest and leaning against the wall.

An idea suddenly strikes Jimin's thoughts. "Have you ever tried replying to them?"

Soomi was not thrilled with his suggestion, however. Her eyes widen, and she looks at him like he was insane, "what? No."

"Well, it's worth a try, maybe they'll accidentally tell you something that'll give us more information."

When he put it that way, it wasn't completely a horrible idea. She agrees with a shrug, telling Jimin she would consider trying it. There wasn't much left for Soomi to try anymore, after all. What was the worse that could happen?

A few moments pass, and the two sit in a comfortable silence. Jimin leans against the wall next to her, and his eyes stare ahead blankly, in his own world. Really, he was just enjoying being in her presence, just the two of them. It was a peaceful atmosphere he rarely got to be in anymore, and he missed it.

Meanwhile, Soomi was leaning her head back against the wall. Her eyes were closed, and she had been trying to clear her head of all the garbage that was happening in her life recently. 

Suddenly, a question floats into her thought process, and she figured she might as well ask, "so, how's Jungkook?"

Jimin hadn't been expecting to hear her ask such, and now that she had, he wasn't sure if she really wanted to know the truth.

"Uh," he stutters at first, trying to find his words, "well, he's been on edge after what happened at your apartment. He was pretty upset you didn't agree to come stay with us instead of them."

Soomi purses her lips, recalling the events that happened that night when she found him on the floor outside her apartment. They'd taken him back to the iKON dorms, and when he woke up, she'd unfortunately heard that he hadn't saw anything. Even then, she'd been glad he was okay, and all he had was a bruise on the side of his head from where they'd hit him to knock him out.

Jungkook had insisted she come live with the guys at their massive home, to which she told him it was okay since she was already staying with her newest friend group. He hadn't been happy to hear it, and at first, was confused because last time he checked, they didn't really know what she was going through.

"You told them?" he asks in shock, his voice still a deep tone from the negative emotions flowing through his veins. Those eyes pierce into her heart, and she breaks their eye contact from how intimidating he always managed to be. But just her expression was enough of an answer for him, "they can't protect you, look at them. That Jinhwan guy is the equivalent of a child, Soo, how is he going to fend off anyone-," she'd cut him off by raising a hand to cover his mouth.

There was an unimpressed look in her eyes when she looks back to him. Jungkook's eyes, on the other hand, just looked sad.

"I'll be fine here, Jungkook," she sternly tells him, "and stop being so rude to Jinhwan."

He ignored her second statement as her hand falls back to her side. "I'm just worried about you," he admits.

"I'll be fine, I told you," she assures him, putting a hand on his shoulder so he would meet her eyes again, "I'll see you soon, okay?"

She sighs as she thinks of it all again. Was Jungkook really upset about that?

"Anyway, then he saw that song you two did together, and I think his blood was literally boiling," Jimin finishes explaining, and she lets out a groan of frustration. Her best friend pats her shoulder comfortingly, "don't worry about it so much, though, you know how he is. He's just protective and jealous when any guy breathes in your direction."

"I know, I wish Jungkook would get a grip sometimes, I'm really stressed with all of this right now," she whined, running a hand through her messy hair, "I don't have time to think about them like that."

"I think he's just worried, as well, Mi," Jimin states, and she looked at him with curiosity, "Jungkook has always had this mindset that the only one fit to protect you is - well - him."

He wasn't wrong. "Yeah, that's true," she mutters.

"But don't sweat it, alright? The choice will come to you, and all of this will be over soon enough, Soomi-ah."

She hoped Jimin's witty words were true.

And Jimin hoped that over time, saying that wouldn't break his heart so much.







43:00 | addiction


There was a distressed look on Soomi's face as she walks down the hall where her own dressing room was located. Her and Jimin promised to keep in touch if she got any other text messages, and she meant it when she said it to Jimin. When it came to Jinhwan or Jungkook, such a promise became a bit more difficult since they became white knights and wouldn't get off her back because of their protective traits. Jimin, on the other hand, was the perfect best friend he had always been since she met him as a mere trainee. He wouldn't bombard her or parade her, he would only support her and give her the advice she always needed to hear. It was why although she adored Junhoe with her whole heart, he would always be her number one closest friend, but Junhoe was a close second.

Her thumbs fiddle in a circle, hovering over her iPhone keyboard as her heels click against the grey tile she walks upon. Jimin's words repeat in her head, and she heaves out a frustrated sigh. It really was worth a try, it wasn't like much harm could be done by texting them. Whoever this was already seemed to know everything about her life, so what more could be done?

And maybe she could get them to accidentally slip some valuable information.

Her green eyes scanned over the last texts by them she was sent. That picture of her and Jimin seemed to mock her as she stared down at it. God, she gawked, that was so long ago. It was crazy to recall how her life was so vastly different then. She could nearly faint from recalling being up on that rooftop, all the way back in her home state of New York.

Shaking her head, she could only chuckle. It'd been a long time since she even thought about them. The last time Soomi's family crossed her mind was when Jinhwan had asked her that night they went out late. It was scary to think of them, which was why she avoided doing it. She hadn't talked to them in so long, she figured they didn't care about her anymore. It wasn't like they tried to ever contact her, right?

Then again, it also wasn't like it was possible for them to. They had no way to get any of her socials unless they looked up her Korean name. However, that would only bring them to her Instagram, where all of her fans crowded her DMs and comments. One of them messaging her on there would be pointless.

She shook the thought away. At one point, she was close with her siblings, but back then, they didn't take her side. All she ever caused for her family was trouble, anyway. Why should she care about them?

"Yah, Kim Soomi!" 

The sudden shout makes her nearly drop her phone. She managed to catch a hold of it, turning it off and shoving it in her pocket as she looks up to who dared to scare the life out of her like that. 

There's a playful grin on his lips, and she gaps in disbelief, shaking her head, "Jinhwan-ah, was that really necessary? I thought you were waiting in the iKON dressing room."

He only shrugs as he moves beside her so they could walk together the rest of the way to her room, "I missed you too much."

Soomi could only giggle at him, "you're so clingy."

"Oh please," Jinhwan scoffs, "I'll show you how clingy I am when we get back to the apartment."

The feeling of her cheeks heating up causes Soomi to cover her face with her hands. It wasn't like she was even innocent in the slightest, but the way he would say those things so suggestively always put her in a mood. Truly, it was difficult for her to handle Jinhwan when he had to speak in such a manner.

"Is something wrong?" he took note of her actions, raising a brow. His tone was so taunting she wanted to hit him, but it would be no use either way. He knew exactly what he did to her, and it made him happier than words could explain.

"Just shush," she mumbles, walking a bit faster so she was ahead of him. 

His cute giggle filled her ears as they reach her dressing room. Soomi wondered then how someone could manage to say something so sexual but then emit such an adorable laugh only seconds later. Although it was a good thing, his duality still upset her. 

Shaking her head once again in dismay, she twists the handle, pushing the door open to see her manager.

He'd been lounging on one of the sofa's, presumably waiting for her. She was probably a bit later than the time she had supposed to of gotten there, and a sigh of relief left his lips as he sees her enter. The second he rises, Soomi made sure to politely bow to him even though it wasn't necessary at that point. She was just thankful to her sweet manager who always tried to make everything good for her.

Jinhwan enters behind her, figuring it wasn't a big deal since the two were allowed to be 'friends' now under the company.

"Soomi, nice to see you," her manager sends her a kind grin, but then his eyes land on the boy entering behind her. There's a look in his eyes neither of them notice, and Jinhwan made sure to bow to him as he hums, "Jinhwan, I didn't know you two were such good friends, but I suppose since you wanted to do a song together, I should've expected such, huh?" he chuckles lightly, and the two send him innocent grins, laughing a bit with him.

"So, was there anything on the camera's?" she cut right to the point, not wanting to talk about her and Jinhwan. It was likely he would try to make some witty comment, and the thought of their song together reminded her much of how that all came to be in the first place.

To think it was all something she said in the spur of the moment. It was quite funny if you thought about it. Soomi had just blurted out the first thing that came to mind that day because Jinhwan had been practically torturing her. Who would've thought the song actually was going to happen? Neither of them did, that was for sure. But yet here they were, having had just performed it at MAMA.

A sympathetic look falls over the manager's face, and he diverts his eyes elsewhere as he sighs, "unfortunately no, I'm sorry, I don't know how none of them could catch whoever would do something like that," he shakes his head dully, "don't worry yourself too much, we'll find whoever did this."

She couldn't hide how disappointed she was to hear that. Soomi was seriously hoping there would be something on one of those cameras. It just didn't make sense how someone got backstage, and into her dressing room, with nobody catching them, not even the security camera's themselves. In fact, it was so upsetting she wanted to puke. Why did this person have to torment her out of all people? She needed a break.

Holding a hand to her head, she waves the other dismissively as she insists with a small smile, "it's all good, manager-nim, thank you for the help like always."

"You don't have to thank me, Soomi-ah, it's my job," he laughs, walking forward to pat her on the back gently, "I'm proud of you, always keep up the good work. I'll keep investigating the camera's and get back to you if I find anything."

She only hums an okay, and her manager makes his way into the hall of the room. Just when she sought her and Jinhwan were alone in the room, his voice breaks through the air once again, "you guys' song was great, but don't get too close, now!"






///





"Let's go out tomorrow," Jinhwan's abrupt demand causes Soomi to slowly look up from her cellphone. Her legs were laying across the rest of the sofa, as she was leaning her head on Jinhwan's shoulder, sitting sideways. The two of them were just hanging out in the living room of the boys' dorm, since they were all so loud it was impossible for anyone to sleep if they wanted to.

There was no telling what time it was at that point. It'd been a long while since they'd all gotten home from MAMA, and the boys all believed it was the perfect time to celebrate the awards they had won. Without further ado, they'd gotten out nearly every bottle of alcohol in the house and immediately starting their celebratory drinking session. 

Since some of them drank rather often, it took them a while to really get smashed. But it wasn't long until they were playing music at max volume from the speakers, and would now be found dancing and twerking (or something of the sort) atop the kitchen table and counters. Soomi could see it from where she sat on the couch, facing the opened kitchen. To nobody's surprise, they couldn't twerk, and it had her wincing in cringe as she covered the view of it with one hand, still holding her phone with the other.

"Hm, go out where?" she finally hums in response to the only other human in the iKON dorm that wasn't drunk. Jinhwan actually loved drinking, as he was even un-ironically called the wine king. The thing was, since he started hanging around Soo so much, he never drank anymore. There was a small feeling she had as to why it was, but she had yet to test the waters on if it was true. Really, it was a subject she was unsure if she wanted to get into, but a part of her felt bad that he never drank anymore since it was something he liked doing. Poor Jinhwan was even refusing to do so on what was supposed to be a happy, bright night. 

He shrugged, leaning his head to the side so it was against the top of her own, "anywhere you want."

Soomi chuckles, "are you trying to ask me out on a date?"

"Nah, I don't even need to ask."

"Fair point."

He laughs in shock, "I was joking, of course I'm asking you on a date, it's been too long since we went on one."

Soomi shifts out of her seating position so that she could face him, looking him in the eyes as she does so, "do I even need to answer?"

Those taunting brown irises peer back into her own. "Well, I didn't ask, I demanded."

Scoffing, she rolls her eyes, "course, mister gentleman."

"Yah!" he whines from her obvious sarcasm, "I am a gentleman."

Her face read only the solemn word no, as she looked to him with that exact expression plastered on it. Before she could utter a word, however, the sudden screeches of a particular boy was heard entering the living room they were trying to peacefully sit in.

"Soomi-ah!" Junhoe whines out, holding two beers in his hands as he makes his way over to where they sat, "why aren't you drinking? It's a night of celebration!"

Looking up from his proposition, she didn't even get a chance to think before a bottle was practically shoved in her face by her close friend. Soomi didn't blame Junhoe, he was shit-faced out of his mind and had no common human knowledge in those moments. It wasn't his fault, really, but she still felt a lump form in her throat as she looked down at the bottle he was holding out to her.

Alcohol, she blinks at it, her hands tempted to snatch it from him as he kept crying out for her to let loose.

Suddenly, it was ripped from her sight. "She doesn't drink, June-ya," the snap of the eldest was bitter as he sets the bottle on the coffee table in front of them.

The drunk boy pouts as he looks to the debby downer, "why are you being so un-fun, hyung?!" he's complaining in slurs, picking up the bottle again. This time he throws it onto Soomi's lap, and the girl's eyes nearly bulge out of her sockets as she looks down at it. "You always act like you control poor Soo."

She really couldn't. Jinhwan was trying to take it away from her for a good reason, one she didn't want to think about.


"Put the bottle down, Soomi!" she didn't care to listen to the endless yells. A playful but yet sorrowful giggle left her lips as she pops the cap off the bottle, before swinging her head back and holding it up to her lips. At this point, she was already so full of alcohol that she could barely manage to even hold the bottle in her small hand. Her arm trembles as she continues to chug the beverage, feeling the sensation burning the back of her throat as she swallows it in such a quick manner one would think she was crazy. In reality, poor Soomi wasn't crazy, she was just lost in a dark place she didn't think she was getting out of any time soon.

She was so obsessed with emptying the bottle down her throat that she didn't take notice of the booming footsteps approaching her. A squeal leaves her lips as the bottle was ripped from her hands. "Yah!" she whines out like a child, spinning to face the boy who'd dared to steal her beloved. 

At this point in time, she had no concept of anything. Her eyes didn't understand what they were reading off of Jinhwan's face when she looks up to him. Those brown eyes of his stare into her soul, and there were tears bound to well in his eyes from how bad it was hurting him seeing her like this. Sure, they hadn't known each other long at all, but he couldn't deny how much he cared for her, how heavily he was beginning to adore her. It broke his heart to see her destroying herself over someone that ruined her, she didn't deserve this level of pain and he wanted to so badly remove it. But it was too late, what could he do?

She had a bad problem, so bad he didn't have any idea how to begin to solve it.

"You're done," he's sternly telling her, but the girl only cries out like a child who's toy was stolen. Her tiny hands grab at the bottle he kept moving away from her, and she was on the verge of bursting into tears from how badly she craved it.

"Jinhwan-ah, please," her voice went from a yell to that of a whisper, barely audible to him if there was anymore noise other than their breathing in the kitchen of her apartment, "I need it..." her voice trails off into the air, seeming to crack at the end of her short statement. Just hearing her say something like that gave him physical pain in his chest, it was awful to see anyone in such a state, nonetheless someone like Soomi.

She had so much going for her, she was the top solo artist in the entire country of South Korea. With how successful and bright she once was, it was horrid to see her breaking over some stupid guy.

He hated him.

"You don't need it, okay?" he tries to tell her as she'd sunk to the ground. Her hands go to cover her face, and she seemed to be sweating profusely. Never had he seen anyone this badly addicted to substances, but it was like her entire body was breaking down. 

"I can't," she breathes out, her breathe heavier with each word, "I can't function without it."

Jinhwan kneels on the ground, having had set the bottle down beside him. Soomi seemed to be on the verge of passing out, and he puts a hand on her forearm to make sure she didn't fall over.

Her green eyes are curious as they wander to the bottle beside him. Even though she was just barely hanging on to her conscious, her small hand reaches out and snatches up the bottle before he could stop her.

Raising it to her pink lips, she intakes one last sip despite Jinhwan trying to grab the bottle from her again. By the time he did, it didn't matter anyway, because her small body had fallen to the tiled floor, and she passed out cold.













44:00 | car rides


"Shouldn't we stay to watch over the others?" she'd asked, having a rather hard time keeping up with Jinhwan's fast pace as they made their way through the parking lot of their apartments. His hand was holding hers so tightly she couldn't get out of his grip if she wanted to, and she was practically being dragged behind him as they jogged towards his vehicle. 

"No, they'll be fine," was all he says as they reach his car. The keys in his other hand that wasn't intertwined with hers jingle in the night air, and the Toyota let out a ding as he unlocked it. Jinhwan goes to the passenger side, opening the door for her. She took his stern tone as a sign he was right, that the drunk boys upstairs in the dorm would be perfectly fine. They tended to drink all the time, right? Nothing would happen to them this one time the two leave.

His hand leaves hers as he closes the door, making his way to the drivers side. The late December air was prickling at her skin as she rubs her hands together, trying to find any form of warmth. Jinhwan hadn't really given her a chance to change into warm clothes, at least not warm enough. He'd rushed her out of the apartment when she was about to possibly start drinking with the others since Junhoe seemed to really want her to. A part of her knew she shouldn't, but when someone was constantly shoving the beverage's in her face, it was impossible for her to say no. 

Jinan knew that, hence the reason he claimed they were suddenly going out. She'd only gotten to throw on one of his hoodies on over the tights and t-shirt she wore, throwing on some slippers that weren't even hers before he'd dragged her out the door.

A sigh leaves his lips as he climbed into the car to her left. Wasting no time, Jinhwan starts the car, the ignition roaring to life. The radio came on immediately, a soft indie song by the Black Skirts plays from the speakers at median volume. Soomi recognized the band, only because he listened to them so much, and she pulls the hoodie sleeves over her hands to warm them, leaning her head back into the seat and letting the music be her relaxation.

Her green eyes watch the apartment become smaller and smaller through the window as he pulls out of the parking lot. Pinpointing exactly where the boys' dorm was on the tall building, she couldn't help but look to her own apartment as well. Really, she couldn't help but miss it. It was always a dream to have her own place, and she adored her apartment even if it was small compared to the iKON's. Soomi had been so excited when she was given the keys to it, being told she got to live in one of the YG apartment complexes was a dream to her. 

That being said, it wasn't only for people in YG, they just rented out certain apartment's in it for their artists. Normal people lived in their building, too, but since the apartments were so expensive, it was usually retired elders.

The apartment building eventually disappeared out of her vision as they drove further and further away from it. Soomi let out a yawn, glancing to the time on the radio screen; 4 AM. She had yet to get any sleep in what already felt like too long, her body was already crying. Pulling her knees to her chest, she uses the giant sleeve of the hoodie to lean against the window. Inhaling, Jinhwan's cologne enters her scent, and she used the familiar aura, plus the fact he was only feet away from her, to fall into a quick nap.

Meanwhile, Jinhwan tried his best to keep his own eyes open as he drove. He didn't actually have any plans as to where they were going, he was just driving. When Junhoe was tempting Soomi to drink, he could see it working in her eyes. She was going to take the bottle, and he knew he couldn't let it happen. Just taking it away from her hadn't proved useful, so he did the second thing that came to his mind.

Taking her away from the scene of his drunken friends would prevent her from even thinking about it. That was the safest, he didn't want Soomi to ever drink as long as he was around. If she ever got how she was back then when they met, he didn't know what he'd do. 

His hand reaches out to change the station, hoping to find something that was lively enough to keep him awake. Jinhwan had yet to get much sleep recently, either. Something managed to happen what seemed like every night now, so both his and Soomi's sleeping schedules were horrible.

Having had been in a rapid motion of switching from station to station, he suddenly paused at the sound of a certain song.


Flowers that bloomed in my dry heart
What a beautiful thing that I think of tonight beside us
Though I can't promise you great things
Let's be together forever


"Anio~," the whining voice of Soomi was heard as she reaches over to turn down the blasting volume that Jinhwan had cranked up practically all the way. Her hope for sleep went down the drain just like that, and she winced at the sound of her own rap.

The boy in the driver's seat is in fits of giggles as he witnessed in the corner of his eye her reaction from hearing the song, "yah, yah, what's the problem? You don't like our song?"

A deep exhale left her lips as she sat up straight from how she'd been laying against the window, "my rap tone in the second verse sounds horrible."

Jinhwan rolls his eyes, knowing she was just being self conscious. Shaking his head, he reaches over to turn up the radio once again, as her rap had ended and the chorus was on again. He sang most of the chorus, but Soomi's background vocals were there occasionally. In their song, her job was mostly the rap and his was vocals, since that was what they were both known for. 

Still, he swore Soomi could really sing.

"Your fingertips, your fragrant lotion, you touch my dry heart," he finds himself singing along to the lyrics he knew with his entire heart. His hands grip the steering wheel a bit tighter as he bops his head back and forth, looking silly on purpose.

Soomi couldn't help but watch him, snickering at the idiotic movements he was doing as he drove. Something about Jinhwan was so irresistible to her, and she couldn't take anymore of whatever 'dance' he was trying to do as she reaches her hands out, whining like a baby, "stop, focus on driving, pabo!"

Giggling at her high-pitched voice he still found adorable to that day, he clears his throat from having had been singing seconds ago, focusing his eyes fully on the road. "I'm not even sure where we are anymore," he admitted in a mumble as he took note of their surroundings.

Her eyes widen at this news, "yah, for real, Kim Jinhwan?" she's gawking in disbelief at his idiocy. Turning in the seat, she squints into the dark night as she peers out the glass of the window. They were on one of the main highways, and she desperately searches for any street sign indicating them of their location.

The two could've just taken out their phones, but they were too silly to think of such. Either way, seconds later she hums in relief, "oh, you're heading towards Seongnam," she informs as she tries to finish reading the sign before they pass, "it's - ten minutes," her mouth nearly fell open like a hippo at the sight. "How long have we been driving?!" she looks to Jinhwan in shock. He seemed just as surprised at the sight as he only shrugged his shoulders.

Both of them were aware that Seongnam was a good 50 minutes away from their beloved apartment in Seoul. How they got this far away - no idea. She guessed they just really lost track of time between her napping and Jinhwan just peacefully listening to the radio while he drove.

"Do you have any schedules until New Years?" the unexpected question causes her to look to the boy in curiosity. 

He wasn't paying attention to her though since he was driving. Pausing to think on it for a moment, she hums after contemplation, "no, nothing until the performance's on New Years for the festival."

A wave of solace splashes over Jinhwan's face, and he seemed to lean back a bit in his seat as he hums, "alright, good, I guess we're taking a road trip then."

Soomi's green eyes nearly bulge out of her sockets. Turning to fully face him, she didn't care he wasn't looking at her as she practically screeches, "what do you mean, road trip? We didn't even bring any of our stuff!"

"So, that's what stores are for, Soomi," he shakes his head as if she should've figured that out herself, speaking casually as if what he just said wasn't absolutely absurd.

Having no idea what threw such a thought into his head, Soomi could only lean back into the leather seat of his car, scoffing in shock. He thought they were just going to buy all the necessities they needed to stay away from home? She understood they were wealthy, but that didn't mean she was okay with doing something as silly as that. 

Either way, as she diverts her attention to the night sky outside the window of Jinhwan's car, she realized she didn't have much of a choice anyway. They'd already wandered all the way to Seongnam, what did they really have left to lose? Soomi and Jinhwan needed some time off as it was, maybe this was perfect for them. It was like everything had fallen into place, and they finally had a chance to get away from all the stress and drama in Seoul, even if it was completely unintentional.

Who would've thought that Jinhwan panic rushing her out of the apartment so she didn't end up drinking would cause them to end up here? 

Deciding it wasn't so bad after all, she leans her chin in the palm of her right hand as she leans against the window, watching the scenery go by. A dent would surely be made in their wallets by the time they got back, and who even knew where they were going to stay, but she felt happy. Here she was, spending a day away with the boy she loved, where nobody could bother them, it wouldn't be bad at all.

The sudden feeling of someone's hand atop hers caused her to jump a bit since she had been in such deep thought. She realized that since they'd been driving, she already missed the feeling of his skin on hers. Jinhwan's hand was warm as he grabbed hers, and she couldn't help but grin giddily as he intertwined their fingers, leaving their hands on her thigh as he drove with the other.

Not a word had left his lips as he continued to watch the road, Soomi on the other hand, was getting lost in just staring at his side profile. He really was too gorgeous for words, she was glad he was apparently too distracted to notice her practically drooling over him.

At least, that's what she thought.

"Take a picture, Soomi-ah, it'll last longer."

She'd definitely heard those words before. Rolling her eyes, a playful smile spreads over the boy's face as she hits his arm that was extended to hold her hand with her right one. It didn't affect him in the slightest, though, and he only ended up laughing at her actions.

Pouting, she looks away from his face to stare out the window again instead. They seemed to be entering the town of Seongnam now, and she made note to watch out for some place to stay as she mutters, "I hate you."

Jinhwan didn't take what she said to heart in the slightest, "I love you, Soomi."














45:00 | playful


The 50th yawn leaves Soomi's lips as she walks, Jinhwan's arm securely over her shoulders as he's carrying the bags from their shopping spree on his other arm. The rest of the bags were in her hands, and she felt like her arms were bound to break from the weight of them all. Their feet click against the floor of the hotel they were walking through, a comfortable silence fell between the two lovers as they make their way to the room Jinhwan bought for them to spend the night.

Upon arriving to Seongnam, Jinhwan decided it was too big of a city for his liking. He wanted to go somewhere where they could truly enjoy their time together, somewhere where it was less likely for anyone to recognize them. He wished it wasn't something he still had to worry about, but the hard truth was the fact even if they ignored it, they still had to be careful about their relationship getting out to the media. Soomi had been through it before, hence her cautious attitude towards them in the beginning. They just couldn't afford anything like that getting out, not at the peak of their career's. 

Even then, it wasn't like Soomi and Jinan were officially together. She thought they would've been a long time ago, all until everything got in the way. By then, they just hadn't had time to focus on their real relationship and where they wanted to take it. This was the first time they were getting to really be with each other truly alone.

Or at least they thought they were alone.

The truth was, there was too much going on for them to make it official. But it sure felt like it was already.

Finally reaching their room door, Jinhwan's arm leaves Soomi's shoulders. He pulls out the key card, sliding it through the insert and listening as a click sounds through the empty, quiet hall they stood in. 

"Here we are," he sings out cheerily, on his best behavior from excitement at the fact they were out together, away from society once and for all. 

The small town of Ilsan-ri was where you'd find the two. A breathe of pure relief leaves her lips as she walks in the room first, followed by Jinhwan, who flicked the lights on as he closed the door and locked it. Illuminating before her, Soomi inhaled the fresh air in their clean, nicely decorated room as she sets down the shopping bags. It was definitely not in her best interest to unpack any of the things they bought right then, she honestly didn't even feel like washing up even though she needed to.

Speaking of such, it was exactly what Jinan was doing as he bent down to pick up one of the bags she set down. A plump sounded through the quiet air as Soomi had jumped onto the bed, her back hitting the plush mattress as she lays back into it, already closing her eyes. Exhaling deeply, he chuckles at her state, taking out things like toothbrushes and toothpaste before setting the bag down again.

"Yah," he calls out to her, chunking one of the toothbrush's towards her. It lands on her lap, causing the girl to open her eyes and peer down at it, a frown forming on her face at the thought of getting up again. "Wash up before you go to sleep," he'd commanded to her like a parent, and she groans at his words.

A whine-y child was exactly what Soomi was, "I don't want to get up, why can't I do it tomorrow morning?!" she's crying as if something as simple as brushing her teeth was a difficult task. 

Jinhwan shakes his head at her, sighing deeply, "I guess I won't kiss you goodnight then, huh?" his teasing words seemed to sink into her skull instantly. He hadn't even looked up at her again when her footsteps were already heard against the carpeted floor, and her petite body rushed into the bathroom he was standing by, flicking on the light.

Giggling, he walked to stand beside her. It was a decently sized bathroom, covered in white tile and homey decor. Soomi's eyes were peering at herself in the mirror as she squirted paste on her brush, before shoving it in her mouth and going to town. Jinhwan found himself lost just watching her, she was the equivalent of a kid sometimes with her childish demeanor. Soomi could be cute when she wanted to, even if her stage presence was the exact opposite. He found it quite funny. Watching her on stage, one would never expect her to act like she really did; a kid.

Well, not always.

But right then, it was all she looked like as she spits into the sink, before dipping down to get water in her mouth. Swooshing it around, she spit that out, too, before turning to the curious Jinhwan with a grin, showing off her bright white teeth.

He raised a brow even though he knew what she wanted, "what are you looking at me like that for?"

She pouts from his act, "still no goodnight kiss?"

There was a tone of voice she always had when she really wanted something. It was similar to a girl sticking out her bottom lip, or giving you puppy eyes. In that same sense, Soomi could heighten the pitch of her voice and slur her words ever so slightly. It'd make her even more irresistible than she already was, and Jinhwan couldn't help it as he exhales in defeat.

But just when he's dipping down to have gently pecked her lips only for a second, she'd triumphantly giggled, spinning on her heel and running away into the main room. 

He was left to gawk at what she just did. "Yah - you're not funny!" he calls her to in a fake annoyed tone, trying not to show how badly he wanted to kiss her.

That tone of voice didn't bother her in the slightest, as she'd only called back from where she was already perched on the bed, "brush your teeth, pabo!"








///






Soomi's chest heaves up and down, her breath was heavy, as if she'd just ran a marathon and hadn't stopped for a break even once. Her heart was beating out of her chest, and her body was crying out for a rest as she falls back onto the mattress once again. Although it'd been clean when they entered, it definitely wasn't now. A part of her felt bad for whatever worker got tasked with the job of cleaning this room after they left.

It'd take her a long while to fully catch her breathe and be able to do so normally again. Jinhwan lays next to her moments later, and she uses her last bit of energy to shift to lay on her side so she could look at him. 

Words really couldn't explain the feeling she got in her chest at just the sight of bare chested Kim Jinhwan. He was one of the most gorgeous guys she'd ever gotten the chance to witness up close in her life. Nothing could amount to someone like him, no one could, maybe not even Jungkook. 

"Can I sleep now?" her weak voice breaks the silence other than the sound of their heavy breathing.

Running a hand through his messy caramel locks of hair, Jinhwan chuckles as he looks to her, "Yeah, you should, it's late," he hums, shifting so he was also laying on his side like she was. His hand reaches out to push her hair behind her ear, and he'd cockily added, "sorry to the pain you'll feel in the morning."

An annoyed glare was shot his way, causing him to only burst into more fits of giggles for the 90th time that night. Soomi couldn't take him and his jokes sometimes, even when he said them so light heartedly. She was too tired to mess with him back, though, as she only yawns once again, her eyes already fighting themselves closed.

There was no telling what time it was then. It was far too late for either of them to be awake, though. If they wanted to be up by lunch tomorrow, they needed to sleep now. Jinhwan knew this as he scoots closer so he could wrap his arms around her. 

Soft breathes are already escaping her lips as she subconsciously lays her head on his chest. Even though he was just as tired as she was from the long day they'd just had, Jinhwan couldn't help but stay awake to admire her. While asleep, Soomi was still so beautiful in his eyes. Just seeing her in such a state, knowing it was because of him, knowing her smile was because of him, gave him a feeling of content that he thought he'd never get to feel. 

Ever since the first day he saw her in the hall of their apartment, he knew there was something special about her.
