He felt it in his gut from the second they made eye contact, and to that day, it remained in his veins. That love he'd felt for her for so long was truly being returned to him. It didn't matter how many girls threw themselves at him, how many confessed to him, or how many claimed they loved him online. At the end of the day, none of them mattered to him if they weren't Kim Soomi, and he'd never meant something so sincerely.

Tightening his grip on her, he found himself closing his eyes, too, as he tried to fall into a deep sleep right along with the girl he loved. It turned out to be pretty difficult for him, though, as there were still so many things on his mind.

Just when he felt as if he was going to pass out once and for all, his phone vibrated from the end table behind him. 

Reaching out for it, he had to remove one arm from around Soomi. This might've woken her up if it was any other night, but she was far too tired to be affected by the small movement.

did you guys go to her apartment or something?

handled that better. everyone was just so thrilled to
drink tonight. we're just sort of sobering up now.

it wasn't so bad. but junhoe was tempting soomi to drink,

46:00 | don't trust coffee


"Wait, wait!" Soomi is crying as she jumps around, "hold that pose!"

She sounded like some sort of paparazzi, and it caused Jinhwan to chuckle as he did what she said. A click sounds through the air as she'd taken the picture with his phone, a victorious look on her face as she walks up to him to show him what she'd taken.

"That's pretty good," he acknowledges, "thanks, babe." He'd wrapped an arm around her shoulder momentarily, leaning forward to leave a kiss on her forehead.

Playfully, she wrinkles her nose at his actions, even though she'd felt a rather painful ping in her chest. Just hearing a name like that roll off his tongue really messed with her emotions. Jinhwan hadn't taken notice, for once, of what it did to her as he was too focused on posting the picture to his followers.

She took out her own phone, assuming it was what he was doing as the two nonchalantly walk back into their room from the balcony. Pulling up the Instagram app, she kept refreshing her feed, until the familiar picture she'd just taken shows up, posted by the infamous gnani____ himself.

        

"Aw, Jinhwan-ah is so pretty," she was gushing as she double tapped the picture. He'd turned upon hearing her words, only to see her staring down at her phone screen lovingly as if he wasn't literally two feet away from her.

He waited a second for her to remember the fact they were on a holiday together and look up. Moments passed, and Soomi was still staring at her phone screen. She'd been busy screenshotting and cropping the photo so she could set it as her home screen.

Then, she had to actually reply to the texts she had gotten over night. Most of them were from the boys, asking where she went. She also got a text from Jimin, just checking in on her and asking what she was up to.

Chim:

Chim:
honestly I'm happy to hear that, you
needed to take a break from this place

Chim:

manager?

Mi:

Mi:

Mi:

Chim:

were busy with stuff but he didn't seem to believe

Chim:

 the two of them. a part of me misses Jungkook too.

hang out with us, but you can come over
anytime you want, Mi

Chim:

"Soomi-ah," a whining boy suddenly snatches her phone out of her hand, putting it into rest mode before chunking it behind them onto the bed. There's a slight frown on his lips when he looks to her, "I take you on a holiday and you don't even pay attention to me."

At the sight of his dull expression, she couldn't help but feel even a little bad. Stepping forward, she wraps her arms around his torso, "sorry, I was trying to reply to everyone!" She mutters into his chest defensively.

She was worried he might actually be upset with her, even if it was for fun. But deep down, they both knew it was impossible for him to ever get mad at her again. Gradually, his arms find a way around her, and he nearly squeezes the life out of her. Squeals of shock leave her lips as he'd hugged her, before bending down to pick her up.

His hands were gripping her thighs, and she nearly had a heart attack from how abruptly he did such a thing. Protectively, her arms go around his neck so she didn't fall to her death.

Jinhwan's eyes pierced holes into her soul as he'd thrown her on to the soft mattress. She barely had any time to react to what was going on as he'd towered over her, holding himself up with one arm. His irises were shiny from the light beaming on them from the windows, and she could hardly breathe at the sight of him above her in such a way. It took her back to last night — or more like early morning — when she'd given herself to him once again like many times.

And like always, it'd left an undeniable pain in between her legs. At this point, however, Soomi was pretty used to it. Her and Jinhwan had been doing it rather regularly, so much that it was beginning to be normal for her to have a hard time walking in the morning. The hard truth was that Jinhwan was rough, but she didn't necessarily have a problem with that. If anything, it was good in her eyes.

His face was so close to hers as he'd dipped down. A lump gets caught in her throat as his breathe fans her skin, and her eyes were having a hard time maintaining contact with his. In times like this, Jinhwan really broke her sanity.

Just when she thought he was going to kiss her, as his lips kept inching closer and closer, a smirk takes over his lips. "Now that I have your attention," he hums out, backing up from her face and climbing off of her. His feet hit the carpet as he rises, acting as if he hadn't just done such a thing to her emotions, "I was going to go get us some coffee."

She could only scoff as she looked to him. There was still that cocky smile plastered on his face, and even though a part of her wanted to slap it off of him for what he just did — they both knew she would never.

"Alright," she sighs, turning away to go find the bag that had clothes she'd bought yesterday in it. Jinhwan had gotten up before her and gotten ready, but she was still in that same hoodie and tights she wore on the way here, "get me whatever you get, I'll stay here and get ready."

He nods in confirmation, walking to where she was standing with a bag, shoveling through it. Once again, Soomi didn't even notice what he was doing until she felt his plush lips against her forehead.

"I'll be back soon, Soo," he assures her, and she only nods to him with a smile.

Her eyes couldn't help but watch as he walks to the door, the key card in hand so he could get in and out. Just as he's unlocking it, he'd turned back to call to her dramatically, "I love you~!"

He's so loud, she chuckles at the childish look on his face. Never had she seen Jinhwan so bright in all her time knowing him. It warmed her heart that the reason for his mood was her.

Seeing him like that gave her purpose.

"I love you too, Jinan-ah," she hadn't yelled it like he had, but he was satisfied by the reply either way. He blew her a kiss with his hand before fully walking out the door.

She could only watch as it closed behind him, before a click sounded indicating he locked it. Soomi was left alone in their hotel room, with her heart on her sleeve and a stupid smile on her lips.

Sure, she truly hadn't made a decision between the two boys yet. At the end of each day, they both had a special place in her heart. Jungkook was her first love, and nobody ever genuinely gets over their first love. There's always going to be a part of her that belonged to him. Jinhwan on the other hand, was her beloved savior. She felt so different when she was with him. It was a feeling she couldn't seem to quite put her finger on, but she didn't think she ever wanted to stop feeling it.

Still, despite the psycho that was threatening her every so often, who she desperately needed to catch so she could live freely and get her life back on track — she also needed to figure this out. It wasn't right for her to keep them both waiting. She knew that Jinhwan was still hurting deep down knowing she wasn't truly his yet. He knew that a part of her still loved her ex fiancé, and he wouldn't be complete until he really knew what her heart desired. Soomi needed to figure it out before something happened. Jungkook apparently wasn't doing great with it on his mind either from what Jimin told her.

Finding an outfit to wear, she hurried to get dressed. Soomi decided she would try to focus on that more often, and there was only one way to do that.

When she was dressed and sought that she looked at least kind of presentable, she walks back into the main room. Exhaling deeply, her phone was clenched in her hand as she clicks on the FaceTime app. Scrolling for what felt like years, she finally finds his name.

Pursing her lips in thought for a moment, she sighs in confirmation, tapping the call button.






///





"Five shots of espresso in each, please," Jinhwan was saying with a kind smile to the worker at the coffee shop downstairs in the hotel. It was a small little place, but he was glad there was at least somewhere where they had coffee. God knew him and Soo really needed something to keep them awake.

Weirdly enough, the place was rather empty. He guessed he shouldn't be surprised since it was such a small town. People staying there were probably just passing through from place to place, and there just didn't happen to be many that day.

"No problem," the lady smiles to him as she takes his money, before disappearing somewhere in the back to make the drinks.

Jinhwan yawns as he awaits for his order to be ready. There was no telling how long it would take, but he was feeling pretty patient that day anyway. What did he have to be upset about? He was getting to spend a holiday with the girl he loved, even if it was last minute.

The only thing that could bother him was thinking about the horrid person that kept messing with her. Nothing had ever pissed him off as hearing about what they kept doing to her. He swore he would murder them if he ever caught them himself.

He didn't think they would have anything to do since they weren't even in Seoul. There was no way they knew they had left, right? He figured they would be fine.

Moments passed, and he felt like it was taking longer than it would normally for anyone to just make two simple coffees with extra shots of espresso. The lady seemed pretty tired that morning though, so he didn't want to be rude and push her. He figured she was just taking her time or something.

Trying to find a way to pass time, he pulls his phone out of his pocket, scrolling through things like socials.

June-ya: hanbin told me I was tempting Soo to drink last night, I want to punch myself

June-ya: I figured you were pretty upset about it, I'm sorry about that

Jinan-hyung: it's okay, the important part is that she didn't

Jinan-hyung: but it was extremely upsetting

The sudden sound of screaming was heard from the kitchen in the hotel. Jinhwan couldn't help but tense up a bit at the unpleasant noise. His eyes wander to the doors that were closed, wondering what could be going on. A part of him was sort of uncomfortable, was that lady alright? Why would someone be yelling this early in the morning?

He would've done something and asked if everything was okay. However, it stopped a bit later, and the lady had suddenly come out of the kitchen with the two drinks in hand.

"Here you go," she says cheerily, sliding them across the counter to him.

Jinhwan didn't even get to thank her when she had spun on her heel and disappeared somewhere in the back of the hotel. She seemed a bit on edge compared to how she was when she took his order. Obviously, it was because of whoever had yelled, and he couldn't help but feel rather disappointed in that person.

If this was another day, he might've tried to do something and made sure she was okay, but there was a girl waiting for him back in the hotel room. Soomi was probably bored out of her mind if she hadn't already fallen asleep again.

Deciding it was for the best, he whistled a song to himself as he makes his way down the hall to the stairs. Unfortunately, this hotel didn't have an elevator, which wasn't too surprising — but it sure was annoying.

It was a lot of stairs to climb up. He found himself wondering how the hell they managed to get up all of them last night.

Jinhwan walks up a couple flights of stairs, before realizing he should probably text her and let her know she was coming back right now. After he does this, he holds the drinks once again in both hands, continuing his walk and sipping the coffee every once in a while.

The taste of it caught him off guard a bit. It was an odd taste for something like coffee, but he guessed it was some sort of country thing they added. They did stuff like that back in Jeju, so it wasn't abnormal to think.

Shrugging it off, he drank more and more of the coffee as he made his way up the stairs. Jinhwan's excitement only rose as he got closer and closer to the floor they were staying on. It seemed like it was harder for him to be away from Soomi the longer time went on. Perhaps he was really starting to get too attached.

Just as he's going up another flight of stairs, a ping hit his head. It was like an abrupt migraine, and he paused for a second to take in what just happened. He wasn't someone to really ever have problems like that.

Trying to ignore it, he continued walking, but then black spots began to take over his vision.

"Jinjja," he's muttering his frustration with whatever was up with his body, "out of all the times."

But still, he forced himself to continue going.

Which was a bad decision on Jinhwan's part. He should've realized there was something terribly wrong, he should've called Soomi.

Because as soon as he reached their floor, his body weakened, and he felt himself losing consciousness. Setting the coffee's down as he reaches the floor fully, Jinhwan holds his hands to his head, wincing in pain.

He wanted to call her, but it felt like he couldn't move. It was too late.

Weakening, he was weakening and he didn't know why or how to stop it.

That was his last thought as he fell to the ground.












47:00 | happy holidays


"A song?" the words fly off her tongue with curiosity. Her eyes peer at Jungkook through her phone screen camera, and there's a tint of excitement in those doe eyes of his. "I didn't think you were that into song writing these days," she boasts, a grin finding its way to her lips, "are you gonna show it to me, or what?"

He breaks their eye contact through the facetime call to look down at the notebook in his hands. The lyrics to his song were messily scribbled across the page. Intently, he stares down at them, and Soomi could only watch his eyes rake across the page that was out of her view. Jungkook figured it probably wouldn't be the best idea to tell her the lyrics right now. After all, it would ruin any chance of him ever making it a surprise like he wanted to.

"Not right now," he finally ventures, "maybe sometime though, it's not done yet."

That had been his attempt to get her off his back about it. However, being the songwriter she was herself, it only made her press even harder as she whined, "even better, let me see it so I can help you finish it!"she brightly offers, but Jungkook only waves a hand of dismissal.

"Anio~," he bleated out in a small voice, "I want to finish it myself."

Soomi still had no intention of stopping her pestering, even if he used that cutesy tone with her. At least that's what she told herself, until he'd peered into the camera, sticking out his bottom lip. Jungkook had a thing with always getting her to do whatever he wanted, making it hard for her to ever do the same to him when she was so affected by anything and everything he did. The sight of him making such a face at her, widening those doe eyes of his ever so slightly and looking straight into her heart, nearly caused her to explode. 

"Okay, fine!" she's crying in defense so he would stop looking at her like that.

Triumphantly, there's a giddy smile on his face as he returns to a normal expression. She could only shake her head in disbelief at the things he did. 

Deeply exhaling, she leans back into the pillow of the king sized bed she laid upon. A comfortable silence overcomes the two, but it was something they were both used to. After knowing each other for so long and dating for well over a year, it was hard for them to be uncomfortable with each other, at least like this. In person, it might be another story depending what Jungkook was mischievously going to try that day, but over a facetime call, she couldn't manage to break a sweat.

Despite what they'd gone through recently, making their 'relationship' rather rusty, she felt at peace for once. Nothing had been heard of from the weirdo that kept bothering her, and her and Jungkook were on good terms. She was glad they had talked about Jinhwan and everything that was bothering him. It really had saddened her when she saw the way he was at MAMA, so seeing that child-like grin on his face again was a blessing to her. Although making him that way required her to remind him that a part of her still adored him more than anything, it hadn't been a lie.

She still didn't know. Soomi knew she couldn't have both boys at the same time, but it was so hard figuring out who her heart really desired.

Jungkook was engrossed in writing his song he refused to share with her again. She was desperately curious to know what it was about, but if he didn't want to show her yet, what could she really do about it?

I'll just steal it when I visit the apartment again.

She pushes the thought away, that was too rude.

Not knowing what to do because she didn't want to disturb his intense thinking session, she could only sit in silence and stare out the view through the window from her spot on the bed. Every few seconds, he would tap his pencil against the side of his hand, biting down on his bottom lip in deep concentration. It was a rather cute sight, seeing Jungkook so focused on something. What could that song be about?

Leaning her head back against the head board, she glances to the door of the hotel room. It'd been seriously long since Jinhwan went downstairs to get some coffee. It wasn't like he was making them a full course meal himself or something, so what was taking him so long? 

Exiting out of the FaceTime app momentarily, she pulls up their texts again to check how long it had been since he told her he was on the way back. Thirty minutes, she purses her lips in confusion, before letting out a dramatic breathe of air, did someone recognize him? It wasn't completely impossible. iKON was one of the biggest groups in the country, they were even well known globally. Even though Ilsan-ri was a small town, they were only an hour and a half away from Seoul. The chances of at least one person knowing either of them were still extremely high.

Soomi wondered if she should text him a few times and ask what was up. It would be the easiest thing to do, but she also thought that maybe she should go downstairs to try to find him. Not that she thought Jinhwan was doing something bad, and it wouldn't really be her business either way, but it would be easier instead of sitting and staring at her screen, waiting for a reply.

Rising from the bed she had been so peacefully perched on, she heaved out a sigh at the fact her time of relaxation was over. 

"Yah, Kookie," she calls to the boy on the call, tapping back onto the call to look at him. Holding the phone in front of her face, she walks into the bathroom to see herself clearly in the mirror, making sure her hair and clothes looked acceptable. Looking back to the screen, she saw Jungkook had looked up from his notebook, a curious look in his eyes asking what she needed. "I'll text you, alright? I'm gonna go downstairs in the hotel."

There's a dull tint that takes over his irises. It made her feel bad, but what could she really do? She needed to go find Jinhwan.

"okay, I'll see you then, Mi," he hums, waving as he reaches to grab his phone from where it had been sitting.

She waves back to him, before hitting the end call button. Jungkook's face soon disappeared, and a part of her ached as she puts her phone in rest mode. Shoving it in her pocket, she heaves out a sigh before leaving the bathroom, checking her reflection one last time.

Grabbing one of Jinhwan's jackets, she slips it over her shoulders before unlocking the hotel door. Soomi didn't even have the key since Jinhwan took the only one, but she was hoping no one around was an angry thief. Taking her purse with her just in case, she throws it over her shoulder, making her way into the hall of the hotel.

Quietly shutting the room door behind her, she lets out a deep breathe, looking either way for any sign of someone. This wasn't something she would usually do, but after everything, Soomi couldn't help but be even slightly paranoid every so often if not always.

She was just casually walking down the hall, humming to herself and trailing off into her own world, when her phone went off for the 90th time that day in her pocket. Soomi was honestly shocked at whoever it could be that time, it seemed like she had already talked to every person she knew on the face of the Earth that day. All the boys had already texted her and asked her where she was, saying they were sorry for what Junhoe did (assuming Jinhwan told them.) As for her best friend Junhoe himself, he spammed her with practically 900 messages screaming about how he was sorry and even sent her a billion selca's doing heart hands and other silly things. I'm an idiot when I'm drunk, don't be mad at me, Soo, were the majority of his texts. To her, it wasn't a big deal. She knew she used to have a problem, but she was okay now. Just because someone was offering her a drink didn't make them a bad person. Taking it lightly, she'd replied to him with aren't you always an idiot? 

Then they'd begun their joking sessions like always. She'd also texted some of the Bangtan boys that morning, as Jimin had let them know what she was up to. They wished her a fun time on her short holiday, saying she should still come see them, and if not, that they would see her on the New Years festival for sure.

I'm starting to miss my writing and producing buddy, Yoongi had texted her hours ago. It was a bewildering thing to read coming from the likes of Min Yoongi, no joke. She had to screenshot it to make sure she was seeing it correctly. Of course, though, he had to add, but also not really. Still, you should visit, pabo.

With how badly her old friends seemed to miss her, she needed to make it a priority to spend some time with them when she got back from her and Jinhwan's rendezvous.

Finally pulling her phone out of her pocket, she turns it on with curious eyes.

Unknown Number:
I love giving you surprises, Soomi. I know you'll really love this one.

She halts in her steps just reading the message. "Surprises," she repeats the word out loud to herself in a mumble, blinking in misunderstanding. Slowly, she looks up from the phone screen to peer behind her, thinking it might be something. It was silly of her to think that coward would bother showing themselves, though, as her eyes were only met with nothingness.

Jimin's words find a way back into her head. Maybe you should try replying, she pursed her lips as she stood in the middle of the hall, thinking about it for a second.

Pulling up the messages on her phone, she taps the keyboard, her thumbs hovering over the screen for a moment before she types.

Soomi:
What surprise might you be talking about?

Soomi:
Did you get me some candy?

She couldn't help but giggle at her reply. Although it was unlikely they had a sense of humor. Her green eyes stare down at the screen, thinking it wasn't going to reply. But only a second later, her phone went off again.

Unknown Number:
Ah, the funny Soomi we all know and love. It's sweet you have the audacity to make jokes when I have everything you've been trying to hide at my will.

Unknown Number:
Even then, I feel like there's something more. You really are a tunnel of secrets, Kim Soomi

Unknown Number:
Don't worry, I'm going to keep digging. I'll find out everything about you, and I'll make sure the entire world knows what piece of shit you are.

Soomi:
All you do is talk but you haven't done anything. Sneaking in my apartment and watching me sleep, stabbing a note into my door, and hurting Jungkook. Why would you do that? I thought he was the reason you hate me but yet I'm not even with him anymore.

Soomi:
And I have nothing else I'm hiding. I'm an honest person.

Unknown Number:
Oh Soomi, I don't care about Jungkook. I tried to get him away from you with those threats for his benefit, because no one deserves to be under your ridiculing spell. This is about you, and how horrible you are.

Unknown Number:
I really do wonder what else you're hiding other than the fact you're two-timing, all behind your company's back, an honest person my ass.

Unknown Number:
You might want to hurry though, your surprise is waiting, and I think they might need medical attention.

Her eyes widen as she reads the last message again and again. Medical attention, she repeats it in her head, they? 

It seemed like they did know where she was, even though they didn't say it. Her eyes widen as she processes the only one they could be talking about. She was only here with one person, after all.

Jinhwan.

"Oh my god," she gasps out, her feet a thought ahead of her as she rushes down the hall as fast as her feet could take her. She didn't care to reply to the psycho anymore, she didn't get any information she really cared for, anyway. All it did was scare her even more. Her hope for having a peaceful holiday was gone, there was something seriously wrong.

Finally reaching the stairs, she could only pray that wherever he was, Jinhwan was okay. He was actually fairly strong despite his below average height. Working out every now and then, she believed he could full fledged-ly defend himself if he needed to. So what could have happened? 

The thought was breaking her insides. Jungkook had already gotten hurt because of her, now Jinhwan? 

Just as her foot steps out, about to have gone on the first step, she feels something hit it. Pausing, her eyes gradually go to the ground, confused as to what would be in her way on the stairs.

Her heart falls out of her chest as a screech leaves her lips. A hand over her mouth, her vision nearly blacked out just at the sight of him. 

"Jinhwan-ah!" she cries out in shock, immediately falling to her knees. Her hands wander all over his body as she tries to process what to do. The anxiety was fighting it's way through her body just like it had when she found Jungkook, but this time, there was no one there to comfort her. She was all alone, far away from Seoul and any of her friends. The only one she had here was Jinhwan, and now he was laying unconscious before her. 

Using two fingers, she checks for a pulse. He was alive, but didn't look like he was waking up any time soon. 

"Fuck, fuck, fuck," she's cursing, pulling at her hair in complete distress. How had this happened? There wasn't a mark on his body, no one had touched him. It meant the only way this had happened was if it was internally.

Holding a hand to her imploding head, she turns to look around for anything. Her green eyes land on the coffee cups, and without a second thought she reaches over to the one that was drank out of. 

Raising it to her nose, she inhales for a split second. There was a rather sour scent that definitely didn't make her think of coffee. 

What the fuck? she thought in dismay. Whatever it was, didn't seem like it was meant for a human to drink.

Tears well in her eyes as she throws it aside, not caring that it splashed over the floor feet behind her. Pulling out her phone, she rushes to dial 119 with one hand while the other remains on Jinhwan's arm, holding it for dear life. She just couldn't ever have one day away.

"I-I need help, my boyfriend was drugged I think, h-he's unconscious," she couldn't stop the cries that left her lips as she whines to the people through the phone. They were telling her to calm down so they could clearly understand her, but she couldn't no matter how hard she tried. If something happened to him, if he didn't wake up, she wouldn't be able to live. "I-I'm at the Hilton in Ilsan-ri," she informed them weakly.

"We'll be at your location shortly, please sit tight."

She didn't know how she was ever going to begin to do that. Her hand goes to his hand, it was still warm like he always was, full of warmth. 

"Please be okay, Jinhwan-ah," she could only mutter with her last bit of hope as she tightens her grip on his hand.

Unknown Number:
Happy holidays, Soomi-ah.









48:00 | moral support


"Ma'am," a sympathetic voice politely calls out to her. Soomi's head jolts up to peer up at the nurse who was slowly approaching her, clearly aware of her vulnerable, lost state. The small girl didn't bother to rise to her feet as the woman stops in front of her, not wasting any time to inform, "it appears your boyfriend was drugged with propofol, but it wasn't a high enough dosage to damage his body," Soomi gets up to face the nurse by this point. There's a shocked expression on her face as she meets the lady's eyes, looking for any sign of that being good news. Just when she's about to ask what exactly propofol was, she was beat to speaking, "unfortunately, since it is a drug used to induce coma's, it appears Jinhwan won't be waking up today."

Her face fell, and you could tell the nurse felt bad for her as she reaches out to em-pathetically pat her shoulder. It was like someone was ripping her heart out of her chest, and she wanted nothing more than to find whoever this asshole was and beat the living hell out of them with her bare hands. Maybe she wasn't scary or strong, but she was definitely beyond pissed. It was one thing to go after Jungkook and knock him out for a few hours - but they dared to go after Jinhwan, her beloved angel. She got passed the first attack on one of her closest friends, but this? This was too far.

"When is he waking up?" she blurts out the question a moment later, trying to remain hopeful despite how badly she wanted to curl up on the floor and cry. When the nurse's sad smile doesn't disappear, she added with a gracious attitude, "soon, right?"

The woman diverted her eyes from Soomi's green irises. A lump forms in her throat at her actions. She wouldn't be acting this way if it was soon, would she?

"Ms. Soomi," she breathes out, putting a hand on the small of her back and guiding her to sit on the bench she had previously been sitting on. Taking a seat next to her, she lets out a dull sigh as she takes the girl's hand for moral support, meeting her eyes once again before slowly consoling, "do you have some friends you can call? Someone to contact so you aren't here alone."

It was clear she noticed the fact Soomi had all of her things with her, including Jinhwan's. When the ambulance had come, she stayed behind so she could get all of their stuff from the hotel. She'd gotten Jinhwan's keys, phone, and the hotel pass out of his pockets so she had something to drive and was able to check out of the hotel. 

Before she'd left, she tried asking for whoever was working that morning who made Jinhwan's coffee. That person had to have seen something if they weren't the culprit of doing it in the first place, after all. She needed to find whoever it was and get answers. But the people at the desk of the hotel told her whoever it was had left early that day. 

She would still be going back, if it was the last thing she had to do. Although she wasn't usually like this, someone hurting Jinhwan was no joke to her. If she had to get her lawyers involved and sue whoever it was that made that coffee, she would. With the amount of money she had, Soomi had space to do whatever she wanted, she would give no mercy.

Not until she received the answers she wanted. She'd refuse to rest. It was time for this escapade to end. She couldn't let anyone else she loved get hurt, she had to step up and do it herself once and for all. What was wrong with her to cower and let other people fight her own battle? In the end, it got her nowhere. Soomi had to solve this herself.

"Uh, yeah," she nods vigorously after realizing she had yet to reply to the poor nurse, "my friends are in Seoul, but I'll give them a call."

Sending her a close lipped smile, she rises to her feet beside her, "he'll be okay, but take care of yourself while we take care of him, okay?"

Soomi only sends the nurse a thankful grin and hesitant nod before she walks away. 

Figuring it was now or never, she hurriedly taps on her contacts on her phone. Watching as they load, she hits the first one that showed up, holding the phone against her ear. The ringing sound echoes multiple times, and she wondered what they were up to on their days off. A part of her felt bad for bothering them when they were probably busy getting over their hangover after last night. The last thing any of those boys needed was to hear bad news about their hyung, but they had to know. 

Just when she thought she was going to get ignored, the bright voice of Koo Junhoe fills her ears, "my best friend Soo, what's up?!" Hearing him in such a good mood only made her feel worse about the situation she was in. 

It makes her pause in saying anything. She ended up opening her mouth before closing it many times, pursing her lips in thought. He sounds so happy, her mind was whining to her, you're about to ruin it. 

"Soomi?" Junhoe hums into the phone a few minutes later, as she still hadn't said anything, "Are you okay, how's your holiday going?" He was already starting to sense there was a problem just by how quiet she was.

Closing her eyes as she leans back into the bench she sat in in the hospital hallway, she prepares herself mentally to share the news.

"June-ya," she exhales deeply as she says his name, trying to sound like she wasn't completely shattered inside, "something happened to J-Jinhwan," her voice cracking at the end didn't help what she just told him. 

There's a short silence as Junhoe seemed to be processing if he heard her right. "What?" he gasps on the end of the line, concern bubbling in his words, "What happened? Are you hurt?"

"No, no I'm fine," she rushes to tell him before he freaked out even more, "t-they followed us here or something, and somehow they drugged him - I don't know how it's possible, I think they did something to whoever was working here. I tried to find what worker was here this morning, but they checked out early," she started rambling everything that was in her mind, "I'm going to go back though and find them, they have to pay for giving into it and hurting Jinhwan - and they also had to of seen their face, right? I won't let anyone get away with this, I'm gonna find whoever this sick piece of fucking shi-," Junhoe had coughed abruptly to cut her off.

"Soomi, Soomi," he blurts her name repeatedly so she would stop, "tell me what hospital you're at, I'll get the others and we'll be there as soon as possible."

She'd had no idea she was crying again until she feels something wet drip onto her lap. Out of frustration, she hurriedly wipes away any tears that had the audacity to stream down her cheeks. Heaving out another deep breathe, she only mutters, "yeah, I'll text it to you."

Ending the call without even saying bye, she hurriedly texted it to Junhoe. He replied with in a second saying they were on their way.

Pursing her lips, she exited off their conversation and tapped on the Unknown Number again. Her eyes narrow with anger as she read the last text they sent over and over again. It disgusted her so much, it was the first time she felt such a incredible urge to puke just looking at something. 

Scoffing, she raises her thumbs to angrily type into her phone.

Soomi:
I'm going to fucking find you

Soomi:
And you're gonna meet real hell

Soomi:
You should be the one who's fucking scared

Telling them that made her feel better, even if it sort of was an empty threat. That was, until her phone vibrates in her hand.

Unknown Number:
You're funny. Look at you all fired up.

Unknown Number:
If anything you should be thanking me that I didn't kill him. He'll be up in a couple of days.

Unknown Number:
That distraction gives me the perfect amount of time to find out everything about you, Soomi

Unknown Number:
Remember, I always know where you are, you can never hide from me:)

Unknown Number:
But poor Soomi, you'll never find me

Her hands were trembling from how upset she was. Soomi probably looked like she was crazy, with how flaming her eyes were as they glare down at her screen, gripping the phone so tightly it was bound to break if she was actually slightly strong.

Not knowing what else to do, she didn't want to reply to them anymore. Her mind reminded her that she told Jimin she would let him know if she ended up ever texting them, and she had yet to tell him. She also needed to update him on what was going on.

Running a hand through her hair with one hand, she dials his number with the other. Holding the phone to her ear once again, it didn't have to ring more than once when it came to her best friend.

"Mi, hey," he hums, and was about to say something more but she cut right to the chase of what was going on.

"Chim," she sighs out, "I have really bad news."




///






A yawn leaves her lips as she exhales deeply, holding her hand over her mouth to silence it. Pulling her knees to her chest, she shifts her seating position in the cushioned chair she was sitting in, leaning her head to the side to rest atop the back of it. That same empty look is in her green irises as her eyes wander to the unconscious boy on the hospital bed. Every time she looked at him, tears would well in her eyes and she felt her chest get heavy. Jinhwan didn't deserve this, and it ruined her knowing it was all her fault.

They would of never gone anywhere in the first place if she hadn't had a drinking problem in the past that worried him. Jinhwan wouldn't be threatened at all if she hadn't ran into him in the hall that day. He wouldn't of gotten hurt or mad if she just didn't come in his life at all. All Soomi had provided him with was problems. This person deserved to only torment her for whatever they wanted from her, he didn't need to get hurt because of someone's hatred towards her.

"I'm sorry, Jinani," her voice is a mere whisper as she breaks her eyes from staring at him. 

There was no telling what time it was by that point. It was probably around 3 or 4 PM in the afternoon, and the hospital workers had decided to let Soomi stay if she wanted to even after visiting times. They felt bad that she was there all alone, obviously freaking out about her beloved boyfriend. The fact she called Jinhwan her boyfriend was the least of her worries, so much she hadn't noticed that she said it.

Not knowing what else to do with herself, she scrolls through her pictures on her phone. There were countless photos of her and Jinhwan, and even some of him just taking silly pictures on her phone. Looking through all of them put a small smile to her face, even if it was of pain, at least she was finding a way to smile.

He'll be okay, she has to remind herself as she glances back up to him temporarily.

"Soomi, Soomi!"

The hospital room door was suddenly burst open so loudly the back of the door clashed against the wall. Her eyes widen from the loud yells and noise entering the once peacefully quiet room, and six boys hurriedly pour into the room, looking like worried parents as they kept screaming out her name.

As soon as their eyes all fall on her, she didn't even get to stand up for more than a second before she was nearly tackled.

"Soomi, oh my god!"

"Are you okay? What happened?!"

"I'm going to kill whoever did this!"

They were all crying out their own thoughts as she was pulled into a choking group hug. Soomi found herself grinning like an idiot, not even caring that they were nearly killing her from how much bigger they all were than her. Her weight was nothing compared to even one of them, nevertheless six. 

Despite them squeezing the life out of her to the point where she could barely breathe as they all pull away, she felt safe for the first time that horrid day.

"You guys are here," she had to say as she peers at them all, "thank God, I was so scared."

Seeing that their hug was a lot for her, they all gave her some space. Well, except for Junhoe, he had been worried sick the entire way there about his closest friend. Never had any of the other five seen him in such a concerned state for anyone other than himself. They were reminded how much Soomi had changed their friend, and Jinhwan, and them as well, with the way he hadn't shut up about her the entire drive there.

Junhoe was standing directly beside her. Their feet weren't even an inch apart. Soomi would've joked about it any other day, but she didn't mind the close contact from someone she trusted. If anything, it was what she needed.

A few moments of silence pass as they allow her to have a moment to think.

"When you're ready to tell us what happened, go ahead," Hanbin assures her with a comforting smile, "take your time though, alright?"

She returns his grin, nodding in understanding as she sits back down. The others all take a seat on the cold, tiled floor around her. Some of them look to her with kind expressions, and others distracted themselves by doing something else. Either way, they all allowed her to take in what had really happened, and not show if they were freaked out over the fact their dearest hyung was unconscious right behind them. 

The last thing anyone wanted to do was upset or scare Soomi even more. They didn't know the full story yet, but whatever happened seemed to be pretty serious.

All the six wanted to do in that moment was let her know they were there for her. After all, what is family for?






49:00 | compassionate friendships you only see in the movies


Jungkook's curiosity always got the best of him. It was why when he heard a bunch of rustling and movement outside his bedroom, he couldn't help but get up to check it out. Whoever it was, it appeared that they were in quite the rush to get somewhere.

He had been stuck in his own thoughts about Soomi, but that was the norm with him anyway. Setting his pencil atop his notebook where he'd been working on that song, he pushes his chair back and rises to hit feet. His shoes click against the wood floor of his room as he'd slid his door open to peek his head out, looking each way for a sign of who seemed to have somewhere to be.

Park Jimin threw his bag over his shoulder, his keys jingling in his hand as he shoves his phone in his pocket with the other. Jungkook rose a brow at the sight, his nosiness dripping off of him as he tried to think of what he could be doing. The thing was, Jimin was carrying a bag that he would only use when he traveled or stayed anywhere that wasn't in their home.

Just with how he seemed to determined to get out the door as he made his way down the hall and into the living room, he had a good feeling of who it had to do with.

"Yah!" he calls to his hyung just as he was about to open the door. Jimin should've expected something like this to happen. He could never seem to leave without Jungkook pestering him about it. This boy really never gave it a break.

Spinning on his heel since he was facing the door, he deadpans in a blank tone, "yes, Jungkookie?"

The tables had turned, as Jungkook could tell something was wrong from the look on his face. Unimpressed with the fact he was stopping him from getting out the door was clearly shown with the dull facial expression, and it had him immediately questioning, "is Soomi okay?" 

Thinking back, Jungkook had sat on the call with her earlier for well over an hour. What could have happened to her since then? She seemed so happy when they were talking. He liked to think it was because she was talking to him and not because of that ridiculous Jinhwan guy. Jungkook really didn't like him. Even though they talked it out and he said everything was fine, he was still jealous, how could he not be? Jungkook wished he could make a song with her, see her at his company everyday, and go on a holiday with her when he pleased.

To think once, he almost got to have that leisure. If only Soomi wouldn't have left their company. He guessed he couldn't really blame her, she was just chasing her dreams. An offer from such a giant company like YG back then was a big deal, it wasn't her fault that she took it, she would've been dumb not to.

Still, he wondered how it could be different if she would've stayed under Big Hit instead.

Jimin is hesitant to reply. He wasn't sure if he should lie or be honest with Jungkook. There was a lot the boy was in the dark on, he guessed there always had been. He didn't really know much of the details of what was going on right now. 

Although, he assumed Soomi probably told him a lot of it since they spoke earlier. But even then, that was before this entire fiasco went down.

Stressed with the current situation, he runs a hand through his hair to calm himself, finally heaving out, "no, she's really not." What was the harm in telling him? Jungkook cared about her, too. He deserved to know what happened even if a part of Jimin really didn't want to tell him. It wasn't like this required him to inform him about the picture and the fact the psycho knew about that night on the rooftop.

God knew he'd never tell him that.

Snapping out of his thoughts, the short boy looks up to tell Jungkook a bit more of the story since he was probably already freaking out about what could of happened, but when he does - he wasn't there.

Blinking in confusion, the maknae was suddenly seen running out of his room like a track runner. He had a jacket in hand, and a large backpack slung over his shoulder. If he managed to pack anything in the short minute that Jimin had stopped paying attention, he had to be insane. He must've gotten the memo that Jimin was going somewhere rather far away from the way he stopped in front of him, pursing his lips in contemplation with all of his things.

Pushing past the bewildered boy, Jungkook was already out the door as he hums back to him, "let's talk in the car."




///




"Soomi, come on, the others reserved us a huge suite at the five star hotel downtown," Junhoe pressed for the 90th time, grabbing onto her arm and pulling her up from the chair she had been glued to for so long. He was the only one still up in the room with her, as the others had left to go get a hotel for them to all stay in while they waited for Jinhwan to wake up. 

A cry of disobedience leaves her lips as he dragged her towards the exit. Soomi had said she wasn't going to leave Jinhwan, and she didn't plan to. It didn't matter how hungry or tired she got, she swore she would stay by his side. 

Junhoe pouts at the sight of her still fighting him. "Soo, it'll be alright, there's good security here and no, it's not the same hotel you guys stayed in," he insisted, hoping it would convince her, "Jinhwan wouldn't want you to starve yourself worrying about him, you know, we can go get orange chicken."

Her stomach grumbled just at the mention of her favorite food. Looking at Jinhwan one last time and his peacefully sleeping body, she heaved out a deep sigh. She guessed it really would be no use to stay there, even if she waned to. The thing was, she just hated the thought of not being around him. Perhaps she always had a tendency to rely on others too much, and it was showing then as she frowned towards where he was on the hospital bed.

She still felt bad, she always would.

But she didn't want to worry the others anymore. "Okay," she reluctantly agrees, and Junhoe let out a breathe of relief. Letting her go since she finally was coming at her own will, the two walk together out of Jinhwan's hospital room, when she adds, "but we better go to that hotel tomorrow morning, I've gotta see if that worker is there."

That was no problem for him, "of course."

Silence falls over them as they walk towards the exit of the hospital. Although she wasn't feeling great, a smile finds it's way to her face as she looked up to her tall best friend that walked beside her. What would she do without him? It was honestly a scary question to ask at that point. Even though she had Jimin, their friendship still wasn't quite the same as her and Junhoe. It was similar for sure, as in they were extremely close (even closer) but it still wasn't the same feel. 

There was something about her and Junhoe's friendship, something that was special. 

In the beginning, she really couldn't stand him when she truly met him. Soomi knew of iKON ever since she was a trainee at YG, and she always thought that Junhoe was crazy handsome. Never did she think she would ever get to meet the boys of the idol group, nonetheless become such good friends with all of them. That night, when she was a drunken mess, she could still vividly remember meeting Junhoe when Jinhwan had dragged her into their dorm. 

"Oh my god!" Soomi gasps as she gets out of Jinhwan's grip, walking towards the giant boy, "you're the super handsome one!" she points a finger at him knowingly, a satisfied smirk on her face.

Junhoe grins at this, not even noticing how drunk she was due to the compliment. He didn't think someone like her would ever be standing before her. 

A chuckle leaves her lips as she recalls it, even if it was in pieces. She could remember because it was the first time she ever met someone who would come to be one of the most amazing friend's she had ever had.

Although their first encounter went that way, when she and Jinhwan's fling began, and she actually went around Junhoe when she wasn't drunkenly trying to flirt with him, he annoyed her to her core. He was always making witty jokes and saying something that made her want to hit him. The rest of the boys were pretty much the same, except for Hyuk and Yun, who always felt bad from all the teasing she received. 

Eventually, Soomi came to realize it was all fun in games with the boys. They didn't mean any harm by their words, and they came to just end up all messing with each other. She found a home in the way the boys helped her, comforted her when she needed it, and reminded her that she was never really alone because she had them. 

Junhoe always was there when she needed an extra shoulder to cry on. He was her moral support when she couldn't face the real world, he provided her an escape when Jinhwan was mad at her, assuring her every second that it would be okay and he would get over it. Through the Jungkook ordeal, he gave her advice and listened to her whines. He was always there when she didn't think she needed someone but really did, whether it was in the dance room or the recording studio, to do some stupid impression or crack a joke to make her smile. Although he couldn't see through her mask like Jimin because he hadn't known her anywhere near as long, he was sure getting close to being able to. 

Since she couldn't be around Jimin much anymore, Junhoe was filling that hole that was there when she lost her other best friend. Now she had two perfect best friends that she wouldn't trade for the world.

Gently elbowing him to get his attention, the giant looks down to her with a wondrous look. Not wasting a second, she wholeheartedly expresses what was on her mind in that moment, "thank you, June-ya."

He didn't seem to get what she was getting at, or maybe he just wanted more of an explanation. "For what?" 

Chuckling, she didn't have a problem telling him what was really in her thoughts as she explains further, "for being such an amazing friend to me, even when I didn't do the same."

A grin spreads over his face from her heartfelt words. It had been a long time since he'd heard her say something like that. But everyone knew Junhoe couldn't help but joke even in special moments.

"Friend, huh? I thought we were on best friend terms," he stuck out his bottom lip dramatically as he says such, even though he knew that wasn't what she meant at all.

Just when she opens her mouth, about to tell him something he was already aware of. Soomi knew he was joking, but she still wasn't done fully sharing everything she felt in her heart. Even though this was a nightmare that she was going through, she was genuinely so thankful that it wasn't alone. Although it made her feel bad to put other people through stress and pain because of her, they stood by her side either way, they made her feel whole. 

Even in hard times, they gave her the support she never got growing up. Somehow, it made what was going on a happy time even if it was only for a split second, because it brought the friends closer.

"We are, I just-," she starts to explain, when a yell coming from the doors they were just about to walk out of breaks through her statement.

"Soomi!"

Both of their heads jolt from having been looking at each other, and the sight of the two boys running at them caused them to both halt in their tracks. Her head felt light when Jimin stopped before her, seeing him right behind him.

"Chim?" she blinks, trying to figure out if she was dreaming. He hadn't told her he was coming, and especially that he was coming as well, "Jungkook?!"



50:00 | just good



"Y-You didn't tell me you were coming," her eyes are on Jimin even though Jungkook was closer to her, standing just a foot to the side so she could still peer at her best friend. Glancing from Jungkook, back to the silver haired boy, she adds in a quieter tone, "with Jungkook."

Having had given a casual greeting to Junhoe, who was beyond lost at what they were doing here much like Soomi. He didn't think she would have called them in the first place, but he guessed it shouldn't be that shocking since Jimin was a good friend of hers after all.

"He invited himself," Jimin explains blatantly, motioning to the maknae who sent him a glare. Ignoring the look from Jungkook, he sighs as he admits, "I just wanted to make sure you were okay."

Soomi is about to speak up and assure them that she was alright thanks to her lovely friends, when all of the words she had in her head seemed to fly out her ears, somewhere far away from her brain. Jungkook had stepped forward to enclose the space between them. The mere foot was nonexistent as he wrapped his arms around her, not pulling her into him but just hugging her. It wasn't a tight hug like he usually did, squeezing the life out of her, but was just a comforting embrace.

"Jimin told me, I'm sorry, Mi," his heartfelt words shocked Jimin as much as they did her. She could only gawk at him like he was some sort of alien as he pulled away from the hug.

It was seriously out of place to see someone like Jungkook say something so selfless. They knew he was clingy when it came to Soomi, meaning the last thing they thought he would tell her was something like that. He expressed his clear disliking towards Jinhwan on the way to Ilsan-ri, and Jimin rolls her eyes from the fact he was probably just doing it to suck up to her.

Really, there was no way he could feel the slightest bit of bad about something happening to Jinhwan. If anything, he felt bad about the fact it made Soomi so sad. Nobody liked seeing her in such a state, and they all wanted to rid her off the stress this person was causing her.

"It's okay," she mutters into the awkward air, her voice being that of a mouse as she diverts her green eyes to the tiled floor.

Jimin steps forward to join the circle that the three were standing in. "We can help you catch this person, I just want to get this over with."

It was surprising to see the two show up here, all the way in Ilsan-ri. But she was grateful for their kind attempt. They just wanted to help and make her feel better, she couldn't get mad at that.

"Thanks, you two," she offers them a sweet smile, "I'm glad you guys are here, you can come eat with me and the other boys of iKON, you haven't got to officially meet them yet!" She just seemed to realize that they hadn't indeed met the other members. Other than Jinhwan eating with them that time, and the fact Jungkook woke up in their dorm, they never officially were introduced, not to every member of iKON.

Jungkook seemed to be thinking about something rather intently, so Jimin cheerily hums for the both of them, "that sounds good, Mi!"

The bright expression on her face was genuine as she jumped around a bit from the excitement she was feeling. She just knew Jimin would love the others, and so would Jungkook if he was in a good mood. He obviously hadn't been when he woke up in pain at their dorm, but it would be different this time.

She always loved the idea of bringing her two close friend groups together. Now she was finally getting the chance, even though there were only two out of seven in one of them. This could be the opening for something great, either way, allowing for her to introduce the other five later on.

So, even though her poor Jinhwan was still up in that hospital room, she found a way to find joy in that horrible day. Even though the psycho was still on the loose, even though she had so many worries, even though her career could be in danger soon — she felt content with her closest friends at her side.

With that in mind, she links an arm in Junhoe's and the other in Jimin's, "let's go!"

And that they did, with Jungkook trailing behind them.



///



"Seriously, our twitter mentions are usually about Soomi these days," Jin had been saying as he shoves a piece of bulgogi in his mouth, chewing and swallowing before he finished what he was saying, "ever since what Jungkook pulled at Melon, remember? They're still convinced we're gonna come out with music or something."

Every was humming to show they were listening, busy eating their own food as they sat on the floor of the enormous suite in a large circle. Everyone's bags they'd bought were scattered in a giant pile in the corner of the room. Even though they had bought the largest suite the hotel had to offer, you could only get such a big room in such a small town. It was why the nine adults were rather crowded in the space that the living room was, but they were happy as they ate their take out either way.

Swallowing a piece of his own meat, Yoongi clears his throat from where he sat, "they aren't expecting music, they're expecting Jungkook and Soomi to say they're together again," he corrects with the blind-fully obvious. Everyone knew it was true, but nobody had the guts to say it except for Yoongi or maybe Junhoe if he was listening.

"So, do you like creep on your fans or something?" Chanwoo speaks up in curiosity, looking to the eldest of BTS.

Meanwhile, Soomi was already pretty much done with her own dinner. What could she say? She'd been extremely hungry since her and Jinhwan hadn't really ate since they got to Ilsan-ri. Her stomach had been crying for a meal like she just had, and the fact it was her favorite food only made it all that much better.

The thing was, though, whenever she ate as much as she just did, her stomach felt like it was going to implode from all the food it was storing. She was full, but could probably still eat more if she wanted to, and her stomach was making sure she knew that. Feeling exhausted from the lovely meal, she sighs deeply as she lays her head on Jungkook's shoulder since he was the closest to her.

Maybe it looked as such, but she didn't mean it as any sort of romantic gesture. The others still took note of it as they saw the two. 

"I mean, sure, sometimes," Jin shrugs, "they're pretty funny, you know, I like to see what's going on with them."

Some of the other BTS members nod in agreement with him, saying that they did the same on occasions. The iKON members, on the other hand, were rather surprised to hear this. Never had they really thought of doing such a thing. They read the comments on their Instagram posts sometimes, but never went and stalked things like their Twitters.

"I might have to give that a try," Hanbin was thinking out loud.

"Actually, a lot of our mentions are about Soomi, too," Junhoe blurts out, bringing all the attention to him.

Soomi internally groaned from her being brought up again. Having had kept her mouth shut, same with Jungkook, the both of them still heard what Yoongi said loud and clear. It put her in an awkward position with how she was laying on his shoulder. There was already enough tension between them, she didn't need anyone talking about the fact they used to be together, nonetheless that the fans still thought they were.

But her friends were just having a conversation. They didn't mean any harm. She was glad they were getting along, so she found a way to deal with it.

The other members of iKON look to Junhoe in pure confusion. "So you creep on our fans, huh?" Yunhyeong raises a brow at him, and he only shrugs as if it was no big thing.

"Yeah, I like seeing what they say about me," he explains.

"What do they say about me?" Yun had piped up as he quizzically peers at Junhoe from across the circle. 

Poor Junhoe's face was rather blank, he opens his mouth to tell him the hard truth when the maknae beat him to it. 

"Nobody says anything about you, ahjussi."

Many of them start cracking up at the bewildered and annoyed expression that takes over Yunhyeong's face. The bickering of the group never ended, and Soomi wanted to slam her head into the floor from the fact it had begun. 

"Yah, I'm literally the second oldest, why does nobody call Jinhwan ahjussi?!"

"Because he doesn't act like a fucking grandpa," Chan retorts in that sassy tone of his, "he's also not with us right now, making you the eldest, ahjussi."

His taunting, playful words made Yunhyeong frown. 

"I'm not old!" he kept crying in defense, not wanting to be called something as awful as ahjussi ever again.

Meanwhile, the Bangtan boys had only listened to what was going on, having a few small conversations here and there. Soomi was still leaning on Jungkook, who was eating his chicken while humming a song she didn't recognize. A part of her wanted to ask him about the song he refused to show her earlier, as it would be something that would get her mind off everything happening other than having to listen to her friends bickering. Those boys never had a filter even when they were around new people, she could only mentally sigh.

"Are they always like this?" Jimin had leaned over to whisper into her ear, being the one on the other side of her.

She shrugs, not caring much to talk about it since the answer was probably already obvious, "pretty much."

They were still going at it as Chanwoo groans from the fact Yun wasn't giving up on defending himself, "whatever you say, elder."

The dark haired prince's chin nearly falls on the floor from how he gawks, "what?! Now I'm an elder?!"

At this point, the boys of iKON were in extreme fits of giggles. Hanbin had stopped eating to bury his face in his hands, wanting to disappear because of how embarrassing these two idiots were. Junhoe was just sitting there as it all went down, probably looking for a place to cut in with his own jokes like he always did. Bobby was laughing so hard that he kept clapping out of disbelief, even laying back on the ground as the hysterics leave his lips, with Donghyuk being a giggly mess right beside him.

"You literally called yourself that before!" Chan's claim wasn't wrong, but it didn't make Yunhyeong look any less offended.

"They're worse than us," Yoongi was muttering from beside Jimin, just loud enough so they could hear.

Jimin chuckles as he continues to watch the exchange before him, "but they're funny."

"I can feel the strength of the friendship," Jungkook finally spoke after what seemed like forever.

Soomi hadn't realized she missed his voice until she felt a grin spread over her face from hearing it. Sitting up straight, she elbows him as she resounds, "ah, so he speaks!"




Jungkook flashes that tiny nervous grin her way, "of course I do."

Her cheeks are flushing, she could feel it, from the way he'd peer down at her from his taller stance even when sitting down. She couldn't help but look to his locks of brunette hair that fell in his face, covering his eyes from how long it was getting. Even though Jimin and Yoongi - and probably most of the others - were watching them, she reaches a hand up to push it out of his eyes and to the side.

The boy wrinkles his nose from the sudden contact, but really, he didn't mind it. "Your hair is seriously getting long," Soomi is muttering is disbelief as she messed with his hair, going off into her own world from how soft it was, "I swear it was so much shorter earlier."

"I thought it'd be fun to have long hair for once," he hummed nonchalantly, watching her hand cover his vision every once in a while as she played around with it.

Pursing her lips in contemplation, her hand finally falls back into her lap once she was satisfied. "It looks good," she compliments casually. There was a lot more she actually had in mind about Jungkook's long hair, but with the others around, she couldn't exactly share it.

He seemed to know it, as he rose a brow inquisitively, "just good, huh?"

She almost physically winced from that sudden deep tone he was using just to bother her. It was clear this was his intention, when he ran his tongue against his bottom lip, and she had to look away so it couldn't affect her anymore.

He really couldn't stop with the games for once second, could he?

"Yup," she confirms in the most confident tone she could muster, knowing well everyone was watching them now as Chan and Yun had finally stopped their bickering, "good."

He knew what she really wanted to say, though.





51:00 | first meetings

2013 | the trainee days


Beads of sweat form all over her body, her muscles ache, her breathing heavy. With a groan of pure exhaustion, the young girl's back hits the wall that was lined in mirrors with a clunk, sliding against it until her bottom hit the floor. Unable to stop her abnormal breathing pattern, she uses the back of her hand to wipe sweat that was dripping in streams down her face. Holding her hand in front of her face, she cringes at the sight of her makeup now on her hand instead of her face from the action. 

Oh well, it wasn't like it mattered anyway. As far as she knew, she was the only one still at the company so late. She'd been staying every night, so much her boss - or should she call him CEO-nim? - was starting to worry about her. "I admire your hard work to debut, but let yourself rest," he'd tell her, but she would only shake it off with a reassuring smile.

"Thanks for worrying about me, but I'll be okay, I promise," she would tell him with confidence. He would only chuckle at her hard-working attitude, before patting her on the back. 

They had the same conversation every night for a while, but eventually her boss came to realize she wasn't going to stop staying at the company so late. He told her it was okay and stopped bothering, just that she needed to make sure she turned all the lights off and locked the doors when she left after making sure nobody else was in the building.

So far, she had never seen another soul in the company as late as her. There were a few other trainees here, but they didn't work anywhere near as hard as her. Maybe it was just because she was the newest addition to the company's line of trainees, and the others had been here for so much longer, that she just had a newcomer's spirit to work hard and it would ware off. But she had no intention of ever stopping her late night working, not until she debuted and made herself known.

Even then, sometimes your body gave up. It was exactly what her's did as her muscles were throbbing in every part, and she whines out in pain, leaning her head back against the mirror and still struggling to catch her breathe.

It'll get better in time, she reminds herself. This was only the beginning of her training stages after all, of course she didn't have good endurance and stamina yet. Over time, however, it would build up, so much that one day this would be a piece of cake to her.

Assuring herself of this is what got her to push through the pain she was in. Shifting in her seating position against the wall, she pulls out her phone to check the time. A text from her aunt shows up on her screen, and she purses her lips as she reads over it.

When are you coming home? You've been working so late every night, are they working you that hard?

She sighs as she reads it. It really wasn't her want to lie to anyone, but she didn't have a choice. Until she had a place of her own, she had to tell her aunt she had an actual paying job. If she were to tell the truth of her being a trainee at some small company, she'd get kicked to the curb immediately, or her aunt might do worse - tell her family. The last thing she wanted was go back to New York.

With this all in mind, she types a reply before hitting send.

I'll be home in a few hours maybe, I am just working overtime so I can get out of your hair faster, auntie:)

Turning off her phone, she sets it beside her nearly empty water bottle. Using all of the strength she had regained during her short break, she'd rose to her feet again. 

"Alright," she mutters to herself, peering at her own slim figure in the mirror, "I should do it a few more times just to make sure I have it right."

A breathe of courage leaves her mouth as she spins on one foot, heading over to the stereo. She'd been working on the dance project for her next assessment with the CEO, and she wanted to make sure it was perfect. If you showed continuous progress and impressed him, after all, you'd get that much closer to debuting. Such a thing was exactly her goal, it meant she had to strive to reach it with every waking second she had.

Pressing the play button, the EDM track begins to play again. Looking back to herself in the large mirrors that surrounded her in the tiny dance room, she made sure her facial expression was right before beginning. 

Really, she was never a dancer. Something like dancing always seemed fun to her, but she didn't think she was good at it. 

That didn't mean she couldn't learn, and the only way to do such a thing was practice. Even if it didn't make perfect, it just needed to make her good enough.

Meanwhile, a certain young trainee himself was making his way through the halls of Big Hit Entertainment. He was tired from a long day of practice, having had stayed over night to make sure he really had the group dance down. Even though he was a solid dancer, he still didn't feel like he was up to par with everyone else. Although in reality, he was the top dancer of his group, he just had a gut feeling that he always needed to practice more and more.

Pushing his dark fringe out of his face, he makes his way through the hall, trying to decide which practice room he was going to use. Assuming they would all be empty since most trainees never practiced this late, he was confused at the sight of a light on in one of the rooms.

Being the curious boy he always was since birth, he walks up to the door. It wasn't see through, so he puts his ear to the thin door, a wondrous look on his face. He was well acquainted with most of the trainees, even in the few months he'd been here that wasn't actually very long at all. Most people knew him since it was such a small company, and he was already one of everyone's favorites. Whoever it was in there probably knew of him or they were already friends. After all, he'd been scouted by SM, JYP, and plenty of other companies. It was astonishing to other people when he told them this, and they'd always ask why would you choose such a small company then?

But something about the small company that scouted him as well had been special to his heart. It seemed like a place where you could find a family, a place that he always wanted to be a part of. The big companies were never like that.

Figuring he had to know whoever it was and wouldn't mind some company during his late night practice, he shrugs casually before grabbing the door handle and twisting it. Pushing the door open with his body, the booming EDM music at max volume blasted through his ears as he enters the room.

Whoever it was didn't notice him since the entrance was on the opposite side, away from all the mirrors. Opening his mouth, he's about to yell out when he recognized who it was, but he didn't. 

The thing was, he didn't recognize them.

One thing he had noticed upon coming to Big Hit, was there were no female trainees. He had maybe only seen a few since he had been here, but not recently. Unsure of the reason, he figured the CEO must not be interested in making a girl group, so he just didn't scout females.

This is why he was so surprised to see just that - a girl. Her dark hair went straight to the middle of her back, and she was wearing some of the most casual, baggy clothes he had ever seen on a girl, complete with her white sneakers.



In literally all his years of life, never had he been so abruptly caught off guard by the sight of a girl. Most of them always tried so hard, even when they were doing simple things like going to the store or well, practicing, and he was bewildered to see one look so casual. Like he'd stated previously, though, he seemed to know every trainee - but he'd never seen her in his life until now.

Something inside of him is urging his mouth to move, for him to speak up and say something, but he felt like he was frozen. Time seemed to stop, and he just stood there, watching her dance to the upbeat EDM song that blasted at full volume from the stereo. She had to be new, so why was she so good? Even he didn't improve that fast, after all, he still felt like he wasn't good enough to even think about debuting yet. So how was this girl already looking like that of an idol?

Complete, utter bewilderment still wouldn't be descriptive enough to describe his current emotion. The look on his face was that as if he'd just seen a groundbreaking discovery right before him, she was just too good. He knew why CEO-nim had chosen this girl even if he didn't want to debut a girl group.

The girl finishes the dance, dramatically posing at the end. If someone showed him a video of her and told him she was an upcoming soloist, he would believe them. It was exactly what she looked like, so much that he was left gawking at her like a weirdo as the song ends, and she walked over to turn off the stereo.

Silence falls over the practice room, and the girl is out of breathe as she falls to sit on the floor again. Before she noticed him, he tries to act like he had just walked in the room, his sneakers clicking against the floor as he walks towards her.

The sudden noise in the peaceful air caused her head to jolt up, eyes full of wonder as they land on the boy approaching her. He thought she would say something, maybe like asking him who he was or why he was there, but she didn't even open her mouth. Hair stuck to her face from how much she was sweating, and he could see the messed up makeup smearing on her skin, but yet it didn't make him think any different of her appearance. There was something about this unknown new trainee.

She'd sat there, lost in her own world, staring at him. It was like he'd looked at her when she was dancing.

Stopping a few feet away from her sitting body, he clears his throat, bowing after a moment and introducing himself, "hi, sorry for walking in on you, I just saw someone else was here late and wanted to ask if you wanted some company."

Even after he says this and moments pass, she says nothing. The brunette was just sitting there in her spot on the floor, holding her water bottle that was now empty, and gawking at him intently. Her eyes were narrowed in concentration, and he began to feel self conscious for the first time in his life from how she seemed to be inspecting all of his features.

Not knowing what to do because he was feeling rather awkward, he's about to ask if she was okay because well, she was quiet even though he spoke to her, when the girl hums, "have I seen you somewhere?"

So she does know me.

"Maybe, the CEO likes to brag about me a lot."

A wave of realization washes over her face, "oh," she gasps in remembrance, "yeah, you got scouted by a bunch of big companies, right?"

He only nods, and she makes a sound of disbelief. "That's pretty cool, this one was the only one I could get into."

Thinking it was okay, he gradually falls against the wall to sit beside her, still keeping his space though. "Which one's did you audition for?"

She didn't have to think to reply, "JYP and SM."

"Oh, they tried to scout me, but I declined for a reason," he tries to make her feel better even though her face didn't actually show any sign of distress, "the big companies aren't as - homey - I guess."

The girl doesn't say anything for a while, she was in deep thought about something he didn't know.

After a few minutes, she speaks again, "you didn't have to bow to me, you know."

"Huh?" he blinks at her in confusion, she seems a bit odd. But it wasn't a bad thing to him, this new trainee was sort of cool.

A chuckle leaves her lips as she explains what she meant further, "you bowed, but I'm probably younger than you, so you don't have to - right?" She had started speaking so confidently, but with each word her voice got higher pitched, and she got to the point where she was clearly questioning herself.

Something about her demeanor had been different since she first talked, and he seemed to finally put his finger on it. "You're not from here, are you?"

Finally, she made a different facial expression from having been blank for the last five minutes. "Aw, is it that obvious?" she pouts a bit, looking like a lost puppy, and he had to look away to hide the smile that spread over his face.

"Maybe."

"New York," she says hesitantly after a few seconds, "I'm from New York, I just got here when I got the email that I was accepted here."

He nods in understanding, this girl was obviously Korean but she grew up in America. It explained a lot to him, actually. "I figured you were new, how long have you been here?"

Not looking at him, she fiddles with her fingers that lay in her lap as she mutters, "only a few weeks, sorry if you saw my dancing, I know it's not that great yet," he opens his mouth to begin to assure her it was really great, but she added before he could do so, "what about you, mister sm and jyp?"

Giggling from the name, he shakes his head as he responds, "uh, I think maybe a few months."

She makes an exaggerative sound with her lips, sounding like a pop as she sighs, "wow, I would've thought longer."

Raising a brow, he didn't wait to question, "why's that?"

For the first time since he walked in there, she looked to him again. Meeting his eyes, he noticed that she didn't have the typical brown Korean eyes, her irises were bright green. It was much similar to the color of freshly blooming grass in the spring, a vibrant color he figured had to be contacts.

You can imagine how shocked he was when he found out they weren't.

"I don't know," she's mumbling, studying him again, "something about you gives off a professional vibe."
