He wanted to thank her, because he guessed that was a compliment, but he reminded himself of the conversation they had been previously having. "So how old are you, since you said you're probably younger than me?" it might seem sort of weird to ask from her perspective, but he was just asking out of curiosity - nothing more.

The girl didn't seem bothered by it, though. "Eighteen."

She was young.

"Only by a year," he tells her, the news causing her to look him up and down again.

"Weird," she mutters, "you look pretty old."

Should he be offended? "Wah, is that a compliment or not?"

A small giggle leaves her lips as she shrugs, "figure it out."

Shaking his head, he subconsciously seemed to have scooted closer to her, because when he moves to face her so he could ask something important, his hand grazes hers, that had been resting on the side of her body. She seemed to jump a bit at the sudden contact, awkwardly bringing her hand into her lap as she looked away to the opposite side of where he was.

Cursing at himself mentally, he tried to act like it didn't happen as he clears his throat, "so, what's your name, new trainee?"

"Creepy that you want to know, mister sm and jyp."

"Yah, chill with the name, it makes me sound like an ignorant brat," he whines, and she laughs at how he sounded like that of a child. Really, they were children.

Silence for another few seconds, when she informs, "Soomi, and you - or should I just stick with mister sm and jyp?"

Groaning at the name again, he shakes his head as he meets her bright eyes, they really were unique.

He couldn't help it that he was smiling at her like a love struck idiot.

"Jungkook."







52:00 | confidential visit



There seriously was nowhere near enough room for 14 people to stay, even in the biggest suite the hotel had to offer. Thankfully, the guys realized this as they were preparing to head to sleep, so Namjoon had gone down to the lobby and bought the other hotel suite located across the hall. They had no idea what they would've done if it wasn't available, but much to her luck, they scored.

It was logical that the boys of BTS would be staying in the other one while the iKON boys stayed in the current one. The only thing was, Soomi didn't know where she was gonna stay. All of her stuff was still in the iKON's suite since she hadn't moved it since she got here. That being said, it would be easier for her to stay there.

But remembering how badly her old friends so called missed her, and how they still hadn't hung out much even though they spent the entire last few hours with her and iKON, she still felt like it wasn't enough. She saw the boys daily now because she lived with them, but her poor old friends and her only got together on rare occasions.

Although it was risky for her to go stay with them because she had a bad feeling of what would happen, she couldn't resist the urge. Soomi missed her old friends, too.

So after informing her newest friend group of her whereabouts, she made her way across the hallway that night. She was beyond tired, and the boys probably weren't even awake at that point since it was getting so late and everyone had plans tomorrow. They'd taken off from any schedule they had for the next couple of days to come stay with her, so she and everyone were going to go out tomorrow in hopes to get her mind off Jinhwan. Meanwhile, the boys of iKON would stay back at the hospital and watch over in case he woke up.

Soomi hadn't come up with this idea. She wanted to stay and watch over him, too, but all of the thirteen had agreed that it was for the best. The poor girl had too much on her shoulders and they wanted to relieve her of some of that stress.

Knocking on the door a few times, she adjusts the strap of her bag on her shoulder, sighing as she waits for someone to open the door. Upon arriving at the front door, she didn't actually have a plan on what she'd do about Jungkook. There wasn't anyway to avoid him even if she wanted to. It was hard enough to resist him as he was, but adding the way he would always purposely bother her and that stupid hair of his now, how was she supposed to maintain her courage?

It didn't seem right to give into him. Even though her and Jinhwan weren't together and never had been, it still felt like it. A part of her still felt off without him, but yet she didn't feel right without Jungkook either. The endless, difficult decision only continued to weigh down her mental state as she sighed. Still, it wouldn't be okay for her to be with Jungkook right now.

Right?

Heaving out a sigh, the world seemed to be mad at her as she had looked up to the sound of the door opening. Assuming it would be Jimin, she was ready to tell him about everything that was bothering her right now. Her rant would probably have gone on for hours with all that was on her mind, all the way from this horrid psycho to the decision she just couldn't seem to make.

"Soomi, you decided to stay here," there's a giddy, child like smile on Jungkook's face from the sight of her. Her stomach drops when she meets those eyes of his. Just like she thought, you could never get away from Jeon Jungkook.

She only responds with a nod, a close lipped grin on her face as she enters. Jungkook had stepped to the side of the door so he could allow her in. Her eyes rake across the suite, which was pretty much the same as the iKON one with just a few tiny differences like furniture. The modernized decorated hotel suite was nearly pitch dark except for a singular lamp that was on in the living room, providing them light to see each other in the dim air. 

Her back was to her ex fiance as she walks deeper into the large living room, not knowing where exactly she was even going to sleep so she just set her bag beside one of the sofa's. Assuming it was the same as the other, there were two rooms, one with two queen sized bed and then one with a singular king sized bed. Knowing her old friends well, she had a feeling they had decided who slept where by playing rock paper scissors, they'd decided everything that way since their trainee days, after all.

From the fact she'd been silent, Jungkook frowns a bit. He knew there was probably a lot on her mind. Jimin had spoken to him before he went to sleep when the boys came to their own room, and the words of Soomi's best friend repeated in his head as he turns to stare at the back of her head.

She's going through a lot, Jungkook, let her breathe and stop making it harder on her.

His competitive, possessive traits were just going to have to calm themselves. It would be hard for him to just turn them off, but if it was really troubling her so much, he would do it.

"How are you feeling?" he asks, walking closer to her even though she still wasn't facing him. Just because he would stop messing with her, didn't mean he was going to stop showing her affection altogether. Everyone knew he couldn't do that even if he wanted to. She wasn't dating Jinhwan, and she still loved him, he could never resist the urge to touch her, which he didn't make sound sexual by intent.

She'd shivered from the sudden feeling of his hand on her shoulder. His thumb rubs circles in a comforting way, something Jungkook had done for as long as she could remember. Something inside of her wanted to turn around, but she was worried, she was worried about looking at him directly, especially when they were both alone in such a dark room.

So, she still resisted the urge to turn to him. "I'm - questionable," she admits after a moment of thought, "I don't know, there's a lot going through my head right now."

Soomi hadn't really thought about ranting to Jungkook. Even though she had plenty of times, it was hard to rant about your issues to someone when they happened to be the issue. There was also a portion of the story she didn't want to tell him, such as the part involving the photo of her and Jimin and the fact she was so hung up between him and Jinhwan. Although, he already knew the second part pretty well.

The two had been there a thousand times. Nostalgia overcomes the atmosphere in that hotel suite as he then uses both of his hands to her forearms from behind her, rubbing up and down after feeling that she was cold, trying to warm her up but comfort her at the same time. Jungkook hadn't been this way in a long time, he hadn't even done this when she called him that first time - not since they started talking again.

It brought her back, and the recollection of that fuzzy feeling made her squeeze her eyes shut. She really missed him this way.

"Do you want to talk about it?"







///


2016


"Yah, yah, where are you going?" a half awake, weary Jungkook calls, rubbing his eyes as he walks out of the bedroom in the hotel suite. His bare feet plop against the wooden flooring, and he could barely see from the fact he just woke up from his peaceful slumber. The sight of his fiance taking the car keys from the vehicle they rented, an anguished look on her face she grabs her purse and the key card to the room. She was still dressed in sweatpants and a hoodie, but the look on her face made you think she was about to go to war. Her hand had just been hovering over the handle of the front door when the voice of Jungkook had halted her actions.

When she doesn't say anything because of the shocked look on her face, he flicks on the light in the suite. Instantly, he could see her nervous expression clearly as he walks across the living room, "Soomi-ah, what's wrong? Why are you leaving?" he pouts, concern dripping from that sharp jawline of his, "are you having second thoughts about tonight?"

From his words being completely off, she gawks up at him for a second, forgetting the fact she had yet to say anything. "No!" she practically yells out in a panicked tone, causing Jungkook to grow even more confused on what was going on with her. Her voice goes back to being a mumble as she shakes her head, looking away to her feet, "no, that's not it at all, I just wanted to - see something," she whispered the last words so quietly he wouldn't of been able to understand her if the lightest sound had sounded anywhere in their vicinity.

Thankfully, the two were alone in that hotel suite. The others bought them their own so they could celebrate their new called engagement. This meant they were granted with a peaceful silence.

"Soomi, talk to me," he pleads to her, reaching out to set his hands on her shoulders. The feeling didn't make her tense in the slightest, as he was rubbing circles on her shoulder pads, calming the nerves that were just freaking out only moments ago, "let me know what's going on, where were you going? I'll go with you, we can go together."

The soft voice of her beloved fiance warmed her heart and every inch of her body. He was so understanding and always worried about her, even when he shouldn't be. She could do something as small as scrape her knee and Jungkook would be at her side in an instant, asking her what he could do to help and putting a band-aid over it. 

He was an angel, one she didn't deserve.

"My family," she finally blurts abruptly, "I was going to see the house, not them, I just -," she heaves out a frustrated sigh, pulling at her hair in annoyance that she couldn't seem to find her words, "I just wanted to see if they were still there, you know? I don't know, I didn't want to actually go see them - well I do - I just don't think they would want to see me."

There was something else bothering her on that night. It wasn't necessarily as big a factor as her family and the fact she was back in New York temporarily, but it was still something she felt guilty and beyond lost about.

Just hours ago, prior to her engagement happening, her best friend kissed her, and she had no idea why. Jimin had yet to even speak to her about the matter, but she was craving answers and some form of understanding. It was practically his fault that she cheated on her now fiance, after all. 

Little did she know, there was something bothering Jungkook, too.

There's a dull look on his face as he sighs, pulling her forward into his chest so he could embrace her. Kissing her forehead, he'd rested his chin on her head in thought for a moment.

"Of course they would want to see you," he expresses after a moment, "no matter what hardships you went through, Mi, they're still your family and they still love you. I'm sure they would be ecstatic to know you're thinking about them."

She opens her mouth to correct him, but she reminded herself he didn't really know the full story.

"So, if you just want to go see your old home and if they're there, that's okay," he hums after another few seconds, "you can always come back and see them again when you're ready, there's nothing wrong with it."

"yeah," she breathes out into his chest, "you're right."

Pulling out of the embrace, he flashes her that goofy, bunny tooth smile, "of course I'm right, but you should've woken me up, I'll come with you."

Knowing he wouldn't take no for an answer, she nods hesitantly before explaining, "I didn't want to wake you up."

Jungkook had ran like a marathon runner to their bedroom to grab his shoes and a jacket. With in a second, he was standing before her again, "you can always bother me, Mi, it wouldn't have been a problem, it's always a blessing to see your face~," he's intoning, and she wrinkles her nose playfully at him.

Turning away, she opens the door, and they leave together, facing the world one obstacle at a time. Jungkook's hand finds it's way to hers, almost naturally at that point. The feeling of warmth was enough to make her remain calm despite what she was doing, and it was what would help her get through the night.

Really, even if she wanted to, she couldn't go see her family. There was no way they would welcome her, at least not nicely. It would be an entire explosive of outrage she was scared to witness, or at least she thought. That was always how it was. After what she caused them and how they'd ended off, she couldn't imagine her parents reaction at seeing her again.

The drive there was mind wrecking. She thought she was going to melt into the seat as she stared out the window, hearing the GPS woman read off to Jungkook where to go every once in a while since he was driving and she input the directions on his phone. Soomi was left to try to collect her thoughts, leaning her head against the cold window and watching the scenery they passed.

She wondered if they had the same idiotic rules, the same speaking patterns, the same harsh standards for their children. She wondered if her siblings ever wanted to run away like she did. Did they think of her?

A part of her told herself not to care, they betrayed her in the end. But they were still her siblings, just like her parents were still what brought her into this world.

Eventually, the outside began to look more familiar. The upper east side didn't look any different, even though it had been a good few years. Just the sight of the large buildings, before they entered her neighborhood, was enough to give her a sick feeling in the pit of her stomach. She was really going here again even though she deemed she never would.

Calm down, she yells at herself mentally, you're not actually going to see them.

"Is this it?" Jungkook asks in curiosity as he slows down the speed of the car. She sits up straight in the seat to peer to her left, just as the GPS lady had called out you've arrived at your destination.

Sure enough, there it was.

The same cars parked in the driveway, the same flowers blooming in the garden, the same decorations on the porch. Her old home, the place she grew up, looked exactly how she remembered it.

"Yeah, that's it."

As soon as she says, this a car had pulled up to the curb of the house. Jungkook parked across the street and turned the car off so there was no light to indicate someone was looking at the house. 

Soomi tenses at the sight of the vehicle, beginning to cough from shock. It causes her fiance to turn to her with worried eyes, "are you okay?"

Waving a hand in dismissal to him, she collects herself as she shares, "that's my car."

"Huh?!" he was in utter shock, "yah, how rich is your family?!"

She ignored his question, her eyes too busy watching the figure who got out of it. The slim body of whoever it was should've told her enough, but she still watched as they lock the car that was once hers, before walking into the lights on the front of the home.

"Chaerin-ah," the name leaves her lips so solemnly.

"Is that-," Jungkook starts, but already knowing what he was asking, she cuts him off.

Green eyes still stuck on the girl who was now on the porch of the house, checking something on her phone, Soomi mutters, "my sister."

He didn't actually know how many siblings she had, but he didn't ask as she seemed to in her own emotions and might not want to talk about it. By this point, Soomi was basically on Jungkook's lap so that she could see clearly out the window, since her house was located to the left that was on his side.

It's when she tries to go back to her own seat and stumbles, that her elbow flies to the right, hitting the car horn button. A shriek leaves her lips as it hits it, and the honk sounds outside into the night.

Eyes falling out of their sockets, she covers her hands with her mouth as if it would do something. Her sister, that had still been standing on the porch, eyes wander around from the abrupt sound. She'd blinked a few times in confusion, before shrugging and heading into the house.

Meanwhile, Soomi leans back into the leather seat of the car, holding a hand to her heart as she breathes to her fiance, "let's go back to the hotel now - please."










53:00 | solicitude


"I'm sorry for everything, Mi," his voice was soft as he spoke out into the dark, "I should of done something back then, I shouldn't have listened to them. That was the dumbest thing I could have done, I loved you so much, and I made you think I didn't."

The small, exhausted girl didn't say anything as she lays there in his lap. Her feet were sprawled out across the rest of the couch, and she only sat, eyes closed but yet not asleep, hearing out everything Jungkook was saying after her own rant. 

"If I would of done something back then, it could have prevented all of this," he sighed out, feeling an evident pain in his chest, "I still love you, I hate seeing you hurt - over anything. Whether it's your family, this Jinhwan guy, or this fucking piece of shit, I just want to make it go away but I can't."

His voice cracks at the end, and only then did she open her eyes. There's a frown on her face, and all she knew to do was reach her hand up. Jungkook didn't flinch at the feeling of her soft fingertips running across his skin, caressing his cheek as a way to calm him.

"It's okay, Kookie," she soothes, "none of it is your fault, you did what you thought was right at the time. Either way, it's all in the past now, we can only face the present." 

"You're right," he agrees after a second of thought, "and in the present, I'm going to catch this absolute fuckwad excuse of a human being."

Soomi chuckles at his words, as he sounded rather fired up about it all. Nothing could be said on her part because she was, too. Nothing would be justifiable for this person, they deserved pain for everything they'd done. She would never be able to even think about forgiving someone who hurt the people she loved.

Not knowing what more to say, she says nothing at all as her hand falls back to her lap. Getting bored again, she takes Jungkook's hands that were to the side of him, messing with his fingers as she thought about everything that was going on.

He didn't mind. It was rather cute to watch her, as she would put her hand against his, comparing the sizes. When she'd see, to no surprise, that her hand was minuscule compared to Jungkook's, a pout would take over her lips.

"Soomi," he blurts before he could stop himself, "I missed you."

But she didn't seem too shocked nor bothered by his sudden confession. Still messing around and not looking to him, she only mutters, "Huh?"

Well, there's no turning back now. Since they were already sharing heartfelt thoughts, what did he really have to lose? Jungkook wasn't saying what he was saying because of any sort of competition, he was saying it because she needed to know, it was what was truly in his heart. 

Her.

"The year without you," he explains further without hesitation this time, "you know how the others said I always checked your socials? I really did, I kept up with every comeback, every picture, every interview. I just wanted to know what you were doing, I wanted to know if you were moving on without me," she pursed her lips as she listened to his emotional admittance. Soomi's brain does a relapse over the year without Jungkook, and she had to keep herself from physically wincing at the reminiscence of memories.

When the scandal broke lose, it'd been early morning that she had yet to even be awake. It was then, that day, that Jungkook had so called broken up with her and claimed he didn't love her, all for the sake of keeping her safe. She wished she would've known it back then, but she was in shambles the second she rushed out of that apartment. 

Only then, on the way back to the YG dorms, did she run into Jinhwan. He saw she was a damsel in distress, and comforted her even though they didn't really know each other. 

And then began their friendship, as they kept running into each other, with Soomi being drunk practically every single time. That was when she developed her terrible drinking problem, one she didn't think she would actually get over without rehab. Thankfully, she avoided having to go to it (it would've caused another entire scandal) because she had her own rehab - Jinhwan. 

Moving forward was when their fling began. By that time, she seemed to forget about what happened with her and Jungkook. Of course, it had crossed her mind every now and then, and she would still check up on him occasionally, but she wasn't obsessed. 

If he lied to you and threw you away like that, Soomi-ah, then he doesn't deserve to even be on your mind for a second, was what Jinhwan had told her, and she really took it to heart. She thought the love of her life was a liar that entire time, what good would it have done for her to sit and wallow over him?

Thinking back through that year, she sighs, "I would've never known, I thought you were fine without me," she shares what was going through her mind, "I mean, I was under the impression you never cared for me, you know?"

There's a dull look in his eyes as he thinks back to all of it, "I know, I wish it didn't go like that," he leans his head back against the back of the couch, peering up at the ceiling. Soomi was peering up from his lap, as well, but she wasn't looking at the ceiling, she was just admiring him. Rethinking how she said before she didn't have time then to stop and admire Jungkook - well, now she did, and she had no shame in doing so. He was so pretty.

"Just think," he gawks in a dreamy tone, "we could be married right now, Mi. You always talked about having the honeymoon in Bora Bora or Venice, I even looked up the cost back then, and it was so much compared to how much we were making off music - but I was so determined to take you there anyway."

She could've fainted from the fact he remembered something like that.

"You looked up the cost, really?" she sputters, her heart beating out of her chest from the feelings he was throwing at her, "aw, Jungkook~."

He giggles lightly at the way she sung out his name in a high pitched tone. Yet, no words left his lips as he was still laying there, thinking about something. Subconsciously, his hands wander to her hair, where he played with it gently as he pondered a certain something.

"I'd still marry you," he professes confidently, "in a heartbeat, and I promise I'd take you to both Bora Bora and Venice, and anywhere else you want to add to the list."

At this point, Soomi could cry. It was so insane to her to hear him confessing all of this. Since they were on speaking terms again, they had yet to really talk this way. Spending time like this with him took her back to everything, the day they met, the engagement, all of it. It all seemed like it happened recently, she felt like she'd gone back in time or she was just reliving one of the many moments they'd had together.

But she wasn't, she was making new ones, with her ex fiance that she deemed was one of the worst people in the world at one point.

She was speechless.

"Jungkook..."

"Don't," he cuts her off, "you don't have to say anything, I'm not trying to make it sound like I'm telling you to choose me," he sighs into the darkness, closing his eyes momentarily so no emotion could well up in them, "I just wanted to remind you that when all of this is over, I'll be waiting for you."

There were so many emotions crying inside of her head, so many things she wanted to do. If she was confused then, she sure was now. Soomi felt like she was on the verge of exploding, and she could only hope that the entire psycho deal was resolved soon so she could focus on another prominent issue in her life - who her heart belonged to.

How would she ever begin to figure it out? She didn't know, not when the both of them kept playing with her like this. 

Sitting up straight, she wanted to kiss him as she moved to sit beside him, close enough they were practically one. Jungkook had leaned up, as well, from the sudden movement, peering back at her with those doe eyes that always tickled her heart strings. Her green irises shine under the singular light of the lamp that was on in the room, and not thinking, she leaned in to do something that wasn't as intense as kissing him.

Ever so softly, her lips linger on his cheek for a few moments. Jungkook was disappointed she didn't kiss him elsewhere, at first, but as she pulls away, he couldn't help but mirror the playful, childish grin on her face.

Kim Soomi, you're special.

These are words that were going though not only Jungkook's head. Across the large living room of the hotel suite, sat another boy. His silver hair was a mess on his head from rolling around in the bed he was trying to sleep in, and there's a pained look on his bare face as he's leaning against the wall in the hall, completely unnoticeable in the darkness. 

Even though he always gave her advice and told her both Jinhwan and Jungkook would be waiting for her and support her when she made her decision, it still hurt him to say those things. I'm just her best friend, he always had to remind himself. In reality, what was Jimin really expecting? What did he think would of happened that day on the rooftop? 

He guessed a part of him just wanted to check, to see if she felt the spark that he felt every time she smiled. He guessed he was hoping she would have a sudden realization, one that they belonged together, that she was meant for him and not Jungkook. 

It was as if she was going to wake up one day, and make the decision and choose him and not Jungkook or Jinhwan. Such a silly dream he had in the back of his head, always continued to linger there no matter how hard he tried to push it away. He hated that this happened to him, he hated that he fell for his best friend and now he would have to deal with it forever.

Now, he was left in the shadows, only to witness and wish that he could be someone else. Jimin wondered if there was something, anything he could of done to change the outcome of this situation. He wondered if in another dimension, Jungkook was put in his place instead.

Pulling at his hair in frustration, the sight of Soomi kissing Jungkook (not knowing it was his cheek because he couldn't see them fully,) was like a stab to the heart. Every time he listened to her say anything about either of them, talk about her undying love for Jungkook or her endless admiration for Jinhwan, he wanted to disappear.

Was he selfish? Probably, it was his own fault in the end.

He brought it upon himself. The competition that Soomi spoke of, despite his hopes and dreams, he wasn't a part of.

And he never would be.


54:00 | justice


Soomi's brows crease downward, her green irises narrow ever so slightly, and she bites her lip every so often when she needed to think extra hard on something. After a few seconds of deep contemplation, she'd hum in satisfaction to herself, before her pen would continue to fly across the paper. Even though her natural handwriting was usually nice, like every other time, when it came to writing quickly due to how fast it would all come to her, the words on her paper looked like that of scribbles.

It didn't matter, though, as long as she could read it.

Clenching the journal a bit tighter in her hand that held it on her lap, she thinks for another moment, before writing down a few more words. Writing songs was a lot easier when you had something like a guitar or piano to help you, but on it's own, it became a lot harder than one might think. Without an instrument to accompany the art, song writing became more like poetry to her. This was what she was treating it as, as she heaves out a sigh, leaning back against the head board of the bed.

There wasn't any telling what time it was. She'd stayed at the Bangtan's room like she said she would, talking and spending time with Jungkook. The nostalgia was overwhelming, but it brought her a feeling of happiness she didn't know she could feel, especially with the current circumstances. Jungkook had brought her mind off Jinhwan, and he made her laugh so hard her stomach had ached.

He'd gotten a room to himself in the suite, so it was where they ended up sleeping. For the first time, he hadn't tried anything scandalous with her. They'd just stayed up, talking, until they passed out next to each other. It warmed Soomi's heart to see Jungkook being so sweet and genuinely selfless, a lot like he used to be, instead of putting on the whole competitive, obnoxious act he'd had going for a while. She'd been reminded why she loved him so much back then.

Having had passed out for however long, she'd been awoken to the sound of Jungkook doing something in the restroom connected to the bedroom. He was dressed and looking ready to go somewhere, but when she asked, he'd just said he was going on a run.

"Don't miss me too much, okay, Mi?"

"Shut up, bold of you to think I would."

He'd only giggled that little laugh of his before waving as he walked out the door.

It was weird he was even going for a run at a time like this, but she figured there was a lot on his mind, especially after all they spoke about last night. Those talks had given her a lot to think about, as well. Spending time apart after that was probably good for them. Plus, she knew Jungkook really liked to exercise, so it wasn't out of place if you really thought about it. She didn't think much of it for those reasons.

All Soomi could think of, was the song lyrics that kept coming to her as she scribbles them onto the paper.

I need you, even though it's hard to say that now

I still want to see you so badly

But I still miss you

You still love me

We can't help it

Abruptly, she stops writing, pursing her lips as she stared at everything she had so far. There was a lot of work that had to be done, like always. Most songs didn't turn out perfect the first time, especially when you didn't have an instrument.

Since she was treating it like a poem, the song was telling a fantasy-like story. Exploring things like that wasn't something she usually did through her music, but Soomi was also always trying to go to new heights with her songs.

Around half an hour passes, and she was beginning to feel sort of helpless with the song. Unsure of the reason behind such a feeling, she'd forgotten the fact she was in a hotel of multiple song writers. The thought causes her to perk up, eyes wide with realization. Stupid, she curses to herself, how could you not think of it earlier?

The first one's who came to mind when she thought of song writing - Yoongi and Hanbin. Soomi had experience working with both of them. Her and Yoongi used to spend days together in the studio when she was still at Big Hit. She helped him write the lyrics for many of their songs for the debut of BTS, as they got to debut a while before she did. It'd been an exciting day for all of them.

Hanbin, on the other hand, had been her writing buddy for the last year. Since they met, they immediately found common ground through music. She hadn't gotten to go into that studio of his until recently, but since then, they had a great time writing together.

Rising from the bed, it took her a moment to really decide who she wanted to ask. Soomi got to write with Hanbin all the time, though, and she was literally in the same suite with Yoongi.

Knowing him, it wouldn't be a surprise to her if he was up right now.

Clenching her journal tightly under her arm, she trots her way out of Jungkook's room. Having hadn't slept long at all, it was insane she had any source of energy left in her body. She guessed she was just that excited about writing this song.

The sight of Soomi walking so jumpily at six in the morning was horrifying to Yoongi. He was just sitting on the sofa, having had woken up around five minutes ago due to the other's horrible snoring, and decided to just down some coffee. Just when he's off in his own world, heavy footsteps clashing against the wooden floor caused his head to jolt up, where he saw that.

At the sight of him being awake like she presumed, she practically catapults onto the sofa next to him, "Yoongles, I really need your help!"

Blinking at her, he had been about to of wished her a good morning, but at the sound of the name, a blank expression takes over his face. "Yah, that name doesn't make me want to help you, that's for fucking sure," he mutters bitterly.

Ignoring his tone, she knew it was only fun in jokes with Yoongi. He liked to act like he was some sort of bad ass, but he had to be one of the biggest softies she'd ever met. This is why she would never stop using that nickname she'd given him from day one, she knew deep down he didn't mind it as much as he put on.

"I'm writing this song," she's already cutting to the point, and at the mention of his hobby, he looks to her with curious eyes, "I don't have much done and I'm sort of stuck, so I was hoping you could take a look at it, being that you're a genius and all that."

He glances from her eyes, to the journal that laid in her hands. After a moment of thought, he shrugs, "genius I am, let me see it."

Chuckling at his cockiness, she holds out the journal to him. Yoongi's eyes instantly begin skimming over the page as he holds it in his hands. Soomi only watched his face carefully, as if she would be able to read his emotions when he saw something he liked or disliked. Like usual, though, Yoongi didn't grant you with something like that. He never showed a sign of emotion on his face when he was doing music, he'd only crease his brows in concentration every once in a while and make hums of satisfaction.

He hadn't even spoken a word to her or looked in her direction. Taking the pencil that was attached to the journal, he leans closer to the page to insert or scribble something. Having no idea what he was doing, she knew she trusted his judgment whole heartedly, so never would she interrupt his creative juices.

Setting the pencil down with a click, he lets out a deep breathe. Putting the journal beside him instead of giving it back to Soomi, she was left a little lost when he rose to his feet, heading casually towards the hallway.

"Where are you going?" She asked blankly, but he already disappeared into the room.

Seconds later, there was clashing and rustling heard from that same hall. Green eyes wide, she couldn't help but gawk at the sight of the boy entering the living room with at least three giant backpacks. How he was managing to carry them all? She wished she knew.

Yoongi showed no sign of distress as he sets the bags all down beside the sofa they sat on. Plopping onto the couch next to her, he began unpacking the contents of the bags.

She nearly fell off the couch.

"You brought all of this — seriously?!" She wasn't disappointed, she was actually rather thrilled.

From his laptop, to the portable keyboard you hooked up to it, to his mic, Yoongi brought all the mixing and recording equipment she knew existed. Soomi could only shake her head, completely flabbergasted that someone could be this invested in music. She didn't think a portable studio existed, but Min Yoongi proved her wrong.

"You never know when you'll feel creative," he shoots her a small smile, and she could only squeal in pure happiness.

Before he could say another word, while Yoongi was focusing on plugging everything in and turning it all on, she scooted over to wrap an arm around his shoulder. The feeling of her giving him a side hug would have usually made him protest, like the time when they first saw each other after so long, but since they were alone — Yoongi didn't mind.

"You're the best, Yoongles."

"I was okay with you hugging me until you said that name," with these words, he threw her arm off him gently. Soomi could only laugh as he does this, knowing it was still a joke.

"You love the nickname, liar."

"I really do not," he sighs out dramatically, "but forget it, we have a song to make."



///




"Excuse me, do you make the coffee here?" Jungkook's voice is that of dripping kindness, his doe eyes are shining under the lights on the hotel ceiling. Those toned hands of his grip the edge of the counter, and he leans forward to peer at the lady with a curious glance.

She looks up from the magazine she was reading, her eyes looking his figure up and down. There's a tint of something in them, as she probably realized who he was. Either way, she tried to keep her cool as she sets the magazine down, walking forward to stand on the other side of the counter from him, "yeah, I do, would you like to order?"

"Sure, I'd like a caramel macchiato with three shots of espresso," he hums and the lady nods with a smile.

Jungkook made sure he was doing that thing that always tended to bother people, Soomi included. Biting down on his lip every so often, keeping eye contact, and showing those bunny teeth of his after he said anything.

The lady seemed rather affected by it from the way she fluttered her eyelashes as she spins on her heel to head to the kitchen, "of course, coming right up."

She hadn't even asked him to pay.

"Wait, could I ask you something?" He calls to her before she could enter, and she turned around almost instantly. "are you the only barista here?" He knew he couldn't just ask this or it would sound suspicious, "if so, I might have to come back everyday."

It was like she was going to pass out as she stares into his brown irises. Jungkook was too good at this to show it, but he was laughing hysterically inside. Girls always foamed at the mouth over him, and he felt bad for how much power he had over them.

The only one that never seemed to completely get affected by him was Soomi. She was always a challenge, it was part of the reason he always adored her so dearly.

There's a small silence between him and the barista.

"I am," she finally breathes out, an evident flirtatious vibe in her voice, "I would love if you did."

Now he could hide the devious smirk that spreads over his lips. Pulling out his phone, he presses the button to stop recording, "Great, cancel my order please, I got what I needed."

The woman was beyond confused at his words. There's a slight fear in her eyes as she looks to his phone screen, "excuse me, what are you talking about?"

Jungkook ignores her, instead dialing something on his phone before putting it to his ear.

"Hello, officer, I've found the woman who drugged a man and put him into a coma," he's humming to them on the other line, "yes, regarding the incident yesterday," another few seconds pass as he hears what they're asking him, "yes, at the Hilton, I have recorded proof of her admitting to the crime."

"We'll be there shortly."

"Thank you!" He cheerily calls before ending the call. Only then did Jungkook turn back to the woman, who was petrified with shock and fear. She wasn't looking at him, her eyes were fixated on the ground or something behind him as she seemed to be trembling, in some sort of trance from what she realized what was happening.

"H-how—," She's muttering to herself or maybe Jungkook, "I-I didn't do it — I didn't m-mean to..." her voice trails off as she whines like a lost puppy. However, it was hard for him to have any sympathy for her. Whether she was forced to do it or not, she still gave into it. It was still her fault even partly.

"I don't care for what you have to say, but I'm sure the cops would love to hear it," he tells her whether she was even speaking to him.

Turning on his heel, Jungkook begins to make his way towards the exit doors. As if on queue, the wailing of sirens is heard in the distance, seeming to confirm his previous words.

"W-Wait, please," the woman's voice suddenly calls out, and there's an unimpressed look on his face when he turns to look to her again, "please I — I didn't do it, she forced me!"

He blinks at her a few times, this was what he thought happened, but another idea comes to mind.

Walking towards her, the sirens only get closer as he does so. Not wasting anytime, he rushes out the question, "She? What did she look like?"

This is what Soomi needed to know. This was the key to who was doing this to her and had to him only over a year ago.

She only trembled from the words that left his mouth. Jungkook peers down at her with hopeful eyes, waiting for her to say anything. But she was practically traumatized, frozen like some sort of statue.

Until she mumbles out, "I-I can't tell you — she said she'd kill me..."

"Police!" The sudden yell cuts him off from saying anything else, the doors of the hotel bursting open. Jungkook turns to see the authorities he called all rushing in, "Heo Hyelin, you're under arrest for carrying of illegal drugs and attempted murder!"

Two of the officers rush to the girl they'd just named, grabbing her arms and forcing them behind her back. There's a clicking sound of the handcuffs, and she hadn't even tried to fight their actions as they dragged her out of the hotel.

It was sort of disturbing to witness. She looked like her soul had left her body, like she'd died.

"Thank you for calling us, young man," the offer he spoke to on the phone hums, patting his shoulder, "it was tragic what happened to that man yesterday, I hope he can have a safe recovery knowing who did it to him is behind bars," he's saying, "but please send the recording to us, we'll review it before taking her into questioning back at the station."

Only nodding, the officer had sent him a close lipped smile before turning to walk away.

"Wait, sir," he calls out, "I think there was someone else involved."

The officer blinks at him in curiosity, "you mean someone made her do it?"

Pursing his lips, he nods. "I just thought you should know that. Also, if you find anything, could you call this number," he's mumbling, turning to the counter to take a napkin before using a random pen to scribble Soomi's number on it. Walking to the officer, he holds it out, "it's the number of a close friend of the man in the coma, if the woman admits to who made her do it, she would be glad to know."

Humming in understanding, he takes the napkin from the boy's hand. "I'll make sure to let her know."

Letting out a breathe of gratitude, he sends him a grateful smile, "thank you."

Watching the police men leave, he was left to stand in the rather dead hotel coffee shop by himself. A yawn leaves his lips from the lack of sleep he received last night, but for once, Jungkook felt victorious. He felt like he'd done something good, like he was one step closer to finding whoever this monster was.

With that, he makes his way out of the hotel, ready to actually get some exercise by running back to the one they were all staying at. He hoped Soomi would be glad to hear the good news.

It was all for her, after all.


55:00 | one step closer


"Well, what do you think?" Yoongi hums, his eyes wandering to Soomi as his hands leave the keyboard of the laptop. Leaning back into the sofa they'd been sitting on the entire morning, they tune out the sounds of the other boys eating and chatting in the kitchen as they'd woken up a while ago, having had been focused on working on this song. 

Her eyes wander to the screen, where he mixed music in what seemed in seconds. She'd forgotten how good Yoongi was at what he did, and he was seriously no joke when it came to music. Although he was cocky about his talent, it was no doubt for good reason, he was amazing.

Looking down to her notebook, there were pages filled with the lyrics to the song that they had wrote and revised in such a short time. Of course, it would still have to go through to the company workers that would overlook it and make finishing touches. Then, you'd go into the studio and officially record the vocals so they were good quality. After that, you send it into the CEO, who looks over the track and decides if he wants to release it or not.

"This is perfect," she boasts, "thanks for your genius touch, Yoongles."

Shaking his head at the name, he figured there was no use in saying anything about it at that point. Like back then, it was starting to grow on him, anyway, even though he'd never admit that to anyone. 

"Sure, the concept was already great," he admits, and she sends him a grateful smile. Her eyes wandering elsewhere, she was off in her own world, wondering where Jungkook was after so long. Did he really go for a run for hours? When suddenly, the boy's voice beside her cuts in again, "but Mi, who's the song about?"

Her eyes widen a bit from the unexpected question. Trying to hide it immediately, she purses her lips in thought as she glances up to Yoongi, who was staring at her intently. Soomi was hoping he didn't have any idea, but from the look in his eyes, he definitely knew. Yoongi could just see through people, especially Mi since he'd known her for so long.

When she doesn't say anything for a moment, only sitting there with that look as if she's speechless, he reassures her, "it's a good song, Mi, you should show it to Jungkook, he'd really like it."

"I don't know about that," her voice trails off into the air, eyes looking away from him.

A sigh is heard from her old friend, before there's a rustling sound. Yoongi rises to his feet, closing the laptop before him and making sure his stuff was out of the walkway, "well, figure out who you're gonna choose, first," he hums casually, causing her head to jolt to him again from shock of what she just heard.

It wasn't a surprise the boys knew about the decision, obviously. She was just surprised at the fact he said that, but what was she expecting from someone as blunt and straight forward as Min Yoongi?

She was at a loss for words once again, but it didn't matter since he'd left into the kitchen to get some of whatever Jin had made for them that morning. Although she was feeling rather hungry herself, she didn't have the mental capacity to go in there. Soomi was left to sit on the sofa by herself, chin in the palm of her hand as her mind wanders.

Jungkook, Jinhwan, just the thought of their names made her want to rip her hair out.

As if on queue, the front door of the hotel suite clicks, before bolting open. Jeon Jungkook waltz inside with a glorious look on his face, feeling like he'd done something right for once in his life. He was never good with decisions, but finally, this one - this one had to be something that needed to be done. This would help Soomi, the girl he loved, which was all he ever wanted to do.

Conveniently, his eyes fall on the girl that was sitting on the couch. She looked rather distressed, and he hoped that his exciting news would ease that feeling. 

"Soomi~," he cheerily sings as he walks towards her, her head jerking up to meet his doe eyes. Falling onto the sofa, Jungkook wraps an arm around her as he gushes, "I have good news!"

Meanwhile, the girl wrinkles her nose from the fact he was sweating presumably from running. Pushing his arm off of her, she scoots a bit away from him as she whines, "take a shower, I don't want your sweat all over me," completely ignoring what he even said.

He couldn't help but roll his eyes at her everlasting dramatic demeanor. "Sure, but you have to join me," he only received a glare for his teasing. Giggling at how cutesy she was just by the annoyed look on her face, he pokes her shoulder, "okay, but don't you want to hear my news?"

Confusion is written all over her face as she peers up at him. Never had she seen him so joyous, not in a while. He was that of a baby as his eyes shine under the light brightly, mouth slightly agape showing those two bunny teeth as he waits for her reply. 

"I don't know, you tell me," she finally replies cautiously, trying to figure out what it could be. What could be so exciting that he discovered while going on a run?

Letting out a deep breathe, he prepares to tell her the story. 

"I caught who drugged the guy," he shares, and she only blinks at him blankly.

Furrowing her brows, she mutters, looking for confirmation, "Jinhwan?"

"Yeah," he nods, and her eyes bulge out of their sockets as she seemed to be trying to grasp such a fact, "she got arrested and was taken to the station for questioning, I gave them your number if she shares anything about who made her do it."

Soomi's eyes become glassy as she stares at the boy before her. As if their talks last night weren't enough, he'd done this for her and Jinhwan. It wasn't something she would expect from Jungkook, and it had her nearly bursting into tears.

"Jungkook," she could only manage to whisper, "thank you..."

He only flashes her that smile of his, one she was more than ecstatic to see on his face in that moment. Unable to hide her joy, she scoots closer to him again, not caring at all about the fact he was still sweaty from his exercising session. Her small arms find their way around his neck, where she squeezes the life out of him - but he still wasn't affected by her small amount of strength.

His heart on his sleeve, he snuggles his head into her hair as he accepts the hug. Soomi, his mind screams, I'll find whoever's caused all of this, I promise.

That he would, Jungkook was only one step closer. He was going to make this person pay, for hurting her, hurting him, and ruining the best thing he'd ever had - an engagement to the girl he loved. 

He'd make sure of that.

"Yah, what's with the sappy reunion?" Jin's voice breaks their embrace, Soomi's cheeks a shade of pink as she sees all of her friends entering the living room after finishing their breakfast.

Jungkook wasn't affected that every saw it. If anything, that made him happier, especially once he took note of the bitter look on Jimin's face as the boys take seats either on the floor or on one of the sofa's. "What, can I not hug the girl I love?" he asks blankly, leaning back into the sofa with an embarrassed Soomi beside him.

Covering her face, she hated when he did this.

"Can you just stop being cringe?" Yoongi scoffs as he picks up his laptop since Jungkook was sitting where he previously had been.

The maknae only shrugs, a toothy grin on his lips.

Like usual, the pointless conversation disappears into the air of the living room hotel. The others begin talking casually about things like the upcoming festival and the songs they were performing, then what they would do for their time off after all the award shows in early January end. Taehyung and Jungkook ended up playing video games on a console they brought and hooked up to the TV (after Jungkook went to shower,) which resulted in a lot of insulting each other and jokes. It was an entertaining sight, and Soomi had only watched everything take place as everyone was having fun and doing their own thing. 

As for herself, she would speak up and join in on conversations every once in a while, like when they would ask if she was looking forward to the shows and what she would be singing, or if she was releasing anything new. She shared that she's always working on music, leaving out the fact her and Yoongi literally had just wrote a new song hours ago. If she were to tell them, they would insist on reading it, which would lead to everyone realizing it was about Jungkook. This wasn't a problem, really, she just didn't want Jungkook himself to know.

Thankfully, Yoongi got the gist she didn't want to share this fact, and didn't say anything either. He was too distracted by something on his laptop and talking to Namjoon and Hoseok.

Jimin had taken Jungkook's spot beside Soomi, and he would bother her and they'd talk every so often. He could tell she was busy with her phone, though, so he didn't want to annoy her.

Although she wouldn't of minded, it was no joke she was distracted by texting her newest friends.

soo: ur at the hospital rn?

soo: thank god. we can get to the bottom of this once and for all.

junhoe: evn tho he hates jinan obv

soo: nope, hopefully i will soon

soo: well kookie said that the lady said something about 'she' as in whoever told her to do it

soo: so i guess we know this weirdo is a girl

soo: i dont think they're obsessed with me in any romantic sense

soo: and people are gay these days, junhoe

junhoe: idk they say weird stuff don't they

junhoe: i know, i live with yunhyeong

soo: they want to ruin my life

junhoe: and idc tell him

junhoe: ok but i could step on u so who's the real child

soo: stfu

junhoe: ok soo love u

soo: keep me updated on jinhwan stupid

"Soomi-ah~," Jungkook is whining like a newborn child from his stance on the floor. Her eyes wander to peer at him, where he was looking at her expectantly as he calls, "come play."

The brunette stares back at the maknae like he was insane. His eyes remained pleading, however, and she could only shake her head in protest, "anio, I don't want to."

At her refusal, Taehyung had turned from where he was also playing beside Jungkook. Raising a brow, he asks, "didn't you used to play games, though, Mi?"

She purses her lips as she thinks of his question and just how she should answer it. Yes, she did used to play video games when she was younger. When she became an idol, though, she didn't have time for such a consuming hobby. Only when she met Jungkook and they started hanging out did she really start playing them again. But when the scandal broke out, she didn't play them anymore because they just reminded her of him. It'd been a while since she had touched one, as all she ever did anymore was watch Chanwoo and Bobby play them. Whenever they'd ask if she wanted to join, though, she'd hurriedly insist she was fine.

To be fair, with the fame and constant working that her job required, even if she wanted to she didn't really have the time to be devoted to them again.

Opening her mouth to speak, Jungkook had beat her to it as he turns to the other youngest of the boys to boast, "we used to play Overwatch, she was crazy good, remember?" 

Tae paused to think for a second, before his eyes lit up in remembrance. "Oh, I remember!" he cries out in excitement, before looking to Soomi again, "come play!" he urges her like Jungkook had seconds ago. All the while, she hadn't gotten to fit in a single word with how excited those two were.

Knowing they probably weren't going to give it up, she heaves out a sigh as she rises to her feet. Making her way around the coffee table, they both scoot over to make room for her. Soomi takes the seat they made for her in between them, taking the controller Taehyung hands to her.

"It's been so long," she's mumbling as they go into the character select. Seeing the familiar screens that didn't look very different gave her a sense of nostalgia, but even then, she didn't mind it as she chooses which one she wants to play.

From that moment on, your ears would be filled with the bright giggles of Kim Soomi as she would playfully mess with Jungkook while they'd play. Whether it was in game or by knocking the controller out of her hand when she was on respawn, it had him getting pretty upset as he was always a try-hard at games. She was in fits of laughter, though, along with Taehyung, who was enjoying seeing her bully the maknae. Even though he was the youngest of Bangtan, everyone knew how serious he was about games, so if you bothered him you'd probably get hit. But Jungkook could never even get slightly mad at Soomi, so she was free to do whatever she wanted and he wouldn't ever do anything to her.

"Yah, you made me die!" he's complaining when she slaps the controller out of his hand again, causing his character to get killed.

Soomi, on the other hand, had just finished practically wiping the entire team. With a satisfied look on her face, she jokingly hums, "I think you're just not very good, Kookie."

There's an offended look that takes over his face as he looks to her with wide eyes, "yah!"

Snickering at him all over again, their fun was cut short when a small boy cleared his throat from where he was sitting alone on the sofa. Nobody paid attention to him, though, and he felt bad to interrupt the fun. But Jimin knew that the call was probably important, as he recognized the name on the screen.

He hadn't been trying to look at her phone or anything. Soomi had just left it beside him and it had started ringing rapidly. 

"Mi!" he calls out, finally getting the attention of the girl. Her eyes weren't mad or anything as she only turned to him in response to hearing her name. Clearing his throat, he holds out her ringing cellphone, "you're getting a call."

Eyes widen, she chunks the controller into Tae's lap before jumping to her feet. "Thanks, Chimmie," she mumbles as she takes it, already busy walking away towards one of the bedrooms so she could talk to him in private. 

Sliding her thumb across the screen, she holds it to her ear, trying not to jump around like a crazy person because she didn't even know if he was calling for a good reason or not yet. After all, it was Koo Junhoe she was talking about. 

"June-ya," she hums cheerily into the phone, waiting for him to say something.

He didn't take long to yell the words, "Soomi, Jinan-hyung's awake, you better get down here!"

Having to hold the phone further from her ear so she didn't get a headache from how loud he was, she feels her heart flutter from the sound of his words. He's awake, her brain is screaming in joy, he's finally awake and okay. 

Already running into the bedroom she spent the night in with Jungkook, she hurriedly grabs Jinhwan's car keys before rushing into the hall. "I'm coming as fast as possible," she tells Junhoe, who only responds shortly before ending the call.

"I'm heading to the hospital, guys!" she calls to the seven boys, not waiting for any of them to say anything as she runs out the door. Soomi didn't have time for anyone to come with her, she didn't have time to even pause for a second. She had to get to the hospital as soon as she could physically manage.

Jinhwan was okay, she tells herself again and again, just making sure she didn't forget. After what Jungkook did, she was also one step closer to catching the psycho. Everything was somehow falling into place, and she could finally live her life normally. Soomi was honestly excited to just focus on her work and of course the ultimate decision she had yet to face because of all of this. 

It would all be over soon.



56:00 | finally united



People didn't hesitate to move out of the way from the sight of a wild brunette rushing down the hallway. Her bag flies every which way on her shoulder, hitting people with a whack as she zooms past them. Usually, she was never one to cause such attention to herself, but this was a serious occasion for her and the boys. She didn't want to miss a single second more of time. In her opinion, she should've been here already, even though everyone agreed she was going to spend a 'relaxation' day with her old friends. The entire relaxation part had already been demolished anyway from the fact Jungkook got the barista woman sent to the police station.

She'd practically mashed the call button on the elevator like her life depended on it, rapidly slamming her pointer finger against it until it was likely to bruise. Completely careless of her surroundings, she didn't even wait for any approaching people as she lets the doors close behind her.

When the doors open seconds later after she vigorously slams her hand against the floor button, before continuing her frantic run right to the door.

"June-ya!" She screeches in excitement at the sight of her best friend, stopping with the group of six that were all gathered out in the hall. Not knowing why they were, however, she's panting from the exercise as she lays a hand on Junhoe's shoulder, eyes raking over the friends, "where's Jinhwan? Is he okay?!"

The boys don't respond for a while. She feels a nervous pit form in the bottom of her stomach, welling into bubbles of worry that pop through out her body. It's evident on her face as she looks from them, to the closed door that her beloved Jinan was on the other side of.

Why weren't they telling her anything? They said he was awake, did something else happen? Did the drugs have some sort of affect on him, did they give him amnesia?!

The immediate worries poured through her skull like hot tea, and she holds a hand to her head in complete distress. Turning to the boys again, the pained look of concern is wiped off her face when she sees their expressions.

Her six, idiotic friends were all standing there smiling at her. Each of them had their own signature grin painted across their lips, eyes crinkling in an evident thrill as they peer at the brunette in dismay, "why the hell are you all looking at me like that?!" She cries in confusion, not understanding what was up with them. Soomi came here for news on Jinhwan and to see he was okay — but these dummies always seemed to be causing something.

After a moment, Donghyuk only giggles out that high pitched angelic giggle of his, "nothing is wrong, we just missed you Soomi!"

A blank look takes over her face. They really freaked her out for nothing, her mind informs her, and she deadpans, "what?"

Instead of something like a reply, the six all jump forward at once. There, in the middle of the hospital hallway, was poor Soomi tackled in a giant hug from the boys of iKON. Seeing them on television, one would never imagine the fact they were the equivalent of children, as they all squeezed the life out of the petite girl, who was gasping for breathe at that point.

"Yah!" She's whining, trying to hide the smile that wanted to form on her face, "you guys are hurting me, where is Jinhwan?!"

It was what she came here for and ran over a thousand people for, after all. Not knowing what was going on was nerve wrecking, as much as she did appreciate her friends cute gesture.

"I missed you guys, too, though," she adds as they all finally let her go.

The words make them grin like children again.

"Hyung is awake," Chanwoo explains, "he's just getting discharged by the doctor right now."

A wave of relief washes over her at this, he was okay. Giddily, she nods excessively, glancing at the door again as she hums, "does he know I'm here?"

"Nope," Hanbin is the first to respond, "we thought it would be a nice surprise."

Soomi only shakes her head in disbelief, but she didn't mind the idea. The sound of a creaking door is heard, causing the six boys in front of her to turn towards it. All of them were so much taller than her (as she is shorter than even the tiny Jinhwan,) so she was the equivalent of a dwarf behind them all.

The poor girl couldn't even see over any of their shoulders. Not wanting to give herself away since they said it was a surprise, she just sits there in the back and waits. The thump of her heart against her chest was the only thing she heard for a moment, anticipating the sound of his voice. It had only been a couple of days, but it felt more like a decade to her.

That was how attached to him she was.

"Hyung!" Bobby was the first to cheer, "you're not dead!"

A few snorts of humor echo through the friends from his poor choice of words. The doctor that had seen Jinhwan had bowed to the friends, before heading off the opposite way down the hall. This meant he was fine to go home as he had been checked out, finally.

Soomi feels a lump form in her throat.

"Pabo, you really have to say it like that?" His voice shot an arrow straight through her heart. She had to hold a hand to her chest to calm herself, unable to hide the giddy smile that spread over her face. Her Jinhwan, he was right there, even though she couldn't see him yet, "of course I'm okay."

"Do you remember what happened?" Yunhyeong had cautiously asked, and a nervous silence takes over the crowd of friends.

He didn't say anything for a good while. Until his hesitant tone breaks through the clouds of silence, "yeah, yeah, I remember, the barista put something in my drink after getting yelled at in the kitchen."

No one shows a reaction of surprise at this. Obviously, they already knew this, and the fact the woman had been found and taken to be questioned by the police.

But there was one thing only Jinhwan could know.

"Did you hear the person who was yelling at her's voice?" Junhoe questions in a rush, "it was a woman, right?" He used what Soomi had told him.

Since the short boy knew nothing of what had happened while he was out, he blinks in confusion as he nods, "yeah, I could tell it was a woman, but I couldn't hear it well."

It was confirmed, at least, the psycho was indeed female.

A silence overcomes the boys again, everyone seemed to be in deep contemplation about it all. Like Soomi, they just wanted it to be over, they wanted their lives to go back to normal.

And poor Soomi still had plenty of other obstacles to face.

"Where's Soo?"

Everyone's head jerks up from Jinhwan's curious words. By then, the guys had forgotten about the tiny girl hiding behind them this entire time.

Upon remembrance, they knew the best way to answer that was to just move out of the way. If they wouldn't of, she would've pushed them all over either way.

Parting like water, Soomi didn't wait to run through the path they created.

"Jinan-ah~," she coos as she rushes forward, the boys eyes widening from the fact he didn't think she was here. In a millisecond, a wide smile takes over his face, his brown eyes brighten like the sun, and he holds out his arms to accept her gracefully into the warmest embrace either of them had ever experienced.

Melting into his arms, she inhales the scent of his cologne that she missed so dearly. Just the feeling of Jinhwan's chest heaving up and down against her, the feeling of him using his might to squeeze her, sent fireworks imploding through her veins. A shot of euphoria circulates through her heart, and she felt her eyes getting glassy as she only squeezes them shut to calm the overwhelming emotion. Burying her face further into his chest, she could only mumble, "I missed you."

Hearing her small voice say this only made him hug her tighter. Jinhwan nuzzles his face into her hair, inhaling that familiar strawberry shampoo she loved to use. The sweet scent radiates through his nostrils, and he smiles, kissing her hair, "I missed you, too, Soo."

Still not opening her eyes because she knew she would cry if she did, she removes her arms from around his waist to ball his shirt in her hands. "I'm going to watch you like a hawk now, you better be more careful!" She's lecturing like a mother, which was both humorous and precious to all of them due to the fact she was the youngest there.

(the boys of iKON are a lot older in this story)

"Yah, I'm sorry," Jinhwan is chuckling as he plays with her hair with one hand, the other still wrapped around her, "I'll be more careful, Soomi-ah."

Hearing her name roll off his tongue in such a raspy way was enough to make her want to do many things with him. She couldn't help any urges she was having, she missed him with her entire being. It was no lie Soo had an undeniable attachment to both Jungkook and Jinhwan. They both had a special place inside her and she felt lost without them. The thing she just had to get to the bottom of, was which one was because of love?

She didn't know it yet. But there was a fine line between infatuation, love, and lust. It was one she had to discover on her own.

"You better," she pouts, finally getting out of the embrace so she could meet his eyes instead. Jinhwan's hands go to hers as they do so, intertwining their fingers and giving her hands a small squeeze. He only grins as she peers at him, taking one hand out of his so she could reach up and push his hair out of his eyes, it was really getting long.

Making sure it was all out of his face, her hand wanders to his cheek. Cupping it with her tiny hand, her thumb runs over his baby soft skin, and she seemed focused until she mutters, "so how are you feeling?" her hand then moves to feel his forehead, "do you feel sick?"

He only chuckles as he watched her practically inspect him. It was adorable watching Soomi do such, she looked like a worried grandmother as she intently stares at every aspect of his face.

"I feel fine, Soo," he assures her, grabbing her hand that was still roaming his face with his and intertwining their fingers again, "they discharged me, we can go home now."

Letting out a deep breathe of relief, she nods, "okay, yeah, I have your car."

He raises a brow, "oh, you've been driving my car, huh?"

Soomi grins, only nodding as they begin to walk towards the elevator. The other boys follow suit behind them, not cutting in on their conversations and instead talking to each other. For once, they were being respectful, wanting the two to have their moments together after being apart.

Swinging their hands back and forth as they walk, Jinhwan giggles, "sounds like you like my car."

"Course I do, it's expensive and luxurious," she informs as if he didn't already know what kind of car he had, "it's cool, I like to see all the things it can do — not that I press random buttons because I'm not," her voice grows small as she seemed to be rethinking what she did when she was in his car (which was press random buttons,) "I'm not stupid."

Jinhwan was giving her a look that showed he knew all too well. "You're cute, Soomi-ah," he shakes his head, "you can drive my car whenever you want, okay?"

"Oh I will, I wasn't even gonna ask."






57:00 | obscene



"Oh, Jinhwan, nice to see you're okay!" Jimin is the first to offer kindly as Soomi enters with Jinhwan to her side and the other six boys trailing behind her. They'd just come from their suite, where they'd all packed up their things and prepared to leave. After a talk on the way here in Jinhwan's car with just the two of them, they decided it would be best to head back to Seoul. Whether they liked it or not, they had the festival the day after tomorrow, meaning rehearsals were tomorrow so they couldn't stay any longer. 

The boys of Bangtan had been planning the same thing, to which Soomi had texted them to make sure. Everyone was already gathered in the living area of the suite, suitcases and other bags beside them as they were either standing or sitting on one of the sofas. 

Jinhwan sends Soomi's best friend a small grin, "thanks, I'm glad to be okay."

"It really is crazy," Jin was saying as everyone took seats to chat before they spoke about leaving their mini-trip, "I can't believe they'd do something as far as drugging you," he scoffs in disbelief, shaking his head as he leans back into the couch.

"Right," Junhoe agrees shortly after, "this psycho needs to be put in a cage."

Soomi chuckles at his words, taking a seat beside Jinhwan on the floor since there wasn't anymore space on any of the sofas. Everyone engages in casual talks, and she felt a feeling of comfort for the first time in a long time, as Jinhwan leans his head against hers. Not minding the action, her eyes are too busy wandering around the room, raking over every person in curiosity.

Nope, her brain confirms, Jungkook's not here.

Wondering where he was, she was about to ask Jimin when her phone vibrates in her pocket. Shifting out of the position her and Jinan were in, she pulls out her phone to see what it was.

kookie: i heard commotion from my room, r u guys here?

mi: yeah, we came to talk b4 every1 heads back to seoul

mi: jinhwan is okay by the way, again thank u for what u did

kookie: sure, so there's no damage from the drugs or anything?

mi: nope

kookie: that's good, mi

mi: yep it is :D

mi: so r u gonna come out of there or wht?

kookie: maybe if u come get me

mi: why, u have legs, jungkook

kookie: soomi

kookie: come here

mi: no u

kookie: i won't hesitate to come out & grab u myself, but it might rile up ur buddy

mi: ok one second

kookie: one second passed

mi: stop being impatient if u want me to come

kookie: ok mi;)

Rolling her eyes, she slowly rises to her feet, shoving her phone in her pocket. Turning to everyone as a few of them had looked up to her movement, she sends mostly Jinhwan a small smile as she informs him, "I'll be right back."

His eyes are no doubt curious, and he already seemed to have a feeling of where she was going, or more like who she was going to see. Trying to hide the anger on his face, he exhales deeply to calm himself. Jinhwan didn't have the space to be angry, really, he had to remind himself. Even though they had been through a lot, so had Soomi and Jungkook. She still loved him, and he had to get over it that they were probably very fond of each other whether he liked it or not.

Jinhwan didn't like Jungkook, not one bit. He understood the story of why he did what he did to her, but in his head, he should've handled it better. It broke her, it gave her a fucking drinking problem, and being the one who had to fix it and witness it all first hand, he would never forgive Jungkook for that. Because of this, and the fact he worried he was pulling her back to being head-over-heels for him, was the reason they would never get along.

Plus, it wasn't like Jungkook was thrilled of his existence either.

Calming himself as he watches her body disappear into a room somewhere in the back of the suite, he tries to focus on something else.

"You look distressed," a voice abruptly critiques.

His eyes jolt to the eyes of Park Jimin. The silver haired boy was staring back at him with a certain kindness he only ever saw in his eyes, but Jinan couldn't help but feel like there was a different emotion hidden within them.

Not minding Jimin talking to him, as he actually liked him and the others well, they had the same humor and were fun to be around, he shrugs, "I am, sort of." Jinhwan had never thought of it, but now that he was friends with them, couldn't he ask them about Soomi? He didn't know if Jimin would be the kind of person to go tell her, as they were best friends, but he was hoping he had the decency to keep a secret if he asked him to.

As if he looked inside his brain and read his thoughts himself, Jimin leans back against the palms of his hands as he raises a brow, "Soomi, huh?"

When he doesn't say anything, the boy seemed to realize he was right.

"Don't worry, if you think she's leaning towards Jungkook, I wouldn't sweat over it," he assures, talking low enough so no one else could hear, "she ran out of here like a crazy person when she heard you were awake, I can see that she adores you." He was glad to hear this, especially from the person that knew Soomi the best out of everyone. Jimin and Soomi were some of the closest friends anyone had ever seen in their lives. Jinhwan didn't even think it was possible for someone to know another person like the back of their hand, but Jimin could see right through Soomi and all her emotions whether she was masking them or not.

"But at the same time, a part of her is attached to Jungkook," he explains further, and there's a sense of pain in his eyes when he speaks next, "it's like how they say you never get over your first love, you know? Those two have been through thick and thin together, they have a connection formed over the last five years," he sighs deeply, breaking their eye contact to stare elsewhere, "it's like how you need a match to light a candle, their flame was brighter than anything for those years, but then they lost their match. Without a match, there's no flame, but they've seemed to have found that match again," he purses his lips in thought for a second, "because of it being relighted, I guess you could say," his eyes are sympathetic when he looks to the bewildered Jinhwan, "I honestly feel bad for you."

For such a deep lecture, he didn't think he was going to end it on that note. "Bad for me?" he repeats, "why?"

Brown eyes wandering to the ceiling, you could tell he was unsure of if he should say more. But really, if Jimin had to choose a side in this war, it wouldn't be Jungkook's.

"Take it from me, there's no breaking that bond those two have," he shakes his head, "it doesn't matter how much they bicker, fight, or get pissed at something the other does, they always found a way back to each other."

Pause.

"And Jungkook, he's not any stranger to competition. He's always been protective over Soomi like some sort of prize, and with the way he's been recently, I don't think he's afraid to get his hands dirty."



///




"Jungkook, are you in here?" her voice is confused as she knocks on the door. When there's no reply for a second, she grabs the handle, twisting it and entering the room cautiously. Why was he ignoring her? She was going to be extremely upset with his lack of response unless he had headphones on at max volume.

"Kookie, I'm-," she starts to call out again, but her calls for the boy are cut off when her back hits the door she just entered. 

In a flash, her breathe is caught in her throat. The eyes of Jungkook are daring as they peer down from his outstanding height compared to her, they're taunting her with that look in them as they rake up and down her figure. One hand is on the other side of her head, and the other raises to cup her cheek. Using the pad of his thumb, he runs it across her skin as he grins, "hi, Soomi."

Still breathless from his sudden action, she only stares up at him like he was insane. "Jungkook, what the hell was that about?"

It wasn't that she minded his fanatical move, but she was just trying to find something to say that would change the subject from what she felt his mind was on. Soomi was trying to ignore the fact their faces were only centimeters apart, and the proximity was sending her heart into a trillion different utopia's in under a second.

When she refuses to meet his eyes, the hand that was on her cheek goes to under her chin, forcing her to look at him. "Mi, I have to really tell you something important," he shares with a alluring tone.

Those eyes, they kept flickering from her own green irises to her lips. Knowing what he was thinking was driving her insane. Reminding herself all of their friends were just outside in the other room, she has to exhale deeply to contain her thoughts herself, asking as casual as she could manage, "yeah, what is it?"

Jinhwan just got back from the hospital, you don't have time for these kind of egregious things.
