But yet with all that she had to take care of, she still wanted to so bad. It didn't change the fact that Jeong Jungkook had to be one of the most irresistible people on the planet.

His breathe fans her skin, sending chills shooting through her veins as she diverts her eyes to the floor again. 

As if it couldn't get any worse, his voice is a rasp as he admits enticingly, "I've been really craving you recently."

He was doing it on purpose, this was the Jungkook she wasn't surprised was making a comeback. The thing was, Jungkook's duality was extremely upsetting. When you looked at the sweetheart he was the other night when they had that heartfelt talk, to the boy that went out at 5 AM and found the woman who drugged her other love interest, to the man seductively peering down at her right now - they had an uncanny difference that made her want to scream.

"Jungkook..." her voice trails off into the air, coming out a tiny whine of plead. She really didn't think they should do anything with their friends right outside. Even though they were all probably distracted laughing and joking, waiting for whenever everyone was ready to leave back to Seoul, Jinhwan was there. The other guy in her life was just discharged from the hospital. It was true she wasn't dating either of them, but that didn't mean she was going to have sex with Jungkook right here.

Although, it wasn't like she hadn't done it before.

"Soomi~," he coos, his hands trailing from her forearms down her body. It was as if he was drawing a line all the way until he reached her hips, where his hands grip, pulling her even closer to him until she felt him against her, "I'm sorry," he cries like a child, burying his face in his neck, "I just really miss you."

Squeezing her eyes shut, she tries to find the strength to just tell him no, Jungkook, this is a bad idea. But she was the definition of a weakling, as her head could only hit the door her back was against as it leaned back. The feeling of Jungkook's lips on her skin, that familiar feeling of his kisses, she realized she missed him, too.

Realizing it was hopeless to ever try to deny him, her arms reach forward to wrap around his neck. He'd been teasingly kissing her, short kisses that weren't as intense as she was really wanting. When her arms wrap around his neck, she uses her small amount of strength to try to pull him forward, as if urging him to go further.

She could feel Jungkook smirk against her skin, before his lips went to work. They trail every which way against her neck, kissing gently and curtly, before they grew longer and softer. Biting her lip, it only got worse when he began to suck on her skin, finding that spot he knew so well and going to utopia with it.

No, Soomi hadn't been planning to give herself to Jungkook that day, but she sure was now. In her head, as his lips roam her skin, as he picks her up by her thighs, making his way to the bed and practically throwing her on it, it was just them. The only people in the world were Kim Soomi and Jeon Jungkook, as his hands go to pull her shirt over her head, and she lets him, before his lips find not her neck, but her own. 

As soft as she remembered was Jungkook, and the two bodies merge into one, with no other thoughts on their minds other than each other.




58:00 | new years bomb



"Mi, we have to go," Jungkook is a giggly mess as he pulls at her arm again, trying to drag her out of bed. He wasn't using any amount of strength as he didn't want to send the girl flying, he was just gently tugging, calling to the exhausted girl while trying not to burst into more fits of playful laughter, "the others are probably wondering what we were doing for so long!"

Poor Soomi didn't have any energy left in her small body. She felt like it had all been taken from her, and well, it sort of had. 

"Maybe if you weren't so," she heaves out a deep breathe in between her words, "merciless."

Giving her a confused look at her voice of vocabulary, he blinks a few times before dropping her hand. Walking closer to the bed, he bends down to pick her up instead, to which she whines out like a child being taken away from it's mother.

"I'm sorry if you can't walk," he tells her, but he didn't sound sorry at all. Flicking on the light with his elbow, he sets the girl on the counter. Her eyes are unimpressed as they peer at him, and from the look, he realized she hadn't taken his apology. "Hey," he pouts, "I meant it when I said I missed you, okay?!"

His defense only received an eye roll from Soomi, who leans her head forward on his shoulder when he steps forward to stand in between her legs. Jungkook giggles again at her action, feeling genuinely a bit bad that their short session really wore her out this much. 

"Come on, Mi," he urges, his hands going to her hair as he tries to fix it. Grabbing his hair brush that was on the counter, he runs it through her hair gently, making sure that it didn't look like they just did what they actually had, "you have to wash up."

Heaving out a loud sigh, she groans as she sits up straight again on the counter before him, "I hate you," she deadpans to the boy, who only continued smiling like an idiot as he brushed her hair.

"I love you, Soomi," he didn't hesitate to blurt, finishing with her hair. Using his hand to smooth down any frizz, in the corner of his eyes he could see the blush that took over her cheeks. It'd only make him grin wider as he admires his work on her hair.

Shaking her head in disbelief, she turns to see herself in the mirror. "Thanks," she mutters as she sees he had successfully fixed it, before turning back to him, "you know, even though it's still your fault it was like that in the first place."

Scoffing, he raises a brow in retort, "but you didn't say no, did you? If anything, you seemed pretty consensual when you were begging for mo-," his teasing was cut off when he raises a hand over his mouth.

"I get it, just stop talking."

He didn't say anything when she removes her hand from his mouth, which she was very thankful for. Sighing, she pushes him back a bit so she could jump down from the counter.

Making her way out of the bathroom and back into the bedroom, Soomi heads straight for the door without another word. She didn't want to take anymore time and keep her friends waiting longer. God knew they were probably already wondering what the hell her and Jungkook had been doing, she just hoped they weren't assuming what it actually was.

Jungkook had been throwing his bag over his shoulder that contained his stuff, before rushing to Soomi just as she was opening the door. At the last second, he reaches out to grab her hand. From the sudden warmth, she sent him yet another look, but he only returned it with that bunny tooth smile.

She sighs in bewilderment for the tenth time that day, not caring as they walk out of the room. 

The friends had been having a conversation neither of them were aware of when they enter. Soomi had a very evident pain in between her legs, making it hard to act like all they were doing in there was talking.

Good thing she was experienced with this.

"It was actually Jungkook that found the woman," both of the two's eyes bulged from hearing Jin casually say this aloud to everyone, including Jinhwan. Soomi hadn't exactly shared that detail when she explained it to him on the way to the hotel. From Jinhwan's shocked face, the eldest nods to him to show he was serious, "he had her sent to the police station for questioning, said if she admitted anything about the psycho to give Soomi a call."

Soomi and Jungkook stop before everyone. Their sudden presence caused all the heads of their friends to jerk to them. A pit of worry forms in the bottom of her stomach when she sees Jinhwan peering at Jungkook. There was no telling what was going through his mind upon hearing the guy he hated did something like that for him.

Not knowing what he should do it this situation, he sends a small, close lipped grin to the confused boy that was staring him down, "uh, it was nothing, I don't think she even ended up admitting anything."

"It was still a nice thing to do for hyung, though!" Donghyuk pipes up.

Everyone was looking to Jinhwan, waiting for him to say anything. He opens his mouth a few times before closing it, not seeming to be able to find the words he wanted to say. After another moment, he clears his throat, looking back to Jungkook, "thanks for that, either way," he finally offers, but his voice didn't sound too sincere.

To be fair, neither was the maknae's, "sure."

Soomi held in the urge to wince, talk about awkward.

Jimin and some of the others seemed to feel the awkward tension in the air. Her best friend sends her a look that seemed to say this is scary, and she only nodded at him with wide eyes. Mouthing the words, I want to die to him, he only shakes his head with a silent chuckle.

A few moments of quiet pass, before Namjoon had rose to his feet from where he sat on one of the sofas. Clapping his hands together, he clears his throat, trying to fix the atmosphere in the room, "alright, so everyone ready to head back to Seoul?"

On queue, the friends all rise to their feet. Soomi was still standing next to Jungkook, who wrapped an arm around her shoulders as Taehyung had cheered, "yay, we're going home!"

"Thank God, I really miss my bed," Chanwoo is sighing out dramatically as they all make their way to the exit.

"Oh, me too, dude," Yoongi cuts in.

"Sucks we won't have time to really rest, though, we have to go practice," Hoseok is pouting as they pile into the hallway.

Groans echo through the group as they recall the festival. Everyone liked performing, and especially seeing fans, but they couldn't change how tired they were after all this. To think they were still going home empty handed, other than the fact they knew the psycho's gender, was rather disappointing.

"Fuck that," Bobby snorts, "I'm going right to sleep."

"You know where I'm going?" Junhoe speaks up, "to fucking In n Out."

Soomi's eyes widen at the sound of the place her and Junhoe loved to go eat at. They tended to go their pretty often in their off time, but since they rarely ever had any, it was rare. 

When he said this, he looked back at her from where she was walking next to Jungkook. "Yes, that is fucking genius!" she points a knowing finger at her other best friend, who only returns the gesture. The others were looking at them in confusion, while the boys of iKON sigh in embarrassment.

"Let's order double what we usually do," he says to her, and she only nods at the idea.

"Sure, but you're paying."

"Yah!" Junhoe cries, "why the fuck do I have to pay?"

Laughing at him, she runs up from behind him, causing Jungkook's arm that had been around her to fall to his side. He only watched her blankly as she'd ran up to Junhoe, jumping on his back and wrapping her arms around him. The boy was so tall, it'd been quite the jump, and he only stumbled a bit as he whines from her action, but nonetheless grabbed onto her thighs and carried her.

"Cause it'd be rude not to," she hums, "just like it'd be rude if you dropped me right now."

"Don't tempt me," he complains, "ugh, you're so heavy!"

The two best friends began to bicker and fight like siblings. It was a funny sight to everyone, especially her old friends that had only learned on this trip how close they really were. Of course, it wasn't the extent of Soomi and Jimin's friendship, because that was impossible. No, Soomi and Junhoe had a different friendship that was cute to see.

"I'm not fucking fat, you lumpy sack of balls!"

"Excuse me, what did you just call me?!" he's in fits of laughing so hard he nearly falls over while carrying her, "that's fucked up!"

"You're the one who told me to go on a diet, and you're saying being called a lumpy sack of balls is fucked up?!" Soomi shouts back. Nobody knew why they were yelling, and they felt bad for anyone else that was in the hotel having to hear it.

The sound of everyone's phone going off at the same time caused them to all look at their phones in confusion. They'd dung in unison, not once - but twice.

Soomi had pulled out her phone along with the rest of the friends. Holding it out in front of Junhoe since it was the only way she could see it clearly, he looks down at it with her, wondering what in the world it could be that all of them received such.

"It's an email," Jimin is the first one to mumble.

"Wait but there's a text, too."

Soomi slides her thumb across the screen to peer at the email. 


Kim Soomi, we'd be honored to see you at
KCON NY 2019!


January 5, 2019
Madison Square Garden
Please join us for a happy New Years greeting at KCON!


Soomi held in the urge to faint, although it pended being pretty hard. New York, she hadn't been there since those years ago with Jungkook for the other KCON she went to. 

Was she really going to go? She guessed she wouldn't have much of a choice, the company would definitely want her to attend.

"Guys," Yunhyeong's voice breaks through everyone's thoughts, "look at the text."

She'd forgotten Jimin had said there was some sort of text. Remembering this, she slides her finger against the top of the screen to see the message in the new group they were apparently all in.

Unknown Number:
KCON sounds fun, see you all there


Unknown Number:
Especially you, Soomi, hope you're ready for a real New Years bomb:)



59:00 | back then




Soomi couldn't stop staring at the screen. Having had asked Junhoe to put her down, she holds a hand to her head as she barely walks anymore. The others don't notice her actions as they're busy talking about what was happening and if they wanted to go to KCON, conspiring about the text they got and most of all, how much they hated this person.

Well, three people did notice Soomi staying behind and staring down at her phone screen. She was practically in the middle of the parking lot herself. Jungkook, Jimin, and Jinhwan all gaze back at her. The same idea was all in their heads, that they should go check on her.

But the first one who actually acted on his actions was Jungkook, being the closest to her because he was walking at the back of the crowd.

"Mi," he offers her name hesitantly, his feet crunching against the concrete of the lot as he stops before her, "are you - are you okay?" He knew it was probably stupid for him to ask, hence the reason he paused.

Of course she wasn't okay, Jungkook's brain snaps at him a second later. She was just invited back to New York, her home town. The last time they were there, they got engaged and she went to see her family's house, but was too afraid to go see them herself. Jungkook knew the most compared to everyone else about her past, he knew she had a lot of trouble with her parents because she did a lot of silly things and it was what drove her to just leave. He just didn't know the exact details, but it was still more than she ever told anyone else.

With the thought of going back, her family was surely on her mind.

A shaky breath leaves her lips as she finally managed to look up from her phone, "I-I don't know what to do," only then did he remember the text message as well, "They found everything, they're going to tell the world." Soomi started rambling before she could stop herself, assuming the worst instantly. How could she not? This person had proved they were no joke after putting Jinhwan in a fucking coma. She had no doubt they found everything about her dark past, it wasn't as hard as it seemed.

If you dug deep enough using her American name, you would come across it eventually. Although her parents made sure to bury every article, everything written about her, to the depths of hell, that didn't mean it didn't exist somewhere on the Internet or in the world.

Even though they hadn't said they found it all, what else could it be? They said they didn't care for her relationships and that they were truly going to dig up her past, after all. It had to be about that.

Everything she came here to do, everything she had finally built for herself, was going to crumble right before her. Her hands run through her hair, grabbing the brown locks as she inhaled and exhales repeatedly, "oh my God, I'm fucked, this is the end," she starts saying the words over and over, forgetting that a worried Jungkook was still standing before her.

"Soomi," he says sternly when her breathing starts getting heavier and her eyes get teary, "calm down, calm down, it's okay."

When she doesn't respond and only continues talking to herself in a panic, he steps forward, wrapping his arms around her without another word. The shock of his body warmth colliding with her own snapped her out of her trance, and only then did she realize what she had been doing.

Before she can speak, though, he does first, "Soomi, you never told me the full story," he starts slowly, "what exactly are they going to tell the world?"


///



"Did you get it?!" Chaerin is screeching in excitement, her heels clicking against the wooden floors as she stops before her older sibling with wide eyes. The green of her irises shine even in the dark room they were in, and Soomi has to lean forward so she could actually hear her with the booming of the stereo.

"You fucking bet I did," she triumphantly shares, opening her palms to show the pills. The sight of how many she had was a blessing to the girls eyes before her, and eagerly, she reached out and tried to take one. Much to her dismay, Soomi closed her palm with a pout from what she just did, "calm yourself, I didn't say you could have any yet."

Spinning on her six inch heel, she turns to walk away. Chaerin had only gawked at her eldest sister's back, about to run after her when someone taps her shoulder.

"Did she give you them?" Juyeon is asking, just as hungry sounding as she was seconds ago — and still was.

The girl only looks to him with a frown, shaking her head. "I don't know what's up with her, she just walked away from me!" She's complaining like the baby she was.

Furrowing his brows from her words, another party walks up to them. There's a drink in his hand, and he's jigging around slightly to the music blasting in the house as overlooks the two he knows all too well. Obviously being much older, he towered over them with his outstanding height, his slick hair styled perfectly and his identical green eyes wondrous as he looks to them, "what's up with you two?" He raises a brow from the defeated looks on their faces.

Not waiting longer than a second, Chaerin steps forward to grab her brothers arm before he could run away with some girl, "can you go tell Solar to give us some?! She said she would and then she just did that!" The complaining of the youngest never ended, and it only bothered her two brothers as they exchanged glances.

"Maybe she went to get more," Juyeon tries to cheer her up, but the girl just scoffs, crossing her arms over her chest.

Shrugging because his attempt failed, he turns to his brother with eyes that ask 'what do we do?' 

"I'll go find her," the tallest boy sighs out, spinning on his heel and walking away towards the direction the other sister went. The two siblings only watch as he disappears, and they groan when a crowd of girls stops him from going any further.

Her fist balling at her sides from her craving and frustration, she resists the urge to throw her heel at the girls flirting with her brother. "I swear if those stupid whores don't—"

"— Chae, he's going now, calm down," Juyeon places a hand on her shoulder. She only looks to him with a dull look, but listens nonetheless when she sees her older brother say goodbye to the girls before heading off further.

The party was at full blast as one would expect from high school students, especially high school students from the upper East side. A party was no joke in high class New York, if you threw one, it had to be thrown right.

Such a large mansion was full of many upper class teenagers, all coming from the well known families in the area. Everyone knew they did things like this despite their strict parents, but it wasn't a big deal — not unless they got caught.

That's why you followed one simple rule, don't get caught.

One of the most popular students of the school walks through the crowds, his drink still in his hand and his body still dancing to the music every so often. Girls would melt at just the sight of him, begging for him to say a mere word to them as he passed. Not having the time since he was always having to deal with the family issues, he waves at them with that glorious smile of his, before continuing on.

He knew his sister well enough to have a feeling of where she was. Stepping out of the main room, he had made his way up the stairs and into the hall without a second thought.

Upon reaching the door, the tall boy doesn't bother to knock. He just twists the handle and waltz right in like he was in his own house.

Why should he knock? He already knew what he was going to see anyway.

"Of course," he nonchalantly calls into the hot atmosphere, "so, is this for drugs or are you two actually into each other?"

The two hadn't stopped eating each other's faces even when they heard the door open. It wasn't as if they would care if someone saw, most people already knew anyway, right?

But at the sound of the familiar voice, the brunette is the first to pull away. The boy before her, however, not seeming to get the message so clearly, instead went to her neck. His lips began to trail kisses up to her jawline as she only blankly looked to the boy watching them whilst sipping his drink.

"Is that really your business?" She deadpans, her hands on the devious boys chest as she tried to get him to calm down with his actions, "what do you want?"

Sighing, he grew tired of seeing the guy doing that to his sister. Frankly, he didn't care who he was or how 'scary' people deemed him to be. Walking behind him as he was sucking on her neck, he balls up the back of his black tee, throwing his backwards and off of her.

Soomi's eyes widen a bit when she sees such, but the boy wasn't too phased as he groans in annoyance, not having been thrown much at all.

"You know what I want?" He raises a brow, pointing a finger knowingly at her, "I want Chaerin to stop complaining for more than one fucking second—"

"I was just coming to get more," the brunette explains her doings, climbing off the bed and grabbing her shirt. Throwing it over her head, she walks over to the boy she was just making out with, "well, how 'bout it?" She gives him a cutesy grin of hope.

A sigh leaves his lips, and he digs in his pocket for something. The sight of the new found bag of drugs was a blessing to their eyes, and Soomi immediately took it when he offered it out to her.

"Nice doing business with you," she offers a small wave before turning her back. The second her back is to him, a look of pure disgust takes over her face.

With that, the girl sashays her way out of the room and into the hall. Her brother follows suit, beside her in a second with his long legs.

"Does Sunwoo really not care that you do this sort of shit?"

She glanced up to him with surprised eyes, not expecting the question, "what, drugs?"

"No, who the fuck — I mean make out with random dealers just to get them free."

A wave of realization washes over her face, and she shrugs as she turns back to where they were walking. "I don't know, I haven't really talked to him about how I get them exactly—" she's cut off when three particular bodies appear in the hall they were walking down, causing the two siblings to stop in their tracks.

From the sight of their youngest siblings, Soomi had a good feeling of what they wanted. She knew Chaerin probably couldn't handle her craving any longer, not even realizing she Soomi was just getting more. The girl had a serious problem, worse than any of them.

But the look on not only her, but the other two's faces, had her confused. They looked concerned as they rush up to them. No one spoke a word yet as Sunwoo had grabbed her hand, already dragging her down the hall.

"What's going on?!" She's calling to him, when the sound of breaking glass and screaming is heard from down stairs, the previous booming music gone.

"Someone started a fire, the police are here, they're trying to take everyone here to the station," her boyfriend informs her, and her mouth fell open from the shock as they all ran down the hallway of the mansion like lunatics.

Rushing down the stairs, the sight of cops chasing teenagers and fire fighters trying to put out the wilding fire would've been horrifying to any normal person. But you were talking about a complete oddball of people, and they only shook their heads as they saw the commotion.

What they were scared of, however, was getting caught by the cops. It wasn't the fact they were at a party, it was the fact they had illegal drugs on them.

"We can go out the back to the side," Jaehyun tells them, and everyone follows his lead as he takes the front.

Just when the teenagers are all going to their individual cars, Jaehyun having his own and the two youngest wanting to go with him, yells are heard from the distance behind them.

"Hey, you five, wait — police!"

"Oh fuck," Soomi is cursing as she hurriedly gets in the passengers side of her BMW, Sunwoo in the drivers, "go, go, GO!"

He ignores her yelling as the cops continue yelling for them to stop, jamming the key in the ignition. The engine roars to life, and he slams his foot against the pedal.

In an instant, they were out of there in her sports car that she was thankful went so fast. Jaehyun was probably way ahead of them by now, and the thought they managed to get out of yet another was radiating.

"Fuck," she sighs as she digs into the bag, feeling like it was a great time. Leaning her head back, she dumps the pill into her mouth before dryly swallowing it, her head hitting the leather seat with a thump as she breathes, "this is the life."


60:00 | a slice of life


// be prepared for a lil SLICE of Soomi's teenage years ps yes her american name is solar smd //



"Sol," a voice cracks through her panicked thoughts, her heart bound to burst right out of her chest, "Sol, are you coming?"

Her hands began to tremble, and she resists the urge to puke—or at least, she thought she did.

"Are you okay?" A knock is heard from the door that was keeping her secret, "are you sick?"

A sigh leaves her lips as she holds a hand to her head, finally having emptied whatever was upsetting her insides. Hesitantly, she weakly rises to her feet, glancing down at what she held in her hands.

How could she bear this? There was only one good way to go about it, it was telling Sunwoo. He was the sweetest guy she'd ever known, he would be there for her, right?

She takes a few steps towards the door as more knocks are heard, and his concerned voice calls out again, "Solar, stop ignoring me, I'm worried about you. I'll call everyone and tell them we won't be there tonight—"

"No!" She accidentally yells louder than she meant to, probably startling the boy on the other side since she had been so quiet. Quieting down, its barely audible as she mumbles, "I'm okay, I was just fixing my makeup and I think I ate something bad."

Lying through her teeth, something she got good thanks to how she's grown up.

"Oh, alright," she could sense the uncertainty in his tone, but Sunwoo knew not to question her further.

Going back to the toilet, her eyes wander down to the test she held one last time. She didn't think it was likely anyway, her menstrual period was probably just changing it up for fun.

With this silly thought in mind, she carelessly chunks it into the toilet before even knowing the answer it held.

Sunwoo was waiting on her bed when she opens the door, immediately rising to his feet at the sight of her. "Are you really okay?" He's asking again, grabbing her arm as she stopped a few feet away and pulling her towards him.

Her head hits his chest, and she closes her eyes to take in the moment, nuzzling her nose into his hoodie as she inhales his scent.

"Yeah," she says after a second, gathering her strength, "I'm good, let's go party."




///





"Jaehyun—every single time—" the girl is saying in bewilderment, but not really annoyance. It wasn't like she wanted to be making out with these people anyway, nevertheless for it to get any further. Actually sleeping with guys like this could likely get her an STD, and that was one of the last things she needed in her life.

She didn't look over to see her brother, who she presumed it was. It was just so common that Jaehyun was the one who would find and save her from these situations before they got out of hand, she knew it had to be him.

Waiting for the boy that was still attacking her neck like he was a vampire to get thrown off across the room, she looks up in confusion when it doesn't happen.

"Sunwoo—"

Her heart falls out of her chest, becoming a broken mess on the floor. This was the last person she needed to know about her antics.

But he's not looking at her. His hand goes to grab the back of the guys shirt, and he gave no mercy when he threw him back. A groan leaves the dude's lips as he hits a piece of furniture from the strong impact, knocking over some glass and sending it shattering on the floor. He sinks to the ground, holding a hand to his head that he hit as he curses, "what the fuck, man?!"

Sunwoo didn't seem to have any intention of speaking. Stepping forward to the guy again, he towers over him as his shoes crack under the glass.

Picking him up by the shirt collar, Soomi winces when the guy doesn't even have any time to react before a crack echoed through the air — then another, then another. There was blood on Sunwoo's knuckle, and she sighed as she rose to her feet and her boyfriend dropped the guy to the ground, his body looking lifeless.

She wasn't sure what to do. Never had she spoken to him about how she was getting the drugs, he probably figured she was using money since she was rich, right?

"Jeez," Sunwoo mutters as he turns back to her, running a hand through his hair, "that was gross, I should've come sooner," he blinks as he sees her disdain expression, "are you okay? Did he hurt you?"

Was he high?

"Are you — not mad?"

He peers down at her for a second, no emotion in his irises. "Huh, I knew about this for a while now," he shrugs, and Soomi has to hold the side of the bed post so she doesn't faint, "Jaehyun told me, he explained it and everything."

She wasn't sure if she should be thankful for her older brother or not.

Unable to even sort her thoughts, she’s abruptly pulled into her boyfriends chest. His fingers rake across her jawline, trailing to her lips where he runs the pad of his thumb over them, biting his own from the soft feeling.

"Plus, I know you're mine, and so does every other person at this party."

Maybe hearing this have been scary to some people, but hearing it as Soomi was joyous. Her heart imploded with thrill, and she steps forward to cup his cheek, bringing their lips together in an instant fashion.

Sunwoo kisses her back hungrily, as if he hadn't eaten in days even though they were such an active couple. His lips mold into hers, the soft feeling giving her tingles as she tries to pull him closer as if it was physically possible.

"Sunwoo," she breathes as they pull away, "I don't know what I'd do without you."

He only grins childishly, sticking his hand in his back pocket. A second later, he holds out a familiar bag containing pills she recognized far too well.

"And you'll never have to, Sol."



///





"Stop seeing him!" Her father's fist slams against the granite counter top so hard that one would've thought it cracked (if it wasn't worth millions of dollars, that is.) his eyes were red from anger at that point, his fists balled so hard they were shaking from the flaming feeling within him. "He and his family are good for nothing, traitor, disgusting pieces of absolute shit and I will not have one of my own children associated with any of them!" He booms, his eyes burning holes into his daughter.

She swallows a lump in her throat, not knowing what she should do.

"Father, I — really like him," she starts out, not having the guts to raise her voice and yell in response to being yelled at this time. She had never seen her father so angry before in her life, "he is—"

"NO!" she's cut off by his scream and the sound of him throwing a dish across the room. The glass shatters against the wall, having had nearly hit her as she moves to the side to avoid it.

"What the hell is going on—father, what are you doing?!" Jaehyun cries when he sees the broken glass and the angered parent peering at his sibling. Stepping in front of the dull Soomi, who was only staring at her feet, he blinks at his father in confusion, "what did she do?!"

"She won't stop seeing that no good fucking kid!" He yells in frustration, "All that kid and his family has ever done is cause trouble, they nearly stole my fucking business!" He outrages, repeatedly breaking more things and going on a complete massacre in the kitchen.

Curious of what all the commotion was, Chaerin and an older woman make their way downstairs in a rush. Soomi was still standing there in a trance as Jaehyun went to his father, trying to stop him from breaking more things.

"Hey, hey, everyone calm down!" Her mother's soothing voice takes over the kitchen, causing everyone to stop what they were doing, "this isn't how you solve problems!"

Soomi looked up to her mother, she was her only hope in all of this. They had always been close, she wouldn't ever—

"— you have to stop seeing him, Solar, your father is right. What they did to us cannot he forgiven."

"Mother..." her voice trails off, and she heaves out a sigh, burying her face in her hands as she sits on the sofa. Chaerin, who was sitting beside her, wraps an arm around her older sister, pulling her in for a hug.

She felt bad this was happening to her. But what could she do to help? There was no budging their parents.

"Can't you see she likes him a lot?" She tries anyway, "why don't you give him a cha—"

"No," their father snaps instantly, "I will not lower myself."

Unable to hear anymore and knowing nothing would make it better, Soomi rises to her feet. The three only watch her leave towards the large stairs to her room, until Chaerin tries to get up to follow her.

"Sweetie, give her some time," her mother stops her, putting a hand on her knee.

Looking from her mothers green eyes to her destroyed sister that disappeared up the steps, the girl frowns. Nonetheless, she knew she had to listen.

Sorry, Soomi.

"Are you really going to do it?"

"No, I can't," she sniffles around ten minutes later into her phone, snuggling her face into one of the pillows on her queen sized bed, "I don't want to."

An exhale is heard from her best friend, "I know, but your parents, Sol," he reminds her, and she only groans.

"I don't care about them anymore, Hee," she blurts, "nothing is ever good enough for them anyway."



///



"Babe, babe!"

People move out of the way frantically when they see the boy running after her. Students stopped and watched, either gushing or staring in complete disgust at the most popular couple in the school.

The brunette turns, her green eyes lightening when they see him, but after a second they grow sullen. It was as if she suddenly remembered something, something she didn't want him to know.

"Hey," Sunwoo hums as he beams down at her, wrapping an arm around her shoulder and walking with her towards the exit, "where are you going without me, hm?"

Pursing her lips, she thinks fast.

"I didn't want to worry you..." she starts slowly, worried about where such a conversation could really go.

He raises a brow at her, rubbing her forearm for comfort as he pouts, "well, telling me that already did, so you have nothing to lose now."

A deep sigh leaves her lips, and she didn't look at him as she mumbles, "I'm going to the doctor."

Confusion spreads over his face, "huh?"

Knowing he was about to start with his twenty questions, Soomi rushes to speak before he could, "I'm okay, just felt kind of sick recently so I wanted to make sure I'm not coming up with anything!"

She watches his face go from freaked out back to casual, and a breath of relief leaves her lips. "Oh, well I'll drive you then," he cheerily states, not giving her an option as he was already taking her BMW keys from her back pocket.

Soomi only rolls her eyes at him, trying her best to act like everything was okay — even when it was the complete opposite.

"I'm — I'm sorry, Miss Kim," the nurse is hesitant to speak as she holds the clipboard in her hand tighter, "but you've had a miscarriage."

The teenage girl on the hospital bed could only gawk as she sat up. No tears welled in her eyes, and no emotion was shown on her face other than shock. She didn't expect this, but at the same time, she probably should've. Someone who drank and did as many drugs as she did, it was likely to happen.

It wasn't like she even wanted the baby. Not because she didn't like her boyfriend, not because she didn't want a kid in general, but because she couldn't have one if she wanted to. Her parents would disown her for being too young, and they wouldn't be completely wrong for that. She wasn't even done with high school, nonetheless prepared to be a mother.

Not to mention if they ever knew who the father was.

"Uh, miss Kim?" The nurse clears her throat awkwardly, confused by her lack of reaction, "I'm sorry, did you hear me?"

Finding her words, she clears her throat as she throws her legs over the bed. Wanting nothing more than to go home, or maybe even to Sunwoo's, she just wanted a fix to forget about what was happening right now. When she didn't know how she should or wanted to feel about something, the best way to solve it was — by forgetting about it.

"Yeah," she shrugs as she goes to get her clothes so she could get out of the horrid hospital gown, "can I leave now?"



61:00 | what it's like to miss someone


I L S A N - R I 





"I'm—um—," the girl struggles to find her words, it seemed as if her entire body was about to shut down. It was like someone threw an eight hundred ton truck at her face, and she holds a hand to her head, stumbling a bit and crashing into Jungkook's chest.


"Mi," He's shocked from what was happening, wrapping his arms around her securely so she didn't pass out or something, "hey, calm down, forget I asked, alright?"


She didn't know what it was. Just thinking about it sent her mind into a panic, like she would suddenly relive it all, and it broke her. She hated to think about it, to think about how she lived, what she went through.


Squeezing her eyes shut, she holds in her emotion and only balls her ex fiancée's shirt in her fists, trying to calm herself. Jungkook, who had no idea of what was going through her mind, only rubs her back as he tries to ease her mind.


"It's okay, Mi, stop thinking about it," he tells her again and again.


Finally managing to calm her emotions, footsteps are heard from behind the two. Only then did she look up. The familiar boy peers at them with worried eyes, biting his lip to suppress showing any other emotion on his face when he sees the two embracing in such a way.


"Soomi," Jinhwan calls, "what's going on, is everything okay?"


Stepping away from Jungkook, she looked to see all of their other friends waiting by their designated cars for them to hurry up. It sunk back into her mind where she was and what was even going on as she clears her throat, slowly walking towards Jinan.


"Yeah," she nods after a moment, "yeah, I'm fine."


He nods, and the two walk together towards his car. Soomi looks back to Jungkook, who was just now moving towards his own friends.


"I'll text you, Kookie!"


It was quiet between Soomi and Jinhwan as they walk up to his car. There was still so much going through her mind, so much she couldn't sort. If they really found out everything about her past, she would never be accepted in Korea. The things she did were normal in American standards, especially in a place like the upper East side. Although they'd still put you in jail, the entire country wouldn't look down upon you.


But in South Korea, there was a massive difference. If this was revealed, she'd be hated, she might even get involved with authorities.


How would she ever begin to tell Jungkook - or anyone - that?! Her ex fiance would be the best place to start, since he was the only one who knew the most of the story, or Jimin. She realized her best friend would be the safest place to start, never would Jimin judge her no matter what came out of her mouth. 


"Soo," the caramel haired boy awkwardly clears his throat from beside her just as they reach her car, "hey, if you want to talk to me about anything, feel free, okay?"


Looking up to his soft eyes, she forces a smile, "of course, Jinhwan-ah."


Soomi didn't know how she could ever tell Jinhwan despite the words that just came out of her mouth. While he was probably more understanding than Jungkook by a land slide, it didn't change the worry she felt about what would go through his mind if he knew. He too knew the story of how she left New York due to hardships, but he never learned what she meant when she said she did something that blew up in her parents faces.


Whatever he thought it was, though, was probably nowhere near what it actually was. Jinhwan saw Soomi as some sort of angel who came from a hard family. She feared if he knew what she was really like back then, he'd never look at her the same. Maybe she knew that Jungkook or Jimin wouldn't do this because she's known them so long, they've seen a lot of things together already. But it'd only been a year for her and Jinhwan having had met.


It wasn't that she thought he was that kind of person, but it was a deep secret she was horrified to share to anyone. How could she be so sure?


Although, Jinhwan did witness her drinking problem first hand. 


But a drug problem was deeper than that, not to mention all the other unthinkable things she did. Thinking of it again makes her head ache as she climbs into his car, not waiting to slam the door closed and lean her head against the cold window, hoping it would soothe her mind.


"Here's to an hour drive back home," Jinhwan's muttering, dreading the boring drive that was about to take place after he'd made sure all their luggage was in the trunk. 


Soomi peers out the window, seeing their friends all climbing into their own vehicles and getting situated for the drive. She wondered when they would all meet up again.


"Hey fuckers, guess who!" both Jinhwan and Soomi jump in surprise from the sudden scream, the car door swinging open.


A massive, goofy smile is on Junhoe's face as he goes in the middle seat at the back, leaning forward on the car rest, "I hope you guys don't mind me joining, I didn't want to ride with Yunhyeong and Chanwoo, they've been arguing for the last hour."


The brunette didn't really mind the presence of her second best friend. If anything, she was happy to have some more company since she wasn't feeling mentally well right now. Someone like Junhoe always knew how to lighten the mood, after all, even if he did get annoying sometimes.


Junhoe looks from Soomi's grinning face, to his hyung, who was doing the complete opposite. At the sight of his dull look, he reaches forward to slap his shoulder, "cheer up, hyung, I'm just here to make poor Soomi happy," he informs, looking back to the girl. She figured he must've saw the whole scene with her and Jungkook, and pieced together the fact of KCON and the message they all received. 


"Thanks, June-ya," she offers a kind smile, "it's not a big deal, right, Jinan?"


Exhaling deeply, he's looks back to the front of the car as he sighs, "sure, it's fine."


"Oh!" the giant boy suddenly pipes up as if he just remembered something. Turning towards the open door, he reaches out and looked to be grabbing something or someone, "and I brought Jimin!"


The sound of her best friend's name only makes her sit up in her seat in pure excitement. A embarrassed looking Jimin climbed into the seat next to Junhoe, "I was worried about you, Mi," he tells the girl with concern, before looking to the boy in the drivers seat apologetically, "also, I'm sorry for intruding, Jinhwan, Junhoe kind of dragged me here."


Not minding Jimin, he only waves a hand in dismissal, "don't worry about it."


With that, Jimin closes the door after getting Jinhwan's permission to ride with them. The boy in the driver's seat shoves the key in the ignition, and it roars to life before he backs out of the lot. 


"Soo, put on some music, I'm trying to get funky," Junhoe was telling her, tapping her shoulder rapidly. 


She looks back to her friend, before turning towards the radio. Figuring it was no big deal, she reaches for the aux cord, plugging it into her phone. Just as she turns it on and logs onto the Spotify app, mumbling, "I don't know what to put on, though," the phone had been ripped out of her hand.


Glaring at Junhoe, there was just a devious grin on his face as he holds it only a centimeter away, tapping against the screen again and again. "I've got a good one to start off this ride."


Soomi nor Jinhwan liked the sound of his voice. It was clear it was gonna be something that would bother at least one person here.


"What is i-"


The familiar bass line cuts Jimin off.


Hey, what's your dream?

I wanna big house, big cars, and big rings.


"Oh you're fucking kidding me."










///










N E W    Y O R K










"Chaerin!" the yells of her impatient mother breaks through her ears, and she heaves out a sigh as she looks up from her tea that she'd just made. Completely not in the mood for the likes of her mother, she never was anymore, she only looks up at the elegant woman as she enters the luxurious kitchen. At the sight of her daughter, she doesn't wait to demand, "go get your sister, I need to talk to her about her dropping grades."


The brunette only continues to stare the middle aged woman like she was insane. Sipping from the cup in her hand, she turns on her phone with the other, using one hand to reply to a message and acting like her mother wasn't standing before her with an annoyed expression.


Of course, Chaerin knew in the end, you couldn't ignore someone like Solji Kim no matter how hard you tried. She wished she could just go invisible or something so her mother couldn't see her. All she ever did was ask her to do things for her.


"Chaerin, are you deaf?" her mother snaps when she had yet to move from her spot in the kitchen, just trying to peacefully enjoy her tea. Before she could process anything, the woman was waving her out of the kitchen in a hurry, "go, I don't have time to wait for you!"


"Yes mother," she monotones, before spinning on her heel and heading into the living room. Once she enters it and her mother is out of range to hear her, she mutters under her breathe, "bitch."


It was no joke Chaerin resented her mother. She knew it wasn't necessarily her mother's fault, if anything, it was her father's, even - but really, it was hers. Chaerin was in fault probably more than her parents, and that was why she couldn't help but not only be angry at them, but herself.


This was why Chaerin Kim never seemed the same anymore. People had noticed it that year when she went back to school, the year one of the magnificent Kim siblings disappeared. Everyone knew why, everyone saw the articles in the papers, on the news. Although they were only out for a mere ten minutes as her parents had them immediately taken down before too many saw it. At school, everyone found out either way, and she had to constantly deal with the questions.


It killed her inside. By the time she graduated, and was now attending college, people still seemed to know of her family there. She guessed it was what she got for what she did. This would follow her around forever.


Exhaling deeply, she pushes it all to the back of her mind as she reaches the top of the steps. Her feet pad across the carpet down the hall until she reaches the door to the room. It was an empty room that no one occupied when she was growing up, her and her siblings used to mess around in it for fun since it was empty.


Not anymore, though. Knocking on the door, she clears her throat as she gently calls, "Yein-ah, mother needs to talk to you."


She waited a few seconds for the door to open, for even a reply. Even if it was a flat out no, she expected her to say something.


However, Chaerin was only met with pure silence. Confused and not having the time for things like this, she twists the handle and enters the room herself. 


"Yein, did you not hear me-" she cuts herself off when she sees the child. Her little sister is laying on her stomach, headphones on her ears, and peering down at something on her tablet screen. A sigh leaves her lips as she wondered what in the world an eleven year old girl could be so interested in, seeing it wasn't any kind of cartoon like she would've expected.


The girl was so invested in her video that she didn't know her sister was in the room. Walking deeper across the maroon carpet, she tries to squint to see what was on the girls screen. It looked to be some sort of music group, something she definitely never took an interest in.


As the music blasts from her headphones, she wondered how Yein wasn't deaf from having it so loud. Was she short of hearing?


On a dazzling and sunny day,

On a romantic, starry night

Wanna be with you

Only you,

Only you~


Listening in, the lyrics sounded as if they were in Korean. She didn't think her little sister even spoke it that well, so she was surprised to hear her listening to that sort of music. But it seemed that Korean pop was getting pretty big these days.


Not caring to hear anymore, she steps forward to fall onto her little sister's bed. The little girl jumps from the impact of the mattress going down, causes her to turn off her video, taking off one side of her headphones as she peers up at her sister.


Glad she finally got her attention, Chaerin sighs as she repeats for the tenth time, "mother needs to see you downstairs."


At the sound of this, a frown takes over the girl's face. She didn't look like she wanted to go talk to their mother at all, and her older sister sure didn't blame her.


"Just go, it's better to get it over with," she assures the younger one, patting her back.


Yein groans as she sits up straight from her previous position, "I don't wanna talk to mommy, she's mean."


She couldn't help but pout a bit from the way she'd said it so sincerely. It was rather sad to hear, and Chaerin climbs across the bed to pull her sister into a hug. "I know, she means well, Yein-ah - really," she felt like she was lying through her teeth. Did her mother really mean well with the things she told them sometimes? She couldn't be so sure anymore, but she wasn't about to tell her kid sister that.


"Chaerin..." her sister's voice is small when she speaks out her name, causing the girl to look down to her in question. 


Confusion is written all over her face when Yein holds out something. She blinked as she took what it was, watching the small girl's green eyes glisten as she scooted beside her so she could look at it, too.


The sight caused a sick feeling to form in the pit of her stomach.


"Why do you have this?" it's barely audible when she mutters this, barely speaking above a whisper. Her eyes were too lost in the picture, it brought back too many things that were hard for her to think about.


It was before so many things happened, before they ruined their lives. Chaerin winces at the thought of how she used to be, what she went through. Nonetheless, what her sister went through. 


The guilt washes over her as she could only gawk down at the photo. It was a family vacation they'd taken in France. Well, not exactly a vacation as it was for their parents to make a business deal, but they decided to drag along all of the siblings as well. The family is standing in front of the Eiffel tower, bright smiles on their faces. 


Jaehyun's shoulder is over Juyeon's, and they're both making idiotic faces but look thrilled either way. Their parents are in the middle, embracing each other. Then you had the three sisters on the other side. Chaerin was hugging her older sister like her life depended on it, being a mere kid, and there's an admirable grin on Soomi's face as she's holding the youngest sister in her arm, doing a peace sign with the other.


Even though they were so young, her big sister looked exactly how she remembered. There was a reason everyone always loved her. She had big, green eyes that had the faintest amount of blue in them from her father, perfectly sculpted lips that were just plump enough to look utterly kissable, and bright pearls for teeth that didn't have one crook in them when she smiled. Never had her sister been a pound over a hundred, with long, skinny legs and a curved body, and her unlimited budget had only added to the natural appeal with what she'd wear.


She was Chaerin's idol. When she would look in the mirror, all she ever wanted to be was like her big sister.


Now it was her fault she was gone.


"When is big sissy coming back?"


Yein's sounded like she was going to cry, and it would be a lie to say Chaerin wasn't. Closing her eyes to hold in the emotion, she wraps an arm around her little sister, who only kept staring at the girl's face on the photograph. 


What did she say?


What could she say?


"I don't know, Yein."













62:00 | getting to the bottom of your wonders


S E O U L



Home sweet home, it was no lie everyone was glad to be back. Although being home only meant the pressure of work and constant rehearsing, but the others were still thrilled to be out of Ilsan. If they were being honest, after what happened, the small town began to creep them out.

The next week felt like it was never going to end. Soomi was on the ball, heading to the company on time every morning for practicing the songs she would be performing. Her and Jinhwan decided to do Only You at the festivals since it was so well received on the charts and by all the fans. Since they had this together, their schedules were combined fairly often, and they got to freely hang around without having to worry. At least that had been a nice change compared to how it was months ago.

Nevertheless, the practice was tiring like usual. By the time the end of the year festivals came around, it was the last thing on her mind. All Soomi had been thinking about was New York, about what was going to happen when she got there. Her family never got off her mind, she just wondered if she would have the guts this time, she wondered if they would slam the door in her face. 

Even though they all betrayed her, she realized it was only her own fault at the end of the day. She was the one who started it all, she set herself up for that when her future appeared so bright.

At the same time, she was thankful for what happened. If it wouldn't of, she would have never left to Korea and joined Big Hit. She would've never been invited to YG only a year later after she met Jungkook and the others boys, where she then met the girls of BlackPink. Soomi would have never grown those friendships, she would've never formed such a bond, and she would've never met Jungkook, the boy who swept her off her feet. 

Maybe he wasn't really her first love, she never thought about it. But it was no lie that she loved him - and still did.

Then there was Jinhwan, someone she couldn't live without. What would she do if she never met any of them? Soomi couldn't picture what her life would be like without her beloved friends that she had now.

When New Years struck, the boys of Bangtan and iKON had all gotten together at their massive mansion located in the country side of Seoul. It had a great view of the Han river, and they popped fireworks as they drank and had a great time. Soomi even drank a few drinks, not enough to get drunk since her tolerance was so high, but enough to find herself letting go. For the first time, that night, she had forgotten about everything.

Unfortunately for everyone, the day after, they'd been called to their respective companies for the KCON meeting. It was no surprise that they all were attending whether they liked it or not. With how big they were all growing with Korean music in general, it would be insane for them to pass up the opportunity.

All the while, Soomi hadn't heard one word from her secret stalker.

So it brought her here, January 4, as she walks through the airport. Her head aches from the thoughts wandering through her mind, but she forces herself to have a bright smile on since there were so many fans gathered to say goodbye at the airport.

Having already sent off her luggage, she was left carrying her take on bag and her phone, while trying to gather the gifts fans tried to give to her. Sometimes they didn't get to, though, since she had so many body guards surrounding her and pushing off anyone who got with in feet of her.

"I love you, Soomi!"

"Have a safe flight, queen!"

They all scream out messages, taking pictures when they get a good angle. She waves and yells out thank you's, trying to smile nicely for each of their photos. It was something she was used to.

Finally, they walk through the gates to head onto the plane. This meant no fans were back there, and she sighs out as she could finally breath normally and stop smiling.

Pulling her phone out of her back pocket, she rushes onto the messaging app.

jinhwan: are u on the plane yet?? imu :(

soomi: omw now, ur already there?

jinhwan: yeah, in our seat ;) waiting for uuuu, i need ur warmth

soomi: the fact u managed to get our seats together

soomi: wowie

jinhwan: impressed? i have my ways

soomi: ofc, i'm glad i don't have to sit by some weirdo

jinhwan: yeah bobby is pretty weird

soomi: i was thinking of junhoe but i guess he works too

jinhwan: whoops

jinhwan: hurry tho, it'll be leaving soon

soomi: yeah yeah i'm almost there 

jinhwan: how r u tho? still worried ab new york?

soomi: yeah how could i not be? that weirdo said they were gonna expose all my secrets or something

jinhwan: did they just mean me and jungkook? is there something else?

soomi: yeah, sort of

jinhwan: you don't have to tell me anything but just

jinhwan: how bad is it? is it really possible for them to find it?

soomi: well, you remember when i told you about my family

jinhwan: yeah!

soomi: that, it's about that

soomi: it's sorta bad

jinhwan: oh:(

soomi: but i don't think they could dig up stuff like that

jinhwan: then they probably didn't, stop worrying so much, ok?

soomi: i'll try my best, jinan

soomi: i'm boarding rn

A deep breathe leaves her lips as she departs from her body guards, waving to them and bowing politely. With that, she heads off her own way down the rows of the plane, until she sees the first class area. There weren't many people at this part, as first class was so expensive these days.

This is why seven boys easily notice her.

"Hi Soo!"

"Soomi's here!"

"Let's get this party fucking started!"

She wrinkled her nose at Junhoe as she walks to her seat next to Jinhwan, "you're cringe," she tells him before offering greetings to the others.

He sends her an offended look from where he was laying comfortably, "well fuck you too then."

Only giggling in reply, Jinhwan takes her bag from her and sets it down, helping her get situated. It didn't take long until she was laying back in the seat beside him, letting out a deep sigh, "oh boy, plane rides," she mutters to herself.

His hand reaches out and takes hers. Just when he opens his mouth to finally say something to her since he had yet to get the chance, another voice breaks through.

"Are you ready to go home, Soo?!" Donghyuk is asking like an angel, and she didn't blame him since the others didn't really know anything about her background other than Jinhwan and Junhoe.

The boy beside her squeezes her hand comfortingly, and she forces a smile on her face as she responds, "I guess so."




///




N E W    Y O R K





"What the fuck do you want?!" the girl curses out in a whisper, her green eyes bulging out of their sockets as she rushes forward onto the porch, shutting the front door behind her. In a panic, she places both her hands on their chest, pushing the figure back until they were out of sight of any of the front windows of the house. Once they're in a place where they can't easily be seen, she holds a hand to her head in distress, "I told you to stop coming here, Solar isn't coming back, it's been five fucking years!"

No sign of emotion shows on the person's face before her. No, the boy only raises a brow, not caring much for what just came out of her mouth.

"No shit," he deadpans, "that's not what I'm here for, Chaerin."

A sigh leaves her lips, and she crosses her arms over her chest impatiently. "Then what do you want, Sunwoo? I told you I didn't have any interest in speaking to you," her harsh tone grows softer with each word, until it was a mumble, "all you do is remind me of her," she finishes dully, her eyes diverting to her feet.

Either way, everything around her reminded her of Soomi. You simply couldn't get away from it, she was always there somehow. The eldest daughter of the family had left a permanent scar on not only Chaerin's heart, but everyone else's, too.

There's a low chuckle from the tall boy standing before her. Shocked at the fact he had the audacity to laugh at her, she's about to knee him where the sun doesn't shine, when he stops her from doing such by speaking, "and you think you don't remind me of Solar? You've got the same eyes, just a bit lighter," he hums, his eyes wandering all over her face as if he was inspecting her features, "the same lips, too. I almost see her when I look at you."

She only rolls her eyes at him, knowing well she didn't actually look anything like her sister. Chaerin and Soomi had a clear difference. The only things they had that were similar was exactly what Sunwoo just mentioned.

"Yein, though," he shakes his head in disbelief, "she's like a replica."

Now that wasn't a lie.

"I know," Chaerin laments, "there's not a thing they don't have in common."

By this point, the two of them were so deep in thought about the girl that Sunwoo seemed to forget what he came here for. Before he could begin to remember, though, an abrupt marvel is heard from behind them, along with the sound of a car being locked.

"Sunwoo, Chaerin!" Juyeon calls as he walks up the driveway, slapping Sunwoo's back as a greeting and only grinning at his sister, "what the hell are you two doing together?"

The short brunette only shrugs as she motions to Sunwoo, "I don't know, this idiot keeps showing up asking about Solar like she's gonna randomly show up one day."

His previously bright face deflates in a second from the name that rolls of her lips. Juyeon clears his throat, uncomfortable thinking about his sister as he sighs, "o-oh, well, it's been five years dude," his voice shakes a bit when he informs Sunwoo of this obviously known fact, "and she still hasn't shown up, so I think you're shit outta luck."

Chaerin watches as he turns away, practically running up the steps to the mansion and zooming inside. She pursed her lips, feeling bad that she so casually brought up their sister in front of Juyeon. If she felt guilty, there was no telling how he felt, she could only imagine. After all, it had all been his idea. 

Juyeon and Soomi had never really been close. He didn't think of what kind of consequences his accusations could have back then, and it only back fired in his face, in all of their faces.

And they could never take it back. Knowing what he felt like made her want to go comfort her poor brother, she didn't want him to do anything dumb, after all. The siblings had deemed to take care of each other after the disappearance of their big sister, no matter what.

"Chae-"

"Look, Sunwoo, I don't have time to talk about Solar with you," she sighs as she turns back to him from having watched Juyeon disappear inside, "I need to go check on him."

Not fully grasping what was going on, he wondered why exactly Juyeon got like that from the mention of his sister. He understood they missed her, sure, it was awful what happened. But it had always been weird to him how the only one who left was Solar, not any of the others. He knew they had all been involved, after all.

He got that he missed her, but was it really still so bad that he'd immediately cry upon hearing her name?

Or was there more to the story he had yet to hear?

When he doesn't say anything, the girl before him heaves out a deep breath, before spinning on her heel and heading back up the steps to her house.

"Chaerin, wait!" he calls, but she ignored him as she disappeared inside.

A defeated look on his face, only then did Sunwoo remember what he truly came here for in the first place. Slowly, his eyes wander to the pieces of paper he held in his hands, the pieces that would help him solve the empty spot that'd been left in his heart.

Although he wasn't sure what to do with one extra, since Chaerin and Juyeon clearly weren't going to budge. He figured they probably didn't even have any idea.

They didn't know what he did. Little did Sunwoo know, they had secrets, too.

Pulling out his phone, he stares down at the fragile papers as he walks back to his car. Climbing inside, he finds the familiar contact while tapping his foot against the floor of the car. Without hesitation, he taps call over the name - Jaehyun.











63:00 | so close but yet so far


N E W     Y O R K 


The day of KCON approached scarily fast. Thanks to the fact they probably should've flew over earlier, the friends didn't have any time to get over their jet lag when they landed in the city. It had been right to rehearsals for them, where they all met up with the Bangtan boys. Everyone agreed they hadn't gotten to do anything since they just got there, so they'd go out to eat afterwards and walk around the city.

It was likely they would get spotted in the city, but since it wasn't prohibited for either of them to be around each other, nobody cared at that point. The media seemed to know Soomi was friends with both the groups of guys whether she liked it or not — they just didn't know to what extent when it came to Jungkook and Jinhwan.

"Are you ready for this?" Jungkook had asked her, placing a hand on her shoulder comfortingly. He knew what she was feeling as everyone piles out of the van. She'd rode with her old friends to the restaurant, so they had to meet up with the others.

Stepping into the fresh American air, she inhales deeply, holding it for a long second as if taking it in before exhaling. Her green eyes rake over her surroundings, and Jungkook didn't mind that she hadn't said a word in reply to him. He only watches her as she turns in circles as they all walk towards the restaurant, gawking at all the familiar sights.

"Aw, look at Soo," Yunhyeong is gushing, "her homesickness is cured."

He gains a few looks from the ones who have any idea of how untrue that was. Nobody blamed him since he didn't know the truth. Soomi stops looking around from his words, finding her thoughts wander back to her family.

Did she want to see them? Would she even be able to?

It was something she'd been debating on endlessly since she was invited. She tried talking to Jungkook and Jimin about it, but they both ended up telling her the same thing.

"If you believe you're ready, Mi, it's now or never."

But she couldn't be so sure if she was ready. At the same time, she didn't want to miss an opportunity to see them again. Even though a part of her was still angry and hurt — in the end, they were still her family whether she liked it or not.

Although she had found a new family in the friends she was surrounded by, it was obvious it wasn't the same as her biological one. Nothing could compare to your own parents, your own siblings. She couldn't replace them if she tried.

"Stop stressing, Soomi-ah," the voice of Jinhwan breaks through her head.

Looking up, she hadn't noticed him walking on her left, with Jungkook to her right. The feeling of the two of them both being right beside her sent her stomach into a frenzy, and she tries to distract herself with other things as she sends the caramel boy a smile, "I'm not, I'm okay."

"You better be, hoe!" She's nearly knocked over by Junhoe when he pushes her from behind.

Stumbling a bit forward from how scarily strong he was compared to her tiny self, she catches herself. Annoyance is written clear over her face when she turns to glare at the giant, "you're lucky I'm too hungry to beat you up right now," she points an incriminating finger at him, narrowing her eyes.

Junhoe only raises a brow as he's still in fits of giggles, "oh please, as if you could ever."

"You wanna fucking find out?!"

Nobody bothered to stop the two when Soomi began to chase Junhoe in circles around the parking lot they were walking on. Some of them were laughing at the idiots that they called friends, while others — like poor Hanbin — only covered his face in embarrassment. Sometimes he really wished they didn't do shit like this.

It wasn't like the petite girl could actually hurt him even if she caught him. There was no point in Junhoe running away from her to begin with, but it was all just part of the fun.

"Why the fuck would you call me a hoe when it's literally in your name?!" Soomi is yelling at her best friend as she continues to run after him. He ended up being too fast for her since his legs were 80% of his body.

"Hey, I didn't say I wasn't!" He shoots back, finally quitting his running because he grew tired.

Figuring that was the end of it as Soomi stops to walk beside him, panting as well from the exercise, everyone's eyes widen at the sound of a yelp leaving the talk boys lips. Turning back to the two, the friends burst into fits of hysterics at the sight of her grabbing onto his ear, pinching it as she pulls his head side ways.

"Okay, okay, I'm sorry!" Junhoe is whining like a baby as he tries to get his ear out of her fingers.

Soomi didn't seem to convinced as she pinched it harder with her sharp nails. A curse leaves the boys lips, and she chuckles at the fact she was finally winning against him, "you should be, whore."

With that, she lets go of the pouty Junhoe.

"Talk about rude," he's muttering as they all enter the restaurant after that ridiculous fiasco.




///



"Soomi-ah, wait!" The honey tone causes her to halt where she had been about to enter the van. 

Slowly, she turns in confusion to meet the eager looking Jinhwan. There's a glint in his eyes, and the night sky was already glaring down upon them since they had gotten here in the middle of the day. By the time rehearsals ended and they went to eat, it was already night in New York City.

But Soomi didn't mind it being dark. It was the prettiest that way. She'd always said the best NYC came out when it's night — for many reasons.

Pursing her lips, she raises a brow at the excited boy, "yes, Jinani?"

He looks to the van instead of responding to her, doing some sort of hand motion. Soomi jumps from the sound of a revving engine behind her, turning in shock when the van drives away.

"That was our ride—," she points in confusion, not understanding why he would do what he just did.

Still, the short boy doesn't inform her of anything as he steps forward. Holding up a hand, a yellow vehicle stops against the curb with in a second.

A triumphant grin takes over his face as he opens the back door, motioning inside as he corrects, "no, this is our ride."

Completely bewildered at whatever he was doing, she honestly didn't care. Climbing inside, she offers a greeting to the taxi driver as Jinhwan climbs in next to her. A yawn leaves her lips, and she leans her head on his shoulder as he mutters some address to the driver.

It wasn't a lie that she was from New York, clearly, but when she said New York she meant the upper east side. The city was a big place, and the only part she really knew like the back of her hand was the part she was from.

But for some reason, she hadn't paid attention to whatever address the boy said to the driver. One, because he made sure to say it so quiet she couldn't make out any words, and second, because she was too tired to make an effort to. Whatever he was planning, she figured it was better off being a surprise anyway.

"Are you tired from the trip?" Jinhwan asks, his hand caressing her thigh as they drive through the busy night streets of New York, noticing she was bound to fall asleep on his shoulder.

Indeed, she was getting rather comfy on his coat. It was so soft that it made a great pillow for her, and she wouldn't mind a good sleep. Soomi hadn't slept on the plane ride like everyone else because of how stressed she was.

But now, her body was catching up with her. No matter how many thoughts filled her mind, it was going to pass out anyway.

"Yeah," she only whispers, closing her green eyes as she waits for dreamland to overcome her. It usually took a long time to get from place to place in NYC anyway, she knew this well. So she probably had time for a nap, right?

Soomi was thankful for the power nap she got. Only around twenty five minutes later, however, was Jinhwan nudging her side gently, trying to wake her up.

"We're here, Soo, wake up."

"Baby, get up," he repeats again when the first attempt didn't work. As if she had been awake the whole time, her eyes shoot open from the word, not believing she really just heard it fall from his lips.

She has to mentally recover, it'd been a while since Jinhwan used any names like that.

Paying the driver, they both offer goodbyes as they climb out of the vehicle. His hand finds a way to hers, and she rubs her eyes from still being half asleep. Blinking a few times to make sure she could see clearly, Soomi takes that time to take in where it was he had taken her.

"So..." she's starting to say, when she realizes where they were. Her mouth falls agape from shock at the sight of the place, and she could only peer up at it like she had seen something astonishing.

The boy beside her watches her expression with a giddy smile.

"Jinhwan," she gasps, "I can't believe you remembered me mentioning this place."

The few times she had ever told him anything about her family was when she talked about the good memories she had. A good amount of those memories took place in this exact ice cream parlor that they were standing in front of, a place she didn't think she would ever come back to. Soomi and her siblings used to sit inside of it after school, laughing and making jokes or talking about drama and other things. The parlor had been their safe place from their parents when they were kids.

"Come on, let's go inside," Jinhwan tugs at her hand, snapping her out of her gawking state.

She didn't hesitate to walk with him towards the door. He holds it open for her, and the two enter as if they were children.

Walking right up to the counter, she already knew what she wanted. "I want two scoops of strawberry and one cheesecake with gummy bear toppings, please," she recites, and if the cashier didn't look up they would've thought she was a kid. Her voice was so tiny that it made you want to squeeze her, it was exactly what Jinhwan did as he wraps an arm around her.

Putting in the order, the cashier looks to him. Not knowing much about the place, he only hums, "I'll just have the same."

Soomi bounces up and down on her feet as she watches them put together the order. She and her siblings used to always all order the same thing, and they would all combine their ice creams to make the ultimate ice cream castle. Just thinking of how stupid they were brought a goofy smile on her face.

Before she knew it, the worker slides the two cups of ice cream scoops over the counter after Jinhwan paid yet again. Soomi would usually bother him about letting her pay instead, but she was far too distracted to even remember you had to pay.

"Let's sit over here," she urges, dragging him over to a familiar booth.

Sliding into the booth together, the brunette excitedly starts digging into her ice cream. She was inhaling it like it was the first thing she was eating in years, even though they literally just came from a restaurant.

He giggles at her cute mannerisms, shaking his head as he picks up his own spoon. Sticking a spoonful of the ice cream in his mouth, it ended up tasting better than he expected.

"Good right?" Soomi notices the look on his face, and he only nods in confirmation. Still eating her own, she admits gratefully, "I'm glad you took me here, Jinan, thank you."

Meeting her bright eyes, he shrugs, "sure, I wanted to help make this trip better for you since you've been stressing so much."

Her heart melts from his words. She could almost cry in that moment as she could only peer up at the angelic soul that Jinhwan was.

"I know there's a lot going on, and I can't help or don't even know everything but," he takes another bite, "that doesn't mean I'm not here. I always notice when you're down, and it breaks my heart when there's nothing I can do to help," he admits dully before piping up again, "so I just want to help you move forward, I know you tend to overthink the worst. But I'm here to make sure you don't weigh yourself down so much, I care about you, silly," she was confused as to why he was calling her silly.

Suddenly, she feels his thumb against her chin. Jinhwan wipes off a piece of ice cream with a chuckle, before finishing, "I hope coming here gives you the strength to fight what you're going through, Soomi, and if it doesn't, I hope my love for you can instead."

If she wasn't gone then, she was nowhere to be seen now. Soomi was a puddle of goo on the ground as she scoots to the side, enclosing the space between her and Jinhwan.

She squeezes him like her life depended on it, and right then, she never wanted to let go.

"Thank you," she mumbles, "thank you, thank you, thank you."

It was as if those were the only words she knew. What more could she say to him? She was at a complete loss.

"Stop thanking me," he's giggling, knowing well she had no idea what to say, "just eat your ice cream, dummy."

Pulling out of the hug, his arm remains around her as she picks her spoon back up. It didn't take her longer than a minute to eat the rest of it.

"There," she says victoriously, "ugh, it's as good as I remember."

The two lovers stay in the ice cream parlor for another good while. Even though they finished their treat, they were just having fun talking and enjoying each other's presence. Soomi was able to let go of her worries even if it was only for a second, and she had him to thank for that.

"Ready to go, or do you want to stay a bit longer?" Jinhwan asks, already rising to his feet so he could go throw away the trash, "we can always come back," he assures her.

The girl rises to her feet, looking around one last time at the parlor.

"We can go, but we definitely have to come back before we leave NYC."

"That's fine by me," he glistens, grabbing her hand again as they head towards the exit and he chunks their empty ice cream cups in the trash can.

Just as the two leave, they pass a familiar two figures. Soomi had been nuzzling her head into Jinhwan's chest as he had his arm wrapped around her since the weather was so cold, trying to steal all of his warmth.
