The moon peers down upon the world, particularly New York. A sigh fills the sole air as the little girl walks, her hand in her older brothers as they make their way towards the entrance of the ice cream parlor.

She felt weird, although she didn't know what it was. Juyeon wasn't paying attention, too focused on something on his phone to see how Yein was turning every which way.

Her green eyes suddenly fixated on two people, it looked like a couple. Squinting, the young girl tries to take in any of their features, but it was impossible since their backs where to her.

For some reason, she couldn't stop staring at them.

She should've noticed. She should've noticed the long, flowing brown hair that was all too familiar to her. She should've noticed the familiar sashay, the familiar bag that hung from her shoulders.

She should've noticed it was her big sister.


64:00 | composing the plan


"Welcome everyone, to KCON New York 2019!" the arena erupts into screeches of the fans, high pitched thunders of their voices in unison echoing across the massive building as the robot announcer goes on from the intercoms, "please welcome your hosts, Joshua and Vernon!"

Soomi watches as the two english speaking members of Seventeen make their way onto the stage. The second they step an inch into public view, more screams explode from the audience. She was honestly worried about getting a migraine having to listen to it, was Korean music seriously this big now? Last time she was here, they didn't need an arena half this big, now it was too full, with even people sitting outside.

Someone like her didn't get stage fright. She'd never felt a day of worry in her life only until this psycho came into it. Just the thought of what could happen, what would happen, had her on edge. It was bad enough there were so many people she had to perform in front of, in her home town, which actually made her a bit conscious. Now, she was adding the fact this person - whoever they may be - was going to expose her life to the world.

How would she get through this? To think she had no idea what exactly they found out. Soomi tries to remind herself of the talks she'd had with Jimin, Jungkook, and Jinhwan. They had all said the same general thing; that whoever this was probably didn't have access to whatever she thought they did. Although this could be true, she didn't know how powerful this person was, what if they really did dig it up?

Her past, the one she so desperately tried to get away from, was it catching up with her after all?

"Mi, hey," she jumps at the sudden contact of a hand on the small of her back. Not realizing she was being dragged somewhere until he was forcing her to sit, "here, drink this, you look pale."

Blinking a few times, she slowly reaches out to take the water bottle. Twisting off the cap, Soomi only mumbles a thanks before practically chugging half of it down. Exhaling deeply in satisfaction, the girl twists the cap on again, setting it aside before looking to Jungkook, who took the seat beside her, "where did you even come from?" she quizzes blankly. She'd been watching the frantic movement of everyone and what was going on on-stage by herself for the last hour, so much she hadn't seen any of the guys around.

"I was in the dressing room getting makeup done," he explains, "that's where the others are right now, too, and probably your other friends."

She only nods, pursing her lips as she rakes her eyes over their surroundings. To think, the last time she was here-

"-Soomi," Jungkook rips her head out of her thoughts when he speaks up abruptly. Her eyes are curious when she looks to him, wondering what was with that certain glint in his tone of voice. The maknae is holding up his hand, and she was confused on why until she saw his ring finger.

Holding a hand over her mouth, she hurriedly pushes his hand back down into his lap with the other. About to have had a heart attack right then and there, she's exasperated, "why are you wearing that - are you crazy?!"

Giggles are all she receives from the taunting boy. She wished then that his doe eyes weren't so twinkly, as maybe then she could actually stay mad at him for more than a second. Those brown irises of him, brightening more under the lights above them, and he diverts them down to his lap, where he looks at the ring on his finger. 

"Something told me to wear it today," he shares fervently, "it brings me back to better times."

It had been just what her mind was on. He wasn't wrong at all; the last time they were here was when they got engaged, but it was also the time when she kissed her best friend. Although it had been so confusing for her back then, there was no denying how happy she was, even though the pressure of seeing her family again had still weighed her down. At least then, she didn't have this many more worries on her shoulders. Now, she was running out of strength to the point she felt as if she could barely walk anymore.

"That's cute," is all she says for a second, staring at her feet because she knew she wouldn't be able to meet his gaze, "just - don't let anyone see it, you know we denied the engagement accusations."

"I won't, Mi, don't worry about it," he assures her calmly, "we have bigger things to focus on, like finding whoever this is that's trying to mess with you tonight."

Upon the mention, she heaves out a long sigh. It was true, if they wanted to stop this, there was only one way to do it. 

"How would we ever?" she frowns, muttering in a complete loss of hope, "it's not like we can get back to the produ-"

"-Soomi, you're a fucking genius."

A bewildered look slaps onto her face. 

"Are you insane?" she asks, holding a hand up to rest it on his forehead as if he was sick, "they aren't going to let you in there."

But there was an intense look in his eyes that let her know he did not care about anything she just said. Jungkook is in extreme focus, creasing his brows as he thinks hard. "Well, I wouldn't think that whoever this person is has free access there, either," his statement makes Soomi's mouth form into an o shape. He's right, she realizes, not unless their stalker was some sort of KCON producer themselves.

Which was very unlikely.

Still, she didn't understand his thoughts. "Okay, but what are you planning to do?" she cautiously questions him, growing worrisome of the devious look taking over his expression.

Not saying a word to her, Jungkook gloriously jumps to his feet. The look on his face seemed to show he thought he came up with the most genius thing ever thought of, as he turns to face the girl still remaining on the bench. 

"I'm gonna fix this, okay?" he leans down to meet her eyes, and Soomi opens her mouth to ask him yet again what exactly he was planning. But she didn't get a chance to, when Jungkook only caresses her cheek before spinning on his heel and practically bolting towards the dressing room hall.

Her hand reaches up to rub the spot he'd just touched, and she could only sigh at where he'd just ran off to, probably to get some of the others involved in his antics, "please don't do anything stupid, Kookie."

Meanwhile, the youngest boy of Bangtan's shoes click against the concrete flooring. His eyes wander from room door to room door, reading every name carefully as he searches for the one he needed. Sometimes, he wondered why the hell they didn't just put the names in alphabetical order, it would make this a lot fucking easier.

Slowing to a walk at this point, he checks behind him every so often to make sure no staff was watching whatever he was doing. He didn't need anyone to ask what he was up to or see where he'd be going, it could get him caught and ruin the great plan he'd came up with in only a mere minute.

You know what they said, drastic times call for drastic measures, and Jungkook would call this a rather treacherous one. Whatever it was that this person didn't for sure have on Soomi, but still could have, seemed to be rather serious even though he didn't know the extent. This is why he needed to make sure he ended this. He'd catch whoever this was, once and for all, and everything would be back to normal.

Finally finding the door after having felt like he was walking for miles, a breath of relief leaves his lips. Knocking a few times, when he gets so response, he realized he didn't have the time to wait anyway. Twisting the handle, Jungkook carelessly marches in, having full awareness of the name on the door; iKON.

"Oh, that's probably our manager-nim now - Jungkook?!" Donghyuk had been saying, as it sounded like they were missing their manager or something. The second his eyes fall on the boy standing at the doorway, blatant confusion overcomes his expression.

The others look up from having heard the name, thinking their friend was crazy. At the sight of the exact boy he'd just mentioned, they end up all gawking, too.

"Hi, Jungkook, what's up?" Hanbin is the first to break the silence of everyone staring at him like he was some sort of ghost.

"You haven't seen our manager by chance, have you?" Yunhyeong raises a brow, a hopeful look on his face, "she's gone MIA on us suddenly."

Not caring much for wherever their stupid manager was, he only shakes his head. The brown eyes of Jeon Jungkook were only looking at one person, a boy that was sitting in the corner of the room on his phone. He was either scrolling or texting someone, probably Soomi asking how she was, and didn't bother to even give him a greeting like the rest of them had.

He couldn't blame him, he probably would've done the same thing.

"Jinhwan," the other six in the room nearly fall over from hearing that was what Jungkook came into their room for. The last thing anyone would think is that he wanted to talk to their eldest, which is why they're even more shocked from his next proposal, "I need your help."

The boy blinks at him, his head gradually rising from his phone. His brows furrow, and he exchanges a glance with Junhoe, who was probably the most confused out of all of them, knowing the full scoop of what went on with the two boys and Soomi (perks of being her best friend.) He was horrified that they were about to go fight each other or something as his hyung as rose to his feet after a second, "uh, alright, what's up?"

Walking over towards Jungkook, if it was any other time, they would laugh at the saddening height difference. But everyone was too worried about the whereabouts of their missing manager and the fact the two boys were associating with each other in the first place. As if the day wasn't already going to be weird enough.

"Follow me, I'll explain to you on the way," Kook had been saying as he makes his way back towards the exit. Jinhwan, still not having any idea what it was he wanted, only hums an agreeing noise before the two exit, closing the door behind them.

Meanwhile, the six that were left only stare at the door. "Does anyone feel fucking lost right now?" Bobby blurts, and the rest of them agree or nod immediately.

Kicking his feet back in the chair, he leans them atop the table in front of where he was sitting. "Probably has something to do with the whole Soomi's stalker thing."

"No shit," Chanwoo looks to Junhoe, who sent him a glare from his sarcasm.

"You know what, pal? I'll step on you right now."

"Dude, that doesn't work on me, we're like the same height."

"Suck my ass."

Hanbin was rubbing his temples from the bickering that had already begun, "can you guys - now is not the time," he pleads, before focusing back on the current situation, "I'm sure they're going to see her or something, but we need to figure out where the hell our manager is so we know what time we're going on stage."

"I'm going to throw your PC off a cliff when we get home," but the arguing wasn't over, even though their leader had begged them to shut up.

"Try it, and you'll never seen your Bob Dylan vinyls again."











65:00 | lost and found



"So, where exactly are we going?" Jinhwan asks blankly, still trailing behind the much taller boy as he walks frantically. They had taken multiple flights of steps, gone down many hallways, and were now heading down an endless path that he had absolutely no idea headed to. It didn't help that he had yet to really tell him anything, only saying it's for Soomi, over and over like he was stuck in some sort of hypnosis.

Sneakers clicking against the concrete of whatever floor they were on by this point, Jungkook exhales with determination as he explains, "to the developer room."

The caramel haired boy is even more lost.

"What?"

Jungkook looks back at him, a taint of annoyance on his face. "You know, where they cast stuff on the screens at and speak into the intercom," he says almost mockingly, as if Jinhwan should know this since he was apparently so much older and wiser.

Not pleased with his tone, he narrows his eyes, trying not to let the devious boy get to him because he was well aware of his games. "No fucking shit, I'm asking what your reasoning is for going there," he snaps.

His hard tone didn't bother Jungkook in the slightest. 

"Well, I figured that's where whoever has been stalking Soomi will be at," he informs his thought process to him, and he only peers at him intently as they walk side by side down the everlasting hallway, "you know, since they said they were going to expose her, it's not like they're gonna be going on stage themselves and doing it. That means there's only one place they could be."

By this point, he fully gathered the extent of Jungkook's plan. Jinhwan was impressed he came up with something like that.

"That's pretty smart for someone like you to think of."

Jungkook halts in his tracks, raising a brow tauntingly as he spins to face the shorter boy. "Huh, someone like me?" he repeats his words in a question, as if wanting him to say them again while meeting his eyes.

But despite their height difference, Jinhwan remained unbothered by his attempt to intimidate him. "Uhuh," he shrugs as if it wasn't a big deal and he wasn't insulting his competition, "I've just heard you're not the brightest around."

The younger one rolls his eyes, turning around to start walking again since his attempt hadn't worked. Knowing him though, he always had many tricks, particularly remarks, up his sleeve. Running his tongue across his bottom lip, there's a smirk on his lips when he hums, "at least I know I make Soomi feel the best."

He didn't need to turn around to know that ticked Jinhwan off. Neither of them were oblivious to the fact she had slept with both of them multiple times. It wasn't something they could get mad at her about, since she had feelings for both of them and usually, it was them tempting her. If anything, it just made them mad at each other, not Mi.

"I wouldn't be so sure of that."

By this time, they reach the developers doors. His hand is reaching for the handle, completely ignoring the sign on the door that read in giant red lettering; do not enter, personnel only.

Even though they had a much more important task on their hands than arguing over something so trivial, he never lost an argument, or his pride.

"Did you not hear her screaming my name that night when you got ba-"

His words aren't cut off from getting hit, surprisingly. They're cut off by Jinhwan when the two of them open the door, being faced with what was on the other side.

It could've gone horribly wrong in many ways. They could've been faced with the actual producers and developers of KCON, who would most likely not be thrilled to be bothered by them. It would've been rather interesting to explain why they were up there in the first place, as it wasn't like they could just claim they got lost.

But their eyes weren't met with any developers or workers at KCON period. It wasn't an empty room either, which would have been disappointing, but definitely less nerve-wracking.

"Hey, stop!" Jinhwan yells, and the conversation that had been going on between the two was left behind in any instant. 

Both of them run into the room, making a bolt at the figure. There was a black cap over their face, preventing anything but their eyes and mouth from behind seen. From their body shape, however, despite their baggy clothing, it was clear it was a woman like they all assumed.

"Who the fuck are you?!" Jungkook is demanding as the two of them corner off the lady. She has a duffel bag over her shoulder, and they had a good feeling of what it contained. 

Hearts beating out of their chests, they try to figure out a way to approach her. If she got away, that was it. This was their only chance to end this once and for all.

Not a word leaves the woman's mouth, she only peers at them with narrowed eyes. If she wanted to say something, she would've, right? Why was she silent?

"Give us the bag," Jinhwan demands as he steps forward, holding out his hand and waiting for his request to be fulfilled.

"And I'd remove the cap if I were you," Jungkook hums from just behind him, cracking one of his knuckles, "I want you to see me clearly when I break every fucking limb in your fat fucking bo-"

"HEY!" Jinhwan yells, leaping forward as he tries to grab her. The two of them hadn't noticed the fire exit escape she'd been trying to itch towards, and had jumped at. 

Realizing what was happening, that she was getting away, Jungkook feels a lump get caught in his throat. Not knowing what to do, he fiddles for his phone as the lady had slammed the door back, and a click sounds from outside, indicating she locked it. So that was how she got in here in the first place.

Dialing the first number he sees, he holds it to his ear, hoping it was answered before it was too late. Jinhwan was messing with the door, slamming his shoulder against it as he curses, "why the fuck is it not opening? She jammed something in the lock from the outside," he realizes a second later when it didn't budge. 

Completely fed up with the fact they'd been so close, he kicks the concrete door out of anger, as if it would make any difference, "god fucking dammit!"

"H-Hello?" Jungkook nearly drops his phone from the small voice, he hadn't noticed he called her out of all people.

Either way, there was no time for him to worry about it.

"Mi, I need you to listen carefully," he didn't bother with a greeting, speaking as fast as he could form thoughts in his head.

But the girl is a mess of confusion, "huh? Jungkook, what are you talking about, is there something wro-"

"- Go outside, whoever is after you is bolting through the parking lot right now. You have to catch them, Mi, they have baggy clothes on and they're trying to leave," he cuts her off, blurting out everything before she could even have time to process what he was saying, "you have to go now, it's your only chance."

His urges sent her into a state of shock. Was he serious?

But after a second, when he only continues his calls to her, telling her she had to move if she wanted to end it, she knew it wasn't any sort of sick joke. That was what he was planning.

"I'm going," she tells him harshly before ending the call, shoving her phone in her pocket. 

The New York sun beams down on Soomi as she pushes out the metal exit doors. It didn't matter if anyone saw her leave, she didn't care. This was life or death to her even if that was overdramatic, she wasn't letting them get away.

Heading out into the private parking, located at the back of the arena where fans weren't allowed, her head whips every which way. "Where are you?" she whispers out, as if it would help her spot them any easier. Her heart is beating out of her chest, dragging along with her as she runs through the parking lot, raking her eyes across every inch of it as she tries to spot the monster that was making her life a living hell.

Now or never, her mind kept yelling at her, you can't let them get away.

Jungkook had done what he could, now it was her turn to fight her own battle. And ending it is exactly what she was going to do.

"Hey!" she screams at the sight of a figure. Using what she knew of them, she takes in everything. 

Baggy clothes, female, carrying a massive bag.

Bingo.

"Hey!" she yells louder, her fists balling at her side as she breaks into a run. Realizing she was wearing heels because she'd been dressed for the show, she rips them off her feet so she could run faster across the concrete. It burned the soles of her feet from the flaming sun, and the pieces of dirt and rock cut her feet as she goes, but it only makes her force herself to run faster, "STOP!"

Only noticing her now, the figure had turned around to peer at her. But she wasn't wearing a cap to cover her face like she had been when she was caught by Jungkook and Jinhwan.

And Soomi knew exactly who she was.

"HEY," she screams, trying to hide the astonishment on her face as she runs. The psycho seemed to realize the fact she was caught because Soomi had no doubt seen her face. A look of defeat had taken over it, before she spins on her heel and makes a bolt for her vehicle, probably thinking that she could still blackmail her anyway.

But Soomi was too determined, she was too outrageously angered. Nothing could compare to the boiling of seething infuriation in her veins. It was an emotion she'd never felt in her life, and she reaches her arm back, throwing the heel in her hand straight at the woman's head.

It hits with a clap before falling to the ground behind her. A screech leaves the lady's lips, and the split moment of shock that had taken over her from being hit Soomi used to her advantage.

"You fucking bitch!" the familiar voice cries as she's tackled to the ground.

If anyone ever thought Soomi was weak, they should see her now. It was no joke the woman was stronger than her, but with the immense furious feeling that was inside of her, she could probably beat up Jungkook if she wanted to.

Grabbing both of her wrists with her hands, she holds them over her head as she keeps her body on the ground with her own. Meeting the eyes of the woman, she could only gawk in disbelief, "y-you're iKON's manager, what the fuck is wrong with you?!" she yells in astonishment, the anger making her eyes nearly bloodshot, "you've put me through so much shit and for what, you fucking psycho?!"

Not saying anything, her breath is heavy as she tries to fight off the girl's anger. But Soomi picks up one of her heels, slamming the sharp heel into her arm. A scream echoes through the air, and the girl hisses again, "don't try to get away bitch, you're going straight to jail for what you've caused," she snaps, "hurting your own artist, how could you do that, you sick fuck?!"

Trying to catch her breath as she was in pain from the attack on her arm, she finally spoke.

"I did it because I had to, so maybe you would stop putting his career in jeopardy you selfish whore-" Soomi slams the heel into her arm again, and this time it cut skin, but it only cut her off for a second. "These poor boys fall at your feet, just for you to use them like pieces of dirt, but yet YG sees you as this angel, it makes me fucking sick!"

"You don't deserve any of this, I know what you've done, you fucking drug-addict," she screams as if someone would hear her in the middle of the parking lot, "I know what kind of person you really are, we don't want you in Korea just like your own parents didn't!"

When the woman looks to her again, her eyes are red. You could barely even see that bright green in her irises, almost like something supernatural was taking over her.

Just like your own parents didn't.

"You bitch, you're going to feel real pain you fucking-" Soomi had raised the heel of her shoe, again meaning she removed one hand from the woman's wrist. It was about to head straight into one of her eyes, where she would then go for the other. She wanted this woman to hurt, she wanted her to bleed, to be left a mess of agony on the ground. 

Just as the shoe is heading straight for her eye, the woman grabs Soomi's wrist with her free hand, stopping her. Having been saving her strength, the petite girl screeches when she flips her over, her back hitting the concrete with a hard thump.

An ache shoots up her spine from the impact, and she struggles to rip the woman's hand off of her. "What was that now?" she mocks with a devious laugh as she towers over her, "you seemed to forget you're nothing but a child, sweet Soomi."

Eyes flaming, she tries again to rip her wrists out of her hand, "fuck you, I'm going to fucking-"

"- Aw, you're going to what now?" the girl's eyes bulge at the sound of something cocking, and she gasps at the sight of just that - a gun.

At the welling fear that takes over her expression despite how she tries to hide it, the woman bursts into fits of booming laughter. She laughed like she never had before, like this was the best moment of her entire life. Really, it was, she'd been waiting for such a great time for as long as she could remember.

All her years of following the girl, they all added up to now.

Taking out the world's trash, one at a time. And it all started with Kim Soomi.

Or should she say Solar Kim?

"Not so brave now, are we?" she taunts, unable to hold in her laughter. The barrel of the gun is cold against Soomi's head, and she winces from the feeling. Trying one last time to get out of her grip, her strength was just too much compared to her, she'd been tricked, and she had to face it.

"Let this be a valuable lesson for you, you should go over it daily wherever you end up rotting," her finger is just hovering over the trigger, and not knowing what else she could do, Soomi squeezes her eyes shut.

She waits, she waits for the inevitable, she waits for everything she had built to crumble before her. Not because she would get exposed, or everyone would know how she really was back then, but because the legacy of Kim Soomi, Korea's number one solo artist, the idol that had taken the industry over night, and the girl who loved more than she could manage, would be gone.

Soomi knew it was it for her.

Until nothing happened.



PART TWO

66:00 | violence is always the answer

A scream leaves the woman's lips when she's picked up by her hair, the gun in her hands being thrown to the ground. It hits the concrete, sliding away, and the other soul picks it up, a deep laugh leaving his lips as he cocks it himself, making sure it was loaded.

Not having any idea what was going on, Kim Soomi slowly opens her eyes. The red was gone in them, they were back to their normal state of green-blue, and it sparkles under the sunlight as she looks up.

Jungkook, Jinhwan, she knew they would be there to save her. After all, they'd been the one's who made up the plan initially, right? They were always her white knights when she needed them, never could she be more thankful.

Only until she saw neither of them standing before her, another laugh echoes through her ears. And it causes her vision to grow faint as she looks up fully, thinking she was hearing things.

"Oh, little sister, always getting into trouble somehow."

"Jaehyun?!" she cries, seeing the familiar, sleeked back dark hair, the same sharp jawline, and the same cocky smirk on his face when he saved her from something. The boy continues to chuckle as he steps forward, holding out a hand and grabbing her. She's pulled up before she could even understand what was happening, and Soomi has to hold onto her brother when she looks to who was still holding the psycho up by their hair, "S-Sunwoo?!"

Holding a hand to her head, it seemed like the world was spinning. There was no way this was happening right now, there was no way, it was impossible.

"How the hell are you guys here right now?!" she yells, unable to contain any of her overwhelming emotions as she tries to grasp this.

Her brother's finger is lingering over the trigger as he raises it, a playful grin on his lips when he holds it up to the woman Sunwoo was still holding. There was no doubt she was gonna have some bad hair loss, if she even lived after this. Frankly, Soomi wasn't sure what they were going to do, as Jaehyun only glances to her, "shouldn't we talk about that later? Let's start on why this crazy bitch thought it would be okay to fuck with my sister."

As soon as this angered statement leaves his lips, he steps forward to hold the gun to her head, leaning until he was directly in front of her face. "Well, ma'am, I haven't heard any explanation yet," he coos impatiently.

The lady only narrows her eyes, and at the sight of the dirty look he was giving Jaehyun, Sunwoo was tired of it. The Solar was standing right before him, and he couldn't embrace her yet because of this idiotic bitch. Not to mention, the fact anyone dared to threaten her life, he wasn't having it.

"I don't really care for one, honestly," he deadpans, and before anyone could stop him, he drops her to the ground. She's unable to reach when Sunwoo raises his foot, before slamming it against one of her wrists. Soomi winces at the crack that echoes through the ground, it was definitely fractured.

He does it to the other, and he only snorts when her screams echo through the air. It was a disgusting sight for anyone else to see, and although it had been a long time for her to see this sort of stuff, she was still used to it.

In a way, at least, until he'd picked her up by her hair again, humming, "oh darn, doesn't seem like you can fight back anymore with fractured wrists, you might want to get that checked out!"

The woman only yells and wails, begging for him to stop. But Sunwoo only chuckles as he holds out his hand for the gun, and Jaehyun didn't hesitate.

By this point, Soomi couldn't take it. She said she was used to this stuff still, but not to this extent. She'd seen them beat up many people, but never this bad, never this vulgarly.

Then again, this woman was about to pop a cap in her skull.

She buries her face in her hands when two gun shots echo through the air. Peeking through her fingers, she sees they'd gone into both of the woman's feet, making it where it would be agonizing if she ever wanted to walk. 

He was being tactical. It'd be stupid for him to kill her here, which was most likely what he was planning at this point. Sunwoo was always involved in bad things, with bad people, it was part of the reason their families never got along. His family wanted to work with hers, but they did so many illegal things that her father had denied it. In result, Sunwoo's family tried to steal his business, which created the entire war between them.

It was why they never wanted her to be with him, but the two had never cared. Their relationship was always strong no matter what people thought of them, and it was never supposed to change.

Poor Sunwoo never really knew what happened. He never knew the full story. All he was ever told by her siblings after Soomi's disappearance was that their parents disowned her because they found out about the drugs. The thing was, all of her siblings had been doing them as well. It was why it never made sense to him on why Soomi was the only one who was punished for it.

He was in the dark up until a few days ago, when called Jaehyun and told him about what he'd found online, about how he found Soomi. Only then, upon them speaking about her leaving, had he told him the full story. Suddenly, it had made sense to him why Chaerin and Juyeon got so iffy when asked about their sister, because it was their fault she left.

The woman's screams continue to echo through the air. "Who the fuck are you people? How does someone always save this slut?!" she's yelling in disbelief, but her words only earn her another gun shot in the leg.

"Call her something else, and it might be your head," Jaehyun's the one with the gun now, eyes burning from what she dared to say about his beloved sister, "I'll gladly make you my shooting range."

"You don't know her!" she continues to cry desperately, "you don't know what kind of things she's done, she's a fucking drug addict, stop protecting the guilty!"

The two boys exchange glances with each other, before bursting into fits of laughter. Sunwoo drops the helpless woman to the ground again, slamming his foot into her stomach and causing her to cough in agony, "sweetheart, we're all guilty here."

Through the tears in her eyes from the pain she was suffering, she looks up again at the faces of the two boys. It seems to wash over her, as she processes exactly what he meant, exactly who they were.

"You're all fucking disgusting," she hisses, "you people don't know what she's done, the amount of guys she's been with when she left-"

Gunshot.

Right in her stomach, causing the loudest scream of the night to leave her lips. Soomi was glad that no authorities or anyone could hear it thanks to the fact there was so much commotion from the arena. It was louder than physically possible for a human to scream.

Finally, she'd shut up after that, probably unconscious at this point.

Turning to Jaehyun, Sunwoo throws him the gun with a sigh, "Throw this bitch in the car, I know where we can take her," they both knew what he was referring to thanks to the sorts of people he was associated with.

But there was written confusion on her brother's face as he nods, before looking at what he was doing - walking away, "where are you going?"

Sunwoo wasn't walking away from them, he was just walking away from Jaehyun, and towards Soomi.

Noticing what he was doing then, the eldest brother shuts his mouth, shaking his head as a grin spreads over his lips. He bends down to pick up the unconscious girl, making his way to the car in a rush so he could chunk her in the back of the trunk before anyone had a chance to see.

"Sunwoo, I-" she starts, not even knowing what words she should say, what could she say? Where could she even begin?

"Solar," he breaths as if he'd just come up from water after drowning, as if he'd been drowning for years, for a decade. His arms wrap around her tiny, shaky body, and he pulls her forward until she hits his chest. "Fuck, I missed you."

She closes her eyes to intake the moment, her arms going under his leather jacket to wrap around his torso. Breathing deeply, she had to keep telling herself she wasn't dreaming, that this was really happening. She never thought she would see him again, or any of her siblings, but here they were, right in front of her. They saved her life, again, like they always had when she needed it most. 

Trying not to cry, she inhales the familiar scent of smoke and cologne. It was a pleasant mix she never minded from him, the boy who she dated for as long as she could remember.

Everyone always thought that Jungkook and Soomi's relationship had been long. They got together in around the end of 2014, dating up for the next few years until the scandal in the end of 2017. But Soomi had known Sunwoo since they were kids, and they'd practically gotten together in middle school.

She never thought about him when she left, she couldn't, it made her sad. And when she met Jungkook, she'd found a way to forget the boy she once adored by falling for another.

But here he was, and now she was in a complete tangle.

"I'm sorry," she blurts when they finally pull away, seeing Jaehyun was back from putting up the body now, "I'm sorry for disappearing like that, for never calling, never letting anyone know where I went," she pauses, trying to stop her eyes from tearing up and looking to her feet. Soomi was never one to cry, at least the Soomi that they knew. Back then, she was as strong as a rock because that was how she was raised to be. But now, crying was so common for her, but she didn't want to look weak in front of them.

"I was just so broken from what happened, when Chaerin and Juyeon blamed it all on me," she admits, blinking away her tears at the sky and forcing herself to go on, "I wasn't welcome in our house anymore, they told me I was a failure to the family, so I thought it'd be best if I just - left."

The two frown at the sight and her confession. 

"It's okay, little sis," Jaehyun says, wrapping his arm around her and pulling her into one of those big bear hugs he was always known for, "I'm sorry that I didn't do more, I should've told them the truth, that all of us were involved and not just you."

"It's okay, Jaehyunnie," she could only mutter into his chest, "it's okay."

"Mother and father realized their mistake," he blurts as their hug ends, and Soomi's eyes widen from his statement, "the house has never been the same, you can tell they're bitter and unhappy since the day you disappeared, and the fact that little Yein is growing up to look just like you has only made them resent her."

Her heart is a shattered mess on the floor when she hears this, she could only frown and shake her head, "oh my god."

"I don't spend a lot of time at the house anymore, I try to work long shifts so I don't have to," her brother wasn't done with the confession, though, as he goes on, "it's always constant screaming, always Yein crying, and she'll always ask me the same thing, when is big sissy coming home?"

Trying not to cry again, she couldn't help but ask, "what about Chae and Ju?"

Fixing his hair, he sighs as he looks to the sky. His same green eyes twinkle under the sunlight, and he thinks for a moment, "they miss you, they're usually just as bitter as mother and father, probably from the guilt," he hums, "Chaerin only yells when your name is mentioned, and Juyeon just locks himself up in his room."

Her mouth is open from the shock, "wow, I-I would've never have thought-"

"- Thought what, that we didn't miss you? Of course we do, Sol, every single fucking day."

Sunwoo's sudden snapping voice causes her to look back to him. His own brown eyes are glassy when they meet, and she lets out a shaky breath, "I'm sorry," she mutters again, "I'm sorry I hurt you guys."

"Stop apologizing," Jaehyun demands, nudging her shoulder gently, "and just come home, Sol."

She purses her lips from hearing his sudden offer. 

Come home, Sol.

Something she never thought she would hear, she'd heard so many things she would have never sought being true. To think she should've called out to Chaerin all those years ago, she should have talked to her. 

Even though she wanted to now, more than anything, she couldn't deny the life she had now. Looking down at her phone to check the time, she lets out a deep breath, "I-I can't now, I have a show," her eyes wander back to them, and she notices the pieces of paper in Sunwoo's pocket. Her eyes light up at the sight, "you guys bought tickets, didn't you? That's how you got here, so uh," she hesitates, a bit worried about what they would think about her music. But since they were here and they found her in the first place, it was clear they had heard it already. So, with a sigh, she grabs both of their hands and drags them towards the building, "come watch my stage!"



67:00 | resentment

"Where is she?!" he's crying frantically as he steps outside. The January air slaps the caramel boy across the face, and there were beads of sweat on his face still. When he and Jungkook came back from the producer's room, they just managed to sneak back before the actual KCON workers had been back to resume the show. With the show resuming, the next artist to perform had been iKON. Jinhwan didn't have a choice but to go on stage, but it was miserable performing when he knew she could be in danger. This psycho was no joke, there was no telling what she would try, and he knew poor Soomi wasn't that strong even when she acted like it.

Jungkook was just standing outside on the porch, and Jinhwan only grows frustrated when he holds out an arm, stopping him and preventing him from going any further.

Confused, he follows the maknae's gaze, landing on the girl. Soomi's brown hair was blowing in the wind, her green eyes glistening under the sunlight. There's a bright, wide smile on her face as she's hugging two guys he didn't know. The sight caused a pit to form at the bottom of his stomach, was she seeing other people that weren't him and Jungkook?

He didn't think Soomi was bad person for still loving Kook, he understood her situation. But would she really be with someone else as well? 

"Chill out," Jungkook says when he notices the bitter look on Jinhwan's face, "that's her brother."

"Her brother?" he repeats in surprise.

"Yeah," he hums, "but I don't know who the other guy is."



///




"Come on!" she's urging cheerily, dragging the two boys along with her whether they liked it or not. Her heels, that she'd put back on, click against the concrete, and she kept having to check the time every so often as they make their way towards the back entrance. Whether the security liked it or not, these two were coming in with her, and she would see to that.

"I can't believe Solar is seriously an idol," Jaehyun had been chuckling behind her with Sunwoo, "Sunwoo told me and I told him he was fucking crazy."

At the mention of her life now, she looks back to her ex boyfriend, raising a brow, "how did you find me, anyway?"

"Well," he clears his throat, looking a bit nervous to tell the story, "I ran into Yein at the ice cream parlor the other day, and she started telling me about the groups she watches," he begins, and Soomi's eyes widen as she listens to his story regarding her youngest sister, "so, she was saying how I should listen to this song called Only You, and I did when I got home."

Her breath hitches in her throat from what just came out of his mouth. Her little sister listened to her and Jinhwan's song? 

"I don't guess Yein ever recognized you, but the second I saw your face on the screen it hit me."

"Talk about a weird way to reunite," Jaehyun is muttering, scratching his head. A sigh leaves his lips, and he steps forward to walk by Soomi, wrapping an arm around her shoulders as he gushes, "but I'm proud of you, sis, you've really created something great."

She sends a thankful grin to her brother, "thanks, Jaehyunnie."

"Soo!"

It was a topic she had yet to talk about, her new name. Hearing it was odd to the two boys walking beside her, and they suspiciously look up at the sight of two new souls running at them.

Clearing her throat as they stop before her, their eyes going from her, to who she was with, she realized just what kind of situation she was in. 

"Jungkook, Jinan," she hums out nervously, "hi guys."

"Who was it?" Jungkook is the first to ask, "you caught them, right?"

She purses her lips in a thin line. This was the tricky part of the entire new situation that had been created. Her plan hadn't been to hurt the psycho, at least not as bad as her brother and ex had. Something like a weapon wasn't supposed to of been involved, she just wanted to send them to jail, although her anger had began to get the best of her back there. It happened to Jaehyun, too, she guessed it ran in the family.

Or maybe it was from their years of doing so many drugs.

Either way, she was dreading telling Jinhwan and the other boys of iKON. How would they react to hearing their own manager was behind all of this?

"Uh, yeah, I caught her," she admits, not meeting their eyes as she was worried they would notice how worried she was, "she's not gonna be a problem anymore."

Jinhwan is confused at her demeanor, "but who was it? Did you call the police?"

Sunwoo and Jaehyun were confused beside her. They didn't have any clue how deep this was, or how serious it had gotten. All they knew was that some woman was about to have blown Soomi's head off, and that was not okay where they were from. As far as either of them were concerned, she had it coming. 

But the two didn't know nor think that she had any relation to either of the random boys standing before them. Death was something that was taken lightly in the upper East side, at least in situations like this, it was no big deal.

"Are you talking about the lumpy bimbo that was trying to shoot my sister?" Jaehyun blurted, and Soomi grabs his arm, trying to signal for him to shut his mouth, but her older brother was always straightforward, "if either of you are concerned, I popped a few caps in her so she would shut her fucking mouth, she looked like some dark-haired jealous slut."

The two boy's faces grow pale from how casually he just said that. A humorous look takes over Sunwoo's face as he looks to them quizzically, "what, did you guys have something to do with her? I'd sure hope you don't being as she was about to hurt her."

Blinking, Jungkook's eyes go from Jaehyun to Sunwoo. He wasn't sure how he felt right then, he always tried to remain cool and collected, like nothing could bother him. But right now, he couldn't help but ask in confirmation, "I'm sorry, did you say - you shot her?"

An impatient sigh leaves Jaehyun's lips, and he's busy taking out a cigarette and a lighter as he mutters in response, "yeah, that's what I said. It's not okay for someone to threaten my little sister's life."

"What are you going to do with the body?"

"Oh, I've got someone I can take it to," Sunwoo hums, "if she's actually dead, that is, if not - they'll probably make use of her in some other way."

Soomi wrinkles her nose from realizing what he said. Slapping his shoulder, she groans, "can you not mention stuff like that? Talk about disturbing."

There's a playful grin on his lips, and he only wraps an arm around her shoulders, "sorry, princess."

Jungkook's mouth nearly fell open, and his eyes light up with anger. Jinhwan couldn't help but look up from the name that fell from his lips as well, having had been in deep thought about everything. They shot her, and he had noticed even after their stage, his manager was still nowhere to be seen. He tried and tried to think of some other way, but the coincidence seemed so right. Was the woman his manager?

"Sorry, but who the fuck are you?" Jungkook couldn't hold it in anymore when he blurts this out, his voice coming out a lot ruder than he intended. He couldn't hide the fact it bothered him that this guy was all over Soomi. It was bad enough he had to deal with Jimin for all those years, and now this Jinhwan guy. How many more could be added to the mix?

The boy wasn't even slightly intimidated by his tone, and that only annoyed Jungkook even more. When Soomi told him about her family and past life in New York, he didn't imagine them being like this. She always said they were rich and upper class, but they didn't seem that way, at least not from these two.

"I'm her ex boyfriend of seven years, and you?" Sunwoo smirks as he tauntingly asks, and both Jinhwan and Jungkook almost fainted.

Seven years. 

"What?" Jungkook blinks, looking to Soomi for any confirmation on this absurd statement, "is that true?!"

Holding a hand to her head, she wanted to sink into the ground as she sighs, "yeah, I dated him before I left to Korea."

The two newest love interests didn't know what to say. Too much was happening, and Jungkook rubs his temples as he replies to Sunwoo's previous question after receiving the answer from Soomi, "great, well I'm her ex fiance, nice to meet you."

For the first time, Sunwoo showed emotion as a bewildered expression took over his face. Jaehyun looked to him, and they exchanged the same shocked glance, trying to figure out if they just heard this kid correctly.

"Did you just say ex fi-"

"- Okay, well I have a stage to do, so why don't we all talk further afterwards?!" 





///





There's an uncomfortable silence at the dinner table. The only sound was the clinking of silver ware against plates and every so often chewing noise and sip of a drink. The now family of five sits at the table that seated seven, nobody looking at each other as they all only eat their dinner prepared for them.

Juyeon is silent, something that used to be very odd coming from him but had turned into the norm in the last five years. That same, playful boy was still somewhere inside of him, and he came out sometimes, but he was covered by a field of gray clouds that never seemed to go away. And poor Juyeon knew exactly what that field was; guilt.

Meanwhile, Chaerin had been trying to just peacefully eat her own food, but she could never seem to do that. The sound of that same stupid song Yein had been listening to for weeks now only continued to repeat itself and that same chorus, she wanted to slam her head into the table. Since she was sitting right next to her little sister, she could hear the song as if she was listening to it herself since the young girl had the volume so loud on her headphones.

"Where's Jaehyun?" mother had spoke up as she swallows a bite of her food.

None of the siblings look up. Juyeon was on his phone, Yein couldn't hear because of her music, and poor Chaerin just wanted to eat her food for once.

Noticing their mother's glare from lack of reply, the two siblings mutter almost in unison, "I don't know."

"Work," Chaerin offers with a shrug.

But their mother only furrows her brows, dabbing her face with the napkin in her lap before shaking her head, "he doesn't work on Friday's."

Still uninterested, Chaerin sighs, "then I don't know, mother."

Hoping that would be the end of it, her mother purses her lips before demanding, "call him."

Setting her fork down, it hits the plate with a clash. "Why do I have to?" she's complaining in annoyance, but her mother just gives her a look of annoyance. 

"Can you just please call your brother, Chaerin?" her father had spoke up, tired of hearing the girl always complaining. She'd been that way all her life, but ever since the disappearance, she'd been more negative than anyone could recall.

Heaving out a frustrated sigh, she pulls her phone out of her pocket. In their house, it wasn't appropriate to have your electronics at the table, at least that's what it was like back in the day. But now, all the children were using theirs, practically glued to them.

Holding the phone to her ear, she listens to it ring with a dull look on her face. The echo of that song kept repeating once again, and she balls her other fist that wasn't holding the phone at her side. It was hard for Chaerin to control herself, and feeling more upset than she could handle, she reaches up to rip the headphones off her little sister's head, "Yein, could you turn that down, please?!"

The little girl frowns from her big sister being mean to her. Not meeting her sister's flaming eyes, she grabs her headphones back, pausing the song she was listening to only momentarily. 

She wasn't surprised by her sister lashing out. The thing about Chaerin, was she did this often. Yein had learned to get used to it all, but it didn't mean she wouldn't cry about it. In fact, her sister and family being this way only made her cry more. 

Yein just didn't get it. She didn't get why they had to be like this, why didn't they look for her? All she ever wanted was to see her big sister again, her real big sister. Solar was never mean to Yein, she was the only one who would help her with her homework and play dolls with her. Without her, Yein was failing in school, and her mother kept yelling at her about it. But no one would ever help.

She could ask her biggest brother, but he was never home anymore.

Still not being done with her food, Yein doesn't say anything to Chaerin. She only silently puts her headphones back on, unpausing the song and trying to mind her own business.

Meanwhile, Chaerin was on the verge of breaking her phone from anger. Jaehyun never answered her calls, and it ticked her off. Her parents always asked her to do everything, and all the other siblings got off for free. Why was Jaehyun allowed to never be home but no one else could leave the house anymore?!

She wished she could go back to how it used to be, to being out every night with her siblings. Chaerin wished she could let go how she used to.

But now, she had no way to control her everlasting anger. 

"Turn it off already!" she cries, ripping the headphones off her sister's head again and throwing them across the room before Yein could take them back, "why the hell do you like that stupid song so much?!"

There are tears welling in her little sister's eyes when she looks to her. Their parents were heaving out deep sighs, and their father excuses himself from the table, not in the mood to listen to the siblings argue. As for mother, she knew it was helpless to do anything, but tried anyway as she calls, "Guys, let's not do this, please."

That song was still playing from Yein's headphones that laid on the floor, and it echoes through the room as silence overcomes the siblings. Realizing how she made her little sister feel, guilt begins to take over Chaerin's face. She couldn't help it, her anger controlled itself.

She opens her mouth to say something, but her little sister hugs the tablet to her chest that she watched the song on like it was her most prized possession. "I like this song," she sniffles, "because it reminds me of my big sister."

With that, she turns and picks up her headphones, putting them on her head again before running off upstairs. In her room, she makes sure to lock the door so nobody could bother her anymore. Yein had no wish to speak to anyone in her family anymore.

As tears stream down her face, she walks over to her bed. Grabbing a picture off her nightstand, she climbs on the pink duvet and crosses her legs, holding the photo while she stares at the picture. She could only peer down at the photo as she cries, listening to the same song over and over. Yein had yet to put her finger on it, but something about the girl's voice in her song always made her think of her big sister, it was why she couldn't stop listening to it.

And she never would, not until she found her again.


68:00 | the breakdown

"So, how exactly did you meet Soomi?" Jinhwan asks as casually as he could manage. The booming of the song that the girl was performing was heard loudly from all the speakers in the arena, and there were what felt like millions of screams as she sang the well-known song. It was astonishing for the two old friends to watch from someone like her. Never did they think when she left she would go in such a direction, but there she was, with a hundred people falling at her feet to praise her.

Although neither of them cared for this kind of music, there was no denying the talent that the small girl named Soomi, or should they say Solar, held.

Sunwoo had been staring at the girl from backstage, still trying to really piece it all together. Even though he was the one who found her thanks to Yein, it was so hard to grasp. His girlfriend of seven years, a girl he grew up, a girl stuck in the horrible chain of family drama in the upper East side. The way it always seemed to be was you didn't have a getaway if you were born into the richest family's of New York, you had to live through it and carry on whatever business they left to you, the family legacy, whether it was legal or not.

With the business profit only rising in a lively place like New York, it was often that the biggest family's went head to head. Everyone wanted to make the most money, get the most fame, have the state fall at their feet; but some family's wanted such so badly they tended to get dirty. It was exactly what Sunwoo was dragged into, and what he thought Solar would be drug into as well.

It seemed, in a way, she saved herself from having to deal with it. The entire drug scandal had almost been good as he watched her bright face now. But he still couldn't help but wish it hadn't happened. Just because everything was okay now, or would be, didn't mean he hadn't been broken for the last five years.

A sigh leaves his lips as he manages to tear his eyes away from her, looking back down to the really short guy he'd met. All he knew about him was he was the other guy in the song that Yein loved to listen to, being in some idol group he didn't care about. You wouldn't catch someone like Sunwoo listening to that genre of music, although he might get into Soomi's.

He was still in shock from hearing she was apparently engaged to the last guy, who had disappeared somewhere in the back. All he said was he had to get ready, before leaving, and no one really cared to ask anything else.

She was going to marry him, he scoffs, unable to help the jealousy that boiled inside of him, wonder what happened.

Even though he knew who Jungkook was since he introduced himself as her ex fiance, he was still completely clueless on this Jinhwan guy. He figured it was just a good friend of hers or something, so he didn't mind answering his question. 

"I grew up with her," he informs, eyes wandering back to the exact girl he was talking about, "our family's went head to head in the business world, and they were friends at one point in time, so we hung around a lot as kids," as he goes on, Jinhwan was listening intently. This guy, her ex boyfriend, and her older brother were insight to what her life had really been like. Hearing the fact they had killed whoever the woman was, who he still needed to ask Soomi about, wanting to know if he put the pieces together correctly, blew his mind more than words could explain. 

Never would he have guessed she would've dated a guy like this, or that her siblings would have been like this. They killed that woman in cold blood, but at the same time, Jinhwan didn't really blame them. Not that he had ever killed anyone or used a gun in the first place, but if she was really about to end Soomi's life, it had to be done. As far as he was concerned in the end, whoever it was deserved it for everything they caused.

The only worry was if they would be able to normally dispose of the body. If they did, would it really be safe wherever it was? Would they be able to keep the secret forever? 

"This fucktard had a crush on her for as long as I could remember," Jaehyun snorts from beside Sunwoo, who only shoots him a glare. Not bothered by it, he only giggles.

"Anyway," Sunwoo clears his throat in annoyance from her elder brother's words, before continuing with his story, "this massive fight broke out in our families, and we were forbidden to see each other, except as school. Solar and I always remained friends despite the rules, and we ended up doing a lot of dumb shit together."

He smiles from the nostalgia, his eyes twinkling under the backstage lights as he watches the girl move on the stage like it was what she was made to do, "We started dating around that time, my family was always about being dirty. I always dragged her around to parties, trying to liven her up because she was controlled by her family so badly, and she got hooked on something this guy gave her," it was off topic at this point, but Sunwoo figured the story wasn't a big deal to share. Little did he know how serious it actually was to her.

"What exactly is something?"

"Drugs, little guy, he's talking about drugs," Jaehyun says, and there's a tint of anger that forms in Jinhwan's eyes from the mocking tone and the incriminating name. The last thing he wanted to do was start anything with them, though, so he keeps anything he was feeling inside. "Fuck, all of us got hooked on it, Chaerin was the worst, especially when she didn't get her fix right away," he's muttering out complaints, completely interrupting Sunwoo's story telling time, "it was like she'd go fucking insane - literally bitches about everything."

Jinhwan purses his lips in a straight line, only knowing to listen. Drugs, partying, he couldn't see a girl who seemed as innocent as Soomi doing that. But he guessed he should've figured she wasn't innocent from the beginning.

"It was fun, back then," Sunwoo shares a second later when Jaehyun had finally closed his mouth, "until we got caught - and that was it."

"Ah, the year of 2013," Jaehyun sighs as he recalls it in his mind.

Trying to understand what he meant by that was it, Jinhwan raises a brow, "is that why she left?"

Sunwoo chuckles, looking to Jaehyun as if asking who should tell the story. Since he knows it the best, he thinks for a second before divulging, "well, it goes a lot deeper than that."





///





"Fuck, this is so exciting!"

"Hey, fuckwad, stop hogging it!" the girl whines, snatching the bag out of the boy's hand. Not satisfied with just that, she takes the beer from his hand as well, taking out a few pills from the bag. Her green eyes brighten with joy at the mere sight, before she hurriedly leans her head back, swallowing the pills with excitement.

The boy beside her was left gawking at the fact she just did that. Nobody had gotten to even try any, and it was clear the girl was crazed as she chugged the beer bottle in her hand. An offended look plasters over his face, as he takes the bag back from her hands. 

Sticking his hand in it, he's taking out some pills of his own. Bouncing in the seat in excitement as the BMW continues to cruise down the road, he's raising them to his mouth, about to have the sweet relief-

"- Hey, idiots, you can't have any yet, I told you!" the oldest girl snaps as she'd turned around to see what they were doing. Leaning towards the back seat, she grips his wrist, taking the pills he was about to swallow and putting them back in the bag before she takes it, too.

A look of bewilderment is left on the two's faces as she sets it in her bag, as far away from her siblings as possible.

"Solar, I wanted more!" Chaerin screeches like a baby wanting milk. It was clear she had a problem, and nobody knew how to help her. All of them were addicted, in a way, but no one was as bad as her.

Wanting to slam her head into the window when the girl only starts complaining about how she didn't have a right to take them from her, Sunwoo chuckles in amusement from where he was driving. One of his hands goes to Solar's thigh, where he caresses it with his thumb to keep her from going insane.

"I told you," she heaves out a sigh at the fact she was still having to repeat it to her sister, "they're for tomorrow."

Juyeon groans from his seat beside Chaerin, "she got to try them and I didn't, look at her," he jolts a finger to the brunette, who was already looking as if she was in a wonderland as she was nearly drooling on herself, "she's already experiencing the highs!"

Solar rubs her temples when she turns to see what he was talking about. How the hell were they going to go home with her like that? She should've expected her younger siblings to do some shit like this. You really couldn't ever trust them with anything.

When she doesn't say anything to his statement, Juyeon leans forward to perch his head on the shoulder of the seat she sat in. His same green eyes twinkle in hope as he looks up to her, not noticing nor caring how hard she was obviously trying to ignore him, "let me try one, please, Sol?"

Her little brother had a certain tone he would use whenever he wanted to get something. Thanks to his good looks and how most people found him irresistible, it usually worked, on everyone that wasn't his big sister.

"No," she deadpans, not even bothering to look at him.

Frowning from her denial, his back hits the leather seat as he crosses his arms over his chest, "talk about fucking unfair."

Tired of the commotion, Sunwoo finally speaks up in disappointment, "you two really need to get a grip, you should be fucking glad she gives you fucking anything with the way you treat her." His deep, booming voice no doubt always scared them. They all knew the sort of stuff Sunwoo's family did, and the sort of stuff he would do, especially if someone ever hurt Solar. The boy got flaming whenever anyone even hit on her or looked at her wrong, you could only imagine what he'd do if someone actually were to attempt her hurt her.

It'd be a shitty day for them, that's for sure.

The two youngest siblings don't say anything to his harsh lecture. They knew not to, which was the only smart thing they ever did.

Finally, the familiar mansion comes into view. There's a nervous pit that forms at the bottom of her stomach as he parks the car at the curb. Closing her eyes for a split moment, it was the first time Solar Kim had ever felt even the slightest amount of worry in her life. Something like worrying wasn't what she liked to use her time doing, but now she couldn't help it. Looking back at her little sister, it looked like she was about to have a seizure from the way she kept mumbling to herself.

How strong were those drugs?

"You sure it'll be okay?" Sunwoo's concerned as he stops her by grabbing her wrist gently, just as she's opening the car door, and preventing her from getting out yet.

Her eyes were unsure as they look to him, but knowing they didn't have a choice but to go home before they cut curfew, she forces a smile. "I think we'll be fine," she tries to assure him, but he only peers at her with disbelief written on his face.

"Maybe you should just wait it out."

Although that was a good idea, she shakes her head, "I can't, Sunwoo, we have to go home now or we'll be in deep shit."

A sigh leaves his lips, and he lets go of her wrist, knowing she was right. Solar only dully grins as she fully climbs out of the car, throwing her bag lazily over her shoulder as she does so. Her siblings were just climbing out, too, and before they could get inside before her, she peeks her head into the car, "I'll see you soon, alright?"

That baby like smile finds a way on his face even through his worry, "okay, baby, I love you."

"I love you too," she hums dreamily, before seeing that Chaerin and Juyeon were already halfway across the yard. Knowing she had to hurry, she rushes to call, "okay, bye!" before slamming the door, calling for her siblings to wait.

Standing on the porch moments later, she holds a hand to her head as she looks to the bitter Juyeon and the mental Chaerin. "Can you guys - please - act normal?" she pleads with them, and the two only peer at her like she was speaking a different language.

Being that she was standing in front of the door, blocking their path from going inside, they had to reply or she wouldn't let them in. Solar shows her determination further when she crosses her arms, staring at them intently.

"Sure, whatever."

"Ugh, I'm fucking on cloud nine right no-"

"- Chaerin, seriously, suck it up."

"Okay, fucking go inside already!" her sister abruptly cries. 

Rolling her eyes at how dramatic she was whether she was under drug influence or not, Solar spins on her heel. Twisting the door knob, with how those two were acting right now, she had a bad feeling about this. The only hope she had left was if her parents were somewhere upstairs and her siblings could sneak away to their rooms safely.

She's the first one to step inside that night. The house seemed quiet. There was no commotion, no sound of Yein playing any games, and no calls of her parents hearing them enter the house.

Solar spoke too soon.

"You're home!" her mother hums, entering the entryway with her father trailing behind shortly after.

Slapping a smile on her face, Solar's the first to greet her parents, "hi mother, father."

Juyeon mumbles a greeting, before pushing past everyone. He's walking towards the steps, or more like stomping, and it was clear he was still ticked off about her not letting him try any of the new drug she'd gotten.

There's a confused look on their faces when Chaerin didn't say anything. Solar was trying to stand in front of her so they didn't see what kind of state she was in, but her plan was deflated when the girl steps out, trying to casually walk to her room.

"Chaerin Kim," her father monotones the second she was a foot towards the steps, "stop right there."

"Yes, father?"

She was trying to act normal, but it wasn't working at all. Solar buries her face in her hands as she witnesses the situation, wanting to be sick from what was going on.

"What's wrong with you?" mother's blinking in confusion, "are you sick?"

A blank look takes over Chaerin's face. Her eyes are full of panic as they look to Solar, who was staring back at her with the same emotion. Trying to think fast, the eldest sister hurriedly steps forward, trying to drag her sister upstairs and ignore what she asked, when a plop echoes through the quiet entryway.

The three siblings all slowly turn to see what the sound was.

Before anyone could jump to grab the bag, it was too late. The only thought in anyone's mind was oh no.

As if her life was flashing before her eyes, her father stares down at the bag he was now holding, trying to hold in his anger as he looks up to her, "Solar, what are these?!"

She was fucked.

"Uh, I - I'm sorry," she starts, not being able to find any words to defend herself. What could she say when she was blatantly caught in the act?

Her mother shakes her head in disappointment, looking to Juyeon and Chaerin who were trembling from fear, "do either of you know about this?"

Solar looks at her siblings with wide eyes, waiting for them to help her. After all, Chaerin was literally under the influence of the drugs, surely she wouldn't say she was just sick. It was clear there was a bigger problem than some sort of sickness. And Juyeon couldn't still be that mad about her not letting him try one.

"No, we had no idea she had those."

"What?!" she cries instantly once such words roll of her brother's tongue. Solar's fists clench at her side, and her eyes burn as she looks to him with shock, "what the fuck do you mean?!"

"Solar!" her father yells, warning her to stop speaking.

Having no choice but to close her mouth, everyone looks to Chaerin. The girl was having a big problem, both mentally and physically. Whatever that drug was was strong and it was keeping her from thinking straight. She had no idea what was going on at that point, as she holds a hand to her head, when Juyeon nudges her, "come on, save yourself," he whispers, and she only knew to listen.

"I don't know what those even are, mother."

"Chaerin, how could you?!" Solar gawks in disbelief, unable to process what her siblings had really just done to her.

Her parents both heave out dull sighs. Never had she seen such immense amounts of sadness in their eyes, and her mother grabs her arm, dragging her away as she calls to her younger siblings, "go upstairs, you two."

Not knowing what else to do, the two only scuttle upstairs in fear of what they just did to their older sister. Only seconds later did the screaming echo through the entire house, bouncing off each wall and booming through their ears even though they were a floor away from it. Chaerin's eyes tear up from the way the drug was making her feel, and she slides down to the floor against the wall, trying to tune out the yelling from her parents downstairs.

Juyeon was a shaky mess of emotions. There's a concerned look on his face, he looked horrified from everything he was hearing and was actually able to process unlike Chaerin.

"How could you put such disappointment upon this family?! I saw the articles, I wanted to believe they weren't true which was why we were waiting for you to get home, but here you are with fucking drugs!"

What had he done?

"It'll take me forever to get rid of all the news about this, you're a fucking disgrace to this legacy, and frankly, I don't want you apart of it anymore!"

The sudden creak of the door opening was heard, and a confused Jaehyun comes out, followed by tiny Yein. 

"What the hell is going on down there?" he asks the two, noticing the fearful state they were in and only growing more worried as he instantly pieced it together when he heard a portion of the yelling, "what the fuck did you two do to Solar?!"

"I-I'm sorry, I didn't know it would be this bad," Juyeon tries to say, but his older brother didn't care.

Pushing past him, Jaehyun didn't care for what he had to say. He was too focused on the heavy footsteps coming into the entryway, with a curious afraid Yein following him like a lost puppy.

"Get the fuck out, from this day on I don't consider you a part of this family," her father was screaming, his eyes bright red from the undeniable anger within his body. He was kicking and throwing anything in his way, and glass hits each wall, shattering and flying everywhere. "You fucking disgust me!"

At the sight of his sister finally, she didn't even look his way as she ran straight for the door. Only having her purse, she hurriedly grabs the handle, twisting it in a rush.

"Hey, Solar!" he cries, not knowing what was going on but wanting to fix it, "don't leave!" he yells, before looking to his father in disappointment, "why would you say that?! What's wrong with you?!"

Breathing heavily from his outrage, his father only glares back at him. His chest heaves up and down, and he didn't actually have any concept of what truly just came out of his mouth. In reality, it'd been his anger overcoming him, he didn't know what he was even saying from how it controlled him.

But it was too late for him to realize this.

"Solar, fucking wait!" Jaehyun cries when he steps onto the porch.

His sister was running across the yard, sobs echoing in the distance. She had yet to look back at them, as she waves her hand in the air desperately. A cab stops by the curb, and she opened the door without a second thought.

Not giving up, he runs as fast as he could across the massive front yard of the Kim mansion, "Sol, don't go!"

Just as she's climbing in the car, about to close the door, her green eyes wander back to her older brother. The only sibling she ever had that took care of her, it broke her heart. It all broke her heart.

Shaking her head, she climbs into the car, suppressing her emotions. 

And she left, she left the family of six there. Jaehyun had tried to run after the cab, but it was a human versus a car, and it didn't look to be stopping anytime soon.

Realizing what had happened, he's on fire from his boiling frustration. He knew exactly what had happened from what he heard, even if it was only a portion. And he couldn't believe any of them would do it. 

His fists clench at his side as he turns to head back towards the front door. 

"Big brother!" Yein was screeching as she runs across the yard, being still around six or seven years old. He knew he couldn't get mad at her, and he sighs, trying to hold in the tears that wanted to spill from his eyes as she stops before him with curiosity beaming in her green eyes, "where is big sissy going? When's she coming back?!"

It took him a moment to find the strength to reply. He didn't know what he should say, but it was probably best not to answer at all.

"Come on, Yein, let's go inside."


69:00 | clarification

"Sunwoo!" There's beads of sweat dripping from her skin, hair sticking to her face from the workout she had just had on the stage of KCON. Her exhaustion was the least of her worries, however. In fact, she had a blast on the stage of KCON knowing the psycho was finally taken care of. Even though she had so many obstacles left to face, she let go and spent time with her fans. It was a moment Soomi was grateful for.

What she wasn't thrilled about was walking backstage once again only to hear the words that were coming out of her ex boyfriends mouth.

"We were in some deep shit, the business my father ran was a lot deeper than anything actually legal. Not that I enjoy doing what I have to do, but the drugs back then were only part of the fun, you-" He cuts himself off when he sees the familiar brunette bolting up to him, noticing the flames in her eyes. Like always, though, Sunwoo's face remains blank as he looks down at her small figure, "you okay, sweetheart? Your performance was cute."

Wanting to pull out her hair, she tried not to let herself get bothered by his comment. Her highschool sweetheart had always had a way with her, and she holds a hand to her head to resist from running out the door and laying in the road to get hit by a car.

"What did you just tell him?" She demands, worry dripping from her tone. It was likely he and her brother just blurted out her entire life story to Jinhwan — which was not good. She wasn't planning to tell him that anytime soon, and if she did, she wanted it to be from her, not her ex boyfriend.

The topic taunting her mind raises a brow, his eyes studying her carefully. A knowing look spreads over his face, and he's smirking as he looks back to Jinhwan, "did you not know anything about her background?"

Realizing what was happening, she heaves out a sigh. Jinhwan scratches the back of his head, looking rather awkward as he tries to process everything, "uh, not that in depth, no."

She could tell from his tone how much he was judging her. In the end, she couldn't really blame him. Jinhwan had no idea what kind of person she was, he didn't know the things she was involved in, that she did herself. Neither did any of the others, and it was for good reason. She knew their reactions would be like this if they learned about it. Things like this that may be slightly common in the upper east side were foreign to a place like Korea.

"Jinhwan, I'm sorry I never told you everything, really," is all she knows to say. It wasn't like she could defend herself, the story Sunwoo told was probably truthful. There was nothing more the poor girl could say.

Pursing his lips, he doesn't show any sign of what he's feeling inside. Finally, offering her a small smile, he shakes his head, "no, it's okay, Soo, I think I just need some time to think."

She didn't have the heart to stop him. If anything, he definitely needed the time anyway.

Turning back to the two, she groans as she narrows her eyes at them, "I can't believe you guys did that!"

"What? It's not like we knew your entire life was sacred to these people," Jaehyun had defended first, his tone stern as if he was lecturing her, "if anything, you should be glad we came clean for you."

Rubbing her temples, she only shakes her head. She hadn't even gotten to enjoy the rush of just finishing a great stage, all because of this everlasting drama.

"My bad, I didn't think it would be a big deal to your friend," Sunwoo had shrugged as he half apologized, but he still sounded pretty emotionless.

That's the thing, she wanted to tell him, we're a little more than just friends.

Soomi didn't even want to think about where Jungkook was. He still didn't know the full story, and she was worried about what he would think after seeing Jinhwan's reaction. Although he'd probably be a lot more lenient since he knew more in the first place — and just knew her in general, she could never be so sure.

There was no telling what was going through Jinhwan's mind. He was probably having rather mixed feelings about her uncovered past. For all she knew, he could hate her guts now.

"Stop looking so stressed, your friend will be fine," Jaehyun steps forward to pat her on the back, before pulling her into a side hug, "cheer up, sis, you're seeing your big brother and — this kid — for the first time in five years."

Gradually, her green eyes wander up to the two that were beaming down at her. Sunwoo had glared at her brother in disappointment, "this kid — really?"

"Well, what the fuck am I supposed to call you?"

"Anything else, fuck-stick."

She snorts at their idiocy, which would get a lot more vulgar than anything she was used to witnessing if she didn't cut in. Wrapping an arm around Sunwoo's neck, she pulls him into the hug with her brother, making it one big group hug.

"I really did miss you guys!" She's gushing, squeezing the life out of them — or trying to — even though they didn't feel a mere pinch from her lack of strength, "you guys have to update me on everything, how is it all going? Did you get into that college, Jae?"

Sunwoo had pulled out of the hug because he felt like it probably looked embarrassing. Instead, he boldly grabs her hand.
