"Yeah, I did," the eldest brother was grinning, "I think that's the only good thing that's happened to me since you left."

She frowns at that, recalling how bad he claimed the family had gotten. "Is it really that horrible at home now?"

"Well, it won't be when I show up with you at my side."

Just hearing him say that was like a wind of pain slapping her in the face. Soomi purses her lips in a thin line, only having to think for a split second before she lightly shakes her head, an apologetic look on her face, "I-I don't think I can do it today," she hesitantly shares, "I've got to check in with my friend's and everything."

"You mean that Jungkook dude?" Jaehyun hums in curiosity, "Does he not know anything either? I was hoping it was a joke when I said your past was sacred."

The look in Soomi's eyes answers his question for him. 

Sighing, he shakes his head dully, "you better get that shit sorted out," he sternly commanded, jolting his finger at her.

Soomi turns to peer around at all the people walking past them, from idols to staffs, but she didn't see any of her friends in sight. Figuring they were all busy back in the dressing rooms, she looks back to the two old friends, "I'm trying to, I didn't exactly know you two were going to show up today. I've been dealing with some other things, you know," she lowers her voice so no one else could hear, leaning forward to snap in a whisper, "hence the woman you fucking shot."

Upon the mention of the subject, they both widen their eyes, having forgotten until then. Sunwoo's shoulders tenses, and he steps forward as if he wasn't close enough to her already, cracking his knuckles as he observes their surroundings, "you said someone was after you, right?" he looks back to the frail girl for confirmation.

"Yeah," he nods slowly, "well, she's not after me anymore, that's for sure."

Despite her joking tone, he didn't break a smile. Sunwoo was always emotionless on the outside, she knew that, ever since she met him as a mere child. But he was full of things on the inside. He just hated showing people what he was feeling, it made him feel vulnerable and weak. The only person he ever granted with knowing his true-self was her, it was why it felt weird to see him acting this way. 

But she guessed she couldn't really complain. It'd been five years, and people change. Sunwoo might not be the same way anymore.

Or mayhaps he just couldn't find the freedom to laugh due to how upset he was about the mention of anyone touching Soomi. He thought when she left that she had gotten away from a world of danger, so hearing that she could still be in it made him beyond upset.

However, a world of a danger meant he was free to cause anyone a world of hurt in return, and he was fine with that - especially if anyone got near Solar.

"Do you know that for sure?" she looks to him in confusion from his sudden question. Seeing she didn't get what he was asking, he clarifies, "do you know it was just her after you? What exactly did she do to you? Most people can't go about things alone when it comes to someone trying to commit murder, especially because you're a celebrity now."

A blank expression takes over her face. Sunwoo exhales deeply upon seeing it, realizing she had yet to think of things like this. Unable to ever get mad at her, though, he only tells her in a stern tone, "you need to tell me everything, okay?"

She wanted to faint upon comprehending how much she had to catch each side of her friends up on. Sunwoo and Jaehyun had to learn all about her life in Korea, and her newest friends had to uncover the truth of her old life in America. 

"Alright," she mutters, "I can just go stay with you or something, do you still have the same place?"

He only nods, and finally, showed any sign of emotion as a giddy grin takes over his lips. "You bet I do, Sol."

Returning the smile of her ex-boyfriend, she nods, "okay, well let me go-"

"-SOOMI, YOU'RE OKAY!"

A shriek of absolute death leaves the petite girls lips, and she sounded like that of a dying animal as she stumbles. Her face was going to hit the ground face-first, when Sunwoo had caught her by her shoulders, steadying her from falling. The boy who was the culprit of it all from attempting to jump on her back with him being a giant, had been giggling away at what happened. Only when he manages to step up straight, did he realize the other two that were with her.

This was especially due to the fact one of them in particular was standing directly before him, eyes red with anger and his face only inches apart from the new soul. He looked like he was bound to beat the life out of Junhoe, not caring to hear anything he had to say. 

Just before he has the chance to spout any words, Solar coughs fakely as she grabs his arm, pulling him back, "Sunwoo, Sunwoo, you can calm down, it's my best friend," she explains hurriedly so he didn't punch Junhoe, who was beyond confused at what was even happening. When his face returns to the usual blank expression, she lets out a sigh of relief before motioning to the tall boy, "meet my best friend, Junhoe."

He peers at the taller boy for a long while upon hearing her say those words, her best friend. Thinking back in time, to the Solar he knew well, he finds himself wrinkling his nose, "Chanhee would be offended."

Jaehyun starts snickering like a child at his words. Solar is left to gawk in shock that he just said that to her, stepping forward, she hits his arm as if he would even feel it, "don't say that!"

Trying to grasp any concept of what was going on, Junhoe grabs her arm, pulling her closer to him so he could whisper, "Soo, who the fuck are the two serial killer looking dudes?"

Holding in her laughter, she keeps a straight face as she pulls away from him. "June-ya, this is Sunwoo, my ex from the States, and my older brother, Jaehyun."

He nods in confirmation, flashing a smile as he introduces himself to them. Junhoe wasn't really shocked from the ex news, since he heard it and plenty of other things from his hyung when he got back to the dressing room. It was actually why he came and found Soomi.

The two exchange hand shakes casually with him, but when it gets to Sunwoo, you could tell the reckless boy was squeezing his hand so hard it looked like it was going to break off. His grip tightens when Junhoe only shoots him a grin, and he had a feeling he must not be a fan of him thus far.

"Okay!" Soomi hums brightly in the best tone she could manage, causing them to break up their hand shake, "Junhoe, I'm glad you're here, I actually needed to talk to you, and for you to pass it on to everyone else."

Pursing his lips, he's massaging his pained hand as he nods, "yeah, I need to talk to you, too."





///




"So, it really was our manager?"

His tone didn't sound sad. It was the last thing she expected as she slowly looks up to meet his eyes again, hesitantly shaking her head up and down, "yeah, yeah, it was her."

"That's weird, but I guess it sort of makes sense."

She only shrugs, "I don't know yet, she could have been working with someone else. Sunwoo's going to find out."

Piping up at the mention, he gently punches her shoulder as he eagerly questions, "so, about this Sunwoo dude, what's with him? He looks like he's straight out of one of those gang movies, I thought everyone from your old life is supposed to be rich people."

She blinks a few times, realizing it was time for her to really tell him everything. Then, before long, everyone else would know when he told them as well. Her life would be an open book to all her friends. They'd know who she really was, what she really did, they'd know Solar.

As she thought about it, maybe it wouldn't be so bad.

"Well, he is rich, and also sort of from a gang, as well," she explains, and the boy's eyes widen before her, "it's hard to explain - in fact, that's what I wanted to talk to you about."

Exhaling deeply, she braces herself for what she was really about to tell him. Really, she should have done it a long time ago.

"So, life in the upper East side wasn't exactly what you all think it was."


70:00 | advocacy

Chim: soo, junhoe sat us down & told us everything!! i'm so sry u had to go thru all of that, i didn't think it was that deep :( 

Chim: but i love u, i know ur not a bad person & u never were, i'll support u forever, mi <3


Yoongles: soomi we heard everything from the crackhead guy

Yoongles: i hope you're pulling through okay, nobody judges you from your hardships. it only makes me see you as a stronger person for getting through them.

Yoongles: have a good time with your family, text me if you ever want to talk.


Namjoonie: you're truly an inspiration, mi. i'm proud of you for pulling through and only getting up whenever anyone knocks you down. don't think this will make us see you any different. getting through that, and now this psycho business only makes us adore you more for how courageous you are as a gorgeous woman!! :)


Hobi: i love u mi!! pls stay strong and u better text me if u ever need a hug!! 


Donghyuk: soo, so we just heard about your life in the states from june. i was really shocked to hear such an angel could come from something so harsh. i'd have to be insane to ever judge you instead of only see you as someone admirable:)

Donghyuk: please don't ever hesitate to talk to us when you need us, we love you, soo!!




The messages from her newer and older friends went on forever. Each one was about the same, how they loved her, didn't judge her, and how they hoped she would be okay through it all. She smiles like an idiot as she reads them all, feeling bad for thinking her friends would ever think of her otherwise.

Although Jinhwan's reaction wasn't the best, she knew he was just overwhelmed. After all, he experienced it all first hand, she couldn't blame him.


kookie: soomi

kookie: i'm sorry for everything, i didn't ever imagine it went that deep. i didn't think your parents would really do that to you, that you really dealt with a struggle like that, and were in a relationship with someone so dangerous.

kookie: even then, sunwoo seems like he cares about you a lot. so does jinhwan. but so do i. so if you go see your family tomorrow, and need help fully clearing your head after that psycho garbage or just want some emotional support

kookie: don't hesitate to call me or even jinhwan, we would love to go there with you

kookie: i talked to him, by the way, he said his phone is dead. but he's not mad at you. he's just shocked from hearing everything.

kookie: and we all are. but in the end, it only makes us realize how much we love and want to protect you

kookie: and soo

kookie: i'm really proud of you

kookie: for everything you've made of yourself

kookie: you're something special

kookie: don't forget that


"Jungkook..." her voice trails off as she stares down at the message. Her heart was on her sleeve, and tears well in her eyes as she kept rereading the things he said over and over.

Soomi's original plan had been to just not talk to her friends for the time being. She thought they were going to judge her, and not want to speak to her for a while so they could have time to really think about what they learned. Just because Junhoe had taken it so well when she told him, hadn't made her think everyone else would.

But they had, and seeing all their loving messages only made her realize that. She was getting through this, she was truly putting her life back together, with so many great friends at her side to help her do it.

Instead of running from her past, she thought, maybe she should have faced it from the beginning. 

However, if she'd done that, she would've missed out on so much.

So mayhaps, what was happening, now maybe this was the right path. Why should she care about the road not taken when this one seemed so glorious?

The car jolting forward from Sunwoo slamming on the break causes her to jump out of her thought bubbles. Her green eyes dare to wander to peer out the window, and she felt a pot of nostalgia pour inside of her at the sight. The old mansion, one Sunwoo had bought for himself so long ago, still looked the same. There were only a few differences to the outside, but it was exactly how she recalled it.

Thinking back to those times, no matter how hard they were, she couldn't deny how happy she had still been through out them. And knowing she was finally achieving real happiness once again, but this time the right way, only made it that much better.

Sunwoo had climbed out of the car a long time ago, which she hadn't noticed since she was too busy basking in her emotions. Her car door is opened, and he holds out a hand to her, peering down at her expectantly. 

Looking up to the boy, she's only thrown into more memories. Sunwoo really was her entire world at one point, much like Jungkook was at one point, too. Each of them, all three, had a special place in her heart that she couldn't deny. But Sunwoo was the only one who had experienced, been there, emerged, in the life that she once had. He knew what it was like because he was in the same boat as her all that time, he went through it with her by his side.

Because of that, because of the bond they formed since the day they met as children, was why she could never tear herself away from him now that he was back in front of her. Soomi wasn't saying she still loved him, because she didn't know what she used to feel for him in the first place. If it was love or not was foreign, but she did know that he'd always made her feel something no one else quite did.

It had been hard to leave, to live without him, when she did. She wondered how exactly he had lived without her, too.

In fact, if she wouldn't of met Jungkook, she might've came back for him.

"Sunwoo?" she blurts out his name in an inquisitive tone just as they're beginning to walk through the grass. He only looks back to her, stopping his movement to face her in wonder. 

"Uh - I wanted to ask about er - your life, you know, after I left," she stutters out, feeling weird asking him something like this.

He didn't seem bothered by the question. As they were already holding one hand, he grabs her other and grips them as if he was scared to let go. Brown eyes twinkling under the night sky that had taken over New York, he stares up above at the stars as he mutters, "lots of work, I was overcome by endless anger when I heard what your parents did to you," he begins, and she swallows the lump in her throat as she could only stare at his features as he spoke.

She really missed looking at him. Sunwoo had always been someone girls foamed at the mouth over ever since they were in middle school. With his defined jawline, pillow-like lips, and bright eyes, he was the ideal type of an casual girl - including her.

"I did job after job, and I killed people out of my anger at your parents for kicking you aside like that," he closes his eyes, as if he was trying to contain himself from getting upset about it even now, "and I was even mad at myself, for not doing something that night you left. Whether it was not letting you go, or just not giving you those fucking drugs in the first place. I blamed myself for everything, because it was my fault you ever touched drugs, making the underlying cause just that - me," she opens her mouth, but he doesn't let her speak as he only takes a short breathe before continuing, "I worried about you everyday, I missed you like hell. I'd go to your house every so often, thinking you might've come back, but no one would ever tell me anything. Chaerin and Juyeon changed, they were always so different, I figured it was because of them having to get off drugs."

"But in reality, it was the fact they didn't want to tell me the real reason you left, was because of them ratting you out instead of taking the fall with you," he scoffs, and he'd started to squeeze her hands out of anger. Remembering he'd hurt her, he calms himself again, "I'm still pissed at them, but I could tell they felt guilty, that they missed you, and what your brother said only proves that further."

Finally, he opens his eyes again, and they stare right into her soul. "So, there you go, Solar Kim, my life has been living hell since the day you left, and there hasn't been a millisecond you weren't on my mind."

She wondered if she could speak now as she only meets his gaze. Till he clears his throat, adding, "it makes me slightly upset you, on the other hand, were busy getting fucking engaged, but that's cool, Sol."

You could tell he wasn't thrilled about that news. She could only imagine his reaction when he heard the rest.

"I'm sorry," she pouts, "I didn't think anyone was having trouble without me, I thought you moved on."

He snorts as if she said something funny, "Solar, I was with you for seven fucking years, I didn't know what life was without you."

Guilt spreads over her like a wildfire. Her eyes avert to her feet, and Sunwoo notices the saddened look. Lifting her chin with a finger, he forces her to meet his eyes again. 

"Don't feel bad, though, okay?" he demands in a stern tone, "I'm not mad at you, you know I can't be, and you're back now," he's grinning again only a second later. His hand falls back to his side, and he lets go of her hand to wrap an arm around her shoulders, "and I hope you know, I'm not letting you go again."

"Kim Sunwoo~!" she's whining as he'd leaned so close to her ear his breathe was sending chills down her skin. Knowing it bothered her, he only giggles as she'd squirmed under his grip. Unable to get out of it since his arm was around her, she groans. Unable to help her temptation, she'd wrapped both of her small arms around his torso as she mumbles, "I missed you, Sunu."

The nickname was one he hadn't heard in years, and it was enough to play with his emotions as they walk onto the porch. He'd shoved the key in the keyhole of the lock so she couldn't see the big smile that spreads on his face.

If he was going to reply, he wasn't gonna get the chance to as the door clicks, signaling it was open. Pushing it open, he leans against it, motioning with one arm to the inside, "welcome home, Sol."

Entering the giant house, the brunette leans her head back. It was a wave of something she couldn't think of a word to describe, not euphoria but not exactly nostalgia either. She just felt ecstatic to be back in a place that brought her so much freedom in a time where she rarely ever had any. A place where she let herself go, found joy, and found herself.

The familiar scent of smoke didn't take long to enter her nostrils. Although she wasn't used to it anymore, it wouldn't take her long to be once again.

"Still the same, huh?" Sunwoo's voice hums from behind her in the dark. There wasn't a single light on in the house.

"Yeah."

A chuckle is heard from him, and she was confused as to why. It wasn't like she said anything funny. Sunwoo walks over to the wall, flicking the light switch, before the room was illuminated before her.

Soomi hadn't been expecting much. He wasn't someone who enjoyed things like interior decorating, so the only decor that had ever been in the house was something she put there. Since it had been so long, she assumed it would look exactly the same.

And it did, but that was the last thing she was focusing on when a dozen bodies jump up from hiding places in the room.

"Surprise, Solar!" they'd all screeched in unison, and the girl's face was of bewilderment.

She would've fainted if she didn't hit Sunwoo's chest when she took a couple steps back. He'd taken that as the chance to wrap his arms around her waist, a giddy smile on his lips as he leans his chin atop her head since he was so much taller than her. 

Her mouth was wide open like a hippo, and she covers it with her hands as she could only gawk at everyone standing before her, "oh my god."




71:00 | evil maknae

"H-How are you guys-," she's trying to say, unable to remove her hands from her mouth because she felt like it was about to fall onto the ground if she did. Soomi's head was spinning, it was like after so long, everything was coming back together. How was she supposed to feel about it? She always told herself she would never seen Sunwoo or anyone else again. Was she really just going to welcome them all into her life once again?

The dangers they threw her into, Sunwoo particularly, would she really let it all happen again? All the pain and suffering had been there, too, along with the happiness she felt because of how hard she'd fallen for him. Back then, she was just a teenager, and she always had her family's protection. Would she still have it now?

And would it be safe for her to grow involved with the illegal acts of someone like Kim Sunwoo, especially when she was a worldwide celebrity?

There's many thoughts that wound inside her head. But none of them diminish the joy that wells in her heart. Tears are forming in her green irises, and Sunwoo squeezes her tightly from behind, setting his chin on her shoulder as he mumbles, "I told them you were back and they really wanted to see you."

Soomi couldn't even look up at him. Sunwoo lets her go at the sight of a boy running at the girl like a mental patient. It was such a rare sight for anyone to see, that anyone else in the room - no matter how excited they were - stepped back and allowed the boy to have first greetings.

Green eyes brightening, she didn't have time to even react when she was practically tackled. She stumbles a bit before catching herself, not having much trouble since they were actually close to the same weight.

"Solar, you're such a bitch, I can't believe you've left me alone with these idiots for so long, do you know the pain I've been put through? Can you imagine having to be around these imbeciles all alone every single day of your life?!" he was rambling as he squeezed the life out of her. Tears were welling in his eyes, too, and Solar couldn't help but cry as well as she finally snaps out of her trance.

Wrapping her arms around his waist, she hugs the familiar boy back as tight as she could manage. "I'm sorry, Hee, I'm really sorry," is all she can say as they stand there in the middle of Sunwoo's home.

"Hey, wait, is no one going to talk about what he said?!" the voice of an offended Haknyeon had spoken up from one of the couches. He'd gotten punched only a second after his words, via Kevin who told him to be quiet and let them enjoy their moment. The punch hadn't affected the boy, though, who only glared at the moon boy beside him on the couch, "what the fuck kind of pussy ass punch was that-"

"-Haknyeon, can you not close your mouth for two seconds?!" Chanhee turns and whines to him in annoyance, his arm still around Solar, "our beloved Solar is back, show some fucking sincerity!"

The boy pouts from his lecture. Solar is left to chuckle at her friends, shaking her head as she walks further into the living room to greet the rest of them. The second they saw her approaching them, everyone jumps to their feet, excited to get their hug.

"Solar, you're finally back!" Jacob was crying cheerily as he hugs her.

One by one, she greeted her old friends, who were all so thrilled to see her that pretty much everyone was shedding tears. No one could comprehend it was her, they all felt like it was some sort of sad dream.

"It's been five years," Sangyeon is humming in awe, "we didn't think you'd ever come back."

She frowns upon hearing the same thing again. "I really thought everyone would forget about me."

"We couldn't even if we wanted to, Sunwoo never stopped talking about you - I mean fuck, we get it, you guys were together for years, but it was every single day-" Eric is cut off by something being thrown at him. It was a cup, and he shrieks as he hurriedly dodges it, causing it to hit the wall behind him and shatter into pieces.

The culprit had only glared at the maknae in annoyance, "shut your mouth, for the love of God."

Solar giggles, shaking her head at the casual events that were common in such a place. There was still one thing missing, though, and it had to be addressed because it was something she never expected to witness in a million years, "so, since when did you guys exactly hang out together?" she looks to everyone expectantly, waiting for an answer.

Silence falls over the friends. Until finally, Chanhee blurts blantantly, "we don't, we really don't."

A look of confusion spreads over her face, and Sunwoo couldn't help but laugh as he saw her trying to figure out everything. "I just called them all because of your return," he informs, walking to stand beside her again so he could wrap her arm around her. Solar didn't mind as he pulled her into his chest, and she only inhaled that scent of smoke and cologne she didn't think she'd ever smell again. "But it's still the same, trust me, Chanhee and your goons fucking hate me."

"Goons?!" Kevin had cried in disbelief, "how come we're goons and Chanhee isn't?!"

Sunwoo sends the boy an impatient look as he deadpans, "looking in the mirror should answer that question for you."

Snickers spread through the room, even Solar's friends who also got insulted by the boy couldn't help but laugh. Sunwoo was pretty funny whether you wanted to admit it or not. His lack of emotion and straightforward comments would always make you crack a smile.

There's a bewildered look on Kevin's face as he shakes his head. Solar purses her lips as she witnesses the two groups together, trying to really believe this was happening.

"Well, it's a little tense, but it's not so bad," she speaks up a second later, "I don't know why we couldn't ever just all get alo-"

"-HOW COULD YOU?! DO YOU KNOW HOW MUCH THIS COST?!"

A defeated look takes over her face at the sudden screaming coming down the stairs. Everyone's eyes follow the sound, and none other than two she had yet to greet had entered the room. "THIS IS SERIOUS SHIT, IT'S LIKE 10 THOUSAND A BOTTLE AND YOU USED IT ALL!"

Hyunjoon sounded like he was about to light the entire house on fire. Solar only then remembered the boy was Sunwoo's only roommate - or more like the only one he ever trusted to live in his home other than her. He was holding some sort of shiny bottle, shaking it vigorously as he tries to calm his nerves.

Meanwhile, the boy he was chasing, and yelling at, looked like he was going to curl up in a ball and cry. "I'm sorry, I didn't know it was so expensive!" he had claimed in defense, and she pouts as she sees her beloved old friend looking so helpless.

"You know what else is expen-" Hyunjoon had been snapping, but suddenly his eyes jolt to see the commotion in the room, and they fall right on Solar. The most shocked expression she had ever seen anyone make in their lives took over his face, and he looks around the room, "nobody fucking told me she was already here!" he's whining, going from sounding infuriated to that of a baby as he takes long strides towards her, arms already wide open, "Solar, you gorgeous fuck!"

"Hyunjoonie!" she's squealing in return, opening her arms as well. They embrace each other, and this time, since his weight actually did compare to hers, he was nearly suffocating her.

Pulling away a second later, he holds his hands on her shoulders, stepping a bit back so he could admire all of her features. A scoff of awe leaves his lips as he shakes his head, "I didn't think it was possible, but Solar got more beautiful over the last five years."

Trying to hide any color that wanted to spread to her cheeks, she hurriedly waves a hand in dismissal. Hyunjoon had always been a ladies man, and particularly loved to gas Solar up. Ever since the day they met. Well, in fact, his tendency to compliment people was how they met.

"She really did!" Jacob cuts in, and everyone makes hums of agreement afterwards.

"Eh, I think she's gained some pou-" Another shriek is sent through the air as the sound of glass causes everyone to not show any sign of shock, but only disappointment as they look to the youngest again.

"Why do you even bother opening your mouth, seriously?"  Sunwoo asks in complete frustration.

"Fuck, he still has no sense of humor, as you can see, Sol."









///



"Ok, but how do you do that thing with your stomach?!" Eric is crying as he stares at the TV screen in absolute disbelief. He was like a little kid watching a cartoon for the first time, looking completely engrossed in the performance that was playing from the YouTube app.

Solar was left to sigh as she leans her head back, feeling embarrassed that all of her old friends were witnessing her music. Well, not all of them were there, as apparently there had been some sort of job to do that she figured was work related. She hadn't bothered to ask any more questions since it really wasn't her business.

When he didn't get a reply from her, he turns to peer at her, before asking again, "seriously, how do you do it?"

Sighing, she leans her head on Sunwoo's shoulder, who was sitting to her left while Chanhee was to her right, muttering, "I don't know how to explain it."

It was clear she wasn't very interested in talking about her choreography. With all that happened today, she didn't even wanna think about Korea and her company.

What was going to happen to iKON now? They knew the woman had been their manager, and they weren't mad about it, but it was still the underlying fact that now — they didn't have one.

"Can you show me?" Eric had asked again when she was hoping the conversation was over. If he was going to watch her performances, did he really have to ask her something about them every three seconds?

Wanting to slam her head into the wall, she's unable to speak up when Sunwoo had snapped from beside her, "how about you shut the fuck up and leave her alone?"

It was hard not to laugh, as Chanhee started snickering as the youngest had poured before turning back to the TV. Solar could only shake her head as she was reminded the tendencies of Kim Sunwoo.

"Soomi, huh?" Chanhee hums after a while of silence, bringing up the conversation about her new name that they had been having the last hour, "I can't believe you chose that out of everything."

His judgmental tone made her frown as she looks to him, "why, what's wrong with it?"

Her old childhood best friend only shrugs as he thinks for a second, "I don't know, I just don't think it compares to Solar, it kinda sucks."

Rolling her eyes at his mean words, she sits up so she could reach out and hit his arm. The boy moves away from her touch, giggling like he had done nothing wrong.

"You're mean, I like my new name!" She pouts, returning to her previous seating position and laying her head back on Sunwoo's shoulder.

"I was kidding, really," Chanhee insists with that sweet smile still on his lips, "but I think Solar suits you better."

After not speaking for the last ten minutes, Sunwoo had cut in to deadpan, "I would assume so being that's her actual name."

His sarcastic comment only earned a not so nice glare from Chanhee. He was clearly not impressed with the likes of the boy as he scoffs, "I don't remember you being a part of this conversation, but okay, smart ass."

Not even slightly intimidated and almost sounding humored, Sunwoo cockily retorts, "well looks like I am now, pretty boy."

"You know, you said pretty boy as if it was supposed to be insulting, but I'm taking that as more of a compliment."

"Yeah, that's cause you're fucking gay."

Wanting to slam her head against the wall, Solar rubs her temples to ease the mental pain they were putting her through. At first, it wasn't clear and she was been wondering why exactly the two groups couldn't hang out back then. But by this point, she was wildly reminded of the reason.

About to get up and go upstairs where she could find some peace of mind, she didn't get a chance to when footsteps are heard coming from the hall that lead to the basement.

"Sunwoo, she's awake, and she's refusing to answer anything," the informative tone of Hyunjae made the three sitting at the couch look up.

There's a knowing look on Sunwoo's face, and all the others as well. In the meantime, Soomi had been talking to Chanhee about what all happened, and was even sort of getting into her entire engagement. Before she could really manage to get too deep though, he and Sunwoo ended up arguing.

Being completely lost is exactly what Chanhee and Solar were. Neither of them had any idea what the gang members could be up to.

Rising to his feet, Sunwoo heaves out a sigh, "alright, we'll see how she feels when she meets me."

She wondered if it would be okay to ask what was going on. Although it was probably gang business, she was also curious about what they ended up doing with the manager.

Converse clicking against the shining wood floors, Sunwoo is just about to head into the hall towards the basement with Hyunjae, when a voice makes them halt.

"Are you talking about the crazy chick that tried to kill Sol?" Eric had called, and Sunwoo had stopped dead in his tracks. The look on his face filled with anger to the point it was dripping from his irises as he turns to face the maknae, who was still sitting on the carpet, with clenched fists.

However, at the sound of what he just blurted, Solar pipes up in utter confusion, "wait, you guys are questioning her?"

Realizing why Sunwoo was so mad now, Eric purses his lips as he awkwardly turns away from the conversations, "oops."

It took all of Sunwoo not to pick the coffee table up and break it over the boys head. He guessed she had to know sooner or later, though.

"Yeah, yeah, we did."

She rose to her feet in an instant. "Shouldn't I be the one asking her things then? You guys don't even know the full story."

The two boys watch her as she stops before them, meeting their gazes expectantly and waiting for them to lead the way. Hyunjae hadn't had to think about it long before he shrugs, looking to the leader for confirmation, "she has a point."

Exhaling deeply, he spins on his heel to begin the walk to the basement.

"Alright, whatever."


72:00 | torture victim

"You didn't tell me you brought her here," Solar is saying as she struggles to keep up with the fast pace of Kim Sunwoo. His usual sweet attitude he only ever showed to her had diminished, he was switched into the cold guy everyone knew him as. It was no surprise that he was this way, it was what being a crime boss did to you.

He doesn't glance at her as they're trotting down the steps to the basement. "I didn't want to bother you with it, I was just going to get her to tell me everything," he explained, and she purses her lips in thought from his words. 

Before she could really get a chance to say anything, they reached the bottom of the steps. The door was closed, and as if on queue, a scream had echoed through the walls. The small girl winces from the sound, wondering what might be going on behind that door. Whatever it was had to be horrible.

And if it wasn't, it was about to be. 

It'd been a long time since she'd seen any of them in action. Their business, or more like Sunwoo's father's, business was no joke. They were normal people on the outside, if you looked past the leather jackets and tattoos, they made jokes and laughed like normal kids. But when it came to work, all of them turned unrecognizable. 

She used to be used to it, back then. Solar had liked Sunwoo so much and known him since she was so young that she didn't care about what he did. It wasn't his fault, after all, his father forced him into running his dirty work. The sight of blood, torture, and vulgarity was nothing to her.

But that was then, and this was now. The people Sunwoo used to go after, and did go after, were for his job. He didn't associate his job with his emotions and personal life, he only did what he had to do to survive. 

This woman, however, had tried to kill the only girl he'd ever loved, the only person he'd ever shown himself to, opened his heart to. His beloved Sol, the girl who disappeared because of her horrid family. He'd finally gotten her back after missing her so badly, and the thought that whoever this woman may be was trying to prevent her from something as precious as living set him off immensely.

He couldn't look past it, he didn't care what anyone said. Sunwoo was associating work with his personal life when he had her taken down there for questioning, and he was going to make sure she learned what real pain was.

Screams continue to boom through the closed door, and Hyunjae turns to look to the leader, waiting for the okay to open it. Before he does so, the tall boy turns towards the girl that was hesitantly standing behind him, "Sol, since you're here, I need you to tell me everything," he demands sternly, "start from the beginning, alright?"

Shocked at his sudden command, there's a look of bewilderment on her face. Her mind rushing through the past few years of her life, she tries to fit it all into a short summary that she could tell him. Where could she really start?

"Well, um," she stutters for a second as she tries to full gather her thoughts, "she started following me when I was signed under the company she works at, I guess she looked into everything about me, and found out that I was engaged which was uh - against the rules, so she started threatening my fiance at the time," she stares at her feet as she recalls it all in her head. It no doubt felt a little awkward talking to Sunwoo about it, but she knew she didn't have much of a choice.

"Because of the threats that I didn't know about at the time, my engagement was cut off," she coughs a bit, too afraid to see the look on his face, "I started getting threats from her one night, and they started out about be being a two timer because I-I was sort of seeing this other guy," she closes her eyes as she tries not to emotionally implode from thinking about it. "I never listened to her even though she had access to my apartment somehow, and then she hurt my ex fiance and gave him a minor concussion when he caught her once," she breathes out, reopening her eyes to peer at Sunwoo for the first time since she started.

He was staring right back at her, his expression remaining emotionless. Even then, she knew he wasn't thrilled about anything he was hearing. 

"She said she was going to find out everything about my past and tell the world, which meant like - the drugs and you guys and my family," she explains further, eager to finish the horrible story so the two stares of Hyunjae and Sunwoo would stop burning holes into her, "then she drugged the other guy I was seeing and uh, sent him into a medically induced coma."

"Jesus Christ," Hyunjae had mumbled, "but why's she hate you so much?"

She could only shrug, "I don't really know. She's the manager for the other guy I was seeing, Jinhwan - uh, his group, so that only makes it weirder."

By the end of her story thoroughly telling them everything they needed to know, Sunwoo had pieced together the puzzle in his mind. He didn't know much about the music business, especially in somewhere so different like Korea, but he did have an idea of why the woman would do it. 

Not to mention, he realized just exactly who those two were. That short kid hadn't been one of her good friends, he was the other guy he was seeing, right along with her ex fiance.

Holding a hand to his head as he turns back to the door, he could only sigh. No, he wasn't mad at Solar, of course she had the right to see people after five years. Even though they never officially broke up, being in a whole nother country for so long definitely gave the impression that they were over. Although it had been in such a sad way, and he had missed her so badly, he still couldn't blame her.

The only person he was blaming was whoever this woman seemed to think she was.

"Alright, let's go."

Hyunjae nods curtly, and the door swings open before them. Solar holds her breathe, anticipating what she was about to see as she could only follow her ex boyfriend inside. 

Upon entering the room, the screams and curses that were so muffled previously were clear every few seconds. It looked just as she remembered it, and she sighs as she follows the two boys across the mahogany floor. 

The sight of the woman made her wince. She was the definition of a mess, with bruises all over her face and body and splatters of blood every which way. The gunshot wounds she'd suffered hadn't been treated, except for the one in her stomach so it wasn't lethal enough to kill her. It was a rather disgusting sight, with the way her previously painted makeup was now a smeared destruction all over her face. Her eyes were swollen either from getting hit or crying, and her hair was a rat's nest of frizzy darkness. 

Around her stood gang members, which she'd previously just been hugging and joking around with like a child only hours ago. The one who always did all the work when it came to questioning people, otherwise known as Hyunjoon and Haknyeon, were standing the closest to the woman that was tied in the chair. The others, who helped when needed in case she were to try anything, sat around on the couches, eating snacks or watching what was going down.

At the sound of new footsteps, everyone's head jerked up except the woman. She was in too much pain to seem to want to move, figuring it was just more of them. 

The previously intense expressions of the gang members turned bright when they see the brunette girl walking towards them. "Oh hi, Sol, I didn't know you'd be joining us," Haknyeon offers a wave and a glistening smile, greeting her as if she just walked into a party and he wasn't literally just beating someone three seconds ago.

If it wasn't for the nostalgia eating her alive and the known fact she'd been here many times before, she probably would've been terrified.

Hearing the commotion, Hyunjoon dramatically drops the chain on the ground he'd been holding. It hits the floor with a clack, and he screeches like a kid as he goes to wrap an arm around her shoulders, "welcome to my chamber, Sol, I've upgraded it since you were last here," he shares with excitement, motioning to the dark red walls that surrounded them. Only then did she notice the equipment that hung upon them, something that was most definitely new.

"Oh," she hums in acknowledgement, "I see that now."

His arm remains around her shoulder as they all stop before the woman. Solar's voice had made her stir, and her head gradually lifted up, using her small amounts of strength to peer up at the girl.

She couldn't lie, it felt sickeningly sweet to see her in such a state. The woman who hurt people she loved and tried to kill her, after all.

Unable to handle the feeling, a smirk finds it's way to her face as she offers, "nice to see you, how did that ending my life thing go?"

Some of the guys start snickering from her comment. Hyunjoon's arm leaves her shoulders as he bends down to pick up the chain, handing it to Sunwoo as he figured he wanted it since he was stepping forward after Sol said that.

But he wasn't interested in what his friend offered. His blood was boiling so high to the point he didn't even notice what he was trying to give him.

The woman weakly opened her mouth to speak despite the flaming boy staring holes into her body, but just as she opens her mouth, Sunwoo had balled one hand into a fist, slamming it down onto her already broken wrist that had been grasping the top of the chair. From the bone already being fractured, the slam of his treacherous strength was agonizing to even witness, and Solar cringed when she heard a crack echo through the air, followed by the scream of horror.

None of the guys' expressions changed, they were probably used to seeing this. Really, she had been, too. Something like this was nothing out of the ordinary when it came to gang business - that was, until he'd walked across the room to pull something out of a drawer. The silver of the knife had glistened under the dim lights in the basement of the mansion, and Solar gulps as she watches him stomp back over to the lady.

"W-Wait," she tries to stutter out, but the brunette didn't care for a word. Carelessly, as if this was nothing big to him (because really, it wasn't in a way,) he sends the knife flying straight across her arm. The slicing of flesh sent an ill feeling down Solar's spine, as the blood immediately gushed from the many inch cut he created from her elbow to her middle finger. Screams became something that didn't effect her ears anymore from how many she'd heard in the last few seconds, but it wasn't satisfying enough to Sunwoo.

He was angry. He was gripping the handle of the knife like his life depended on it, shaking with anger as he'd draw back his arm again before slashing it across her cheek. Pleads of mercy fell from the lips of someone who was trying to kill the love of his life only hours ago, but it was absolutely nothing to him.

All he wanted to cause her was pain. He still needed answers from her first, yes, but his anger was overcoming him to the point where he was bound to just kill her right then and there.

"Sunwoo," Younghoon had advised as he watched the younger leader only continue to stab her like she was some sort of voodoo doll, "didn't you need information? If she loses more blood, she'll probably die."

Everyone else had realized this except for Sunwoo. Just when his hand is raising itself, prepared to strike again, the hand of another being grabbed his wrist, his combating strength preventing him from going any further with the bloody stained weapon he held.

His eyes are still flaming when they look to the one who dared to stop him. "Calm down, you're having all the fun without me," Hyunjoon whines as if they were playing a game. A wave of realization washes over Sunwoo's face as he then glances to the woman, seeing the bloody mess she was in and the fact she was probably about to pass out. Knowing she wouldn't be able to take anymore if he wanted her alive, he heaves out a sigh, chunking the knife somewhere to the side of the room. 

"What'd she do anyway that you brought her in here and have all this anger towards?" Hyunjoon is mumbling into the air as he watches his leader clench his fists by his side. Solar had taken it as the time to step forward, reaching out and grabbing his arm with her small hand. Her electrocuting touch almost instantly calmed his nerves, and when he met her eyes his breathing pattern gradually returned to it's normal state. "I've never seen Sunwoo so mad at anyone before," the boy was still mumbling as he picked up his preferred weapon once again, peering down at the barely conscious woman, "just who the hell are you, miss?"

Heavy breathes leave her lips as she manages to look up to the dark haired boy. He was no doubt a handsome guy, someone she would be interested in if this was any other circumstance. With a lip ring that shone under the light, and tattoos covering his arms, he had toned muscles that would scare anyone who dared to mess with him. Hyunjoon was a courageous looking guy, with the way he'd taunt with those eyes of his, having you on your knees before you even spoke a word. 

It was part of the reason he'd been so popular in school. The casual play boy was exactly what you'd call him, if you had to give it a title. Poor Hyunjoon had never truly cared for a girl, though, all he ever paid attention to as far as female's went - genuinely - was Sol. But it wasn't like that, he knew Sunwoo marked her a long time ago. She was just the only girl who ever struck him as someone he could trust, was all.

Ever since he'd met her, he'd deemed to protect her, just like the others. Even her own friend group with Chanhee were protective of her, they watched her like geese, hence why they hated Sunwoo's gang so much. The thing was, Hyunjoon and the other gang members had yet to truly hear what this woman had done to her.

You could only imagine what would happen when they did.

Solar knew this, she knew it would get bad. But did she really care? Maybe in Korea she was the kind hearted soul everyone loved, the one who was sarcastic but not mean, because no one believed she actually had a mean bone in her body. The naive girl who tried to make everyone around her happy.

But now, she wasn't in Korea, she was in New York. And the real Solar, the one who didn't go by the name of Soomi, didn't care at all.

She wanted her to hurt.

"You could call her a stalker, I guess," she breaks the air when the woman didn't seem keen on telling Hyunjoon anything about her identity, "she followed me, hurt my friends, and tried to ruin my career," she clears her throat as Hyunjoon's eyes had widened from her words, deeming the worst part of it all, "she was going to shoot me if Sunwoo hadn't gotten there."

The chain he was holding dropped to the floor with a clack, and it was hard not to smirk like a demon when his eyes had narrowed, jolting back to the captured soul. Sunwoo had been mad, beyond angered, but now Hyunjoon was, too. With both of them pissed, it would only get worse for whoever it was focused at.

"Are you fucking serious?!" he snaps, his hand going to something tucked in his belt. Before anyone could stop him, which they knew not to, he pulls out the gun that was inside. 

This time, when the scream echoes, she laughs. He'd shot her wrist, then her arm, before he'd slammed his foot into the chair she was sitting in, making her and it collapse to the ground, "you really thought you could fuck with Solar, huh?"

Within the next few minutes, Solar went ahead and explained to him the details of what she did. The way his stomach was churning with anger was worse than anything she'd seen, similar to Sunwoo. In fact, just hearing the story again had riled up her ex boyfriend again, but she didn't let him move as she held onto his arm, letting Hyunjoon do whatever he desired.

Gunshot.

"Are you happy you found out everything about her, huh?"

Gunshot.

"Do you feel proud of yourself?" he snarls, "You really ruined her career, didn't ya?"

Gunshot.

"If anything, I hope this teaches you not to stick your nose where it doesn't belong," he hisses, the gun now going to her head, "you should've figured that trying to hurt Solar was a bad idea when you saw she was connected to a fucking crime boss and his gang, but you still tried it," he laughs bitterly, slamming the gun tighter against her skull, "why? What did she do to you?!"

It was clear she was having a hard time breathing, as her stomach heaves up and down. No muscle in her body seemed capable of moving, but Hyunjoon didn't care, not until he got the answers he wanted.

When he doesn't, he cocks the gun, about to no doubt blast a bullet wherever he wanted. The sound of it made her tremble, and she finally attempts to cough out, "s-she stole everything!"

Stole?

"I was like her once, but she came to YG and - suddenly - everything was about her, the CEO was obsessed with her," she belches out, sounding like she was on the verge of puking, "everything about her was apparently just so great."

"I never thought she was good, I was better, but he was too blinded by her disgusting fucking-," the gunshot sent through her arm cut her off, followed by a screech of pain.

Coughing a bit, she goes on, "I looked into her, followed her home, and saw how many rules she was breaking - I wanted to get her out of the company so I could reclaim what I deserved."

Arms crossed over his chest, there's an unimpressed look on Sunwoo's face, "but nothing worked, so your last resort was to wipe her completely?"

Her expression confirmed such to be true.

"Sounds to me like you're a fucking psychopath," Hyunjoon mutters, shaking his head, "but so am I, because I love to play this game," he taunts as he removes the gun from her head, twirling it around his finger. "I haven't thought of a name yet, I think I might call it-" he cuts himself off purposely when he abruptly raises the weapon again, shooting another bullet into her shoulder, catching her off guard and making the scream of result even louder than the last.

"See?" he triumphantly giggles, "fun!"

"That's a real shitty reason to go after someone to the lengths that you did," Sunwoo shakes his head in disbelief, breaking away from Sol's grip as he walks up to the woman, "someone was working with you, who is it?"

He'd already come to conclusion she hadn't done all of this alone. From everything he'd seen and knew, there was no way something like that was possible. It left too many holes in the plot.

Upon hearing him ask this, a laugh escapes her throat. Although her breathing was irregular and she couldn't move in the slightest, she couldn't help how funny it was. 

"You-You think I would tell you that?!" she booms with more hysterics, "you'll never fucking know."

Gunshot, right through her other shoulder. "Not like it matters anyway, one person is absolutely nothing to any of us, I could probably kill them while I'm sleeping," Hyunjoon snorts, but his words only receive a glare from her. At the sight of the way she looked at him, he narrows his eyes before slapping her across the face with the hand that wasn't holding the gun, "you should really learn some manners, sweetheart, unless you're into that."

"She'll be dead soon, she's lost too much blood," Younghoon is mumbling from one of the couches.

Feeling beyond stressed, Sunwoo runs a hand through his hair. He was right, there was no way this chick would be alive for any longer than an hour. But so was Hyunjoon when he said that it didn't matter if anyone else was after her, because she had them. 

"Alright, whatever," he sighs, turning back to everyone after a deep thought, "doesn't matter, we've got everything you collected on her and I already burnt it to a crisp."

"You think that was the only existing copy of that I had?" she snorts, "my partner's got another, this isn't over, it doesn't matter if you kill me."

Any kicks or punches she received now, she didn't feel them. Her body was already shutting down. 

"Don't worry, we'll blast extra mags in your partner's skull, too, just for fun," Hyunjoon hums as he reloads his gun, before placing it against her head. It was time to do what had been coming to her, there was nothing more they required.

"You'll never find them," she snarls, "they're smarter than me, they'll never be found."

Tired of hearing this, Solar finally steps forward. "Can you do it already? I don't want to listen to her anymore."

At her request, Sunwoo nods to Hyunjoon, wrapping an arm around the girl's shoulder. She wraps her frail arms around his torso, peering devilishly at the woman.

"Night night, cupcake," Hyunjoon taunts as his finger hovers over the trigger.

Everyone waited, anticipating for the sound of the gunshot. They waited for her body to fall limply on the ground like it should've been.

But just before he presses it, she'd met the green eyes of Solar, they were burning with desperation and pure infuriation dripped from her lids. She didn't think she would have the strength anymore to say anything with the fact most of her blood was in a puddle around her on the floor, but just as it was bound to happen, she'd screeched out with her last bits of courage, "they'll all know, Solar, they'll know about who you really are. All of these people you're with, they'll know, they'll know about every little thing you've done - the drugs, the murders, and most importantly, your baby-"

Gunshot. 


73:00 | mortification

She gasped, not from the bullet that was sent through her head, but from the last words she managed to utter out into the air before she fell lifeless onto the ground. Her once living body hit the puddle of blood that had formed around her and the broke chair with a plop against the wooden floor, and there's a wave of silence that falls over everyone.

Her breathe is caught in her throat, she can't manage to meet Sunwoo's eyes. She knew he was wondering, she knew he was trying to understand what she'd been talking about. It only meant one thing, since it had all been about her past, and upon hearing the last words that he never knew about, it was only so long until he put the pieces together.

The other members seemed to be doing that, too. Their faces scrunch with confusion as they all exchange glances. Nobody knew if they should speak, until Hyunjae clears his throat, looking for any amount of confirmation, "did she say Solar had a baby?"

Unable to face what was to come, she pulls out of Sunwoo's grasp. Before he could grab her, her feet rush to the door, swinging it open and rushing up the stairs. Each step the took up them felt like a million, and it was a miracle when she finally burst into the main room.

Tears are rushing to her eyes, and they didn't wait to stream down her cheeks. She was messing up her makeup all over again as if it hadn't been bad enough, and the sight of her childhood friends all sitting in the living room was a blessing to her.

"Sol, hey," Kevin was the first to say as she ran to them.

"Hey, did you find-" Chanhee was saying as he rises to his feet from the couch, only then did everyone see her state, "what the hell happened? Was it that bad?"

Solar could only burst into uncontrollable sobs as she runs into her real best friend's embrace. Her beloved childhood, closest friend that had really known her better than anyone had.

Not knowing what the cause of her troubles were but only assuming it was something to do with what went on, Chanhee can only embrace her tightly. He was squeezing the life out of her with his warmth he was always known for only providing her, and she was more than thankful to feel it again after so long. 

The other three friends of Solar's were beyond confused and massively upset. Solar that they had known wasn't someone that cried if ever, even the day she got screamed at and kicked out of her house - she didn't shed a tear, not until she'd bought the plane ticket. It was when reality sunk into her skull that she dared to cry, but even back then she never let herself. Crying wasn't something that was okay where she was from, but the girl who left to Korea was a lot different than the girl who lived in Korea.

Her emotions were vulnerable, and none of her childhood friends knew what to make of it. Changmin only frowns at her state, hugging her from behind so she was enclosed in a massive hug between him and Chanhee. Setting his head on her shoulder as her face was buried in Hee's chest, he only mutters words of encouragement to try to help. Poor innocent Changmin wanted to cry just seeing his friend cry, he hated it.

The first assumption they thought, was that it had to be because of all the violence she'd been introduced to. However, the friends soon realized, as they shushed her comfortingly while exchanging confused glances, that Solar never minded violence. She dated Sunwoo for seven years, and the sight of someone getting shot was a regular for her because that was what Sunwoo did. It was his life, and she had no choice but to deal with things like that if she wanted to be with him.

Sure, it'd been five years, but they didn't think something like an insane woman getting killed would traumatize her when she'd seen so much worse.

So what was the real problem?

"Sol, Sol," Jacob is soothing her with his angel like tone, rubbing circles on her back as the five are all crowded around her, "just breathe, breathe, okay? Tell us what's wrong."

It takes her a moment of heavy breathing to calm her sobs. Her breathe kept getting caught in her throat, and finally, she manages to shake her head, lifting it from Hee's chest for a split second to mutter, "n-no, I can't here..."

"Do you want to go back to my house? My parents would be really glad to see you," Hee offers as he pats her back, his eyes wavering with concern for his closest friend, "Don't worry, they won't tell your family anything."

Meeting his soft eyes, she only nods before burying her face back into his t-shirt.

"We'll come, too," Kevin assures in a cheery voice, everyone of them just trying to cheer her up from whatever had caused her to break down, "I can go get ice cream and Starbucks."

The sound of her two favorite things, it was funny he remembered. Then again, how could he forget their late night runs to the store?

"Kevin, it's night time, she doesn't need caffeine and sugar," Chanhee lectures in disappointment, "she needs relaxation and rest."

Sighing at the fact he was such a mother, the boy rolls his eyes, "alright, so, face masks, hot cocoa, and Gossip Girl?"

Solar giggles between her silent tears. No way, she forgot how well they knew her compared to any of the guys in Korea. Sure, Jimin watched Gossip Girl with her plenty of times, but even he didn't ever care much for the show. He just liked it because she did.

"That sounds like a dream," Jacob gawks, "I need some Blair and Chuck in my life."

"Serena and Nate should've stayed together."

"God, no, she's better of with Dan."

"But he's a traitor!"

"Kevin, Jacob, please just go get the stuff," Chanhee cuts them off, still in the middle of comforting her with Changmin.

The two scoff at each other before turning towards the door. "I'll go, you can stay here," Jacob hums, "I'll meet you at Chanhee's in thirty!"

With that, the angel boy jogs to the door and disappears outside in a flash. She could only watch him go from the small crack she had of vision that wasn't completely covered in Hee's t-shirt.

"Alright, let's just go now before-"

"-Solar!" Sunwoo, for the first time in his life, is in genuine panic as he nearly busts down the basement door that enters the living room. He piles into the room, breathing like he just ran a marathon. The friends of the girl are confused as they see him in this state, although when the rest of his gang members follow suit behind him, it was clear Sunwoo was just fought them all off from them holding him back, trying to keep him from bothering her.

But God knew you couldn't hold Sunwoo back from doing what he wanted. Their attempt had failed as fast as it started.

The sight of him causes her breathe to hitch in her throat, balling Hee's shirt in her hands as she tries to calm her nerves. This secret, this secret he found out, it was one she pushed to the very back of her head, the darkest of her thoughts, to never be found again. It was something she refused to ever let herself think about, so much so she'd forgotten it until that day.

And remembering it killed her inside.

She never told Sunwoo for a reason, and now he knew, what could she do?

Wanting to disappear into the ground forever, Chanhee seemed to get the message as he wraps an arm around her just as Sunwoo was walking up, reaching for her wrist. Flashing him a warning glare, the boy stops his actions and returns the annoyed look to her friend. "Sol, please just talk to me about this," he pleads, the pent up frustration becoming clearer with every word he spoke, "you can't just push me away."

She'd heard those words before, and it makes her want to slam her head against the wall. That was always her result in these situations, after all, pushing people away. It was too hard for her to face them most of the time, she was scared. Despite the act she put up, despite how she tried to be strong, she was the exact opposite.

Especially when it came to her miscarriage.

"I don't think she's ready to talk to you, Sunwoo," Chanhee sternly scolds him, shaking his head disapprovingly, "sometimes people need time to think, not everyone acts on a limb like you."

The pink haired boy only receives a look of absolute irritation. "This doesn't involve you, pretty boy, I need to talk to Solar."

Although she could hear what he was saying and how desperate he was to know if it was true, she didn't move a muscle from her position. Everyone saw this, and Kevin sighs as he steps between Sunwoo and the two, "she really does not seem prepared to talk to you about whatever this is about," he tells him harshly, "just let her be for right now, she's had a hard enough day and she's going to see her family tomorrow for God's sakes."

His abrupt lecture struck something in Sunwoo. Groaning, he throws his head back as he spins on his heel, feeling like his entire world was crumbling around him all over again. 

Either way, he forces himself to mutter into the air, "whatever."

A silence overcomes the living room as he'd zoomed up the stairs. The sound of his door being slammed seconds later flew through the entire house, sending shivers up Solar's spine as she lets out a shaky sigh.

The three friends of Solar's had stared at the gang members. No words were exchanged between the two different groups who hadn't spent time together really ever, and Chanhee pats the brunette's back to grab her attention, "we should go, Sol."

Calming her nerves, she slowly rises from Chanhee's chest for the first time in a while. Rubbing her eyes, she didn't look back to the others until she was at the front door with Kevin and Changmin behind her, Chanhee being in the front with her hand tightly engulfed in his own.

"We love you, Sol!" Hyunjae had yelled from his stance across the room, and the others follow with the same sweet comments, sending her waves.

It was funny, to see their duality, when only around half an hour ago they were torturing and killing someone as if it was no big deal. The crazier part was that it was something they did daily, it was their job.

But really, it wasn't their faults. Their families forced them into that life, they forced them to do their dirty work so their businesses stayed thriving, so they remained at the top of the business world and never fell. It was how some people liked to run their companies, unfortunately. Over time, the majority of the world was ran by people like that - who played dirty. The only families still left in it were the one's standing in the room staring right back at the dirty players.

"You're a gorgeous bitch, Solar, don't fucking forget it," Hyunjoon points a finger at her, winking, "whatever happened, true or not, you're still the baddest fuck I've ever met, and my love for you will never change, and neither will that bitch boy upstairs, he's not mad, you know?"

Hyunjoon's words make her giggle, nodding her head. He was right. What he said about Sunwoo was undeniably correct even if it sounded crazy. He couldn't be wrong, after all, he was the boy's best friend and the only one who knew him like the back of his hand.

Even Solar didn't know him like he did, not anymore.

"Thanks, guys," she manages to tell them as Chanhee is dragging her out the door, "I'll see you all soon."


74:00 | clique

"Thanks for this, guys," Solar mutters out, a mouthful of popcorn in her mouth. Chanhee would've scolded her if she was literally anyone else other than Solar Lee, but he didn't care about the fact she was speaking with her mouth full as he leans his head on her shoulder.

Grabbing a handful of popcorn out of the bowl in front of them, he'd shoved it in his own mouth before humming, "since when did you thank us?"

He wasn't wrong. The Solar they all knew wasn't someone who casually threw around such words. It wasn't that she was ungrateful, she had always been loving towards her friends. However, it was more of the fact she didn't ever say what she felt. Solar preferred to always show it instead.

"Yeah, you don't have to," Jacob assures her with a smile as he was sipping his hot cocoa, shifting to a more comfortable seating position on the blankets they were all sat on top of, "we're your best friends, that's what we're for."

"Catch, loser!" Kevin had been yelling to Changmin, who squealed like a baby as he threw a piece of popcorn at him. Changmin leaned his head back like some sort of animal with that long neck of his, a giddy grin taking over his face when he manages to catch it in his mouth. The two boys erupt into cheers and giggles, not realizing what they were interrupting due to their fun.

Solar didn't mind it at all, she was happy to see them happy.

"I know," she agrees a second later in regards to Jacob's statement, "I just felt like you should all know, you really are the greatest friends. I never thought anyone would welcome me with open arms, that's why I didn't come back," she spills, staring to the ground as the Gossip Girl episode was blaring from Chanhee's flatscreen, "I thought everyone would hate me for leaving, I was scared out of my mind."

Having had listened into what was going on, there's a massive frown on Changmin's previously bright face, "Aw~, Sol, how could you say that?! We couldn't ever hate you!" he cries, wrapping an arm around her shoulder and pulling her in for a short comforting hug.

"That's silly of you," Kevin had pouted as he chewed more popcorn from the massive bowl, "it wasn't your fault, you did what you thought was right, you were going through a lot."

She could only purse her lips in a straight line and listen to them. A part of her still couldn't believe where she was right now, it seemed surreal to her. The last time she was in Chanhee's bedroom was when she was still in highschool, obviously. She'd practically lived here, it was her second home after her own. Chanhee's parents came to love her like their own daughter since their only child was Chanhee, and being that the two were so inseparable, she basically was his sister.

This bedroom, was a place she couldn't erase from her memory even if she wanted to. Even though she forced herself to never think about them, or anything regarding her life in America, it was still her home. She still grew up here, no matter how much she tried to run from it.

Her initial plan that night she left to Korea was to start fresh. But maybe she never really needed to.

The truth was the same; you can't run from your past.

"We made a pact, remember, idiot?" Chanhee is scoffing playfully as he nudges her side, "no matter what, we're always here for each other," he recites.

As if in unison from how well they remember it, they all chorused, "always."

Solar could only grin like an idiot from the nostalgia that overcame her. Not wanting to forget everything that was happening, she pulls her phone out of her back pocket. Urging her friends to gather around her as she held up the camera, they all screech cheese as she'd tapped the photo button.

There's a smile that remains on her face, one that stretched from ear to ear. No doubt, Solar had had the hardest day of her life, thus far (maybe even harder than the day she left,) but it was all coming together. She still had so many obstacles left to face and climb over, so many choices unsolved, but yet, she still felt content as she stares down at the picture.

She'd had yet to tell her friends about the entire thing at Sunwoo's house. They had focused on cheering her up instead of egging her to tell them, being the amazing friends she always remembered her as. The four knew that when she was ready, she would tell them everything about it. And until that time came, they were prepared to wait for her as the best friends they all were, just like they had the last five years.

"Oh, Sol," Chanhee is sighing as he's leaning his chin on her shoulder to peer down at her phone screen, which she didn't notice until it was too late, "I can't believe I've missed out on so much in your life."

She was confused on which part he was referring to, until her green eyes gradually fall on her phone screen. Solar had completely forgotten her home screen was a photo of her and Jungkook, with her lock screen being her and Jinhwan.


Before she can turn off the screen to hide what it revealed, Chanhee snatches the phone from her hand, already one step ahead of her like always. She couldn't even pinpoint exactly how old the picture was, it was ancient, that was for sure. It dated back to sometime when she had no cares in the world, that she would acknowledge at least, and it was a time when all she was focused on was her relationship with Jungkook. The two were young in the picture, but looking at it always brought her back to a safe place.

She'd filled in Chanhee on bits about how there were two guys she was involved with in Korea, and about the woman who was after her and tried to hurt them. He'd pretty much heard it all, along with the others, but she had yet to have provided them with any names. She didn't think, in her defense, they would actually know who they were anyway.

And with everything that happened that day, it wasn't her fault she'd forgotten the fact her and her newest friends were all famous.

"Hold the fuck," Chanhee cuts himself off as he holds the phone closer to his face, squinting like a mad man, "is that Jeon fucking Jungkook?"

"Oh God, you like BTS?" Kevin sighs in disappointment, "sorry, Sol, I think I'm gonna have to break the pact."

"Hey, their music isn't so bad," Jacob had defended against his rude joke, hitting his arm.

Solar could only bury her face in her hands, knowing they had no idea what they were about to see. Chanhee had leaned forward, practically shoving her phone down their throats to make sure they saw what he was seeing.

All any of her friends knew to say was oh my god.

"Well I didn't think she liked them that much," Kevin mutters in bewilderment, causing her to snort in amusement.

"Wait," Chanhee trails off as he turned off her phone, then back on so it would reveal her lockscreen instead. When he sees it, another chuckle of awe leaves his lips, "no fucking way, Solar Lee!" he screeches like a school girl, hitting her arm rather hard from his excitement, "why did you leave out the massive detail of the guys being massive celebrities?"

She blinks at him in confusion, knowing he seemed to be forgetting the fact they weren't still in high school and how much had changed.

"Uh, because I'm a massive celebrity?" It sounded idiotic coming from her lips, and she couldn't help but start giggling afterwards.

Upon the words, it all seems to sink into the pink haired boys brain. This wasn't high school anymore, and Solar had left to Korea for five years. Even though her return felt like she had never left, he couldn't deny the new life she had created for herself.

"I forget," he sighs out dramatically, "I'm so proud of my dear Solar."

Chanhee had wrapped an arm around her shoulder as he says this, and she wrinkles her nose as he'd forced her into his embrace. The others are chuckling, still thinking hard on everything they had learned about their old best friend thus far.

"That's so cool," Changmin mutters, "so all the idols are friends?"

Pursing her lips, she thinks a second from his question, "sort of, depends. I'm friends with pretty much everyone, but some of them don't like each other."

Her mind wanders back to Jungkook and Jinhwan when she says this. Talking about them sent a ping through her body. They were probably so confused and worried about her, she really needed to talk to them soon.

Not to mention, Solar couldn't just throw her new life away because she was becoming one with her old one. She had worked hard to build a career for herself, to make herself known. It would be a shame for her to throw it all away. She couldn't do that to her fans.

She had been debating hard since she got to Hee's on what she was going to do. If she was truly planning to make up with her family tomorrow, it would mean she would probably have to stay for a while. This meant she wouldn't be able to take the plane back to Korea when everyone else did. But how was she going to just tell her manager and CEO she was going to stay?

She could only hope they could understand. Solar was in dire need from a break after all this, too.

"Do you know NCT?" Kevin suddenly blurts the question into the peaceful silence.

Jacob had sighed in disappointment, and Chanhee rolled his eyes. Solar wasn't sure what to think of their reactions, but also wasn't surprised to learn people like Kevin were fans of groups. She probably should've figured that a long time ago, but she just never thought about it. It was funny none of them ever found her earlier, and that out of all people to discover her, it was Sunwoo.

"Uh," she trails off in thought, recalling the group he was talking about. She wasn't stupid, of course she knew who they were. But she'd never actually spoken to them before. Like her, they were one of the top in the industry right now.

"I've never met them officially," she explains after a moment, "we're just acquaintances."

He pouts at that, "dammit."

Raising a brow, she couldn't help but ask, "why? Are you a big fan?"

"You don't want to know," Jacob groans, rubbing his temples as if it pained him to talk about.

Solar chuckles, "hey, it's okay to be a fan boy, they make good music!" She tries to defend her friend.

"But yet I'm the one who gets called gay," Chanhee's words are dripping of salt, causing everyone to laugh from how saddening it was.

Kevin gawks, clearly offended by the words that just came out of his mouth, "I'm not gay, okay? I just like their music!"

But none of them looked convinced. The guys started to pester him, to which he looked rather helpless. It was funny, but at the same time, she couldn't help but feel a little bad for poor Kevin.

"Guys, it's okay, let him be," she tells them, "plus, they're cute, you know? Have you ever seen that one guy?"

Everyone's eyes jolt to her.

"What guy?"

Their eager eyes melted Solar as she blinks a bit from the fact they wanted to know so bad. She was starting to feel like they might be bigger fans of the music than she thought.

"I don't know his name," she shrugs, "I told you, I don't really know them."

"Wait — I'll show you a picture, tell me who you're talking about!" Kevin had been insisting in excitement as he was unlocking his phone.

The others sigh in disappointment, shaking their heads at him. Wanting to change the subject, Jacob nudges her shoulder as he hums, "hey, what about this whole Jungkook and Jinhwan thing you've got going on, though? Were you seriously engaged?"

She could only dread telling them everything. It was going to be a seriously long night.


75:00 | telling the truth

Solar couldn't sleep.

She tossed and turned from side to side the entire night. Chanhee's guest room was basically her own room, or it had been back then from how often she stayed at his house. It had been the only place her parents freely allowed her to go, and was the escape route to her getting out so often.

Some of her old things were even still in there, which she was beyond shocked to see. She guessed she should've expected such, though. Chanhee never moved out of his parents house because he didn't really have that sort of luxury. Being an only child, he was the beloved gift in his parents eyes. Since they were in New York, he was attending an Ivy League school that was easily still within driving distance of his home. Simply put, he had no reason to move out.

The others were staying in Hee's room with him, probably playing a game or something. Solar had checked out early after she told them the gist of her life in Korea. All the way from Jungkook, to how they got engaged at KCON, to the threats and the massive scandal. She explained in depth her drinking problem and how Jinhwan saved her, and how the psycho woman that Sunwoo killed had been Jinan's manager. It was one crazy story that had them all gawking as if their minds had been imploded, and really, they had.

After telling them so much, she realized she still had yet to explain what that entire thing was about at Sunwoo's. They knew this, too, so she just wanted to go have some time to herself before one of them felt obligated to ask. It had been too much in one day, and she couldn't take dumping anymore on her friends.
