She wasn't afraid to tell them necessarily. Solar trusted them, they were her childhood friend group that grew up together. The five of them were the inseparable idiotic friends you saw in the movies.

However, the thing about her friends were that they were good. When they were in high school, none of them ever made a grade lower than a 90%. They weren't late for classes, they rarely missed days, and they took every AP class that was offered. Every year they were the top four of the class, with no one ever able to beat their scores. Even then, her friends had always had an undeniable cutesy look to them, which had girls interested.

They were never as popular as Sunwoo and the others though. It was why her friends hated when she was with him, they were scared for how it changed her. Her drug problem was technically his fault, after all. They didn't want to see Solar throw her life away when she had once been just like them.

But she had always been too attached to Sunwoo. Was it love? She couldn't tell you, but she sure thought it was back then.

So if you looked at it, maybe that was exactly what she did — throw it all away. All for puppy love, drugs, and fun.

Solar sighs, the thought of seeing not only Jaehyun but all of her siblings tomorrow creating a pit in the bottom of her stomach. She felt sick just thinking about it, being so worried that they wouldn't want to see her or something bad would happen. Although Jaehyun had texted her (they exchanged numbers when they parted) telling her they would be thrilled and nothing could go wrong, that didn't make her worries disappear. It had been five whole years, for God's sakes.

Her head was starting to hurt thinking about it. Because of it, and the miscarriage, and everything else that had happened today right before her eyes, she was feeling overwhelmed. She was home, but it didn't feel right all at the same time.

Not knowing how to fix it, she holds a hand to her head as she tries to calm her panicking emotions. Everything will be fine, is what she tells herself, but she couldn't make her mind believe it.

Picking up her phone, her heart speaks for her as she peers down at the names on the screen.

"Soomi, hey, are you doing okay? How is everything?"

His voice was like heaven when it seeped into her ears. A grin spreads over her lips as she holds the phone tighter against her ear, just wanting to hear him speak more and more.

She was so happy to hear his voice, so much that she completely forgot how they left off last time until he cleared his throat, "Soo, by the way, since we haven't got to talk, I wanted to tell you I'm not mad at all."

"Actually, it just made me love you more," he admits, "seeing what you've grown into after the way you were treated, what you went through, it makes me realize how you really are the love of my life, Soomi-ah."

"And about our manager, we contacted the company already and they're finding us a new one, so don't sweat it at all, okay? Everything is alright, and we all love you, especially me."

She didn't know she was crying from his heartfelt words until she felt something wet dripping onto her lap. Wiping under her eyes, she sniffles as she responds weakly, "Jinani, you really are such an angel."

"That's what you make me, Soo."

"Hey, do you want to come over?" She suddenly blurts before she could process the words that were coming out of her mouth. By the time she realized what she just asked, it was too late to take it back.

There's a long silence between the phone call. It was so much that Soo thought the connection might be bad, or that he was just not going to respond at all. She had basically ran out of KCON after seeing her ex and her brother, leaving poor Junhoe to carry the weight of telling everyone about her past because she was too afraid to do it herself after seeing Jinan's reaction. She had overthought what he was really thinking, which hadn't been negative at all, but absolutely positive.

A part of her was mad at herself for thinking someone as sweet as Jinhwan would think illy of her. What could she really have expected though? Her past was dark, and she knew that even though all of her new friends were telling her they didn't think much of it — it had to really be bothering them deep down.

She hadn't exactly told Junhoe what kind of business her ex boyfriend had been involved in, she just said it was illegal and extremely bad if they were ever caught. Every other detail she shared, of course other than the fact she was pregnant, but it wasn't something she wanted to share with anyone.

"Come over?" His voice finally repeats, trying to confirm if he just heard her words right, "aren't you at your exes house?"

"No, no I'm not," she hurries to explain, "I'm at my childhood friend's, he really nice and not involved in any of that bad stuff, I think you would get along really well," she tried to persuade. Closing her eyes, Solar means her head against the headboard of the bed, trying to take in everything currently happening in her life. "He wouldn't mind at all, I think my friends really want to meet you and Jungkook."

Upon hearing this, an idea struck in Jinhwan's head.

"They haven't met Jungkook, right?" He hums casually as if he wasn't plotting something in his brain.

Solar chuckles from his idiotic question, not having any idea of what he was really thinking. "No, stupid, you're the only one I've contacted out of everyone."

"Wow, Soo, that makes me feel special."

"Cause you are!" She insists brightly, before calming down again, "but are you coming or not?"

He acted as if he was hesitating as he sighs into the phone. Really, Jinhwan had it thought out well. He thought back to the words Soomi's best friend in Korea, Jimin, had said to him.

I honestly feel bad for you, Jungkook's not afraid to get his hands dirty. There's no breaking that bond they have.

But he said that before they learned about Soomi's past. Now, there was a gate open for him, one that had no connection to the likes of Jeon Jungkook yet. It was one that opened the door for him to get his own hands dirty, just like Jungkook loved to do.

Even though he wasn't that kind of person, if this was what he had to do to make her realize the right decision, then he would do it. Jinhwan would have no sympathy for someone like Jungkook, he knew he hated him, after all.

Soomi's — or should he call her Solar now? — old friends were the key. The key to winning her heart for himself, and finally ending the horrid love triangle they were stuck in.

The only thing was, he didn't know that there were still more secrets a girl like Solar had hidden under her belt. Just when you think you really know someone do you learn you didn't all along, and Jinhwan had no idea what he was in store for. Someone as dangerous as Kim Sunwoo wasnt someone you wanted to mess with, and he loved to get his hands dirty — but not metaphorically like Jungkook.

He was a whole nother obstacle one would have to face, and Jinhwan didn't think about what could come of such as he'd sighed, "sure, Soo, text me the address, okay?"

"Okay!" She cries in excitement, glad she could fix the hole that she felt from missing her old friends, "thank you, Jinan, I'll see you soon!"

"I love you, Soomi."

It was a long time since she heard him say those words. Remembering the endless times they had said it to each other shot her heart with a ping, and she exhales deeply as she pulls the phone away from her ear, hitting the end call button.

She loved Jinhwan, she also loved Jungkook. There are many ways you can feel love for someone, and she knew that much. The thing Solar could never figure out was which love she felt was the true love they spoke about in the movies. How did you know?

And what about Sunwoo? How was she ever going to make herself come clean about this? She shouldn't be so afraid, it was in the past. There was nothing anyone could do, but she just felt so guilty.

Sunwoo wouldn't be mad at her. He was the kind that had a temper, sure, but when it came to Solar, he turned into the complete opposite — at least, that's what he used to be like. But they had never actually fought or had a problem like this, through out the seven years, they never had an obstacle like this one. In the end, she had no idea how he was going to respond to such a thing.

Holding a hand to her head, she tries to stop herself from thinking so hard. The thing was, she was bottling this up for so long, and as soon as she managed to forget and move on — it came right back to slap her in the face.

How stupid was she to think she could so easily run from her old life and all of the problems she once had? They would always follow her no matter how far she went.

Jinani:

I'll be there soon, taking an Uber now

She purses her lips in a thin line as she reads his text. He would be here soon, and she would be introducing one of her love interests to her old friends, there was no telling how it would go.

But before she had to face such, she knew one thing — she couldn't wait anymore. Solar needed to tell someone, it was eating her alive. She had never felt such a feeling before. It was like not only her mind and her body itself was caving in, giving up, but like the world around her was, too. Everything was fuzzy, everything was falling, as she shakily opens the bedroom door.

The wood floor is cold against her feet, and she stumbles a bit as she makes her way to Chanhee's room. She didn't want to worry anyone, she didn't want to stress anyone. Solar hated causing people trouble, it was all she had ever been doing with all of the catastrophe's.

But she was breaking.

"Sol?" Jacob was the first to turn and see her as the door creaks open. The other boys had been singing karaoke, and they all follow suit to Jacob from hearing the name fall off his lips.

As soon as they all see the look on her face, the music is turned off, and the three friends are running at her in concern. It had seemed like she was fine earlier after she got everything off her chest, so why was she crumbling in front of them all over again?

"Solar," Chanhee is before her, his hands going to hold her up by her shoulders when he noticed the way she kept wavering, "what's going on? You couldn't sleep, are you alright?" He taps her cheek a few times with his finger, seeing how she kept blinking like she was losing control of herself.

"Hee," she breathes out, closing her eyes again so her body wouldn't shut down from all the stress she was feeling, "I have to tell you all something, I-I can't keep this inside any longer."

The friends exchange worried glances, feeling uneasy because of the obvious questionable state she was in. 

Before she could say anything, Jacob slowly speaks up, "don't you think you should sleep first? You've had a hard enough day," but the girl vigorously shakes her head, her green eyes still squeezed shut.

Her body was yelling at her, but she ignored it.

"Sol, don't force yourself to tell us anything today," Kevin steps forward to pat her shoulder comfortingly, "you don't look so good, you should really rest."

"Kevin is right, for once!" Jacob nods in agreement.

She shakes her head again, causing Chanhee to frown in confusion. "Solar Lee, whatever it is is going to have to wait," he was in his motherly form in no time, lecturing her like he was actually any older than her more than half a year, "I can tell how much this is all weighing down on you, I can't stand seeing you like this, your body needs to rest."

"No," she mutters, barely audible, but the room was so quiet that they were able to hear it, "listen-"

"- Tomorrow, I'll help you into bed, come on," Chanhee instructs.

Just as he reaches out to grab her wrist to escort her away, she hurriedly steps back abruptly, causing the three friends to blink in surprise from her sudden jolt of energy when she had been standing there so lifeless. No one knew what to think, they'd seen Solar in many ways before, whether she was angry, high, or drunk. There seemed to be no way they hadn't seen her with how they all grew up together since they were mere children. But right now, they'd never seen her like this - she looked like she was going mental as her breathing becomes abnormal, as if she had just ran a marathon.

They were all worried for her in the state she was in. But no one knew what to do.

"Sol?" Kevin hesitantly calls to her, "are you feeling okay?"

"No, I'm not feeling okay!" she abruptly blurts, her hands going to her hair where she pulls at it in frustration, "I can't hide this anymore, it's killing me inside!"

"Then tell us, we're here for you!" Jacob cheerily reassured her even though he was feeling a bit afraid with this new side they were seeing.

Breathing out deeply, she turns away from them, closing her eyes once again. Now or never, she told herself something she had been commonly thinking these days. To think she was letting all of her precious secrets out, all the things she swore no one would ever know, the things she ran away from those five years ago. She should've known they were going to resurface.

She should've known.

"Five years ago, before I left for Korea, something happened that I kept from all of you," she gradually lets the words fall out, slowly turning to look back at her friend's blank faces as they wait for her to explain further.

It took the last bit of courage for her to say the words.

"I was pregnant," she whispers just loud enough so they could hear, "and I had a miscarriage."


76:00 | how to unhear

"Oh, you're the guy!" the orange haired boy cries in surprise, pointing a finger at the short boy that was standing on the doorstep, staring at him in confusion. Meanwhile, the naive boy that was looking back at him from the inside of the mansion only grins from ear to ear. He wondered if this was the infamous best friend he heard about, it sure wasn't the kind of person he expected.

Clearing his throat, he shifts his weight from foot to foot awkwardly, "uh, what guy?"

Changmin had chuckled before he brightly informs, "one of the love interest's that swept our Sol off her feet, I've heard all about you!"

Jinhwan's mouth forms an o as he takes in what he just heard. He didn't think she would have told them everything, but he guessed he couldn't really be surprised. These were her childhood friends, the people she truly knew all her life and grew up the same harsh ways she had. He thought that Jimin knew her inside out, but this was an entire new level. They knew how Solar lived because they lived through it with her. In that way, they had more of an advantage over any of her friends from Korea that could never be competed with.

Because of this, he was beyond determined. The approval of her truest friends was important to Jinhwan, he wanted them to love him. And the fact that he was meeting them before Jungkook would only make it better for him.

"Oh," he giggles lightly, nervously looking at his shoes and he nods, hands shoved in the pocket of his coat to fight the cold, "yes, that would be me, I'm Jinhwan, nice to meet you."

The eyes of the boy he had yet to know had looked him up and down. He wished he knew what he was thinking about him, but he always had the same smile on his face. There was no way someone could always be so happy.

"Changmin," he greets, "come inside, it's probably freezing out there," he moves aside, motioning with one arm and urging the boy inside. The heated air hits him as soon as he enters the mansion, and seeing the inside of such a place made it hard for him not to gawk like a child at Disneyland. He couldn't believe places like this were real, it was the kind of place that he thought only existed in the movies. As if the outside wasn't cool enough, the inside was insane.

He noticed the place was rather secluded. It was located in a forest like area almost in the mountains, with trees surrounding each side. The mansions, which he didn't count exactly but there seemed to be about six, where a couple miles apart, all upon the massive hill as if they formed some sort of pact. It seemed like a cozy neighborhood, but after the stories he heard, he could only wonder what it was really like.

"Everyone's upstairs," Changmin hums, and Jinhwan only nods in response.

In silence, the two begin their walk up the stairs. Changmin is humming a song Jinhwan didn't recognize. He tries to calm his nerves that were getting the best of his mind, recalling that Soomi told him everything would be fine and that he needn't worry about her childhood friends. He couldn't be so sure that he would perfectly get along with them, especially after seeing that ex of hers he didn't know about until yesterday. But after meeting this one, he realized they must not be anything like him.

He definitely had a lot to catch up on.

"You must be the one who helped her through the drinking problem, right?" Changmin suddenly hums as they reach the top of the stairs. He turns, halting his steps to peer at Jinan who wasn't all the way up yet, "you're really sweet to our Sol, I'm glad she had someone like you to look after her."

Pursing his lips, he stops at the top of the steps, too. "You don't have to thank me, I just want to take care of her."

He giggles a little as he looks Jinhwan up and down once again, gawking in disbelief, "huh, you're a lot better than someone like Sunwoo. I almost wish she didn't come back and get involved with him again," he sighs as he says this, a hurt look in his brown eyes. Changmin had stared at his feet, seeming to think hard about the words he just spoke.

They'd put a lot of thoughts in Jinhwan's head. He figured it would be now or never to start asking, trying to get a full grasp of the sort of life Soomi had. Although he heard pretty much everything about her family and what she dealt with, he didn't know exactly why Sunwoo was so bad and what he did that was illegal. After knowing he killed his manager that was after Soomi so casually, he could only worry about how bad it was. Not to mention, now that he was understanding Sunwoo was not involved with Soomi's childhood friends, it was making him feel a bit better.

"What exactly is up with him, if you don't mind me asking?" he says cautiously, watching the expression on the orange haired boy's face carefully to make sure he didn't mind his question. The happy-go-lucky Changmin didn't appear bothered at all, so Jinhwan adds, "I heard about most of her life, the drugs and everything, and I know that Sunwoo killed the woman that was after her, but what exactly does he do?"

Changmin hesitates, not knowing if it was okay for him to tell him. After hearing Solar talk about how much she liked this guy that was standing before him, he didn't think it would be a big deal. If they were really that infatuated with each other, they would have to know everything about their pasts eventually. What was the trouble in him telling him now since he already knew so much?

"Well," he sighs, knowing it might take him a minute to explain so he leans against the wall in the long hall they were standing in, "what all did you hear about the environment she grew up in exactly?" he clarifies first.

Pausing in thought for a second, Jinhwan recites, "I just know that her parents are business folk along with a bunch of other families in the same area, and they're really harsh on their kids. She was expected to go to some sort of big college, right? And she was with Sunwoo or something but they didn't like that, and she started doing drugs, and then they found out. Her siblings did them, too, and made her take the blame, and I think that's all I can really remember right now."

Changmin blinks a few times as he takes in what he just heard, having had received the full scoop. He had to confirm it all in his head, before he exhales deeply, "did you happen to look around when you came here?" he asks, and Jinhwan shrugs in confusion, not understanding what that had to do with anything.

"It's a little neighborhood with some mansions - but how is that relevant?"

"There's six, the residents in each are one of the wealthiest, well known business CEO's in the country, their wealth allows them to reside in this place, and it's a really big deal here," he begins, crossing his arms over his chest and breathing dully as he recalls everything, "any business folk that don't reside here envy anyone who does. The six families who reside here are mine, Solar's, the rest of our friends in there, and Sunwoo's."

It sounded like some sort of drama, and Jinhwan was eager to hear more about this weird place as he nods in understanding.

"The family that used to live in Sunwoo's was killed one time, and before we knew it, Sunwoo and his family moved in. It was really weird, and nobody knew how they died, but everyone kinda forgot about it," he could feel a nervous pit forming in the bottom of his stomach, Jinan already knew how this story was gonna end, "eventually, we found out his family killed them, as they had been the one keeping them from being able to move into the castle."

The Castle, he tries not to laugh. It was a funny name to call the neighborhood, but with how big the mansions were, he guessed it wasn't so far fetched.

"So, the way it works here, is if you're born into one the families, you're life is ruled. We're expected to make the highest marks in school, and when he graduate, we have to attend one of the best business school's in the world so we can become the next generation business CEO's for our families."

"Usually, the oldest son is expected to take this responsibility, but Solar's older brother wasn't the brightest, he always had other things on his mind. Solar, on the other hand, always focused on her studies and ended up deeming to be a better candidate than her brother, so she was supposed to take the crown for her father's company instead."

"She knew Sunwoo since she was a kid, and she always had this crush on him. His father, he's a CEO as well, but he plays dirty, and when he say he does illegal things - we mean they kill people, whether it's to get what they want, prove a point, or get revenge."

Suddenly, Changmin closes his head, leaning his head back against the wall as he tries to hold in his emotion. Jinhwan wondered what it was that he was about to say that could be making him feel that way.

"We didn't think they were like that at first. Solar's father and his father were associates in the beginning, but then they had this argument. Apparently he tried to sue him and take his company, and it created this big uproar. My father worked for Solar's, and he was the second in line at the company, so to get back at her father, he was killed."

Oh my God.

"It wasn't Sunwoo, because he was still too young to understand any of it. But eventually, that business was left to him instead, and it became his job to do his father's dirty work," he opens his eyes again, a sad look residing in them as he stares at his feet, "but she was head over heels for him, she always said he was different from his dad and stayed by his side anyway, even though he was killing people."

"She was different, is what we all realized as she got older, and eventually we found out she was on drugs," he frowns, hating the horrible story that was their teenage life, "there wasn't anything we could do even though we were so worried about her. I guess in a way, the things Sunwoo had to do aren't really his doings, cause he has no choice. It's not his fault he was born into something like that, but at the same time, he's not a good person."

Looking up, he meets Jinhwan's shocked eyes as he warns, "I can tell you really care about her from everything she said. If you cross him, be careful, he's not that different from his father like she'll tell you."

He was afraid to ask, "what do you mean?"

"I don't know what kind of things you've dealt with between that other guy she was speaking of," he sighs, "but Sunwoo is different, when I say he's dirty, I mean he'll kill you."

Speechless.

"Anyway, don't let it get to you, though," he shakes his head, sending him a small smile of assurement, "that's the castle for you, and the fucked up way of life that goes on here. It almost makes me wish Solar would've permanently stayed away from it."

There were still more questions he had, but he didn't have the strength to ask them then. Jinhwan already had a lot more to think about once again. He kept learning more and more by the second.

"We should probably go, I'm sure they're wondering what's going on," Changmin abruptly pipes up again as if he didn't just tell such a depressing, dark story. Spinning on his heel, he doesn't utter another word as he begins to walk down the carpeted hallway of the mansion.

Only muttering an okay, Jinhwan slowly follows behind him. His eyes wander the walls, looking at the pictures that hung upon them. He wondered what her other friends were like, honestly a bit excited to meet them since they seemed like such nice people.

The pictures, though, caught his eye. He noted that Solar was in a lot of them, being really young in those that she was. She was standing with a young couple, a bright grin on her face. But the thing that caught his eye was the boy standing beside her, he was the same height as her, black fringe falling in his soft face as he had his arm around her with the same massive smile. He wondered if this was the best friend, as it seemed like they were really close from all the photos.

Changmin had suddenly paused at a certain door, and he casually stepped forward to open it.

"Guys, guess what~," he had been sing-songing in excitement, knowing Solar would probably be really excited to see the guy he was with.

But he had no idea of the conversation that had been taking place behind the door.

77:00 | by your side

Changmin freezes in the doorway, mouth wide open like a hippo as he tries to process what he just heard and walked in on. The short boy behind him nearly passed out on the floor as he leans a hand on the wall beside him, trying to calm his brain as he attempts to think calmly about the word's that just came out of her mouth. Was he dreaming? Did he just hear Soomi say she was pregnant before?

Stop panicking, he tells himself in annoyance. It wasn't like that was something she had to tell him ever, he couldn't be upset about it. He guessed he was just in shock that she went through something like that.

"What?!" a voice he didn't recognize had cried in disbelief.

Sniffles are heard from the girl, as she had no idea that Jinhwan was there and just thought it was Changmin. By that point, she had honestly forgot the boy was even coming. She was too caught up in the emotions that were bursting out of her as tears stream down her face, "I-I'm sorry I didn't tell you guys."

Just over Changmin's shoulder, he sees a pink-haired boy frown. His feline looking face was easily recognizable as the one in the photos, and he watches as he steps forward, engulfing the girl into a massive hug. "Sol, Sol, it's okay, don't cry about it," he soothes her, rubbing her back as he calms her nerves. Solar had only whined like a baby as she wraps her arms around him. "I can't believe you went through that all on your own," he's gasping in surprise, petting her hair, "that really makes me want to beat Sunwoo's head in, that fucking useless garbage can."

"Is that what the fight was about?" Kevin had been questioning, trying to understand.

Wiping the tears from under her eyes, she nods, "yeah, the woman he killed found that out about me and she managed to blurt it out before she died," she explains, and everyone nods in comprehension. "I just didn't want to face him," she admits with a dull sigh, "but I guess I'll have to eventually within the next few days."

Just over Changmin's shoulder, Jinan can see Solar's friends pulling her into a big group hug. Changmin was still standing in the doorway, keeping Jinhwan's cover, and he nervously glances back to him to see his reaction. The orange haired boy knew that Solar's new friend from Korea probably was not supposed to hear that.

Jinhwan was speechless. He didn't imagine anything in Soomi's life to be how it actually was. A girl like her didn't seem to come from such a distraught past, with so many messed up things in her life. Not with the way she always smiled, laughed, and tried to make everyone around her feel as bright as she appeared to be. It was astonishing enough to hear about her drug problem, her exes illegal work, and the fact her siblings sold her out on it — now he was learning on top of all of it, she was once pregnant with Sunwoo's child?

Something like a miscarriage was never good, and he wasn't going to say he was glad it happened to her. But the thing was, with what he knew about her family and her ex boyfriend, he didn't think her having his kid would have gone over too well.

"Shh~, it's okay, Sol," Chanhee had been soothing as he rubs her back, and her sniffles had slowly began to stop.

"Sunwoo won't be mad at you," Kevin pats her back, rubbing circles on it to ease her emotional stress, "he still loves you, Sol, you know he would do anything for you — and something like that isn't your fault."

A shaky breathe left her lips as she pulls away from the group hug, hesitantly asking, "b-but isn't it? I knew I was pregnant a few weeks in, but I kept partying, I kept drinking and doing drugs — that has to be why it happened, right? So isn't it my fault then?" Her voice cracks at the end of her question. Overwhelmed with the guilty thoughts cowering her brain from all of her past mistakes, Solar falls to her knees, burying her face in her hands as she mutters out, "I killed my own baby."

"Hey," Chanhee had snapped, a sudden stern tone in his voice as he kneels beside her, "stop talking like that, or I'll call Sunwoo right now so maybe he can get it through your head that an occurrence like that isn't your fault."

Her green eyes are bulging when they look up to him. She knew he wasn't just saying that, he really would call him if she didn't listen.

Thrilled that his threat got her attention, he lays a hand on her shoulder, peering deep into her irises as he repeats, "it was not your fault, understand? You and all of your siblings had an addiction problem, and that's dangerous without proper help," he informs, "it was just an unfortunate thing that happened. It hurts, and it breaks your heart, but think about it, Sol," he pauses for a second, "could you have really taken care of that baby?"

His question left a dent in her heart. Knowing everyone in the room and her already knew the answer, her eyes wander to her lap, "no."

"So stop beating yourself up about it," he hums wittily, "Look at you now, you've grown and become the best you that you can be if you overlook all your guy problems, so don't dwell on the past and focus on what's to come." Chanhee's words were as sincere as she could remember. He was always an incomparable person for a reason, and she was being greatly reminded of that now.

"So, after you sort out your family, go see Sunwoo, okay? He's a dimwit and an absolute piece of shit, but God knows how much he cares for you, Sol."

She only nods, taking a few seconds of silence to just think about what he said. His words had crept deep into her skull, and she knew that he was right, more than anything. She couldn't sit around and cry about what happened, she had to open up about it in order for her to move on.

The same went with her family. It was time for her to fix everything, so then she could truly focus on what was most important — herself, and what she wanted in the future.

"Okay," she finally sighs in agreement.

A triumphant smile is on Hee's lips as he rises to his feet again, holding out a hand to help her up. Once the two are standing, and everyone sees Solar is feeling better after the heart to heart, Jacob claps in joy, "I'm gonna go get some drinks to calm our nerves, okay?"

He's walking towards the doorway, where Changmin was still awkwardly standing to hide Jinhwan, when Chanhee harshly calls, "no alcohol."

"I wasn't going to bring such," Jacob gasps, as if offended he would even say that, "I was thinking watermelon juice, since it's Sol's favorite."

Everyone hums in acknowledgement, and with that, he walks to the door. Blinking in confusion when Changmin didn't move out of the way, he's about to ask, when he looks over his shoulder. The sight of the new figure he had never seen before brought even more confusion over his face, until he remembered Sol telling them she invited one of the guys she had a thing with over so they could meet him.

"Oh, hi," he offers a welcoming, "You must be Sol's friend."

Swallowing the lump in his throat, he could hear the gasp from Solar. Changmin winces in worry as he steps away inside Chanhee's room to bury his face in a pillow. This left Jinhwan to be revealed, and the small girl was horrified to say the least.

"Hi, nice to meet you, I'm-" he was kindly offering to her friend, but the screech of the brunette had cut him off.

"Nani?!" She cries, "how long have you been here?"

Judging from the nervous look on his face, her question appeared to be answered. Face falling, she pouts as she mutters, "you heard everything, didn't you?"

Not wanting to interrupt, the friends keep quiet. Knowing he couldn't let anything else between them happen, Jinhwan refused to let himself run away like he did when he heard the truth about her life the first time. None of it was her fault, and he needed to comfort her, not stress her out even more and make her feel bad for the hardships she's gone through.

This is why he steps forward, padding across the carpeted floor until he's right before her. In a swift motion, Jinhwan grabs both of her hands and pulls her towards him until she softly falls into his chest and he could securely wrap his arms around her.

"I heard, I'm sorry, but don't think I'm mad at you or something," he mumbles as he kisses the top of her head, "I didn't mean to intrude, I know you probably didn't want me to hear it, but I won't mention it to anyone, okay?"

A small grin forms on her lips as she accepts his embrace. All of her friends were probably gawking right then, but she didn't mind it as she snuggles into his chest. The familiar cologne was a blessing to her, and hearing his voice was a miracle she didn't think she'd ever get again. With him in front of her right now, she was convinced everything in her life was right.

And it almost was. She only had a few more obstacles to overcome, but the worst part was those were going to be the hardest.

Either way, she finds a way to smile, "thank you, Jinan, I'm glad you're here."

It was clear she didn't want to talk about the subject of her miscarriage anymore. Such a thing was something between her and Sunwoo, and it would be rude of them to invade anymore of their privacy.

With that, the two pull away, and knowing her friends were about to explode, Solar turns to their expectant faces. "Guys, this is one of the guys I told you about, Jinhwan," she introduces, motioning to the short boy who politely bows to them even though he was in America.

After all, he was trying to make a good impression.

"Wow, he's handsome, Sol," Kevin is observing, "definitely an upgrade from Sunwoo."

Jacob had come back into the room by this time, giving everyone their drinks before joining the conversation.

"That's mean," Solar frowns, shaking her head, but not really wanting to say anymore about the topic.

Jinhwan curiously looks to her, but doesn't ask anymore. "Nice to meet you all," he grins to them.

"I heard what you've done for our Sol," Chanhee was humming gratefully, "I'm thankful she had someone like you to look over her during my absence."

He's about to speak up and thank him for his nice words, when he'd added, "but I'm back now, so don't think you can take my place."

Sighing in disappointment at her childhood best friend, Jinhwan only giggles, "don't worry, that's not my spot, but there are a couple of people you might want to talk to about that."

Solar winces as she's reminded of her best friends in Korea. The remembrance of Sunwoo saying Chanhee would be offended when he met Junhoe only made the thought worse.

"Don't mention them, please," she whines, hitting Jinhwan's arm who only looks to her blankly. If only he knew the damage he just caused.

Perhaps he was already getting an idea when the pink haired boy pipes up, "excuse me, who might we be talking about? You replaced me, huh?"

"Chanhee," she breathes in a pleading manner, rubbing her temples and hoping he would have some sympathy. But he only crosses his arms over his chest, continuing to stare at her.

Groaning, she insists, "I didn't replace you, I just met some close friends in Korea!"

"She's being honest, they're nowhere near as close as you two, I can already tell you that," Jinhwan cuts in.

Heaving out a deep sigh, he glances between the two for any sign of a lie. When he doesn't spot one, he hesitantly agrees, "I believe you, but I'm still going to need to meet them."

Rolling her eyes, she shakes her head, "that's for another time, Hee."

"Yeah, yeah," he waves a hand in dismissal, "I already know I'm irreplaceable anyway, oh, and my name is Chanhee."

"Beyonce said what's good—" Kevin didn't even get to finish his joke when Changmin smacked him with a pillow.

"Ow!" He was complaining as he shies away from his hits until he stops. By this time, he looks to Jinhwan, who was having a hard time holding in his laughter from whatever he just witnessed, "I'm Kevin, but you can call me Beyoncé's apprent—"

"Okay, okay, it was just a joke!" He was whining as he was being chased with a pillow again.

Shaking his head in disappointment at the two, Jacob is the last to introduce himself, "I never got to finish earlier, I'm Jacob, nice to meet one of Solar's friends."

Meanwhile, Changmin was still chasing Kevin, and didn't seem intent on stopping anytime soon. Chanhee kept telling them to cut it out because they were grown men and were acting like fetuses, but it wasn't stopping their actions.

"Just ignore them," Solar tells Jinhwan.

He only shrugs, "it's okay, it reminds me of home."

He wasn't wrong at all.


78:00 | softie for solar

"What the fuck!" Kevin had abruptly screamed down at his phone screen, earning a disapproved glare from Chanhee and an annoyed look from everyone else due to how loud he was.

They were just trying to watch a horror movie at 6 AM, which wasn't really anything new. This was a common event done by the group of friends back in high school. You know, before Solar kinda ditched them for Sunwoo instead.

Well, it had either been that or studying.

"Can you shut your mouth? It's about to be the best part!" Changmin snaps in frustration. Attitude from someone like the soft boy wasn't anything you'd see often, but it was just because he was seriously into horror movies.

Everyone is either trembling in fear or leaning forward in interest from the intensity of what was going on. It was what always happened in the scary movies. The eery music would start playing, and one of the characters would be going through something hesitantly before the expected jump scare would occur. It didn't matter how much you anticipated, sometimes you couldn't help but still jump even a little bit.

Solar giddily grins to herself as she lays her head on Jinhwan's shoulder, his arms secure around her waist as they sat in the crowd of her friends on Chanhee's bedroom floor. They had all talked a lot the last few hours, the majority being her friends practically instigating the poor boy. She felt like they were probably pretty annoying, as it was a really different atmosphere for him and he probably wasn't used to it like she was.

It made her a little worrisome. She didn't know how he would react to hearing about the 'castle' and the way of life around it. Upon hearing that Changmin had pretty much explained everything to him, though, she honestly felt sort of relieved since he didn't seem bothered.

Although, she didn't know how much exactly her innocent friend had told him.

Either way, Solar couldn't be more thankful for someone like Jinhwan in her life. She had called him instead of Jungkook for a reason. He wasn't competitive, he wasn't rude even if he didn't like someone, he was mature and kind hearted. It was why she adored him to no limit, and she was thankful he was so welcoming to her friends even when they were questioning him on every small thing they could think of.

He actually seemed to get along with them even better than she thought he would. The fact that at last, her two worlds were beginning to collide, was a utopia to her. Nothing as great as this did she ever imagine happening, but here she was.

And she believed it was only going to get better from that point on. She was going to meet her family head first tomorrow. This was a time for change.

With how tired she was from the long day, the movie didn't seem interesting to her anymore in the slightest. All that Solar could think of was the day ahead of her, and how badly she needed sleep.

"Sorry, I just saw something exciting," Kevin had been muttering after the scary part of the movie ended. He set his phone down to look to the others, who sigh as they peer right back at him unimpressed. "NCT announced a new comeback."

"Oh my — can you stop with that already?" Chanhee groans, rubbing his temples with one hand, "you must seriously be gay, you look at all these groups but yet you never ran into Solar?"

He shrugs, "I heard her music before but I didn't expect to see my best friend as an idol under a different name!" He cries in defense, frowning, "and I'm not gay, there's nothing wrong with liking a kind of music."

"Maybe if you would stop talking about it for three seconds, there are literally two idols in this room witnessing how much of a psychopath you are," Jacob shakes his head, not having noticed one of the two idols spoken about was fast asleep.

Jinhwan chuckles as he noticed this himself. Messing with Solar's hair with one hand, he hums, "well, there's only one that's awake, but yes."

"Say, Jinhwan, do you know NCT?!"

"I'm about to kick you out," Chanhee points an incriminating finger at the moon boy.

He only pouts, but doesn't really care about the threat. Instead, he turns his head to look to the short boy sitting across the floor.

Blinking a few times from the question in thought, Jinhwan hesitates as he replies, "I mean — yeah, I know them, I guess."

Before anyone could say anything to stop him, Kevin nearly explodes, "dude, you have to introduce me!"

Heaving out a sigh, Chanhee rolls his eyes, "what, are you going to take a flight with them back to Korea?"

Not caring for the question, he nods, "sure I am."

Queue more disappointed sighs. It was clear the old friends were not fans of how much Kevin liked NCT.

"Maybe," Jinhwan giggles lightly, "I think they might be at KCON this year."

He should've known it was a bad idea to say that.




+



Chanhee hums a song to himself as he flips the pancake on the skillet. His melodic, pretty voice flows through the air in sound waves, and if one were to hear it, they would probably assume the cute, pink haired boy was famous. In fact, it was shocking to know he wasn't. Really, Chanhee gave off an idol vibe, with the way he had his hair styled, the fancy clothes he wore, and how good he could sing. 

But a life like that wasn't one he could ever take. He and Solar always liked to sing together growing up. Ironically, they even joked a few times about being famous. Though neither of them ever imagined it was what they would actually do, at least he didn't. What happened with Sol and her family, though unfortunate at the time, had actually given her the opportunity to get out of the harsh reigns the castle had on her. Her parents kicking her out gave her the ability to actually pursue what she wanted to do, instead of being forced to go to business or law school to take on the family business.

He almost wished he could've had the same opportunity. But as the only child of the Choi family, there was no way he was ever getting out of something like this. His father's company was depending on him, and he was already attending Columbia University. This was what he had to do, no matter what he wanted.

Either way, Choi Chanhee was most definitely not someone to sit around and dwell on things. He was a vastly different person from his best friend, Solar, which was why he always had to remind her on what really mattered and keep her straight all the time. It seemed that without him, she'd been straying away from doing just that. It's okay, though, because she was back, and he wasn't intending to lose his best friend ever again. Even if she had to leave back to Korea soon, he would bother her to the ends of the Earth everyday.

It was early in the morning, and he wasn't surprised none of the others were awake. Always being a morning bird, he wasn't really phased by the fact he hadn't slept for more than four hours. Actually, Chanhee was used to it, he went to Columbia University, after all. Sleep wasn't in his forte. 

Humming along, he sets the last pancake onto the plate, that was piled high with over a dozen. He figured he needed to make a good amount if he was going to be feeding six people including himself. 

Satisfied with his creations, he claps his hands together triumphantly. Trailing across the kitchen floor of the mansion, he's about to fall onto one of the chairs at the breakfast table when the doorbell lets out a ding. It echoes through his ears, making them bleed from annoyance at the fact he was finally going to get a break.

Turning to the entryway that leads to the front door, he rubs his temples in frustration. It was probably the Fed-Ex delivering a package early or something, but he still wasn't thrilled about the interference. 

Flicking on the entryway light, he clicks the lock on the double doors. Pulling each handle open, the pink haired boy peers out into the morning world, not expecting to see who was actually standing on the other side of his front door.

Wind blowing through his hair and making it a mess atop his head, his leather jacket is hanging off one side of his shoulder, ear piercings dangling from his ears, and a intent look plastered on his face. Then again, Chanhee wasn't surprised as he peers at the boy standing on his porch, a blank look on his face as he sighs, "oh great, just the person I wanted to see at 10 in the morning."

Kim Sunwoo rolls his eyes, not caring much for the sarcasm Solar's best friend always sported. He wasn't here for him, anyway, so he cut straight to the point as he crosses his arms over his chest, deadpanning, "where's Solar? Let me talk to her."

No matter how intimidating most people found Sunwoo, Chanhee would never be one of them. Even though he could literally step on him if he wanted to, it didn't bother him in the slightest. He knew for a fact that Sunwoo could never actually lay a finger on him. Chanhee, after all, was Solar's beloved childhood, closest friend, if he ever hurt him, she'd hate him. 

Which was why he took it in his best interests to freely annoy the living fuck out of Sunwoo whenever he pleased.

"No," he hums blankly, "can't do that, pal."

Narrowing his eyes, he clearly wasn't pleased with the response he was given. "Are you fucking kidding me, is it really the time for this shit?" he snaps in bewilderment, impatiently, "I just need to talk to her about all of this, I'm not mad or anything, this is serious!"

Chanhee yawns from the fact Sunwoo wasn't impressing him and he was still tired. Leaning against the opened door, he shrugs, "she's not ready to see you, come back tomorrow."

Heaving out a deep exhale, Sunwoo's fists clench at his sides. His temper was always so bad, and it was clearly showing. It was taking all of him to not explode, as he closes his eyes momentarily to calm himself, before reopening them to look to Hee again.

"Chanhee," he breathes, "I know she's going through a lot right now, I know she's seeing her family today," he says calmly, "but please, I can't wait any longer, this is killing me. I just want to comfort her, don't you get that? None of it's her fault, I just want her to know she should've told me, that it's okay."

His abruptly heartfelt words took Chanhee back for a second. The shock is shown on his face, and he raises a brow from surprise as he takes in what he just heard from the cold-hearted gang leader.

For once, Sunwoo was looking at him pleadingly. Honestly, it was sort of scary. So much, that he finally groans, "alright, but just stay in here, I'm going to talk to her first," he commands, pointing a finger at him as if he could ever be terrifying. Sunwoo chuckles at the attempt nonetheless, gladly taking the invitation as he steps into the Choi mansion.

"I won't move," he assures him as they walk into the living room.

Already walking to the stairs, Chanhee takes a few steps before he turns back to Sunwoo, "you're really still that fucking whipped for her, aren't you?" he snorts, a knowing look on his face that told Sunwoo he didn't need to even reply. Giggling from the fact he knew it too well, he spins on his heel, "oh dear, this is one big mess," he mutters as he begins up the steps to talk to the girl.

He didn't think Sunwoo heard him, but he had. And he knew that he was no doubt right about that. 


79:00 | forward

"Huh?" Solar blinks as she searches for any sign of it being a joke in Chanhee's eyes. When she doesn't find any, her face falls, "fuck, I didn't expect him to be here today, this early!"

The pink haired boy is unimpressed as he stares at her from their height difference, "it's not early, Sol, it's 11," he informs her slowly, "and of course he's here, it's Sunwoo, do you expect him to be patient?"

Upon realizing his words that were too true, her green eyes fall to look at the ground. "No," she mumbles after a moment, "I just forgot how he was, I guess."

"Well," Hee sighs out, a hand going to her back to guide her towards the door, "welcome home, Sol, but I think you should talk to him, get one obstacle out of the way so you can face the most important one."

She squeaks like a child as he practically pushes her into the hallway of the second floor. They had been talking in the bathroom, so she could make sure she didn't look like a mess and none of the others overheard them. As far as they knew, they were all still asleep, including Jinan, but you could never be too sure.

"I didn't want to today!" she whines as he's guiding her to the steps, "this is a lot, you know!"

"Solar," Chanhee deadpans in that motherly tone, the one he used before he gives those lectures that make you feel like an idiot, "Sunwoo isn't mad at you, in fact, he just wants to comfort you. You know, it's been five years and he doesn't love you any less. Even though he's a worthless, ignorant, arrogant, psychotic, violent, vulgar, delinquent, mentally-lacking, piece of shit-"

"-Hee, I get it, you've never been very fond of him."

"Right, but he's soft for you, he always has been. It doesn't make me hate him any less, and God, I am not doing this for him," he wrinkles his nose in disgust at the thought, "I just want you to be able to focus on what matters instead of possibly stressing over a past incident that surfaced as if it's something horrid when it's not at all. You just ripped the band aid off, but the thing is, Sol, it's already healed."

She's staring at him as if he was mentally insane. However, after thinking about what he just said and his lovely analogy at the end, she nods hesitantly, "okay, you're right, honestly even about all those insults."

Giggling, he pats her back, urging her towards the stairs, "when am I not? Now go."

"Okay!" she puts her hands up in defense, already on the first step that heads into the living room. Chanhee blows her a kiss when she looks at him one last time, descending the steps more. Rolling her eyes at him, she takes one last deep breathe as she steps onto the carpeted floor of the mansion's living room.

Elegantly decorated, family portraits were nearly everywhere you looked. If it wasn't a family one, it was Chanhee, as he was the only child of the Choi family and therefore was their pride. She adored his parents and how much they loved him, even if they did work him hard. They had still raised him to be a smart, amazing person in the end and she was thankful to them for that.

She didn't have the chance to really look around at the home that was once practically her second. As soon as she stepped onto the floor, the sound of someone immediately jumping to their feet was heard, and only a second later had his voice cut through her ears, "Solar?"

There's a lump in her throat she didn't know was there when she tried to speak, resulting in her coughing as she bends over slightly to cover her mouth. Nothing had really changed, when you thought about it, just because they got older didn't mean their feelings for each other ever truly resided. Solar moved on to other people and forced herself to forget about Sunwoo because he was supposed to be a part of the past she never planned to revisit - until now.

Yet, here he was, standing before her. Sunwoo, her boyfriend through out her teenage years, had finally found out about her biggest, deepest secret she deemed she would keep forever. The truth of her miscarriage had never been something she wanted to share with anyone, hence why even her best friend since practically birth hadn't known about it. She tried to tell herself when it happened it wasn't a big deal, that it was for the best. But it didn't mean that the event hadn't left a permanent scar on her heart.

She tried to think, in that moment, what Sunwoo must be feeling. Of course, Chanhee said he wasn't mad at her (because it was impossible for him to ever be,) but she knew he still had to be hurting. Never did he like to show emotion, he always tried not to. Sunwoo wanted to appear cold, mean, ruthless to the outside because it was how he was raised to be. It'd taken years for him to finally warm up fully to Solar and actually show her what he really felt inside.

Closing her eyes, she dares herself to open them as she stands up straight again. There had been a silence in the living room of the Choi mansion, only containing the two, and she didn't think Sunwoo had been walking up to her until it was too late.

The feeling of his arms wrapping around her, her body flailing forward to hit his chest with a soft thud, caused her to let out a small squeal of shock like a child. Her heartbeat is pounding out of her chest by that point, and her breathe hitches in her throat as she stands there in his embrace, not knowing what to do.

"Sol," he mutters a second later, his chin resting atop her head from how much taller he was than her, "Sol, I'm so sorry, I can't believe that happened," he's whispering in disbelief, his words getting quieter and quieter as he went on, "I can't believe you went through it all alone, I can't believe I didn't notice my girlfriend was pregnant."

Awkwardly, she clears her throat, her arms still by her sides as she only inhales the scent of the cologne and smoke, "I was good at acting."

"I'm sorry, I'm so fucking sorry," he ignored her small comment, clearly too overwhelmed with the emotions he was feeling, so much so that the cold-hearted Kim Sunwoo was letting them all pour out at once, "I know you must've been scared, I know your parents wouldn't accept it so you were hiding it, and I know you didn't tell me because you thought I'd be mad or do something stupid."

She didn't say anything. He knew her well enough to have been correct.

"Which all of those aren't wrong, but I love you, and even if I did freak out - I want you to know I would've still loved that baby," his voice is so low it's barely audible. If he wasn't so close to her she'd have no idea what he was saying. "So, I'm sorry, Sol, I'm sorry that it happened to you, I'm sorry that the world took our baby from you, even if it would've been an inconvenience."

Silence, she was in a state of shock, and it'd overcome any ability of speaking she once possessed. Her head was spinning, and as if it couldn't get any crazier, she jumped out of her thoughts from the feeling of something wet dripping onto her shoulder. 

A sniffle is followed, and she slowly steps back, out of the embrace of her ex boyfriend. Worried about her seeing the state he was in, Sunwoo immediately dropped his head as she stepped back to peer at him. His fringe falls in his face, covering every aspect of it so she couldn't see it. But either way, she knew all too well what was going on.

Here he was, the gang leader, vulgar, Sunwoo crying his eyes out before her. Such an incident only made her realize that he really was hurting from this, it wasn't just her. That baby that she carried at one point in her life, it was Sunwoo's too, and it made it all the much worse that she'd hid it from him for six years.

"Kim Sunwoo," she exhales, stepping forward so she could cup his cheeks and force him to look at her. Seeing those usually hard irises of his glassy broke her heart, so much that it was hard for her not to tear up herself as she met them. 

Blinking rapidly so she didn't cry, she hurriedly raises her thumbs to wipe the tears that fall from his eyes away. "It's okay," she assures him in a small voice, "I was planning to tell you soon, you know?" she begins to explain. Sunwoo had finally met her eyes from the sound of her telling a story, and he grabs her wrist, leading her over to the sofa so they could sit.

He'd practically pulled her onto his lap before she could even try to sit herself. Solar didn't care, though, since she was in the middle of telling the story. 

"That day, you caught me when I was leaving to go to my doctor's appointment, I told you I just wasn't feeling well," she begins hesitantly, and Sunwoo purses his lips as he listens. Thinking back to their highschool years, a wave of realization washes over his face when he seemed to know exactly what day she was speaking of, and he nods intently for her to go on.

"Yeah, I was going for a check up that day, and uh - the doctors had gotten really quiet when they were doing the ultrasound," she clears her throat, recalling back to that day in the hospital, "I didn't think there was anything wrong, I mean I had been getting cramps and hadn't been in the weather but I-I didn't think it was that."

Frowning, all the boy knew to do was hug her. His arms are securely around her waist as he squeezes the life out of her, his head resting on his shoulder as he patiently hears everything she has to say.

"I didn't know what I was going to do with the baby, I was still thinking about it and when I decided I was going to tell you," she explains, feeling tears well in her eyes as the guilt nearly eats her whole, "then when they told me - I just didn't know what to think. I didn't feel anything, I wasn't sure if I should be happy or sad - but thinking back to it now, I realized it was my fault I had the miscarriage in the first place, I'm a horrible person, aren't I, Sunwoo?" she rambles all over again like every time she tries to talk about this subject. 

"Sol-"

"-I just feel like it's my fault, I was always drinking and doing drugs while I was carrying a kid, our kid! I didn't have any consideration or sympathy that I was carrying a living, breathing being inside of me, one that had a right to live, and it lost it's chance thanks to my idiocy, maybe I don't even - ow!"

Sunwoo had used two fingers to pinch her side rather hard, earning a curse from her as she was rudely cut off by her story.

"Solar, stop," he demands sternly, "none of it's your fault, and if it is, I'm at fault, too. It was our baby, and I'm going to regret how I wasn't there for you and didn't notice for the rest of my life," he sighs, "but I cannot stand seeing you beat yourself up over this, alright?"

"Okay," she mutters, not used to receiving these sort of words from the likes of Sunwoo.

"Sol, I'll always love you," he gushes, "and that baby, our baby, doesn't blame you, okay?"

"Okay."

"Good," he hums satisfied, picking her up by her hips and setting her on the sofa beside him so he could rise to his feet. She's confused what he was doing when he instead kneeled before her, going between her legs and reaching up to wipe any tears that dared to leave her eyes away. "If I wasn't allowed to cry, you're not either," he informs, "all we can do now, Sol, is move on. You're a better person now, and you've got a big day ahead of you."

She nods in agreement as his hands fell from her face. Standing up again, he grabs both of her wrists, forcing her back to her feet with so much force that she flew right back into his chest. Of course, this was his plan the entire time as he cheekily hugs her all over again.

"Are you really going to see your family today?" he's asking a second later, wanting to clear the air of the touchy subject. It was what he always did since he wasn't a fan of showing emotions for long. "I'd offer to go with you, but I think that'd ruin the reunion."

Not minding the fact they were dismissing the other subject, she braces herself mentally to really let it go. The miscarriage had been following her around in her shadow for six years, and she had finally let it out in the open. She learned from the mistakes of her past, and it was time for her to grow from them and move forward with herself.

What good would be found by her dwelling on them?

"Yeah," she hums in his chest, finally accepting the embrace by wrapping her frail arms around him, "I don't think it'd be a good idea for you to tag along though."

"Another time?" he jokes, raising a brow.

There's a disapproved look on her face, but Sunwoo's remains hopeful. Honestly, she was just glad to see him showing joy, so much that she gave in, "alright, maybe."

Silence overcomes the two highschool sweethearts, but it was a comfortable one to say the least. There they stood in the middle of her best friend's living room, hugging each other like there was no tomorrow. In that moment, there's a feeling of bittersweet that sinks into their veins. It was a feeling of nostalgia, from the fact they missed how it used to be back then. In a way, even, they almost wished they could go back, maybe relive it all over again but just do some things differently. Maybe they could've saved their child, and ultimately, maybe Sunwoo could've saved Solar from leaving.

But they'd have to face the fact there was no going back. Their high school days were over, even as saddening as it might be sometimes. At the end of the day, they were grown up, and it was time to face the music that the real world was. 

It was time for Solar to go home.

"Sol, are you ready?"

80:00 | my jinani

A shocked cough full of awkwardness leaves her lips at the unexpected voice that trailed from the bottom of the stairs. Not knowing what else to do because she had been in this situation many times, the frail girl attempts to step back out of the embrace she was in so it didn't look as bad as she presumed.

She should've known, however, that the competitive boy wasn't planning to let her go just for the sake of making Jinhwan think otherwise. The thing was, he wanted him to think what he was thinking. He wasn't aware of the talk he and Changmin had, about how Jinhwan was aware the kind of person he was. Sunwoo had no idea that he was smart enough to come up with an entire plan for winning over Solar. It didn't matter to the short boy that there was a new interest in the mix, in fact, it only made it easier for him.

A smirk is on the gang leader's lips as he pulls the girl tighter against his chest. Solar heaves out a sigh of annoyance, knowing he was doing it on purpose. It was a good thing she was soused to dealing with the jealous, competitive type. After dating someone like Sunwoo for seven years, and then Jungkook, it helped her tolerate from wanting to knee him somewhere he wouldn't like very much. She was also thankful that even though Jinhwan could get a little competitive, too, he wasn't petty like the other two.

In a way, Jungkook and Sunwoo had a lot of similarities. Sunwoo was just the vulgar, dangerous version of Jungkook, which made him look a lot more innocent than he was when you put them side to side.

"Uh, yeah!" she blurts before anyone could say anything, responding to the question he asked as soon as he got down here. This was her queue for Sunwoo to let her go, but when she tries to step back out of his grip again to go to Jinhwan who was waiting for her in the middle of the room, he doesn't.

Not pleased with whatever he was pulling, she turns her head in his grip to glare at him as a warning. To think they just had such a sweet, heartfelt talk, only for him to turn around and start acting like this. She sure hadn't missed this aspect of his personality.

"Sol, do you want me to drive you there? I won't go inside or anything," Sunwoo offers, and she rolls her eyes at him as she turns away. There was only one reason why he was offering that, and they both knew it well.

She didn't even get a chance to speak when Jinhwan had cleared his throat, directing the attention in the living room to him. A jingle sounds through the air, and the keys to a Lykan shone under the chandelier. She knew exactly who's car it was even before he said it, "no worries, Chanhee was kind enough to offer me his car," he informs as he flashes that innocent grin their way.

He played it well. Jinhwan knew what he was doing. You could tell it made Sunwoo's blood boil. However, he shows no amount of emotion on his face as he slowly lets the girl go, "alright, nevermind then."

"Okay, well I need to get going then, I've got a big day ahead," she tells her ex boyfriend with a small smile, but doesn't move from her stance. She was waiting for him to leave first.

Sunwoo didn't move a muscle at first. His brown eyes kept jerking from Solar to Jinhwan back and forth, something clearly was running through his mind.

Finally, he exhales deeply, shoving his hands in the pocket of his leather jacket, "good luck, Sol, make sure you come back by to see us, everyone already misses you."

She only nods to him in reassurance.

Knowing there wasn't much more to be done here and that he had stuff to take care of, Sunwoo purses his lips in thought as he makes his way to the door. He'd only taken a few steps when he pauses, a wave of realization washing over his face.

Spinning on his heel, he gawks in disappointment at himself, "Oh, Solar, I forgot."

Confused at what it could be, she blinks at him as he nears her again after having about to of left. His eyes are narrowing as he grows closer, and it sent a worrisome feeling up her spine. There's nothing good on his mind right now, she noted to herself, about to of stepped back because she had a bad feeling about what he was planning.

But before she can, he reaches out to grab her wrist. One hand grips it so tight it was bound to break from her arm, and the other cups her cheek. Before the girl could react, he'd leaned in to leave a lingering kiss on her lips.

She's left in a state of shock, not kissing him back because she was trying to process the fact he actually just pulled something like that. A look of deviation is left on the boy's face when he pulls away, his boots against the floor the only sound in the room.

It was dead silent, and knowing why he did such a thing, Solar didn't want to see Jinhwan's expression.

"See you, Sol," he hummed out as his hand was on the door handle, before meeting the darkened eyes of the short boy across the room, "you too, uh - sorry, I don't remember your name." A short snort of humor had left his lips, and not caring to hear what they had to say, he whips open the door and heads out into the distance, slamming it closed behind him.

A breathe leaves her lips as she watches him disappear to his car out the window. Wanting to handle what just happened now rather than later, she turns to Jinhwan, her words rushed, "I did not think he was gonna do that-"

"- It's okay," he cuts her off with a shrug, "I know he was just trying to mess with me."

She peers at him in bewilderment, "well, yeah, but I thought you might be a little upset about it."

Jinhwan only shakes his head, sending her a smile, "no, it's okay, Soo. I know the kind of person he is, plus I've already been dealing with Jungkook."

Surprised from how nice he was being about it, her eyes wander to her feet. "That's sort of different," she mumbles, not expecting Jungkook to have come into conversation as well.

"I know," he says again, and his footsteps echo through the room as he walks up to her. His hand reaches out, grabbing hers and engulfing it despite how small it was. From the sudden proximity, her eyes look up to him again, and his brown irises are bright when they meet them, "I know you're going through a lot right now, I'm not going to get mad at you for liking anyone else or for having someone liking you," he assures her, squeezing her hand affectionately, "I love you, Soomi, and I don't blame them for loving you, too."

"Nani," she mutters in disbelief, not used to having someone so sweet in her life. Like she said, being with people that were understanding wasn't usually something she experienced. Even her and Jinhwan had their issues with jealousy, but over time, he turned into more of an angel than he already had been. She couldn't ask for anything better.

So maybe it was him.

"I love you, too," she says aloud before she could stop herself. Those three words were something he hadn't heard in reply for a long time, and hearing them fall from her lips again sent his heart into a frenzy. Fireworks implode in his chest, and he feels it clenching from the way she nervously flashes a small smile at him when he looks to her in shock.

At a loss for words, all he knew to do was pull her in for a hug. Jinhwan knew her heart wasn't full decided, he knew she didn't know who she wanted to be with yet. There was still problems left unsolved, still things to sort out, and he wasn't expecting her to make her decision any time soon.

But hearing her assure him that even if she loved someone else, that one of them was him, was enough to make him hold onto her until she did know.

He could only hope it was him.

"Nani, you're gonna kill me, you know," she's gushing from how tightly he was hugging her. The boy quickly lets go when he realizes this, an apologetic but giddy look on his face when he pulls away.

She only shakes her head at how adorable he was, not minding it at all. His hand remains in hers as he drags her towards the door, jingling the keys in his other hand.

"I already told your friends we were gonna go," he tells her, "they said they would be texting you, so you should probably check your phone."

"Oh, okay," she nods as he opens the door, and the two walk outside into the open world of the castle. The other mansions are able to be seen in the distance, but only the ones that were around the Choi's, since they were rather spread out. Jinhwan's grip remains tight as they go down the steps onto the road, walking towards the expensive car her best friend owned.

"You know," she says as they're about to reach the car, "I'm shocked you're taking this all in so well."

Jinhwan purses his lips as her words. It would probably be the time to tell her that Changmin had pretty much explained everything he didn't know ono the way to Chanhee's room. He waits until they both climb into the front seats on the expensive car to clear his throat, "your friend kinda told me about this place you grew up in, or more like what I didn't know. He also told me about Sunwoo, so I figured the best way to deal with him is by just ignoring him."

Solar chuckles, nodding in agreement. Honestly, she wasn't bothered by Changmin telling him, it was whatever. At this point, nothing in her life was a mystery, she wanted to be an open book to her friends in Korea. And the first one she was showing everything, was of course Jinhwan.

"Ah," she hums, "yeah, that's probably smart. I haven't told him we have any sort of thing, but we didn't exactly break up when I left," she explains casually, inhaling the fresh, flowery scent from that freshener Chanhee still kept in this car. "So, I guess he doesn't really know, but being that he's Sunwoo, he's going to assume we're pretty much still a thing," she goes on, and Jinhwan nods intently as he starts the car, pulling out of the driveway of one of the castle's, "plus, all the gang stuff is honestly sorta crazy to me now after living in Korea."

"Yeah, it is," the caramel boy is agreeing, "I didn't think there was actually people like that, thought it was just in the movies."

Giggling, she leans her head against the cold glass, "well, I would, too."

"It's also sort of dangerous, like what if anyone finds out you're associated since you're so well known now?"

She sighs from his question. This was something she had been thinking about recently, along with the rest of the things on her list of problems. The truth about it was, she didn't have an answer, which is why she could only mumble, "I really don't know, Jinan."

Not wanting to worry her because she was already nerve-wrecked enough with what had been going on, he pipes up, hoping it would take it off her mind, "don't worry about it, though, I don't think you have anymore stalkers, Soo."

But you couldn't be so sure. Thinking back to the night Sunwoo had killed the manager, she did say she had someone else. Although it could have just been a scare tactic, her attempt to always keep them all on edge. Maybe there wasn't really anyone and she just made it up last second so that they were always looking, always paranoid.

Although that could very well be factual, there was still a likely chance she meant it. Someone else had to be working with her, and Soomi was annoyed with herself that she didn't think of it sooner. Sunwoo had been right that night in the basement. Her being the only one left too many holes in the plot.

How would she have pulled that off all by herself? Would she really have been following her all the way back to when she was engaged to Jungkook? That was the only way she would've ever known all that, the only way it would make sense for her to be the stalker because they had been threatening Jungkook back then, too.

How would she have lured out the producer's at KCON all by herself and still have managed to be in the producing room to channel the things onto the main screen? How would she have gathered all that information on her alone? She always seemed to know where she was, who she was with, what she was doing. Something like that, when you really thought about it, wasn't physically possible for one person to accomplish. Not to mention, her apartment key.

It was too confusing and worrisome for her right then. To think the fight wasn't really over.

"yeah," she finally breathes out, not wanting to bring it up to Jinhwan, "yeah, it's okay."

Gripping the steering wheel as they drive through the streets of the castle, Jinhwan glances over to see the distressed look on the girl's face. A pout takes over his lips knowing he caused it by bringing it up. Reaching over, his hand rubs her thigh comfortingly, and when her green eyes jolt to him he sends her a reassuring smile, "Cheer up, Soomi-ah, following every day is a better tomorrow."

She'd been peering at him blankly. It taken her a moment, but when she fully comprehends what he'd just said, she starts chuckling in disbelief. A proud, giddy grin is slapped on the boy's face, and Solar can only shake her head as she gawks, "I can't believe you even remember that."

The girl was so focused on thinking about what he said, being brought back to those times, that she didn't notice the familiar house he was parking in front of. Glad to see her feeling better, Jinhwan shrugs, "I remember everything involving you, Soo," he informs, "like when I took you to our dorm for the first time at like 3 AM, and you screamed out bling bling when you saw the pictures on the wall."

Bursting into fits of laughter, she holds a hand over her mouth to stifle it. Her eyes brighten as she looks to him, waiting for him to say it was some sort of joke. But his face remains completely serious.

"What?!" she gasps in shock, "I don't even - I did that?! That's so funny!"

He nods, "yeah, you don't remember because you were drunk, but you definitely did that. You saw Junhoe and called him the handsome one, then said he was better looking than I was," she only continues to look at him like a mental patient, "I was pretty hurt, honestly."

"Shut up," she snorts, shaking her head as she tries to remember any aspect of what he was explaining. But all any of it brought up in her brain was a blank slate, "That's fucking insane, I can't believe Junhoe never told me that," she cringes, not even wanting to imagine it, "and the fact I said he was better looking than my Jinani," she frowns.

A lump forms in Jinhwan's throat when the girl turns back to him, the fact she just called him hers nearly causing his breathe to hitch. He remains calm as she unbuckled her seatbelt so she could scoot over to him. The leather seat squeaks under her weight, and there's a devious grin on her lips when she trailed her thumb across the boy's cheek.

"Sorry I said that," she mumbles a second later, her hand falling back to her lap where Jinhwan's hands were resting since there wasn't really anywhere else to put them. Soomi grabbed one of his hands, messing with his fingers as it was one of her many habits, "I must've really been blind, huh?"

Raising a brow, he couldn't help himself. "So, Junhoe's not better looking than me?"

Rolling her eyes, she hits his chest lighter than usual, so he didn't even budge from the action, "No, idiot."

"Yah," he cries from the name, "that's a rude thing to call someone, Soomi-ah."

"Oh please," she chuckles, "have you heard the things you call me?"

Already knowing what she was referencing, he giggles, "that's in a different situation," he corrects, "and since when did you make the sex jokes?!"

"I don't know, I was channeling my inner Kim Jinhwan."

"Oh - wait what does that even mean?!"


81:00 | here goes everything

"Soo, we've been sitting here for an hour," he clears his throat hesitantly, "I don't want to rush you, but how are you feeling?"

Jinhwan was, like usual, being nicer than he needed to be to her. She didn't deserve the angelic being that Kim Jinhwan was, it was proven to her every single day. The brunette had let out a deep breathe as she looks out the window again, peering across the road at the house she grew up in. The place she lived her life in, the place she never thought she would leave so soon. Most importantly, the place her family was, her home was right before her, and she was finally returning after so long. 

Five whole years had passed since the day her parents found the drugs, since the day her siblings blamed it all on her, since the day her father lashed out and told her she was no longer a part of the family. They said that time healed wounds, but she could only agree with such a saying partly. Although it had been so long that it didn't make her cry and fall to her knees anymore, it didn't mean her heart didn't still clench in agony at the remembrance of how she was treated.
