At the same time, she couldn't really blame them. Soomi had done it to herself, she had taken the drugs she was once offered, she had gotten her siblings to do them as well. She started the mess, so was it really wrong for her to have been blamed? 

Drugs weren't something that was openly accepted by anyone except by people in a community like Sunwoo's. Anywhere else in the castle, it was a serious crime. She should've known doing them would catch up to her eventually.

Exhaling deeply, Jinhwan continued to rub her thigh with his thumb, a way of comfort he had been doing fairly often that day. He didn't mind the fact she had yet to answer him, because it was clear she was thinking hard. This was a big deal to her, and honestly to him, too. So he didn't care that she was taking a long time, he understood.

"Yeah," she blurts abruptly into the silence of the car, surprising both herself and the boy that was right next to her. 

"Huh?" he blinks at her, and she had slowly met his confused eyes, "are you sure? That was really sudden."

"Yeah," she repeats, letting the words slip out of her lips before she changed her mind and she stopped herself. Quickly, the brunette fiddles with the door handle before pulling it, swinging the passenger door open. The air slaps her in the face as she climbs out, and an eery feeling creeps up her spine. 

Not knowing what to do with the sudden eager Solar, Jinhwan gradually climbs out of the car, too. Locking it after they both close the doors, he makes way around the back so he could stand next to her, making sure she was okay every step of the way. More than any other time, Soomi needed support right now. She'd called him out of all people to be the one to stand here by her as she did something so massive, as she came home for the first time after five years. 

She chose him, not Jungkook, or Sunwoo, or anyone else, but him. And he was thankful for that, it gave him hope that maybe he really was the one she wanted to be with after all of this was over. 

"Are you ready?" he slowly asks into the empty air, "do you want me to stay back a bit?"

They hadn't really talked about if she wanted him to meet her family or not. Jinhwan had sort of assumed he would just be here for moral support, not that she was really going to introduce him to them. 

Of course, if she had something else in mind, he surely wouldn't mind. 

"Uhuh, let's go," she nods, a small close-lipped grin on her lips as she seemed to be trying to assure herself as well as Jinhwan. Before she had some sort of seizure from the nerves that were climbing around her body rapidly, she grabs his hand. Jinhwan intertwines their fingers as she took the first step towards the house, having to cross the street to get there, "and I'm going to introduce you to them. I think they'll really like you, especially since my best friend's did."

She had yet to talk about that with him. But it was clear her old friends really loved Jinhwan. Although he was sort of quiet at first, through out the night they had gotten along great. Solar was honestly shocked. Chanhee in particular never liked anyone other than her and their tiny friend group. Never was he quick to trust, or trust period, so the fact that he welcomed Jinhwan as a love-interest in his best friend's life was insane. She couldn't believe her eyes when she saw how well they were all associating. Not to mention the fact he let him drive his car.

His beloved car that his parents bought him when they were still in high school. He barely let Solar drive it, so the fact he just gave Jinhwan the keys - how much crazier could it get? 

But the fact that her old friends really liked him gave her hope that her family would love him as much as she did. 

"Oh," Jinhwan coughs a bit in surprise, realizing what was really happening, "o-okay, that's fine."

They crossed the street, and were standing on the curb of the mansion. Soomi had glanced to Jinhwan, noticing he was sort of nervous now that she told him the news. To reassure both him and her, she gives his hand a squeeze.

It'd seemed to snap him back into reality, as he glanced to her. A smile forms on his face, and he grips her hand tighter as they finally begin to walk across the green yard. 

Jinhwan couldn't be nervous. He had to be Solar's wall, so it would only make it worse if he was also scared like she was. This was a bigger deal for her than it was him. If she said they would like him, he was just going to have to believe her.

"Here we go," Solar mutters is disbelief, exhaling deeply to calm herself once again as they're stepping up the steps to the porch. The marble had clicked under their shoes, and she inhales as she glances around the porch in reminiscence. It didn't look much different. The only thing she noticed was that there were new cars parked in the driveway, which wasn't shocking since her parents liked to buy new vehicles every year. 

The only one she recognized was her car that Chaerin seemed to be driving now, last time she checked. She guessed her sister just never had the guts to sell it. 

"I'm right here, Soomi," Jinhwan reminds her when he notices the way she kept practically trembling, "don't forget, okay? Everything will be fine."

"I know," she nods vigorously, and Jinhwan could only peer down at her in worry. He knew how much she was going through right now, and how it was probably weighing down on her mind. She really was strong for going through all this. He couldn't admire her anymore than he did for that.

"Whenever you're ready," he tells her, rubbing the back of her hand with his thumb. 

She exhales again, closing her eyes momentarily to really take in what was going on. Her mind wanders for a long moment. It wanders back to the day, to the flight to Korea, to her living with her relatives as she trained at Big Hit. It wanders back to her lying to them, to when she met Jungkook, to when she left to join YGE. Her engagement, Jimin, her debut - how her music instantly blew up in Korea, how she became an immediate hit on every chart, and everyone was desperate to see everything she had to offer. 

She was deemed the number one solo artist ever since she debuted. No one could top her, her fan base was solid, and she tried her hardest to appear bright and untouched to the world around her. She never really said anything to the media about her life, she always wanted to appear strong, like she had never experienced anything difficult in her life. 

It wanders and it wanders, through out the scandal, through out her meeting Jinhwan. The boy who hadn't necessarily swept her off her feet, but who she fell for over time. He was always there for her, he helped her through the drinking problem she didn't think she would ever get over. The boys of iKON, who she could stand at first, but that became her brother's over time. Her beloved Junhoe, the girls of BLACKPINK, Melon, MAMA, the fight that broke out between her and Jinhwan. 

Everything flew through her brain, and it followed the events of her life like a timeline. It was surreal to really process what you went through. Never did it seem like it was as extreme as it was until you truly thought about it. Only when it went through your head like a movie did one pause and realize - their life had been a maze, a wondrous ride. It made her ask herself, through out the entire roller coaster, did she like where she ended up at?

Was she glad she was here right now?

Snapping herself out of her deep thoughts, she looks to her side to answer that question. There's a bright smile on Jinhwan's face when he met her gaze.

The sight was enough to give her courage. She clings onto Jinhwan like her life depended on it, because honestly, it did.

"Don't ever leave, Jinhwan-ah," she mutters as she was dreamily staring at him. The words caught him off guard because of the situation the two were currently in. He didn't think she was going to blurt something like that out when they were standing on the porch of the Lee mansion, but he should've known since someone like Solar was unpredictable.

"I'm never going anywhere," he hums in response.

Pursing her lips, a child like grin takes over her face. Muttering words of encouragement to herself, she steps forward, Jinhwan's hand still stuck in hers like glue. Her heart is on her sleeve, and she refused to think about the negative's that might come out of this situation. Instead, she lets the moment take over, she lets her mind turn itself off. 

Solar lives in the present for the first time in her life as she reaches up, using all of her courage she'd gathered inside of her.

"No turning back now," is all she could mumble. 

Here went everything. 

82:00 | what now?

"Hey, did you talk to Solar?" one of the boys had hummed as soon as the sight of their leader entering the mansion was seen. His leather jacket was falling off his shoulders, and there's a rather annoyed expression that's on his usual blank face as he slammed the door behind him. Boots clicking angrily against the wooden floors, the boy who dared to ask such a thing first had restated his question, "did everything go okay?" as he watches Sunwoo maneuver around the furniture, falling back onto one of the sofa's around the other's. 

Everyone watched his actions in confusion, exchanging glances to see if anyone had any idea. None of the guys had talked to Sol since they saw her last, though, so they were completely in the dark as to what the problem was. All they knew was Sunwoo had been going mentally insane - more so than usual - due to the fact she was back in the first place. So, the news that she was once carrying his child but it was miscarried was a nuke in his heart, even if he didn't want to show it.

That was the thing about him. Sunwoo didn't show emotion, ever. Solar was his weak spot, hence her being the only one he ever allowed to see him in vulnerable states. 

With this in mind, you could imagine the bewildered expressions that took over the group's faces when he heaved out a frustrated sigh, leaning forward with his elbows on his knees and burying his face in his hands. Sunwoo was muttering inaudible things to himself, sounding like he was genuinely unstable, as he kept running his hands through his dark hair repeatedly, grumbling about.

"Uh," Eric had cleared his throat from beside him, knowing someone was going to have to ask him about this eventually, "did you and Solar fight?"

Sunwoo doesn't reply despite hearing the youngest's hesitant question. He only continues to sit, a bitter aura radiating off him as he kept heaving out deep groans. Everyone had the obvious idea it had to do with their old friend down, it was just the wonderance of what the hell had happened between them. 

But they didn't think anything would have. Sunwoo had been in irregularly high spirits when he left to go see her. He'd been talking about how he was going to fix everything, how he was going to comfort her, make sure she knew how much he loved her. It was horrifying to hear from someone like him, but because of how much they knew he adored her and had for as long as they could remember, the boys easily got used to hearing such things. 

It wasn't like Solar had been mad at Sunwoo. It also wasn't like there was anything for them to fight about, right? At least not regarding the baby. This is why everyone is left in bundles of confusion as they try to piece together an assumption in their minds.

Eric opens his mouth to start bothering Sunwoo again, upset that he ignored him, but Younghoon stops him as he warns, "Eric, it doesn't seem like he wants to talk about it, let him be."

However, immediately after the words left his lips, an exhale was heard from the brunette, "No," he monotones as he sits up straight as if he hadn't been on the verge of breaking someone's bones seconds ago, "we didn't fight, we're great."

The fellow gang members only peer at him like he was insane. Honestly, they all were - but they'd never seen anything like this before.

"Well, okay then," Hyunjae mutters, "so what the fuck is up with you?" he finally blurts the question everyone had been wondering since the second he walked in the door. 

A dark sigh leaves Kim Sunwoo's lips. His fringe falls in his face as he leans his head back against the couch, and he doesn't bother to move it, instead raising a hand to rub his temple as he clears his throat, "the past doesn't matter anymore, there's still a clear fucking problem here," he speaks in annoyance because they should've already known this. When everyone only continues to stare at him blankly, he groans in more disappointment, "there's someone else after Solar, did you fiends forget?! The woman said it herself, she has a partner that's still trying to ruin her life."

There's a long silence that overcomes the massive living room. Everyone was thinking, trying to make sense of what they were now reminded of.

"But they don't want to kill her," Eric stated the obvious, "they're just trying to ruin her career or something, aren't they?"

The blaring glare that Sunwoo sent Eric was enough to send shivers up not only his spine, but everyone's. Before he could sit up again to grab the boy and probably punch him, Hyunjoon had sighed from his seat across the room, "oh dear, Eric, he's talking about how her partner has the same information she had. It contains everything about her past, which includes us and what we do, which cannot be known to the public anywhere. Our entire operation after all these years will be ruined, and God knows what Sunwoo's father would do," a wave of realization washes over the blonde's face when his friend had explained such. "Plus, I refuse to let anyone ruin anything of her's," he adds, shaking his head bitterly, "can't wait to skin them alive, it'll be real fun."

It was scary to say he meant the threat literally.

"How the hell are we going to find them?" Haknyeon asks in concern, "might have to follow her all the way back to Korea if we're really taking on this job." It was clear he was joking as he chuckled at the end of such a statement, but it honestly wasn't wrong. 

Nobody knew what to say to the question. How were they going to find them? It was important to them to stop this person before they told the world about Solar's past, that would include ratting out the entire organization they worked so hard to build. They'd all go to jail for life, and none of them were very interested in that thought. 

But it wasn't like they would be right out in the open. They didn't know anything about this other person, they barely had any idea what had been going on this entire time with their poor Solar. 

That didn't mean they wouldn't find out. These boys had been doing this for far too long, it was what they were born to do. This was their lifestyle that they were more than accustomed to. Therefore, the last thing that they'd be beat by was an amateur. 

"I don't know," their leader finally breathes, "but it can't be too hard."





///






Jungkook felt like he was dying. 

His heart was aching every time he would look down at his phone and not see a reply from her. He really thought those texts he sent her would mean a lot to her. How could she just ignore them when he poured out everything he felt inside into them? 

The boys of Bangtan had yet to perform at KCON this year. They were supposed to be in the line-up for Day Two, which took place a few days after Day One. He knew Soomi would be performing at Day Two as well, so they were going to have to see each other eventually. 

A sigh leaves his lips, and he dully leans back into the mattress of his hotel bed. His body sinks into the plush, and he lets his head be buried by the soft pillow as his brown irises blankly stare up at the boring ceiling. 

He couldn't be mad at her for not replying. She probably hadn't spoken to anyone. Ever since everyone learned about her past from her close friend Junhoe, they had all texted her nice messages to make sure she knew they still valued her. Junhoe had said that she was going home to her old friends and to see her family. Knowing she was finally facing her biggest fear that had kept her down in the dirt for so long warmed Jungkook's heart. He was thrilled to know she was finally going back home and fixing what was destroyed, especially now that he knew the full story.

Jungkook hoped she could really find a way to be happy with her family again. He hoped everything would be okay, and understood that she was probably busy and overwhelmed with all that was going on. After all, they just found the stalker after all these years, and her crazy ex boyfriend had killed her. That was scary enough, so adding the fact she was going home only made it a hundred times worse. 

He accepted the fact she probably just didn't want to talk to anyone. This was why he refused to be upset with her, because he loved her and understood what she was going through. 

Therefore, Jungkook could do nothing but wait patiently. 

The other boys had been out and about, exploring the beautiful city of New York and doing fun activities. He'd joined them for a while because they had to film for Run, but afterwards, he came right back to his hotel room to just lay down. Relaxing had been all he felt like doing this trip, as he was too busy wondering about the girl he loved and worrying about if she was okay. 

She must've gone with one of her old friends, he assumed, but it still couldn't help but sadden him a bit. He'd been hoping she would of asked him to go with her to see them. 

Pushing it to the back of his mind, he closes his eyes to hopefully drift off into a peaceful nap, when there was an abrupt, heavy few knocks on the door.

"Jungkookie!" the lively voice of Park Jimin had cooed as the door creaked open only a second later, "I brought you some food since you haven't ate, it's your favorite!"

The aroma of a burger was filling his nostrils as soon as his friend said this. A tired groan leaves the maknae's lips as he sits up in bed, seeing the silver haired boy carrying in bags of food. There's a bright grin that remains on his lips as he sets them on the bed, before climbing atop it and perching himself at the end. 

"Thanks," is all Jungkook mumbles, reaching out to grab one of the bags. The continuous scent of food entering his nose was making his stomach growl.

Jimin purses his lips as he casually hummed no problem in response. It was clear what was going on with Jungkook, they all knew it. Soomi hadn't replied to any of them, so seeing him so down only made them assume she probably didn't reply to Jungkook either. 

And he was obviously rather affected by it. It worried him sometimes how attached he was to her. She had a lot going on, and as her best friend, he didn't think that she ever really was going to decide who she wanted to be with for a while. This meant that as Jungkook's friend and band-mate, it saddened him to see him always in such a dull state because of how badly he just wanted to be with her. 

He'd never tell him, but he could relate. Jimin had been feeling such a thing for as long as he could remember. 

But as time went on, he forced himself to forget about it. By this point, he just ignored any feelings whether they were still there or not. 

"She's fine, Jungkook," he finally assures him confidently as he's swallowing a bite of his own burger. The words he spoke caused the youngest to glance up from his own food quizzically. "I haven't spoken to her, either," he says to his obvious confusion, "but I know she has to be doing well. You know she would've come back if it didn't go good with her family."

He had a point. 

"Yeah," he sighs, "yeah, you're right."

Sending him a close-lipped smile, Jimin reaches out to pat his shoulder, "Course I am, so just eat your food and cheer up, Kook."

Nodding, he leans down to take another bite of the burger. Chewing, Jungkook swallowed the food before he heaved out a sigh, "I just miss her," he admits, "I know she's busy with her family life now, but I can't help but miss her."

He thought Jimin was going to call him selfish, or even stupid. But when he looks to his friend, he was only peering at him em-pathetically. 

"Me too, Jungkook," he mutters, "me too."


83:00 | sibling love

two days ago




"I don't care!" the screams of Chaerin were exasperated, and there's a confused look that takes over the tall boy's face as he slowly closed the front door behind him. Clicking the lock, his sneakers tap against the tiled floor as he ascends further into the house, curious and worried about what was possibly going on now. 

The sound of breaking glass echoes through the kitchen. It sent a spike of nostalgia up his spine as he stood just outside the doorway, looking for some insight on what was happening. 

"I don't care anymore!" the second youngest sibling in the family had cried out again, her voice weaker with each word she wept out, "I don't want to be the person you're molding me into anymore, father!"

"Chaerin," their father's voice was stern, warning her to let the ridiculous subject go and just move on with her life. All the siblings knew it was no use to ever argue with their parents, they knew you could never change their minds about their morals and what they believed. It'd been proven with what happened to their big sister. 

Especially when it came to their future's and who was going to take over their father's company when he passed. The likely candidate now that Solar was gone - little did they know - was Jaehyun, but the thing about him was, he always declined when they spoke about it. The eldest sibling of the Lee family didn't take the usual approach. When they spoke to him about it, he'd simply tell them he wasn't interested. Similar to his father, he was hard-headed, and his nonchalant attitude seemed to make them realize they wouldn't get through to him. He was only allowed to get away with it because he was the oldest son, and he was attending the best Ivy League school in the country with the highest marks. 

Otherwise, he'd be dead meat. 

The second candidate with the missing of the oldest sister left Chaerin. Of course, you still had Juyeon and Yein, but Juyeon was the definition of an airhead and Yein was much too young to begin to be molded into what they wanted. 

With the fact she always scored the highest on every exam and was number one in her class for years, Chaerin was the light in their eyes when they worried about who their last resort was. It was why they began to pressure her about joining college programs, taking business classes, and many other things she didn't ever think would be thrown onto her. Chaerin was smart in the basic school subjects, hence her grades. But she never took anything like a business class, it was not in her forte. 

At first, she tried to go along with it. She was just glad her parents were giving her some sort of credit. After all this time she finally felt like she was being appreciated.

But she should've known that it didn't matter. They were only using her as a pawn, and if she made anything lower than the highest grade, it was trouble in paradise for Chaerin. 

She tried studying hard, to the point where she was constantly sleepless. Her temper was getting worse and worse, and she felt like she was bound to break at this point. She hated it, she hated the way this family was.

All she wanted was a normal one, not one that was based off of your scores. She felt like her parents only saw her as valuable if she made good grades. 

The thoughts that crowded her head, choking her alive, was the reason her grades began to broke. It was also the reason that lead her to where she was now, breaking down in front of her angered father.

"No!" she screeches, "I won't let this go, I can't do it! This isn't me, I don't like any of this," she chokes on her own words, trying to stop her emotions from pouring out of her as she was beyond overwhelmed with them all, "I don't want to be used anymore!"

Their father, who had a history of lashing out when he got angry, had already broken one of the dishes thanks to such a talk with his daughter. He'd been trying to calm his nerves by drinking some water, but at the words he heard fall from her lips, the glass is clenched so tight under his grip it was likely it was going to break.

That was, until he chunked it across the room. Chaerin cries in shock as she watches it fly past her head, a look of bewilderment wiped onto her face as she whips her head back to her father. She didn't think he would really do that, but he guessed it was true when Jaehyun spoke about how he treated Solar. He'd just done it to her.

Tears well in her eyes as she recalled. Is this how it felt? Is this how my sister felt when I lied?

"Just go upstairs, Chaerin," their father snaps in a cautious tone, gripping the ends of the counter top as he tries to hold in his undeniable anger, "we'll talk tomorrow morning."

There's a million tears that flow from her cheeks. The makeup she put on this morning was turning into messy blobs on her skin, but she didn't care at all. She wanted her father to see how he had broken this family, how he was destroying his own children. Chaerin was trying to make him see, to make him understand, so maybe he would stop what he was doing. She didn't want to be pressured anymore. Why couldn't she do what she wanted to do, why couldn't she be her own person?

"I hate you," she blurts out before she could stop herself, her voice remaining strong that time despite the tears that continued to roll down her cheeks. Her green eyes are surrounded by the black of her ruined makeup, and her entire being was the equivalent of the shattered glass that surrounded her on the golden tiled floors.

Her father gawks at his daughter from the words he just heard. He couldn't seem to believe she had the audacity to tell him that, not after all he had done for her, all he'd given her. 

Anger was more evident in his expression than ever. He was boiling, and Jaehyun knew it was time to step in.

But just as he even thought about stepping foot into the kitchen and before his father could say anything else, Chaerin had muttered one last thing in complete agony and distress that had the both of them left in disbelief.

"No wonder Solar left."






///





"Hey," he didn't even bother to wait for her to grant him access as he gently knocks on the bedroom door, already opening it and stepping into her room without another thought, "what was that about?"

Sniffles are heard from the dark haired girl, who was a ball on her bed, face buried in her mess of pillows as she poured all her soul into the tears she cried. She'd weakly looked up from the sound of her oldest brother's voice, and the way her makeup was in streaks on her face, the way she looked so broken, shattered Jaehyun's heart. Never did he think he'd see the hard-headed, strong-willed Chaerin so torn.

Knowing how alone she was feeling, he closes the door behind him and walks across the room. Falling onto the bed, he pulls his little sister into a tight, comforting hug, rubbing her back as she could only cry her heart out until she felt content.

It was horrible. All the siblings could empathize with each other. They all felt the same way, they all felt like they were nothing more than pawns to their parents. 

Hearing what she said about Solar really made him think. He wondered what not only Chaerin, but the entire family was going to do when they saw she was back. Maybe she was a massive part of the way the Lee family had fallen apart, but would her returning truly fix all the problems within the family - or were they deeper than that?

He was beginning to think that not even his beloved sister's presence could fix all of this.

"I just don't want to be their experiment anymore," she's weeping into his chest, his hoodie becoming a damp mess thanks to her cries. Jaehyun could care less as he only listens to his sister explain how she was feeling, "I hate everything they're making me do, I hate the way they treat me, I hate the way they treat all of us."

He'd opened his mouth to speak, but she wasn't done. "I've been thinking recently, Solar was lucky for getting out of here when she had the chance."

Chaerin sounded more serious than she'd ever been. It caused a frown to take over his face, "Chae, don't say that."

Clearing her nose, she sits up straight, wiping her eyes. Exhaling deeply, she'd sat there, on the edge of her queen size bed next to her brother, "Am I wrong? Imagine how peacefully she must be living now, not having to live under their standards, not having to be controlled, it sounds so nice."

He has to purse his lips to stop himself from spoiling all the information he knew. "We don't know that, she could still be struggling in different ways," he corrects her, "and you know she's probably sad. She was still kicked out and disowned from her family, not to mention what you and Juyeon did."

Guilt takes over her face. It hadn't been his intention to make her feel bad, but he couldn't help but remind her. If he couldn't tell her the honest truth about what her older sister had been going through and all her hardships, he could at least say that to get it into her head that she was being sort of silly. 

"You're right," she admits, her head hanging low as she sighs in disappointment at herself for saying such a thing, "I can't imagine how she must have felt. I hope she knows we're sorry, wherever she is."

There's a dull look on Jaehyun's face as he reaches out his arm, patting Chaerin's back comfortingly. "Don't worry," he hums, "I think she knows that well."

"I hope so," she didn't even think before she said so, "it's never been the same without her. I never realized she was the key to this family, she kept everything together," she reminiscences, laying back on the plush of her mattress as she thinks deeply about her sister.  "She was supposed to be the inheritor of the company, but she never complained about what they made her do, she never said it was too hard," she mutters in awe, "she always did what she was told, even though they treated her like that when they found out she was with Sunwoo. She still managed to be the top in her class even while she was doing - that," she shakes her head, hating the remembrance of the drugs. "I never thought about how strong she was," she sighs out sadly, "and even though she did everything for our parents, they didn't hesitate to kick her to the curb."

Really, that was the saddest part.

"Hey, they regret it, though," Jaehyun speaks up, not liking how badly she was hating on their parents. Sure, they had horrible morals, but they were still their parents. They still raised them all, and even though it wasn't so clear anymore, they were once a real family - or closer to one.

"I know it doesn't seem like it, but they love Solar, and they love us all, too," he assures her with an exhale, "they've just got some - priority issues, I guess."

Chaerin snorts, "an entire book of them, you bet they do."

"Chae, do you know where my-" Juyeon had cut himself off as he whipped open his sister's bedroom door, his eyes being met with not only her, but his older brother as well. Confused, he enters, closing it behind him and suddenly not caring for what he was going to ask in the first place anymore, "why wasn't I invited to this sibling meeting?"

His bitter but playful tone made Chaerin roll her eyes. Nonetheless, she didn't care to say anything as he sat on her bed with her and Jaehyun. 

"Because I didn't want you here, obviously," she'd retorted a second later.

"Nice to know you're still an asshole."

"Has anyone ever told you to watch your mouth? I think you should try it," she groans in disappointment, rubbing her temples from how childish Juyeon was. It made her genuinely sad to know he was older than her.

A giggle left his lips as he raises a brow challengingly, "you're one to speak, Chae."

Annoyed with him already even though it had only been a few seconds, Chaerin grabs one of her pillows. Before he could react, she was beating him with it with all of her might, which actually hurt, if he was being honest.

"Holy shit, okay!" Juyeon was crying as he blocked her hits with his hands, "stop, I'm done with the jokes!"

Sighing, she hesitantly set the pillow back down before situated herself back to her previous cozy spot on her bed.

Jaehyun had been watching the two younger siblings with amusement. He didn't get a chance to say anything when Juyeon curiously pipes up, "so, what the hell were you guys talking about that Chaerin looks like the chick from The Ring?"

He couldn't help but snort out of humor from hearing that one. Chaerin, on the other hand, wasn't very happy with his question. In seconds, the youngest brother was being beaten with a pillow all over again. By the looks of it, she had no intention of stopping anytime soon.

Maybe he'd stop it in any other circumstance, but Jaehyun was happy to see the way his siblings were casually messing around. It brought him back to better times, and also gave him hope for what was bound to happen soon. He had high hopes that Solar would come around, he had high hopes that their family could still be saved. 

It made him happy to see that they weren't letting the guilt from what they did to their sister control them, at least not fully. Although it had taken a massive toll on everyone in the family and the family's health as one, it didn't mean they couldn't still smile. 

Because in the end, they always had each other. Even though they failed to protect their beloved big sister, even though they did something horrible to her. It made them realize that as siblings, they couldn't ever let anything happen like that again. They were blood, and that meant they had to always stay by each other's sides.

Their bonds became stronger thanks to what happened. This didn't mean they were glad that it did, because they'd do anything to take back what happened that night, but it did mean that they would never let anything taint their love for each other again.


84:00 | escape

Even though the Lee siblings had bonded that night, the two brothers doing their best to comfort their saddened sister. They all realized how their parents treated them, how they were forced to live under their high standards, unable to do what they wanted with their future's. Everything, ever since they came out of the womb, was a plan to their parents. They'd be deemed unworthy of being included in the family name if they didn't make high grades, make the top of their class, and get into an Ivy League school. It was a requirement to be in the family at this point.

They hated it. It was becoming harder for them to conform to this way of life. Upon understanding the only way out was to do what Solar did - although it wasn't by her decision - broke their hearts. 

Once upon a time, before they had grown up and gotten into high school, their family wasn't so bad. Although they still had to act mature and have manners, although they still attended tutor sessions and had private educator's from even that young of an age, they weren't treated this way. Their parents would take them on fun trips, they'd spend time together, they felt like a family.

But ever since the breakdown of Solar's disappearance, everything became worse. The rock to their family had left, and without the foundation that she brought, they were destroyed. 

None of them knew how she could ever live the way she did and still manage to act like everything was perfect. It was no wonder she started doing drugs.

Despite the quality time they finally spent as siblings together, laughing and comforting each other in their times of need, it didn't mean it would save Chaerin from the wrath she would soon be facing. With each day that passed, it only got worse. She tried to stand strong, to tell her father she wasn't giving in and that she simply didn't want to do what he was asking of her anymore. The classes were becoming too heavy, they were ruining her mental state. She didn't have the sort of time to study for so many things at once. 

But he didn't listen. He refused to allow her to do such as dropping out of them. 

She should've known it would be the best to give it up. But like always, the hard-headed girl that Chaerin was came out at the worst of times. 

"You will not dare drop any of those classes," her father snaps harshly, narrowing his green eyes at her as he spits the words in frustration, "I told you to let this go, I don't want to hear it. You should be thankful for all I do for you!"

Heaving out a sigh, she only stares at her father in disbelief. How could he not understand? Did he feel no sympathy for the fact this was weighing her down so bad? She didn't think her own parent would be so careless. Wasn't she his daughter?

Or was she really just a pawn?

"But they're hard, it's too much for me!" she insists, losing her previous angered tone and trying to approach it calmly for the first time. This was her last attempt, her last hope at making him give in. If this didn't work, she didn't know what she was going to do. She didn't feel like she could handle all of the work anymore. "I don't want to take over the family company, why don't you ask Juyeon instead?!"

A groan leaves the middle-aged man's lips. He spins around in his leather chair, pushing his glasses up his nose before he rises to his feet. Chaerin was weary of why he was doing this, preparing herself to duck in case he suddenly grabbed something to throw. 

However, he only places the palms of his hands on his shiny desk, leaning forward to peer at the second youngest daughter that was only feet away, standing in the middle of his office. In a singular breath, he states, "I didn't choose Juyeon, I chose you, Chaerin," his voice is monotone but yet calm, "you are the last of your siblings I believe can handle this, so hearing that you think you cannot," he pauses himself to hold in the anger that so clearly wanted to seep out of him. His temper problems were something Chaerin no doubt inherited from him, but his were actually a million times worse than hers - which was really saying something. 

Closing his eyes, he inhaled and exhaled deeply before opening them again and continuing to sternly speak, "hearing one of my children say they cannot do something is simply infuriating," he shakes his head in disappointment. 

"But, I really don't want this-"

"- It's not about what you want," he hisses out, tired of her pestering him about such an idiotic subject every day. He thought by now she would come to her senses and agree to continue doing what she needed to do. The fact she had yet to do such sent his emotions into flailing, pent-up fury. "It's about what you were born to do, Chaerin."

There it was, the words she didn't think she would hear. It made her heart shatter, like she really was nothing more than a necessary product in their business. That's all it was ever about, wasn't it?

Fed up with what she was hearing, she didn't say anything else. Instead, she spins on her heel, rushing straight out of the office doors without another word. 

Chaerin was beyond upset, she was beyond flaming with it all. Was it that much to ask for them to just allow her to do what she wanted? It wasn't like she was asking to drop out of college, it wasn't like she was trying to do anything bad. She just simply wanted to study what she wanted, not what her parents told her to.

At this point, she felt like she just wasn't allowed to be her own person. None of them were even allowed to leave the house anymore after what happened with Solar, not unless you had a really good reason. It meant they couldn't get away from their usually angered father, always complaining or bothering them about something they did that wasn't good enough. They could never get away from the horrid reality that their lives were, not even for a second. Every single day, the siblings were stuck in this place, the castle that so many wanted to live in that they'd do anything to leave.

The only exception was Jaehyun. He could pretty much go out freely because he was always working or going to classes. This made pretty much everyone envious of him. Since he was one of the eldest, he got rights the others didn't. This made the other siblings envious of him, as they would do anything to get away from their father. It was stupid to Chaerin he was the only one who got a free pass when it came to taking over the company, simply because he just didn't want to. Why didn't she get the same luxury because she didn't want to?

Unfair, was all she could think. She didn't get what she did to be treated this way. She didn't get why all the family had to be about was the stupid company. It was all stupid to her, and she was tired of it. 

"Chaerin!" her father had called back to her from his office, but he didn't make an effort to stop her. He guessed that she just needed time to cool off, so he was allowing her to do whatever she was doing.

Which wasn't wrong, because she was on fire. 

Her car keys jingle in her hands as she pulls them out of her pocket. Just as she's entering the entryway, footsteps are heard heading down the stairs of the mansion. The dark haired girl didn't even bother to look up at who it was, as she was too focused on heading straight for the front door.

When it was only feet away, however, someone grabbed her wrist. Angrily, she tries to rip it back out of their grip so she could finally leave, but her strength was no match to theirs.

Frustrated that someone had the audacity to stop her, she whips around to peer at the soul of her two brothers. 

"Chae, where are you going?" Juyeon is asking with a worried tone, "I thought we agreed to not let father get to us."

"I'm leaving," she states bitterly, still trying to get her wrist free of his tight grip, "I can't help but let him get to me. You can say that because you're not being forced to do everything so you can take over that fucking company!"

The two brothers exchange shocked glances from her words. 

"You're leaving?" Jaehyun repeats hesitantly, "I know it's hard, but this isn't the way to solve this situation."

A sad look is left in the younger brother's eyes. He felt like he was about to lose another sibling, something they all agreed would never happen again. 

"You can't leave," he mutters, "I already lost one sister."

Chaerin gawks when she hears the barely audible words leave his mouth. She realized she didn't exactly state that she was just going on a drive to clear her mind, maybe crash at a friend's, but she would be back. Although it hadn't been implied or stated, she guessed she wasn't surprised they had assumed the worst. After all, it was exactly what their sister did.

Solar, her big sister, she missed her more than anything right now.

When the reality of what he truly said sunk into her skin, she felt her eyes growing glassy. The emotions that only continued to weigh down on her began to pour out through her green irises, and she blinks away the tears hurriedly. In the last few days, Chaerin had been crying too much. She told herself she'd stop with the crying because it became a daily activity when Solar left, but now it'd seemed to start all over again. She was growing mad at herself, deeming she was too weak, but at the same time, she couldn't help it. Everything hurt, she felt like she was breaking.

The truth was, the family had broken a long time ago. Now it was finally taking a toll on it's members.

"I-I was going to come back," she mutters a second later, having to clear her throat so it didn't come out hoarse, "I wouldn't leave you guys, I don't want to lose another sibling, either," her voice grows quiet towards the end of her statement. 

The two brothers pout at they look at her. It was killing Jaehyun then, not to say anything about what he knew the last few days. Especially now that this was all happening. He hoped Solar would show up soon before the family crumbled to the ground and it was too late to save it.

"Sorry, I should've figured that," Juyeon sighs, letting go of her wrist as his head hangs low so he stares at his feet, "I just - think about Solar a lot."

"We all do," Chaerin nods in understanding, a sad smile on her face as she slowly turns back towards the door, "but I really need to take some time to myself, guys."

The two brothers make sounds of agreement. They faced the fact it would be the best for Chaerin to think this one out. In fact, it was probably what they all needed to do. For the last five years, the Lee siblings had been going through a lot.

"Stay safe, Chae," Jaehyun says lightly as her hand is on the handle.

Her finger is already hovering over the unlock key to her older sister's BMW. Clicking the lock to the front door, she turns back to nod to her older brother. "I'll be back," she offers to them.

When she turns back to open the door, there's more tears that form in her eyes she didn't want them to see. She was tired of appearing weak, even though weak was just what she had become. 

Was there anyway to save this family?

As if on queue, there was the sound of breaking glass coming from somewhere in the house, most likely the kitchen. Yells of their mother are heard, followed by a response just as loud from their father. She figured it was probably about her. Ever since she asked if she could drop the classes she was being forced to take and the other activities, her mother had been the one to actually hear her out about it. She tried to tell Chaerin's father that maybe it wouldn't be so bad if they let Chaerin do what she wanted to in school, and he was not very happy upon hearing this from his wife.

This had led to not one, but many arguments between their parents. It'd gotten so bad to the point they were fighting about even the smallest of things.

But if you asked them, they figured it wasn't really just about Chaerin. The biggest thing they were probably fighting over was Solar, and the pent up anger they had at each other for what they did and didn't do that could've prevented how they lost their beloved oldest daughter.

That assumption was correct.

Sending a small wave to her brother's, they offer one back before turning and heading towards the kitchen to save their mother. With that, she realized she didn't have the mental energy to be around this anymore. She could only imagine how poor Yein was feeling perked up in her room.

But Chaerin was too overwhelmed to even think about her poor littlest sister. 

Whipping the front door open, she closes her eyes as she steps outside. The fresh air enters her nostrils, providing a sense of comfort to her she didn't know she needed.

She had no idea where she was going to go, all she knew was that she needed to get out of here for a while.

Reaching the steps of the porch, she opens her eyes so she could look where she was going. Just as she's peering down at her feet, taking the first step, Chaerin clicks the button to unlock the car that was parked by the curb.

She had yet to realize the Lykan that was parked across the road from it. This meant she also had yet to realize the all too familiar brunette that was standing just by the driveway in the green grass of the front yard of the Lee mansion. 

"Chaerin?!"


85:00 | sass queen

Solar hadn't had the guts to ring the doorbell in the end. Although she had believed she could do it, something seemed to stop her. Perhaps it was the sound of the screams inside, the sound of how her family was ruined that she could hear through the door. It made her question many things, it made her wonder if she could really believe what her big brother had told her.

Was this truly what her family turned into after her disappearance? She didn't want to believe it. Jinhwan had escorted her into the yard, calming her obviously rapid nerves by holding her tightly and assuring her she would be okay.

"Take your time, whenever you're ready," he whispered to her as he rubbed her back, "I'm right here, Soo."

They'd stood there, embracing each other in the front yard of her childhood home. She hadn't even thought about the fact one of her family members could simply look out the window and see her standing there. Solar was too caught up in her head, in what she was feeling, in the disbelief of what her family had turned into. Her heart ached in her chest so badly that all she knew to do was allow Jinhwan to hug her, breathing in his sweet scent and letting it calm her nerve-wrecked mind.

It was when the sound of the door opening had been heard that she hesitantly stepped out of his grip. Her eyes were wide when they saw her younger sister on the porch, and she could feel her entire body exploding with perplexity as she tried to process what she should do.

But Solar couldn't seem to think. Jinhwan had grabbed her hand to let her know he was still there for her through it all, and it seemed to give her the guts to open her mouth, letting the girl's name leave her lips in a shout before any part of her could stop such from happening.

Chaerin thought she was dreaming.

At first, she didn't recognize the voice. It had been too long since she had heard it, after all. Five years was a long time, and it was definitely long enough to forget the sound of someone's voice, especially after going through so much.

Halting in her tracks across the sidewalk towards the car, her green eyes blink as they take it in. Not knowing who it was yet, her head jolts each way, trying to figure out where it came from.

That was when they saw her.

Her mouth fell open, and she was left to gawk at the sight like a mental patient. She hadn't touched drugs in five years, but she was convinced that she must be under the influence right then. There was no other explanation, there was no way what she was seeing before her was actually reality. It wouldn't make any sense, would it?

The brunette is staring into her soul. Her brown hairs flows down her back in slight waves, her eyes just as bright as ever, and her smile was still as pearly as she remembered when she flashed it her way. Unable to process anything, Chaerin was left to stare in awe, repeatedly blinking as she tries to understand. Nothing was making sense, she couldn't believe it. What was going on? was all her mind was asking.

Her body couldn't move. It was like she was super-glued to the floor.

Solar had taken a few steps towards her, slowly calling out her name again, "Chaerin?"

The tears fly to her eyes like darts, but for the first time in five years, she could confidently say they weren't tears of agony. These were tears of joy.

Not even thinking about the endless assumption that her big sister must hate her guts, not even thinking about if she was mad at her for leaving, if she resented her, Chaerin let none of that enter her mind. All she could do was gasp as she finally finds a way to use her feet. And when she did, she ran straight for her big sister.

"S-Solar?!" she screams, not caring if anyone heard her. In fact, she wanted the world to, she wanted the world to know her big sister was back, she wanted everyone to know she was home. "Solar, Solar, is it really you?!"

The sister's embrace each other, and Solar closes her eyes as she takes in the moment. Chaerin let out a shaky sob as she buries her face in her shoulder, crying like she was a newborn baby and it was all she knew how to do. Her heart was on the floor, and she didn't care anymore if she appeared weak. She was weak, she was so weak, and she wasn't afraid to admit it anymore.

"Yeah," she remembered her voice clearly by that point, "it's me, Chae."

More sobs leave her lips, she couldn't hold them in if she tried. Solar rubs her back as she hugs her sister tightly, trying to ease what she was going through. The thing was, she was crying, too.

"Solar, Solar, I missed you," she's weeping into her shirt, "I never thought I'd see you again, my big sister," she whines on. Before Solar could even say anything to comfort her, she cried out the words she didn't expect to hear, "I'm sorry, I'm sorry Solar, I didn't think! I was so stupid to listen to Juyeon, I was so stupid to not tell the truth! I'm sorry I was such a mess, I'm sorry I wasn't there for you when you needed me," her face was red and puffy from the tears she cried as she pulls away to meet her sister's eyes, "I'm sorry I failed you as your sister."

"Chaerin," Solar gawks, left in a state of speechless shock as she took in everything she just heard. She didn't think this was what she would hear from her siblings, but after what Jaehyun said, she guessed he really was right. They really did regret it.

"It's okay," she finally assures her, wiping the tears from under her eyes and forcing a smile on her face, "everything is okay now, you didn't fail me. I'll always love you, okay? We're sisters."

Not knowing how to respond, how to fully grasp what was happening, all Chaerin knew to do was pull her sister in for another hug. This time, she squeezed her sister until she felt her own bones going weak from holding her so tightly. Solar used to be taller than her, but by this point, Chaerin had grown more. She embraced her sister to the point she was surprised she wasn't complaining for her to let her go.

But really, Solar was happy, so happy she didn't care how harshly she was being hugged. It was something she had needed for a long time. Seeing her big sister again was a breath of fresh air she had been craving. Now that she finally had it, she was never letting it go again.

"I can't believe you're here," Chaerin is repeating to herself, completely insane by that point. Her breath is heavy as she pulls away from the hug again, wiping her tears off her face and angrily sniffling. She had yet to even notice the poor Jinhwan that had been standing behind, not wanting to interrupt the sweet moment he had witnessed.

Instead, she reaches out and grabs her sister's hand. She holds it in fear that if she let go, her sister would disappear. If this was a dream, it was the best one she had ever had, and she never wanted to wake up.

"Come on!" she's urging excitedly, already dragging her towards the door she was too scared to knock on moments ago, "Come on, you have to see everyone!"

Before she could even say anything, she was vigorously being dragged towards the front porch. Solar squeaks as her sisters undeniable strength was showing, only knowing to worriedly call out her name, "Chaerin, Chaerin, stop!"

Obeying her calls, her little sister was that of an angel as she innocently turned to look back to her. You would think that Chaerin Lee was actually a sweetheart from the way she was acting, not how she really was, which was bossy, full of attitude, and rather blunt which usually resulted in her being rude. She was just excited, overwhelmed, with the fact her big sister was standing in front of her after all this time. The girl who she always idolized, the girl she realized now was stronger than she could ever be.

Through the last five years, she had a long time to reflect on her decisions. One of them was that her sister was her biggest role model in her life. She regretted being part of the reason she left more than anything. Always having a fear that Solar hated her had eaten her alive, making her meaner towards everyone and overall a colder person than she was.

But now it was okay, or at least it would be. The beginning was here, the beginning of making everything good. Knowing her sister didn't hate her, it was the perfect time for her to be back. With all the horrible things happening in the family right now, Solar might be the only hope at saving it.

"Sorry, am I too eager?" She hesitates, pursing her lips in contemplation, "it's just — there's been so much going on the last five years. It's bad, Sol, I don't know what to do. You're the only hope at saving this family."

There's a saddened look that remains on her face. It shines under the sunlight in her green eyes, almost the same as Solar's.

Growing up, she always acted strong and hard headed, but Chaerin was showing she was horrified. She was hurt and destroyed with what was happening to her family. The craving for everyone to be happy, the want for it to just be normal, was ruining her insides.

"Chae," Solar sighs out, squeezing her hand that she was still holding, "I know it's been bad, I saw Jaehyun first a few days ago. It's a long story, but he told me a bit about what's going on."

The dark haired girls eyes widen from the words she just heard. She couldn't believe her older brother knew she was back and didn't say a word to her or Juyeon.

It wasn't like it really mattered anymore.

"Father has been making me take all those classes you used to be in," she explains before she could stop herself, wanting Solar to know the entire scoop, "I have to do activities, help at the company, and a bunch of other stuff I really don't understand — I hate it, Sol. I don't know how you ever did this."

With every word she says, Solar's face only falls more and more. There was so much she had to tell her entire family, but first, she had to hear about what had been happening, what they'd been going through. It was something she realized now she'd been dreading, as it only hurt her to hear what Chaerin had been having to deal with because of her disappearance.

"Well," she pauses, her voice a mere mumble as she's in thought, "I know you're not interested in that stuff, neither was I. I just did what I had to do, because if I was dealing with the burden of being the next CEO of the company that meant none of you had to," she almost wanted to cry. However, she'd cried too much recently and she refused to let herself do it again. Instead, she dully exhales as she looks to her feet, "just knowing that was always good enough for me to go on."

"Sol," Chaerin weeps, and when her big sister looks back up she sees more tears in her eyes. The girls had already cried, but it was bound to happen all over again.

She probably wouldn't be able to resist crying at least ten more times on a day like this.

"I'm so glad you're here," she breathes, at a loss for words as she wipes the tears from under her eyes, "nothing has ever felt right. You were what held us all together. Without you, everyone fell apart."

Pulling her in for another hug, she rubs her sisters back as she breaks into tears again. "I know, Chae," she soothes, "but it'll be okay now, I'm back, alright?"

Squeezing the life out of her big sister that was now smaller than her, she buries her face in her jacket as she hesitantly agrees, "right."

When they pull away, Solar runs her hand through her messy hair. It was chilly outside indeed since it was still early in the year. Either way, she told Chaerin she wasn't prepared to go inside just yet. After thinking about it, the little sister realized that was fine, since they had plenty of time anyway.

So instead, the two girls sat at the front of the drive way, where they were unseeable from the windows of the mansion and were blocked by all of the expensive cars parked in it. The afternoon sky was a shade of grey blue, clouds beginning to form as it was probably going to rain with in the next couple of hours. Either way, the New York sky was as pretty as she could remember.

They sat, for a while, talking about how their lives had been and what the family was going through. Solar heard all about how her father had been treating not only Chaerin, but everyone. Even Yein had been getting scolded by her parents from her grades, and Chaerin admitted she felt bad for not really helping her. It was heart breaking for the oldest to hear — she was always her littlest sisters rock, or more like both of their rocks, so knowing that even small Yein was feeling the wrath of the broken family made her want to scream.

She couldn't let that continue. Yein was too innocent and sweet to be treated that way. She was also going to solve how they were treating Chaerin. If she didn't want to take the classes, she didn't have to as far as she was concerned. This didn't mean Solar was going to take up the responsibility of becoming the next CEO, because she didn't know if she had time to start college again (obviously she didn't,) but it also wasn't in her interests either.

Her father was just going to have to face it. And she knew she could be the one to make him to that.

Chaerin had been bewildered at the news of what Solar's new life had been like. She thought she was just making up some sort of insane story as she went, so much so that after she finished sharing about the stalker and her love triangle (or was it a love square now?) she was staring at the brunette weirdly.

Solar catches sight of this look and blinks in confusion, "why are you looking at me like that? I told you, this isn't a joke."

Scoffing, she shakes her head, "that's fucking mental, okay? You seriously became a famous singer?" she giggles from how funny it sounded. Her sister was always popular, loved by everyone, and had people swooning over her. But she never talked about singing or anything. It wasn't really something anyone in the family cared about, except for Yein.

"Okay, fine," Solar heaves out a sigh. Before she could ask what her sister was doing, she pulled out her phone. Seconds later, she was pulling up a song Chaerin was positive she wasn't going to know. The title only you appeared on the screen as Solar shoves it in her face, "watch this first."

First? She could only anticipate what else she was going to show her. It was scary to hear the actual hardships her sister had been going through all by herself. She really was strong.

It took a minute, but when the song loaded, she screeched from shock — literally. Solar had paused the song to stare at her oddly, and Chaerin hadn't waited to share the information, "Yein — she loves that song!"

Remembering how Sunwoo had shared that, a knowing look is on her face. "Oh, yeah, I heard about that," she nods, "it's ironic, isn't it?"

Chaerin doesn't reply because she was busy watching the phone screen. No doubt about it, that was her sister. She was crazily talented, more than she imagined. Never had she heard the song Yein always listened to out loud, but now that she was, she was beginning to understand why she loved it so much.

It was crazy to think Yein never knew it was her sister. She guess she never looked up the singer and just listened to the song itself.

Ironic indeed.

"That's one of the guys?" She asks for confirmation, pointing to Jinhwan that was on the phone screen. Little did she know he was actually standing right behind them. Solar had texted him and told him that she would introduce him after they talked about everything. That time was coming right about then. That was, until she'd hummed, "he's cute."

A snort of humor accidentally left Solar's mouth, earning a confused look from her sister. "Sorry — yeah, that's one of them," she purses her lips as she watches the video herself, "my sweet Jinhwan."

Her sister wrinkled her nose, "I'm shocked you moved on from Sunwoo to someone actually attractive."

Bursting into laughter, Solar shakes her head in disapproval as she judges her sisters shoulder. Taking her phone back, she pulls up the other page she had been saving. It was one of the last few things she was showing her sister, at least for now, so she could truly get a grasp that she hadn't been joking about any of it.

"What's this?" Chaerin is muttering as she takes the phone, already skimming over the article. It dated back to a little over a year ago.

"Jungkook of BTS and Soomi confirmed to be together," she was mumbling parts of it out loud. It took her a good few minutes to read it all, but when she did, she looks back up in shock, "first of all — so it's seriously against the rules to fucking date someone? Isn't that a normal thing people do? The fuck does it matter?" Solar only nods, and it only enrages her more, "I think that tops one of the dumbest things I've ever heard, and I talk to Juyeon on a daily basis."

"Also," she notes as she looks back down at the article, "this is fucking insane. Isn't that guy really famous too — that's the other one?"

Solar nods again.

"What the living fuck have you been up to, Solar Lee?!"


86:00 | the cute guy

"This is crazy," Chaerin's muttering as she runs a hand through her dark locks. Her brain was having a hard time catching up with all the information she had been given about her sister. Although it seemed like she was acting like she didn't want to hear anymore, that was only on the outside. Really, she was thrilled hearing about how Solar's life was. Even the bad parts pended to be interesting to her, and she was happy to hear them anyway because she was finally back with her sister. They were finally talking like they used to, except now that they were reunited, their bond was stronger than ever. Chaerin understood what she did was wrong. It was why she'd never let anything like that happen again. 

Their little chats about her life in Korea also took the poor girl's mind off the horror that was going on in their own family. Solar said she wanted to wait a while before they went inside, and Chae was fine with that. In fact, she was having the time of her life, happier than she'd ever been, as she leaned her chin on her sister's shoulder so she could peer down at her phone screen. The idols on the screen looked like robots to her from how poreless their skin was, and the way they looked like they genuinely had no flaws. Even Solar sort of looked like that now, but she guessed she always had. Chaerin had just never seen so many groups of people with no flaws like her sister.

"I think he's cuter than the other guy," she's sharing her thoughts, pointing to the phone screen where Solar had been showing her an iKON video after having had shown her BTS. She was honestly shocked to hear her sister's opinion on this. Never had Chaerin openly really spoken about guys, she never even really had a thing with a guy except for one time. It'd ended badly, so she pretty much hated them all from then on. This was why she was surprised to hear her so casually complimenting someone.

Honestly, though, it made sense since it was Jinhwan. 

However, Solar couldn't help but burst into fits of giggles, remembering the fact that exact boy was hiding around the cars out of her sister's sight. He was still waiting for her to motion for him to come out so she could introduce him, but she just never got around to it. The two girls kept getting involved in conversations, so much she accidentally forgot about poor Nani that was waiting back there.

"Why are you laughing?" Chaerin asks with a slight pout from the fact her sister kept snickering every time she would speak about Jinhwan, "Is there something wrong with him? I mean, he seems fine to me. He's got talent, he can sing, and he has some serious thighs-"

"- Chaerin, please," she begs, turning off her phone screen and setting it in her lap so she could bend over in fits of hysterics. Hearing her sister talk this way was almost scary. Jinhwan really did work magic on people's brains.

"Okay!" she cries in defense, but there was a giddy smile on her face as she watched her sister continue to die of laughter beside her. "Seriously, I don't understand the problem. I know you're not used to be actually talking about guys, but come on, he deserves some appreciation."

Unable to handle what she was hearing anymore, Solar tries to steady her breathing as she sits up straight again. If anyone was witnessing these two that actually knew Chaerin, they probably wouldn't believe it was her. With the way her hair was blowing in the breeze, her green eyes were shining under the sunlight, and her smile was so wide it practically stretched from ear to ear. She looked like a new person, when really, it was just the fact she was finally happy after so long. 

"You know," she sighs dramatically, "you should tell him that yourself then."

Her sister was staring at her like she was a psychopath. "What is that supposed to mean?" she asks in confusion, "he's your man, are you trying to set me up or something?"

She tried to hold it in, but she couldn't help but explode into more giggles. Tired of her sister's clear joking, she reaches out and shoves her shoulder slightly, only making Chaerin laugh too. Still, her sister was completely in the dark of what she really was talking about. Knowing Jinhwan was hearing all of this without her aware only made it that much funnier.

Not wanting to deal with it anymore no matter how entertaining it was, she rises to her feet. Chaerin is lost as to where she was going as she turned to walk somewhere in between the cars parked in the driveway. She thought her sister was being paranoid or something, and was honestly sad it had ended their session of jokes. 

Jinhwan was giving her a humorous look when she walked to where he had been leaning against one of the cars. She returns the look, smiling as she grabs his wrist, dragging him out into the open.

"Sol, what are you do - what the fuck?" Chaerin squeaks, her voice going up a few pitches like Solar's did when she got utterly shocked. Her eyes are bulging out of their sockets. "That's the guy - oh God," once she realizes the fact he heard all she said she didn't even care about why he had been hiding there. It was easy to comprehend he escorted Solar here, and the embarrassment that rid up her spine was enough to kill. All she knew to do was heave out a sigh, her hard-headed demeanor remaining, "well, dude, you're welcome."

"Here's the infamous Jinhwan, who you apparently really have the hots for," Solar motions to the boy beside her as if he was a masterpiece, dramatically swaying out both of her hands.

He shakes his head as he gently pushes her hands down, not wanting her to act like he was some form of royalty because he wasn't - especially compared to them and their family. Instead, he sends her sister the sweet smile he was known for, "I didn't mind what you said, honestly thanks," he hums playfully, "nice to meet you."

The girl had been looking him up and down, seeming to take in everything about his appearance. One thing she did notice was the guy was short, just barely taller than her sister which wasn't saying much. Chaerin was actually positive she had a good few inches over him.

"You too," she responds casually as if she hadn't been on the verge of dying from embarrassment seconds ago. That was just part of her personality, she didn't let things small like that bother her. "Chaerin, the middle sister of the family."

Now that they had gotten the fact he was there out in the open, Solar could finally breathe normally. They'd taken seats again next to Chae, with Solar in the middle. The clouds were becoming more grey in the sky, but no one really seemed to notice. All the three cared about was speaking to one another, and fully catching up on all they had missed. 

However, this had consisted mainly of Chaerin interviewing Jinhwan. It was like she was trying to decide if he was worthy of being with Solar. But after she asked him so many questions, she shrugged as if she had just been asking them all out of curiosity, "hm, I don't think I'd mind you as my brother-in-law, my actual brothers are pretty fucking stupid."

At the mention of her marrying Jinhwan, Solar could feel her cheeks heat up. Thankfully, the sky was getting more grey from the clouds forming in it that she didn't think anyone saw.

"Nice to know," Jinhwan chuckles, "I'll note that."

"Great, so when's the wedding?"

Solar buries her face in her hands, feeling horrified with what she was hearing. To think she hadn't even explained to the boys or made her decision in the first place yet. Hell, she still needed to talk to Sunwoo. And here her sister was, whom she just reunited with, speaking about a wedding.

"Chae," she warns in more of a pleading tone than an intimidating one.

"I'm still thinking on that one," Jinhwan had hummed from beside her, as if this was a completely normal conversation.

In the end, the two of them had pretty much ignored her existence. "You should hurry," Chaerin advises. 

Jinhwan could only grin, he really wished he could do that. At this point, he would jump at the chance to spend the rest of his life with Soomi - or Solar. (He still wasn't sure what he wanted to call her now.) Knowing now that he had her childhood friends liking him, and now her sister, was filling his confidence bar more than his heart could handle. He wanted to be with her, so badly it was killing him. All he could hope was that she took how much the people closest to her adored him and realized he was the one she should be with.

It wasn't that he was trying to be selfish. But as Changmin and Jimin had both advised him before, this was how it had to be played. Both Sunwoo and Jungkook were worse than he could ever be. All he was doing was showing his love for the only girl he wanted, and making sure she understood how desperate he was to receive it in return.

The minutes turned into hours, and the three remained in the driveway as if they didn't have a car in the world. Those pesky clouds above them kept getting darker, until it had begun to sprinkle. It hadn't been a big deal until the sprinkles turned into actual rain drops, and before they knew it, it had begun to pour like no other.

"New York rain," Solar is sighing as they all rush onto the front porch, "can't say that I missed it."

She was acting like everything was fine, as Jinhwan's hand held her's tightly. Really, knowing he was right beside her was probably the only thing keeping her sane. Chaerin also knew what was coming up, since it had been a while that they had been talking, and Solar had said she wanted to wait a bit before she went inside to face everyone else. By now, that time was up. They didn't have anywhere else to go but inside with the rain that was pouring in the outside world.

It was time for her to finally face the rest of the noise, where the heart of all the problems were. Not only the rest of her siblings, but mainly, her parents.

She didn't think she could ever prepare herself for that. It was one thing her siblings ratted her out all those years ago, but they weren't the one's who kicked her out. Although, the blame was mainly on her father. He even treated their mother poorly, who tended to be harsh on them as well just to make him happy. He was the one who told her she wasn't a part of the family, the one who kicked her to the curb, he was the one who made the decision that she should leave - no one else. 

With that in mind and the remembrance that only five years ago she'd been leaving the house heart broken, deemed a disgrace to the Lee name, was why she could never emotionally prepare herself.

Jinhwan and Chaerin knew this well. Wanting to do anything to help her, Chaerin reached out to grab her other hand, sending her a small smile as she hesitantly asks, "are you ready to go inside?"

Solar meets her sister's green eyes with a scared look in her own. Seeing her sister that way made her frown, and she tried her best to reassure her, "Sol, you're freaking out too much. I know it doesn't seem like it, but I swear that mother and father have fallen apart without you. Father knows what he did was wrong, and he's regretted it ever since. Even though he hasn't said it, it's obvious," she sighs bitterly, shaking her head. Eyes wandering to her feet, she mutters the last part, "why do you think he's been trying to turn me into you?"

Not wanting to hear anymore about the horrid things that had been happening, she squeezes her hand. "Alright, alright," she breathes, reminding herself she had to save her family from the destruction it was currently in, "let's go inside."


87:00 | baby yein

Yein was shattered. She felt like her entire world was falling apart, and she didn't know what to do to stop it. A girl that was only eleven wasn't supposed to have to feel this way, not at such a young age. This was the time when a kid was meant to be just that - a kid. She was supposed to have tea parties, play with dolls, go on play-dates. She was supposed to be a kid and enjoy her childhood, not be pressured about her grades, not be told she wasn't good enough, not be compared to other kids that were smarter than her. 

Balling her hands into fists, she rubs her eyes as she tries to remain strong, but she couldn't hold on anymore. She wanted to leave, she wanted to pack up her things and runaway. All Yein wanted was to find her sister. She wanted to be with someone that reminded her she wasn't worthless.

"Yein, are you even listening?!" her father sighs, his brows creased inward as he was obviously in distress. His arms were crossed over his chest as he leans back in the leather chair in his office. Despite the sight of his daughter breaking before him, he didn't appear to be bothered as he only continues to lecture, "I didn't buy you those books so you could throw them on the floor, I bought you them so you could study and get better," he sternly informs her, "so why do you keep making these sorts of grades?!"

His voice rose louder and louder with each word. The sound of her being yelled at caused her to wince, not wanting to hear anymore. Her head falls, and she only stares at her hands in her lap as she tries to hold in her tears. This ultimately failed because of how torn she was inside, and the droplets began to splatter over her lap, much like the rain was outside in that current moment.

"Yein, I don't hear an answer!" her father boomed in frustration, "I don't understand why you refuse to better yourself! Do you want to be a part of this family or not?!"

Hearing that caused her to nearly sob. Holding a hand over her mouth, she removes it to instead wipe away more tears. Weeping there in the chair that sat in front of her father's desks, the small girl slowly raises her head to stare at him with teary eyes. 

But his cold expression didn't change, even at the sight of her in such a way. 

"I-I'm sorry..." she trails off, not knowing what else to say. It was just too hard for her, she didn't understand it, nothing made sense to her, was the truth. But she knew saying that would only make it worse. This was proven from what was currently happening to Chaerin.

She could only wonder where her other sister went. Yein heard she left a few hours ago, and she still wasn't back. It only made it all that much worse. What if she didn't come back? What if Yein lost another sister? 

Little did Yein know, the uncanny resemblance between her and Solar only made her case worse. Her parents resented her so heavily because all she did was remind her of them. Their guilt, their regret, ate them alive, so in response to it they took it out on her with their anger. Such an unhealthy tactic was ruining her life, it was ruining her. She lost track of who she was, of what she wanted, of everything.

Yein was lost.

"I don't care!" he cries, his voice practically shaking the walls as he slammed his hand on his desk, "none of you put in any effort anymore, all any of you ever say is the same thing! I don't know what I'm going to do with so many useless children!"

All she knew to do was bury her face in her hands. More than anything, Yein wanted to run out of there and never return. She knew she couldn't leave, though, until she was dismissed, because it would only get her in more trouble. Yein didn't want anymore trouble, she just wanted it to be over. She just wanted to be happy.

Squeezing her eyes shut, she pretended that she was in another world, trying anything that meant she was tuning out her father's yells.
