The sound of the door opening and being shut didn't even cut her father off. In fact, his yelling was so loud and erratic that neither of them even noticed it. All he was focused on was taking out his frustration and fury on his eleven year old daughter.

Meanwhile, Solar was bewildered when they entered the mansion. It looked exactly the same as she remembered, except for the family photos that had been changed on the walls. She noticed a lot of them didn't include her anymore, guessing it was probably because her family tried to forget about her. It was a sad thing to see, but she had to remind herself the things her two siblings had told her. She had to remember the task she was here to complete. Solar couldn't let this eat her alive anymore. She'd have to come home and face this eventually.

She'd thought the house was quiet, until she heard the booming of her father's yells through the walls. It was clearly coming from his office, the same place she received lectures from. He always spoke to the kids seriously in there, and when you were called inside, it was rarely for anything good unless you were Solar. 

But since the disappearance of the oldest sister, it had never been anything good. 

"What the hell?" Solar mutters out in disbelief at the sounds of his screaming. Her father always had anger problems, he always struck out when he didn't mean it and did things he shouldn't. A lot of the times they had been to her because of her relationship with Sunwoo that he always forbid. But because of her grades and the fact she was the only one willing to take on the business who showed they could do it, he allowed it even though it made him so mad. 

However, even then, she had never heard him so mad through out her entire life. Was this what it had really come to? Is this what they'd all been dealing with for all this time?

"Who is he yelling at?" she asks Chaerin in worry.

Her sister's eyes are concerned when they meet hers. The three were still standing in the entryway, and Jinhwan was rubbing Solar's back as he tried to calm her clearly ecstatic nerves. When Chaerin doesn't reply for a second, Sol was already jumping to horrible conclusions. She refused to believe it was the worst one, until her sister finally sighs dully, "probably Yein."

"What?" she gawks, not wanting to believe it. He really had the audacity to treat little Yein like that?

"No fucking way."

"Sol, I don't know if you should-" Chaerin tried to call out when her sister had angrily stormed down the hall to the left, the one that went straight to their father's office. But she stopped herself when she realized there wouldn't be any stopping her. Either way, what she was doing was probably what needed to be done. 

Forcing herself to eliminate any worry she had, she let her sister go, and remained standing in the entry with Jinhwan at her side, looking rather complexed about the whole situation. 

"Is she gonna be okay?" he's asking Solar's sister, who was only sighing as she watched her sister's silhouette disappear down the long hallway.

Glancing to him, she met his soft eyes. It was heart warming to see how much he cared about Solar, so much that she felt like he could use some reassurance, "yeah, yeah, she'll be fine, trust me."

Jinhwan could only purse his lips and nod in response. He knew the majority of what was going on from having hearing so much about it recently, but he still couldn't fully grasp how this family was because he didn't grow up in it. Never would he be able to completely understand the intensity of how family's lived in the castle. It was just something one had to experience to believe it.

Solar's sneakers click vigorously against the wood floor as she walked. The large, double doors to her father's office soon came into view. For once, she could confidently say she had no fear as she approached them. Wanting to save Yein, to put an end to this once and for all, was her fuel to go on, and it had her more upset than she had been in a long time.

As she grows closer, the yells became more audible. Actually being able to audibly hear what he was telling a kid only made her that much angrier. 

"All of you - all of you can't do anything, I wish I had decent children at least! Some that were capable of doing what they're asked and not the equivalent of delinquents!"

"I-I'm sorry, I'll try to be better..."

Yein sounded like she was on the verge of death from how broken she was. Hearing her voice nearly above a whisper was what made her nearly kick the door down. The handles slam against the back of the wall, probably breaking it, as she clenches her fists at her sides. Her green eyes are flaming, probably red from how upset she was. Brows creased downward, she might've appeared to be a new Solar - Soomi if you may - but she was still the same on the inside. And that girl didn't allow anyone to treat her siblings that way, especially not her beloved Yein.

"Is that true now, we're all really that useless, huh?" her voice snaps before she could stop it. But then, she didn't care to reunite sweetly with her father. Knowing he was saying these hurtful things about not only Yein, but all of them, was enough to set her off.

The look on his face was indescribable. He looked like she was some sort of ghost, like he was suddenly caught in a trance, as if someone shot him with a sleep dart. Not knowing what to think, unable to comprehend the situation he was thrown into, her father gawked at her for a moment. His previous angered expression was wiped and replaced with that of disbelief, as he kept blinking repeatedly at the girl that was standing in the doorway. The girl he thought he would never see again, his daughter, his oldest daughter.

"S-S-Sol..." he kept trying to make out her name, but couldn't seem to fully say it because of the shock he was thrown into. His hands grasped the edge of his desk, his mouth twitching as he kept trying to say her name. He kept trying to understand, have any recollection of how this was happening, but none of it made sense. She was back, she was standing before him. 

And she heard everything he just said. 

"You should be ashamed of yourself," she hisses, not caring about the sudden look on her father's face, "speaking of your children like that. How do you sleep at night?!"

"S-Solar," he finally manages to gasp, rising to his feet in a rush. Yein hesitantly rose her face from her hands at the sound of the name, the voice finally sinking into her ears. "Solar, Solar, my daughter!"

"Don't," she holds out a hand to stop him when he tried to take a step towards her, "I don't have anything to say to you right now, not after the way I just heard you speak not only to Yein, but about all of us."

"No, no, I didn't mean you! I didn't mean any of that!" he was crying, looking as if he was being thrown into insanity as he held a hand to his head, "I didn't know what I was saying!"

But the oldest daughter only shakes her head. Her father needed time to think about his actions, about the way he was treating them. It already seemed like just her presence was enough to make him do that. Maybe she didn't need to say much more, she realized, but she did need to make sure her little sister was okay.

"Yein," she calls out in a small tone, as her little sister had yet to turn around to see if it was really her. Yein didn't think this could be real. She thought she was dreaming it all up, and she worried that if she turned around thinking it was her sister, she would disappear into the air like she always did in her dreams. "Yein," she called again.

It was when she felt a hand on her shoulder that she jumped from surprise. Hesitantly, the small girl turns in the chair to see what she didn't think was going to actually be there.

But it was.

"Sissy?" she breathes, reaching up her hand to place it over her bigger one that was still resting on her shoulder, "it's you - it's really you?!"

Yein scrambles out of her chair, nearly tripping on the ground as she runs to her sister. "Sissy, it's you!" she screeches, "you're real!"

Solar accepts the girls embrace, hugging her tightly against her as the child weeped into her chest. There was no telling what Yein had been going through, but from what she heard already from her siblings and just now, it must be really impacting her. 

"I'm real, Yein, I'm real," she whispers, bending down to pick her sister up. "Let's go somewhere else, okay?"

The girl only nods eagerly, trying to stop her crying as she wrapped both her arms around her sister's shoulders. 

"Solar!" her father had been yelling as she left the office with her littlest sister, "Solar, wait, we need to talk!"

Never in a million years would the girl from five years ago had done it, but she guessed then that maybe she wasn't exactly the same person as she slammed the double doors with her foot and her hand that wasn't carrying her sister. 

"No."

88:00 | solar's home

The youngest clung onto her big sister like her life depended on it — because it did. All her life this moment was what she waited for. She just didn't think it would ever come in anywhere other than her own mind, but it had. Her big sister was back, she was really back.

"Solar's home!" She was screaming out as her tiny arms were wrapped around Solar's neck, who was carrying her upstairs with Chaerin and a short Jinhwan behind her. "Big sissy is home, she's really home!" Her voice echoed through the walls of the mansion, no doubt alerting everyone inside. By this point, Solar didn't mind, because the only ones who had yet to know were just her mother and Juyeon.

"I missed you, sissy!" Yein had gone back to crying as she hugged her sister again and again, "I thought about you everyday!"

Her heart ached as she remembered what she heard about Yein. Solar had been the only one who ever helped her with her school, after all. It broke her to know that she was being scolded so hard simply because she needed just that — help.

Thinking back to what her father said about all of them only made her even more upset.

"I know, Yein, but it's okay now," she tried to assure her as they enter her bedroom. It wasn't that different, except the walls were now painted a light shade of purple and she had newer furniture since she was older now.

She'd set the girl down, looking around her room still to take in everything. Yein had been walking to her bed, and Solar's eyes soon followed her. As the little girl had climbed onto her soft mattress, that was when the oldest had noticed the pictures she had scattered across it.

Green eyes wide with shock, she made her way over to the bed. Leaning over, she examined all the photos she had of just the two of them. It looked like pretty much every single one they ever took.

"Oh, jeez, Yein," Solar mutters, only feeling worse about what her little sister had been going through the more she learned. Did she really keep these around and look at them all the time? "You poor thing," she sighs, sitting on the edge of the bed to pull her sister into another hug.

Yein was silent for a while as she accepted the hug. She knew Sol had figured it out, and she didn't want her to feel bad. "It's okay, sissy," she hums in the strongest voice she could manage, "it's okay now, remember?" She repeated the words Solar had just said with a small smile on her lips.

Seeing her little sister happy was enough to make her smile, too, "okay, yeah, you're right."

Yein cries in excitement from the fact she agreed, leaning into hug her again. That same scent of perfume their sister always wore, she never wanted to forget it. She never wanted to not feel the warmth of her big sister right beside her, the only one who ever helped her, the only one who understood.

She wouldn't be alone anymore. Solar was back now, it meant that the lectures would stop, the fights would stop. Everything would be okay, right?

The two sit in silence for a while, and Chaerin and Jinhwan remained awkwardly hidden in the doorway. Upon glancing at them, Solar remembered the fact they were even there in the first place. Not wanting make them wait like she did to Jinhwan outside, she cleared her throat as Yein sat up straight again. Was it really the time for her to start bringing up her new life casually? She knew kids feelings were delicate, and she didn't want to upset poor Yein again.

Unsure as to if she was really okay since she had just been screamed at by her own parent, she's hesitant. It was when the small girl had shuffled across her mattress, reaching for a tablet on the end table beside her bed.

With curious eyes, her big sister watched her, wondering what in the world she could be doing. She did know about how she liked to listen to her and Jinhwan's song, and she also knew that she had no idea it was actually her in the first place. But she didn't think she was so casually just going to play it right then, but she guessed the song really brought some comfort to her. It warmed her heart to know that somehow, even if she wasn't there with her, Solar had been able to help her in some way.

"I've been listening to this song," Yein is muttering as she was on YouTube, typing in something, "I think you'll really like it!"

A playful grin is on her lips as she reaches out to take the tablet when Yein had clicked on an audio video of it. Already knowing it was Only You, she goes back to the videos, instead tapping on the music video that actually showed her and Jinhwan's faces.

Yein was lost as to why she was doing that. She never really cared to watch the actual video. Either way, she shrugged it off as her sister holds the tablet out before the both of them. Seconds later, the song had begun to play.

The sound of the familiar beat made Solar grin to herself. Nostalgia had run up her spine, sprinting through her veins, as the beginning scenes of the sun and nature had shown. Briefly, she recollected the times when they were recording the video. It was when so many complicated things were happening, when there was so much conflict, but she still remembered how happy she was just being with him. Just knowing her and Jinhwan had made such a great song, one she would remember forever, had made the shooting so fun.

Even though it had been so scary and she kept fearing they were going to make it obvious that they were actually a lot more than friends. It had been one of her many horrors back then, but they got through it just fine. In fact, even if they had slipped up with the way they smiled at each other in the video, it only made the fans thrilled, screaming about how much chemistry the two stars seemed to have.

It wasn't wrong at all. They had a lot of chemistry.

"I've never seen this before," Yein is muttering as she watches the prettily colored scenes of trees and the sun. Only You was meant to be a summery song, something that made you want to just get up and dance no matter the weather, or go to the beach. So when they filmed it, that was exactly where they went. 

Solar knew in a few seconds her sister was going to be shocked. It showed Jinhwan first, since his vocals started out the song. Yein wasn't really impressed as she watched the boy on the screen. Her eyes followed his dance moves, and there was a slight tint of a smile on her lips as her head bopped along to the music. It was a catchy song, you had to admit, and it would always have you moving in some way. 

Meanwhile, Solar could feel her heart beating out of her chest. Jinhwan was truly ethereal, and she adored the way he looked in the video. He had his long hair parted, that was only getting longer by the day, with a loose shirt hanging off his shoulders. It showcased a portion of his chest, making that little cross tattoo on his collarbone visible. Seeing him that way while knowing he was literally right there, feet away from her hidden, was sending her into a mental frenzy.

There were bigger situations going on though other than her desire for Jinhwan. The sound of her rap was heard, and it had started without her on the screen at first. Before you knew it, there she was, her brown hair blowing in the wind as she walked across the beach. She'd been wearing a very showy outfit, but her and Jinhwan were both barefoot. When his part had come up, he'd walked into the frame, and they both performed the dance to it together as it went into the chorus.

Hearing the song and seeing the cute video honestly made her want to actually do it right now. Instead, she dared to glance down to Yein, who reached out a finger to pause the video. Taking the tablet, she holds it up with one hand, a confused look splattered over her face as she lifted it up. Holding the tablet beside Solar's face, she blinked rapidly as she compared the two faces beside each other.

"I think I'm going crazy," Yein is muttering in her small voice, scratching her head, "there's no way that would make any sense."

Chuckling at her innocent sister, she takes the tablet from her hand, hitting play on the video. "I've heard you listened to this song a lot because it reminded you of me," she's humming as her and Jinhwan continue to dance around the beach on the screen, "that's because it is me, Yein."

The girl stares at her in disbelief. Her green eyes kept going from the screen, back to Solar's face, then back to the screen. Trying to take it in was pending to be pretty hard for her. Her sister was a famous singer? 

"Wow," she gasps, knowing the resemblance between the girl on the screen and her sister beside her was too unreal, "you're famous?!"

Her childish ways would always be adorable to Solar. Nodding, she giggles as she pats Yein's head, "yeah, I guess, I'm sort of famous."

"That's so cool!" Yein cries, "that's so much better than being stuck here! You get to dance and sing and wear pretty clothes!" she unpaused the video again, leaning until her nose was practically touching the screen to inspect the girl on the screen. She had minimized the video so she could see the title, "but why is this girl named Soomi?"

"That's my Korean name," she shares, and Yein's mouth forms an o as she took in the information.

"Whoa," she continues to gawk, "that's so cool, I want to do that, too! What's my Korean name?!"

Unable to stop her snickers from how sweet her poor sister was, she wraps an arm around her as she hums, "I don't know, Yein, you don't have to actually change it, you know. Your name is Korean."

"Oh," she mutters, not seeming to understand the difference between a Korean and an American like name. The girl had slowly looked back down to the video, her eyes wandering around it as she tried to truly understand everything. It was a funny sight, as Yein kept glancing from the screen back to her sister every once in a while. She seemed to be intently thinking about how her sister was really the girl in the video, no wonder she always reminded her of Solar. 

Honestly, Sol was just glad that she was distracted by something other than the clear issue in all of their lives. 

The two had sat and talked for a bit about what famous people did and if Yein could "change her name to a cool Korean one too." It was when Solar thought her mind was finally off the topic since the video had ended a long time ago, that the young girl abruptly blurted, "so was that your boyfriend?"

Caught off guard for a second, Solar looks down to her, blinking a few times she processes the words she just heard. It wasn't hard to tell who she was talking about, but she didn't expect her to bring Jinhwan up. Only then did she look down further to her lap, seeing the tablet on to reveal a photo of her and Jinhwan at the first day of KCON.

She tried not to mentally explode at the fact he was once again, standing right over there. Instead, Solar purses her lips as she shrugs, "I guess you could say that, Yein."

"Wow!" she squeals, "I can't believe big sissy is with someone that's not Sunwoo, what about him?" there's a tint of sadness in her eyes. Yein really cared for people when they showed her kindness. Prior to how he was to literally everyone else, Sunwoo had always had a soft spot for her little sister. It was probably because of how she was the equivalent of a mini-Solar, not to mention, her undeniable cutesy charm. Remembering how Yein had always liked her ex made Solar feel a bit bad as she frowned, "I bet Sunwoo is sad, he just told me the other day how much he missed you, sissy."

Not knowing what to do because Yein looked like she was going to cry about Sunwoo, Solar hurriedly clears her throat, "no, no, Yein, it's not like that. Me and Sunwoo are still good, I saw him a few hours ago."

Since she was just a kid, Yein wasn't too hard to convince - at least not when you're Solar. The girl would probably believe anything she said, which was why a smile crept on her lips again, "oh, okay!" she cheers, "Yay, Solar and Sunwoo are good!"

Chuckling at her sister's bright aura, she pats her head as she nods, "we are indeed. Say, would you want to go somewhere with me and Sunwoo this week?"

Yein's eyes had lit up at this proposal, "really?! We could do that - are you sure? Mother and father don't let me leave the house because I'm not studying enough," she was pouting again as she says this, her voice barely audible by the time she was done. Yein seemed to remember what was really going on in her life, and upon it, her mood couldn't help but deplete.

"Oh, Yein, don't worry," her sister was quick to assure her brightly, "I'm taking you no matter what, and I'll help you with all your homework, okay? Just like old times."


89:00 | the queen

Only minutes later, Solar finally explained to Yein that the boy in the video - who was sort of her boyfriend - was the one who brought her there. Yein was bewildered at first to hear there was another famous person in the house (as she put it,) and before you knew it, she was excited to meet him. 

"Hi Yein~" Jinhwan had leaned down to the girl's shorter height, a sweet grin on his lips, "I'm Jinhwan, I've heard so much about you!"

Solar felt her heart ache as how cute he was when he spoke to children. She could already tell he must be good with kids. Yein seemed thrilled to see him, as she beams up at him with a wide smile. The other two sisters in the room were wondering why she had yet to say anything, when she suddenly jumped forward, extending both her arms to hug Jinhwan's waist.

The caramel boy was shocked at first. He did not expect her to do that, but either way, he didn't mind it as he accepted the hug from the young girl. 

"You've helped my sissy so much, thank you!" she was muttering into his sweater, "thank you for bring her back, thank you, Jinawn!" the way she pronounced his name in her tiny voice made both Chaerin and Solar burst into fits of giggles. There was no denying how precious the moment was, though.

Jinhwan met Solar's eyes as he hugged the girl back, and she sent him a grateful smile to show she agreed with what Yein was saying. Hesitantly, he looked back down to her, "no problem, but I didn't do much, your big sister is a really strong person."

That she was.

Stepping out of the embrace, Yein pursed her lips as she took a few steps back from the crowd. There's a tint of mischief in her green eyes as she looked to both Solar and Jinhwan. 

"Hm," she ponders, tapping her chin with her finger in thought, "you two look really cute together!"

Feeling her throat grow dry, she tries to keep an unbothered smile on her face. Solar wasn't too sure of how Jinhwan felt about her sister's comment, but he seemed pretty thrilled about it as he exclaims, "thanks, Yein, that means a lot!" he sounded so cheery that it made her want to cry because well - he was Jinhwan. Her heart only aches further from the emotions that were welled inside of it as he threw an arm around her shoulder, pulling her into his side. "I'd say we do look pretty good together."

There wasn't any hiding it anymore. Yein's green eyes were wide as she saw her sister's expression, and she burst into giggles, pointing a finger to make the girl's embarrassment worse, "aw, sissy is blushing!" she walked a bit closer to the two. Sol was too busy burying her face in her hands as Jinhwan had been deviously grinning down at her, so she didn't notice when the little girl walked beside him, pulling his sweater sleeve and motioning for him to lean down. When he did, she cupped his ear with one tiny hand, leaning into whisper, "I think sissy must really like you, Jinawn."

Jinhwan bit his lip as he stood up straight again, giving the girl a look to say he wasn't fully in belief. But she only nodded immensely to show she was serious. "But be careful for Sunwoo, he and sissy love each other too!" Poor Yein didn't seem to understand that it didn't exactly work how she was thinking, "wait, so is sissy with Sunwoo and Jinawn?"

By this time, Solar had heaved out a sigh, not wanting to hear anymore about this. She knew none of her family members knew about the miscarriage, and they weren't going to as far as she was planning, but it still burned her insides to casually talk about Sunwoo. Sure, they were on okay terms, but she knew that he was thinking they were going to go back to how they used to be. She didn't know how she was going to manage to break it to him that they couldn't.

It wasn't that she moved on, it was that she couldn't handle the chaos being with someone like him brought. He was a gang boss, after all, which brought a lot of violence and illegal acts she couldn't be associated with - and really shouldn't be period - especially with her as a world wide star. She'd already dealt with a scandal before. If a dating scandal with Jungkook almost ruined her career, what would one with someone like Sunwoo do?

Solar didn't want to find out. 

Plus, it was safe to say she might've moved on. She confirmed this fact when she looked up to the boy that still had his arm around her. Jinhwan wasn't focusing on her, though, because for the last few minutes he had been calmly explaining to Yein that no, Solar couldn't be together with more than one person - at least not officially. 

"Huh, so that means someone is gonna have to be sad," Yein's still contemplating it deeper than she should be. 

Not wanting her to start whining again, Solar is about to speak up again and change the subject when the door had crept open behind. The brunette turns, Jinhwan's arm leaving her shoulders and instead grabbing her hand with his own. Her green eyes blink as she processed the fact she had yet to notice Chaerin had left, but had returned just then.

The thing was, she brought company.

Chaerin was the only one who had fully entered the bedroom. There's a nervous look on her usually blank expression, and she had been in the middle of talking to someone that was trailing behind her.

Solar was unaware of who it was until she heard the family voice. "Chaerin, I don't think this joke is funny at all, I was trying to go see what it is your father is so worked up about this time," the mother of the Lee siblings had sighed in disappointment. Her mother was always a calm woman who wanted to appear collected to the outside world. Whenever they would attend events or go out, she always tried to act and tell everyone that their family was perfect, that they didn't have a single problem in the world. It only increased the popular belief that life in the castles was pleasant. 

She never wanted anyone to know it wasn't. Just like she never wanted her children to know how much she struggled. Never did she want to hurt any of them, who would want to hurt their own kids? All she wanted was the best, but she wasn't given a choice when her husband ruled everything she did. It wasn't ever her decision to pester them, to yell at them, to scold them. It was their father's. 

But yet she didn't have the courage to leave him. She had to face it; without her husband she had nothing.

Mrs. Lee had always tried to overlook his problems, his anger. She tried to make him calm down, to get him to stop treating everyone so poorly and blaming them for all of his troubles. A part of her always wanted to believe the person he once was was still inside of him, that he could be saved. But after the loss of her daughter, she came to understand there was no saving him. 

That was, of course, without ever thinking of the effect of her daughter's return. None of the Lee's had believed she would ever come back.

Which is why when Chaerin had only groaned, pulling her mother's arm so she was dragged fully into the room, that Mrs. Lee gasped. She gasped so loud it was practically a scream, and at the sight of her petrified expression, the last of the family - that was unaware of her presence - had entered the room fully as well.

Juyeon and their mother looked like they were deer caught in headlights.

Solar sighs as she realizes this was it, this was the last portion of her family she had yet to face. Collecting her courage, she steps forward, letting go of Jinhwan's hand, to politely bow to her mother, "hi, mother."

She wasn't mad at her. Solar knew her mother didn't have any say in what happened, she was shocked and overwhelmed, but never would she have wanted to kick her out like her father did.

Juyeon purses his lips, not knowing what to do. There was so much guilt that had flooded through his veins, so many regrets he thought he would have to hold onto forever. Could he finally fix it now? Did his sister hate him for what he did? Worry is plastered on his face as he couldn't make eye contact with her, instead letting his head fall to stare at his feet.

"Solar?" her mother whispers, voice trembling as she takes a gradual step towards the girl, "my dear Solar?!"

Her entire body began to shake with emotion. All of the siblings had grown emotional from seeing their sister again, and they showed it through different ways even if it wasn't crying. But no matter what, none of their sentimental value had reached that of their mother's. After all, siblings or not, she had birthed her. Her beloved first daughter, that she raised with all of her might with Jaehyun, who she always believed would accomplish so many great things. Her sweet Solar, that she could've never been more proud of. Even though she was dating that boy, she still remained to be the best daughter she could ask for.

When she found her with drugs, it made her upset. But after having so much time to think about why Solar must've turned to something like that, she came to understand. She was stressed, how could one not be with all the responsibilities she had as a young teenager? Came to be, she just couldn't blame her or even care anymore. 

"It's me, mother."

"Oh, my Solar!" she's crying, breaking into uncontrollable sobs as she leaps forward. Her entire body was still trembling so heavily she was worried her mother was going to faint. Solar accepts her massive hug, burying her face in her mom's shirt as her mother wept and wept. "You're back, I didn't think you'd ever come back here," her voice kept cracking as she tried to form what she wanted to say, "I'm sorry for what happened, Solar, I'm sorry we were so hard on you."

Patting her mom's back, she tried to calm her down from her waves of emotions. It was obviously really hard for her. "It's okay, mother, I understand," she soothes her assuringly, "there's no need to apologize anymore, I did something wrong, and I needed to be punished for that."

"Solar," her mother sighs as they pull away a couple of minutes later. The look on her face worried her that she was about to bring up the past, but it was evident that they needed to all do so eventually. The time had come, she had to remind her, she has to face it all now.

"Wait," a voice blurts before their mother could say another word. Everyone was confused as the sight of Juyeon stepping up. He looked like he was going to have a heart attack as his green eyes met Solar's, and he anxiously cleared his throat, "i-it wasn't all Solar's fault." When the words left his mouth, their mother was looking at Juyeon in complete confusion.

"Juyeon, sweetie," she sighs.

"Really, mother," he persists, "I-It was me and Chaerin's, we always pressured her, we made her take the fall for us."

Solar's mouth was wide open without her knowing from how surprised she was at what she was hearing them say. Of course, she knew this well, but she never thought she would see the day either of them owned up to it. 

"Juyeon's right," Chaerin had hesitantly cut in, "he's telling the truth, mother."

Even though the fact didn't matter anymore, their mother exhales deeply as she takes it all in. It was sort of a relief for her to hear, after all this time, but it also only made her feel worse. Turning back to her daughter, she links her arm with hers as she looks to the other siblings, "Downstairs, we should discuss this there, unfortunately with your father."

Upon her words, all the siblings exchange horrified glances. There was no telling what they were in for. 

"Mother, are you sure that's a good idea?" Chaerin nervously speaks up.

"Course it's not, and I don't even think it matters anymore," she dully replies, "but this has to come out, even though none of us want it to."

They knew she was right. It causes all of them to sigh, and Yein had grabbed onto Solar's other hand out of fear of facing their father as they all piled out of her bedroom.

"It'll be fine, I won't let anything happen to you guys," their mother had been saying, trying to make them feel better even though it should be the opposite. Only moments ago, the poor woman had been crying her eyes out. It was clear where Solar inherited that strong trait from. Just as they'd made their way to the door, she finally noticed the elephant in the room. Jinhwan felt like he was sinking into the ground when he made eye contact with Solar's mother, feeling rather awkward that he was intruding on this family situation. But she didn't appear very upset as she only blinked, "oh, hi, have we met before?"

A lump formed in Solar's throat she looked to Jinan. She really forgot to introduce him again. It was becoming a serious problem of hers. 

He made eye contact with her, and he seemed to be trying to ask her something she was unsure of. She only shrugged to him, sending him a small smile. Jinhwan purses his lips as he looks back to her mom, "hi, Mrs. Lee," he bowed slowly, "I'm Jinhwan, I came here with Solar."

It felt really weird calling her that.

She didn't appear that phased to hear this, as it was probably what she assumed. Solar's mother had yet to be aware of her new life, and the fact she was famous. It was going to be a ride telling her all of that, and she could only dread it as she kept looking Jinhwan up and down.

Finally, she nods, "nice to meet you, I'm grateful to you for bringing my daughter home."

"No problem, Mrs. Lee," he offered her another sweet smile.

There's an improving look on her face as she waved a hand in dismissal, "Solji, dear, call me Solji."

Jinhwan only nods, and by this time her mother had turned back to her daughter. "I'm so joyous you moved on from your last boyfriend."

Chaerin started snickering from their mother's words. Solar only sighed at the fact she was going through this again, but really, what did she expect? It was her doing to bring Jinhwan in her own home to meet her family, what else were they going to assume?

And was it really such a bad thing?

"Yeah," she confidently hums, "I am too."


90:00 | the new beginning

Jinhwan felt undeniably awkward. No doubt, he was going to bother the living hell out of Solar later for the fact she finally so casually agreed to him being her boyfriend. Hearing the words fly of her lips was a slap in the face to Jinhwan, and not a bad one, either. He'd never experienced such heart ache because of how much he adored someone. It was known to him now, clearer than ever, that he didn't want anyone else. His heart belonged to Kim Soomi.

Even with the fact he should be jumping around with joy for how much he'd scored that day, he still couldn't seem to find a way to be happy in the current atmosphere. Of course, he was happy for Soomi and her family. He was proud of her for finally gaining the courage to come home, to reunite with the most important people in her life she always said she'd never see again. This was a huge obstacle she was leaping over, and he couldn't be in more awe at how well she was taking it. 

But it was heart breaking to see the reality of how torn the family was. He didn't think there were really family's like this out there, so when Solar had explained that this is how it usually was in the castle - as her friend Changmin and everyone seemed to call it. You didn't have a choice, you had to be at the top of your class, score the highest marks on everything, and do every extracurricular activity there was. Never did he imagine there would practically be requirements to be considered part of a family, something you were born into, something that wasn't supposed to be forced. Although he hadn't saw it himself since he let Soomi go into her father's office herself, he'd obviously heard the way he was screaming at such a mere child. After meeting Yein, it only made him feel worse.

The way her eyes brightened whenever someone would say something nice to her, it made it clear she didn't hear it often. She was a kid, and it tore him apart to know she was growing up in this atmosphere where she was constantly resented and treated like she just wasn't good enough. 

Not to mention the other problems clear in the family. There were so many and it went so deep that it would take hours for Soomi to really sit down and explain it all to him. By this point, as they were all sitting in the living room, he felt like he was intruding horribly.

Nervously, he tries not to show the fact he was beyond uncomfortable. Solar had already introduced him to her father, this time as her boyfriend straight up, but the man didn't look too fond of him. He'd heard about how he really hated Sunwoo, who wouldn't? He sort of understood. It wasn't like the guy gave off friendly vibes, after all. He didn't think he himself was that bad, though, but when her father had made eye contact, he looked like he wanted to puke.

He'd bowed politely when Soomi introduced him, offering a greeting before taking a seat next to her. Her father, in response, hadn't said much regarding him. He'd only looked him up and down with a grimace wiped on his face. The way he clearly already had a distaste for him made him feel sick, so much he couldn't pay attention to anything anyone was saying - not that it was his business to, anyway.

What kind of vibe did he give off? he pondered in contemplation, feeling under the weather from this change of events. Sure, he understood her father was just not a very good person, so it was expected he probably didn't really like anyone since he even treated his own kids like trash. But after everyone in Soomi's old life having had loved him, even her mother, he had high hopes still.

Pouting to himself, he stares at his feet as he continues to think hard about it. Maybe he had a dirty look on his face without meaning to. Jinhwan wasn't exactly the ideal type a father would have in mind for his daughter, sure. He wasn't necessarily muscular, and what muscle he had wasn't very visible. His height was an entire different story. Usually, a father would envision a tall, buffer sort of man that could 'protect' their daughter, and he figured he probably didn't really fit the category with his skinny jeans, caramel styled hair, and dangling ear piercing. 

But that didn't mean he wouldn't give his all to protect her, especially after everything she's been through. At the same time, Jinhwan was most definitely not weak. And when it came to the girl he loved, he'd probably kill someone for her - you know, just like Sunwoo did.

"What's going on?" her father had spoke into the awkward, dull silence, his eyes hesitantly meeting his daughter's, "I thought you'd never be back," he gawks in disbelief, "b-but here you are."

Compared to his yelling tone earlier when they entered to now, he didn't sound like the same person. The man that was verbally abusing his eleven year old daughter now looked like he was going to cry for his oldest daughter. It was a strange sight, and Jinhwan couldn't help but wonder if it wasn't because he genuinely missed her, but more of the fact she was apparently the only child willing to take over his company that had the potential to do so. With his lacking grasp on the family, though, he didn't think it was his right to assume something like that. 

"Glad you're happy," Solar bluntly states, "but I really wasn't when I came home for the first time in five years to hear you screaming at Yein and calling all of your kids worthless."

Her immediate comeback had everyone in states of shock. Chaerin and Juyeon were clearly nervous about what they were going to have to say and how already south it was turning, as their heads hung low and they anxiously tapped their feet on the floor. Yein looked like she wanted to cry, hating the way the family was always fighting, but appeared to find a peace of mind as she clung onto her big sister's arm. Their mother was sitting with Chaerin and Juyeon on the sofa in the middle, her arms crossed over her chest as she peered at their father expectantly, a look of disappointment evident in her eyes, showing she wasn't a big fan of her husband and his actions, either. 

Lastly, the empty spot was clear as well. The oldest, dear Jaehyun, wasn't there as he'd left for work without knowing his big sister was back. Since he knew she was coming sooner or later, though, it wouldn't be a big surprise to him. However, he was absent on a rather important meeting.

The father of the Lee family had opened his mouth before closing it repeatedly. Ticking of the large clock on the wall was all you could hear, until he clears his throat, at a clear loss for words, "S-Solar, everyone, I didn't mean what I said," his words sounded a bit forced. But the family members peer at him in intent anyway. "Everything had been hard recently, and I-I always had this weight on my shoulders after you left, Solar," his words becoming heartfelt made everyone stiffen. Solar could only stare at him blankly, letting him say what he wanted, because it was the first time she'd ever heard her father speak of his true emotion. "The guilt, I could never get over it, just knowing I kicked my valedictorian, highest marks in the entire school, star student daughter out," everyone sighed, unimpressed when he started naming off her academic achievements. What did they expect though? "It made me resent everyone in this house because I couldn't take back my own mistake."

Silence.

Solar didn't want to say it, but she was lost for words.

Really, she was serious when she said he had never said anything about how he really felt. He was a rock, someone who only ever spoke of what he needed from people and what they were doing wrong. Never did he show an emotion other than anger. 

But here he was, her hard-headed, cold, father that kicked her to the curb five years ago, breaking down across from her.

Pursing her lips, she remained strong as she retorts, "so, was treating them all like trash and trying to make Chaerin a replacement for me the answer? Was making your wife feel like she has no meaning in your life the answer as well? Was making your eleven year old daughter think she has no right to be considered your daughter because she's not smart enough the final answer?!"

She raised her voice without meaning to. The sight of their almost perfect daughter so riled up had everyone bewildered, too shocked to do or say anything to interrupt. It was clear she had a lot piled inside of her, and she wasn't anywhere near done yet.

"I did drugs," she hissed, "because of what you turned me into," her fists are clenched in her lap as she tried to hold in her anger, her frustration, but most of all, her pain. "I couldn't even date anyone, and yes, I understand what his family did and does, but that's not the point. You never listened to me when I told you he was different, you never cared about how I felt, and maybe if you would've said something to me when I stayed up - all night every day - studying for the exams, SATs, ACTs, EOCs, other than don't make anything lower than a 98%, maybe I wouldn't of had the will to turn towards drugs," her breathe is heavy, her brunette locks falling in her face. Every word that left her lips got quieter, but the more she thought about it, the more she got riled up again. "All you ever saw me as was something you could use for the better of your future, never in your eyes was I your daughter, was I, father? You know why," she pauses again from the feeling of someone grabbing her hand. Solar's eyes met Jinhwan's in a quick glance, and she knew she still had one last thing to say despite his worried eyes. 

"Because that night, you wouldn't of thrown me out like that if you cared, would you? You wouldn't of told me to leave without saying a word if you ever valued-"

Jinhwan squeezed her hand so hard it caused her to jump from surprise. She hadn't realized how much she was apparently crying all over again until she felt the wet tears dripping from her cheeks. Trying to calm her vigorous breathing, she heaves out a sigh, leaning back into the couch as Jinhwan had rubbed the back of her hand with her thumb in concern. 

It was clear he wanted to do more, to say something, but couldn't with everyone around.

"Don't cry, sissy," Yein had whispered for him as he tugged at her other arm.

Everyone else in the room was silent. Her father's mouth was slightly agape as his head fell, and he seemed to be trying hard to think.

Just as he looked up again at his torn daughter, opening his mouth to finally speak after her outbreak, something stopped him from doing so. "Wait!" Chaerin had blurted, jumping up from the sofa she sat on.

Everyone looked to the two, the only one remaining in the dark as to what they wanted being their father. Juyeon had stood up with his sister, and they both exchanged horrified looks. Swallowing the lump in his throat, he slowly spoke, "t-that night wasn't Solar's fault, the drugs were for us," he rushed his words out confidently. Each one was a knife thrown straight at their father, who's eyes bulged as they heard this. "S-She got most of them for us, and instead of standing with her, I-I told Chaerin to blame it all on her," there's tears in his eyes, as it seemed like everyone couldn't help but cry on such an emotional day. Turning away from facing his father, Juyeon instead turns to his big sister, "I'm sorry, Sol, I'm so sorry that I did that to you."

"Me too," Chaerin had cut in, even though the two sisters already made up, "I regretted it everyday. When I had to do everything you did, it only made me realize how fucked up it was even more. I don't know how you got through all of this, Solar," she shakes her head, words shaky as she sighs, "but I sure as hell think you're the best sister I could ever ask for for not hating me after it all."

Having had sat up from how she was laying, she could only stare at her siblings. They didn't think she was gonna say anything, until she gets up, walking across the room to wrap one arm around either of them. 

"It's okay, you two," she pats their backs at they engulf in a group hug, "it's okay."

The sound of someone clearing their throat cuts off their moment. Everyone was wiping their eyes, even their mother, as they turn back to their father. Solar had gone back to take her seat next to Jinan and Yein, and Solji had wrapped her arms around Juyeon and Chaerin as they sat down again, hugging them for comfort.

"I'm proud of you two," she whispered to them, rubbing their forearms.

Neither of them had any words. They'd never heard that from her in their lives.

"I understand," her father finally breathes into the quiet, breathing irregular as he only stared at the ground, "I understand everything you said. I was never an ideal, acceptable father to any of you - or husband, and I-I'll be forever remorseful of that fact that I've realized now."

Unsure what to think, the man suddenly rose to his feet. His eyes rake over everyone, even Jinhwan, and he cleared his throat, "Chaerin, you can drop the classes, go to the college you applied for and don't worry about the pay, alright? I'll take care of it all."

Chaerin's jaw dropped to the ground. Covering her gaping mouth with her hand, she didn't get a chance to say any words when he spoke again, "Yein, my youngest daughter, you are my child. It's okay if you don't make the best grades, because yes, you're still my daughter. I shouldn't be pressuring you, you're free to be the you you wish to be, okay?"

"Juyeon, I never meant to make you feel stupid. You're smart, just like your other siblings, but you have a talent for something else, and that's okay," he sighs out, closing his eyes as he went on and on, "so, if what you told me is really what you want to do, I want you to do it."

"Solar," his voice cracks as he turns to his oldest daughter, "Solar, you are everything I dreamed of in a child, you are the embodiment of what I thought I'd never have. The truth is, I was always proud of you, but I let my anger oversee me ever showing you that. I was so mad that you were dating the Kim's son, I was so mad that things weren't perfectly going my way, it blocked my vision that I should've just been happy with how successful and dedicated you were to doing all that you did, because you did it for me, not because you wanted to, but just because I wanted you to." His feet click against the floor as he stood before her, and for the first time, his green irises dripped with emotion. "So, I'm forever apologetic, Solar. All I ever wanted to do was take that day back, all of it back, but I can't, all I can do is stand here now, and ask if you'll do me the honor of forgiving me as my beloved daughter."

Everyone was anticipating this moment more than anything. Here it was, right before their eyes, the event no one in the Lee family thought they would see in a million years. Their father, their cold hearted father that only ever showed hatred towards anyone, apologizing and coming face to face with the wrong he had done. Although he couldn't take it back, although you couldn't eliminate the scars, that didn't mean you couldn't still start anew. The fact he was doing that, the fact he was taking this step, was a miracle only Solar could cure.

The beloved Solar that they were all blessed to be seeing right then. 

"Okay, father."

"Okay?" he repeats, unsure and cautious of her blatant tone.

Abruptly, Solar rose to her feet. There's no emotion on her face at first, until she stepped forward, and without warning wrapped her arms around her father. It was the first time she'd hugged him since she was a child, and it took all of her courage to do so.

"I forgive you, father," she breathes as she pulled away seconds later, "but that's only if you change."

He was silent for a while.

"Alright, I'm done with all of it," he nods slowly, "I've come to terms with what I've done wrong, I don't plan to do it again."

The last thing she ever expected happened, she smiled. Widely, ear to ear, along with the rest of the family members as they all exchanged glances.

This was it, they realized, this was the new beginning they all waited for.


91:00 | but is it better now?

"Here," Solar mutters as she was typing in a text, sending to her family members phones, intently. She hits send, and a triumphant grin spreads over her face, "it'd mean a lot if you guys came to this address tomorrow night, it'll show you all what I did after I left."

Their phones all ding, and everyone looked down at the message. Chaerin was the only one who had a good idea of what it was since she heard all about her career and why she was in America. Juyeon still had no idea she was a singer, and neither did her parents. As for Yein, she just didn't have a phone to know what was going on.

"Okay," Solji hums, stepping forward to pull her daughter into a tight hug, "we'll be there, dear. Can't wait to see this surprise, but I'm sad you're not staying the night."

Accepting her mom's embrace, she hugged her like her life depended on it. "I'm sorry, mother, but I'll be back, okay?"

"Bye sissy!" Yein cried as she hurriedly ran to hug her before she could take a singular step out the door, "now that I have your number, I'm gonna text you from my iPad!"

Chuckling at her sister, she only nods, "alright, Yein, I'll always respond, and when I get back I'll help you with your homework, kay?"

"Okay~!" she cheerily hums, though you could see the sadness in her eyes from them having to leave. Before anyone else could get a chance, the little girl had stepped out into the cold New York air, where Jinhwan was standing waiting for them to finish their goodbyes. He already bid goodbye to pretty much everyone, and didn't have much to say since he didn't know her family well. But he didn't get to say anything to Yein, as he figured there was no way he actually made much of an impact on her. This was why he was surprised when the girl had ran up to him, wrapping her arms around his waist from the side.

"Goodbye Jinawn, I hope I see you again!" she tells him brightly, beaming from ear to ear as she looked up at him, "please continue always keeping big sissy happy!"

He couldn't lie, even though it had only been half a day or less, he had grown attached to her and even the way she mistakenly kept pronouncing his name. Jinhwan grins at he bends to his knees so he was basically eye level with the girl, reaching out to grab her hand. "Don't worry, I'll see you tomorrow with Solar, Yein," he assures her, petting her head and ruffling her hair, "and I'll never let her be sad again, okay?"

She jumps from excitement at everything she just heard. Yein was so thrilled at everything going on that she hugged him again around the neck this time. "Thank you, Jinawn!" as she pulled away, she curiously reached up and touched one of his caramel locks, "hm, you have cool hair."

Giggling, he didn't mind as she played with his hair. She was just a kid, but she was genuinely the kid version of Soomi. It was no joke when they'd said she looked like her. Yein had the same eyes, the same smile, the same hair, she was just younger.

As if he didn't already, it definitely added to the soft spot he'd grown for Solar's little sister.

"See you tomorrow!" Solar waves and calls to her family, already being a few feet down the side walk. Yein had still been clinging onto Jinhwan like her life depended on it, but the boy didn't seem to mind as he squeezed her back. The young girl pouted when he had finally pulled away, informing her that they really had to get back so they could practice tomorrow morning before their show. He'd jogged to catch up with where Solar was already mid-side walk, her breathing a bit irregular as she had finally finished waving away to her family.

Glancing back one last time, he notices they finally all poured back inside the mansion. He took that as the time to grab her hand, intertwining their fingers as he calls to her in worry, "Soo, are you doing okay?"

The brunette seemed to have been in some sort of trance, as she shook herself out of it, her eyes wide as she met his. Soft, brown irises that poured into her skin like milk chocolate, they seemed to snap her back into reality.

"Yeah, these last couple of days have just been a lot," she mutters as she broke their eye contact, instead diverting them to the night sky. Never did she think any of this would have happened. All Solar had been doing for the last year was running from her problems instead of solving them, but here she was, and all of them were falling into place so quickly. She just couldn't seem to mentally keep up with any of it.

There's a frown that takes over the caramel boy's face. His hand left her own so he could instead wrap his arm around her, and the scent of the perfume he wore was a blessing to her nostrils. With a shaky sigh, she wraps both her arms around the small of his torso, snuggling her face into the sweater he was wearing as they walked across the street towards Chanhee's car.

"I know, I admire you for how strong you've been through this," he shares as they'd reached the vehicle. Jinhwan walked around with her to her side so he could open the door, still speaking, "and I'm really happy you chose me to be there with you through it all."

A small smile slaps it's way onto her lips when he shut the door after such words left his mouth. Her eyes follow him as he walks around the car to his own side, climbing in. The leather seats squeaked against his weight, and she pursed her lips as she tried to make the big, childish grin leave her face. She'd been so overwhelmed only seconds ago, but this was just how easily he could change her mood.

"It was for a good reason," she mumbles a second later as the engine had roared from Jinhwan shoving the key in the ignition. "I thought I didn't know, but something told me it should be you."

From the fact he hadn't expected her to even say anything in response to his previous confession, the boy was left in a state of shock. As if he'd been thrown into a trance himself, he was left to peer down at the steering wheel he sat in front of, trying to comprehend the words she just spoke to him. Something told me it should be you, did it have more meaning than she was letting on? Jinhwan didn't want to get his hopes up, but yet, that was all he had been doing.

Was it not clear?

Her family thought he was her boyfriend, after all. Her old friends loved him, and they didn't even know Jungkook.

He knew it, he knew it then.

Because it was clearer than ever.









+






"Ow, you know this really is not a fucking blindfold!" Solar whines like an elementary student, her tiny hands pawing at the material that was so tightly bound around her eyes. She was extremely positive that when someone made a belt, they had not intended for it to ever be tied around someone's head. But yet, here she was, with the short but lovable guy she was honestly not very happy with right then for his actions. Her attempts at taking the leather from her eyes failed every time as he continued to guide her, his hands tightly over it so she had no choice but to follow.

"Soo," he giggles, his breathe fanning the side of her face as he watched carefully where they were going, "stop being so over dramatic."

"Me?!" she gawks, "Kim Jinhwan, you're real lucky I can't see the area your balls are in right now."

There was silence for a second. "I mean, depending on the meaning, I'm not sure if that's luck-yah!" he didn't get to say the last syllable when the girl tried to elbow him abruptly. She'd failed, as he grabbed her arm with one hand, forcing it forward as he started giggling all over again, "Soomi, you're really something else."

The girl acted like she disregarded the comment, though they both saw the smile that lowkey took over her face. "But cut the shit, where are we going? You still have Chanhee's car, you know, that's literally his baby."

"Calm down," he's giggling again like a school girl as he continues to guide her through the unknown place, "he lended it to me with good trust."

But Solar didn't sound confident from hearing his words. She never thought she would hear someone like Hee doing that in all her years of life. "Impossible, you must've bribed him with your good looks."

"But he's not gay, right? That's Kevin."

She snorts from his comment that was a lot funnier than he knew, but shakes her head, "yeah, but Chanhee still admires a beautiful man when he sees one."

She couldn't see the smirk that took over his face for once, "so I'm a beautiful man, huh?"

Usually, the stubborn girl that never let anyone other than herself have their way would've refused to admit to this fact even though she basically implied such. If it was a previous time, he would've never caught the light of day. But there was a different aura around her that night, there had been ever since they left the Lee mansion, and it was one Jinhwan had never seen on her before in his life.

And because of that aura, she sighs out in defeat, but you could hear the smile on her lips without even needing to see her face, "yeah, you've caught me, so can you take this fucking leather off my eyes now?"

Jinhwan chuckles again, before suddenly halting their movement. An impatient breathe leaves the brunettes lips, and he hums to himself when he turns her with his hands on her shoulders. Slowly, he removes the material from her eyes. A confused look spreads over her face as she looked around, but they were just in some random hallway.

"Idiot," he shakes his head, "we aren't there yet, I just wanted to talk to you."

Not understanding what he meant, the green eyed girl peers up at the soft boy. "You've been talking to me," she states, and he rolls his eyes at her smart-ass comment.

"Not about your feelings," he assesses, "I want to know how you're doing. Are you okay? Do you feel better, like you've fixed everything?" He questions in concern, a depth of passion forming in those irises of his. A canvas of care painted itself across Jinhwan every time he spoke seriously like this to her, and it always softened her heart to a mushy mess of goo. She purses her lips as he finished what he was saying, "is there anything else bothering you? I just want to make sure you're happy now, Soo, how you deserve to be."

She opens her mouth to speak, but suddenly, he remembers something else and beats her to it. "I mean — I understand you still have your decision to make, okay? I'm not talking about that, I just know that with the stalker thing and your family, it all must be a lot for you. I want to make sure you're handling it okay."

Solar breaks eye contact. There wasn't a direct reason as to why, she was just abruptly overwhelmed with the words he spoke to her. Since it had all happened, she guessed no one had really asked her that question. Now that they had, she didn't know how to answer it.

But it was Jinhwan, and whenever it came to him the feelings just seemed to pour out of her before she even knew it.

"No," she mutters, before stopping herself, "well — yes — I'm better, I think," she pauses, "I'm better now that I have my family back, but I'm worse in the sense that there's still problems I have to face that I never really understood the depth of."

Jinhwan blinks at her in curiosity, egging her on to explain.

"D-Don't get me wrong!" She rushes, shaking her head, "This is all a miracle to me, I never thought we would all be okay again. But the decision between you two, I postponed it for so long because of everything else, I never really got to think or listen to what my heart was telling me."

"Well, do you know what it's telling you?"

"I-I think so," she mumbles, but there was something else bothering her that she had to get off her chest. "But Jinhwan — I have to tell you something."


He stares down at her expectantly. Although he desperately wanted to ask about what it was her heart was feeling, he wasn't going to cut her off about whatever this was. For all he knew, she was about to confess to him once and for all.

"I-I left out this detail when I was talking to my friends because I didn't want them to worry but — when Sunwoo killed her — s-she said she wasn't working alone."

His eyes bulge when they hear her utter the words. Out of shock and worry, his hands subconsciously grab hers and squeeze the life out of them, "Soo, what — are you saying what I think you're saying?"

Swallowing the lump in her throat, Solar hesitantly nods. "She wasn't working alone, there's someone else out there, Nani."


92:00 | they deserve it

"Soo," he clears his throat cautiously, reaching out to put both his hands on her shoulders, "calm down, we don't know that for sure, okay?"

But the brunette only shakes her head, refusing to see any truth in the words which he spoke. Her body was trembling ever so slightly, and her breathing became rather shaky as she thought about it. It wasn't over, she kept thinking, it was horrifying to her. The way she had said they would never get caught like she had, that they would finish what needed to be done. The hard reality was that she was still in jeopardy, the fight wasn't over yet, was it? Soomi had no idea, but she was terrified to let her guard down even though she should be.

A pout takes over Jinhwan's face. His eyes wander her face, taking in her expression for a second as he thinks deeply about what she'd said. When Soomi has yet to say anything in response to his words, and only continues to tremble before him, he took it upon him to step forward so he could embrace her. His arms wrap securely around her, and she buried her face in that fuzzy sweater, trying to return to her normal state. 

"It's okay," he whispers, his lips grazing the top of her ear as he tried his best to comfort her like he did every other time. This action was no stranger to him anymore, it never had been. After all, he was the one who was there for her in these sorts of times since day one, at least - when she let him be. "Nothing will happen to you, Soomi, I won't let it, especially now," he assures her confidently in a louder tone. His grip became tighter, which had taken her off guard as she was still processing what he just said. 

Jinhwan also thought about telling her she still had Sunwoo and his gang, too. But he didn't even want to bring that subject up. Honestly, the entire situation irked him, and just the mere thought of Sunwoo made him upset. Previously, he had understood it wasn't really his place to get upset about things that people did because they loved the same girl he did. He and Soomi weren't together, and they still weren't now, but he felt like they were getting close, to the point where he couldn't lie to himself about the anger that Kim Sunwoo brought upon him.

Never could he be sure about who she was going to choose to spend her life with when it was all said and done, but he sure didn't think it would be Sunwoo. But yet the guy had his mind so set on her, as if they were practically still together. It would annoy anyone to their core if they had feelings for Soomi, not to mention the history the two had. They went further back than her and Jungkook, so much that he could only imagine how it would make him feel if he saw them. 

Upon hearing what Soomi was concerned about now, he knew one thing for sure. Jinhwan might've not have been the one who saved her the first time, he might've played a minimum part in finding the stalker that ended up being his own manager. It made him feel worthless when he thought about it. The woman that had been threatening her life had been right under his nose, but yet he was too stupid to figure it out.

But he wouldn't let that happen this time. It was Soomi's turn to find true happiness, and he was going to make sure she got to do that. She deserved it, she was finally back with her family and her old friends, she deserved to smile and mean it. 

He'd find whoever it was, if they were still out there. And this time, he'd make sure he was the one who got to have the pleasure of killing them. 

"You'll be okay," he could only whisper, knowing to do nothing more than reassure her and take her mind off of it. Though it was unlikely that their manager had been working alone because too many things didn't add up, he wasn't going to confirm it until they had a sign. "Just focus on the good that's going on now, okay? If anything happens, you tell me immediately, alright?"

He pulled out of the hug, rubbing her forearms before grabbing her hands. Those brown irises pour into hers, and her green eyes shimmer under the hotel lights in the hallway.

A long moment of silence passes, and she slowly breathes out, "Okay, you're right - and I'll tell you, Nani."

Glad that she was cheering up, he sends her a small grin, "okay." 

Before she could ask any questions most likely about where it was that they were, he stepped just a centimeter forward to cup her cheeks with his hands. His thumbs graze over her jawline as he leaned in for a split moment, his lips only touching her gently at the beginning, before he leaned in further and fully connected their lips in a melodic kiss. Soomi melted under his touch, her tiny hands reaching up to grab his wrists that caressed her cheeks as they became one with each other. 

What was supposed to be a quick kiss to show her all would be well turned into something more, they were addicted. The addiction to each other's touch, feeling one another, overdid it and possessed them into only wanting more. After the hard last few days she'd had, Soomi wanted nothing more than just that - to feel him, to really feel him.

That was how it all started, after all, stress relief. And stress relief was exactly what she needed.

"OH MY GOD, KIM FUCKING SOOMI!"

The glare in Kim Jinhwan's eyes was about as red as it had ever been. It was insane that the tall boy hadn't been able to feel the lasers that were sent straight into his soul as he had ran full force at the two who had once been having a lovely moment. At the immediate sound of his scream, they'd disconnected from their kiss, but Soomi didn't have enough time to turn to see what was about to happen since she was the one with her back to him. 

Thus being said, she didn't expect to nearly be tackled - no matter how many times it had happened to her. That same squeal leaves her lips as she stumbles forward, and would have fallen if her beloved Jinhwan hadn't grabbed her first. 

"Junhoe?!" she gawks as she steadies herself, turning to see the boy even though she was still in Jinhwan's grasp from him having caught her. There's a goofy smile on his face, and Soomi could only inhale as she took in just where Jinhwan had been taking her all along. "I'm gonna fucking kill you, how many times have I told you not to do that?!" 

Jinhwan knew it was best to let her go when she bent down to take her shoe off. Before you knew it, the tree boy's life was in danger as he began running around the narrow hotel hallway, screeching like a three year old playing tag. 

"That's rude!" he's scoffing as he hid behind Jinhwan, a fake look of offense on his face, "not even a greeting after we haven't seen each other in so long?!"

Not wanting to maneuver around Nani, she narrows her eyes at him as she halts her movement. "Junhoe, it's been like three days."

"Uh, yeah, that's too long," he states in a sassy tone as if she should've known that already. 

Soomi rose a brow in question at his words, "careful, it's starting to sound like you care about someone's being other than your own."

Wrinkling his nose, their joking session never ended as he laughs dramatically, "disgusting, I would never, Koo Junhoe is the only important being on this Earth." Soomi couldn't help but giggle at the weird tone he tended to use when he was being an extreme idiot (which was almost always.) Jinhwan was only staring at his friend in annoyance as he stepped around him, and before anyone had any idea what was going on, he pulled her into a big, warm hug.

Shocked at first, it didn't take her long to accept his hug. It was weird to anyone else, but hugging Junhoe was actually becoming sort of normal to her. He was not someone who was touchy, and he never had been, but he seemed to always make an exception when it came to her. Soomi had seemed to change that trait in him when they were with each other, as all he ever seemed to do was annoy her by touch. 

"But I do care about you," he's muttering, just low enough so that Jinhwan didn't hear feet away, "you're my best friend, Soo, and I'm saying this quietly so that Jinhwan doesn't get jealous."

Soomi had started giggling like a schoolgirl, and it only got funnier when Jinhwan perked up from behind them, "what's that?"

Junhoe pulled away from the hug, sending his hyung a grin as he hums, "nothing, hyung." Cheerfully, he turns back to his best friend, wrapping an arm around her shoulder as he gushes, "let's get the fuck inside though, it's time to fucking party now that you're back," he fist pumps the air with the arm that wasn't around her shoulder. Soomi snorts in humor at his idiotic looking action as they walk towards the suite door of the hotel, "and I assume you come with great news, as Jinhwan told me."

The bright smile of Koo Junhoe was enough to help her begin to take her mind off of her worries. Soomi told herself that Jinhwan was right. They didn't know for sure, and she shouldn't immediately start jumping to assumptions and worrying over things that might not be true. This was supposed to be the best time of her life. After five years, she was reunited with her family, and they had finally all made amends with the horrific standards they had been forced to live under since birth. Her confirmed stalker was dead, and she had even come clean about the miscarriage she'd had with her exes baby. Everything was falling into place to her, but it was still a long road. She had yet to reach the end, but there was no need to rush.

She needed to stop and smell the flowers, focus on the positive's of what was finally happening to her all this time. She was home in New York City, and she got to go home to her family.

But really, she realized that it hadn't felt like home there, not anymore. 

As Junhoe swung open the door, and she was met with the other five grinning faces, she realized something else.

She'd been home all along. 

"Welcome back, Soomi!" Bobby had screamed at the top of his lungs, not giving a single shit if they got noise complaints from anyone else staying in the hotel. Their suite was huge, as it was a five star hotel in fucking New York, so the living room was the size of an apartment in Korea. The boys all had glasses in their hands, and music blasted as they closed the door behind the three who had entered.

"SOOMI!" Donghyuk and Yunhyeong had cheered in unison, both giving her side hugs. She was chuckling like a kid as she hugged them back, following her greeting everyone else.

"Where's the fucking alcohol?!" Junhoe whines, his eyes trailing around the room. They land on the kitchen island, where he excitedly walks to grab the wine glass they'd opened. Picking up two glasses as it was all he could carry, he walks over to hand one to those who didn't have any.

Like always, they assumed he one was for him and the other was for Jinhwan. Soomi never usually drank, she only had once on New Years with them and the Bangtan boys, but it was barely any. Everyone knew they should probably keep her away from alcohol because of her past.

However, he was aware of what had been going on because Jinhwan had filled the guys in on it. Something inside of him was telling him it was okay. Handing Jinhwan a glass, the boy watches as he pours wine inside of it, and didn't wait to take a sip. It'd also been a while since Jinhwan had gotten to drink, as he usually didn't get to anymore these days so he could watch over the others. But tonight was a special occasion. 

Then, the tall boy turned to Soomi, who had been laughing away with Hanbin and Bobby. She was leaning against the kitchen counter, with Jinhwan at her side, and Hanbin and Bobby feet away leaning against the island. Junhoe was to her other side, and she hadn't been looking at him. This is why she was shocked when he abruptly shoved a glass into her chest, urging for her to take it.

"Junhoe," she mumbles in surprise as she takes the glass so he didn't drop it. The boy only sends her a grin as he pours the alcohol inside the glass, before sending her a wink.

"It's a special day, Soo," he shrugs, "I think that calls for drinking."

She pursed her lips as she looked down at the substance. Jinhwan had yet to notice because he was now engrossed in a conversation with Double B. It came across her mind the lengths he previously went to last time just so she wouldn't drink. That was what made them go on that road trip after all, which had turned into one blissful disaster within itself, all because she couldn't ignore the temptation back then.

But was it really harmful to her if she drank this one night? She wasn't addicted anymore, and she didn't have a reason to be - not anymore.

So before the short boy turned and could notice, she looked to Junhoe, who was standing before her with his own glass now. He rose a brow in question to her, asking with his eyes if she was going to drink or not. Soomi curtly nods, and a grin spreads over her best friend's lips.

In a swift manner, they clink their glasses, and she raises it to her lips. It'd been too long for her, but here she was, letting go. 

But she wasn't going it to get rid of her pain. She wasn't doing it because she was hurting inside. She was letting go because she deserved to, because she wanted to celebrate all the good things in her life that were finally falling into place. 

Which is why when Jinhwan looked to her only to see her drinking wine casually, giggling away with Junhoe, he didn't get mad at all. He knew she was okay, he knew that she was strong enough. Soomi was facing her past now, and her drinking problem was just that - in the past. It was something she would have to face eventually, but as of now, she had overcome it. He wasn't worried about it at all.

Still, Soomi hadn't thought he would react in such a way. She jumped at the feeling of him wrapping an arm around her shoulders, pulling her into him as he sipped his own wine. The girl grinned when she looked up to him, only to see he was already smiling down at him. 

Bending his head back, Jinhwan downed the last drops of his wine. The glass being empty, he removed his arm from her to retrieve the bottle that still sat on the island.

Leaning against the counter, he was one big smiling idiot as he poured more in her glass that was nearly empty, too, before drinking straight out of the bottle. Soomi laughed in awe at his actions, shaking her head as she whines, "excuse me, it's not fair you get to have such a privilege!"

Raising a brow at her, he holds out the wine bottle to the girl, "hm, have at it then."

So she did. 

She drank her mind away, right along with the rest of the seven boys. They lived their lives not like they were celebrities who's every moment was watched, but for once, like they were normal kids, like this was all they had to do. It didn't matter to them that they had schedules in the morning.

Because they deserved this. 


93:00 | hangovers

"Soomi, I'd love to stay in bed, too, but we have a rehearsal to get to," Jinhwan sighs, tugging on her arm again for the 90th time. Everyone knew he could easily get her out of bed by actually using any amount of strength, but he didn't want to do that in chance it might end up with her hurt on the floor. So, he resulted to him gently pulling her arm every few seconds like a child, urging her to move a muscle so they could all get to work. "I know it's the first hangover you've had in like a year, but aren't you used to them?"

The girl groans from under the sheets of the king sized bed in the bedroom the other boys so kindly let them use. Jinhwan wasn't wrong, she was used to hangovers - just not anymore. 

"That was then," she cries in agony when he heaves out a deep breathe, before ripping the sheets from where she had been holding them over her face.
