Soomi tries to grab them back because the sunlight he had allowed to come through the opened curtains was making her brain ache more than it already was, but Jinhwan holds the sheets just out of her grasp. "I don't have that sort of superpower anymore," she pouts, instead covering her eyes with her hands since she sheets were no longer an option, "I don't drink often like you, you know, I haven't in a year."

He couldn't help but feel sympathy for her. Soomi drinking again like a normal adult was a huge step in a good direction. All the guys loved to drink, and it made them feel bad when she could never join them in the fun. The fact she seemed to be over what the past brought upon her was great.

But since she wasn't like that anymore, it also meant when she was hungover, it sucked bad. 

"I know," he hums in understanding, "I'll get you some medicine for it, but you know we can't miss the rehearsals."

A dull sigh left her lips as she finally sits up straight in the bed. Leaning back against the headboard, her eyes wander up and down Jinhwan's body. He was already dressed, as he had gotten up a long while before her. His skinny jeans ripped at the knees, he was sporting a coat that looked to cost way more than a coat should. Since it was rehearsals, he hadn't really styled his hair like recently, and just let the long caramel locks do their own thing. 

She wished she could find a word to explain the way it made her feel.

But like always, Soomi was at a loss for the weird feelings Jinhwan gave her. She'd never felt them to this extent, this heightened. It was like someone had taken everything she had known about the meaning of love, and times it by ten. It was impeccable, and she cleared her throat awkwardly, trying to rid herself of all the thoughts, and broke her stare that had been burning holes into that body of his.

"Oh, Soomi," Jinhwan chuckles, well aware of the moment she had been having. He'd seen it well, and because he knew her like that, he understood exactly what it was about. "Get dressed, okay? I'll go get you some Advil."

Soomi mumbled a nearly silent okay, as Jinhwan had walked over to the bedside. His lips lingered over her forehead as he kissed it softly, and he sent her that devious but irresistible grin, before heading out the bedroom door, closing it behind him.

She held a hand to her head after she was sure he was gone. What was up with her today? It was probably the hangover.

Reaching over to the bedside table, she grabbed her phone. She had a good amount of missed text messages, many from her old friends that were just boasting about how excited they were to see her perform today, some from her family checking in, but what really caught her eye, were the texts from the Bangtan boys. 

She felt sort of bad. Jungkook was always the one she envisioned going with her to see her family again. They talked about it so many times, and before, he'd even told her that he (or Jinhwan) would love to go with her. She knew then and she knew now, that even though he had included Jinhwan in that mix, he hadn't actually meant for her to take him. But she had, and she had a feeling that if Jungkook knew that, he'd be torn.

That seemed to be what worried her to speak to him since it happened. She'd been keeping in touch with the others off and on, but mainly Jimin. Since he was her other best friend, she updated him on what was going on when they got to the hotel yesterday. He was happy for her, more than happy, but she'd asked him to leave out the part to Jungkook about who she actually went to see her family with. As far as Jungkook knew, he assumed her older brother and Sunwoo had taken her, to which he was fine with. 

She didn't want him to find out otherwise. 

Why? She wasn't exactly sure. Lying had never been something she enjoyed doing. Soomi was going to have to make a decision soon, it was her next obstacle. With the fact they didn't know for sure if the partner of the stalker was out there or not, the fact she had reunited with her family and solved the life long issue they'd had, it left the main thing that had been following her for years in the open - ready to be solved.

Her choice.

A shaky exhale leaves her lips, and she responded to her texts before climbing off her bed. Who did she want? Wasn't it obvious, or was this some sort of sick illusion? There was a part of her that was sure she knew the answer, but another part of her was fighting for the exact opposite. What was it that she really needed? 

The worries were endless for her.

With everything that was already crowding her brain and the fact she had this terrible hangover, it sure didn't feel like Soomi's day. Falling to her knees, she pulls open her suitcase, digging inside for something to wear to rehearsals. She had no flying idea what the weather was, but being that it was New York, she couldn't really guess either. It was January, but it was cold or hot at the most random times in this state, she knew that well.

Not having any clue what she should choose, her movements of angrily digging halt when a certain blouse catches her eye. Her eyes widen slightly as she looks at it, and a chuckle leaves her lips as she slowly pulls it out of the suitcase. 

She didn't even remember bringing it, but here it was. The same blouse she wore that day on the rooftop, the day she kissed her best friend, but most importantly, the day she got engaged to who she thought was the love of her life. All the events that had happened then, they all seemed meant to be. 

Now she wasn't so sure anymore when she looked at it. Either way, something inside of her pushed her to slip the blouse on.

Before she knew it, she was dressed and ready. One look in the mirror, and it almost felt like it was back then, only a little over a year ago now. 

"Here you are," the sing-song of Jinhwan is heard as he pushes open the door with his shoulder. A bright smile remains on his lips as he holds out a glass of water to her and a couple of pills. 

"Thanks, Nani," she gushes, taking them and immediately chugging water along with the pills. A deep breathe of relief escapes her lips as she sets the glass down, before turning back to him with a thumbs up, "my migraine should be gone in no time!"

There's a glint in his eyes when she meets them. Before she could ask what was up, he slides across the carpet, reaching out and pulling her towards him. Soomi didn't complain as she snuggles into his chest, and his arms squeeze her as he mutters, "you look really gorgeous today, Soomi."

A tint of pink beats it's way onto her cheeks before she could stop it. "Shut up, don't we have a rehearsal to get to?"

Giggling at her embarrassed reaction that she still tended to have today whenever she was caught off guard, he pulls away and grabs her hand. Swinging it back and forth, Jinhwan drags her towards the bedroom door as he hums in reply, "Yup, we sure do."

with that, the two lovers head into the massive living room of the suite. The other six friends had been waiting there, lounging around on the couches and laughing about whatever idiotic thing they were talking about. At the sound of new footsteps, they all glance up, and brighten at the sight of their friends.

"Soomi, good morning," Donghyuk waves cheerily.

"Morning, Hyuk," she returns his soft angelic grin.

The others greet her, too, and just when she was thinking it was going to be a peaceful day - Junhoe appeared before her. "Jinhwan-hyung finally got your fat ass to get up, huh?"

There's an annoyed look on the eldest's face as he holds Soomi back from attacking the tall boy, but only so he could do it himself. "It's not the time, Junhoe," he scolds in disappointment, punching the boys arm just hard enough for him to wince.

"Fuck, okay," he curses in awe, walking over to fall onto one of the couches with the others.

Soomi was snickering at what happened, and her and Jinhwan walk to sit down, as well. Apparently they still had a while since the van would get here, which was actually probably just a few minutes. Either way, it gave them some time to socialize, and who could complain about that?

"Do you guys remember what happened last night?" Bobby had spoke up after a few seconds, "my head is pounding, so it makes me assume we must've had fun."

"Not a thing," the leader is the first to say, "but it's probably better that way, honestly."

"Yeah, true," he nods, "all I know is Soomi finally got drunk for the first time in forever."

"Yup, and now I'm feeling the wrath," she groans, leaning her head on Jinhwan's shoulder, who rose a brow down at her. "But like you said, whatever we did really must've been fun."

None of them knew if there was any truth to that statement. Like they said, no one remembered, not even Jinhwan. They'd all been intoxicated out of their minds to the point that they probably never would have any recollection. 

At least that's what they thought, but there was one member who hadn't been drunk enough last night to forget it all in the morning. In fact, his hangover wasn't even that bad. He had a good, vivid memory of what happened, of what he saw.

But he wished he didn't. And he'd make sure none of them ever found out. 


94:00 | jen

Jinani: come to my dressing room :(

Soomi: we're really going thru this again huh

Soomi: iTs DanGERous

Soomi: jk I'm coming :D

Jinani: did you just make fun of yourself?

Jinani: oh my soomi

Jinani: truly a comedian

Soomi: yes I did

Soomi: I know thank you

Soomi: maybe one day I'll be as funny as Junhoe

She couldn't help but giggle to herself at herself. Was it really wrong to find yourself funny sometimes, or did that just make you self-absorbed? Soomi was unsure, but she was feeling too great then to really mind.

Since they got to rehearsals, she'd had lots of laughs with the guys. It was nice to be able to openly hang around with them whenever she wanted without having to worry. Now they were free to be friends, and everyone was aware that they were. The only thing the public was a little short on was that she was more than friends with one member, but it wasn't like she was going to out that anytime soon.

Even though the harsh reality of her decision was weighing on her shoulders, she was trying not to let it bother her. There was lots of things regarding such on her mind, but she didn't have time to worry about it then. It was her time to be happy, she wanted to bask in that feeling before she focused on more worries.

So, with a deep exhale of motivation, she heads on her way to the dressing rooms. She'd already rehearsed her performance early, and the boys would be going up soon. They were just hanging out in their dressing room since then. 

"Good job today, Soomi, you'll be great tonight!" her manager had stopped her, patting her back with a bright smile.

"Thank you, manager-nim," she returns the grin, bowing kindly to him. He only sends her a wave as she heads off.

She still had to rehearse one more time, as her and the boys would be doing their songs together, too. But those had become a piece of cake, so it would be no big deal. The truth was, she was excited about tonight. For the first time in forever, she finally got to just enjoy doing her job, what she loved, and spending time with her fans. She didn't have the fear that some crazy woman was going to out all her secrets anymore, or try to kill her.

Humming a song to herself, everything seemed to leave her mind as she walks. With her hangover pretty much over since her migraine was gone, she easily ignored the slight feeling of fatigue that was also there. Even though she hadn't really been used to having them anymore, that didn't mean she didn't know how to handle them.

She was fine.

Only You was the tune she was currently singing to herself. It really was such a good song, and the story behind how it came to be was a funny one she would never get over. She was so thrilled that her family was going to get to see her perform it tonight, she was sure they would like it.

"Soomi!" a sudden call from somewhere behind her slapped her straight out of her thoughts, and she was left in a state of shock so much that she couldn't seem to make herself move any further, "Mi, wait!"

Grimacing, she tries to hide the despair that wiped over her face when the boy jogs to her, stopping before her. His brown eyes pour into hers, and she closes her own as she tries to collect herself and her emotions. It'd been a long time since she spoke to Jungkook, so long that she forgot everything she wanted to tell him. 

Mentally, she punches herself for the fact she forgot to talk to him with everything that was happening. How big of an idiot could she be?

Well, and she was also sort of avoiding speaking to him, but now she didn't have a choice.

"Kookie," she breathes, gathering the courage to open her eyes and meet his.

"Hey," he greets slowly, "how are you? How did it go?"

"Um," she blinks, trying to think of what she could tell him that would summarize it all. But there really wasn't any short sentence she could say that would give him the gist. With a sigh, she steps forward, grabbing his wrist, "come on, I'll tell you somewhere more private."




+




"Wow," he gawks at her, his eyes glistening under the lights of her dressing room, "Mi, that's great news, I'm so happy for you."

Sending him a gracious grin, she nods, "yeah, yeah, it is, I'm happy too. You can meet them later, they'll - uh - they'll be here."

Pursing his lips in thought for a split second, his eyes divert to the ground. Shoving his hands in his pockets, he hums, "alright, that sounds good."

"Yeah," she mumbles, before looking away, too. There was no denying they were being extremely awkward with each other. It wasn't even this awkward when she walked in and found him in her dressing room for the first time. It wasn't even this awkward when he walked in on her training at the company, when they met for the absolute first time ever. The point was, it had never been this awkward between them.

So why was it now? 

It was an obvious question. They were both thinking about the same thing.

"Mi," he sighs, knowing he couldn't hold it in anymore, "so, is it a safe time to ask?"

He didn't even have to finish the question because she already knew what he was referring to. "Uh, not really," she shakes her head, turning away from his gaze that was slicing her into pieces, "I haven't really gotten to think about it, you know."

Jungkook could tell she was shying away from him. His boots click against the floor as he maneuvers around the furniture, until he was standing right in front of her, making him the only place she could look.

Those fingertips of his are soft when they graze her cheek. His breathe fanned her skin as his eyes kept flickering from one place on her face to another, "Does that mean I can still freely kiss you, then?"

It was like every part of her body was fighting a different one, like she was in a war with herself. A part of her wanted it, wanted to kiss him so bad, but another part refused. The two parts were battling one another, and it left her mind in a daze of confusion on what she should do.

Jinhwan, one side seemed to scream at her, you only want to touch Jinhwan.

So why did her heart ache to feel Jungkook so badly?

Without another word, she gave in, cupping his cheek with one hand, she slammed her lips onto his own like it was the last time she ever would, because maybe it was. 

The boy wasn't caught off guard in the slightest, he knew that Soomi could never resist him. He'd missed her more than words could explain, and his friends were well aware of how miserable he'd been without her recently. But Jungkook was starting to come to terms, even if he wasn't sure yet.

Which is why he grabs her hips, his nails digging into them as he pulls her closer against his body. Their lips kiss each other hungrily, like they were wolves having their last meal for decades. It was clear, it was clear they both knew something they thought the other didn't.

The two were out of breathe when they parted. Soomi's hand fell to rest on his shoulder, and she uses the other to push her hair behind her ear that had fallen in her face. 

"I missed you," Jungkook mutters after a second of them just standing there in each other's embrace, breathing heavily, "I didn't expect you to reply to my text messages, I just felt like you needed to read them."

She purses her lips in a thin line, looking to the ground as she thought about what he just said. 

"What you said," she hesitates for a moment, "it meant a lot to me, it gave me the strength to get through it - I want you to know that - you've given me the strength to get through all of this."

Jungkook doesn't say anything. He just sends her a small smile, but she could see there was a burning sadness in those brown eyes of his. Even though he was trying to hide it, it was there, and it worried her what was going on in that mind of his.

But she was too scared to ask. He only wrapped his arms around her, pulling her into an actual hug, and all she knew to do was accept it.

"I love you, Soomi, always remember that."

He pulled away faster than she thought he would. Before she could process, he was leaving.

"Yah," she snaps to the boy, who was already standing at the door, "Jungkook."

Raising a brow, he slowly turns around to look at her again. His hand was gripping the door handle, and he let out a dull sigh, "you're just as beautiful as you were that night, Mi." His brown irises raked over the blouse she was wearing, the familiar blouse, one last time. 

It took her a second to realize what he was talking about, as she forgot her choice of apparel. As soon as she did, she called out his name again - just to see he was already gone.

A shaky sigh leaves her mouth as she holds a hand to her head, only staring at the door he had just walked out of. It had been so unlike Jungkook but so like him at the same time. What was up with that? Why did he look so sad? Had Jimin told him about the fact Jinhwan went with her to see her family? She knew she sure hadn't.

Or had he just guessed it? It wasn't like Jungkook was stupid, but she also hadn't thought she made it obvious.

Feeling more conflicted and confused than ever, her lips tingled when she touched them with her fingertips. The thought of how they'd kissed only moments ago dangled around her mind, and she shook her head in despair as she turned towards the large mirror that was on the wall of her dressing room.

"Who is it, Solar?" she asked herself, but her eyes just stared blankly back at her. It was like she thought her heart would burst out of her chest and say the name for her. 

But the only one who could answer that question was her.

Not wanting to think about what just happened anymore, her phone buzzed in her back pocket. Rubbing her temple with one hand, she pulls it out with the other. 

Jinani: are you ok? it's been a while

Soomi: yeah sorry something came up, tell you in a second

No more secrets was she going to keep from him. She'd told herself when she left her family's house that this was a new beginning, which meant no lying. It also meant she had to sort out all the confusion going on inside her head.

Feeling more stressed than she planned to feel that day, Soomi holds a hand to her head as she shoves her phone back in her pocket. Taking one last look at herself in the mirror, her reflection stares back at her. Although she felt so lost in that moment, when she met her own green eyes, she noticed something.

The girl staring back at her didn't look very lost at all.

Not knowing what was going on inside her own head was enough to make her want to scream. All she knew to do was make her way to the only place she could seem to solve it - Jinhwan.

The door creaks as she opens it. A heavy sigh leaves her lips as she thought about it again. Why had Jungkook been acting so weird?

She didn't want to believe it, but there was only one thing that came to mind.

Mi: Chim, did you tell Jungkook?

Chimmie: Tell Jungkook what?

Mi: idiot, don't play stupid

Mi: we both know what I'm talking about sir

Chimmie: soomi

Chimmie: I'm trying to help you

Mi: Jimin, this isn't helping! Have you seen Jungkook? He seems destroyed

Mi: when he found out that Jinhwan came with me, I wanted it to be from me, now I just look like a horrible person for keeping it from him

Chimmie: he understands, Mi, did he not just come talk to you?

Mi: well yeah, he asked me about my decision and then he kissed me and told me to always remember he loved me. But he looked absolutely destroyed and wouldn't listen to a thing I had to say

Mi: hence why I figured you must of told him

Chimmie: exactly

Mi: excuse me? what do you mean exactly? I don't want Jungkook to be upset over this, it was a heat of the moment decision!

Chimmie: oh, mi, you're so naive sometimes

Mi: HOW?!

Chimmie: gtg, rehearsals Mi

Mi: Park Jimin, I'm going to kill you

No reply.

She waited in the hallway of the dressing rooms at the KCON arena, staring down at her lit phone screen like a complete idiot. Soomi had no idea how long had passed, when she finally groaned, turning off the screen and shoving it in her pocket before she threw it on the ground from frustration. What the hell was going on today - not just with her, but everyone?!

The brunette had yet to look up when she took a step forward, figuring Jinhwan was probably rather confused as to where she was. Just as she does so, her body appears to collide with someone else's, causing her to squeak as she steps back, steadying herself.

"Sorry!" she's crying quickly, "I'm out of it today."

"Oh, Soomi!" the girl had screeched.

Her green eyes bulge, and she only then looked up. "Jennie?!" she gawks in response, "oh my God, hi!" 

The girl giggles as she steps forward, and the two embrace in a tight hug. Jennie was squeezing the literal life out of Soomi, but she didn't mind, for the first time. Honestly, a hug from a close friend was something she needed right now.

"I didn't know you were gonna be at KCON," Soomi is saying in confusion as they pull away, "you guys weren't in the meeting."

"Yeah, it was kind of a last minute decision, we just got here for Day 2," she explains, "I didn't plan to come either, but here I am! New York sure is pretty, you're lucky to be from here."

Soomi nods, pursing her lips as she thought about just that - how she was from here. "I suppose so," she shrugs.

"What's up with you, though?" she asks immediately after, reaching out and gently punching her shoulder, "you said you were out of it, why's that?" Before she could even begin to answer, Jennie had exhaled deeply. She puts her finger on her chin, narrowing her eyes at the girl before her as she seemed to be thinking intently, "Hm, you look different, but I can't tell why."

Sighing, Soomi could only shake her head, trying not to overthink about that last comment, "it's a long story, Jen."

"Aw," she pouts, "well you're gonna have to tell me, when we get back to Korea, girl's night, okay?"

"Yeah," she instantly agrees, realizing that sounded very overdue, "yeah, that sounds great."

"Alright, well our rehearsals are coming up, the other girls are going to be jealous they didn't get to see you - but maybe later!"

Soomi opens her mouth to speak, about to bid goodbye to her friend, when a voice from behind her cuts her off from doing so. 

"Soo?"

There's a devious look on Jennie's face when the boy appears besides her. As soon as he stood before them, the member of BLACKPINK bows, offering a small smile. 

"Jinhwan?" she breathes, feeling her heartbeat accelerate from the sight of him, "Sorry, I got sort of caught up."

Chuckling, he didn't look the slightest of surprised. "I figured," he turns to Jennie to offer a wave of his own, "hi, nice to see you."

She returns the kind words with a muttered you too, but there was clearly something else on her mind. Those brown eyes of hers kept flashing between the two, and she finally blurts the inevitable, "huh, I guess Jisoo was right."

Soomi wasn't the only one confused. "What?"

Giggling, she holds a hand over her mouth to stifle her hysterics. "No, no, it's just - Lisa was convinced you and Junhoe had a thing, but Jisoo swore it was you and Jinhwan, cause you know, you guys made the song together. They made this bet, and it's really funny, I didn't think it was either, but here you are, right before me," she holds a hand to her head, having had barely got the words out because of her own laughter. The two before her are only gawking at her, and she lets out a deep breathe, "Whew, you've both got literal heart eyes, it's cute. I've never seen you so whipped over anyone in my life, even when you were with Jungkook back then," she shook her head with another light chuckle. 

Neither of them had a chance to speak, or maybe it was the fact they were at a complete loss for words.

"Alright, I need to get to rehearsals, but you take care of her, 'kay?" she points a finger at Jinhwan, "you're a lucky fucker."

"Yeah, I sure am."

"Hey, but," she stops herself as she had already been a few steps away, "you guys be careful, alright? The rules in this industry are harsh, I don't want you guys to be deer caught in headlights - so in other words, watch out, alright?" 


95:00 | shadow

"Stop laughing!" Soomi is complaining in frustration, but the caramel boy only continued to giggle away like a child behind her. At the sound of him clearly not stopping his idiocy, she groans, stomping her feet against the concrete floor as she bitterly walks into the iKON dressing room.

"Sorry, I just never expected that!" he's crying in defense behind her, shutting the door before he follows her into the main part of the room.

The other boys had glanced up to the sound of them bickering at each other - or more like Soomi just bickering at the eldest. A few exchange looks, telepathically asking each other if they should speak up or not. 

They'd seemed to all agree not to. That was, except for one particular soul, which was why no one was surprised when he blurts, "the fuck is wrong with you two baboons?"

Flipping her brown hair over her shoulder, she had just about to of sat next to Yunhyeong. However, hearing her best friend utter such an insult caused her to stop dead in her tracks. A humorous look is wiped on the tree's face, but Soomi was very far from amused. "Baboon, seriously, a fucking baboon, Junhoe?!"

"Calm down, grandma, it was just a joke," he mutters in dismay so she would stop glaring at him.

But he should've known - well, he did know - not to say those words. Clenching her fists at her sides, everyone was feeling a little afraid of Soomi as she practically screeches, "I'm younger than you!"

Junhoe bursts into laughter at her loud response. He had no idea why she was getting so easily upset, which brought him back to what he asked about in the first place. But he didn't get a chance to ask about it again because someone beat him to it. Donghyuk's eyes were kind as they peer at his close friend, and in worry that she might beat up Junhoe, he reaches out a hand to assuringly grab her hand, "you're different today, Soomi, what's up with you and hyung?"

Jinhwan opened his mouth to speak, but Soomi beat him to it. "Nothing, we're the same," she shrugs in a rush, falling onto the couch next to Hyuk and Yun, "actually, he's just being annoying."

"I am not," Jinhwan chuckles for the 800th time that day, turning to his friends, "she's having mood swings or something because her friend Jennie thinks we're dating."

"I mean, that's not really a far-fetched assumption," Junhoe spoke up, "you literally live in each other's asses."

Tired of his shit even though it had been less than five minutes, Soomi points an incriminating finger at him from across the room, "you're really fucking testing me, you tree."

"Well, are you?" Yunhyeong asked the question everyone was wondering, "You two have been very different since you got back from Soomi's house."

For the first time that day, the bright look was wiped off Jinhwan's face. It was replaced with a rather conflicted one, looking more lost than anything, as he helplessly looks to Soomi. She looked distraught, as her green eyes nervously stared down at her feet. Noticing she didn't appear to want to say anything, he clears his throat, "Not exactly," he hesitated. When he looked away, he felt Soomi's eyes burning holes into him, "Like she said, we're the same."

Everyone in the room was looking at them in ware. No one believed the words that just came out of Jinhwan's mouth. They knew how horribly in love their poor hyung was, but they didn't know about Soomi. It was clear she loved him, but she had always been so conflicted with Jungkook, and now that other boy that was in her life. But yet, she seemed odd.

Soomi was glad when one of the other's brought up something random, changing the subject. Rubbing her temple with one hand, she pulls out her phone with the other. Her heart seemed to be speaking for her, as she didn't even realize what she was doing until she had already sent the text message.

Solar: meet me at the castle park later, 11 pm




+





That same feeling was a feeling you could never get tired of. Just hearing the screams, the cheers, and knowing they were meant for you. The sound of everyone cheering her name, was enough to make her go on. Even though there was sweat dripping like waterfalls down the sides of her face, even though her body was crying because of fatigue, she couldn't be happier. Her stages at KCON had finally ended, but she finally could say she ended them with great memories. In fact, she couldn't be more thrilled that she had gotten on that plane to NYC.

Her makeup artists and other stylists had ran to her side, using pads to dab the sweat off her face and fix her makeup for her. She thanks them kindly, and they'd only replied with telling her she did a good job. Soomi was happy to hear it as she breathes heavily, still trying to calm herself down from the adrenaline train she had been on from how great the stage had been. 

No words could explain how much she adored Only You. Every single time she performed it with Jinhwan, it only got better. Today, that performance had felt different than ever. She felt so alive, so free, and the experience of being on stage only got more amazing when she got to experience it with someone she loved.

"Oh, my talented daughter!" the squeals of Solji were ear piercing. Even though there was a lot of commotion backstage with everyone trying to be in the right spots, her mother never spared to be the loudest presence. With her bright smile, and illuminating aura in general, she was an eye-catcher. Her light brown hair falls in waves down her back as she rushes to her daughter, her arms wide open as she engulfs her in a massive hug, "I am so proud of you!"

"Mother," Soomi gasps from the fact she was genuinely squeezing the life out of her. Her mother was a lot stronger than she looked, and it felt like her lungs were about to pop. Still, Soomi found a way to get over it as she accepts the hug, "thank you, mother."

The rest of her family appear, walking up from where they had been watching her stage. Although she was previously nervous, when she pulled away from her mother's hug only to see their ecstatic expressions, it all became better. 

"You're godly, Sol," Chanhee had gushed as he hugged her too, followed by her other old friends.

"I never would have guessed this is where you'd end up," her father had cleared his throat, looking a bit indifferent about it all. His eyes wandered around the backstage, looking from person to person, trying to take it all in. Finally, he sighs, "but everyone out there, those thousands of people, they love you. You've really become someone, you've created a legacy, all by yourself."

Soomi swallows a lump in her throat as she met his eyes, shock written well in her own. "And that," he sighs, "is why you're my daughter."

Gawking up at her father, she tried to keep herself from crying. The father that never complimented her a day in her life just said that to her, the father she always worked so hard to please but never could seem to. He was finally proud, proud to call her his own. And it hadn't required her to take over the family business, to go to a fancy college.

"Thanks, father," she smiles from ear to ear.

For the next while, Soomi and her family and friends engage in many conversations. Since her parents knew all about her life now, she went on to explain how she got started, how she stayed with her aunt, and trained for years. As she explained, they only became more proud of how she got to where she was now. 

She wasn't aware where Jinhwan was, but she figured he was busy with the others. He had gone off the opposite way when their stage ended, so he was probably in the dressing rooms. She didn't think much of it.

Though, being away from him for such a short amount of time, she already missed him.

Either way, she wouldn't let herself worry about him right now. She wouldn't let herself worry about any of her guy problems. After all, she was going to have to face one later today. Right now was the time for her to spend with her family, her childhood friends. It was time for her to begin to make up all the moments she had missed the last five years of her life, starting now. 

"Can you teach me how to dance?!" Yein had been whining as they're all hanging around the backstage, tugging her sister's hand.

Solar had been in the middle of talking to Juyeon, who she learned had always wanted to pursue music like her. She never would have guessed it was her brother's dream, but she realized it didn't seem unlike him either.

"Sure, Yein," she chuckles, finally replying to her baby sister. Kneeling down so she can look at her, she takes her hand as she raises a brow, "what do you want to learn, hm?"

"The dance you just did!" she cries instantly, "with Jinawn, please, I want to learn that -," Yein suddenly cut herself off. Her green eyes grow confused, and she turns to look around the backstage area, before looking back to her sister blankly, "sissy, where is Jinawn?"

Pursing her lips, she glances around, too, expecting him to randomly appear like he did earlier. Briefly, she checks her phone, too, but there wasn't a sign of any text messages. "I don't know," she shrugs to her sister, "probably with his friends in his dressing room."

Yein pouts, "can we go see him? I miss Jinawn!" she whines. Before Solar could say a word, she was already pulling at her wrist, urging her to take her to wherever her 'boyfriend' was located at.

But Soomi was rather comfortable where she was sitting now on the floor. She groans as Yein continued to tug at her arm, "Yein, I'll take you in a bit, let's just watch the show for now, okay?"

Though she still looked sad about it, she sighs, "okay, fine!"

As if on queue, the intercom had blasted loudly from the stage, "KCON fans everywhere, please welcome our next performer - a special stage by the one and only - BTS Jungkook!"

The screams were so loud in the arena it hurt everyone's ears backstage. Some of her family members plugged their ears from the screams, but they didn't think much of the performer that walked out on stage all alone. Soomi's eyes were falling out of their sockets, meanwhile, and the only one's who had any idea were her close friends and Chaerin.

"Oop," Changmin had mumbled when her childhood friends walked to stand beside her as she rose to her feet, walking closer to get a better look at the stage, "it's guy problem number two."

Jungkook was carrying a guitar, something he could play rather well but didn't tend to anymore. In fact, Soomi used to always make him play it for her back then. Now that she thought about it, it'd been a good few years since she saw him play it.

He'd cleared his throat as she sat on a stool before the mic. At the sight of him even breathing, the arena erupted into more screeches and cheers. Jungkook had chuckled nervously, doing that thing he did were he bit his lip and diverted his eyes to his feet for a mere second, smiling all the while. 

She couldn't say it still didn't do something to her when he did that.

"I wrote a song recently about someone special to me," he finally speaks into the mic, "I wanted you all to hear it."

A song, her mind yells, he wrote a song.

Immediately, her brain wanders back to the FaceTime call that morning her and Jinhwan went on the road trip. He wouldn't let her hear the song yet, saying it wasn't done. But he said she'd get to hear it someday - this was what he meant.

It was meant for her.

Her mouth was hanging open as if she was a hippo. Putting together the pieces, her friends glance to her with wide eyes. 

"Solar, please don't tell me the song's about you," Chaerin had spoke from behind her. As soon as she saw the worried look her big sister sent her, her fear was confirmed. "Holy fucking shit."

She wanted to scream, she wanted to walk away from this entire situation. "Calm down, Sol, you haven't even heard the song yet," Kevin had tried to make her feel better. All of her friends could tell she was about to have a heart attack as the boy on stage began to softly strum those guitar strings. "Although he does look rather heart broken, did you tell him you chose Jinhwan?"

"Ow!" She didn't even get to say anything in response to that horrible question he asked when Chanhee had harshly elbowed him, "Sorry."

Your light stains me with a shadow

Even when the sun goes down and this place becomes blurred,

Wherever you are, always go ahead

Your light illuminates my surroundings 

You're the light that brightens this world

She holds a hand to her head to ease the bundling emotions inside of her. As much as Soomi wanted to believe this was some sort of dream, it wasn't. Her ex-fiance, one of the boys she had been so torn between for this long, was singing a song to her out there, in front of millions of people and her family and friends. 

Yeah, I'm a shadow

I'll become deeper in the darkness

You just smile brightly like always and shine

Although my darkness can't reach your light

Nobody else can replace you

"Wow, this isn't even about me and I think I'm gonna cry," Changmin is muttering, wiping under his eyes. It was clear that the orange haired boy of sunshine was trying to lighten the mood. Solar appreciated his attempt, but nothing could take her eyes off that brunette boy on stage.

Except for one thought - Jinhwan, where was he? She winced at the thought that he was hearing the words to this song. She still had yet to see him, she had yet to talk to him like she had been meaning to, and now this was happening.

To think she still had to go to the castle park tonight.

I'm just a shadow, I'm getting bigger

More and more because of you

Pretty things can't be hidden

We don't talk, but that's okay

I'll just stand by

And protect you

I'm always by your side

Even if you don't see me in the shade

Even when the sun goes down, you can't stop me

Shadow is dyed by your light

It'll always be you

"Sol, are you okay?" she hears her sister ask her worriedly, but she couldn't make her mouth move to utter a reply.

Even if I don't have you, I'm behind your shadow

I just want to protect you

The song ended with a few final strums against his guitar. Jungkook offered a thank you into the microphone to the thousands and thousands of screaming fans for him. No doubt, everyone was deeply wondering about who such a meaningful song could be meant for. But any fan that knew of the history well, had a good idea.

How would you not know? Soomi knew it well as she could only gawk as he began to walk backstage. The staff took his guitar from him, stylists wiping off his sweat and fixing his dampened hair. Even though so many people had been cheering for him, he still looked empty.

Until his eyes met hers.

"No," she's muttering to herself in absolute trauma. Before she could process what she was doing, Jungkook was walking right towards her. Her friends call out to her, trying to ask her if she was okay, but she couldn't hear them. All her mind could think about what the words he just sung to her, the fact Jungkook seemed to know, the fact she hurt someone she cared about. 

She felt selfish, she felt like some sort of terrible person. 

"Soomi!" his voice yells out to her, but it was too late. 

Jungkook jogs a bit, trying to catch her, but she was faster on her feet than he'd ever seen her. Reaching out, his hand just barely manages to graze her own as she turned away, making an immediate break for anywhere else that wasn't here. Her shoes click against the tiled floors, and she eagerly pushes past anyone who stood in her way. Soomi couldn't be here, she needed to breathe, she needed fresh air.

So she headed outside, through that same exit she went through only days ago when she was searching for the woman who had been ruining her life. Back then, all she wanted to do was find her, it was all she thought about. Soomi blamed that woman, iKON's manager, for endlessly trashing everything.

But as the cold January air slaps her in the face, and she hurriedly wipes her cheeks with the back of her hands from the unknown fact she'd been crying, she realized one thing.

The only one who had trashed her life was herself.


96:00 | jungkook or jinhwan?

"Soomi, Soomi, can we please talk?" 

Jungkook closed the door behind him, and his shoes tap against the stairs as he heads down them in a rush. The slight breeze blew through his messy brown hair, but those irises of his shine as he makes his way in concern towards the girl. 

"Mi, don't cry," he coos out to her, frowning from the sight of her so destroyed. It was the last thing he intended to happen today, "talk to me."

When she doesn't say a word for a while, all he knew to do was comfort her. Heaving out a deep sigh, he steps forward to enclose any space that was between them. His toned arms wrap around her, pulling her into him without another word. Soomi's still, like a statue, for a while, before she sniffles, hesitantly laying her head against her chest.

"Sh, Mi, there's no need to cry, okay?" he whispers, rubbing her back as she weeps like a child into his shirt.

He was trying to make it better, but he didn't know how to anymore. Jungkook didn't even fully understand why she was crying. He thought he was doing the right thing, so why was it backfiring?

"I-I'm sorry," she finally blurts, balling his t-shirt in her fists, "I'm so sorry-"

"- It's okay, Mi," he cuts her off, and upon hearing these words, she slowly breaks away from the hug. Her glassy green eyes meet his, searching them for any sign that he was lying. "I know, Soomi, I knew it even before Jimin told me he went with you to see your family."

There were many things she wanted to say to this, but the first one that came out was the word, "H-How?"

Running a hand through his hair, Jungkook dully chuckles as he breaks their eye contact. "I've known you for a while, Soomi, maybe not as long as your friends in there, but to me - those five years were a long time," he begins, but Soomi only blinked at him in confusion as to where he was doing with this speech. Still, he continued with a sigh, "I've seen you in all your emotions before, sad, angry, surprised, and I thought happy," he pauses for a second, closing his eyes to try to suppress any tears, "but - I've never seen you as happy as you are when you're with him, Mi. Your eyes, they do this thing, where they're so bright, they illuminate the room when you look at him. And your smile, you smile like you've never smiled before, not even the day I asked you to marry me."

"Jungkook, that's not true-"

"- It is, Mi, I knew it long before this. But I love you, I love you so much that I just wanted to have more time with you, keep you believing that there was still a decision to be made."

Not being able to grasp anything he was saying, she called again, "Jungkook!"

But he wouldn't let her say a word. "But from the beginning, there was no decision to be made. You love him, unlike you've ever loved anyone before - you just have to admit that to yourself. I saw it from the first day he walked in on us kissing in your dressing room."

When he stopped talking this time, she didn't instantly try to speak up. No, Soomi was at a complete loss for words. Her mouth hangs open as she tried to think, tried to sort what was going on in her mind, but she couldn't deny that there was truth to everything that just came out of Jungkook's mouth.

She had been trying to grasp the past because she was scared. This entire time she had been fighting her mind to make a decision about the boys in her life, but instead, she should've just stopped for a second and listened to her heart.

When it hits her, she couldn't help but cry. This time, she doesn't sob, though - the tears are silent as they stream down her cheeks, and she breathes deeply as she tries to steady everything going on inside of her. "I'm still sorry," she shakes her head, "I'm sorry I hurt you."

"Don't be sorry, Soomi," Jungkook lightly chuckles, "I deserved this when I thought it was a good idea to break your heart."

Shocked at the fact he just said that, an offended look takes over her face as she snaps, "but that wasn't your fault-"

"- It was, Mi, I could've handled it better," he cuts her off sternly, "we've went over this."

Finally, she looks up to him fully. There's angry, hot tears that stream like a waterfall down her face as she refuses, "but you don't deserve to hurt, no one does, Jungkook. You're a good guy, that song proved that."

Silence.

Until Jungkook exhales, stepping forward to stand before her again. The concrete cracks under his feet, and he slowly reaches out to grab her hand. His own brown eyes are teary as they look into hers, and he reaches his other hand up to graze her cheek with his fingertips. They're soft, his touch was always soft, and it only made it hurt that much more.

"That song," he mumbles out, taking another deep breathe, "was me letting you know it's okay to let go now."

Soomi could only shake her head, unable to fight the guilt she felt.

"It's okay, I've accepted this," he assures her, tracing his thumb along her soft skin for the last time, "I know that you're scared to face what you feel, I know that you hate to hurt people you care about, but you shouldn't be - because I'm happy if you're happy, Soomi. It's all I've ever wanted you to be."

A sigh breaks through her lips as she hears his speak these words. Maybe they were what she needed to hear. Here he was, her ex fiance that had to be one of the most competitive, jealous, scheming, men she had ever met, telling her to follow her heart - telling her to go for the other boy because he knew she loved him. 

And he was right.

"A part of me will always love you, Jungkook," she still felt the need to let him know, "you'll always be special to me, don't forget that."

"I know, Mi," he nods, a melancholic smile residing on his lips, "and you know I'll always love you. I'm here if you need me, okay?"

She knew it would be the last time they could be like this. Soomi knew what she had to do, she knew what she should've already done. The obstacles that kept her afraid, it was time for her to finally run over not one, but all of them. She didn't have many left now after this. But this would be the last time she'd feel Jungkook's touch for a long time. Because of that, she wanted to cherish it, even if it wasn't for long.

Closing her eyes, she rests her head against his hand that was on her cheek. The boy found a way to smile when she did this, taking in the moment while he still could.

Jungkook was torn, destroyed, broken, he was many things that he would probably never fully recover from. But that was it, Jungkook was everything he had once made Soomi feel. He was everything she once felt like because of him, everything she would've never have gotten over - if it wasn't for Jinhwan.

Whatever the reasons may of been, for that, he felt like he deserved her. He admitted defeat in the war that was never really a war to him, as he'd known it from the beginning. They were meant for each other.

He accepted that.

But for one last time, Jungkook wanted to feel her. He wanted to be able to hold her as if he had won this 'war,' as if he was ever the ultimate choice in her mind. Even if it was for a second, he wanted to feel like that. 

So he wraps his arms around her a second time. He didn't have to pull her, she accepted his hug immediately. Soomi let out a shaky breathe as she leaned her head against his shoulder, just taking in the scent of that cologne. She was sad about this, she was sad that she knew he was hurt because of her. But she knew this had to happen, as afraid as she was, she couldn't postpone it forever.

"Jinhwan's stage should be over by now," he whispers, his lips just above her ear as his chin was gently atop her head. 

She pursed her lips upon hearing him say this. Jinhwan, she forgot he still had to do the main stage with iKON, that was where he had been all this time, preparing for it. 

"Are you gonna be okay?" she felt the need to ask, even though she probably knew the answer.

She also knew Jungkook probably wasn't going to tell her the truth. "Yeah, Mi, I'll be okay."

Soomi only sighs, not knowing what else to say. She still had a long day ahead of her, as she sat there in his arms. Jungkook really had been her happiness at one point in time. He really had saved her from the horror she felt, how alone she felt when she came to Korea all alone, pursuing what seemed like a silly dream to everyone else. From the first day at Big Hit, he believed in her. Through out the time they trained together, he taught her how to have confidence in who she was. That was how he saved her, and it was what made her love him.

But perhaps the love she had for him, was a different type of love she didn't understand. She adored Jungkook because he pulled her out of a dark place, he was a reliable friend to her whenever she needed one. However, he'd destroyed her at the same time, he ruined her, trashed everything she once thought she knew. For a year, she was left without understanding, not knowing what she did wrong. She was left all alone as her life seemed to crumble before her.

Then she met Jinhwan. From the first time they made eye contact in that elevator, all he ever did was help her. Even though they had fought before, he always saw his wrongs, he admitted to them, and he grew into a better person. Never had he done her wrong, never had he made her feel less, all he'd ever done was praise her, make her feel like she belonged here - like she belonged with him.

Which was it, that was the only place she ever felt she belonged. When she was with him, everything felt right, like the world had finally fallen into place. Although she found happiness in Jungkook, she found love in Jinhwan, she found euphoria, she found who she was meant to be with.

Despite everything wrong she had done in her life, Jinhwan loved her for it all. He didn't get mad if she did something, he accepted it, he saw her side, and he helped her through it. He was selfless but selfish when he needed to be. No matter what, he always seemed to say the right thing. He'd found out all of her darkest secrets that she was always so afraid to tell a soul because the fear of being judged, but they only made him love her more, not the opposite.

"Soomi?" her heart explodes with joy at the sound of such a voice.

Jungkook dully watches the way he explained, the way her green eyes would brighten. Slowly, he let her leave his arms, he let her go to him, even as much as it hurt him to do so.

"Jinhwan," she gasps out, as if she had just come up out of the water for air. Her feet slam against the concrete, bringing her right to him. The caramel boy is shocked when she wraps her arms around his torso, burying her face in his chest and clinging onto him for dear life. He'd never felt her hug him so tightly, so meaningful, and it caused him to send a confused look Jungkook's way, trying to gather what was going on.

He was still a sweaty mess from his stage, but even then Soomi didn't care. The feeling of him enclosing his arms around her, squeezing her gently back as a sign of affection, made her want to scream - not out of anger, but just because she was so happy. She was absolutely ecstatic whenever she looked at him, whenever she saw him, and she finally wasn't afraid to say that.

Jungkook's doe eyes meet his, and he only sent him a close-lipped, bitter smile. The sight only had Jinhwan feeling more lost. What was up with that? Jungkook hated Jinhwan, so why would he ever look at him like that - like he lost?

With that, Jungkook bowed politely, before spinning on his heel, and walking away. Jinhwan watched him leave with the most blank look on his face. He hadn't heard the song Jungkook wrote because he'd been preparing for his own stage and getting ready in the guys' dressing room. So, he was completely clueless as to what could be going on that day.

Still beyond confused and curious of the answer, he looks down to the girl that was squeezing him to death. "Soo, I was looking for you, you missed my stage," he hums, running his fingers through her hair as she only continued to further snuggle herself into his chest, "I tried extra hard for you, too, I even did those adlibs you always complain about me skipping," he giggles lightly, referring to how she always bothered him about such, "but it's okay, I can see that you must've been busy. Do you want to talk about it?"

There it was, one of the reasons she adored him so dearly, in a different way than she saw Jungkook or Sunwoo. A way she could only explain with three words, three words she had never meant so intensely. 

"Nani," she's breathless as she pulls away from the hug. Jinhwan looks down at her with curiosity, and his eyes widen when he sees the tears in her eyes. His hands reach up, and he uses his thumbs as he cups her cheeks to wipe them away. Before he opened his mouth to speak, most likely about why she would be crying and what was going on, she blurts out, "I love you."

Since it was something they were used to saying to each other, he didn't understand. "I love you, Soomi, but why are you crying?" he pouts, continuing to wipe the tears away.

She laughs, she laughs loudly and obnoxiously, probably loud enough for people inside to hear. Through her tears, she laughed like she never had before, like this was the funniest thing ever. "I'm crying because I'm happy," she admits, placing her hands atop his that were still cupping her cheeks, "I'm happy just being right here, with you, happier than I've ever been in my life."

Jinhwan was peering down at her like she was crazy, but it was something he loved about her. He grins like a kid, nodding slowly as he tries to understand what she was getting at. Knowing he still wasn't aware of what she meant, she continues to giggle away. 

"You're cute," he states, "and I'm happy that you're happy, but what was with Jungkook looking like a zombie?"

"Nani," Soomi sighs dramatically, poking his nose with her pointer finger and shaking her head, "you still don't get it, do you?"

Raising a brow, he shrugs, "Soo, I'm not sure what it is I'm supposed to be getting."

"You," she practically yells in exasperation. When he only continues to stare down at her in contemplation, she chuckles, lowering her voice to a mere whisper as she informs breathlessly, "I choose you."


97:00 | a new friend

Jinhwan and Soomi couldn't exactly cling onto each other the way they wanted to since they were still backstage at KCON. Despite this, it was still obvious the brunette was a jumpy, giddy mess as she practically ran back inside with him behind her. Jinhwan kept giggling away at her cute actions, clearly thrilled himself with what just happened.

It was over, he still couldn't comprehend it, Soomi was his. After all this time, he got to be with the girl he loved. He'd never felt such an explosion of emotions in his life. His veins were pumping faster than ever, with only endless adoration for her, his girlfriend. Could he really call her that now? It didn't seem real.

In fact, he was so happy, he didn't even think about what Soomi was going to do about her other ex boyfriend. Little did he know, she was going to see him later.

There's plenty of chatter going on in the backstage area. Now that everyone was done with their stages, they could check in with their staff and head back to wherever they wanted. Soomi jogs to where her family and old friends were, only to see the other boys of iKON were there too.

As soon as she joins the group, Chanhee's eyes immediately fall on her. Stepping closer to her, he crosses his arms over his chest as he sighs, "Solar, dear, your new best friend is making my brain bleed."

Raising a brow at him, she really didn't expect anything less. "I'm seriously offended you replaced me with that," Hee goes on with a disappointed sigh, "it's not even funny, how would that ever assist you with anything?" he motions to Junhoe, who was currently dancing around with Yein. The girl was in fits of laughter as she watched him, who was waving his arms around in a wiggly manner.

Sighing, she holds a hand to her head. This was going to happen sooner or later. "Hee, we already went over this," she shakes her head, "also, he's not even who I went to for advice and stuff, you haven't met Jimin."

"Jimin?" he hums, "where might I find him?" he turns to look around at the crowds of people, expecting this Jimin to pop out of nowhere.

Solar groans, tired of her bestest friend doing things like this. He had always done this, from her friends to her boyfriends. Although he was just looking out for her in a way, he was also the definition of petty.

"Are you bothering Sol again about her newer friends?" Jacob sighs as he walks up to the two, followed by Kevin and Changmin.

"dude, get over it that you're not the only best friend she has," Changmin pouts, "she's always had us too, you know!"

Hee rolls his eyes, "sure, but you've never been as high on the spectrum as me."

Seeing the offended look that takes over their faces, Solar clears her throat, knowing she should interfere now. "Can you guys cut it out? This is idiotic," she exhales deeply, "we aren't ten anymore."

Figuring it was over, she was glad she could finally tell them what happened. Before she even got a chance to open her mouth, giddily about to share with her friends that she was now taken, Kevin had practically screamed so high-pitched it made all of their ears ache. The four turn to him in awe and frustration, about to ask what his problem was, but he beat them to it with the explanation, "Holy shit, look!"

Just hearing him say that, they all knew what it was. Annoyed sighs leave everyone's lips except Solar's, as she could only peer at the figures Kevin was pointing to. She had obviously seen them before, and she knew who they were. However, she had never spoken more than a mere greeting to any of them.

"Talk about being fucking ten," Chanhee mutters.

"It's NCT, it's them!" Kevin ignored his other friends comment, turning to the famous one out of them all, he clung onto her arm as he begged, "Sol, you have to introduce me, please - I'll do anything!"

"Kev," she whines, "I don't even know them!"

"But don't you talk to each other since you're both famous idols or whatever?!"

"It does not work like that!"

"Just leave her alone, idiot, she doesn't want to be apart of your childish antics," Chanhee snaps, punching the boy's shoulder, "if you're so desperate, go introduce yourself."

Kevin pouted like a child who had been denied candy. Solar groans as she thought about his request, but she seriously didn't want to randomly walk over there and start talking to a bunch of guys she didn't really know. She just became official with Jinhwan, how would that make him feel?

Speaking of, she realized, looking around in confusion, where was he?

"Guys," she clears her throat, glancing to her friends, "I'll be back."

Her friends hum in understanding, and she pushes past them to walk over to the other six boys of iKON that had been talking to her family. They were all rather occupied, so she taps on one who seemed the least involved in the conversation.

"Bob, do you know where Jinhwan went?"

He pursed his lips, thinking about it for a second. "Probably to change out of his stage clothes and check in with our new management team so we can leave," he informs with a nice smile.

"Ah, okay," she nods, and before he might ask another question, Solar spun on her heel, and jogged away.

Letting out a sigh, her heels click against the concrete flooring as she walks. She was glad she finally got a hang of finding dressing rooms at this point.

Pulling her phone out of her pocket, she checks for any messages from him. She guessed he didn't really feel it was necessary to text her about something like that. But was she also expecting a text from someone else?

She couldn't lie, she was worried about Jungkook. All she could do was believe that everything he said, he meant it. But even then, knowing he was hurting because of her made her feel horrible.

Not to mention, the other boy she would be seeing tonight. Should she tell Jinhwan about it? She knew they agreed not to keep secrets, but she didn't want to stir up anything. They just got together, it was what they'd both been waiting for this entire time, it just took Soomi longer to realize it.

It wasn't that she still had feelings for Sunwoo anymore. They hadn't been together since she was seventeen. Everything she did with him was in the past, and she was not planning to relive it anytime soon. In fact, the entire reason she was meeting up with him was to tell him that. The thing that worried her was just him, Sunwoo. There was no telling what he would try, but it would probably be something. Solar wasn't trying to cheat on the first day of her being with someone.

Groaning, she shoves her phone back in her pocket. "It'll be fine," she assures herself aloud, not caring if anyone heard her and thought she was weird, "you'll get through this, just resist whatever he tries to do."

She'd been giving herself such a confident pep talk, that she nearly screeched when she suddenly heard something she hadn't expected. A laugh, there was someone laughing, brightly and loudly, all in a giggle that sounded undeniably precious. It was a type of laugh she had never heard before, causing her to glance up at the direction it came from.

That was when she realized they had been laughing at her.

The boy's honey brown eyes meet hers, noticing she saw him giggling at her. Still, he didn't appear distressed about it as she stops, only sending a smile her way, "ah, sorry," he hums, "I just admired the pep-talk you were giving yourself."

Solar frowns, not believing a word this stranger was saying to her, "yeah, right, you were making fun of me."

He pouts a little, "hm, no, I wouldn't do that," shaking his head vigorously. There was a certain brilliance when he smiled again, and Solar cleared her throat, diverting her eyes from the brightened face of this new presence.

He was clearly an artist, why else would he be here? She swore she knew who he was, but for some reason her mind seemed to be messing with her. Why couldn't she think of his name or what group he belonged to? She was usually good with stuff like that because she tended to know everyone.

Solar is snapped out of her trance of deep contemplation by the sudden close proximity between her and the new soul. His eyes are sparkling when they look down at her, he was tall. Not as tall as Junhoe, but taller than Jinhwan, she had to look up directly to meet those eyes. Finally, he cleared his throat, "we've met before, but I don't think you remember me," he explains, but didn't sound offended at all, "Haechan."

Haechan as in full sun? She had to hold in her giggle, what a cute name. 

Glancing down, she noticed he was holding out his hand to shake her own. Pursing her lips, she accepts the exchange. His palm was warm under her own, and her hand burned when it fell back to her side. Only then did she manage to speak out her name, "Soomi - and uh - I'm sorry, I don't remember us meeting before, I have a sucky memory."

He shrugs it off, not appearing a have a worry in the world, "don't worry about it, it happens. I think you know my members though, I'm from NCT."

Oh right, she tried not to gawk, clearing her throat, Kevin would be crying right about now.

Thinking about her poor friend's obsession with them gave her an idea. Maybe it would be convenient for her to make friends with one of them, then she could make little Kevin's dreams come true.

"Ah," she nods, "you guys have that one song that always gets stuck in my head - Regular!"

Giggling, he grins from ear to ear, "yeah, that's ours!"

"I always liked that one 'I'm so clean, so fresh,' part."

"Really?" he beams, "I'm thrilled to inform you that's my part."

"Funny," she hums, before Haechan spoke up about how he really liked her music, too. 

The next ten minutes you'd find them just laughing away. Believe it or not, he was a crazy nice person with a very interesting sense of humor. If she was to compare it to anyone, she'd probably say Junhoe, whom she really could not stand sometimes but at the end of the day, he was her best friend. With this in mind, Soomi felt like she and Haechan could be great friends.

She also figured Kevin would be very pleased to her this news.

A rapid dinging of incoming text messages began in her pocket. Solar wouldn't of noticed them if she hadn't heard rapid footsteps coming down the hallway, and a screeching little voice that ripped her from the conversation she was having.

"Sissy, sissy!"

"Yein!" she gasps as the child ran up to her, immediately grabbing her hand and clinging onto it like her life depended on it, "Hey, why would you run back here yourself?! That's dangerous."

But the little girl only shrugs, "Bobby said you would be back here and that if I couldn't find you I should just scream."

Solar sighs in disappointment, "sounds like something he'd say."

Yein nods, a grin on her adorable face, before she slowly turned to notice the stranger. Blinking a few times, she narrows her eyes in concentration, seeming to look over every detail of the boy. Solar was going to introduce them since she was here in front of him, but she didn't get a chance to when her sister pointed a finger at him, "wait, it's the guy from the group Kev likes, sissy - eNsitea!"

Her pronunciation made Sol sigh and Haechan burst into fits of giggles. Haechan gushes as he bends onto his knees to reach eye level with the kid, "hi, I'm Haechan, I'm friends with Soomi."

"Hi, I'm Yein!" she waves brightly, but couldn't help but look up to her sister in confusion, "you told Kev you weren't friends with any of them."

"Well, I wasn't," Solar scratches the back of her head awkwardly, "until approximately twenty minutes ago."

Yein pursed her lips as she turns back to the boy. Her same green eyes peer at him in focus, and no one knew what she was thinking. Until she blurts, "sissy, he's cute," before looking to him again, "do you think my sissy is cute?"

"Um," he chuckles, unsure of what to do, "sure."

"Oh my God," Solar could only groan in disappointment at her little sister, "Yein, cut it out, I just met him like three seconds ago, and you know it's not okay to do that," she sternly lectures her playful baby sibling.

Pouting, she thought for a moment before her mouth formed an o, "uh oh, I forgot, you're with Jinawn, I hope he's not mad at me!" Her eyes pool with worry, and before you knew it, she was pulling on her sister's arm. Apparently, Yein had no idea that Jinhwan wouldn't have any idea about what happened since he wasn't here to witness it. Solar guessed her sister just had a big heart, as she cried out, "come on, sissy, take me to Jinawn so I can apologize!"

"Yein, you don't have to apologize," she told her sister, not really wanting to bring this up to her boyfriend. Exhaling, her sister only continued to try to pull her down the hall. Turning to Haechan, who was just patiently standing there, she offers him an apologetic smile, "it was nice to meet you, Haechan, sorry about my little sister - she's different."

"Don't worry, she's cute," he waves a hand of dismissal, "but it was nice to meet you too, again - I'll see you around, Soomi."

She nods, before shortly waving as she finally let Yein pull her away. Haechan chuckles as he watches them leave, shaking his head in awe. He could hear Solar sighing in exhaustion as she informed her sister, "you don't even know where the room is, why are you dragging me?!"

Inhaling, he spins on his heel away from the scene to continue where he was going in the first place. The others had probably texted him a million times about where he was, but they probably got distracted messing around backstage that they forgot to come find him. It was what usually happened, anyway.

The entire walk back to his friends, he found himself wondering what it was Yein had been talking about. Soomi, the biggest solo artist, was dating someone? He pondered in confusion, but wasn't that prohibited?

Pushing it to the back of his mind, he tried to shrug it off, even though the name she had uttered was stuck in his mind. It wasn't his business, anyway. 


98:00 | sister talks

"Jinawn!" Yein had been screeching as she ran into the dressing room, far ahead of poor Soomi who had tried telling her little sister to not bring up what just happened. She should've known Yein wasn't going to listen, and sure enough, there she went. "Jinawn, I'm sorry, I hope you can forgive me!"

Jinhwan had appeared to of just changed out of his stage clothes. A red hoodie loosely hung on his body, and a pair of skinny jeans hugged his legs. He was staring at his reflection in the mirror, fixing his caramel hair as he slid a beanie over his head. Soomi's breathe hitched in her throat when she saw him turn around, brown eyes twinkling when he saw who'd entered the room. There's a welcoming grin on his lips, only until he heard the words Yein said. 

Confused, he furrows his brows as he looks to her older sister for context. Solar sighed as she met his eyes, shaking her head as if to tell him not to listen to her. Jinhwan purses his lips as Yein had wrapped her arms around his waist, hugging the life out of him with her tiny arms. 

"What is it, Yein?" he hums, "You know I've never be mad at you."

Her green eyes widen as she pulls away from the hug. "Really?" she asks in shock, "But I accidentally asked Haechan if he thought sissy was cute, I completely forgot I shouldn't do that!" she whines in frustration to herself, her voice only growing that of a higher pitch with each word she spoke. Jinhwan had been grinning at her from how cute she was, but when those certain words came out of her mouth, his smile faltered. 

"Uh," he blinks, not understanding, as he looks up to his girlfriend, "who's Haechan?"

Yein gulped as she realized she was making it worse. Grabbing onto the sleeve of his hoodie to get his attention again, she tugs on it so he would look down at her, "don't worry, he didn't answer!" she calls up to him from her little height, "but he was really nice!"

"Yein," Solar snaps in a stern tone, and her sister frowns as she looks to her, "didn't I tell you to cut it out?" 

"Sorry, sissy," she mutters as she lets his sleeve go. Hanging her head low as she worried about what she just did, she walks to distract herself elsewhere, climbing onto one of the sofas. 

Meanwhile, Sol exhaled deeply as she looked to her lost boyfriend, "I met one of the guys from NCT in the hallway," she explains, figuring it really shouldn't be a big deal. Because it wasn't at all, she had wanted to avoid this idiotic conversation in the first place, but yet here she was, "he's nice," she shrugged, "we didn't talk about anything more than music."

When Jinhwan's blank expression didn't change for a while, she began to grow a bit worried. It hadn't even been a day yet of them being officially together, was it already ruined? She was not looking to have her first fight on day one of her relationship with Jinhwan. Soomi had waited way too long for this.

Her heart was dropping, until that smile spreads over his lips. Shaking his head, his sneakers click against the tiled floor as she walks up to her. Soomi inhales his cologne in relief when he wraps his arms around her, she thought she was about to die. 

"Silly, you don't have to tell me that," he shakes his head with a chuckle, resting his chin atop her own, "what Yein said made me think he was flirting with you, I thought I was going to have to go find him."

"No," she whines, wrapping her own frail arms around his torso and burying her face in his hoodie, "it wasn't like that at all."

"Good, because now you're finally mine, and I won't tolerate any of that," he gushes, pulling away so he could kiss her forehead. Soomi's cheeks had burned with rose as she could only look up at him in awe, she shouldn't of taken so long to make this decision. It annoyed her that it had been clear for so long, but she was too stupid to figure it out. 

Although, she'd always love Jungkook, too. But it was different from the love she had for Jinhwan.

"Anyway, did you hear? Your parents invited me and the guys over for dinner," he shared brightly, grabbing her hand and intertwining their fingers, "I don't know what time it is, but we should probably get going. I think they already left by now to prepare it."

Soomi giggles, "oh, they must really like you guys," she cleared her throat, feeling the need to correct him on something, "but my parents don't do the cooking - they usually have staff for that."

Jinhwan raises his brows in surprise, "right, I forgot you're that rich."

Rolling her eyes, she nudges his side, but he only laughed at her. Jinhwan looked to the child that was on the couch, "Yein, are you ready to go?"

At the sound of her name, the girl pipes up in excitement, practically scrambling off the couch, "yeah, I'm hungry, and I want to see Joonhay again!"

Solar bursts into fits of laughter from how she said his name, as if Jinawn wasn't enough. "God, I really need to teach you pronunciation."

Jinhwan only shakes his head in awe, holding out his other hand for Yein. The girl grabs it instantly, looking like the happiest child in the world. Seeing her poor little sister so bright made Solar feel good. Happiness was what Yein deserved, and she was finally getting that. 

She wouldn't want it any other way. She was thankful for the decision she had made, but it took a lot of help for her to do it.

She had plenty of people to thank for it.

"let's go."



+





Mi: how is he, is Jungkook okay?

Chimmie: yes, mi, he's fine, you're acting like he's dying

Mi: is it wrong for me to worry abt him?? I just broke his heart

Chimmie: omg no soomi you did not

Chimmie: Jungkook accepted it a long time ago

Chimmie: you heard his song. you don't have to be so hard on yourself for feeling emotions.

Mi: sometimes I really hate you for being rite

Mi: but a part of me really loves him, I want him to be happy

Chimmie: he will be, he's happy for you deep down

Chimmie: it'll just take some time but he'll be back

Mi: I hope so

Mi: I can't live without my dosage of Jungkook, you know

Chimmie: I'll make sure to let him know

Chimmie: but get back to spending time with your family, dufus

Chimmie: this is for another time

Mi: ughhdhuwe but he keeps finding a way onto my mind

Mi: OKFINE

Chimmie: see, he is perfectly fine

Mi: he looks dead inside

Mi: JIMIN
