Mi: OMG OK


Groaning, she sets her phone down, not wanting to look at it anymore because it would onloy continue to tempt her. She hadn't actually tried to text Jungkook herself, hence why she went to Jimin first because she just wanted to know how he was doing. It was true, he most likely needed a while away from her whether he would admit it or not. Just the fact she knew he was hurting, though, was really messing with her head.

A deep exhale leaves her lips as she turns back to her face in the mirror. Her green irises peer back at her, but yet she couldn't truly decipher the emotion within them. What was she feeling right now? Soomi was pretty sure she was happy, she should be. Jinhwan and her were finally together, and they were having dinner with her family whom she never thought she would be able to face again. The stalker that ruined her life for months was gone, and though there might be another one on the lose, there was no proof of such. For once, she almost had nothing to worry about.

Except for Sunwoo and Jungkook.

Groaning, she shakes the thought away as she checks the clock on the bathroom wall. She had yet to eat dinner as it was only 7 PM, she still had a while until she had to go to the castle park to meet him.

Trying not to think about it, she realized she should probably get downstairs to see what was going on. Jinhwan and the others were probably confused as to what was taking her so long. 

Just as she fixes her hair and makes sure her makeup isn't tainted, a knock sounds from the door. "Sol, are you alright?" her sister's voice breaks through her mind, and she purses her lips as she heard what she asked.

She could talk to Chaerin about all this, couldn't she?

"Chae," she sighs as she clicks the lock on the door, pulling it open to reveal her sister's concerned face, "do we have a while till dinner?"

Not expecting the question, her sister thinks for a second. "Probably, I just came to check on you because your boyfriend seemed like he was on the verge of death without your presence," she snorts, "he's real attached to you, what do you even do to get guys like this?"

Solar rolls her eyes, reaching out and grabbing her sister's wrist. Pulling her into the bathroom, she closes the door and turns to her shocked expression. "I need to talk to you."

Blinking at her a few times, Chaerin nods slowly, "Oh, okay, what is it?"

The two sisters used to have heart-to-hearts rather often. This was something they were used to, even despite all the fights they used to have when their relationship got rocky. In the end, they knew they could always come to each other for serious things. It was what sisters were for.

Running a hand through her hair out of stress, she sighs, leaning against the locked door, "this whole thing, me and Jinhwan just became official today, you know. I broke my ex-fiance's heart who a part of me still loves, and I have to go meet up with Sunwoo at 11 in the park to break the news to him, too," she vents to her intently listening younger sibling. "I don't know how he's going to react to all this, and to make it worse, I have to go back to Korea within the next couple of days."

Her eyes widen at the last sentence, "really? You're going back this early?"

Scratching the back of her head, she pouts guiltily, "Yeah, I'm not sure if I have a choice otherwise."

Breathing out in a dull manner, her sister shakes her head, "Jeez, Sol, you've always had such a package of problems with you."

"I know."

"Maybe if you'd stop doing whatever it is you do that makes every guy want you, you know," Chaerin scoffs playfully, and Solar only wrinkles her nose in response. "I think it might not of been the best idea for you to get into a relationship right now, sounds to me like you've still got a mighty crazy attachment to Jungkook."

"But I love Jinhwan," she insists, "it's just - with Jungkook it's different."

Chaerin stares at her blankly for a second, before she deadpans, "you're really fucking complicated."

"Ugh, let me live!" Solar cries in frustration, "I just want everyone to be happy!"

"Sometimes it can't work that way," she shrugs, "you can't date both at the same time, Solar."

"Obviously," she groans, before turning towards the door again. It wouldn't be long until they had dinner, meaning someone was probably going to get them. She didn't need any of the guys hearing about what she was saying, she didn't want to create anymore problems. "Just don't tell anyone about this stuff, okay? Especially not Jinhwan about the fact I'm meeting Sunwoo, I have a feeling he'd freak."

She nods gradually, "Sure, Sol, I owe that to you, but be careful sneaking around," she warns in concern, "you know it only works out for a while before it blows up in your face, we learned that from experience."

"Yup," she agrees, "I know that very well."


99:00 | sneaking around

The night was hectic.

Solar tried not to show she was feeling like she was on the edge of her seat. Crossing one leg over the other, she only continued to laugh and join in on the small conversations happening around the table. The majority of the dinner was just her parents and siblings getting to know her boyfriend and those she'd grown close to in Korea. To Solar's thrill, it actually went great. They got along amazingly, more than she would ever have guessed. It wasn't like her parents were the nicest people, they were judgmental of those that weren't like them. And it had been clear from the beginning that the boys of iKON weren't exactly rich business folk.

But yet her parents seemed to love them. They laughed and made jokes, before talking about how the boys careers started and came to be. It even got to the point where they spoke in depth about Solar's life, and how Jinhwan helped her out of a hard time (though she didn't specify the specifics like her drinking problem.) Either way, her parents hearing about what an impact Jinhwan had made on her only made them like him even more.

Jinhwan couldn't help but implode with joy. This was all he had ever wanted. He and Solar weren't pretending anymore because finally, he could say it was real. He didn't have to think about the plan he had created in his head those days ago, he won.

By the time the dinner had ended, Solar had gathered her friends and brought them upstairs to show them around the mansion she called home. There were so many rooms, it took a good while, but the boys loved exploring such a place.

"This is cool, who's room is who's?" Chanwoo had been asking as he admires the room they just walked out of.

Heading into Solar's, she sighs as she falls onto her own bed, trying not to think about the obvious on her mind. "Uh, it doesn't matter, play rock, paper, scissors or something," she shrugs. The guys had pumped up in excitement at her idea, before immediately getting to it.

She rolled her eyes at their antics, sometimes they were really something else.

While they were in the midst of deciding who would get what room while they had the luxury of staying in the Lee mansion (since her parents ended up loving them so much,) Sol took her phone out of her back pocket. There's a hesitant look on her face as she opened her text messages, exhaling deeply as she hid the distress she was feeling so the guys didn't ask questions.

Sunu: so you're coming?

Sol: yeah, I'll be there

Sunu: ight, I didn't expect such a text from you honestly

Sunu: but I'm glad, we haven't got to hang out since you returned

Sunu: I've missed you

Sol: I know, Sunu, but I'll see you soon, okk?

Sunu: 'course, Sol

Sighing, she turned her phone off when she felt a sudden presence. The thing she couldn't seem to sort in her head right now was what in the world she was going to tell them when she had to leave. Not to mention, Solar hated lying to her friends, especially her newfound boyfriend.

But this had to be done. She was just going to talk to Sunwoo about the fact she was truly with Jinhwan now, it wasn't like she was doing anything bad. The only reason she wasn't sharing this information with him was so he wouldn't freak out about it. Jinhwan knew about the kind of things Sunwoo did, but he didn't understand that he would never let her get hurt. After everything they've been through, she knew him well enough to know that she was safe with him. She always had been.

A part of her honestly missed the thrill sometimes.

"I think I choose this room," Jinhwan is joking as he falls onto the mattress beside her.

Solar rolls her eyes at his comment, about to say something witty in reply when an idea strikes her mind.

"Nani," she mutters, "did you ever return Hee's car?"

The boy purses his lips in thought for a second, "uh, no, it's still out front, he just told me to bring it back soon but he didn't give me a date."

She fakely winces, shaking her head, "jeez, no wonder he's been bothering me about it."

"Shit, really?" he frowns, looking as if he genuinely feels bad because he believed her, "should I bring it back to him now?"

"Eh," she shrugs, "I can do it, I actually needed to go see him and my other friends anyway, I promised them I'd hang out with them after the show today but I didn't."

Jinhwan bought her white lie like nothing. He sat up straight and she followed, looking a bit dull at what she said but not minding it in the end as he nods, "oh, okay, you should do that," he assuringly urges her, "I'll keep Yein company in the meantime."

She grins up at him from her stance, feeling her heart ache in her chest. I'm sorry for lying, she mentally pleads to him, but he was completely clueless of what was going on inside her head. Instead, she reaches out her hand to grab his arm, leaning her head on his shoulder as hugging him, "you're the best, Jinhwan."

He giggles that little giggle of his, kissing her forehead, "I have to be, that's what Solar Kim deserves."

There's a giddy ping in her heart when he said her real name.

Her green eyes glance elsewhere, though, to the clock that hung on the wall. 10:50, she didn't have much time until it struck 11:00, and she didn't want to be rude and make Sunwoo wait.

"Alright, well, I'll get going now," she announces, rising to her feet. Looking around her room, she notices the other boys were no longer present inside it. They must've gone to their own rooms, or maybe they were talking to her siblings. Either way, it wasn't a worry to her as she turns to Jinhwan after picking up her purse and the car keys to her best friend's vehicle, "I'll be back soon, I love you!"

"I love you, see you then!" he calls back, blowing her a kiss as she walks out the door.

Going down the steps, she runs a hand through her hair, feeling rather stressed. The castle park was actually within walking distance, but since she needed to bring Hee his car anyway, she might as well drive.

Managing to slip outside without anyone else in the family noticing, she figured if they did see she was gone Jinhwan could just fill them in. Solar wasn't feeling like lying to anyone else.

The cold air pricks at her skin, goosebumps forming up her body as she walks down the steps of the mansion. Clicking the button to unlock the car, the beep beep sounded, and she eagerly trotted towards it to get out of the cold.

New York weather, she sighs to herself as she shoves the key in the ignition. The engine revs, and the brunette wasted no time to pull out of the drive. Street lights covered the roads that made way around the castle, and she looked out at every single mansion she passed, reminiscing about the times she spent in each one.

It felt like only yesterday that she left.

Putting the car in park, she stops against the curb. There was never great lighting in the park, so she had to squint to try to make out Sunwoo's figure, standing out by the benches, but no doubt, it was him. This assumption was only confirmed when she saw a pile of smoke leave his lips, a cigarette dangling in between his fingers.

Letting out one last deep breathe, she swings open the car door. Slamming it behind her, she clicked the lock button before shoving the keys in her pocket. She wasn't wearing anything more than a thin material sweater, which was no battle for the cold night in New York. It caused her entire body to shiver as she makes her way across the grass, approaching the boy with each step she took.

When she was in a close enough distance, she calls out, "Sunwoo!"

Kim Sunwoo didn't need the stars to twinkle when he saw her. His eyes glisten under the night sky of black, and he immediately chunked his cigarette to the ground, stomping on it before he instantly made his way towards the girl.

He thought she would be happy to see him, but she looked rather sad. It was the last emotion he ever wanted to see on her face. This is why his brown eyes scan her closely as he halts right before her, reaching out, his hands grab her forearm so he could force her to meet his eyes, "what's wrong?"

There's a long moment of silence. Solar was stuck in a trance, all she could do was stare up at him like she was hypnotized. What could she say? Sunwoo really was her first everything, she had to of loved him at some point, even though she wasn't sure what that meant back then. He'd taught her so many things, he taught her how to live, how to embrace what she felt inside.

For seven years, he was the only one she ever wanted. Every waking moment was spent with him. The entirety of her teenage years were spent running around by his side, watching him do dangerous things to people she didn't know. You never felt anything less than a rush of adrenaline when you were with him. It was always fun, you were always on your toes.

She hadn't lived like that in a long time. Even though she tried not to act like it, a part of her had missed him. No one liked Sunwoo that she was close to, everyone knew what he did and how he had always been bad for her, but that didn't mean he was ever bad to her.

Now that they were finally alone, with no one around but them, now that they didn't have that burden of her miscarriage on their backs. Now she was finally able to talk to him, to truly catch up on the lives they'd had without each other. Even though they had already done a lot of that, there was still missing pieces.

Before she risked ruining the friendship and relationship they had had with each other since they were children, she wanted to cherish it again — just one last time. It hadn't been in her initial plans to do so, but something came over her, gave her the urge.

"Nothing," she finally breathes out, her heart making her decision for her, "I just think we should hang out for a while before I talk to you about it."

He peers down at her as they pull away from the hug. His brown eyes try to search her own for any hint as to what the issue was, but she only offers him a small smile.

"Sol," he clears his throat, a sudden awkward expression taking over his face. Scratching the back of his head, he sighs, "I didn't want to tell you this but — my fathers back in town."

Never were these the words she thought she would hear on a night like this. An abrupt flash of wind blows, nearly making her fall from the force, but she paid no attention to it as she only gawked up at him, "huh?"

"Yeah," he exhales in a dull manner, "I — I was going to keep it from you but — before I left he told me I had to go do a job for him."

Her face fell at the sound of this. All she knew to respond was, "oh."

Sunwoo taps his foot against the concrete of the sidewalk they stood on. His leather jacket clings to his toned arms, and his messy dark hair was falling in his face from the wind. Under the moonlight, there was a certain tint that found itself on those irises of his, one that made him glisten more than usual. He was what he always was, the typical bad guy you saw in the movies that everyone caught themselves falling for.

But everyone always fell for Sunwoo for good reason. The thing was, he was nearly impossible to resist. The only girl who had ever managed to do such was — well — Solar Lee.

Trying to stop anything her mind wanted to think about him, she clears her throat, "so — what does this mean?"

Pursing his lips, he diverts his eyes from the sky to meet her own again. They're even more devious looking in the darkness, him only being visible thanks to a streetlight a bit away.

"Well," he hums, "you can go home to your boring friends or we can still hang out, just the old fashioned way," there's a smirk that's forming on that face of his. She wanted to look away, but there was something preventing her from doing so. "A call for the old times, Sol."

He obviously wanted her to go. Sunwoo wouldn't ever let her get hurt, right? She had before but it was never badly, after all. Solar couldn't count the amount of times she had ran around with him on jobs, seen people die right before her. It used to be so normal, she thought it was okay, all because they were bad people — usually.

But by the time she was leaving, she learned they weren't all bad people. After all, they'd killed Changmin's father, who was a normal man who just wanted to support his family.

She wasn't sure if she could take seeing anything like that again. Solar used to do it because she understood it was what her boyfriend had to do, it wasn't his choice. Sunwoo used to hate what he did for the most part, but seeing him now, it almost seemed like he enjoyed it after what he told her.

It left her with an unsure feeling. He could detect that in her face, and he groans, nudging her side, "don't be boring, Solar, where's the girl I used to know?"

Pouting at the fact he just called her such, she wrinkles her nose, "I'm sorry, Sunwoo, I'm not exactly one for seeing people die anymore."

"Look, you don't even have to come inside to see it, you can just wait in the car and the we'll go out to eat after, okay?" He raises a brow, a smile dangling on those stupid lips of his. There wasn't anyway she would be able to say no, but to make it worse, he added, "we'll even go to Cane's, I know they don't have that in Korea."

Heaving out a frustrated noise, she punches his arm as she cries, "okay, fine, I'll go — but it's just for the Cane's."


100:00 | one last shot for the old times

"You're waiting in the car?" Eric snorts, clearly judging her choice as he shifts in his seat to look at where she was sitting in the front passengers, "what happened to you? You're more of loser than you used to be."

Heaving out an annoyed sigh at the youngest who never stopped bothering her, she didn't even get to say a word when Sunwoo practically hissed, "I'm going to leave you for dead if you open your mouth one more time."

Some of the others start snickering at Eric's bewildered expression. The car is silent once more as it smoothly drives down the road, and Solar lets out a sigh, leaning her head against the window. They were already in the part of New York that she didn't recognize, as it was just another rich neighborhood she had never been to. At least she didn't think, but it was too dark for her to really be able to make out anything.

She thought about asking what kind of job they were even doing, but knowing would probably make it worse for her. She was better off being unaware of who they were after.

"Here we are," her ex boyfriend had informed everyone, parking the car across the road before pointing to the house on the other side. Everyone's eyes follow where he was pointing, examining the mansion they were about to infiltrate.

She didn't bother to look, though. Like she said, it was better off with her not knowing anything that would make her wonder more.

"So, we aren't killing them — yet?" Hyunjae calls from the backseat.

Sunwoo only responds with an mhm.

"We're holding them for ransom," Sangyeon explains, "Sunwoo's dad is trying to get something from their father."

Hearing what they were saying was raising questions in her mind. She didn't think they were openly going to talk about the job in front of her, but she also wasn't surprised. Now she knew — they were holding a company owners daughter for ransom to get him to do something — she wondered what it was.

But most importantly, who?

"Still sad I don't get to have any fun," Hyunjoon is muttering sadly.

"Enough talk, let's just get this done," the leader had announced, heaving out a sigh as he climbs out of the car. Solar watched as the others followed him, all bidding her a goodbye in the meanwhile until they returned.

She offers them a small wave in return. Her eyes watch as they go up to the front door, pulling out weapons and hammers to break down the door. The house wasn't brightly lit on the outside, so she couldn't decipher if she had ever seen it before.

Company owners daughter, the words kept repeating in her head. It left a nervous feeling in her stomach, surely it wasn't anyone from the castle, right?

Trying not to think of the worst, she hoped that Sunwoo's father was done messing with family's on the inside. All she could do was hope, as she leans back in the leather seat and pulls out her phone.

soo: omg ihy nevermind starve

She couldn't help but laugh is disbelief at the way he was. Everything he did just made her feel a certain type of way, one she couldn't explain with anything other than that one word.

"Throw her in the back!"

The sudden calls of her friends were heard, causing her to look up from her phone. She didn't have a chance to see what her boyfriend said because she was too focused on what she just heard. Her green eyes widen at the sound of the back door opening, and sobs fill her ears. They were coming from a female, and a clunk sounds through the air as the boys had thrown her body in the backseat.

Solar cleared her throat, not wanting to look at the girl because it would only make her feel bad. It was bad enough she was tagging along with them and didn't even tell Jinhwan, she didn't need anymore guilt on her back.

The guys had climbed in their own seats, all happily chatting about random things. Like always, it was no big deal to them they just kidnapped someone. They were used to doing these things in their day to day life. Once upon a time, she was too.

But not anymore.

Solar holds a hand to her head as Sunwoo climbed in the drivers seat. His eyes wander over to the distressed girl, "you alright, Sol?"

Not wanting any of them to think she was pathetic, she nods slowly, "mhm, just hungry."

He peers at her from his stance for a second. Knowing her as well as he did, it wasn't likely he bought it. But Sunwoo didn't pry that time, because a voice called from behind him, "you heard the queen, fucking drive us to Cane's!"

Chuckling at Hyunjoon and his weird personality, she shook her head. Though these boys looked dangerous because they were, you wouldn't really guess with the way they acted towards each other.

At first, the drive had been uncomfortable because the hostage kept crying. Her sobs were rather quiet, but she would let out a loud one every once in a while. Though Solar had yet to see her because she didn't want to, she could hear that she was horrified. She could only imagine what it must be like, being taken against your will from your own home, kidnapped. It sounded terrifying even to her.

Trying not to think about it, she only watched as Sunwoo had later pulled into the parking lot of Canes. The boys were all piped up in excitement, ready to eat after a long day.

"Finally, my stomach is fucking churning," Eric is sighing eagerly, being the first one to climb out and practically bolt to the entrance.

"Think she'll be okay?" Younghoon had asked Sunwoo, referring to the hostage and if she could potentially escape.

Sunwoo didn't even glance back at her as he nods, "yeah, there's no way she can untie herself."

"Alright, let's go."

"If she does, it sure won't be pretty," Hyunjoon giggles in a small voice despite the mortifying words that he was saying. His eyes are devious as they turn to look at the hostage in the back seat, "hear that? I'll cut off your fingers one by one, then your toes, then your legs, then your arms, until I reach your—"

"—okay, let's go inside now!" Sunwoo abruptly clears his throat, cutting the poor boy off from his reminiscing.

"Oh, right, I can't wait to have some chicken!" Hyunjoon cheerily cries as if he wasn't just talking about cutting off someone's body parts three seconds ago.

They really were something else.

"Are you coming, Sol?"

The brunette clears her throat, pulling down the mirror so she could stare at her reflection. "Yeah," she mutters, "go without me, I'll be there in a second."

Figuring she was checking her makeup, he nods. "Okay."

With that, he closed his own door and made his way inside with the others. Solar was left to bask in the silence, the girl suddenly not making a sound.

She figured her mouth must be gagged. Either way, she was not about to try and mess with that. All she was doing was making sure there was nothing messed up with her makeup.

Closing the mirror, she sighs, quickly replying to Jinhwan's text before turning off her phone. Shoving it in her bag, she gathered her things before reaching for the handle.

That was when she heard the whimper, "S-Solar?!"

Coughing out of shock, she holds a had to her head. There was no way this is happening, she tried to tell herself that, but she knew that voice.

"Stella?!" She screeches, trying not to pass out from the shock she was feeling.

The company owner was from the castle — it was Kevin's father.

Bile rose up her throat like a volcano, and she couldn't help the way her breathing became irregular.

"P-Please help, I don't know why they're doing this," she whimpers, her voice trembling more with each word she spoke, "w-why are you with them, Solar? When did you get back?"

All the sudden questions getting thrown at her were a lot. She had absolutely no idea what to do as she nervously looks to the doors of the fast food place, making sure none of the gang members had come out to check up on her — yet.

"Uh — I — I'm not with them, I used to date Sunwoo," she explains slowly, a shameful look on her face as she watched Stella's eyes bulge, "I came back into town to see my family for a while, but Sunwoo kind of dragged me along," she paused again, hesitating, "b-but I didn't know this was what he was going to do."

"Kevin never told me you dated," she mutters, her view on Solar most likely changed forever. Either way, she had still known her since she was a mere kid, never could she hate her. It was why Solar was dying inside from the fact she didn't know what to do. The obvious thing was to help her, it was Kevin's sister, after all. "I don't think you're a bad person, Sol, j-just please," she whines, "please get me out of here, I don't know what they want but I don't want to be involved in any of this!"

"Okay, okay," she bushes her, "don't worry, I won't let them do anything to you." She heaves out a deep breathe, grabbing the car door handle, "I'll be back, Stella, sit tight."

With that, she opens the car door, slamming it behind her. Her sneakers click against the concrete angrily as she practically runs to the entrance.

The scent of chicken was heaven to her. It had been so long since she had her favorite food place since they didn't have any of them in Korea. But eating was the last thing on her mind as her eyes land on the table where the gang members were located.

Sunwoo seemed to notice her immediately, as if he had some sort of sensor in his brain. His eyes glint as she reaches them, "look who finally showed up, hungry?" He hums, motioning to a basket of chicken he ordered for her.

That was, until he saw the immensely pissed off expression on her face. Her eyes were burning with anger, her fists clenched at her sides. Solar looked like she wanted to beat Sunwoo, as if she could.

Knowing too well what it was, he sighs in disappointment, "what is it, sweetheart?"

"Stella Moon, fucking seriously?!" She snaps, not caring how loud she was despite her trying to keep it down. Since it was midnight, though, there were barely any people inside. "You wanted me to tag along, and you thought it was perfectly fucking fine to kidnap my best friends sister?!"

Sunwoo doesn't look bothered by her yelling at him. Actually, his face remained blank as he only rolled his eyes. Leaning back in the leather of the booth, he pops a fry in his mouth, not even bothering to look at her.

"Sunwoo, let her go," the brunette demands, "don't involve my friends or their family in your bullshit, I thought you were done with that."

None of the others dared to say a word as this went on, they knew cutting in would be bad news. Even Eric was keeping his mouth glued shut, far too busy inhaling the chicken before him.

"I did too!" Sunwoo suddenly blurts, probably tired of hearing the girl go on and on, "you failed to remember — this shit isn't my fucking call! If my father tells me to do something, Solar, I don't have a choice, that's how it works in the castle, remember?"

His sudden explosion caused her to be quiet for a moment. A pout forms on her lips as she breaks their eye contact, thinking hard on what he said. Sunwoo was right,

"But it's my friends sister," she mumbles, "you can't let him make you do that to her, she doesn't deserve it."

Sunwoo exhales, pursing his lips as he tries to think of what to tell her. In that moment, she found herself wondering if a soul like him actually felt any remorse for those actions. He used to, there was no doubt about that, because Solar always made him into a better person. She made him see that people deserved second chances, they deserved to live their lives, whether it was to the fullest or not. No one deserved to die, not even by their own standards. She made him feel guilt, hesitation, despair — she made him feel human.

But she had been gone for the last five years. After her disappearance, everything got worse, especially mentally for him. He killed with no regrets because he couldn't bother to care anymore. Sunwoo had he convinced that no cause of death could amount to the pain he felt.

How was that changing now that she was back, right before him, telling him how wrong it was?

He didn't know.

"Solar," he heaves out, burying his face in his hands as he tries to think of anything, "Solar, there's nothing I can do for her. She's just a means to get Kevin's father to make some sort of deal with my father."

The girl frowns, thinking about it for a second. Finally, she chuckles in disappointment, looking away because of the shame she felt when she looked at him. Sunwoo could tell, and it made his heart clench in aching agony, he hated knowing she was thinking of him that way.

"Really? You're just going to torture an innocent girl?" She pushes, crossing her arms over her chest, "I told myself I wouldn't get involved in all this, but that's my friend, Sunwoo — please."

"I can't," he persists, shaking his head before popping a piece of chicken in his mouth, "it's not my call to make."

Tired of everything going on, tears of guilt and anger rush to her eyes. Hurriedly, she reaches up to wipe them away, looking around as if she thought someone was going to show up and save her from this. She didn't want to be around any gang members, Solar was right, she couldn't handle this anymore. This lifestyle was never cut out for her, but if she could get through it then, she couldn't now.

She felt horrible, she lied to Jinhwan, and now she would have to lie to Kevin about where she knew his own sister was. What kind of friend was she? What kind of girlfriend was she? She was hanging out with her ex boyfriend that was a gang leader.

"You know what," she breathes, finally looking back to him, "I'm glad I got to see you again, Sunwoo, see what you've turned into — because it made me realize my life was better without you in it."

"Solar—" he gawks, saying her name in shock from what he just heard. It was the last thing he ever expected.

"— I've been happy, even through out everything going on, I've been able to get past the stalker, the scandals, the heartbreak," she rants, holding a hand to her head, "but this, this isn't cut out for me, and I won't stand by and watch you do it. I have a life now that's better than this, and I love Jinhwan."

"So, the thing I called you in the first place to tell you was — we can't be around each other ever again, Sunwoo, this is goodbye."


101:00 | life in new york

Solar ended up dropping off Chanhee's car like she said she would. Her friends were confused to see her over at such a random time, though she did swear she would come hang out with them. They'd been sad to hear she wasn't staying, that was, until she told them she would be back.

soo: please come outside

soo: please

Her steps crumble under the driveway of the Lee mansion. She kept having to wipe away tears under her eyes, and it was making her feel like a pathetic kid. All it felt like she did anymore was cry, yet she was supposed to be strong. Solar didn't feel strong. She hated keeping things from people she loved, but most of all, she hated acting like it was okay.

The air is cold against her skin but she didn't care. She didn't want to go inside, it was why she sent a text instead.

She needed him, she needed him to hold her, to give her peace of mind and tell her what the hell she was supposed to do. Jinhwan was the only one who could ever successfully do all of those things.

Solar was too caught up in her thoughts. But most of all, she was caught up in the last thing Sunwoo said to her.

"You're in danger, Solar, there's still someone after you, I was tracking them — I can protect you, but if this is what you want, you don't have my protection anymore. You're on your own."

"It's not what I want, Sunwoo, it's what I have to do."

The sound of the front door opening causes her head to whip that way. She only glanced at Jinhwan, who was rushing down the steps, a coat drenched over himself and another one of his winter coats in hand. A sigh leaves her lips as she hears his steps approaching her, turning away and peering up at the night sky again.

There was silence as he engulfs her with the coat he brought, securing it over her shoulders. Afterwards, he wraps his arms around her torso, hugging her as he asks, "Soo, what's wrong? Are you okay?"

Hearing those words from his mouth, broke her more than she was planning. Still, she told herself she was done with the crying. Crying wasn't going to fix anything, it wasn't going to get her anywhere. Everyone kept calling her strong, so she needed to be that.

"No," she sighs, "I'm not okay."

Jinhwan was obviously waiting for her to continue. She didn't waste time to do so. "I did go see Chanhee today," she starts, "but I also saw Sunwoo, I went to tell him that we couldn't see each other and that I was with you so we couldn't be the way he wanted."

Silence — Jinhwan was processing everything he was hearing. But he wasn't mad about it, he was actually glad she got that over with. "You should've told me, I could've come with you."

"I know," she agrees, "but that would've made it worse, he might've tried to hurt you. And it's different, you know, I'm not calling you weak, but he's a trained gang leader."

He thought about it for a moment, and knowing she was right, he nods, "okay, what else? How did it go?"

"I — he dragged me along on a job with him, I wasn't going to go but something in me thought it would be fine," she groans, "I don't know why, I thought that I should just spend one last moment with them, cherish the old times. I thought I could handle it like I used to."

"And?"

"I couldn't, they were talking about kidnapping a company owners kid," she shakily shares, and Jinhwan squeezes her tighter upon hearing this, "I thought surely it wasn't anyone in the Castle — but it was Kevin's sister, Nani, his fucking sister."

His mouth is open from shock. "They have his sister and they're holding her hostage doing God knows what, and I couldn't do anything to save her," she cries, "I haven't even told Kevin because how can I? How do I tell someone that?"

"Soomi, Soomi, calm down," he soothes, rubbing her forearms from behind her as she only breathes heavily. Her heart felt like it was crumbling onto the ground before her, all she could do was listen to his silky voice, "you can tell him, it's the right thing to do. There was nothing you could've done to save her, not in front of a bunch of criminals, the only thing you can do is tell him where she is."

"But what if there was?" She insists, shaking her head, "I might be able to get them to release her, but I just blew up and told Sunwoo I didn't want to see him again."

"Soo," he snaps sternly to get her to stop talking nonsense, "I know you want to help, but it's dangerous for you either way. This is all you can do, but anything more than that isn't your fight to fight — you just need to be there for your friend."

Sighing, she lets his words enter her ear and process through her brain.

"Yeah," she nods hesitantly, "I guess you're right, but, that's not all that happened."

"Alright, what else?"

"He told me I was in danger, that there's still someone out there, the partner," she admits, "that he was tracking them, but now he won't help me because you know — I kind of told him to leave me alone."

Jinhwan purses his lips. The wind blows through their hair as he puts his hands on her shoulder, turning her body so she was facing him.

Putting a finger under her chin, he tilts it so she's forced to look up at him.

"Soomi, I told you before that I'll protect you, you don't need him," he assures her deeply, "I might not be a trained gang leader but I'm your boyfriend who loves you more than anything, and I'll be damned if I let anyone touch you."

"Jinhwan—"

"—No, Soomi, I promise you, whenever this person shows their face, I'm going to hurt them, and I'll have no regrets for what I do."

Although she swore she wouldn't cry, there were tears welling in her eyes. She didn't care about anything anymore except for being there with him. That was it, she didn't need Sunwoo, she didn't need an illegal gang to protect her.

They could do this.

"I love you," she mutters in a small voice, wrapping her arms around his waist and hugging him like her life depended on it. At this point — it did.

"I love you too, Soo."





+




"That's it, Yein!" The brunette beams in excitement, clapping out of cheer as she wraps an arm around her little sister to give her a side hug, "you did it right!"

The youngest of the Lee family looks up to her big sister with big eyes. Twinkling under the chandelier in the dining room that burned a million different lights, she couldn't help but ask in disbelief, "I did?"

Unable to resist chuckling at her cute sibling, Soomi ruffles her hair as she nods, "yup, you sure did, you're a smart kid, Yein."

She gawks at this. It had been so long since she heard those words, and even longer since she actually understood anything in school. Feeling complete, she closes her homework book and shoves it back in her book bag, "thank you, sissy!"

"Sure, Yein, I said I'd help you," she hums casually, patting her shoulder, "you come to me whenever you need anything, okay?"

Zipping up her bag, she turns back to look at her sister. About to of nodded in agreement, her green eyes suddenly land on something behind Soomi. Without another thought, she curiously voices, "what about Jinawn, can he help me, too? I bet Jinawn is smart like you, sissy."

At the sudden mention of her boyfriend, she'd grown confused. Soomi had gotten up early today despite how she was up so late last night. Her poor Jinhwan had spent the night comforting her and making sure she was okay, so much so she didn't think he would be up anytime soon.

It's why she jumps out of shock at the feeling of two hands on her shoulder. She didn't have to turn around to know it was him, it was obvious enough when the scent of that cologne entered her nostrils.

"I can help you, too, Yein, but your sister is probably the better option."

Raising a brow, the petite girl wrinkles her nose as she glances up to him, "but don't ask him, Nani's stupid."

"Hey!" He cries in offense, poking her cheek with his finger as if it would bother her, "I made great grades, thank you."

"Don't be mean to Jinawn!" Yein complains with a pout, and Soomi giggles at her little sisters worry for them.

"I was just kidding," she informs them both, though Jinhwan obviously knew that.

There's a small silence for a second, before Jinhwan clears his throat, "Yein, do you mind if I borrow your sister for a second?" He asks innocently.

Rolling her eyes at how he tended to make her heart hurt, her little sister seemed to be feeling the charm of Kim Jinhwan just as much, "sure, Jinawn!"

Shaking her head, she lets him pull her to her feet. She had no idea what he wanted, but it probably wasn't going to be anything she wanted to think about right now. Either way, she was going to have to face everything eventually.

On the bright side, her list of worries was slowly getting smaller in a way. She was with Jinhwan, meaning she finally made her decision — and no doubt was she happy. But she also still had to check on Jungkook, and on top of that, now she was on permanent bad terms with Sunwoo. He was the best bet she ever had on finding the other stalker she had on her hands that refused to make a move, but she threw him aside.

Even though it might've been rude, she saw it as the right thing. He kidnapped her best friends sibling — which she had yet to talk to Kevin about. She already knew that he and his family were aware, as the friends had spoken about it in the group chat this morning. It made her feel like shit, knowing that she knew. She was going to have to tell him sooner than later.

Jinhwan walks upstairs with her hand tightly in his own. They make way into her bedroom, where he closes and locks the door behind her. If it was any other time, she would've had something else in mind, but she knew that was most definitely not what he was thinking right now.

Before she could ask, he lets out a deep breathe, "we go back to Korea soon," he suddenly states.

Here it was — the dreaded subject of the day. The fact that they were all going to have to leave New York, clearly. It wasn't like they were suddenly going to live there, Korea was their home and they had work to do there. The thing was, even though Soomi knew that well, she wasn't sure what her family had been thinking.

"I know," she mumbles, not knowing what else to say.

Hesitant at first, he finally asks the question, "are you planning to stay?"

"Huh?" She gasps. That hadn't been much of an option in her mind at all. "There's no way CEO-Nim would allow something like that," she shakes her head, "I have to get back to work, make music, I can't postpone that anymore than I have the last year."

"You just reunited with your family after half a decade, Soomi," Jinhwan asserts sternly, "there's nothing wrong with you needing to take some time off — for real this time."

She frowns, knowing he was right just like he always was. Rubbing her forearms out of worry, she mutters, "but would he allow that?"

His footsteps grow close to her until he's right before her. Those dreamy eyes of his pour holes into her as he reaches out, taking both of her hands in his own. Jinhwan's thumbs rub her knuckles, a sense of comfort melting within her as he hums, "if you explain it to him, I'm sure he'll understand. You can send him and the staff music that you work on from here, you know, the beauty of the internet."

Letting out a deep breathe, Soomi nods, "you're right, I'm sure that would work," she agrees. But another thought soon clouds into her head, "but what about you?"

Exhaling deeply, there's a dull look in those usually shining brown irises. He runs a hand through her hair, something he liked to do when he was in deep thought.

"There's no way he would let me stay," he explains, "it would be too obvious, we already made a song and have been seen in public together. How would me asking to stay in New York with you look?"

Soomi sighs — another time he was right once again. With everything always happening, she tended to forget the fact their relationship could never be open to the public, not in the way they wanted. Never did she think she would see a day when being in love was forbidden and could ruin your career, but here she was, with her own in jeopardy.

It saddened her.

"Yeah," she whispers, "you're right."

"But it's okay," he assures her brightly again, cupping her cheek with one hand so she was forced to meet his eyes, "you need this, Soo. You deserve some time with your family, don't let me stop you from having that, alright? We can call every single day, it'll be like I'm right here with you."

"Okay," she nods, a sad smile on her face as she places her hand over his own that was still cupping her cheek, "we can do this."


102:00 | rooftop jokes

For once in late January, it wasn't freezing cold in New York City. In fact, the sun had been beaming down that morning like no other. The weather had taken a bipolar turn for the better, causing the entirety of the city to exchange their heavy coats for tank tops and shorts.

Solar Lee wanted to see that burning sun as a sign. She wanted to believe that this good weather was telling her that this was right.

Early that morning she called her manager, planning to have told him about what she was trying to do first. Since he was who she was supposed to check in with always before anyone else, right? The thing was, her trying to contact him ended up being a dead end. It left Solar in evident confusion, to which she instead contacted the YG office directly herself.

Thanks to the fact she was so well liked by her boss and that she was basically the most famous artist in the country, she was able to talk to him personally. Like last time, he didn't mind the sound of her having some time off. It was even a better response since she had been working so hard on releasing songs, plus they had been doing well. He actually thought the idea of her spending time with her family back home was wonderful. When she shared that she was still planning to work, he only grew more pleased.

"That's not problem, you take care of yourself, Soomi. I'll have the staff cancel your flight back to Korea."

His words repeat in her head as she walks, hours later. There was no telling what time it was, she had lost track. If you asked her right now what her plan was for what she was doing, she wouldn't have any idea what to tell you.

But something brought her here. Something inside of her urged her to leave the house, to head to the airport even though she wasn't the one leaving today.

The thing was, Jinhwan and the boys of iKON weren't leaving yet either. Their flight was much later in the day.

It was insane, really, she wasn't sure how she was going to spend this newfound time off here in New York. She wouldn't have Jinhwan with her, Junhoe, she wouldn't have anyone she felt safe with her — except for her family. She would be living in her old home, the one she left, as if nothing ever changed.

But things had changed. She had someone after her still. Did they know of her location now? Would they stay in New York? Where they already here and she had no idea? There were thousands of unanswered questions that scared her. All of them which she only would have an answer to if she wouldn't of thrown Sunwoo aside — which remained to be her next issue.

Sighing, she pulls the mask further up her face, adjusting her sunglasses as she jogs through the airport. Her eyes jolt every which way, in search for seven souls she needed to see.

The sound of screaming filling her ears seconds later was a give away. Her green eyes land on the seven boys, walking together after they put their luggage away. Body guards crowded them like prized possessions, but it didn't stop the eager fans from trying to tackle them.

She winced at the sight, thankfully none of her fans were that crazy.

Pulling her phone out of her pocket, she hurriedly types in a text message.

mi: bathroom, now

It was rather unexplanatory and would probably leave him in a bundle of confusion. But she was sure he would get the gist after a moment. This was proven when she saw his head hang down as he stared at his phone screen, before glancing around him in search for the girl.

All she did was rush to the restrooms in the airport, hoping that the boy would soon follow. She still had a lot to do today, so she needed to just say farewell quickly and then get back. Jinhwan would probably be looking for her, too.

"Soomi?"

The bathroom door closes with a clunk. Jungkook's eyes are filled with disbelief, until she slowly removes the shades and mask from her face. Those green eyes couldn't belong to anyone else. A smile spreads over his lips as he gawks, "what are you doing here?"

Standing right before her was her poor Jungkook. Even though what he did wasn't necessarily right, she would feel bad for hurting him forever.

"I thought I should properly say goodbye," she breathes, "it'll be a while before we see each other." His face grew more lost as she says this, so she doesn't wait to fill him in, "I'm staying here in New York, I don't know how long."

His eyes widen at first. He didn't expect for her to have made a decision like that.

"Oh," he mutters, "wow — I guess that's good for you. Is Jinhwan staying with you?"

"No, he has to go back with the others," she explains, "it'll just be me."

He nods in understanding, no emotion really showing on his face. "Well, that's good that you're taking some time here, I'm really happy for you, Mi."

She sends him a small smile to show she's grateful. An awkward silence falls over them, and Jungkook cleared his throat to fill the air. His shoes squeak against the tile as he glances anywhere that wasn't at her. It felt as if they were strangers — she hated it.

"I can't stand this," she abruptly blurts, causing his eyes to whip back to her instantly. "It's not right, you and me aren't supposed to be like this."

"What do you mean?"

"This, Jungkook," she motions between them, "it's like we don't know each other. I can't stand it, we were friends once, why can't we be like that again?"

A deep sigh leaves his lips. That was just the thing. Jungkook was trying to be nice about all of this, he thought he did the right thing for once. But when he said all of that to her, he didn't plan for them to really stay close like they were when he was waiting for her to make her decision. He couldn't resist Soomi, and sooner or later he would do something he'd regret. The only way for her to be free and happy like she deserved was if they it didn't include him.

But none of it changed how he felt about her.

"Cause," he exhales, "I still love you, Mi — I can't deny what I feel when I'm with you and eventually I'm going to do something I'll regret."

She frowns at this. It wasn't what she was hoping to hear when she came here on such a mission. "I just don't want to lose you."

"You're not," he corrects sternly, "but I can't emotionally handle being your friend right now, Mi."

It sucked to hear him say it, but she should've seen it coming. Jungkook was hurt, she had broken his heart. What result was she really expecting?

"But still, thank you for coming to say goodbye, it means a lot."


+



"I can't believe I really have to go back to Korea today," the dark haired boy is mumbling, a dull sigh escaping his lips as he dramatically states, "and I can't believe you aren't coming with us!"

Soomi pouts at the fact he genuinely sounded upset about it. Before she could speak up to explain, Jinhwan had flared at the tall boy in raw annoyance, "don't be a selfish dick, Junhoe, she needs to spend some time with her family."

"I get that," he shrugs, "I'm just bummed we didn't get to explore the city, and how am I supposed to survive God knows how long without my best friend?" He giddily grins, elbowing her side. It was a rather hard action, but by that point, she didn't feel it as much as she would've.

Raising a brow, she looks to her side where her best friend sat in curiosity, "wow, sounds like Junhoe is gonna miss me."

"You know what? Just because you said that I'm not giving you the satisfaction."

His sass had no end, but it was becoming one of her favorite things. Shaking her head, she couldn't help but admit as she glances around to all her newest friends, "I'm going to miss all of you guys — a lot."

"We're gonna miss you, too, Soo!" Donghyuk cried, "how are we ever going to keep Junhoe in check?"

"Me?" The boy gawks in response to his friends comment, "what about this old geezer over here? He won't be able to survive without Soomi."

Jinhwan was the first one to react — rather negatively too — to Junhoe's words. "Excuse me?" He blinks in awe at what he heard, meanwhile Soomi was practically on the floor from laughter, "did you just call me an old geezer?"

Junhoe didn't even hesitate, "yup."

Soomi sighs, holding her stomach so she could stop herself from laughing so hard. Waving her hands in the air to silence any bickering, she clears her throat, "I want to spend some time with you guys before you leave," she voices her thoughts.

The others cheer in agreement to such. She grins as she finishes her idea, "so, who's down to explore the city?"



+



"Out of all the places, this is where you want to spend our last moments together?" 

Soomi groans as she sends an unimpressed look towards her best friend, who was just walking out the exit onto the rooftop. The wind slapped him in the face immediately, causing his hair to fly every which way, and he winced as he tried to fight the cold. Pulling his coat tighter around his tall body, he walked to stand next to his best friend, who was busy overlooking the city they were surrounded by.

"This rooftop is very significant to me," she snaps to him matter-of-factly, sticking her nose in the air in a sassy manner, "the fact I brought you here should mean a lot, asshole."

He'd pursed his lips in thought, brown irises raking over the city. It was pretty, there was no way you couldn't think such. Junhoe couldn't help that all he ever did was use sarcasm and make jokes, that was just - Junhoe. Still, he found a way to make an exception for his best friend, "this is really cool, Soo."

She didn't show any shock on her face if she felt it. Soomi knew him well, she knew that it was weird for him to not make jokes and witty comments. That didn't mean Junhoe was actually a self absorbed asshole like he played off, if he was, she wouldn't call him her best friend. 

"I know," she hums, nudging his arm with her elbow, "let's take a photo, stupid."

Not that he looked opposed to her idea, but that didn't mean he wasn't going to act otherwise, "you know, I don't think I really want to take a photo with someone who called me stupid."

She rolls her eyes, that time shoving him as if it would even slightly affect him. Still, he got the memo as he giggled, "okay, chill."




"Wow, nice job," he's muttering in a bitter tone as he looked down at their taken photo. Soomi looked over his arm to peer at his phone, not understanding what he was saying it so rudely for. But before she could open her mouth to ask, he confirmed her wonder, "you're too ugly, it ruined the photo."

"You're really fucking annoying," she states as he burst into laughter as her bewildered expression. Sighing in disappointment, she shakes her head, turning away from her giggly best friend to look out at the view again, "just send me the photo, you geezer."

"Hey that's my word!" he whines as he sent her the photo before standing beside her, "plus, I'm like two seconds older than you, seriously?"

"So?"

"So it's not fair that you call me that, I'm not your decrepit boyfriend."

The breeze blew through her hair, sending shivers up her spine. Soomi gripped the ledge of the rooftop, turning to look at her best friend with frustration evident in her glare, "okay, do you really want to spend our last moment alone talking about this?!"

Chuckling, he doesn't respond to her question, but judging by his lack of word, she assumed the answer was no. Instead, Junhoe grabs the railing of the rooftop himself, sighing as he peers out at the city of New York, "Sorry, I still haven't grasped the fact you're really staying here."

For once, he was having a normal conversation. "You say that like it's permanently, I still have a job," she reminds him, trying to brighten the mood again because Junhoe's face looked as if it had fallen with his mood.

"And a family," he abruptly adds, "you've got a family in Korea, too."

As much as she wanted to tease him, she decided against it. Smiling from ear to ear at how happy his words made her feel, she nods, "I know, Junhoe, I couldn't ever forget," she sighs, "but I'll be back, sooner than you know."

Exhaling deeply, he runs a hand through his windswept hair, still peering out at the city. It was hard to think this would be there last moment together for a while. Soomi couldn't even come say goodbye to them at the airport, as it would raise more speculations. The fans knew she was friends with them, but they couldn't make it look like they were as close as they were with them crying and hugging each other. Therefore, everyone decided it would be best for her to say goodbyes back at the house. 

"You better be," he finally mutters after a second of thought, "you're my best friend, Soo, I'm gonna have a hard time functioning without arguing with you everyday."

She snorts in humor, shrugging, "we can still argue over text and FaceTime calls."

He shrugs like she did, "it's not the same."

A sad smile resides on her face. It was rare to see genuine emotion out of Koo Junhoe, but all the friends had been seeing it more and more within the last couple of weeks. The thing was, Soomi brought that emotion out of him. Through their friendship, she'd turned him into a better person. Even though he had known his members for so long, even then he never liked to talk to them about what he was feeling. But when it came to Soomi, he felt like it was safe to voice those emotions. 

"It'll be back to normal in no time," she shares, laying her head on his shoulder with a deep breathe, "just make sure Jinhwan doesn't die, okay?"

He wrinkles his nose, "ugh, shouldn't you tell Yunhyeong and Donghyuk that? I refuse to take that responsibility."

"Just do it, Junhoe."

"Fine," he groans, "just because you're my best friend."

Chuckling, she stands straight again to take out her phone, voicing her wonder in the air, "what time is it, anyway? The others are probably waiting for us."

Sighing, he finally turns away from the rooftop. That pretty view would be carved into his mind forever, he was sure of that. Junhoe's sneakers click against the concrete as he walks to stand by Soomi, who was busy looking down at her phone. Seeing the time at the top of the screen, she was right. "Yup, guess we have to go meet your clingy ass boyfriend now."

Turning off her phone, the sentence he just spoke as they walked to the exit seemed to strike something in her. Minus the extra adjectives, she realized, that was a familiar statement. It caused her to swallow a lump in her throat, she was just thinking about that day because she was on that same rooftop. 

And what she was not going to do, was make this rooftop significant not because she spent time up here with her closest friends, but because it was where she kissed both of them. Just the thought of her possibly ever kissing Junhoe made her sick. Though Jimin might have some sort of small crush on her, that was most definitely not evident in Junhoe.

She giggled at the thought.

"His clingy-ness is cute," she's telling Junhoe as they began to descend down the steps to ground level.

A snort is heard from her best friend, who was a bit behind her, "no, Soomi, it's sickening."




103:00 | we'll meet again

It's a scary thought, a scary thought that everything you once knew is about to change. The life you were so accustomed to over the last half a decade was changing. It's a scary thought, that turns into a horrifying one, but after you think about it long enough, it almost becomes comforting.

The reason being — it's for the better. This new life that's been created for you, is one that will make you happier. True joy isn't something everyone gets to experience, but finally, it's your turn.

Solar Lee was finally getting that shot. She was getting break, no matter how long, from her job that had worked her lifeless. She was getting a break from being on the run, in constant fear from someone who tried to kill her. She was getting a break from the life she had made for herself in Korea, that had been a stressful, but good one nonetheless. However, in the end, it had all been missing one big milestone she had forced herself to believe she didn't need.

And now, she was finally getting all that back. The realization that she was finally reunited with her family after so long was what kept her going through out that day. Even though she wouldn't be seeing her friends for a while, it was so she could continue to make things right with her family. Her home had been broken for a long time, and it would take just as long to begin to fix it.

Still, after becoming so accustomed to being around seven people daily, it becomes hard to comprehend you won't be seeing them for a while. It became even harder when one of those seven happened to be your boyfriend.

So even then, even though she knew she was making things right and that she had to do this, it still hurt. It hurt that the time was dialing down, so much so that she could barely keep her eyes off her phone to check the time.

The last hours since she got back with Junhoe had been spent with the eight friends. They gathered around in her room, making memories and taking stupid photos together. Then, they'd went to the garden where she had told them tons of super idiotic stories about her as a kid. They'd explored the entirety of the upper East side, with her as their designated tour guide.

Until it had winded down to only a couple of hours. They'd have to be at their airport, checking in by then. It would be bad if they were to miss their flight.

So, as an attempt to cherish the time they had left, Soomi and Jinhwan went to one place.

"This is funny," Soomi is muttering as she clings onto his arm, the bell above the door dinging as they entered. The sight of the interior of the ice cream parlor was the same as she remembered it the last time she came here with him. Still, she couldn't help but giggle as they all stand in line.

"Funny?" Jinhwan raises a brow as he glances to her, that glint in his eyes that shone every time he did so, "I'd say it was more depressing, but okay."

She frowns at his words, "I was trying not to think about that part, I just meant us coming here again - you know, with the added additions." Soomi starts chuckling again when she turns around to see their friends who joined them on such an event.

Crossing her arms over her chest, the dark haired girls heaves out a sigh as she motions heavily ahead of the two, "Sol, can you stop being an insolent and order?!"

"Is the insult really necessary?" Jaehyun looks to her in disappointment, rolling his eyes, "is it really?"

The two began to bicker like the children they mentally were, and not wanting to hear such, she turns around to see that they were the next in line. Jinhwan looks to her as if to ask if she wanted the same thing from last time, and she nods in confirmation.

"Yein, what do you want?" he turns then to the girl who had been clinging onto his other hand, bending down so he was at her eye level.

Her big green irises glisten with excitement, and she doesn't even have to look towards the menu as she boasts, "three scoops of milk chocolate with sprinkles - oh, and gummy bears!"

From the interesting combination, he nods slowly, "sounds good," he hums cheerily. Returning to his feet, he orders their three ice creams, before turning back to the other three beings behind them, "you guys order, too, I'm paying."

Juyeon was the first one who piped up from hearing such, as if they were poor, "dude, really? yes!"

"Idiot," Chaerin mutters as he excitedly rushed to the front of the group to order.

Meanwhile, Solar shakes her head as she walks over to Yein, tapping her shoulder. "Yein, let's go sit down, kay?"

The girl nods, grabbing her older sister's hand and following her towards the tables. She takes a seat at the same one they always used to sit at, while Solar pushed another table to combine with that one so they had enough room for everyone.

"Jinhwan can bring us our orders," she informs her, sliding into the booth seat next to her sister.

Yein was silent for a long while. Assuming she didn't have anything to say, Solar didn't say anything either. This was practically the only time she would have to enjoy being with her siblings and her boyfriend, so in that moment, she was just trying to take it all in. She wanted to cherish the little moments she had left before she didn't see Jinhwan for who knew how long.

"Sissy?" the girl suddenly speaks up beside her. Snapping out of her daze, she looks down to her in wonder, only to see her cellphone in her little sister's grasp. There she sat, peering down at her phone screen in utter confusion, "who's that? That's not Jinawn."

Staring down at the home screen in a trance, she clears her throat, ripping the phone away and turning it off. "It's my friend, Yein, his name is Jungkook," she sighs, "you saw him at the show, remember?"

Slowly, she nods as she recalls the Bangtan boys. "But you looked so close," she mutters to herself.

Feeling a little uncomfortable at the fact her kid sister was gathering onto the fact her and Jungkook had been something more, she wasn't able to inform her that that was not the case. The sound of heavy footsteps were heard, but they didn't belong only to her siblings and her boyfriend. A body slid in smoothly next to her, but they didn't hug her or anything similar. No, they punched her arm, a lot harder than she would've thought. "You bitch, you really didn't think it was necessary to invite us to this outing?!"

Rubbing her arm, she sighs in dismay as she looks to her best friend, "Hee, that was painful, you know."

About to implode from the fact she ignored his question, Chanhee exhales deeply. Opening his mouth, Jacob hurriedly speaks up from beside Yein, "I'm sure she was just too busy to think about it, she's got a lot going on, Chanhee."

At his genuine words and soft tone, the boy seems to calm. Heaving out a deep breathe, he instead looks to her again, "actually, I heard about it. You really told Sunwoo to leave you alone forever?"

Not wanting to talk about it, she should've figured her friends would ask her about such. Word got around fast, especially in the castle. There was no way she would of ever hid something like that from happening, and the members of his gang were probably sure to spread it around quickly. With that, she only nods, not having much to say on the subject anymore. Since the story everyone had heard wasn't exactly factual, she didn't wish to continue the talk further.

She was just thankful Chanhee was covering for her like the saint he was.

Everyone had taken their seats, having to slide over yet another table to fit all of them. Solar sighs, a melancholy feeling creeping through her veins as her eyes rake over her old friends, to her siblings, before falling on her boyfriend that sat right beside her. Her and Jinhwan were practically one, sitting so close they were basically tied to one another. She laid her head on his shoulder as Chaerin had sighed in shock, "still can't believe it, ever since that first day in the living room as kids I didn't think you two would ever really be apart."

Pursing her lips, she should've known her sister would be the one to mention such, "well, we were apart for five years, you know."

She only shrugged it off as she ate another bite of her ice cream, "eh, but not in his mind. Now that you've actually told him that, there's no telling how he's feeling."

Diverting her eyes from looking at her sibling, she looks down at her ice cream instead. She hated this subject. What happened with her and Sunwoo simply had to be done. It was terrible for her to be in contact with him not only because it was risking her career, but it was also risking her relationship. All around, it was dangerous for her.

It had to be done.

But even then, a part of her felt bad. Sunwoo was her childhood crush, her real first love, the first guy she ever dated. He was her first everything, and that made him special. They grew up together. Whether she liked it or not, losing him was going to hurt even if it was minimal.

"God, I bet he's depressed," Jaehyun mutters.

"He deserves it," Chanhee rolls his eyes, "he's deranged, and needed to get it through his head that our Solar is a taken woman - this had to be done, he had it coming."

"True," Chaerin nods, pointing a finger at the pink haired boy, "he really is an asshole."

Before anyone could remind her not to curse, the small child at the table had asked in wonder, "what's an asshole?"

Everyone heaves out sighs of disappointment, their eyes all falling on Chaerin. She, however, remained unbothered as she snorts, "it was inevitable, okay?"

"It's a really mean person, Yein," Juyeon had bent over to whisper to the little girl.

Blinking at her big brother, the brunette turns to everyone again. "I think Chaerin is an asshole."

The entire table exploded with laughter, unable to hold in how funny it was even though it was bad the little girl just said such a word. Chaerin was the only one in a state of shock, but she didn't appear offended. After a moment, she shrugs, mumbling that the girl wasn't wrong, before laughing along.

"Just don't say that at home, Yein," Jaehyun informs her, "Mom and Dad really hate that word."












+















The long hangout at the ice cream parlor could only last so long. After endless amounts of jokes and fits of laughter, the group had to head home. Chanhee and the others parted from the group to head to their own houses, bidding farewell to Jinhwan and telling Sol they would see her soon.

Once they reached the house, the boys of iKON were already packed with their things and ready. They had spent their last day messing around and with Solar, before she went to the rooftop with Junhoe, then to the parlor with Jinhwan. Even though they had been together for that time, it still didn't feel like it had been enough. This was proven when they all made eye contact as Solar and Jinhwan had walked up the drive way. Just the sight of her friends, with their bags surrounding them, dressed for a long airplane ride, broke her heart in two.

They'd carried Jinhwan's things out, too. He was wearing just a casual outfit, so it wasn't like he needed to change for the ride. They were all ready to go, and the Uber to take them to the airport would be arriving before she knew it.

"This doesn't seem real," she's muttering, pouting like a baby as she walks up to all her friends.

The six of them were sitting around on the porch, dull looks on their faces. Her and Jinhwan join them, sitting down on the steps. A sigh had left his lips as he wraps an arm around her shoulder, rubbing her forearm to ease her nerves, "it's okay, Soo, we'll get through this. You need this, remember?"

Rubbing her temples, she wanted to believe he was right. But seeing them all like this made her think otherwise. "I'll just miss you guys," her voice basks out, barely above a whisper, "we've been together everyday for the longest time now, it'll suck without you all here."

"I'll still be here, okay?" he assures her, kissing the side of her head soothingly, "you can call me whenever you want."

"Yeah," she sighs, "yeah, I know."

"We can still play games together online," Bobby speaks up to try to cheer her up, too, "you just won't be able to come to my room and beat me with your controller."

Chuckling because she actually did do that, she shrugs, "yeah."

"You'll be fine, Soo, you'll be with your family," Donghyuk reminds her cheerily.

"That's the thing," she abruptly blurts, "you guys are my family, you have been for the last year."

The seven boys were left in a daze from hearing her utter those words. In the end, it left smiles on their faces, and tears in their eyes.

"Soo, you're family, too," Yun admits with a grin, "we'll keep in close contact, always."

Suddenly, before anyone else could say what they wanted, the sound of a van parking against the curb sounded. It screeched as it halted to a stop, blowing the horn once to get their attention.

They all knew what it was, their ride was here.

Everyone had let out a deep breathe, rising to their feet as they tried to prepare themselves. No amount of time would have ever felt like it would be enough because of how attached the friends were to each other. That was what it meant to really adore someone like they did.

"Here we go," Hanbin sighs, "it's now or never, we don't want to keep them waiting." A frown spread over his lips, he turns to Soomi to pull her into a hug, "take care, Soomi, and if you work on any music feel free to send it over."

Hugging her friend back tightly, she giggles as they pull away, "of course, and you take care of that girl of yours, yeah?"

His cheeks grow a shade of pink, as he clearly was not expecting her to randomly mention such. Still, he grins from ear to ear as he nods, "always."

One by one, she began to say her goodbyes to each of them. Jinhwan was still standing beside her while the others boarded the van. They said their sad goodbyes, eventually shedding tears, before there were only two boys left standing in her yard.

"SOOMI!" Junhoe squeals, his voice going more octaves higher than she ever sought possible for such a guy. He tackled her, almost, just like he tended to do every single day. And even though everyday, she would get mad at him for such, she didn't mind at all that day. When he squeezed the life out of her, she squeezed the life out of him back. "I am going to spam the living shit out of your phone every single day, and if you don't answer me I will hold Jinan-hyung captive in a box, understand?"

It took her a minute to fully realize what he said as she was also trying not to suffocate from his grip. "Alright, I'll answer you unless Jinhwan makes me mad."

"Wait, what?" Jinhwan suddenly realized what was going on, "how the fuck would you fit me in a box?"

The best friends finally pull away. Junhoe rolls his eyes at his oldest hyung, "it wouldn't be hard, you're like a toddler."

It was scary how fast his expression changed from blank to angry. "Yah!" he snaps, but Solar grabs his arm before he could attempt to beat up Junhoe.

"I love you, Sol, be fucking careful around that weird Sunwoo dude," he snaps sternly, hugging her one last time, "I'll make sure Jinhwan doesn't watch any porn or something-"

"-Dude, what the fuck?" Jinhwan cuts him off impatiently, unhappy with the bullshit he was spewing that was wasting his precious time.

"Okay, calm yourself," he holds up a hand to him, before turning back to her one last time. "Remember, Jinhwan - box," he states, before spinning on his heel and carrying his luggage to the van.

Despite what was happening, she couldn't help but laugh at him. Like always, Koo Junhoe knew how to make any treacherous situation into a hysterical one. It was his special talent that was both frustrating, but also made her thankful since it eased her ache. Her heart was falling out of her chest and he made it hurt less — only until she turned to see Jinhwan.

Just the sight of him brought the reminder back, the reminder he was leaving to the other side of the world and she was staying. For the first time in a year of them knowing each other and clinging onto one another, they would be millions of miles apart. The fact the two had just newly entered an official relationship only made it that much harder. When she realized she wanted to be with Jinhwan for sure, she didn't imagine it like this. She wanted to be with him every second of everyday, his mere presence was a drug to her. How was she going to be without it?

"Nani..." she breathes, her voice cracking at the end of his name leaving her lips. Before she could stop herself, she broke. A weep of vulnerability leaves her lips as she steps forward into the dull boys open arms.

Deeply exhaling, he squeezes her tightly, rubbing circles on the small of her back. Solar buries her face in his neck, trying to inhale that cologne for the last time. His warmth sends her into that same euphoria, and she found her eyes watering more than she planned. She wanted to be strong for this, she wanted to stop being such a baby all the time, but this was different.

Upon hearing her whines and feeling the wet of her tears against his neck, he squeezes her even tighter. "Soo, stop crying, we'll be okay," he coos to her, "you said it wouldn't be that long, remember?"

He was trying to assure himself more than he was her. Although he acted like the strong one all the time, Jinhwan hurt, too. He hated what was happening, he hated leaving the love of his life here, even if it was what had to be done.

"We'll be okay," he repeats again, mainly to himself as it was so quiet he wasn't sure she heard him.

They stood there for a long while, breathing in each other's scents and trying to cherish their last moment. Maybe some might say they were being overdramatic, but this was a big deal to the two lovers. After all the hardships they had gone through, they just wanted to be with one another. It seemed that as soon as everything was good, they couldn't be together. That fact alone was frustrating, even though it was Soomi's decision to stay. She knew she needed to, she couldn't be selfish when her family needed her.

"You're right," she finally says confidently as they pull away from the hug but remain at the proximity, "I'll be back before you know it, we've got this."

A sad smile is on the caramel boy's face, "that's the spirit, baby." His hands leave her arms that they were gripping to cup her cheeks. The pads of his thumbs were soft against her skin, leaving a graceful tingle against it as he wipes under her eyes to rid her of the silent tears that kept escaping.

Under the moonlight, a slight breeze blew, and the green eyes of Solar shone brighter than ever. A twinkle of adoration was permanent in Jinhwan's brown irises when he looked at her. You could see the love the two had for each other by just seeing them that way. It was a melting sight, and it was heightened more than ever from the current emotion of the events occurring.

"I love you, Jinhwan-ah," she mutters, losing herself in those glistening eyes of his.

Placing her small hands atop his that were still on her cheeks, he didn't say a word. Instead, he leaned forward to enclose their lips in a savory kiss. His grip tightens against her face as he gives it his all, his lips slamming against hers passionately. It was a tender kiss, one that told her everything she would ever need to know about how he felt. But still, as they pull away, he whispers out, "I love you, Soomi-ah."

Breathing out, she allows him to step away from her. His hands left her face, and he turns to pick up his things. Using one hand to carry his luggage, the other grabs her own.
