But he didn't remain right before her. His steps crunch under the grass as he walks towards the van that was waiting for him. Still, she allowed their fingers to intertwine one last time.

Only until he grew too far, too far that their fingers began to disconnect. Their hands suddenly weren't locked with each other anymore, she suddenly couldn't feel his warmth anymore.

But yet, his touch remained on her hand, sending sparks of longing throughout her body like lightning. The euphoria she felt only moments ago began to fade as she watched him walk up the steps of the van, his eyes locked on hers, but yet she could still remember that scent of his cologne.

And even though she thought the worst part was over, nothing compared to the abrupt stab at her chest when he finally turned away. The van doors closed behind him, and it drove away before she could process what was happening.

Just like that, he was gone. And with him was her heart.

It was for the best, she had to tell herself that so she didn't chase the van and get on it with him.

All she could do was watch it disappear down the street. Tears well in her eyes again, and she angrily wipes them away. She needed to be stronger in this situation, she would see him soon enough. This wasn't the end of the world.

"Sissy?" The tiny voice behind her cuts her thoughts in half, "did Jinawn leave?"

Solar turns to face her little sister, who had walked across the large yard to stand feet away from her. As soon as they made eye contact, Yein saw the destroyed look on her big sisters face. Just the sight answered her question, and it made her sad seeing her sister in such a state.

"Don't cry, sissy!" She tries to cheer her up, "you can see him soon, tomorrow will be better!"

Her odd words made a certain saying pop into her head. With a chuckle, she shakes her head, muttering to herself, "you're right, Yein, following everyday is a better tomorrow."


104:00 | revelations

The new day felt weirder than ever. Her eyes flutter open to the sound of bursting laughter and held in snickers. Confused, she stretches out her arms, still not used to the atmosphere of her childhood bedroom. Blinking those green irises that matched the color of the spring grass, she rubs her eyes before slowly looking around her, following the sound of giggles.

And there sat the baby of the Lee family, dear Yein perched on the side of her bed where she wasn't located. She kept bursting into laughter from the silly faces her big sister made when she was asleep, but at the sight of her suddenly sitting up and peering right at her, the little girl squeaks in shock.

Solar would've probably killed any of her other siblings had she caught them doing this, but she could never do a thing to Yein. A yawn leaves her lips as she fully sits up in her bed, her voice hoarse, "Yein, what are you doing? It's gotta be like six in the morning."

Her little sister stares at her blankly for a second. As if trying to comprehend, she looks down at the device she was holding in her hand, before stating confidently, "sissy, it's 3 PM."

Upon hearing something so astonishing, she practically falls out of her bed. A gasp leaves her lips, and immediately she throws her legs over the side of the king sized bed, hurrying to her feet. "You're kidding, how is that even possible?!" She's gawking in disbelief. Had she really stayed up that late? She wasn't exactly sure.

In her defense, she did try to go to bed early. But all she could think about was her boyfriend and how she would be away from him for what felt like so long. She couldn't stop thinking about Jinhwan until her mind wandered to all the things that had happened to her recently. All the way from the questionable person that may still be after her, to Sunwoo. She couldn't lie when she said she was wondering about her ex. A part of her was going to feel bad about what happened whether she liked it or not.

"You seem sad, sissy," Yein is mumbling as she watches her sister walk to her vanity. Solar was obviously distressed as she began scrambling through the makeup atop it, trying to get ready for the day ahead of her.

When she doesn't say anything, Yein figured it was time to tell her why she was even there in the first place. Climbing off the bed, she walks over to her sister, holding out the device that was in her hand. "Hee-Hee was spamming your phone and it was really loud. I tried to wake you up but you sleep really heavy," she explains.

When her words sink into her brain, Solar slowly looks to her phone that she was holding. Why would Hee be calling her so much? There were actually many reasons, and she didn't assume any of them were actually serious. Chanhee was the kind of best friend that just spam called you when he wanted anything — whether it was to hang out or for you to come to his house and help him with something stupid.

A sigh leaves her lips as she turns on the screen, revealing the fact that there were about 30 missed calls from him. Rolling her eyes, she sends a small smile to her sister, patting her head, "thanks for telling me, Yein."

"Sure," she mutters in response. Spinning on her foot, Yein doesn't leave the room. Instead, she climbs back onto her sisters bed and sprawls out on the mattress. She wasn't trying to invade privacy, but she just wanted to be around her big sister. It wasn't like Solar minded, anyway.

Tapping the call back button, the brunette sighs as she holds the iPhone to her ear. The ring only sounded once before a hectic voice had blasted through the speaker, "Sol, fucking finally, you lazy bitch!"

Still, she didn't feel that shocked from his words. "Whatever it is, was it really necessary to call me thirty times?" She asks in disappointment, "It's my first day back and you can't even let me adjust."

"Solar, I would love nothing more than to allow you to sleep until 8 PM, but I need you to come take care of the nuisance standing in my living room."

She blinks a few times as she tries to process what he just said. "Huh — don't tell me Kevin is still doing that thing where he plays Beyoncé and starts twerking on tab—"

"—No — well, yes, he still does that — but that's not the issue at hand," he breathes on the other line, "honestly, it's worse."

She was beginning to worry it was actually something serious. Closing her eyes to mentally prepare herself, she hesitantly questions, "well, what the hell is it then?"

"Just get over here — now, Sol."



+


Solar walked to Chanhee's house with an ecstatic Yein behind her. The younger girl was as happy as could be, though extremely curious at to what it was Chanhee had called her big sister so many times for. Her endless adoration for her sister and the fact she was back had made it practically impossible for her to separate herself from her. This being said, Yein refused to not go wherever Solar went. After trying to fight the kid for a while, Sol realized that it was whatever and just brought her along.

She was just going to Chanhee's house, it wasn't like it was going to be anything bad. All she was expecting was something he was overreacting about, it was what he usually tended to do.

"Wanna be with you, only you, only youuu~," she could hear Yein lightly singing to herself the entire walk. Really, it was ironic and cute that the girl liked her and Jinhwan's song so much. She wanted people to like it, after all, the song meant more than anything to her. But hearing someone sing it only made her think about the featured boy in the song that she was missing already.

The "castle grounds" were actually enormous and went on for miles. Each mansion was separated greatly so that the residents actually had personal space. Though those in the castle were closely acquainted, it was still important to not be too close.

However, this meant that the Choi mansion was a rather tiring walk. It was one she used to walk everyday, so Solar assumed it wouldn't be a big deal. But it had been so long it felt like it was taking forever.

After listening to Yein him Only You for what felt like the 90th time, the mansion finally came into view. A breathe of relief left Solar's lips as she speeds up her pace, Yein following by her hip.

"What do you think Hee-Hee wants?" Yein has been asking as the two walk up the drive way, "is it Kevvy again?"

Trying not to fall into fits of laughter because of the nicknames her little sister always had for everyone, she shrugs, "He said it wasn't Kev, but I wouldn't be surprised."

Yein doesn't say anything because by then they were walking up the steps. In reality, there were a thousand questions her little sister had. Yein was curious about a lot of things in Solar's life. After hearing everything her and the others talked about at the ice cream parlor, she was even more confused — and simply just too young to understand everything that her big sister was dealing with.

Solar heaves out a sigh as she turns the handle, knowing the door would be open. There's a blank, neutral expressions that remains on her face as she steps foot into the mansion. That same rose scent of air freshener Mrs. Choi always used for everything entering her nostrils like waves. Just like always, it felt like a second home.

"Hee, I'm here!" She yells into the house as loud as she could manage, "I hope this is actually important because I was having a great slumber!"

Yein is humming to herself again as she walks over to one of the sofas. Climbing on it, she messes with the hem of her skirt as she waited for something to happen.

Solar remained standing in the middle of the living room, waiting for any sign of life in the Choi household. As soon as she was going to scream her lungs out again, hectic steps are heard from one of the hallways.

"Sol, thank God, I can't handle this degenerate anymore!" The gasp of her best friend is heard, and the brunettes brows furrow as she turns to meet his eyes.

"What are you—"

"—Sol."

Her face falls and her heart sinks, tumbling into the shining wood floors beneath her with a plump, "Sunwoo..."

"Sunoo!" Yein had screeched before another word could be exchanged between the adults. The little girl scrambles off the sofa, running over to the tattooed boy with her little arms spread wide open.

There's a small, forced smile on the boy's face as he bends down to accept the child's hug. He offered her a sweet greeting like he always had, being the rare angel he turned into when he spoke to Yein. After talking to her for a short moment, he slowly rose to his feet again to make eye contact with the bewildered girl across the room from him.

"Hey," he clears his throat, "I need to talk to you, I tried going to your house but Chaerin practically chased me out with a knife."

Pursing her lips, not a word leaves her mouth. All she does is nod, sounds like something Chaerin would do.

"I tried to tell him to fuck off, Sol," Chanhee speaks up from behind, "but I think it's serious, more than Sunwoo's weird obsession with you."

Sunwoo's once straight expression turned abruptly cold as he looked to the pink haired boy. "Really — obsession?" He snaps in annoyance, "you have quite the mouth for someone I could break right here."

"Careful, Hee Hee, I don't think Sunoo's kidding," the child had cut in from her spot on the couch.

Sighing, Chanhee only rolls his eyes, "we already went over this, we both know I've got immunity."

Sunwoo only glares at the boy in pure anger. Being the violent guy he was, if it was anyone else, they probably would've gotten injured. But Chanhee was right — he did have immunity. He was the best friend of the girl Sunwoo loved, and he knew that no matter how much he wanted to, he couldn't hurt him.

"Lucky," is all he says, before turning back to the brunette, "listen, Sol, I know we're done or whatever you spouted, but I can't not tell you about this."

Having had been silent the entire time, she took it upon herself then to finally speak, "o-okay, so what is it that's so big?"

It was awkward — maybe not to him — but Solar felt like she was going to sink into the ground any second. She didn't plan to be talking to Sunwoo any time soon after she practically told him to never talk to her again. And after she couldn't stop thinking about him and guilt tripped herself into thinking it wasn't actually the right thing to do — here he was. But Sunwoo didn't appear bothered at all, like usual. He looked as fine as ever, as if he thought she had been kidding and it was all some sort of joke.

She didn't know if that was a good thing.

His brown eyes glance from her little sister to her best friend, and he hesitantly clears his throat, "let's talk upstairs." Not waiting for a response, he was already walking towards the wrap around steps of the mansion.

Chanhee rose a brow as he made eye contact with Solar, who only shrugged to him with wide eyes. Not knowing what else to do, she slowly follows. "I'll be right back, guys."

"You two better not!" Chanhee had been yelling as they reached the top floor, "Keep it PG, please!"

She was going to hit him for that later. Even though he was trying to lighten the mood and was just using his terrible jokes to do so — she was still going to hit him later.

Sighing, she could only follow a silent Sunwoo into the guest bedroom of the Choi home. It's as quiet as ever as he closed the door behind her, and she walked over to take a seat on the edge of the bed. Placing her hands in her lap, she fiddles with her fingers as she waits for him to say something.

He doesn't for a while. Clearly, he was thinking hard about whatever it was that was so important.

Feeling like she was on the verge of dying from embarrassment, she clears her throat, "so, what is—"

"—I found who's after you, Sol, they're here — in the city."

105:00 | mesmerizing

It was like being thrown into a nightmare. Did Sunwoo just say what she thought she heard? There was no way it was true. She had been doing just fine convincing herself there wasn't anyone on the loose that wanted to ruin her life anymore. Now this was happening? All of it had been enough, she couldn't see anymore violence, anymore pain.

Her throat was dry and she was on the verge of breaking down mentally if she hadn't already. Sunwoo could tell, and he sighs as he knew he still had to go on, "I know you didn't want me to keep looking out for you, but I couldn't help myself when I saw a suspicious vehicle park down the road and walk to the side of your house the other day."

No, no, she couldn't let herself believe this. This was too fast, it was too much. Not that she was doing fabulous recently, but nonetheless she was okay. With everything, her and Jinhwan were happy. She was finally happy. Now something was going to just trash that, by the likes of Kim Sunwoo, it wasn't possible.

"Sunwoo," she breathes, "if this is some way to get us to be friends again, it's really messed up."

There's a blank, unimpressed look that takes over his face. After a second of staring at her and searching for any sign of her kidding, he doesn't find one. Before he could help himself, he starts snickering like a crazy person, "what? Do you really think that?" He asks in bewilderment, giggles still leaving his lips every few seconds. "Sol, we both know you didn't mean what you said that night. There's not a bone in your body that wants to stay away from me. I'm telling you this because I love you, and I want to keep you safe."

At these times, she hated having an ex that she grew up with and dated for seven years. She couldn't pull anything she did with Jungkook or Jinhwan with him. He knew her almost as well as she knew herself, meaning he could see inside of her and know just those things. He knew damn well she regretted what she did and had been feeling bad about it, and looking even deeper, he knew she didn't mean it.

The brunette breaks their eye contact, a pout on her lips as she fiddles with her hands in her lap. She felt defeated and nonetheless horrified. "Y-You said they were at my house? Are you sure it wasn't just a gardener, a maid, one of the kitchen workers?" She questions in frustration, desperate to uncover this was some sort of misunderstanding.

Knowing how she was imploding inside, Sunwoo's boots click against the floor until he falls onto the bed beside her. The scent of smoke filled her nose before he even touched her, and she closed her eyes as she tried to tell herself not to let him do so. But she needed comfort, she needed to know more, and it was already destroying her.

Her face hits the soft of his t shirt, going past that leather jacket of his. His arm was secure around her shoulders, those pillow lips like heaven when they kissed her forehead a few times. After a moment, she wraps both her arms around Sunwoo's waist, breathing in and out to try to calm her nerves.

No matter the issues they had, at the end of the day Sunwoo would never let a thing in the world hurt Solar. He protected her at all costs, he always had. And the thing was, he had the assets and guts to do so. It was no joke that he was violent and had an angry streak.

Because of his tendencies, she felt safe and worried at the same time. Though she could handle it once and though it wasn't like they wouldn't deserve it, she still didn't like seeing anyone get hurt.

And even through it all, a part worried for Jinhwan. She couldn't tell him she was talking to Sunwoo again, she couldn't create more problems. But then, how was she going to tell him about the stalker?

Stressed more than ever, she only squeezes the boy tighter.

After what felt like forever, he clears his throat, "it wasn't a worker, Sol. But I tracked their vehicle, they're staying in the city, at a hotel downtown."

A shaky breathe leaves her lips. She could only try to guess how long they had been here, watching her, collecting information she didn't need to disclose to the world and ruin her reputation.

"What am I gonna do?" She mutters, "There's no telling how much they know. They could even tell the world about you and your operation — and the fact I'm close to you."

Obviously, the gang leader knew this well. "I know," he nods with a sigh, "which is why we have to find that motherfucker before they even think about it — and we will, okay?"

Not saying anything for a while, the girl was too busy steadying her breathing. To calm herself, she let herself inhale Sunwoo's scent of cologne and smoke, telling herself like he said, they would find them.

Sunwoo was no joke, she would be okay.

"T-Thanks," she finally blurts, pulling away from the long embrace to meet his eyes, "I don't know what I'd do without you in times like this, Sunu."

Her voice was quiet, and she didn't say another word. Solar's mind was wandering all over the place when she thought about the boy next to her. She had told him she loved Jinhwan, that he only created trouble in her life, that they couldn't see each other again. But through out it, Sunwoo still looked out for her.

Even though it was such a sweet gesture, she couldn't stop thinking about one thing.

"I let her go."

"Huh?" She gawks in confusion, not sure if she understood what he was talking about.

Sighing, Sunwoo repeats himself more clearly, "I let Stella go, we didn't touch her."

The words were like a slap of bliss to the face. Her mouth is open like a hippo, as she's only left to stare up at him from her shorter state as if he was insane. Sunwoo pursed his lips, waiting for her to say anything, as it was clear he wanted a reaction from his unusual good deed.

"Thank you," she boasts as soon as she finally seems to take it in, "you did the right thing, Sunu, but what about your father? What about Stella, won't she tell?"

Knowing Sunwoo as well as she did, she came to learn that the only thing on the Earth that made him nervous was his father. After all, it was the man who made him the way he was, the man who had complete control over his life. Disobeying him was serious, and Solar had seen the sorts of consequences it had — never had they been pretty. From what they had been through, her throat felt a little dry at the thought. Did his father know she was here? Because she would be the perfect punishment for his disobedience.

She was too scared to ask.

"She said she wouldn't say a word for you," he explains, "and uh — I haven't told him yet, I've been trying to think of any other way I could get Kevin's father to make the deal, but it's kind of been the last thing on my mind as soon as I found out about this fucking insolent after you."

Exhaling, she nods in comprehension, looking at her hands that lay in her lap. "Well, I'm glad you did that, but what are you gonna do when he finds out?"

"I don't know."

The curiosity was killing her. "Does he know I'm back?"

"No," he assures instantly, "and I sure as hell won't let him find out, we know how that'll end. He definitely won't be happy when he finds out about Stella, but he knows there's nothing other than you he could hurt me with."

There's a lump in her throat she had to force herself to swallow. She was proud of Sunwoo for doing the right thing, for stepping up. It made her realize that maybe, somewhere deep down, he wasn't horrible — which was what she always thought. A part of her felt bad for ever thinking so in the first place. He was raised to be the way he was, he didn't know any better. Though it was still partly his fault for how he hurt people more than he needed to, it wasn't completely on him.

But this was adding a lot more to her plate than she planned. Staying in New York wasn't supposed to be this way for her. This was supposed to be her getaway from the drama, but it turned out it was only creating more.

"I'll be careful," she mutters, "I don't need him knowing I'm here and adding to my list of people who want to hurt me."

"You know I won't let any of them touch you, Sol," Sunwoo reminds her, "I don't care if you hate me so much it irks you, I'm going to protect you."

A small smile fought its way on her face before she could try to hide it. Sunwoo was already staring down at her when she looked up to him, and she could only shakes her head in disbelief, "Kim Sunwoo, I tried to but I can't hate you — I can't even be mad at you."

For the first time, he smiled, brighter than ever from ear to ear. His smile was contagious, it tended to light up the room and catch you in a daze. And the smile of Sunwoo was doing exactly that, as she found herself getting lost in him for a moment. There were many things about him that were mesmerizing, it always made her heart heavy. He really was a sight to see.

It was just how he was raised, how he had to grow up. If you looked deep enough, Sunwoo was a soft soul covered by the darkness his life had turned him into. She knew he wasn't as tough as he appeared to be, that was just how he was taught to appear — how he had to appear for the live he led.

It made her wonder, if he was born in a different lifestyle, how they could be.

"Yeah, I know," he hums confidently, "I know you can't resist me, Solar Lee."

Rolling her eyes, she lays her head on his shoulder with a sigh, "I hate to say you're right but — you're right."


106:00 | if only it were an insect

"Solar, answer your fucking phone!" Chaerin abruptly screeches in annoyance, "it's been going off for half an hour, my enjoyment of this movie has deflated!"

The siblings all sigh at how dramatic their sister continued to be. No matter what age she was, she remained to be one big, overreacting toddler. Though it was mentally exhausting, at the end of the day they loved her for it.

Rising from her seat in the theatre (there's room for these things in mansions, especially in the castle,) she walks across the room where everyone left their phones on the tables in the back. They were next to the popcorn and cotton candy machines, laying practically in a big pile. It was easy to spot hers since it was angrily vibrating, her ringtone of an iKON song blasting everyone's ears and making it rather hard to focus on the movie.

"Sorry~!" She apologizes to her siblings, before jogging out of the theatre. Her parents absence that day made it easy for her to answer without a worry. As soon as she saw the name on the screen, there was no one stopping her.

Her feet are quick, catapulting onto the sofa in the living room. Pursing her lips to try to hold in her excitement, she slides her thumb across the screen.

"Kim Jinhwan!" She screams like a child at Disneyland for the first time, her attempt at holding anything in dramatically failing. Her hand was trembling as it held her phone, and she could only bask in the fluttery feeling she got as she saw his face appear on her screen.

It was around early morning in Korea since the time difference was so drastic. The caramel boy had obviously just woken up from what had probably been an extremely long nap to fight the jet lag. His eyes look exhausted still, his hair a mess atop his head, but still, he found a way to glisten. As soon as their eyes meet through the screen, he was smiling as if he didn't have a care in the world. It was what love tended to do to a silly guy like him.

"Soo," he boasts in awe, just taking in the beauty of his girlfriend, "I miss you, how are you doing? Is everything going okay so far?"

At the questions he was asking, you could tell how light they were. He wasn't expecting for anything bad to happen, because Jinhwan had no idea she had just made up with Sunwoo — completely unplanned — and found out that there was someone still after her who knew her exact location. Why would he assume any of those things would happen? This was supposed to be her time to getaway, have a peaceful break with her family. It was disappointing it was turning out to be the exact opposite. What did she have to do to finally catch a break?

The last thing she wanted in the end was dragging her family into this. She just came back, and she was already putting their lives in danger. In that sense, she needed Sunwoo and his gang. They were the only group of people close to her that she knew would be able to protect her, to help her. She didn't have any other options. No matter how dangerous they were, dangerous was what she needed. The only problem in the end was the tendency that rose when her and Sunwoo were together.

It was going to be a big problem.

"It's great," she clears her throat, "yeah, we're just messing around and hanging out, it's like nothing changed except it has — you know, for the better," she sends him a small smile, "but I miss you, too!"

Jinhwan stares at her through the screen for a long moment. She knew if he was here in person he might see right through her. But Solar was getting better as throwing on a fake smile after all this. And he wasn't here to shake her shoulders and make her peer into those pretty irises of his, so she was in the clear.

It was better if he didn't know. She didn't mean to keep hiding things from him, but he was busy. He had his own life to live without being so concerned a about her own. She had no right to constantly bombard him, no matter if they were together or not.

"Aw, I'm happy to hear that, baby," he hums, and she admired the familiar background as he was moving around his room. She couldn't lie, she missed the safety of being in that bed of his. "Everyone misses you here, the company feels empty, but we started preparing for our next comeback."

"Oh, I'm sure," she nods, "but your next comeback, huh? Hanbin hasn't sent me any songs yet."

He flopped onto his bed, laying down with his phone above him. A proud grin spreads over his lips before he announces, "well, actually, I've been doing a lot of the writing this time around."

Her eyes widen at that, and she could only gawk, "oh — no freaking way, Nani, and you haven't showed me anything?!"

Giggling, he pouts like a baby as he stares at her, "I will soon enough, don't be so greedy. Music is how I'm going to stay connected with you through these times."

"That's cute," she frowns, feeling an ache in her chest from how badly she wanted to touch him, "I'm going to start working on some stuff, too, as soon as I get the time."

"Send me it, I'm not Hanbin but I'm working on it."

"Can do, Jinhwan PD."

"Cutie, please, don't ever call me that."

She snorts, "why? It kinda fits you, you know like the iKON TV episode."

He sighs in dismay, staring up at his ceiling, "Soomi, no."

Suddenly, a loud smashing sound was heard through the phone. Solar could only watch in awe, trying to figure out what the loud sounds were. That was, until a loud scream was heard, "IS THAT MY BEST FRIEND? KIM FUCKING SOOMI, I FUCKING MISS YOU!"

"JUNE-YA," she yells back, trying to hold in her fits of laughter. There's a priceless look of annoyance on Jinhwan's face as he was glaring at Junhoe. "I MISS YOU TOO!"

Before the short boy could stop him, the tree had ripped the phone out of his hand. She shakes her head, glad to see they hadn't changed at all, as Junhoe had giddily ran out of his hyung's room. He's shining with joy as he looks down at the screen, "so what's good? Got any other men yet? I know dating Jinhwan must be especially boring long distance."

"Do you seriously think you're funny?!" An exasperated snap from the eldest was heard. His footsteps echo on the floor as he walked to Junhoe. "June-ya, give me the fucking phone, I will not hesitate to break a bottle over your head."

"Do you hear this?" He had ignored his hyung, looking down at the camera dramatically, "this is what he's like without you, he just yells and threatens everyone. You're the only one who can control this ahjussi."

"Junhoe, I really don't think—"

"—oh shit, he actually picked up a bottle—WAIT, NO!"

And that was the day Jinhwan threw a bottle at Junhoe.





+





The long day had eventually turned in a dark night. After she had gotten back from inevitably making up with Sunwoo and hearing about the second phase of her psycho, she had felt rather lifeless. Here she had been, thinking it was finally over — or at least wanting to think that. At first, it seemed like it was. It seemed like she was finally catching a break to just be happy with her family reunited.

Too bad something like that happening in her life was too good to be true.

Even then, the last thing she was going to do was parade any of the people in her life with her problems again. This time, she was keeping her siblings out of it, but more evidently she was keeping Jinhwan and the boys out of it, too. She wasn't even telling Jungkook (if she did talk to him anytime soon) about this occurrence. There was no need to worry any of them. She wasn't in this alone, after all, she had a literal crime boss on her side.

And even though her and Sunwoo had their hardships, she knew he would protect her at any cost. That was the perks of knowing someone most of your life.

Jinhwan had to go a few hours into their call because the guys had heavy schedules upon preparing their new EP. That was fine for her, she was always happy to hear them boast about working on music. It was what they loved, and with their talent, it always came out as promising as one would expect.

Plus, some time on her own gave her just what she needed - time to think. Though, she wasn't exactly one with a talent for catching stalkers.

About an hour later of her just helplessly staring at her wall, trying to think of who would of been connected to someone like iKON's manager, she found herself being bothered by something else. The thing was, she could actually confront this other problem.

Mi: i hope so :(

The brunette heaves out a sigh, staring down at her phone as she tries to think about what more to say. A part of her wanted to talk to Jimin about everything happening. She wanted to at least tell him she made up with Sunwoo, but she knew it wouldn't be the best idea in the end.

A sudden crackling sound causes her to snap out of her head. Her brows furrow in confusion, head jolting up as her eyes rack every which way.

What the hell was that?

It happens again, a crinkle against glass, almost like rain drops hitting the window — except said raindrops were more like rocks.

Rising to her feet, she slowly makes her way across the carpeted floor of her bedroom. Velvet curtains were draped over the massive window located on the second floor, and she grips them with her fists, pulling them back with a rapid motion.

If there was someone there, she could only imagine. What if it was the stalker? But would they really do something as minimal as throwing rocks at her window?

Her hands were shaky as she finally pulls them fully back. The night sky made it hard for her to see anything, even despite the massive street lights that surrounded the streets. She wasn't sure what to expect — what kind of note they would leave.

"Sunwoo?!" she whisper yells, eyes bulging out of their sockets as she grips onto her curtains like this was a massive shock. She probably should've thought of this outcome, but what could she say? It would've been more probable to her for it to be a note from her second stalker rather than the sight of Kim Sunwoo hanging from a tree limb and grabbing onto her window seal.

Being that any house in the Castle was one of the largest houses in all of New York — this was terrifying to see. She didn't even know how he managed to climb said tree. Sure, it was something they did as kids, but now? If he fell, he would probably break more bones than she knew the body had.

But somehow, despite this fact, Sunwoo's face remained unbothered. Through her window, his eyes only shone in the darkness, and he sends her a smile, pointing to the bottom of the window inclining her to open it.

Holding a hand to her head, she sighs as she turns to look to her opened door. Even though she kept telling herself this wasn't happening, like always, it most definitely was.

Rushing to the door, she makes sure to close and lock it before making her way back over to the window. Clicking each lock on the sides, it takes more strength than it should for her to open it.

Until finally, Sunwoo was casually tumbling into her carpet floor.

"Surprise, sweetheart."

"You're fucking crazy," she states the obvious as he'd rose to his feet as if nothing happened.

Sunwoo chuckles, rolling his eyes, "sure, we all know I'm crazy for you."

Groaning as his idiocy, she closes and locks the window again. Turning back to him, Solar crosses her arms over her chest, "so, why are you here?"

Just because they made up earlier — completely unplanned — and he had helped her with finding her stalker — didn't mean he could just nosy up to her bedroom. At the end of the day, she still had a boyfriend.

"I'm here to tell you some new adjustments," he hums, padding across the carpet and gracefully falling onto her mattress. Unimpressed, the brunette remains in her position, staring at him impatiently. Noticing she didn't seem to be in the mood, he sighs, "I'm having some of the guys keep watch at night, but I thought I'd give you a more personal visit."

"That's nice," she nods, "but the personal visit isn't necessary."

"Don't be stubborn, did you not explain that these people literally broke in your apartment and watched you sleep?" He raises a brow inquisitively, "Shouldn't you feel more comforted with me at your side?"

As much as she didn't want to admit it, he had a point.

"But that was still different, there's no way they could break in here with the sort of security this place has," she corrects him, "We live in the fucking Castle, Sunwoo."

Just when she thought he was done, he shakes his head, holding up a finger as if to say wait, "however, they still managed to find a way in the Castle gates, no? Who knows what they could've been doing outside your house those nights? They were probably planting something, looking for a way inside, anything."

He was smart. She had to face it, Sunwoo was above her when it came to things like this. This is what she got for being so close with a literal criminal.

"What do you think?" She asks hesitantly, curious of his thoughts on this since he knew so much.

He's silent for a minute, sitting up straight and letting his eyes rake over the bedroom. The way his expression became more serious than usual was unsettling. She tries to not think so hard about what was going through his head as she finally sits on the mattress beside him, still keeping her distance nonetheless.

Closing her eyes, Solar tried to take deep breathes. She needed to look on the bright side of this. Even though this entire thing wasn't over, even though her career and job was at risk in more ways than one, she had people that would help her and take care of this. She wasn't in this alone, now she had professionals.

"Sol," Sunwoo's deep tone takes her out of her thoughts. Her eyes flutter as they look to him, but he wasn't looking back at her.

Confused, she tries to follow his gaze, assuming he was gonna ask her about something silly. But it looked like he was staring at nothing.

"What?" She mutters blankly, "what are you staring at?"

"Sol, has that always been there?"

More lost than ever now, she nudges his shoulder like a whiney child, "Sunu, you're gonna have to be more specific, I have no fucking idea what you're talking about. It's been five years since you've been in this room, you know, some things change over time."

"No, princess," he exhales deeply at how slow she tended to be. His boots sink into the plush carpet as he walks to the corner of the room, just by her window, but far up by the ceiling. He didn't have to stand on his tip toes since he was almost a mere 6'0, and with two fingers, he pricks something off the wall.

When he did so, a rapid beeping filled the room. Solar jumped from shock, covering her mouth with her hands as she gawks, "what the fuck is that?!"

Shaking his head is disbelief, he chunks it to the ground, before stomping on it with his boot repeatedly until the beeping sound went out. Once he knew it was broken, he looked back up to her.

"A bug, Sol, it was a bug." But he didn't mean an insect.

They had found a way in, hadn't they?

"You've got to be fucking kidding me."


107:00 | missing soomi club

"How are you doing, Soomi?!" Donghyuk was the first to ask in excitement, his face only being slightly shown in the corner of the FaceTime call.

The brunette girl, in reality, was feeling more stressed than ever. It had never been this bad before. At first, it was only her in danger along with the boys. The difference was, they had all been aware of the threat, and it was no joke that any of them could easily defend themselves.

But now she had done something she never wanted to do. She was putting her entire family in danger, who were all unaware of the situation at hand. Telling them would only make matters worse, not to mention make her feel more awful. The thought that she was risking her poor Yein's life broke her heart into a million pieces.

"Sol stop," Sunwoo's words repeated in her head from last night, "I can tell you're doing that thing were you think too hard, but I promise you, it'll all be okay. I won't let a thing happen to you or anyone else, alright?"

A promise from someone like Sunwoo was also no joke. She believed he would do everything in his power to keep them safe. The thing was, she was worried what that would be — what this would escalate to.

And not to mention, who would get caught in the crossfire.

Knowing that this person seemed to have some weird way into her home made her feel nauseous. If Sunwoo hadn't been comforting her most of the night, she wasn't sure she would've gotten a mere minute of sleep.

Really, that was all she did get.

The boy had left in the morning before any of the family found out he was there, they both knew it would be better that way. All she wanted was for none of them to find out about what was going on, the only ones who needed to know was Sunwoo and his members — and well, maybe Hee.

Though she wouldn't say it, she also preferred if her siblings were unaware how close she was to him again.

"I'm great," she clears her throat, peering down at the phone screen and forcing a grin on her face, "feeling as lively as ever here in the big city."

Jinhwan was staring at her weird. It caused her to swallow a lump in her throat, hoping he wasn't going to ask the obvious.

"Aw, I'm so happy that everything is going well for you there," Yun boasts, and she sends him a gracious smile in response.

But her boyfriend was still eyeing her in wonder. He was clearly getting a little curious of her overly bright state. As hard as she was trying, it was becoming difficult for her to mask all the worry she was feeling.

But she swore she was going to keep them out of it. They were preparing a new album, having to practice practically 24/7. The last thing they needed was for her to parade them with this again. She knew Jinhwan would want to jump on a plane immediately if he heard about it — and she wouldn't be able to stop him.

"You know, this dweeb never apologized for literally throwing a bottle at me!" Junhoe's voice was heard calling in the background, "I have a fucking bruise, Soo, a god damn bruise!"

His joking attitude was exactly what she needed. It always took her mind of her worries, which was why she loved her beloved Junhoe.

"Wow," she hums, raising a brow at the annoyed Jinhwan glaring somewhere behind him (most likely at the tree boy,) "that's dirty, Nani, why would you do him like that?"

"He's annoying."

"I'm what?!" A gasp of shock is heard, until he came into view right behind the eldest, "the fact you have the audacity to say such words to me, fiend, like I can't put you in a box and duct tape the top."

"See?" Jinhwan heaves out a deep breathe, rubbing his temples as he whines to his girlfriend, "he's so much worse without you here."

"Oh — and you're not? Every single day, all you hear from him is oh, I miss Soomi, I wonder what Soomi's doing, I really wish I could stick my—"

And that was the day Junhoe got hit with a controller — via Yunhyeong.





*

Meanwhile, thousands of miles away sat seven boys. Drinks were in their hands, and laughter left their lips as they all sat in a large circle on the wooden floor of their apartment living room. An idiotic cartoon played from the TV, but no one was watching it as they were too busy messing around, in their own worlds.

There was a feeling of peace that resided in the iKON dorm, one they hadn't gotten to feel in a while. For once, everything seemed to be going well. They had a big comeback coming up, and finally, nothing was threatening them or anyone they cared about anymore — at least they thought.

"Dude, it feels empty without Soo here," Bobby is muttering as he chugs another portion of the bottle.

Everyone looks around, but eventually their eyes land on their poor eldest. The caramel boy looked rather dead inside even though everything was so seemingly great, his drink in hand was nearly empty, but his head was full of millions of thoughts he couldn't get rid of. It might be the alcohol speaking by that point, but he felt like there was something a little off about Soomi. He couldn't quite pinpoint it because it was just through a phone screen, but she didn't seem as sincere as she once had when she claimed everything was amazing.

Maybe he was just reading too far into it. After all, what could really be troubling her there that she wouldn't want to tell him?

"Look at this poor loser," Junhoe snorts, finishing off his own bottle and reaching for another one, "even with everything great happening, he still looks like a lonely fuck."

"Do you genuinely think you're funny?" Jinhwan snaps in annoyance, clearly not in the mood for any jokes.

The others snicker at how angry he got so quickly. Everyone knew that Jinhwan got rather mad about everything when he was very intoxicated. It was something Soomi had never experienced straight up because when he was with her it was different. They wondered what her thoughts would be if they saw how nitpicky he was being now.

Junhoe, however, was unbothered as he shrugs, "I mean, yeah, I think I'm fucking hilarious."

The small boy only heaves out a sigh of disbelief.

"Hyung, chill out, it's okay," Donghyuk tries to assure him to ease the setting, "Junhoe is just being Junhoe, we know you miss Soomi, we all do."

But that wasn't the entire problem.

The only one not looking a little worried about their hyung and his bitter attitude was the maknae. Chanwoo looked like he was stressed — but his was for a completely different reason. It was one he wasn't sure what to do about.

Sometimes, you hear or see things you probably weren't meant to see. It can either be a blessing — or in this case — an absolute curse. Before you knew it, you become unsure what to do with the new information you're given, to the point were it eggs through your veins.

That appeared to be what he was going through.

Every time he looked at his particular friend, he recalled it. It was almost traumatizing, he knew that wasn't supposed to happen that night. He knew there was so many problems with it.

Worst of all, he knew that he was the only one out of all of them who was fully aware of what went on.

"She'll be back before you know it," Yunhyeong sighs, "though there sure is an odd void without her in the meantime."

"I miss her, too," Junhoe suddenly admits, "I don't have anyone to joke with anymore, all of you are fucking boring."

"Way to ruin the empathetic mood," Hanbin looks to him in disappointment.

Like always, Koo Junhoe just didn't give a fuck.

Chanwoo wondered if he would still remain that way if he knew what he knew.

"You're right," Jinhwan nods, telling himself that whatever he figured was going on with her was probably just him being stupid. "She'll be back soon."


108:00 | sweetheart

A deep exhale leaves the brunette's lips, strands of her dark hair falling in her face as her head peers downward, eyes squinted in concentration. The pen in her hand scribbles angrily across the journal in her hands, that same journal she had always used. It wasn't a diary, though, it was her song writing journal.

In a sense, it could be seen as a diary. But Solar liked to write about her life in a different way than most people — through music. She was lucky she was talented enough to be able to turn her world into songs.

She wrote a lot of songs about people in her life, people she had encountered, people who impacted her whether it was negatively or positively. A lot of her songs were meant for someone, but she never told that person.

Basically as soon as she got to Korea and met Jungkook, every melody she seemed to write was aimed at him. It was like what Hanbin suggested that day at the studio, how she should have a muse. She supposed the entire time she always had one, she just never thought about it in that sense.

Every bright song was meant for him, every song that explained the meaning of love, of finding yourself in someone else. All of them were for him, he was the story behind the music. As she sat now, pen in hand, on her bay window, she wondered if he knew that.

She felt bad. She would always feel bad. No matter how many times Jimin tried to cheer her up, insisted he was doing okay, she was still going to feel bad. In the end, Jungkook hadn't done anything wrong. He was just a scared boy who didn't want to put the girl he loved's life at risk. Even though the way he did it wasn't necessarily the best way it could've been solved, she didn't blame him for it — not one bit.

The truth would always be that her and Jungkook had stupid chemistry. They didn't get along like her and Sunwoo did, which is like childhood friends who can read each other inside out. But they also didn't go like her and Jinhwan did, which was love at first sight, awestruck moments that make you feel like your dreaming. Her and Jungkook got along like best friends, they could talk about anything and everything. She wasn't afraid to tell him things thinking he'll get mad or be upset.

Sure, Jungkook had always been competitive and jealous. He always schemed to make sure she understood how much he loved her. But he wouldn't ever hurt anyone. He was a jokester, he could laugh at his mistakes, and he had a big heart whether he would admit it or not. Her love for him would always be different than the way she loved Jinhwan, and although she was so positive that the one she felt for Nani was what she was looking for, why did she still feel this hole in her chest?

Pulling her phone out of her back pocket, she wondered where Sunwoo was. He was taking longer than usual.

There's a dull expression that lingers on Solar's face as she stares at the phone screen. It was nice to talk to Jimin, he always had such genuine insight on everything. She didn't know what she would do if he wouldn't of come back into her life. Though she had Junhoe, she knew she could never go to him with these things. He didn't have the same personal experience, he hadn't been there to see everything with her.

The hard truth was, her beloved best friend was just one of a kind.

Sunu: here, hurry up

Pursing her lips, she rises from her bed. Shutting off the phone, it's shoved into her back pocket as she grabs her bag, making her way out of her room.

Though she was an adult now, being back at home and leaving so late still made her feel a little anxious. It wasn't like she was really sneaking out technically, she was free to do what she wanted now. Solar knew her parents weren't going to get angry and try to control her — you know, unless they knew she was going somewhere with Sunwoo.

Which was why she was extra quiet when she slipped out the front door. The last thing she needed was having to explain to her siblings or parents why she was hanging out with a crime boss.

The dark night is menacing as she walks down the sidewalk. Cold air nips at her skin, leaving goosebumps as she pads towards the vehicle.

"Hello gorgeous!" Hyunjoon is the first to greet her as she climbs in the front. The others follow suit, and she returns the greeting.

"Didn't think we would ever see you again after what happened at Cane's," Sangyeon is humming somewhere in one of the backseats, "it seemed serious."

Scratching her head, it was clear she felt a little awkward about the subject. "I guess I was having a bit of a moment," she shrugs, trying to laugh it off lightly. Though it was a lie— she had a bigger motive when she did what she did.

The brunette was trying to get comfortable in the leather seat when she felt the fingertips of Sunwoo against her thighs. Tingles rush through her body despite her sending him an annoyed glare, "everyone knows Solar could never let go of me," he glances over to meet her fiery gaze, but only winks, "right, sweetheart?"

"You're a pain in the ass," she states, causing the others to laugh.

Sunwoo's mental was never tainted though. He only chuckles a bit, before focusing back on the road. He appeared to know he was a pain, too. But they both knew at the same time, he helped her more than other people could.

It wasn't like she could've protected herself from these people. She would've never known they were even in town, nonetheless found a way into the castle.

And especially a way into her own home — once again — with all the security it had. They somehow managed to plant a bug, which she wouldn't never known if it wasn't for Sunwoo.

At the end of the day, he deserved a lot of thanks, even if he was violent, ignorant, and completely egotistical.

The van became silent, at least in her head. There was the sounds of the guys yelling along to the music on the radio, before Sunwoo turning it down to discuss whatever mission they were doing. However, she tuned that all out since it had nothing to do with her.

Though she was still a little curious. Sangyeon didn't usually come with them on their violent missions, he was part of the brains in the gang. When he came, it meant they were doing something with technology — like hacking or programming.

She couldn't lie, that was rather worrisome.

Trying to keep a clear head, she distracted herself by replying to Jinhwan's texts. He texted her more than she could keep up. After what her and Chim talked about, she was feeling weird whenever she saw his name come on her screen.

Maybe she needed a break, but it wasn't like she had the guts to tell him that. Once again, she was left at a crossroad with her emotions.

Thinking back to Jimin's words, she could only imagine how easy it would be if it was really like he said. It was true, she always had so much to worry about that she was never able to bother with her emotions. He thought all of it was over — but to her dismay, it was only starting all over again.

With the bug Sunwoo found, she could fear what they got on it — and what else they were capable of. All she could do was cling onto Sunwoo and hope he was smarter than them.

"Here we are," Sunwoo sighs as he pulls around an alleyway. They were somewhere in the city, wedged around massive buildings. Her eyes squint as she looks around outside, but it was too dark for her to recognize any of the buildings.

From the look of the street they were just on, it seemed like they were in the business part of the city. It was where all the company buildings were located at.

Though she wasn't sure which one they where at.

"Do you have everything you need?" The leader had turned to look at one of them.

"Yeah, ready to hack into the security system," Sangyeon is muttering, busy on the laptop that sat in his lap, "just need to be closer to get a better signal."

Before anyone made a move, one of the boys rolled down the window. Before she could process what they were doing, the sound of a suppressed gun went off, so slight only they could hear.

"Cameras gone, this door is safe."

Exhaling, Sunwoo is already opening his door, "let's go before the guards loop around this way, don't have much time."

She was feeling more anxious than ever as she watched them pour out. Was this really safer for her than her own house? What the hell would she do if she got caught here?

Sometimes she questioned why she listened to Sunwoo.

And which company building is this? She could only hope it was some unknown one not big enough to be in the Castle. Solar would kill him if he was pulling another act against one of her friend's family's. Whether it was his fault or not, he still had a say in his actions. She would not forgive him again, even if he did the right thing in the end.

It wasn't like he let Kevin's sister go because he felt like he should to do the right thing, anyway. He only did it for her. 

As much as she didn't want to admit it, Sunwoo really was a bad person.

Yet here she was, sitting in his car while he robbed God knows what from an unknown company. What choice did she have? He was the only one with the power and strength to protect her. 

In the end, she still had a soft spot for him.
