Hee: all you do is lie

Groaning, her head slams into the leather seat as she breathes out in frustration. She had told Hee she was still hanging out with Sunwoo, and he was really the only one who knew. But she didn't know if she should tell him the reason, even if it was making him assume bad things.

She didn't want to get more people involved in this mess than she already had.

In that moment, she wished an angel would come from the skies and just tell her what to do. This was turning into a lying nightmare and making her feel like such a bad person. Did she tell everyone and put them in danger or continue to make up stories and lie? Which was the right route to take? How was she supposed to know when they both seemed so rough?

"Hello, sweetheart," there were only two people on Earth who had ever called her that. They, were both similar in more ways than one, but there was an easy way to divide them both. One had been her highschool love, one adored her more than anything, one would protect her at all costs. The other hated her, the other was a monster, a monster who had always wanted her dead.

And at the sound of the voice she just heard, she knew which one it was.

Closing her eyes for a brief moment, Sunwoo's words repeat in her head from all that time ago, "my father's back in town."

Immediately, her reflexes kicked in, hand gripping the door handle. With all her might she tried to open it, only to hear a click from the drivers side. 

Through the darkness, she could almost see his menacing stare. "H-How?" she's muttering, knowing that Sunwoo wouldn't have done something as idiotic as leaving the car unlocked.

The old man turns on the light, just to dingle a set of keys in her face. "You think I wouldn't have a set of keys for myself?" he laughs, "I can see you're as smart as ever, Solar."

His features were so close to Sunwoo's, as father and son tended to be sometimes. It was sickening because of the feelings she had for one compared to the other. His smirk is devious as he clears his throat, "doesn't seem you have much to say, hm? That means I can do this quickly."

Her eyes widen a bit, "Excuse me, do what quickly?" she hisses, "what could you possibly want? I'm not going to be here for long, just leave me alone."

"what do I want?" he repeats in a mocking manner, "Let me think, you're only doing the same thing you've always done, making my son fail at the very reason he exists - to do my dirty work." His hand reaches out, fingers rough as they graze across the skin on her cheek, "and you see, Solar, that's a big problem for me. Dear Sunwoo was doing so well after you left, I can't have you back and ruining his mentality all over again."

"How about you actually run your company instead of blackmailing and trashing oth-" the brunette was cut off by a harsh slap to her cheek. A squeal leaves her lips as she holds a hand to her cheek to ease the immense pain. Wincing, her eyes narrow at the man in the driver's seat, "you sick motherfucking piece of-"

"Now, now," he shakes his head, "that's not what you should say to someone about to kill you."

The words hadn't fully sunk in for a few seconds. All those memories of her running away from this man, of him coming after her on so many occasions, appeared like a nuke in her brain.

She should've expected this, been more prepared. But how could she have been? There had been bigger issues.

Slowly, he pulls a gun out of his pocket. It's shining in the darkness, being plated with gold. 

Solar took that as the time.

"You bitch!"

When he wasn't looking, she pressed the unlock button, swinging open the door quicker than she ever thought she could manage. Her feet scrape against the concrete of the alleyway as she made a run for the first place she could see. 

It was the door to whatever company this was. She didn't care if she should be "sneaky" or not, she needed to get inside and find Sunwoo. 

"He's still doing his job!" she cries as she tries to stall, growing closer to the door, "just fuck off!"

"Oh, but you've already caused enough," the old man laughs, not even running after her. He was calmly walking, gun in hand, "you've caused so much trouble for me, ever since you were a kid."

Almost there.

"This was inevitable, Solar."

Gunshot.


109:00 | a new level of pain

"Passwords, files, bug planted," Sunwoo is mumbling to himself, checking off things on his mental list, "we're done," he states to his members.

"Thank God, this place is a shit hole," Eric is muttering as they all make way to the door they came out of.

Footsteps clicking against the tile floor, this was the only sound you could hear amongst them. Until Hyunjoon hums, "this was pretty boring, I didn't get to kill anyone," glancing around at all the camera's they shot out, he sighs, "they should really get some guards inside or something, it was really a s-"

"-Wait, shut up," Sunwoo holds up a hand, silencing him, "what the fuck was that?"

They didn't know what he was talking about. Sunwoo had just heard mere yelling from outside, but he didn't know who it would be other than one person. What would she be yelling at? Anxiety creeps up his spine like an angry family of ants. Despite this feeling, he remained emotionless, speeding up his walking to get to the door.

"What is it?" Eric asks in confusion.

That was when a gunshot went off.

"The fuck?!" Hyunjoon hisses.

"Oh my fucking God," Sangyeon mutters, being the first one other than Sunwoo to realize.

But the leader didn't say a word. No, he broke out into a run like an absolute mad man, throwing himself at the door to push it open. Everything in his body was screaming, in anger and agony at the same time, everything was aching. He was someone who rarely felt pain, but realizing what it had to be was tearing him apart from the inside out.

"SOLAR!" he yells as he bursts outside into the cold air. The wailing of that exact girl filled his ears, and he followed the sound to see her limp body on the ground. No, no, no, his mind whines, body crumbling as he runs to her, falling on the ground. Hurriedly, he tries to aid her body, searching for where all the blood was coming from.

He wasn't a doctor, but if he could at least stop the bleeding even a little.

The others finally step outside, gasps leaving their lips when they see him there, with a lifeless girl in his arms.

"Where the fuck is that motherfucker?" Sunwoo is angrily spouting, taking off his jacket and pushing it onto her wound, "I will fucking skin him alive and laugh as I do so, he'll fucking pay for this, for everything he's done."

"Sunwoo, calm down," Sangyeon says softly, bending down beside him. "Here, I'll tend to her wound the best I can, one of you call an ambulance," he orders the others, who eagerly nod. "Let me check her pulse."

While Eric was on the phone with 911, Hyunjoon took out his gun, walking around the area. He felt terrible for what happened, angry though it wasn't the same level as Sunwoo. Solar was dear to him, and if she didn't make it he didn't know what he would do. After just getting her back, it was too early to lose her again.

But no matter where he looked in the darkness, there wasn't a sound or sight anywhere. They all knew who had done this, but where had he gone off to so fast?

Walking back to where everyone was with Solar, Sangyeon clears his throat, "there's a pulse, but it's barely there."

Sunwoo's breathing was irregular, heaving in and out in a heavy manner. He was more angry than he had ever been in his entire life. There was nothing that compared to this. When they were teens, his father had tried to hurt her endlessly, but he was always there to stop it. No matter what, he saved her. 

But he hadn't this time after swearing to protect her.

"You sure it wasn't her stalkers or whatever?" Hyunjoon pends as a precaution.

"They don't want her dead, not this way," Sunwoo states blankly, too deep in thought to say much.

Suddenly, in the dark night, his phone lit up in his back pocket. The ringing made everyone jump from surprise at how focused they had been.

Blinking, the boy snaps himself out of his head to pull it out of his pocket. He already knew who it was, and just seeing the name on his screen made him grip the phone so hard you could hear the crack of the phone beginning to break under it. Still, he slid his finger harshly across the screen.

"Where the fuck are you? You think you can do this and just leave?!" 

"Where are my sons manners?" his father chuckles into the speaker, "I can tell you didn't enjoy my gift much."

"Show your face so I can fucking show you how much I enjoyed it," he hisses, eyes piercing around the perimeter in case the man jumped out anywhere.

A sigh is heard through the phone, "now, now, calm down, I didn't actually kill her. I shot her in a place that wouldn't end her life just quite, but I made sure it would make her cling onto it," he begins to laugh at his act. "This is a lesson for her and you that whatever you're doing needs to end," he orders, "I don't want to have to do the real thing, because we both know that I'll do it, you aren't kids anymore."

"Don't fucking touch her," Sunwoo growls, breathing in between his words from how mad he was, "you're fucking lucky I don't kill you and feed you to fucking sharks."

"We both know you can't and won't do that, stop the bluffing, son."

He was right. 

Sunwoo couldn't.

Not with the security, not with the fact his father had everything. Without him, he was left with nothing.

"Go fuck yourself, if you lay a hand on her it's over for you," he still snaps, "better hope you don't show your face again tonight, old man."





+




"Sol," a deep voice beams. Just hearing how bright the usually taunting voice sounds makes her perk up, green eyes glistening under the sunlight as she looks up to meet those eyes. "Hey, you're here," he says as he reaches her, sneakers crinkling under the concrete of the parking lot they stood on. 

She smiles up at him, before furrowing her brows as she realizes what he said. And why was he dressed like that? Not saying a word, she turns to take in her surroundings.

Upper East Side High School

"Huh?" she mutters, seeing the familiar school before her, "wait, why are we here?" she asks in confusion, turning back to Sunwoo.

His smile fades as he looks down at her from his tall stance. "What are you on about, stupid?" he hums, "We're here because we go to school, Solar - well, sometimes."

School?

She wasn't in school anymore, neither was he.

"Sunwoo, but-" she shakes her head in disbelief, turning back to him after looking at the school again.

"-Sol, you're such an idiot, how do you even forget that you attend school?" he sighs in disappointment, reaching out a poking her nose, "either way, that's just one of the reasons I love you."

Her eyes widen as she recalls what he just said. She kept waiting for him to admit this was some stupid joke, but he doesn't say a word as he pulls out a cigarette, followed by his lighter.

This is - this isn't right.

"Soomi!"

Solar screeches as her surroundings change, wood appearing under her feet and the walls of a practice room around her. It was a place she should be used to, and once was. Except for the fact that there was the words Big Hit Entertainment plastered on the walls.

"Soomi, are you alright?"

She felt like her head was about to implode. Holding a hand to massage her temple, she turns to follow the voice she was hearing. Her eyes land on the small, innocent looking boy, and the sight of him that way nearly makes her fall over.

Coughing from shock, she bends over and puts her hands on her knees, trying to calm down. But whatever was happening to her right now was driving her insane.

"Soo," Jungkook gasps, rushing over to her side and patting her back, "hey, hey, you're okay," he soothes her. She finally manages to stand up straight, breathing a bit crazily as she looks at him again.

There was a vast difference between how she had just seen Sunwoo versus how she was seeing Jungkook. Sunwoo back in highschool hadn't changed much appearance wise, he just got more tattoos and piercings. But the boy had matured early on.

Jungkook, on the other hand, was a different story. He looked like a baby when they met, and it had been a while before he slowly matured into the older physique he was now. But back then, he had a pair of obvious bunny teeth, a massive baby face, and big doe eyes that looked as if they belonged to a toddler begging for candy.

It had been a long time since she'd seen him that way. And with how much she'd been missing him, it caused her a bit of an issue.

"Kook," she mumbles as she peers at him.

Being that it was all she had said, he blinks at her, "Yeah, I'm right here, Mi," he waves a hand in front of her face, "why are you looking at me like that?"

She didn't know what to say. But her reaction was pending to be a lot different than what it was with Sunwoo.

"I missed you, I guess."

There was a second before he burst into giggles. Those little baby giggles were he smiled from ear to ear, bunny teeth protruding. "Wow, I was only gone for a minute to get water," he hums, "I feel loved, Mi."

Before she could say another word, he steps across the wooden floor to engulf her in a hug. His body felt so warm that she relaxed into it, slowly laying her head against his chest. Though her arms didn't move, she was still lost and hesitant at what was going on.

"I love you, Mi."

He pulls away, and just as she was staring at those doe eyes of his again - he was gone.

"Jung-" she starts to call out, before everything around her was different.

Green eyes blinking rapidly, her vision bolts from side to side. "Huh?" she whispers, "my apartment lobby?"

Whatever this was, it was the weirdest thing she had ever experienced. She hoped it was over soon because it was starting to make her head pound. Why was she flipping through years and phases of her life like this? When was it going to end?

"Soo," as soon as she heard that voice, she knew it had to be over soon. A lump forms in her throat as she dares herself to turn around at the sound of that voice, that voice that did so many things to her.

"Soo, hey, I need to talk to you," Jinhwan's voice is closer this time, until she could feel his presence right behind her.

Did she turn around? Or did she start running for the door like a crazy lady to try to get the fuck out of whatever this was?

She couldn't help herself.

"Um," she blinks, recalling what was going on as she turns to him. It was like she was reliving things that happened to her before, previous memories stored in the back of her mind, but yet she had the power to change them. 

His eyes are hopeful as they look at her. Breaking their contact, she looks around the lobby, "are you sure? There's a lot of people."

He looks around too, before shrugging it off, "we're fine, no paparazzi can get in here."

Not saying anything, she nods.

"So where were you off to?" he suddenly asks, "you're not going to the bar again, are you? I keep making you promise not to, but you keep breaking them."

Right, she remembers with a mental sigh, this is what we used to go through. Thinking back to all her old issues she made him deal with made her feel terrible.

"Sorry," she frowns, "I'm an idiot."

The black haired boy chuckles, looking down at his feet for a second. When he does, those black strands fall in his face, even though they were so nicely parted in the middle. Soomi recalled how much she always liked that hairstyle on him. 

Hands shoved in his hoodie, he speaks up, "you're not an idiot, you just have a lot going on," he meets her eyes again, "but I want to help you."

"Huh?" she blurts, not really remembering what happened.

"Soo, I really like you, I know it's stupid because it hasn't been long but - you're just - you," he stumbles, sounding a bit confused with himself, "I think I lo-"

"Solar!"

"Who's that?" Jinhwan cuts himself off, eyes narrowing at he looks behind her to the boy running at them.

Eyes bulging, she looks away from Jinhwan to see what he was talking about. There was no way, surely she heard that voice in her head. 

But there he was. 

Sunwoo was dressed like he was when she last saw him. Leather jacket, white t-shirt, covered in tattoos, he was the typical bad boy everyone loved. Every time their eyes would meet, there would be a sparkle in those brown orbs. Even now, she loved to see it, it made her see a different aspect of him, one that was sweet, caring, capable of love. It was the innocent part of him.

However, when their eyes meet, there is no sparkle.

"S-Sunwoo?" she stutters, not understanding why he instead looked rather angry, "what's wrong with you?"

"What's wrong with me?" he retorts, "what's wrong is that you're a fucking lying whore, Solar, an absolute waste of my time that I could've used for working instead."

What?

"The hell?" she gawks. Not that it was a shocker to hear him say that, but it was a shocker to hear him say that towards her. "What are you even talking about? Why are you here?!"

"He's right," Jinhwan hums, causing her head to whip back at him. His eyes are burning with anger, just like Sunwoo's. Instead of his previous look, his caramel hair was back, long and falling in his face. He was that pretty boy she always bragged about, alright, but he was looking at her with disgust. "I should've never bothered with you."

"Nani," she mutters in disbelief, "you don't mean that."

"Oh, he means it," out of everything, she didn't expect him to be there too.

Turning around, Jungkook was standing there in all his glory. Long brown hair was past his ears, earrings shining in the light, but no bunny tooth smile was in sight. Somehow, he managed to hurt her the most when he hissed, "Wanting to marry you was the dumbest thing I ever did, good thing I sent that letter."

"But you said you regretted it?!" she corrects, "you were framed, Jungkook!"

He snorts, "that's what you think, I just needed to get away from you."

"Fucking fake," Sunwoo snaps from where he was, "you deserve to pay, Solar, all of your lies were going to catch up to you eventually."

"Cheating whore."

"How are you not ashamed?"

"Guys," she winces as they close in on her, circling around her with fury in their eyes. All their eyes that she had only ever sensed love in. Never had she seen them like this, never had she thought they would say these things to her. 

Her head begins to throb, an agonizing pain she had never experienced. Holding a hand, she tries to ease it somehow, trying to breathe normally. But it felt like her body was fighting her.

Falling to her knees, the three boys were only glaring down at her, throwing more insults. "Please, I'm sorry," she whines to them, but her voice was so soft they probably didn't even hear her. "Make it stop," she cries, burying her face in her hands.

Why was this happening? How did she make it go away?

Suddenly, it felt like a truck hit her stomach. Screaming in pain, she looks down to see insane amounts of blood pouring out from a wound. Unaware of where it came from, she holds her hand over it, trying to stop the bleeding. 

"Fuck!" she screeches as her throat begins to hurt. Blood splatters everywhere as she pukes it up, her body seeming to retaliate against her. Everything inside of it was fighting her as she coughs, begging for it to end.

"Pay, Solar, pay for what you've done."



110:00 | sweet sunwoo

"No, no, no!" the five boys in the hospital room had been rudely awoken from whimpers. They belonged to the girl they were all here for, and hearing her say something even if it was a scared plead caused them all to bolt to their feet.

Thinking she might be awake, to their dismay she wasn't. Obviously, she was alive on the bright side. However, when they brought Solar in the ambulance she was unconscious. She'd stayed that way for hours, and Sunwoo figured he should call her best friends, telling the other guys to go home. When she had woken up, the doctor's hadn't allowed anyone to talk to her, as apparently she hadn't been fully conscious. They'd said she needed more rest before her body and mind were ready, so they'd given her medicine to put her into what was supposed to be a sweet slumber.

But seeing the way she was rolling around, a look of pain on her face, her eyes squeezed shut, it appeared as if she was having the opposite.

"What the hell did they give her?" Chanhee is angry, clenching his fists at his sides, "She's having some sort of nightmare, this is not fucking okay!" 

No one said a word in response. They all knew to keep their mouths shut during this time. Chanhee was mad, more mad than anyone had ever seen him. And even Sunwoo wasn't trying to mess with him, mainly because of the guilt he felt for what happened.

He knew her friends weren't happy with him about it. Sunwoo was never going to live this out.

"Please," Solar whines in her sleep. There's weak cries leaving her lips, even though her eyes were closed. Whatever she was seeing must be terrifying her.

Unable to see anymore, Chanhee spins on his heel, "I am getting a doctor."

The room is left in silence after he leaves. No one knew what to say, they could only watch their poor friend in worry.

"I'm sorry!" she screeched, and hearing the way she cried out was too much for Sunwoo.

Before anyone could stop him, he stepped forward by her bed. "Sol, Solar," he calls out, gripping her hand with one hand and reaching up to caress her face with the other, "Sweetheart, wake up, it's a bad dream - just a bad dream."

The others in the room exchange glances of shock. Yeah, they were pissed at Sunwoo for what he was already getting her into, but they had to admit they had never seen him this way - actually nurturing.

"Sol, princess," he continues to call, "I'm here, okay, I'm not leaving you without supervision ever, you'll be okay."

She didn't move, other than the occasional whines that kept leaving her mouth.

At the sight of her this way, he could only continue to blame herself. How was he gonna get over this? He failed, he was a failure. The girl he loved was back, the girl he lost, only for him to let her down when she needed him the most. He was pathetic.

"Solar," his voice cracks, and so the others couldn't see the tears welling in his eyes, he buried his face in her side, sitting on his knees, "I'm sorry, I'm so fucking sorry."

The door had opened, and in came a frustrated Chanhee. There was no doctor behind him, hence his extreme anger. He's about to speak up about how terrible the service at this hospital was, when he saw the scene going on.

Knitting his brows together, he looks to his friends for an explanation. They all send him blank eyes, motioning for him to just keep quiet.

"Wake up, I promise I won't let you down again."

"So, Kim Sunwoo sheds tears now, huh?" 

The soft fingers had lifted his face up from under his chin. Brown eyes bulging, he hurriedly reaches up to wipe any evidence off his face. Solar's breathing was still irregular from her nightmare, droplets of sweat dripping down the sides of her face. She looked like a mess, but yet when Sunwoo met her eyes he grinned from ear to ear.

There's the sparkle.

"Fucking hell, Solar," he gasps, leaning forward to engulf her in his arms, "I'm so fucking sorry, I'm so so fucking sorry."

Wrapping her arms around his neck, she inhales the scent of smoke and cologne. Through it all, she was happy to smell it. She was happy he was here.

"It's okay, it isn't your fault," she assures him.

Sunwoo pulls away when she says that, blinking at her in confusion. They both knew it was his fault, and he didn't feel like fighting her on that fact. However, he was lost at the fact she was just having some sort of horrible experience seconds ago, and not to mention, she got shot. How was she still so bright?

"Does it hurt?" it was the first time she had ever heard him sound so lost and innocent in a long time. She guessed she was acting like she was fine, only because she didn't want to worry anyone. 

Trying to show everyone she was fine, she tries to sit up straight, "yeah, I'm fi-fuck!"

"Lay back down, you idiot!" Hee hisses as he rushes to her side to force her to do so, "You're not supposed to be casually moving, we know you're not fine."

Pouting at her failed attempt, she sighs, "okay, yeah, it hurts."

Looking back at the dear boy, there was a sadness welled in his eyes again. He was feeling guilty and everyone knew he deserved to.

"Did you have a nightmare?" Chanhee speaks up, "I told them to not fucking give you that weird medicine."

She winced at his question. The vivid images of her dream flooded back into her brain. It felt so real even though it obviously wasn't. She wasn't sure if she would ever forget their words, the glaring hatred in their eyes as they berated her. It made her feel so worthless, so hopeless, but most of all, like such an awful person.

Was she really wrong for everything?

Was it really her fault?

Did she deserve to hear those things?

"Uh, something like that," she mutters, "but it wasn't real, so it doesn't matter."

It was a lie, but a good one at that.

"Okay, I'm gonna go try to find a doctor for the 90th time so we can figure out what to do," Chanhee explains, changing the subject because he could tell she didn't want to talk about it, "you'll have to take meds, and they'll probably keep you here for a while to make sure they got all the bullet pieces out."

"Yeah, I know."

"Alright, hold up."







+






"Sunwoo, you really don't have to-"

"-I'm staying," the boy snaps matter-of-factly, "you aren't getting out of my sight again."

There was a part of her that felt special to hear this, a part of her that was honored this cold, hateful boy only wanted to protect and care for her. But there was another part that felt like a problem, like a kid who needed to be babysat because they were incapable of taking care of themselves. She felt like a hated job, an annoying obligation.

"Don't overthink," he notices that look on her face, "I just want to keep you safe because I love you, Sol."

Her eyes soften when they meet his. That soft spot she had for him sometimes became her enemy. But he really could be a sweetheart when he wanted to be.

"Alright, that's wonderful, but can I at least talk to my best friend alone for a few moments?" Chanhee demanded more than he asked. Sunwoo sighs in annoyance as he glares at the boy who somehow managed to ruin so many things. 

As bad as he wanted to say no, he nods, "sure."

Chanhee waits until he leaves before sitting on the edge of her bed. A deep breathe leaves his lips, it was one she recognized well. That was the breathe he would take before he gave her a lecture.

Finally, he clears his throat, "Are you going to call Jinhwan?"

She didn't have to think to answer. "No."

Expecting that exact word, he groans, "Solar, I try my best to support you in all your idiocy but is that really an okay thing to do? You have a boyfriend but here you are, doing that weird shit you and Sunwoo do."

She frowns at what he said, "what weird shit?!"

Rolling his eyes, the flower boy scoffs, "oh, you know what I'm talking about, you guys do that 'half-ass flirting' garbage that makes me want to fucking die."

"We don't!" she cries defensively, before her voice grows quiet, "I wouldn't do that."

He raises a brow, "would you?"

"Why is this what we're talking about?"

"What about Jungkook?"

Solar groans, not wanting to hear that name at all right now. Burying her face in her hands, she tried to push him to the back of her mind again. She wasn't going to admit how she missed him, and how she wasn't sure if she would want Jinhwan or him here more.

"I read through your texts, I know you still have a thing for him, stupid," Hee suddenly blurts, completely unbothered despite the shocked look that takes over her face.

"You fucking what?!" she screeches, "that's not okay, why would you do that?!"

He shrugs, "They gave me your phone for safe-keeping, and you still have the same pass code," he explains, "I've just felt suspicious since your home-screen is literally a photo of both of you from God knows how long ago."

"It's hard to explain, okay?" she insists.

"No need, I read through texts, remember?" he hums, only causing her to glare at him. Really, it wasn't a huge deal in the end since it was Hee out of all people. "Maybe you should call him instead."

"Huh?!" her eyes fall out of her sockets as she gawks at her best friend, "are you insane?"

"Nope, but if you don't want to tell your boyfriend that you're lowkey ghosting, you might as well inform your ex-fiance you still miss."

"Don't say it like that," she pouts, breaking their eye contact to stare at something else. She was tired of this boy reading through her so easily.

He exhales deeply, seeing she was getting fed up with his bothersome attitude, "Sol, just don't keep this from everyone you care about. You've gotta figure out what you're feeling."

"Yeah," she whispers, "yeah, I know."


111:00 | are you high

Explaining to her family what happened turned out to be one of the most difficult things she had ever done. She tried her best to dial it down, trying to get away with a soft lie.

"I got hit really hard," she was saying to them days later when she came home from the hospital to her freaked out family who had only received a text that read 'something serious happened, be home when I can.' To say the least, they were pretty pissed about the scare she had given them. Sol felt bad she was leaving them out on so much, but it was for the best. "You know, accidents happen."

They had all stared at her in hesitance. "Sweetie, I know you're an adult now - but we still worry about you," her mother frowns as she lays a hand on her shoulder, "keep us more informed, okay?"

Nodding, she sends her mother a warm smile to help assure her she was fine, "course, mother."

Her mom gave her a hug before allowing her to go off to her room. It took everything in her body to take her things up the stairs, trying to act as if she had just merely been hit in the stomach instead of shot. 

The hard truth was that it fucking hurt, bad. She had pain medications, and she was actually thrilled to take them once she got in her room. 

As soon as she reached the top of the stairs, she exhaled, stopping how she had been walking because it was making the pain worsen. Solar limped the rest of the way to her room, wanting badly to just go to sleep.

"Can I go see sissy, please?!" Yein had been whining downstairs, tugging on Chaerin's arm as she begged. The girl was searching for a 'yes' out of someone, as their parents had strictly told them to let their big sister rest. 

The girl tried to be mature and ignore her, but she just kept tugging. Closing her eyes, she tried to keep her composure, but everyone knew Chaerin had a temper.

"No, Yein!" she yells, fed up with the kid bothering her, "You heard what they said, Solar needs rest!"

The child pouts from her sister raising her voice. Letting go of her, she scoots to the other side of the sofa to sit by herself.

You'd think Chaerin would feel bad, but she only groans as she rises to her feet, heading upstairs.

Meanwhile, Solar moans in agony as she finally enters her room, making sure to lock her door behind her. Throwing her bag on her bed that contained all her things she had at the hospital, she eagerly digs inside for the meds. Pulling them out, it was heaven when she downed a few of the pills to ease how she was hurting.

Moving her bag off her bed, she sits on the edge with a deep sigh. The doctor said it would take a few weeks for her body to heal from it, which wasn't too bad at all. She knew the pain would lessen as time went on, she would be back to normal in no time. Her recovery should be easy since she was taking time off anyway, she didn't need to tell a soul back home.

The thought reminds her of what Hee had lectured her about. She didn't know what he was trying to do with her mind when he suggested she call Jungkook instead. It wasn't like she could or would do that, they weren't even on speaking terms right now. But did she secretly want to?

Maybe.

And Jinhwan, she would never tell him what happened. Somehow, now that he was her boyfriend she was more afraid to tell him things like this. How would she ever get out the truth? She hated lying to him, and telling more lies would only make it worse in the end. On the other hand, telling him she was close with Sunwoo again would probably upset him bad. It was the last conversation she needed to have with him.

It was weird, really. A part of her kept thinking it would be easier to tell Jungkook about it all. Even though he was the one who was always selfish, the one who schemed, caused mischief, and tended to be rather competitive when it came to loving her. Though he was one who was more intense and dangerous when angry, something inside of her just felt that way.

She didn't know why.

A knock on her window cuts off her mind. Already knowing who it is, she winces as she forces herself to get up from the bed. Slowly but surely, Solar makes her way to her window, sliding it open for the boy.

She groans as she plops back onto her mattress. Sunwoo lets himself inside, closing and locking the window behind her. He pulls the curtains over, which made the room pitch black since there was no light from the street peering in. 

"How are you feeling?" is the first thing he asks.

"Pain, but I took some medication for it," she tells him weakly, not wanting to talk much.

"You need to sleep," he states, "but first, I need to check your walls and everything. You haven't been home, which makes it the perfect time to plant or do something."

The girl only mumbles an agreement, too drowsy to care. He was just trying to help anyway, and after the bug he found in her room last time, she was glad to accept it. Burying her face into the plush pillow on her queen-sized bed, it was a lot more comforting than that horrid hospital bed she'd been stuck in.

Sunwoo turns on a lamp, and kept his footsteps soft as he walks around her bedroom. Brown eyes narrowed in concentration, he inspects every inch of her room, making sure he didn't see a single thing. He said he wasn't going to let her down again, and making sure her room was safe was part of that promise.

"Seems good," he says more to himself than her, figuring she was already asleep.

Sighing, he takes out his phone to make sure the guys are keeping an eye out. With that, he sets his phone on the end table, before sitting on the bed beside her slumbering body.

It didn't matter how many times he thought it or said it, Solar was the prettiest girl he'd ever seen. A longing smile takes over his face as he peers down at her, happy to see her finally relaxing after what he'd put her through. Though he was smiling when he looked at her, there was an evident sadness in that smile.

"I'm still sorry, Sol," he admits, the guilt only continuing to torture him, "I don't deserve you, I never did."

Exhaling, he turns away from her to reach and turn off the light. With that, he lays down on the bed fully, kicking off his shoes. Sunwoo was being unlike himself, keeping his distance on the bed so she could have a lot of room to rest. Thinking she was already asleep, he didn't want to disturb her sleep with the slightest thing like his body heat.

The thing was, Solar was awake. She heard every word he had just said moments ago, and was currently having trouble contemplating it. 

Sweet Sunwoo, she thought dully, a part of me adores you just like we're kids again.

But it was different now.








+







It was 8 AM in South Korea. The morning sun was beaming like no other, which was a special occasion since it was usually so cold and cloudy. It seemed like a unique day with such rare weather, and that was turning to be true in a certain mansion located in the very city of Seoul.

The house of the Bangtan boys had been rather quiet lately. Well, they still hung out every night and made jokes, played games, but it was quiet because of the recent absence there had been. Their beloved Jungkook seemed lifeless, he hadn't done anything in what seemed like weeks. It was like every living part in his body just gave up, he was simply a walking corpse.

Some days, he found a way to smile, like when they were on camera or he got to go somewhere that was dear to him. Small things had made Jungkook find the smallest amount of happiness, and it made everyone thrilled. They didn't blame Soomi for what happened, but they just hoped that he would get over it with time.

Which is why no one expected Jeon Jungkook to bouncily enter the kitchen, especially Park Jimin, who was the only one inside of it. The blonde boy was leaning against the counter, glass of water in hand as he had woken up only moments ago. At the sight of Jungkook looking so weird, he stares for a moment in confusion.

"Hyung," the boy walks right up to him, "I want to pose an idea to you. Tell me if I'm crazy, okay?"

Jimin gradually blinks at Jungkook, trying to guess what the hell he was about to hear. But he had no fucking idea, and it worried him.

Still, he just went with it since he seemed to be in a good mood, "what is it?"

Clearing his throat, Jungkook let out a deep breathe like he was about to give a speech. "I want to go back to New York."

"You what?" Jimin was genuinely unsure if he heard him correctly.

"To see Soomi."

"You what?" he repeats again, now extremely unsure if he heard him correctly. Jimin was wondering if he even woke up in the first place, this had to be some sort of odd dream because of the conversation's he and Soo had been having. There was no way the actual Jungkook just walked up to him and said that.

"I don't know what it is," he admits hesitantly, staring down at his feet, "I've been spending this time trying to get over her, then I realized it wasn't possible. I thought a lot about what happened, but I think I might've got it all wrong."

"Are you high?"

"No, I think I'm right this time, and even if I'm not right, I don't want to give her up so easily," he shakes his head, "I mean, I fought so hard just to hand her over like nothing, what does that say about me?"

"You're high, didn't she literally tell you she loved Jinhwan? Didn't you literally say you knew she loved him?"

There was a certain tint in Jimin's voice, one that made Jungkook suspicious.

"Yeah, but I just said - what do you know?"

"Nothing," he immediately blurts, but that only made it more obvious.

"Hyung," Jungkook says in a warning tone, stepping closer to him to narrow his eyes. Jimin looks away from the maknae, refusing to make eye contact. The blonde was hoping for some kind of miracle to happen, something that would get him out of this situation. He meant it when he said he wasn't going to tell Kook about what Soomi had been saying. It would only make the 'healing' process harder for them if they both knew how much they were missing each other. Yet here Jungkook was, wanting to suddenly go to fucking NYC to see her. He didn't get where the random will would have come from. 

Unless they seriously had some sort of weird connection, which was some voodoo shit he did not want to think about. It didn't help that idiotic Soomi was more confused than ever. She ended up dating Jinhwan just to be longing for Jungkook. He knew it wasn't her fault for what her heart kept choosing, but it was starting to become a big issue.

So what the hell was he supposed to do? He wasn't trying to keep her and Jinhwan together, even though it was lowkey great payback to Jungkook. He just didn't want Soomi to keep hurting people because she couldn't make up her mind. As sad as it was, Jimin knew what it was like. 

While Jimin had been in deep thought, Jungkook had continued to bother him like a kid begging for candy. Tuning back into reality, the shorter boy sighs as he meets the youngest's eyes, dreading what he was about to say, "the thing is, Jungkook, Soomi's been missing you, too."

"Really?" he gawks, eyes brightening from what he was hearing, "See, I didn't think she would be happy with Jinhwan. He doesn't really seem like her type."

Heaving out a sigh, he raises a brow, "the hell does that even mean?"

Jungkook shrugs, "just look at me and that Sunwoo guy, we have a similar demeanor. Then you add in Jinhwan, and it just doesn't make sense."

"I guess you might have a point, he's nice compared to both of you assholes," Jimin hums in thought, earning an unamused look from the maknae before him. "In all seriousness, though, you can't go to New York to see her, idiot," he concludes, turning back towards the sink to set his glass down.

A confused look formed on Jungkook's face, who followed him when he walked to sit at the table. "Why?" he asks, sounding rather defensive, "I don't want to ruin her time at home, I just feel like I need to talk to her - bad."

"Well it can wait," Jimin snaps, not in a rude way but more in a way that was trying to get Jungkook to think rationally, "it hasn't been long since she last saw Jinhwan. Time away from both of you and being back at home has obviously riled up all of her emotions, and she needs time to sort that out before you come running up her doorstep, okay?"

Jungkook's face fell in defeat. His hyung was right, and he fucking hated to admit it.

"I guess it hasn't been long, this is just killing me," he mutters, falling onto the chair across from Jimin.

The boy looks at him with sympathetic eyes, "if it makes you feel any better, I can keep you updated. Just know that she's longing for you right now, too, Jungkook."


112:00 | bad boy

Solar's recovery and being with Sunwoo had taken all of her attention. Time had been lost in her world, and before anyone knew it, it was weeks later. Her wound was lots better, only a mere scar left she covered with a bandage. The time of recovering had been hard, as it had still hurt horribly for the first week and a half. Since her family was lead to believe it was just a minor injury, she had to walk around and go downstairs to eat like it was all good. But her wound had still ached with every step she'd taken. It had been a nightmare no doubt, so she was more than grateful it was finally well.

Just like he had swore, Kim Sunwoo had yet to leave her side. He was practically glued to her hip, making sure she was okay in every waking moment. It had been overwhelming and even annoying in the beginning, but she realized Sunwoo had helped her a lot through her recovery. (Well, and her best friends. But even Hee wasn't as clingy as him.) She knew he felt so bad about how it was his father, but Sol wasn't mad at him. They'd been through this too many times, his father always hated her. That wasn't his fault.

She really wouldn't of been able to get better if he hadn't been there for her. Not to mention how he was still on the look out for her new stalker.

The truth was, she owed him a lot for all he was doing for her.

Through all of the good times she had been having being back home with her friends and Sunwoo, she tended to let those in South Korea drift to the back of her mind. She still talked to Jimin pretty often, and every once in a while one of the other Bangtan boys would hit her up just to ask how she was. As far as they knew, she was having the time of her life. Never did she let a detail slip about the wound, and she never would since it was over and done now.

As for the boys of iKON, they hadn't spoken a word to her recently. Junhoe checked in the most out of all of them, which was kind of a problem when one remembered she was dating Jinhwan.

But no, the clingy, loving, touchy boy hadn't tried to even text her for about a week now.

The worst part about it? Solar had barely let it cross her mind.


"SORRY!" the girl had been in the middle of crying, dropping the phone in the sand from how hard she was laughing, "Chanhee just actually looks like he's about the cut Sunwoo's fucking throat - holy fuck, look at his face!"

The four boys in the picture had rose to their feet from crouching down on the sand. They all walk over to peer over her shoulder so they could see what she was on about. As soon as they catch sight of Hee's expression, they all start laughing, too.

"Is that statement wrong, though?" Hee shrugs.

No one answered, they already knew he hated Sunwoo. He hated him even more after what happened. Unlike Solar, he did blame Sunwoo for what happened. Chanhee knew well that wherever Sunwoo went, trouble followed, and due to the obsession he had with his best friend, it tended to always chase her.

"I'm hungry," Kevin announces, "we've been here all day, let's go get food."

"Well you can wait," Hee hits his shoulder, "we weren't done with our photo shoot yet."

The boy groans at the words he'd heard.

Solar was the only one that had yet to have a turn. She felt bad because it did seem like everyone was hungry. Clearing her throat, she assures her friends, "we can go eat, it's looking kinda cloudy, anyway."

Chanhee is the first to look at her with doubt. He knew how much she liked to take photos and post them on her feed with stupid captions. "Are you sure?"

She nods with a smile.

"Thank you, dear Solar!" Kevin pipes up in joy, "I'm fucking starving!"

Without another word, he starts bolting towards Chanhee's car, which is what they had all crowded into to get to the beach. As he runs, he starts slipping in the sand, causing him to nearly tumble over on his face multiple times. Changmin and Jacob start running after him, screaming that it was a race.

But before you knew it, all three boys were tackling and falling on top of each other.

"Sometimes I wish I didn't have to babysit three delinquents," Hee sighs, finally beginning to follow the three.

Sol chuckles as she watches her friends. There's a smile that remains on her face as she turns to Sunwoo, "are you coming?"

The boy seemed to be in a trance. His eyes were looking somewhere she couldn't decipher. Confused, she goes on her tip-toes to wave a hand in his face, trying to snap him out of it.

"Huh?" he blinks, seeing the silly girl laughing as she moves her hand around. Without a word, he grabs her hand from in front of his face, bringing it to his side were he grips it. "Before we go, let's go take some pictures."

She opens her mouth to protest, but he was already dragging her away back towards the sea. "Idiot, we just agreed to not do that so we could eat, they're waiting for us!"

He only rolls his eyes, urging her to go stand. "They'll wait."

"Sunwoo," she whines, but goes to stand by the seaside anyway. Solar wouldn't say no to taking photos when she got the chance, she couldn't help herself.

Pulling out his phone, Sunwoo bends down a little to get a good angle. She'd never seen him so focused on something as small as taking a photo. Still, it made her happy to see him that way. Like every other moment on such a nice day, she cherished this one.







"Wait, one more, princess," he holds up a hand to stop her from moving.

The girl sighs, going back to posing. Just as she feels like it's perfect, and Sunwoo is positioning the camera in front of her, they both feel something.

A water droplet.

"Uh," she opens her mouth to speak, but more started falling down. With in seconds, it was an angry waterfall of rushing raindrops. "Fuck!" she cries, holding her hands above her head as an attempt to keep herself dry.

It was no use, she was already soaked.

Sunwoo is in laughter, hysterical laughter, as he takes off his jacket. Walking over to her, he raises it over both of their heads. That was when she heard his laugh, something she hadn't heard in so long. Sure, he would chuckle every now and then, but it had been a long time since the boy full on laughed like this. He was laughing like everything was fine in the world, like he had finally found peace.

Because he had.

He looks to her, eyes twinkling under that leather jacket of his, before yelling the words, "run."

So she did.

The two ran through the rain, giggling away like there was no tomorrow. Sand plopping under their feet and making a mess of their shoes, they looked like typical kids having fun.

Except they weren't kids anymore. They were grown "adults" still struggling to understand themselves. But it seemed the last few weeks, they'd been reliving that life they used to, and it was a carefree one.

"What the fuck did I just watch?" Chanhee is saying as they're climbing in their seats in the backseat. Turning around to look at the drenched kids, he winces, "careful, don't make a mess!"

Solar apologizes as she tries to keep from getting her messy shoes all over the place. On the other hand, Sunwoo rolls his eyes, "close your mouth, pretty boy."

"Excuse me?!" Hee snaps, "is it wrong for me to not want my car messy?"

"Is a little sand really gonna hurt you?" Sunwoo raises a brow, words dripping in disappointment.

Not in the mood to deal with him, Chanhee sighs as he turns away, starting the car fully. He waits a bit for the windshield wipers to clear up the windows, before backing out of where they were parked at the beach.

"I forgot how crazy the weather is here," Solar is humming as she watches the rain angrily slap against the windows, "I also forgot what it's like to live so close to the beach."

"Well, good thing you're back," Jacob beams, "we'll be glad to remind you of how great being home is."

She smiles with nostalgia as she nods, "yeah, you guys have already done a great job with that."






+





Solar lets out a breathe of relief when they enter the hotel room. Feeling exhausted, she throws her bag to the side, it landed somewhere on the floor with a plop.

The door is closed behind her as Sunwoo follows. As she said, their homes were close to the beach. But with how bad the weather was and how much no one really wanted to go home and face responsibility, they planned to stay around for a while.

They'd all gotten three rooms. The others would be roommates with each other, and of course, it left her and Sunwoo together.

"Thank God," she groans, "I'm so tired—wait, why's there only one bed?" She raises a brow, turning to him, "you said you got a room with two."

The boy only shrugs, "oops."

She didn't care that much since he had been sleeping in her bed a lot of nights. It was just funny he always pulled things like this.

Seeing her blow it off like nothing was still weird to Sunwoo. He thought back to how closed off she was at the beginning, all because of her 'boyfriend.' But he hadn't been hearing her talk about him at all, and she was clearly more open and free than before.

Her attitude almost reminded him of the old Solar.

The girl stretches out her arms, falling onto the mattress. Taking off his leather jacket, Sunwoo didn't bother to go to the bathroom to change. What did it matter?

Casually, he slipped his shirt over his head. Solar hadn't been paying attention, as she was in a daze staring at the ceiling. It was when the sound of a zipper filled her ears that her eyes jerked to him.

"Sunwoo— what the hell?!" She squeaks, holding up a hand to cover her view from his exposed body, "you know there's a fucking bathroom!"

He scoffs as he looks to her, "oh please, I know you don't actually care. You should be thanking me."

"Sunwoo," she breathes, not wanting to have whatever conversation he was obviously trying to ease into, "just put some clothes on."

She didn't deny it though.

"Yeah, no," he didn't even have to think as he dead toned, "I like to sleep comfortably, thank you."

"Oh my God," she's muttering in annoyance, burying her face in her hands as she sat up, "you're pulling this now on purpose, and it's not funny."

The boy chuckles that demeaning chuckle he would do. "Calm yourself," he says assuringly, before lazily slipping on a new pair of skinny jeans. However, he didn't even zip nor button them up.

Solar refused to look, trying to calm how badly she wanted to fucking hit him. That was, until he appeared right before her, and the first thing her eyes landed on was his unzipped pants.

"Are you fucking — Sunwoo!" She snaps, but he only grins, "cut it out, whatever you're trying isn't working."

He bends down from his tall height until he was eye level with her. Their faces were only centimeters apart, and she tried not to show any emotion as he teases, "are you sure about that?"

Groaning, she breaks their eye contact. He wasn't going to give up easily, though, he never did. A finger makes it's way under her chin, and he forces her to look at him again.

"You're such a bad liar, Solar," his raspy voice seemed to sink into her skin. His brown eyes taunting her, she still had to hold herself back. She was not about to do something she would regret later. Solar was still a taken girl.

"Stop, Sunwoo," she tries to tell him sternly, but her voice came out weak, "you know this is wrong."

But he was inching closer to her. The worst part about it — she wasn't backing up.

I can't. I can't, I can't.

So why wasn't she moving? She swore it was her hormones controlling her. What was she doing? She was confused between Jinhwan and Jungkook. She'd been missing Jungkook for so long, but here she was.

"I don't see you stopping me, Sol."

Meeting his eyes again, her own can't help but rake across his body. Sunwoo was well fit, not because he worked out, but because he was a trained killer. Obviously he was expected to be fit when he had the sort of job he did. But there was something about his toned arms, the scars that lined his body from the fights he'd fought, and the ink that was drawn across nearly every part of his skin.

He was something unlike everyone else, he was bad.

So bad.

"Don't," she finally blurts, and it was all he needed to hear.

A triumphant smile on his lips, they collide into a symphony. His lips were so fluffy against her own. Only then did she realize how long it had been.

His hands roam her body, his lips doing the rest of the work. Solar bites her bottom lip as he moves to her neck, sucking on her skin before biting it every now and then. It was such a crazy feeling, sending electricity through her body.

He's so bad that it's good.


113:00 | regret?

junhoe: soomi we gotta talk

junhoe: there's something you need to know




Regret.

That was the only thing she could feel when she got up the next morning. Well, that — and a slight pain between her legs. It had definitely turned into a night of nostalgia after what they did. And Solar knew she was never going to feel any less guilty about it.

How shitty of a person could she be? She had gone back and forth between guys for so long. As soon as she becomes official with one of them, she goes and has a fling with her childhood ex.

She was stupid.

It was the first time she had really thought about Jinhwan in a while. That only made her feel worse.

"You're being weird," Hee had said when he dropped her off at home earlier. Sunwoo had already been at his own house as he said he had to do some things and would be back at her house later to continue to watch over her. "Why won't you just tell me what happened?"

"What are you on about?" she tried to push it off as nothing, "don't assume something happened."

The boy had rolled his eyes, knowing her well enough to see through it. "I know you'll break and tell me eventually," he sighed.

He was right. She always ended up telling him everything. But Solar knew he would be not only disappointed but annoyed that he had been right.

Back in the 'safety' of her bedroom was where you'd find her. A lifeless feeling resided in her chest as she sat on her bed, journal in her lap and pen in her hand.

There was a lot on her mind — there was always a lot on her mind. She wished it would just go away, even for a second.

Looking towards her window, she found herself reminiscing. That New York sky, as pretty as it was, in all its glory. She loved her home state, she loved this place.

But she was starting to miss Korea. All her weeks of recovery and living carefree with Sunwoo had been knocked out after what happened.

That was when she decided to check her phone. Seeing Junhoe's name on her screen was something that would usually cheer her up. After all, he tended to have a magic power to make everyone laugh around him.

Though as soon as she read the words, she was left in a sea of confusion. It took her a minute to think, before she typed back.

soo: um, maybe, i kind of lost track of time, ya know? it's crazy here

junhoe: i was gonna tell you as soon as i found out a couple of days ago but i had to process it first

junhoe: i'm sorry soomi

junhoe: i want you to know he isn't going to live after this

soo: the fuck are you on about??

soo: you're scaring the shit out of me, june-ya

soo: if it's this serious spit it out, did he break something? is he hurt?

junhoe: no.. but he will be

junhoe: he's cheating on you

Her expression grows blank. Everything around her seemed to fall, and shatter on the ground. Unable to comprehend what she was reading before her, her green eyes narrow, reading over the words again and again. But no matter how many times she read them, she couldn't seem to process it in her brain.

Jinhwan — cheating? Those words together didn't even sound right.

soo: and just hard to believe

junhoe; this is seriously wrong

junhoe: aren't you mad???

soo: kind of

soo: i just need to think

Setting her phone down, a knock on her window prevented her from any thinking. The sight of Sunwoo, perched on that tree branch, sent something through her.

She slumps over to the window, pulling it open without a word. Flailing back onto her bed face first, a blank Sunwoo had climbed through, closing it behind him.

"Sol," he says quizzically, "what's up with you? Still overthinking about what happened?"

When she doesn't say a word, he grows more lost. Sure, she was pretty freaked out from guilt this morning but it didn't make her give him the silent treatment. What the hell was the problem now?

"Hey," he calls, sitting beside her on the bed, he pokes her side, "talk to me, sweetheart."

A shaky sigh had left her lips. Finally, she turns around to lay on her back. When she did, his eyes are met with the sight of tears streaming down her cheeks.

"The fuck happened?" He demands immediately, filled with fury at the sight of her crying. He knew she didn't feel bad enough about it that she would cry — this was something else.

Solar doesn't say anything still, she only picks up her phone, unlocking it, before holding it out to him.

Slowly, he takes it, and she watches as his eyes scan over everything.

As soon as he was done, he gradually looked back up to her. She could see the anger welling in his body. Even though Solar had yet to decide if she had a right to be mad, you could tell Sunwoo was without a doubt.

"I'm buying tickets to Korea," he abruptly states, "I'm going to kill this motherfucker."

Wiping under her eyes, she extends her arm to grab his wrist, halting his actions because he'd actually taken out his phone. "Sunwoo, don't do something stupid, calm down."

"Calm down?" He gawks, "he's been cheating on you this whole week versus us having sex once, that's a whole nother level."

"But is it?" She frowns, "it's still cheating."

"What he's doing is worse, Solar," Sunwoo corrects, "stop trying to justify what's happening."

She sighs, diverting her eyes elsewhere. He was right in a sense, she just didn't want to believe it. Maybe Junhoe was just being overdramatic, maybe this was being blown out of proportion. She really didn't see Jinhwan doing that. He wasn't like her, he didn't made stupid mistakes because he was considerate, caring, nurturing.

The Jinhwan she knew loved her, more than she thought it was possible to love someone. So how the hell could this make sense, how could this be true?

"I don't want to think about it anymore," she mutters.

"Then don't," Sunwoo lays beside her, hand reaching out to run his fingers across her cheek, "but as far as I'm concerned, that relationship is over."

She shakes her head, "it doesn't work like that, we're going to have to talk eventually."

"But do you really still feel like you're together?"

Solar is hesitant. As mean as she had mentally been to Jinhwan, even missing Jungkook more than him, it didn't mean what she felt for him was gone. This all still hurt, and it hurt to answer Sunwoo's question honestly. "...no."

"My point exactly," he hums, "you'll be okay, Sol, put that in the back of your mind."

His words were sweet, and for a second, they made her smile.

"Plus, you have me. What more would you need?"





+






As days began to wind down for her time in Korea, Solar questioned if she was ready to go back. It was ironic days ago she was missing it, now she was dreading getting on a plane to go back.

How was she going to go to the company? She felt so scared and awkward. As much as she had been trying to keep an open mind, knowing that Junhoe might not know the truth or full story, it was becoming hard.

All she could think about was who the girl was, if she was better than her. She was turning into a self conscious mess despite how Sunwoo had been trying to cheer her up and help her.

He wouldn't be able to be next to her forever. She was leaving soon, and he would be staying her to continue doing his dirty work.

So how was life going to be in Korea now? She didn't know the answer. Whenever she spoke to Jinhwan, it would either be relieving or a nightmare.

But one thing she did know, was they shouldn't be together. Not with everything still going on, not with all the confusion and mixed feelings.

Though it was true, it still made her sad.

114:00 | party pooper

"Maybe the guy who told you was lying," Kevin suggests, "like, you said that you don't see Jinhwan as that kind of person, you know? I don't get how someone can change and suddenly pull something like that."

Solar didn't say anything as she continues to eat her ice cream. The others followed suit, until Chanhee sighs, "that 'guy' who told her was supposed to be one of her best friends — for a reason I am very unsure of — why the hell would he lie?"

He was still salty about her new best friends, she already knew that silly Hee would never get over it.

"Also, maybe he was that way the whole time," Jacob chimes in, "maybe he's just a whole big ball of fake."

"Whatever it is, he fucking sucks," Kevin shakes his head, "no one can treat our Sol like that."

Changmin laid a hand on her shoulder, being the considerate boy he always was. "Are you okay, Solar?" He asks in worry, "I know this is hard for you."

She swallowed a bite of ice cream as she met his kind eyes, "yeah, I'm fine, it's just a massive shock to me."

The look in his eyes says he doesn't believe that she was fine in the slightest. He would be right not to. Solar wasn't fine — not even a little. It didn't matter if she had feelings for another guy, it didn't matter if a part of her had been missing Jungkook, and it didn't matter that she might of cheated on him only a singular time with her ex from New York. She had chose Jinhwan for a reason, he was supposed to be her angel, the boy that got her through everything and never hurt her.

So how could he turn into the worst of them all? It was so astonishing, so heartbreaking that every time she thought about it she felt a physical ache in her chest.

She wondered, she wondered who this girl was that managed to take him away from her. She wondered if she was just that horrible of a girlfriend that he just got bored. She wondered so many things, that she began to doubt herself an insane amount.

All she wanted to know by then was why. She wanted to know what she did to deserve this, and why someone she trusted more than anyone else would turn on her like this.

"Stop thinking," Chanhee abruptly pulls her out of her thoughts, pointing a finger at her, "I know you don't like to talk about this, but it needs to be spoken about. We're trying to sort out your thoughts and help you, so stop tuning out every word we're saying."

She frowns, not having anything to reply because everything Hee accused her of was right. The others look at her with sympathy, "but we are sorry," Jacob says, "are you gonna wait to speak to him in person?"

"Yeah," she mutters, "I think it would be the best, at least break up in person, you know?"

"Look on the bright side," Hee hums, and she worried about what his next words would be, "now you can freely go to Jungkook, right?"

Her mouth is open a bit from surprise that he just said that. "Hee, shut up," she snaps, "I so don't want to hear that right now."

He shrugs it off, knowing she actually didn't mind. "I'm just saying, you have all these men, don't let one get you down."

What he said was stupid, so stupid she found herself giggling at it. Solar wished it was that easy. She wished she could push every moment, every cherished memory, right out the back of her head into oblivion. Like she could just not care about Jinhwan, move on like it was nothing.

But she couldn't. There were a million memories stored in her head, and she had held them all so close to her heart. How was she going to just trash them? How was she going to truly process he wasn't who she was lead to believe he was?

Frankly, she never would.

She sighs, feeling rather mixed about it as her mind only went on. "You know," she breathes, finishing her ice cream and setting the cup down, her eyes wander around the familiar place she was in. One that he had taken her to, back then as a surprise, and thinking about it made her angry. "I feel kind of — mad."

"Good!" Kevin cries, causing everyone to glare at him like he was a monster, "no — I mean like, good that you're mad and not sad. That's like, the next step."

Hee rolls his eyes, "should've said that to start with, idiot," before he looks back to his best friend, "but you should be more mad than sad. We all know you didn't do anything to deserve this. You can miss someone you still love while being with someone else, but it's a lot different than actually cheating. There is no excuse for what he did, alright? And you should be fucking pissed he had the audacity to do it to Solar Kim."

His speech left her peering up at the ceiling in deep contemplation. "So, do you think I'm free now?" She blurts, and all of her friends blink at her in confusion, "like, technically since he's done this, we aren't together, right?"

The four boys exchange glances — worries glances. "I really don't think I like the sound of that," Jacob mumbles.

"Solar," Changmin cautiously says, placing another hand on her shoulder, "whatever you're thinking probably isn't the answer to this issue."

They all had a bad feeling about it. But the girl shrugs as she rises to her feet, "don't worry, guys, I already know the answer to my question."

Chanhee suddenly had a good idea. "Solar fucking Kim!" He snaps, grabbing her wrist just as she tried to walk towards the door, "you are not going to start going back to stupid old habits as a way to act out against this."

"Huh?" She blinks down at him, "no, I'm going to go back to get over this."

With that, she yanks her wrist out of his grip. As her best friends call out to her, even rising to their own feet to try and catch up, she was already out the door.




+




"So — I can't hold this in anymore," the boy had sighed, leaning against the table in the kitchen. His arms are crossed over his chest, and there's an uneasy expression splattered on his face as he taps his heel on the ground in worry. It was obviously something that was really hard for him to even think about, he couldn't seem to muster the words to say it.

Meanwhile, the sweet 'grandpa' of the house had sent him a small smile, reaching out from where he was seated to pat his arm, "calm down, it's only me, and whatever it is — I will keep it to myself for the time being, alright? Don't let your assumptions get the best of you."

His wise advice made the anxious boy breathe out again. Running a hand through his hair, he tries to intake his friends words and do just that — calm down.

But how could he say this?

"That night — when we got drunk..." he pauses, squeezing his eyes shut as he tries to get the words out of his mouth, "Soomi and Junhoe — they hooked up."

The boy's mouth falls open at what he heard. As if he wasn't in enough shock — he wasn't the only one.

"They fucking what?!"


"Solar?" Younghoon had blinked down at her in confusion, "I didn't expect to see you here."

The girl purses her lips, knowing what he meant when he said that. Getting right to the point, she blurts, "is he here?"

"No," he answers immediately, and she lets out a breathe of relief, "he took off after what he tried, think he knew Sunwoo wasn't going to forgive him for a while. We haven't even gotten any word on any jobs lately."

This was a surprise to her. His father had done things like this before, he had tried to hurt her many times in the past. Since he'd began making his son do all of his work he'd had it out for her. She was the one who brought out the good in Sunwoo, she made him think twice before he did bad, and it made his father furious.

But every time back then, she'd been saved. She guessed this was the first time the odds weren't quite in her favor.

"Huh," she mumbles as she walks inside the house, inhaling the scent she was so used to, "that sounds like a deal for you guys."

"Sure," Younghoon shrugs, locking the door before following behind her, "but are you alright? You seem to not even be slightly bothered about the fact your exes father tried to kill you."

"Well, look at all the shit I've dealt with," she brushes it off, "it's all the same, really."

Though that wasn't so true.

The boy knew that, but he didn't say anything. He could sense there was something off about her. She was different than how she had been since she got back — she was like the old her.

"Guys!" She screeches, arms in the air as she enters the basement where the gang members were always found. Sure enough, there they all were, lounging on the couches with blunts or drinks in their hands — or both. Drugs were scattered around the tables, and there were a few strippers they must've invited lounging around with them. It was a sight she wasn't surprised to see, especially now that she heard they had some time off.

"Sol, you hot fuck!" Hyunjoon was the first one to yell, pushing the two strippers he had on top of him off. Rushing across the floor, the blunt is hanging out of his mouth as he pulls her in for a hug, "finally you fucking visit, how are you? Feeling brand new?"

His hands are on her shoulders, and he was peering straight into her soul. She only knew to shrug, "there's been some shit happening as if me getting shot and having a stalker wasn't enough," she explains, "that's why I'm here to forget about it."

Raising a brow, he gets the gist immediately. Pointing a finger at her, he winks, "ah, I see what you're saying. Drugs and alcohol help everything, but you can always go skin someone for fun, too. I find that more therapeutic."

The way he said it so casually would always get you.

"That's okay," she waves a hand in dismissal, "the drugs and alcohol will have to do, Hyunjoon, unless I get real bored."

He giggles before turning towards Eric, "Ric, get her something, idiot."

The others had been listening in by now, and were rather lost at what was happening. "Are you really okay, Solar?" Sangyeon had spoke up, rather weary of her sudden change.

"Fine," she deadpans, walking over to Eric and holding out her hands. The boy turns to her with confusion despite her eager eyes shooting holes into him.

"What happened to the pussy bitch Solar?" The youngest hums, "finally going back to being fun, huh?"

"Just give me the fucking drugs, Eric."

"Okay, fuck, still can't take a joke."

Rolling her eyes, she snatches the bottle and pills out of his hands instantly. Everyone could only watch in either awe or cheer her on proudly. But just as she was about to down them, a hand had gripped her wrist, causing her to drop the bottle she was bringing to her lips.

A yelp leaves her mouth as it falls to the floor, exploding into millions of pieces and the substance spilling everywhere. The crack had definitely grabbed everyone's attention, and the girl could only gawk as she turns to the culprit of this occurrence.

"Sunu, what the fuck?!" She cries in frustration, not understanding why he would've done what he just did.

The boy narrows his eyes at her, seeming to read every emotion she was feeling, before he sighed. "As happy as I am that you want to 'have fun,' I'm not going to let you drink yourself to hell because of some idiot."

Her eyes grow sad faster than the blink of an eye. Sunwoo had read her like a book. It wasn't difficult to see she was only looking to ease her pain with substances, which would only end up causing more pain for her in the end. He had her best interests at heart, even though he was who he was, and it would always be something that amazed her.

"Sol, what's he talking about?" Haknyeon asks, and everyone else was looking at her with curious eyes.

She only sighs, not wanting to talk about it at all. That was why she came here, so she could forget, not have to tell the story all over again.

And gang members were supposed to be fun.

"Come on," Sunwoo sighs, grabbing her wrist and leading her away before the others could bother her anymore, "you can do all the drugs in the world when it's not to get over some delusional retard."


115:00 | cravings

"Sunwoo, you can fucking let me go now!" she's crying in annoyance as he'd dragged her into his massive bedroom. Being that it was the master bedroom of the house (since his father resided elsewhere in another state to expand his business) it was practically the size of a country. By then, she expected him to let go of her wrist — but it appeared he had no intention of doing so.

He only glances at her from were he was holding his phone in his other hand, raising a brow, "someone mad, sweetheart?"

"You fucking think?!" She snaps, and he only chuckles at her reaction, "I came here to have fun and forget, not for you out of all people to stop me," she scoffs, shaking her head in awe, "how the fuck does that even work?"

He laughs a bit before sighing, "I told you this, you're pissed off over some absolute fuck wad, that's the only reason you want to do what you want to do."

But she only narrowed her eyes at him, before berating loudly, "why the fucks it matter?!"

Rolling his eyes, he pulls her to the king sized bed, before forcing all of his strength to chunk her onto it. She screeches in shock at what he just pulled, her wrist aching slightly as she lands on the mattress. Just when she's about to ask him if he was a fucking psychopath, he clears his throat, giving her that serious look that made her shut her mouth. It meant he had something to say — and that he was most definitely not in the mood.

"Listen, when you came back, I expected you to be eager to go back to your old life," he begins, tone harsh, "and I was fucking thrilled to bring you back to it, I didn't see any other Solar other than the drinking, drug addict, party animal Solar, the one who used to not even flinch when she'd see someone shot, the one who didn't care about other people's lives."

Not understanding what he was possibly trying to say, she shakes her head, "Sunwoo, the fuck are you—"

"—Solar, for both of us, shut the fuck up," he holds up a hand to silence her further, and afraid of his frustrated tone she rarely heard towards her, she did just that.

"Even though I'm kinda heartless, even though I kill people without a care, I'm the most compassionate person you'll ever meet when it comes to you," he breathes, "when I saw what you accomplished all by yourself, all alone, how much you shone when you were in front of those people. When I saw how happy you were, so much happier without things like drugs or alcohol, it made me realize that I don't prefer that old Solar, alright?"

"Sunwoo..."

"However," he blurts, "I'm just saying, I'm proud of you, sweetheart. I don't want you to make a mistake and ruin all you've achieved. You know what drugs do to people, are you sure you would be able to handle them anymore?"

Pursing her lips, she wasn't sure she had fully taken in everything he just said. Never did she expect to hear a speech like that from the likes of Kim Sunwoo. But he'd said it, in his own way, that he didn't necessarily want her doing drugs and going back to her old ways.

Though it was odd, she found herself wondering if he really meant that. Maybe he was just doing it for the better of her more than his own desires.

Finally, she shrugs, "I'm sure, it's not like I won't be used to it anymore."

"Well, you sure weren't used to hanging around with us."

She was aware he was referring to the first job she experienced with them since her return. "That's different, you kidnapped my friends fucking sister!" She hissed, and he chuckles.

"Kidding, princess," he hums, "look, you've got less than a week in New York, alright? I'm not giving them to you right now — but I'll make sure you remember your last days here."

She could only peer at him in curiosity. Sunwoo snorts at her innocent expression, shaking his head as he pulls a cigarette from the box in his pocket. Lighting it, she watched as he inhaled before exhaling the smoke, waiting for him to say something else.

But he didn't.

"Okay," she blinks, "so when is that starting?"

Even though his speech hit a soft spot in her veins, she still hadn't really decided against doing what she planned to. She just wanted to feel that release again, to fully be able to forget everything that was happening to her, even if it was only for a moment. Solar wanted to remember what it felt like to let go, to be free once more, have no worries. A part of her even missed it.

At least before she left, she needed to have those good times again. Her good attitude had nearly blown away with the wind after this, what did she have to lose anymore? She no longer feared her innocent protective boyfriend finding out, not anymore.

"Didn't you hear me? I said not now," Sunwoo hums.

Oh how she hated the patience game.






+






As the days went by so did Solar's sanity. There wasn't a singular part of her that was ready to face Jinhwan, though she might not admit that. It wasn't like she wasn't still hurting. He was the person she came to trust the most, the boy she chose. The last thing she ever wanted to happen was to find something like that out.

The day after her attempt at releasing some stress failed, she ended up falling into a pit. First, she found herself wondering if it was her fault. It was like when she had that nightmare, were they all said she was this horrible person. She began to ask herself if maybe that was the world trying to tell her something, maybe this was karma catching up to her.

Then she started to contemplate if she was in the wrong for being so sad about it. Once upon a time, she had basically been with Jungkook and Jinhwan at the same time while she was making her decision. She knew that must've hurt the both of them a lot. Not to mention, what happened between her and Sunwoo the same day Junhoe broke the news to her. If she had done it to him, was she wrong to be mad he did it, too?

But from what Junhoe said, it had been going on for a while. While she didn't want to believe it, it did explain the packing texts she began to receive from Jinhwan. By then, they hadn't spoken at all other than a few texts asking how each other was.
