"Kim fucking Soomi!" a scream is heard from the doorway of her studio. It was like God had been listening to her thoughts due to the exact person she had been thinking of talking to showed up. She turns to hug Junhoe, when she's met with not just him, but five other boys, too. 

"Whoa, hold u-" she tries to say when they all jump at her full speed. The sound of a dying goat was what emerged from her lips when she was brutally tackled in affection by five boys much bigger than her.

Even though it felt like her lungs were giving out, she couldn't say she wasn't happy to see them. The boys who were suffocating her in a loving group hug finally realize what they are doing to the tiny girl, letting go and stepping back a little. 

That was, all of them that weren't Junhoe. The boy was glued to Soomi by the hip. He wasn't going to admit, but he had clearly missed his best friend.

"How are you?" Donghyuk is the first to ask in concern, "is your family alright now? Everything sorted out?"

She felt a little bad that she hadn't got to catch up with them all. The girl sighs as she recounts everything that happened to her in New York. It started off positive, that was for sure. She had fixed her family problems, reunited with them, strengthened a bond she never thought she would experience again. But this person that was after her had made moves, moves she didn't expect. Hell, she even got shot by Sunwoo's father, one of the richest men in the US, just because he hated her for being so close with his son. She found out about Jinhwan thanks to Junhoe, and it led to her doing drugs again after six years. 

And the part she didn't want to mention, her relationship that formed with Sunwoo. 

Just thinking about it all made her head hurt. She holds a hand to her head, massaging her temple as she steps back to take a seat on the booth that surrounds the walls of the studio.

The others exchange glances, realizing it must be a lot that she has to tell them. Without another word, they follow her actions. 

"Should I get popcorn?" Bobby hums into the silence.

He was trying to lighten the mood, but Soomi still wasn't looking forward to having to explain all of this. 

With a sigh, she had no choice but to begin. She'd tell them everything that wasn't about the new stalker of hers.



+



"That's a lot of good and bad in one," Hanbin mutters as soon as she's done. By this time, she was dead inside, leaning her head on Junhoe's shoulder with a dull look on her face.

She had left out more than she planned. By the time she got to parts like her hook up with Sunwoo or the fact she was doing drugs again, she realized it would be in everyone's best interest to not tell them. 

"Yeah," is all she replies, voice barely above a whisper.

The six boys frown as they see their poor friend. Soomi wasn't doing great, but yet here she was, at the studio having just recorded a new song. She had just got back from everything that they now learned happened to her in New York and was already working.

They felt bad for her.

And it wasn't like they didn't know about what happened in the hall or with Jinhwan completely. Chanwoo had briefly seen the exchange and went back to tell the others. No one knew if it was safe to ask her about their hyung. It was obviously a sore subject for the both of them right now.

"We'll always be here for you, Soo," Yunhyeong speaks up instead, laying a hand on her shoulder so she would look his way, "I know you're going through so much, but just remember you can lean on us, alright?"

The others nod or pipe up in agreement to his statement. She sends them a thankful smile, shaking her head in disbelief that she had them in her life, "thank you guys, really."


+



A deep sigh leaves her lips as she walks through the YG lobby that evening, wanting nothing more than to go home, or more like to see Jungkook. She was more than mentally exhausted after literally meeting the girl Jinhwan was suspected to have been cheating on her with. And she would never be too sure about the words he spoke. It made sense, it wasn't like she'd ever seen him as the type to cheat. He was a sweetheart, her saving grace, not a piece of shit who would do something like that behind her back.

But at the same time, it was too clear. Junhoe hadn't been so far fetched. She saw the way the girl clung onto him, the way she was dressed. He could say that he tried to get her to leave him alone, but would he not have been able to if he really wanted to? It seemed too weird to her.

All she wanted was to talk to Jungkook, and only Jungkook. Never did she think she would be here, but here she was. Maybe she really did make the wrong decision.

Sighing, she's just about to open the doors, desperate for the cold outside air to hit her skin, when a voice calls from behind her.

"Ms. Soomi-ah!" She turns to see one of the many staff workers, a polite smile on her face. Noticing she caught the girl's attention, the worker bows kindly, before clearing her throat, "CEO-nim wanted me to pass this onto you, he wants the entire family to be there to mourn a fellow YG member's passing."

A confused look is wiped over her face. Before she was able to process what this woman was talking about, she's handed a pamphlet or invitation. 

"Oh," she's muttering blankly as she takes it, her green eyes daring to glance down at what it was. In that moment, she seemed to have forgotten everything that happened, everything she went through in New York, all the trauma. For once, she had just been thinking about her childish boy problems. For once, she forgot, just for it to be thrown right back at her face.

And there it was, that woman's face plastered on the front page. There's a smile on her face in the picture, the words in memory of wrote in cursive above it. Her eyes are kind, but yet she remembered them being so evil when she'd held a gun to her very head. 

A funeral, she processes, they're having a funeral and they want me to attend. 

Swallowing the lump in her throat was like swallowing a brick. Forcing a smile on her face, she did anything but show how disturbed she was by this as she nodded, bowing gently, "I will surely be there, thank you."

Before the staff member could even bid goodbye, she turned and practically bolted out the entrance doors. As soon as cold air hit her face and she knew she wasn't in sight anymore, a deep exhale left her lips. She was in disbelief. How could she not have thought of this? 

She saw that woman die right in front of her. She'd never forget it, not after she spouted out every one of Soomi's secrets right before she did. 

Holding a hand to her head, she tries to sort her thoughts. She wanted you dead, Solar, she tells herself, it had to happen. But every time she would glance down at the pamphlet, she would feel bad all over again. She let her die, she let someone die right in front of her, she laughed as they were tortured.

What's wrong with me?

It was like her guilty conscience came back. All she could do was try to stop it, but it wasn't doing any good.

Drugs, that's what she needed. But it wasn't like she had any on her. How could she help herself? Was she really a bad person?

Soomi didn't realize she was crying until she felt droplets, thinking it was suddenly raining. It was helpless, her emotions were out of control, and all she had to thank for that was herself. She had been such an idiot when she thought nothing would come out of her doing drugs again. Look at what happened to her.

She couldn't act like everything was okay. She was a mess. A mess of confused, jumbled emotions that she wasn't sure she would ever be able to understand. The thing was, there was nothing she could do about this. She, and presumably the boys of iKON, would have to attend that funeral with their heads high like they knew nothing. She'd have to print that smile on her face and pay her respects, push anything else to the back of her head. 

Pretend like it never happened. 

But that was so hard when she saw her die right before her.

"Mi," a voice calls from somewhere in front of her, she was too busy trying to wipe away the tears to see where she was going, "Hey, Mi," it calls again. It was obvious who it was, and the sound of shoes scraping against concrete was heard. That's when she slammed right into someone's chest, Jungkook's chest, sniffling like a child. 

"Hey," he says again in a whisper, trying to comfort her, "what the hell happened?"

All she could do was exhale deeply, recollecting herself, and hand him the pamphlet. The boy takes it, eyes scanning over everything on it, and as soon as he puts two and two together, his face falls.

"Shit," he mutters in disbelief, realizing just like she had that they probably should have expected this. "Come on," he urges, his hand on her back, "we need to go."

Only then did it really sink into her troubled skin that they were indeed in the parking lot of YG. Though they were at the back where no one ever really was, it was still fucking YG Entertainment. Her breath hitches in her throat when she comprehends, looking to Jungkook, and immediately she bolts to his car.

Not her smartest move, she sighed as she was safely in the seat, slamming the door in hopes that all of this would somehow be okay, but then again, none of this was.


127:00 | sticking together

"Just breathe," Jungkook had been telling her, his doe eyes worriedly falling on her nervous frame every few seconds as he drove. One hand was gripping the steering wheel while the other was holding onto her own hand as if he was afraid she was going to fly out the window. The truth was, Jungkook was having a hard time processing this, too.

"So I guess they didn't forget about her so easily," he mumbles into the stiff air, only saying things she already knew, "did the pamphlet say anything about what happened to her or the cops being involved?"

Sighing, Soomi picks up the folded paper again. Her eyes scan over each page hesitantly, afraid of what she'll find. 

"Uh," she stutters, turning to the middle. There were a whole lot of sweet words about the woman, which she quickly threw away in her head. Nothing seemed to really spike what they were looking for, until she looked at a paragraph at the bottom of the page.

At KCON NY, she was believed to be kidnapped and eventually murdered in cold blood. For the safety of everyone and to ease the hearts of the mourning family, the police and FBI are looking into her kidnapping. Any updates/news found will be reported under YG ENT. 

"Fucking hell," she curses, almost breaking down into cries all over again. This time, she decided to be the bigger person she needed to be. Shaking her head, she informs the boy in the driver's seat, "YG has the cops and the FBI trying to figure it out, they presume it was a kidnapping, and they know it happened in New York."

His eyes widened slightly, "you've gotta be kidding."

She could only pout, "I wish."

Trying to think quickly on what to do, he clears his throat, "shouldn't you call Sunwoo? Tell him about this, they would be the prime people that all of it would lead them to, no?"

She thought for a minute, before nodding, "probably, it would lead them to Sunwoo and me."

"Which would be very bad."

"Right."

Without a doubt, she had to tell Sunwoo, he would know what to do way more than anyone else. Soomi tries not to freak out, containing her emotions the best she could, and dials his number. Putting it to her ear, she listens to the sound of the ringing.

Though it only rang twice before his silky voice burst through her ears, "sweetheart, to what do I owe the pleasure?"

Typical Sunwoo, he never really got old.

"Sunu," she breathes, "I have bad news."

There's a bit of shuffling on the other line. He was probably moving to a quieter place to speak, as she could hear the screams of the boys. After a moment, he speaks, in a much more serious tone compared to the one he was just using when he answered, "Alright, nothing your favorite crime boss can't take care of, what is it?"

"I got a pamphlet from one of the staff today," she begins, looking down at the paper she held in her lap, "they're having a funeral for the woman that was stalking me. My CEO wants me to attend," she pauses for a second, breathing deeply, "and they've got the FBI and the police on the case, they know it happened in New York."

There's a long silence. 

Soomi had to make sure the call didn't end or she didn't have service. Neither of the two were the case, he really just wasn't saying anything. 

Jungkook glances over to her in the passenger seat, confusion written all over his face. "What's happening?" he mouths, but she only shrugs. 

When she's about to call his name, that's when the sound of a light chuckle is heard. It's faint at first, until it became prominent enough to tell that it was no other than Sunwoo. Soomi is bewildered, not understanding why he would laugh now of all times.

"That's all, sweetheart?" he sighs, and the sound of his exhaling air was heard loudly, presumably him smoking, "I thought it was actually serious, like another word from the new stalker, not about the one that's dead."

"Huh?" she blinks, "that's the thing, though, what do you mean that's all?! Aren't you worried they're gonna find you?!"

The boy giggles on the other line, except it was more menacing than it was cute. "Sol, how many times do you think the cops and the FBI have tried to find the most dangerous gang in the States, seriously?" he hums, humor tinted in his tone, "Now, don't think too hard on it, it might wear your brain out."

Her mouth is agape as she processes. 

He had a point.

"Shut up!" she cried in annoyance as soon as he started laughing, "I get that they might not be able to track you, but what if they figure out it's me?!"

His laughter dies down after a second. The sound of him taking another whiff of his cigarette's heard, before he exhales, "unlikely, Solar, don't think I was dumb enough to leave a trace of me or you. It's possible they could figure out that you were both MIA from the event at the same time, but they still wouldn't be able to trace you. I wiped out any camera that was on the outside of that venue, you're fine."

She's silent for a while. Sunwoo really was smart.

"If they do decide to call you in for questioning because you were MIA at the same time, you can just play it off, you've done this before, remember? Don't act like this is new to you," he continues, "you and your little friends can stick up for each other. They won't be able to figure anything out more than the fact she's dead and not coming back. The police and the FBI are as dumb as the normal townsfolk, they just get a fancy name to scare you."

"You're right," she agrees after a moment.

"Just keep your head up, sweetheart. Go to that funeral and make it look like you're really paying your respects, pass the message on to your little friends, too. It might be harder for them being that they were closer to her."

"Okay, Sunwoo."

"And if you need me, just call, okay?"

"Yeah, always."



+
