She got so distracted texting her younger sister that she didn't realize her manager had left during that time. With a shrug, she rises to her feet from the booth she was seated on, grabbing her bag and throwing it over her shoulder. She really was starting to miss having her own car since now she had to call Jungkook to come get her. It was obviously easier with Jinhwan since they were usually at the company together.

Being under different agencies made it a lot harder than she thought.

A sudden knock at the door grabs her attention. With a sigh, she walks over and swings it open. Solar was just expecting one of the guys of iKON, stopping by to say hello. 

She was not expecting her.

"I've come to deliver an important message to you," the girl informs, her high pitched voice already making Solar want to jump out a window. Completely not interested in hearing whatever this demon had to say, Solar only pushes past her, closing and locking the studio door behind her.

She seriously was not in the mood. All she did was ignore her as she headed down the hall to the elevator.

But of course, psychopaths obsessed with one of your love interest's don't give up that easily.

"Uh, excuse me?!" she snaps in annoyance, trailing behind the brunette, "God, you're so fucking rude, honestly. I'm going to write the worst review about you to the media-"

"-Okay, what do you want - what's your name?" Solar pauses, not remembering the name of the girl on purpose. Shrugging, she stops to face her, not caring enough.

Scoffing, the girl looks at her with disgust, "I just wanted to tell you that you need to stay away from Jinhwan. He doesn't want you anymore, we're becoming an official couple again!" The way she said it made her sound like she was from a Disney movie. Just the sound of her voice made Solar start snickering.

Until her snickers turned into full laughter. She had to cover her mouth to contain it, shaking her head in disbelief. 

"What do you think you're laughing at?!" the girl gawks, anger spreading over her face like a wildfire. 

"Would you like me to pay for your therapy?" she asks in the kindest, soft but fake tone she could muster, "or do you just want me to call the cops?"

Realizing that Solar had no intent on listening, she rolls her eyes. "You're so funny, you'll see," she snaps before turning on her heel and walking away.

The brunette watches her leave, before turning and heading back to the elevator. It was really hard to believe that girl wasn't acting. How could a human be like that? It was so disturbing. She had to remember everything Jinhwan told her, and she really wasn't doubting any of it anymore. He would never get in a relationship with that. 

Reaching the elevator, she hurriedly presses the button to the first floor, just wanting to go home already. This day was more than over for Solar, she was ready to pass out. 



+


"She what?" Jungkook raises a brow in question, humor tinted in his tone as he looks to the rather angered Solar that was pacing around his room. 

The girl halts at his question, turning to face him. Solar flails her hands in the air to show her disbelief, crying in bewilderment, "she literally tried to command me like I'm a fucking dog! I've never seen someone so mentally unwell."

"Are you sure about that?" he coaxes, "what about that childhood boyfriend of yours?"

At the sudden unexpected mention, she pauses all of her actions. Solar blinks for a second, lost in thought as the certain boy makes his way into her brain. Thinking about how lovey dovey her and Sunwoo got when she was in New York made her heart nearly implode. There was a certain adrenaline rush she got when she was with him, a rush that took her back to the old days. It was why a part of her would always love him unlike anyone else, he was her childhood love, after all.

Those details that happened between her and Sunwoo were details she was not planning to ever share with Jungkook or Jinhwan.

So she pushes it to the back of her head as she rolls her eyes, "Sunwoo's not mentally unwell, the way his life is isn't his fault."

"Sure, I was mostly joking," Jungkook shrugs, "at least he's not a pussy, can't really say the same for dwarf boy."

"Jungkook," she says in a warning tone, and the boy only puts his hands up in defense.

"Just saying," he hums innocently. 

The brunette exhales deeply, finally quitting her pacing around the room and instead walking to fall onto the bed beside where he was sitting. At the sight of her finally sitting down, Jungkook sets the journal he had been looking at aside to wrap an arm around her. 

"Hey," she complains, "I wanted to see what you were writing."

"Yeah, no," he shakes his head, "I'm not spoiling my next song for you."

"Oh, so it's for me?"

"Of course," he responds instantly. Before she could blink, he was pushing her down against the mattress. Her eyes widen ever so slightly as he climbs over her, holding himself up with both of his toned arms. Jungkook was no Sunwoo, but he did work out a lot. When he was towering over her, eyes narrowed as they meet her own green irises, only then did he finish in a seductive tone, "you, Kim Soomi, are my muse, after all."

Soomi didn't even get a chance to speak a word. Jungkook had dipped down, and she already knew what was coming. Her eyes flutter closed, and she didn't waste time to wrap her arms around his neck so she could pull him closer. In no time, her body was aching for him. It had been too long, she craved touch. 

And as their lips collide in a symphony, she could admit that she didn't have anything else on her mind, not even the stalker, not even the weird ex girlfriend she was dealing with today. She was happy, at least in those moments. 



+



"Mm, where are you going?" Soomi groans, far too tired from the night before to be bothered to move. However, she was forced to when Jungkook gently set her aside from being against his chest so he could get up. The thing was, she didn't remember him having a schedule today. She'd been hoping they could spend the day together to help her get her mind off of everything. She hadn't really got to do anything fun since she arrived back in Seoul, after all. 

"I have work today, Mi," he informs her, already across the room throwing on a loose t-shirt. 

Even more confused at his words, the girl stretches, preparing herself. With that, she sits up straight to watch him get dressed, reminiscing about the events of the night before. It almost distracted her from the matter at hand - almost.

"Wait, but you don't have work today?"

"I don't mean that kind of work," he chuckles, walking back over to the bed when he was finally dressed. Since she was by the edge, Jungkook is able to reach out and grab her, pulling her forward so she hit his stomach in a small hug. "I'm working on planning the rest of your birthday today, sending out the rest of the invitations."

She purses her lips to try to hide the smile. Jungkook really was a sweetheart. He definitely didn't have to throw her a big party, but here he was, trying to help her destress. 

"You really don't have to," she tries to say, but the boy scoffs, poking her nose playfully as he shakes his head.

"No shit, Mi, but you bet that I'm going to."

Pouting, she couldn't help but wonder, "who are you even inviting?"

"Hmm, just artists that I know that would love to come, you know, people that we're distant friends with," he looks to the ceiling in thought, "oh, I invited your gay lover."

She chokes on air in shock and confusion. "My what?!"

"Jennie."

"Oh my god," she burts into fits of hysterics, "stop, you didn't fucking call Jennie my gay lover."

"I don't know, I had her number from meeting her at award shows," he explains, "I called her to tell her about the party and she just started going on and on about how she can't wait to come spoil you and then she started asking me questions about who the hell you're with now. By the end, she was threatening me and telling me that you would just be better off with her, it was honestly sort of intimidating if I didn't know I was like a foot taller than her."

Solar is shaking her head in disarray, "that kinda sounds like something Jennie would do."

"It's okay," he shrugs, "rather her than Jinhwan."



134:00 | familiarity

"Kim Soomi!" Jennie exclaims, waving her arms in the air like a crazy person as she jogs to the girl at full speed. Her eyes are twinkling under the lights in the hall of YG ENT, and she giddily screeches like a child as she pulls the girl in for a hug, "I've missed you, it's been so long!"

Solar accepts her hug gratefully, the girl nearly squeezing the life out of her. "I missed you too, Nene," she gushes, poking the girl's cheek in a playful manner as they pull away.

"You have to come stay with us, we need to catch up," she urges with a dull expression, before a certain thought seems to come to her mind. "And you need to explain to me what the hell is going on with your love life, I know I saw you with Jinhwan at their manager's funeral," she pauses for a second, looking to the ceiling as if paying her respects, "it really was sad, you know?"

"Yeah, it was," is all she says, because Jennie was not done talking. Whenever she got excited, she could speak for hours on end without breathing. It was awkward to think about the boy's manager that she witnessed die, the woman who tormented her and wanted her dead, but she knew she couldn't go about and tell Jennie that. Not that she didn't trust her, it was just better that way. Those events were something Solar needed to put behind her.

"Anyway, so tell me why Jeon Jungkook is throwing you a birthday bash?!" she whispers in an eager-to-know tone, linking her arm with Soomi's and dragging her away to an empty dance practice room so they could talk privately.

Closing the door, Jennie locks it before turning back to her. Solar had been pursing her lips, trying to figure out the right words to say, but she was at a loss. Where did she even start anymore?

"Well?" Jennie hums, raising a brow, "I knew you were a bad bitch, but I didn't think you'd two time two of the biggest idols in the industry. Whew, my dear Soomi."

"No!" she cries, chuckling in disbelief, "no, really, it's not like that."

"Okaaay," she crosses her arms over her chest, "so what's it like? I already know you're cheating on me."

Solar's cheeks blush a shade of pink and she hurriedly shakes her head, "Yah, Jennie!"

The girl only giggles in response. By that time, she figured it was enough stalling, "I was dating Jinhwan around the time we were at KCON. And of course, you know Jungkook is my ex fiance."

"Mhm, the infamous dating scandal that was the talk of 2019," she recalls, "how could I forget?"

"yeah, well, Jinhwan got caught up with his ex-girlfriend when I was in New York," she explains, "so we broke up, and I've just sorta been with Jungkook since I got back. I never didn't have feelings for him, and I adore Jinhwan, too. It's just like, I can't figure out who I want, you know?"

Jennie pouts, shaking her head as she processes everything she's being told, "wow, I can't believe he'd cheat on you - unless, you're talking about that Jimin chick, huh? She's fucking crazy, I always see her roaming around the halls like she's even an artist here."

Solar nods in exasperation, pointing a finger at her, "yeah, yeah, her! That's why I feel sorta bad, like she's just obsessed with him and won't leave him alone."

"Ugh, I think that you two were a cute match though," she shares in contemplation, "but Soomi and Jungkook back together? The media will eat that one up."

"Yeah, it seems that everyone is more supportive of it nowadays, though."

When she looked back from having had been staring at the ceiling, Jennie was smirking at her like some sort of psychopath plotting something. It was a look she only made when she was about to say something really dumb.

"Don't say it," she tried to say, but it was too late by then.

"You know the media would like Soomi and Jennie together more."

"Oh my god."


+


A deep exhale leaves the lips of Solar, her boots clicking against the tiled floor of the hallway. It was exactly four days until her birthday and the huge party Jungkook was throwing her, and a week until the release of her new EP she'd been writing and recording ever since she was in New York. Today was the only day off she'd have until the release, not including her birthday, as she'd be having to film the music video and perfecting the choreography. With her hectic schedule for the next week, it left her with something to sort out on that day, which was the only reason she was making her way through the halls of YG Entertainment. 

There was no telling how many times she'd walked this exact route. As soon as she sees the familiar door with that same logo printed on it, she doesn't even bother to knock. 

Solar waltzes in the dance studio as if it was her own. Since the walls were sound proof, she'd had no idea that they were mid practicing a new choreo. The sight caught her off guard as she entered, no one seeming to notice her at first from how into it they were.

The brunette was planning to walk right up to the boy and practically drag him out of there by the ear. That was, until she got distracted by whatever they were performing. It had to be a new song due to the fact she knew every one of their songs and didn't recognize it. The lyrics seemed to poke at her, even though she only heard the smallest of parts, hypnotized by the sight of a certain caramel boy.

When finally, Donghyuk notices her through the big mirrors that surrounded the walls. Turning off the stereo that was playing the song, he turns to greet her with a grin, "oh, hey Soo, what's up?! I thought you were off today."

The others turn to the words their member spoke. Jinhwan was no doubt caught highly off guard from the fact she was here unannounced - especially after the events from a few days ago. It still fueled him with anger just thinking about her being with Jungkook.

But he had to accept it, at least they were on good terms now, right?

That was what he thought.

Until Soomi doesn't speak a word. The look on her face was rather scary, and the boys grew confused and almost afraid when she started speed walking towards them. The only thing was, her eyes were only fixated on one individual that happened to be the eldest.

"Looks like someone's in trouble," Junhoe leaned over to whisper to the others, which only resulted in him getting slapped by Yunhyeong.

She still doesn't say a word. Her two fingers only grab on Jinhwan's ear, and without a second thought she began dragging him towards the door. As soon as her fingers landed on his ear, the boy blinks in confusion, "yah, what are you - jinjja, what the fuck?!"

"We gotta talk," is all she deadpans, finally letting him go once they get to the door.

Rubbing his ear, Jinhwan blinks to her in bewilderment, "you could've just said that."

The girl only shakes her head at him, before motioning for him to follow her out of the room. Jinhwan purses his lips, clearly feeling a little anxious about whatever this was about. Though he had a good idea of what it had to do with, he couldn't be so sure, it could be Jungkook or even about the stalker for all he knew. He could only glance back to the others in worry, who just send him a smile and a thumbs up, urging him to follow her.

So he does, down the hall and into her empty dance studio since it was the closest, most private place they could speak. 

Solar waits with the door open until he enters before she closes and clicks the lock. As she turned around, she was aggressively hit in the face with things she didn't expect. The certain familiar feeling flushes through her when she meets Jinhwan's expectant eyes, and she nearly melted right then and there.

It was just too familiar.

Trying to push all the memories of them to the back of her head, she swallows the lump in her throat. Finally bursting out, "so you want to tell me why your weird ex came to my recording studio to tell me to stay away from you because you're happily back together?"

Solar sort of knew the answer to the question already, didn't she? What was she really looking for? Or was it something else she was doing this for?

"Huh?" confusion washes over his face, brows knitting together as he processes what she said. Eyes diverting to his feet, he shakes his head in disbelief, "you're kidding, did she seriously do that?"

"Uh, yeah," she snaps matter-of-factly, "she was acting like we're still a thing or literally all over each other or something. We've barely talked other than at the funeral," she was ranting in annoyance, crossing her arms over her chest and leaning against the wall, "and why does this chick think she can command me?! God, you better not date anything like that, seriously!"

By the end of her exhilarated rant, Jinhwan was staring at her with a goofy smile on his face and a humorous tint in his eyes. "Soo, I'm definitely not getting back with a psychopath," he tells her slowly, "but - if we were, why do you seem so vividly upset by it?"

Her mouth is slightly open from his question. Solar's eyes meet his once again, and there's a tiny smirk on his face. It was the first time she had ever experienced Kim Jinhwan in such a teasing manner, and upon realizing what she was doing, she resisted the urge to slam her head into the wall. 

"No!" she cries immediately, holding a hand to her head, "I just can't fucking stand some weirdo trying to command me like a dog and tell me who I can't be with!"

Still not believing her, Jinhwan only nods, "right, well, that's Jimin. You have to ignore her, I already got the staff to stop letting her in and I'm working on another restraining order much harsher this time." At his words, Solar lets out an unplanned breath of relief. Jinhwan chuckles, leaning in a bit to whisper, "so you have nothing to worry about."

"Shut up!" she cries, hitting his face hard enough for him to back up. He was laughing at how fun it was to mess with her, before the mood slowly got serious again once he noticed she wasn't feeling too humored. 

She obviously had a lot she wanted to say. Jinhwan's lips form a straight line in contemplation as he only watches her, his head spinning as he tries to put any pieces together as of what to do right then. More than anything, he missed her, he missed the feeling of her skin against his, the feeling of grace that he felt when she was by his side. Every aspect of Kim Soomi was something Jinhwan missed. 

But he thought they agreed she was going to try it out with Jungkook. She was going to give him a chance so she could come to fully understand everything she felt for the two of them. 

Though that didn't mean she didn't miss him, too. He knew that that was the real reason she was standing there in front of him. 

"What's going on with you, Soo?" he blurts, "you're doing good with Jungkook, no? I know that you didn't believe what that crazy girl said to you, so why are you really here?"

A look of defeat flies over her face. Having had her arms crossed from throwing her tantrum, they fall to her side as a breath of frustration leaves her lips. Solar ends up pacing away from Jinhwan as she tries to think of what she wanted to say. 

"I don't know!" she finally cries, "me and Jungkook are great, he's throwing me the party, and he's so excited about it. I keep just trying to let go and enjoy everything that's happening for me right now but I'm still confused and afraid."

"Soomi," Jinhwan tries to say but she only kept going.

"I really did just want to tell you about what happened because I wanted to make sure you're getting rid of that girl, she's literally crazy. But of course I also wanted to see you, okay? It's not that I don't feel comfortable or am happy with Jungkook, but I don't want to burst his bubble right now," her voice falters, and she shakes her head in anger from how she couldn't seem to find the right words, "you just bring me comfort no one else seems to, Nani."

Silence.

She thought he was going to leave or something. Maybe he was really just not in the mood for her and her confusing problems anymore. She didn't know how Jungkook or Jinhwan could deal with her, fluctuating one to another day after day.

Until a familiar scent of cologne filled her nostrils, and the warmth of his body overcame her as she was pulled into his chest. 

And there it was, that feeling of safety, that feeling of content. It was a feeling of familiarity from how her life had been everyday for the last year and a half. It wasn't like Jungkook didn't give her that, but she couldn't explain it. It was different.

Always different.

And she felt horrible for everything she seemed to put everyone through. Not just them, but her friends, her family.

All she could do was sigh as she buried her face in his chest, "I'm sorry, Nani."

Rubbing circles on her back, he kisses the side of her head. "Don't apologize, Soo, you've always had so much going on. You never get a break from anything despite how much you deserve one."

There's still a dull look on her face when they depart.

"And I'm always here for you, even though we're not together, even though you're technically with Jungkook, I'll always be here," he states sternly, "don't be afraid to come to me when you can't take him anymore."

Despite how down she was suddenly feeling, she chuckles at his words, "shush, Jungkook isn't that bad."

But the boy was not interested. "Don't lie, he's the cockiest asshole I've ever met."

Solar wrinkles her nose, "you don't remember Sunwoo?"

"Sure, but at least he had room to be cocky - you know, mafia leader and all."

She shrugs, "yeah, true. Jungkook is just confident," she pauses, glancing to the boy who was now leaning against the wall like she had been moments ago, "hey, you're the one who popped him in the nose the other day, though."

"Yeah, because he literally fucking asked for it!"

"Okay, calm down, tiger," she snorts, receiving an eye roll from Jinhwan, "I'm messing with you, he probably deserved it."

Her phone buzzes in her back pocket, cutting her off from saying anything else.

mi: ILL BE BACK SOON OK I LOVE U PLS 

kookie: ugh


"Duty calls?" Jinhwan hums from his stance, seeming to already know.

Solar only nods.

"Do you want me to take you home?" he asks when she's already heading towards the door.

"It's okay, Junhoe said he would," she shakes her head, pursing her lips as her hand is on the handle, before turning back to face him, "I'll see you at the party?"

Thinking for a second, he finally sighs, a gentle smile forming on his face, "yeah, of course, Soo."

"Okay, see you then, Nani."

"You too, Soo."


135:00 | the best surprise

Days passed before she knew it, which was always what happened when she was hard at work preparing for a comeback. Schedule days would fly by like wind. Except for on that day, it wasn't the release of her new EP just yet, it was her birthday. Solar, unlike Jungkook, was not that excited for her birthday. Her birthday had never been the most memorable for her since she came to Korea. Last year, it was just spent with Jinhwan trying to help her as a drunken mess, after all...


"Kim Soomi!" The boy is crying, a rather distressed look wiped on his face. Yet, somehow, when he looks at the helpless girl, he smiles like he never smiled before. Ear to ear, brown eyes twinkling in the dim light of her apartment's kitchen floor. "I'm not letting you do this to yourself on such a special day," he states sternly, bending down to put his arms under hers so he could lift her up to the ground, "look, we're going out, okay? You can't be like this on your birthday."

But as soon as he tried to let go of her so she could stand on her own, the small brunette only stumbled to the ground again - that was, before he caught her again. There was a lifeless look on Soomi's face, one he had gotten used to seeing since this was how her state tended to be these days. Jinhwan often wondered what it would be like to see this girl happy, to see her free, careless. He wished that he could do that for her, it was what she deserved.

His hands subconsciously gripped onto her hips to hold her up. Soomi wasn't focused on that, though, she was only longingly staring at the ground. Finally, a weak mutter leaves her lips, "I miss Jungkook, Nani. We always spent my birthday together..."

And just like every time he heard her talk about how much she missed her ex fiance, a ping stabbed at his heart. Jinhwan decided a long time ago that nothing in the world would add up to the hatred he had for Jeon Jungkook, absolutely nothing. 

Look at what he'd done to the only girl Jinhwan had ever liked.

"Come on, Soomi," Jinhwan urges, forcing a smile on his face, "he doesn't deserve having happy memories living inside of you. You can still be happy on your own - or with me."

The drunk girl suddenly hiccups, a hand going over her mouth to suppress them. Blinking a few times, she seemed to be attempting to take in what he said, when she sighs, "but it's so different w-without him here."

"Different isn't always bad, Soo," he informs her, "you just have to embrace the change."

Her brows knit together as she tries to understand, "e-embrace the change?"

Squeezing her hips, he nods triumphantly, "mhm, which is why we're gonna get you a cold shower and then you're gonna do some push ups!"

Suddenly, the girls green eyes whip to him in shock, "what?! I don't want to do push ups!"

But Jinhwan was already dragging her by the hand out of the kitchen and down the hall to her bathroom, "you don't have a say. We're sobering you up so we can go celebrate at your favorite ramen place."

As soon as she heard ramen, she was in, anyway. 

So that was where you'd find the girl, the girl who was supposed to be miserable. The media had no idea what happened to Kim Soomi since the scandal broke out and her company reported she would be going on a hiatus to recover from the damage it "caused on her career." Really, it was the damage it caused on her health. Everyone seemed to know that was the case, which is why everyone assumed she was probably cooped up by herself as a complete and utter disaster.

Though the assumptions weren't wrong, they sure weren't right either. Because she had Kim Jinhwan. 

Her savior - even if he was making her do push ups at 5 in the morning.

He still did nothing but help her. All he ever wanted to do was make her happy, and she would hold that dear to her heart for the rest of her life.

No matter what happens.

"Let's order soju!" 

Jinhwan's eyes are bewildered but his face was humored as he looks up to her from his food. Chopsticks in hand, he was just about to take another bite of ramen when she said such. Shoving the food in his mouth, he sighs as he shakes his head, "Soomi, you're not even sobered up yet, we're not ordering soju."

The brunette rolls her eyes, sticking her tongue out at him like a child. "You're so lame, Kim Jinhwan, not even on my birthday."

"Yeah," he hums, "that's why I bought you $1000 worth of clothes," he sarcastically retorts, and the girl glances to the shopping bags besides her.

With a shrug, she nods, "fine, you win. I gratefully accept your gift."

He sends her that bright smile of his, and like every other time, there was that twinkle in his eye. Though when Soomi met his eyes that morning, he swore he saw that same twinkle in her own.


"HAPPY BIRTHDAY!!!" a chorus of screams filled her ears, and she wasn't able to mentally prepare for the experience she was about to have. 

"HAPPY BIRTHDAY TO YOU, HAPPY BIRTHDAY TO YOU, HAPPY BIRTHDAY DEAR AWARD WINNING, SONG OF THE YEAR, ALBUM OF THE YEAR, BEST FEMALE RAPPER, BEST GIRL CHOREOGRAPHY, SINGER SONGWRITER RAPPER-"

"Dude, just fucking get on with it."

"HAPPY BIRTHDAY DEAR SOOMI!"

The girl looked like an absolute train wreck as she sat up in Jungkook's bed. He clearly wasn't beside her anymore since he was too busy leading the party of boys who just screamed the birthday song at her. Rubbing her eyes, it still hurt to open them as she looks to them in awe, "you guys are fucking insane."

"Would you still call me insane if I told you I was forced to do this?" Yoongi asks from the back of the crowd, earning him a very harsh glare from the likes of the maknae. Once he catches it, he chuckles, walking to the front to give the girl a side hug, "kidding, happy birthday, Mi. Though you might want to fix that mop on your head."

She rolls her eyes but sends him a smile anyway, "I appreciate it, Yoongles."

The boy only made a fake gagging sound at the nickname.

Before anyone else could say anything, Jungkook giddily jumps forward to grab her hands, practically yanking her out of bed. "I hope you're ready for your big day," he's humming cheerily, "I'm making up for last year today and making sure you remember this forever."

"Yeah, it's definitely not that good of a par-" Jin tries to say, but Jungkook lunges a pillow at him since it was the closest thing to grab.

"As I was saying, I worked very hard on this day," he finishes, "so make sure you're ready by 7 PM, okay?!"

Solar could only gawk at him like he was crazy. Really, he sort of seemed like someone had drugged him. She had never seen Jeon Jungkook so excited for anything in his life. 

"Okay, Kookie," she finally breathes, "you got it, 7 PM."

He grins from ear to ear in excitement, "I probably won't see you until later, though," he pouts as he realizes, "I still have to make sure everything is set up at the venue."

Soomi's face falls at this, too, because she figured that she would be with Jungkook for most of the day before the party. 

However, the boy starts grinning that devious grin that would scare anyone who saw it. The girl looks at him weirdly, trying to understand what was going on. She guessed she could hang around with the other boys, particularly Jimin. It wasn't like that would be so bad. Some quality best friend time was also a perfect thing on a birthday.

"Which is why Jungkook called the best of the best, of course!"

The voice nearly made her fall on the floor. She thought she must be intoxicated until a familiar scent of perfume fills her nose. The girl whips around, and as soon as she sees them, a high pitched screech of joy leaves her mouth.

"Choi fucking Chanhee!" she screams, running forward like a psychopath and nearly knocking him over from the hug she threw onto him.

"Indeed, it's me, you bitch," he hums right back, squeezing the life out of her, but she loved every second of it, "you think I'd miss your birthday after all these years? I'm offended."

"Of course not!" she cries, overwhelmed with everything that was happening as they pull away, "it's just - Korea? You came to Korea?!"

"Mhm, your little lover boy over here called us and personally asked us to come," Chanhee informs, motioning to the doe boy still standing feet away behind her. Solar looks to Jungkook in bewilderment, her eyes asking, you really did this? But he only nods with that big bunny teeth smile on his face.

When she turns back, her eyes were met with the expectant expressions of Changmin, Kevin, and Jacob. "Where's my love, Sol?!" Kevin is gasping.

Shaking her head, she pulls him in for a hug, "right here, idiot."

"Happy birthday, Solar!" Jacob is cheering with Changmin as she's greeting them, too. 

"I still can't believe you guys are here," she's coughing a bit, trying to suppress any emotion that might escape her. 

"Oh God, don't tell me you're going to cry," Chanhee monotones, but the girl only looks to him with teary eyes, "fucking Christ, you're actually going to cry."

Even though his demeanor was rather harsh and sassy, it was just how he showed his love. Chanhee still pulled the girl in for another hug. As soon as they saw Solar was crying, the other three best friends joined in. Before you knew it, they were in one big group hug.

"Sorry!" she's crying as they pull away again, "this is such an emotional day, okay?"

"Don't worry, we understand, Sol," Changmin assures her kindly.

Turning back to Jungkook, he was just getting some stuff together he wanted to bring to the venue. As soon as he sees that she's coming up to him, he halts his actions.

"Thank you, Jungkook," she offers breathlessly, "for everything."

"Of course, Mi," he gushes, leaning in to leave a short kiss on her forehead, "this day is for you. Have fun with your best friends, okay? I'll see you tonight."

"Yeah," she jumpily hums, already thinking about everything she wanted to show her friends in Seoul, "I'll see you tonight, Kookie!"

"I love you, Soomi-ah."


+


"You should've punched her," Chanhee deadpans, a clear look of anger on his face from the story he just heard, "matter of fact, let me come to the studio, I will gladly punch her - repeatedly."

The friends weren't the happiest after hearing the story about Jinhwan's psycho ex-girlfriend. They also weren't all too fond of Jinhwan after what he'd done, but she insisted to them that he wasn't so bad and it really wasn't his fault. As much as they were iffy about it, they believed her.

"Wait, Sol!" Kevin pipes up abruptly, eyes turning into giants from how excited he was at the thought, "I heard you met Haechan from NCT!"

At first, she was confused. With everything else she'd been dealing with, the last thing she remembered was that. Especially after catching them up on everything including the funeral and the weird silence that had been going on between the other stalker, the last thing she remembered to tell specifically Kevin was that. 

She guessed Yein covered that for her.

"Right," she chuckles in disbelief at how obsessed her friend was, "I sure did."

Taking another bite of kimchi, the boy gawks, "and you really didn't introduce me?!"

"Probably because you're a fucking freak," Chanhee cuts in. 

"I am not!" he defends himself with a hand to his chest, "just because I own all of their albums and merely enjoy their music."

The pink haired flower boy only rolls his eyes, scoffing under his breathe, "yet I get called the gay one."

"Chill, Kev," she tells him, "I was sorta dealing with something then, didn't really think about it."

He sighs in sadness, "it's okay, I don't know what I'd honestly even say."

She shrugs, "you could just go straight in and ask them on a date."

The others start snickering from the fact Solar out of all people just said that to him. There's a proud smile on Chanhee's face, and Kevin's mouth was slightly agape as he tried to think of what to say. 

"I'm not gay," he finally states sternly, "I know you all think I swing that way but I do not."

"You're a bad liar," Changmin hums. 

"I'm not gay!" he cries, much louder than planned. This earned the looks of many people sitting around them in the cafe. Thank God he was speaking English so they didn't know how odd of a conversation the group was having.

Laughing at how defensive he always got over such a subject, Solar chortles, "I was kidding anyway, sort of."

Kevin only groans, "stop! I don't want to talk about this anymore!"

"Me neither, it's disturbing to hear you attempt to cover up," Chanhee is clearly disappointed. Instead, he looks to Solar again, "so, Sol, tell us about your utterly confusing love life."

So that was what she would do. For the rest of the day, she would update them on everything she possibly could. The friends were so happy to see Solar actually seeming to feel a bit better. They were worried about how her mental state would hold up after the events of everything that went down in New York. But surely enough, their Sol was stronger than they ever anticipated.

Even with everything, she found a way to smile, she found a way to be happy. Solar felt like today really would be a special day for her, especially now that she got to spend it showing her best friends around the beautiful city of Seoul. 


136:00 | birthday extravaganza


"This is the place according to the address Jungkook sent," Chanhee had been saying. The group of friends were only in awe though once they got out of the Uber, paying them before bidding goodbye. Solar leads the way, dressed so nicely you would've guessed she was the mess she was this morning. Her green eyes only widened the more she looked up at the venue, it was one of the most expensive in Seoul. And Jungkook had really rented it all out just for her.

"There's even a red carpet at the entrance, I feel like I'm famous!" Kevin is gushing in excitement as they're walking towards the front doors.

"Well, you're not, idiot, but Sol sure is," Chanhee hums, "Sol, stop by the door and let me take your picture!"

The girl wrinkles her nose at her best friend's words. Still, she stops in front of the giant double doors, turning to face him.




"Perf- actually, your face looks a little odd, but it's fine!" Chanhee throws his hands in the air cheerily, walking to catch up with her.

She only shakes her head in disappointment, "even on my birthday you insult me."

He only winks at her, "it's because I love you, Sol."

The girl only glares at him, a clear look of annoyance on her face. Chanhee was right, though, that was how he showed affection. 

"Don't worry, Sol, you look great," Jacob pats her on the back assuringly, that angelic smile making her believe him.

Grinning giddily, she reaches her hand up to pinch his cheek, "thanks, Baecop."

"Let's go inside already, I can't wait to see the venue!" Kevin is jumping around, "Oh, and aren't there gonna be a ton of idols here?!"

Heaving out a groan, Chanhee rubs his temples, "Kevin, please shut the fuck up, kindly."

The moon boys face falters, "okay."

"We should go inside though, Jungkook's gonna be mad because it's 7:05 and not 7:00 sharp," Solar is chuckling, turning to open one of the big double doors.

And just as she's gripping the handle, a voice causes her actions to halt.

"Aw, come on, little sister, wait for us."

Mouth falling open like a hippo, she turns on one foot in awe, "no - fucking - way."

But despite her disbelief, there they were, standing feet away from her. 

"Hey, you gorgeous bitch!" Chaerin screeches, arms open wide as Solar runs full speed at her, "you're so fucking old!"

"Fuck you!" she cries, except her voice was as happy sounding as it had ever been. Solar hugs all of her siblings with all her might, "Jungkook seriously invited you guys, too?!"

"Mhm, Yein couldn't come, though, for obvious reasons like the fact our parents are controlling," Jaehyun informs, wrapping an arm around her shoulder. He almost smelled like a gang member, the same lingering scent of smoke and cologne, minus the fact he wasn't one. "I gotta say, I'm impressed, this guy seems real into you to rent out a place like this," chuckling, he hits her back rather hard, nearly causing her to fall over, "you know how to pick 'em, little sister."

Solar pretends to punch him, narrowing her eyes, "at least I'm not fucking lonely."

Jaehyun snorts, putting his hands up in defense, "hey, don't take it as an insult. And I'm pretty far from lonely."

She wrinkles her nose in disgust at the oldest Lee sibling, "TMI, Jae."

The boy only laughs in response.

With that, Solar leads the group inside the venue. There's a huge grin on her face, she'd never felt like everything was really coming together so well. She couldn't believe that Jungkook really got her siblings and childhood best friends to come. She was starting to understand what he meant when he said he wanted to make it memorable. He really was such a sweetheart.

For the first time in too long, Solar Lee's head was in the clouds. Too bad that it was one of the worst times for such a thing. 

She was lucky she had so many people that had her back. It wasn't as if she was dumb, she had guessed that the stalker was planning something big. She just had hoped for the best, hoped that she could have one day to be happy, to allow herself to let go and feel free with the people she loved. 

But it would be far from that.

As soon as the girl's back is to the siblings, they exchange knowing glances, nodding to each other. They were there to celebrate with their sister, but that wasn't their only purpose for attending that night. This was shown by the hidden weapons in their pockets, after all.

Good thing Solar didn't notice.

Entering the venue, it was practically pitch dark. This caught her severely off guard, her brows knitting together as she tries to maneuver around. Her friends and siblings are following her, confusion also written over their faces.

"It's a bit - empty in here," Changmin is mumbling.

That's when all the lights came on in the flash of an eye. Neon lights surround her in a wave, and her eyes bulge as she was nearly blinded by the sudden commotion. That's when a chorus of yells burst out, people jumping from their hiding places as they scream, "HAPPY BIRTHDAY SOOMI!"

Holding a hand over her mouth, her green eyes shine as she looks around at all the people. Solar had a fine amount of idol friends in the industry, but it wasn't like she spoke to any of them that often. Her life had been far too messy for her to be in contact with anyone other than her "inner circle." Yet, here they all were, even some she didn't know that well, jumping around and cheering happy birthday to her.

All she could do was gawk in awe, shaking her head in disbelief as her friends are beaming beside her. "Holy fucking shit," she hears Chaerin whisper. 

"I've never seen so many famous people," Kevin is muttering, his voice higher than a mouse. He sounded as if he was about to faint, and Chanhee scoffs, elbowing him in the chest and telling him to shut the fuck up.

"I've invited you here tonight to celebrate Soomi's 24th birthday," a voice speaks, causing everyone to turn to the stage that was in the middle of the venue. There he was, Jeon Jungkook, dressed as dazzling as ever, a black loose shirt hanging off his shoulders. His hair was long, waves flowing down the sides of his head, and under the spotlight of the stage, he was glowing. "Soomi, as you all know, is very dear to me, and she's been going through a lot of stress recently you probably don't know of," he explains into the mic, and a chorus of aw's are heard, "I want tonight to be a time for her to finally let go and enjoy the beautiful life she's been giving along with the amazing people she's blessed to call friends."

A chorus of cheers explode from the "audience." Jungkook grins with those bunny teeth of his, leaning in the mic to say one last thing as his eyes wander over to meet hers, "in a bit I'll also be performing a new song I wrote just for Mi, I hope you look forward to it. But before then, feel free to bombard Soomi with love!"

There's that twinkle in his eye as soon as his eyes meet hers.


Mesmerized, that's all she was and ever would be when Jeon Jungkook walked around looking like that. She could only watch as he waved before walking off the stage. Meanwhile, her friends and siblings were still looking around at everyone, in complete awe of the scene.

When suddenly, Kevin starts screeching. It was similar to the sound an ambulance would make, and being that there were already people making their way over to say hi to Solar, it was not something that he needed to be doing.

Chanhee, completely not in the mood, elbows him once again, cursing under his breath, "can you stop having a conniption in front of every celebrity in South Korea?!"

Trying to contain himself, the moon boy holds a hand to his head, his breathing as heavy as someone would be if they were having a heart attack. "Chanhee, fucking NCT is here," he breathes, his eyes fixated on the group of boys, "oh my god, Solar has to introduce me."

The flower boy turns to look at the girl, who was obviously busy chatting away with people, "I think she's a little preoccupied."

"I need to meet them!" he's whining, and Chanhee rubs his temples as he resists the urge to beat up Kevin Moon.

On the other hand, Solar was receiving more hugs than she had in god knows how long. Everyone was so sweet and ecstatic to give her gifts and wish her a happy birthday. She was getting to talk to idols and celebrities she hadn't seen in what felt like forever. It felt great to catch up with everyone.

"I should leave you alone now, but happy birthday, Soomi, really!" Chungha had been telling her, her eyes full of kindness as she hugs her one last time.

"Thank you!" she gushes back at her, waving as the girl heads back to where she was sitting.

After it felt like she had talked to every single person - well, not really - in the venue, she was more than ready to sit down. Solar was extremely curious what the song was that Jungkook would perform. If it was as emotional as Shadow, she was honestly afraid.

But surely this would be a much more positive song than the one he performed at KCON that day.

"Mi!" a bright voice she'd recognize anywhere calls out to her. She turns to see the sight of Park Jimin running up to her, his arms engulfing her in a giant hug, "happy birthday, you look great!"

"Thanks, Chimmy," she beams as they pull away, "do you know where Jungkook wants us to sit?"

The mochi boy turns to glance around at the massive venue full of guests, blinking in thought. "Hmm, probably in front of the stage, it's near the iKON guys and us, too," he informs, "here, follow me."

She nods, motioning for her siblings and friends to follow as well. With that, she heads after Jimin through the tons of tables and booths people were seating at, chatting away, drinking, and overall having a good time. This meant she ended up having to talk to and greet many more people, but it wasn't a problem for Solar. She loved everyone that was there, after all, they were all dear friends to her.

"Good to see you!" she's waving and calling, "thanks for making it!"

Until finally they reach the table, a deep breath leaves her lips as she's able to sit down after all that. Solar gets situated before she glances around more at all the guests, still having a hard time believing that this was real. 

Would she really be able to let go for one night?

That's when her eyes land on him. After all, Jimin had told her they were seated in the same area.

Kim Jinhwan in all his glory.

The boy catches her stare, and a kind smile makes it's way over his face. "Happy birthday," he mouths to her, making a weird face afterwards. She only shakes her head at him, trying to fight the smile that was forming, until he holds up two fingers to form a heart.

He was cute.

Solar feels her cheeks heating up from his antics, laughing a bit as she looks away. She was surprised, she thought Jinhwan would be in a much more bitter state from attending her birthday party that Jungkook planned.

Perhaps he was just cheering up for her, it was like him to do something like that.

"God, you're so confusing," Chaerin is sighing from beside her, having had seen every second of the interaction.

Almost ashamed she got caught technically flirting with her ex, she pouts, "leave me alone, okay? You know it's complicated."

"He's lucky I don't go break his balls," she states, sounding extremely serious because she was. Chaerin was scary, and Solar didn't doubt that she probably really wanted to do that.

"He's not like that, okay?" she insists sternly, "it was a huge misunderstanding."

"It's still not okay, Solar," she refuses to accept it, flipping her dark hair over her shoulder, "he can rot."

Solar only rolls her eyes, knowing that her sister was just being biased. She literally loved Jinhwan only a month ago, but she could understand why she was still suspicious towards him after what happened. That was just her being a sister.

"Oh my God, OH my GOD, is this real? Someone pinch me," Kevin had started panicking all over again, causing everyone at the table to turn to him in annoyance.

"What is it this time?" Jacob asks.

"Wait, be cool, Haechan from NCT is coming over, Solar!" he's nearly screaming, turning to Changmin that was beside him, "Changmin, is my hair okay?!"

Chanhee makes a sound of disgust as Changmin was gently fixing Kevin's hair, "God, you're so fucking gay."

"Not gay," the boy holds up a finger, "just tasteful."

"Right."

Meanwhile, Solar had turned to see if what he said was really true. She knew she did see the boys of NCT here, a whole whopping 23 of them. Solar never knew them too well, but she guessed Jungkook must have invited them. That day at KCON was the one and only time she ever spoke to Haechan, so it was weird to think he was coming over to personally greet her.

But she didn't mind it. He had been quite the sweetheart when they met.

Though as soon as she turns around, there he was, his sweet eyes peering down at her. She didn't know much about this boy, but he almost seemed nervous as he softly grins down at her, before bowing, "hey Soomi-ah, I don't know if you remember me from KCON?"

Solar giggles, nodding vigorously, "yeah, of course I do, Haechan!" she motions to the empty seat beside her that had been Jaehyun's, but he was missing somewhere - probably in the restroom, "here, sit down!"

He looks surprised at her insistence, but nonetheless obliges. Haechan smiles at everyone else sitting at the table as he hesitantly takes his seat, swallowing a lump in his throat. "I just wanted to wish you a happy birthday," he offers, "this is a super cool party Jungkook's set up."

"Yeah," Solar agrees, "I appreciate it! He really seemed to work hard."

Just when her and Haechan were beginning to chat about stupid things that didn't matter, she caught sight of Kevin. The poor boy looked like he was going to have a panic attack and start screaming at the top of his lungs. Knowing how much this probably meant to him, she clears her throat, motioning in his direction, "Actually, Haechan, I wanted to introduce you to someone. This is my friend, Kevin, he's a very big fan of NCT."

"Ahh~," Haechan hums in understanding, looking in the direction she was motioning. His eyes fall on Kevin, presumably the one she was talking about since he looked like he was about to explode, and he bows politely, "nice to meet you!"

Everyone looks at Kevin, waiting for him to say something. "I-It's an honor!" he bursts out, and Chaerin and Chanhee start snickering at how embarrassing this idiot was.

Solar is laughing, too, in a light hearted manner as Haechan still nicely engages in conversation with Kevin. She really admired how polite and sweet he was. 

Though there was something she was curious about - or more like, a few things, as she leaned over to whisper to Chaerin, "hey, where are Jaehyun and Juyeon?"

Since there wasn't the best lighting in the venue being that it was a rented out nightclub, she couldn't see the way Chaerin tensed up. She hated lying to her sister, especially when it was something she felt like Solar should know about, but what could she do? Her brothers, and especially him, would kill her if she just blurted out what they were doing. In the end, anyway, it wasn't like they were causing any disruption to the party.

So, swallowing the lump in her throat, she shrugs it off like nothing, "no idea, probably flirting with random women."

Solar gawks from her words, "oh fuck, you're right, they're going to embarrass the fuck out of me."

"Don't worry about it, Sol," Chaerin insists, "they'll be fine."

Since Chae wasn't one to say things like this, Solar seemed to believe her. "I guess so," she exhales, "I've got bigger worries right now, anyway." Her green eyes wander to Jinhwan, who was chatting away with some idols she didn't know that well. A part of her wanted to go talk to him so bad, to all of the boys since she really hadn't gotten to, but something just kept telling her she couldn't.

It sucked.

Thankfully, those two boys were all that was on her mind. For once, she wasn't worrying about the stalker, the person after her, the person who was smarter than the last. 

But maybe she should've been.


137:00 | not so safe

"Is everything clear?" the youngest brother had asked to the two boys, his hand still securely holding his weapon in his pocket.

Jaehyun only shrugs, not seeming to know the answer. Just because the two of them didn't initially see any signs of someone or an attack didn't mean it wasn't there somewhere. They both look to the boy who would know the answer, however, eyes insistent.

Kim Sunwoo runs a hand through his hair, his dark leather jacket hanging loosely off his shoulders. A headband was on his head to keep the hair out of his face, his eyes dark and full of concentration as he glanced around the venue, "we haven't seen them," he states slowly, "but they're here."

"What?" Jaehyun blinks, "but where?! I swear we scoped out every fucking inch of this place."

"No, they might not be here physically," he explains, "but they're here - they're definitely here."

Juyeon is vividly nervous about his words. "Can't we just tell Solar? This is dangerous and it's making me feel uneasy."

Sunwoo glares at him in annoyance that he would ask such a thing, "if it's making you feel uneasy, imagine how it'll make Solar feel," he heaves out a sigh, turning away from them and shaking his head, "just do another perimeter check."

The boys only nod in agreement, turning and heading the way to do exactly what he said. 



"Aw, he's so in love with you," Chaerin is gushing as they watch Jungkook walk away. He'd just come to their table to tell Solar a personal happy birthday and that he was about to perform his song. After leaving a tiny kiss on her forehead and flashing that big bunny smile, he'd jogged off towards the backstage, leaving her to sit there in awe.

Solar only sends her a disapproving glare, not in the mood for Chaerin to start going on about things like that again. Her sister didn't seem to intend to stop as she only raised a brow, eyes fixated on something else, "ooh, doesn't seem like Jinhwan liked that very much."

She was worried about what exactly she meant by that. Glancing in Jinhwan's direction, his eyes caught hers almost immediately as if he was already staring. Pursing her lips, her mind is in a trance as he attempts to smile at her, yet she could see the clear hurt in his eyes. It hurt her, too, knowing she was hurting someone who had helped her through so much. Jinhwan had always been so selfless towards her, but when he makes one mistake she throws him away. What kind of person did that make her?

It broke her heart the more she thought about it, the more she couldn't understand anything she was doing in her life. Was she really going to get back with Jungkook? It made sense at first in her head, it seemed like the right decision. But was she ready for that? Wasn't it wrong to rush into a relationship when you still loved someone else?

Solar couldn't really talk on that one.

Despite Jinhwan's efforts, she didn't have the heart to do much in return. She tries to smile back at him to the best of her ability even though her brain was thinking so hard.

Be happy, she tells herself mentally, this is your night.

That's when her phone buzzes against her foot, as she tucked it into her boot. Breaking eye contact with Jinhwan, who was evidently worried about her since he could tell she was thinking hard again, she bends down to pull it out of her shoe. 

Jinhwan's eyes don't move from her as he wonders what she was doing. Solar turns on her phone screen, figuring it would be social media alerts from fans or maybe a text from her parents. 

Unknown Number: happy birthday, "Solar." I've got the best present for you. :)

Her entire face falters, eyes widening in horror as she tries to decipher what she was reading. It didn't take a genius to know who it was, she knew from the second she saw that it was from an unknown number.

But why? Why on such a day?

All she wanted was one day.

"Guys, I'll be back, I need to run to the restroom," she tells her friends, sister, and Haechan, who was still sitting with them. They nod in understanding even though some of them were looking at her weirdly.

With that, she takes her phone and rises from her seat. When she does so, her eyes meet Jinhwan's again. He was clearly trying to get some form of information out of her with his eyes melting into hers, but she only looked away, rushing towards the back hallway where the restroom was located.

Jinhwan knew it wasn't good. He knew what it had to be. It was why he rose from his own seat, only to be stopped by a confused Hanbin.

"Where are you going? It's rude to just leave when someone's about to perform."

"You think I care?!" he snaps, because all of them knew the last thing he wanted to hear was Jeon Jungkook sing, "something is wrong with Soo, I'm going to check on her."

The boys all exchange nervous glances, knowing that didn't sound like the best idea. But they also knew Jinhwan was the one who'd be able to calm her down. 

"Okay, just don't be gone too long," Hanbin finally agrees, letting go of the short boy, but Jinhwan didn't bother to respond as he rushed after Solar.

Meanwhile, the girl found herself crumbling as she practically ran into the restroom. Thankfully, it appeared to be empty. Setting her phone down on the counter, she buried her face in her hands as she wept into them, feeling more hopeless than she had in a while.

She was so mad, so frustrated that she couldn't just live her life. What did she really do to deserve all of this? Just because she joined YG, because CEO-nim took a particular liking to her, it meant she deserved to be tormented, killed? What kind of people would think that? It wasn't her fault.

Yet she did technically kill someone. She assumed that was this new person's motive, but really, it still wasn't her fault.

Even though they didn't make any direct threats, she knew it meant something bad was coming. What else would a text like that mean? She should've known a big party like this would lead to something bad, but Jungkook swore the place had high security and it wouldn't be possible for them to pull anything. She should've reminded him just who they were dealing with, this person was more than an amateur.

They were more than any of them could take. She could only think of one person who could.

Sunwoo, but he wasn't here, he was all the way in New York. The only one who could possibly come to figure out anything about this person wasn't anywhere near her. He was right when he said none of them would be smart enough to protect her. He was right.

All Solar could do was cry, cry out of complete and utter fear for herself and her beloved friends. Though there were many better things she could be doing like telling Jungkook, she was breaking. She was falling apart until she couldn't think rationally anymore, just like she had many times before. It felt like her life was a montage of hurt.

As she sat there, knees pulled to her chest and her face buried in it, weeping like that of a child, the door burst open. The girl didn't even care to move, she didn't care who it was. It could be the stalker for all she cared, coming to just kill her once and for all, and she didn't care.

That was, until two arms wrap securely around her, pulling her into their chest. Hesitantly, her eyes flutter open to glance up at whoever it was, but as soon as that cologne filled her nostrils, she didn't need to.

"Jinhwan..." she whispers, eyes glassy as they peer up at him. 

"Soo," he whispers back, rubbing her back comfortingly, "what did they say? Tell me what happened."

The girl only sniffles, moving out of his arms to reach up to the counter and grab her phone. Weakly, she hands it to him, before snuggling her face back into the black shirt he was wearing. With one arm, he hugs her tightly, using the other to unlock her phone - that still had his fingerprint in it - it already being on the text she received.

His eyes scan over the words quickly, anger spreading over his face like a wild fire as he spouts, "this is fucking sick," with a sigh, he sets her phone down so he could wrap his other arm around her, "I'm sorry, Soo, this is disgusting. You don't deserve any of this."

Wiping her nose with the back of her hand, she shakes her head as she stares up at the boy, "I'm glad you're here, Nani."

Confused at her sudden words, he looks down to her curiously, knowing she meant that he was the one who came and checked on her. "I knew something was wrong the second you pulled out your phone," he breathes, "I'd never let you go through that alone."

Though she had finally stopped crying, when she looks into his eyes, her own start tearing up again. Jinhwan wrinkles his nose playfully, reaching his thumb up to wipe away the tear that was trying to escape her eye, "no more crying, Soo, I'm here with you, you're okay, I promise."

Solar only sniffles again, shaking her head in disbelief as she reaches a hand up to push one of those blonde locks out of his face. There was a certain phrase hanging on the tip of her tongue during that moment. 

Little did she know what was happening back in the main venue. Jungkook had walked on stage moments before Jinhwan had rushed after Soomi. He was completely oblivious to anything that was happening due to the fact he had been preparing for his performance backstage.

The boy didn't notice her absence as he sat down, clearing his throat into the mic and holding his guitar closely to his chest. "I've been working on this song for a while now, it's a special one I wrote for Soomi," he explains, "sort of like Shadow, but much happier this time, I promise."

A few cheers sound through the crowd, and a couple of screams are heard from the Bangtan boys, cheering on their beloved maknae. Jungkook is beaming as he starts to play the song, the soft melody making everyone want to sway to it. The boy closes his eyes as if he was becoming one with the song, leaning forward into the mic to sing. 

I could make it better,
I could hold you tighter on that long road,
You're the light
The only one who recognized me,
This eternal night with no end in sight,
It's you who gifted me the morning
I don't want to hear other people's noises,
Your fragrance still penetrates and breaks me down
Let's go back to that time

The words were soothing to anyone who could hear them. With each one that came from his lips, it was evident they had imminent meaning. Jungkook's fingers strummed the guitar he held so carefully, the melodic sounds making some people playfully dance around to the song. 

Everything was useless,
Anything other than you,
Just touch me like that time
Just hold me silently like you did that time,
The reason I survived in hell
It was for you, not for me
I know the sea without you is the same as a desert

The song wasn't anywhere near being over. But Jungkook had opened his eyes to look to Soomi so he could see her expression and if she was liking the song. It was supposed to be what he would play right before he asked her to be his girlfriend again officially.

But she wasn't there.

That's when the background music he'd been using and playing the guitar to was cut off. It was abrupt and clearly unplanned, causing everyone to look around in confusion as Jungkook slowly stopped playing his guitar. 

That's when the lights went out.

A chorus of gasps spread through out everyone. You could hear whispers and people asking what the hell was going on. Jungkook had set his guitar down, trying to head backstage so he could figure out what was happening.

Though he had a good idea of what it had to do with.

That's when the big screen that had previously been displaying pictures of Soomi and her siblings, friends, and anyone close to her, turned on again. Though it didn't go back to displaying more photos of her. No, it was a video, a live video.

A live video showing her and Jinhwan in the restroom.

And there Jungkook was, on the stage right in front of it, witnessing it first hand up close.

Now everyone was extremely confused. Most of the people attending, or more like all of them, were professional idols who weren't gonna go spread stories to the press. Jungkook wouldn't of invited them if they weren't. He knew that whatever happened would stay in their bubble safely.

He also knew that Solar and Jinhwan were never going to be quite "over," but he never thought he would see something like this, displayed on the big screen, at a party he spent weeks planning for her.

Though he had to keep a level mind, knowing well enough that Solar looked very distressed. She must've received word from the stalker before this happened.

Not knowing what to do, Jungkook makes his way to the mic again. "Uh, hyungs, can you escort everyone out safely?" he looks to the direction his members were in since he couldn't clearly see from the lack of light.

His members call back in agreement, rising to their feet to tell everyone to make their way to the entrance.

Jungkook runs a hand through his hair, completely stressed that the party was going downhill thanks to whoever this psycho was. He turns to look back at the screen, which was when it changed once again.

But this time, it wasn't any pictures.

You're all guilty, time to admit to your crimes.

You'll pay.


138:00 | almost

"God dammit!" Solar curses in dismay as she reads the text she got from Chanhee, telling her about what was happening in the main venue. Turning to Jinhwan, she frowns as she explains, "I guess there's a camera in here, they displayed us on the big screen. Then there was some creepy message about us needing to admit to our crimes or something."

Jinhwan's eyes widened from all the information he was just told. Trying to stay calm for the sake of Solar, he sighs, "don't worry, I'm sure Jungkook will understand. You needed someone to calm you down and he wasn't there," he assures her, before his face grows more serious, "but our crimes? Oh, you think they mean-" he cuts himself off as he looks at her, both of them seeming to think the same thing.

"Yeah," she breathes, "that's definitely what they mean."

"Do you think they have proof of our involvement?" he asks her, knowing she was more likely to know than he was.

Solar only shrugs, "possibly, knowing how smart they seem to be."

Jinhwan exhales deeply, walking forward to engulf her in a hug again. Solar closes her eyes as she sinks into him, the warmth of his body being the only thing that seemed to calm her down. 

kookie: we're escorting everyone out to their cars safely

kookie: and i'm not mad at you

kookie: i know you're not okay rn

kookie: i'm sorry your party was ruined

kookie: i should've been more careful 

mi: i'm sorry too jungkook

mi: it's just a lot and i thank you for understanding

mi: i appreciate u kookie :(

With that, she slips her phone into Jinhwan's back pocket since she didn't have one. The boy is staring down at her when she does so, waiting for her to finish.

"It'll be okay, Soo," he tells her sternly, "we'll find this person, okay? We just all have to be more careful."

"Yeah, I know," she hums in agreement.

Silence fell over them, and she could only bury her face into his chest. The both of them didn't know what to do, they didn't know if it was safe to leave the bathroom. Though since it was clear the stalker did know where they were, Jinhwan was thinking it might be a good idea.

"Come on," he urges her, pulling away from their embrace but grabbing her hand tightly instead. It was refreshing to intertwine their fingers just like the old times, except Jinhwan had a much bigger motive. He was going to protect this girl with everything in his body. Pulling her closer to him so she was practically one with him, he tells her, "we should get out of here, it's too dangerous."

"Okay," is all she says, following his lead. 

Jinhwan is in the front, of course, opening the bathroom door first. Holding Solar tightly behind him, his eyes scan to the best of their ability through the darkness. He had to make sure there wasn't anyone suspicious in the area before they just waltzed out there.

And it all looked clear, not a soul in sight.

Until his eyes landed on the fire exit door at the back of the hallway. It was a figure, a figure holding a giant bag that was likely full of the equipment they had been using to hack into the technology at the venue. Since it was so dark, he couldn't see much more than their frame and height.

But something inside of him was telling him it was them.

"That's them, Soo," he tells her in a rush, his words nearly incomprehensible. Soomi still heard them, but was beyond confused.

"What? Jinhwan, that's imposs-," she tried to say, but was cut off when the boy broke into a full speed run down the hallway, dragging her along. 

"HEY!" he screams as they're opening the door, "Stop right there, you piece of shit!"

It was almost like deja vu, like the events of that day at KCON were reoccurring.

At the sight of someone on their trail, the person glances back before rushing out the door and into the back alley of the venue. Jinhwan was so intent on catching them that he let Solar's hand go, not wanting to drag her into any trouble. All he wanted was to end this once and for all.

Breaking into the fastest speed he could manage, he bursts out the back door to follow them through the alley.

Solar could only watch in fear. "Jinhwan, are you fucking crazy?!" she's calling after him. He was clearly underestimating the power and smarts of this person, and it was going to get him hurt.

Not knowing what to do, she looks around in search of anyone that could help. She'd never wished Kim Sunwoo would appear more than she did right then.

But it was just her, she was the only one there. The only one that would be able to do anything.

So without another thought, she rushed after him.

Meanwhile, Jinhwan was still chasing them like his life depended on it. Since there were tall street lights surrounding the outside, it provided for him to get a much better look at the person. They were wearing a black beanie over their face for obvious reasons, though. This meant he couldn't make out any features.

He chased them as hard as he could. No matter how hard he ran, how much he pushed himself, they proved to be faster. He was too far away to catch them, and this was proven when they hurriedly jumped into a car.

But Jinhwan was also smart.

A blue Zenvo ST1, he recognized the car. They were already speeding off as he tried to squint at the license plate, but he still got the first three letters.

A8K.

They molded into his brain as he was trying to catch his breathe, watching the car speed off down the road. He failed to catch them once again, but this time he had actually gotten some useful information. There weren't many people in South Korea that would be able to afford a car like that.

"Kim Jinhwan!" a cry of frustration is heard from behind him. The boy turns to see Solar making her way out of the long alleyway, also breathing hard from how she had been trying to catch up with them. 

Walking up to him, she attempts to push him to show her annoyance. "What's wrong with you?! You're lucky they were intent on running away or you could've gotten seriously injured!" She's lecturing him. It was clear she was just afraid for his safety. 

Jinhwan only grins down at her, happy from his discovery. "It's okay, Soo, I promised we would find them, remember?"

Confused at why the hell he was smiling, she blinked at him, "yeah, so?"

The caramel boy sighs, pursing his lips as he looks away again. His eyes stare blankly at the road they had sped away on, thinking harshly. He swore something inside of him was telling him he'd seen that car before. But where was it?

"Well, I know what car model they have and half of their license plate," he explains, "I know it doesn't seem like much, but I feel like I've seen it somewhere before."

Solar is looking up at him like he's insane. He sort of did sound like he was. How was he going to guess who it was based on a few letters of a license plate and the kind of car they had? It sounded far-fetched to her, it wasn't like he was Kim Sunwoo.

"It's fine, Nani," she shakes her head, "don't stress yourself over it, at least no one got hurt."

He turns back to her again, his eyes peering into hers. There were so many words Jinhwan wanted to say in that moment, so many things he wanted to tell her. More than anything, he wanted to just feel her skin on his. He wanted to prove to her that she would be safe, she would be okay.

But it wasn't the time or place. He knew he couldn't.

"Come on, we should go find everyone," he tells her, grabbing her hand again.


+


"Solar!" Chanhee is screeching as if he's an old woman, running up to her dramatically and engulfing her in a hug, "My God, I thought you fucking died."

She accepts his hug just to be bombarded by literally everyone else there. They were all standing in the parking lot of the venue after everyone else had left, trying to figure out what their next move was from here.

"I'm fine, everyone," she insists, "I was more worried about this idiot here." She looks to the caramel boy beside her, gently shoving him with her shoulder.

Jinhwan grins like the lovestruck idiot that he was, shrugging it off, "I saw them," he tells everyone, "I couldn't catch them, but I did see their car and license plate."

"That's great, Jinhwan," Jimin is the first to say with wide eyes, "we could use that to find them for sure."

"Yeah, even though he literally could've died," Solar chimes in, still unhappy about his insane actions. 

Jinhwan giggles at he looks to the girl, seeming just too happy about his discovery - and the fact it was almost feeling like the old times. Reaching out a finger, he pokes her cheek playfully, "poor Soo was so scared for me."

Swatting his hand away, she sticks her tongue out at him jokingly. "I'm sorry I didn't want you to get shot, stupid."

Everyone was watching the way they were messing with each other wearily. No one knew what to think after what was displayed on the big screen, not to mention the message. And here Jinhwan and Soomi were, acting all lovey-dovey.

"What the hell do you guys think that message meant, anyway?" Bobby is the one to ask.

No one knew the answer.

"I would think it just means they're planning something big and bad," Jaehyun advises, "and I don't think it's just for Solar, probably for all of you," his eyes wander to stare at his little sister, who was busy still messing with Jinhwan as if they were children, "or most likely, Jinhwan, Jungkook, and Solar."

"That would make the most sense," Chaerin agrees beside him.

"Everyone just needs to be careful," Jungkook speaks up from his stance, sick of watching Solar and Jinhwan, "watch your backs and keep an eye out."

Everyone hums sounds of agreement.

With that, Jungkook pushes through the friends to get to Solar. The girl stops when she sees him, a smile forming on her face as she looks to his serious expression. Jungkook was no doubt salty from seeing her spending so much time with her ex. And the fact his entire party had been ruined, he didn't even get to finish his song or ask her out officially.

Though, after seeing the way her and Jinhwan still looked at each other, he wasn't sure if he should still.

But everyone knew Jeon Jungkook was selfish, he was selfish and greedy. So much so that he wouldn't be able to keep himself away from her no matter what. It was a conversation they needed to have, but it could wait, because right then he just wanted to hug her.

Which is what he did.

"Hi to you, too, Kookie," her voice is muffled into his chest. 

"I'm glad you're okay," he tells her sweetly as they pull away, his brown eyes beaming down at her, "I'm sorry about all of this. It was supposed to be a special night for you."

Solar only shakes her head vigorously, smiling as she assures him, "don't worry, it was still a great night I'll remember forever, Jungkook. Thank you for everything you've done for me tonight."

He found himself smiling from her words, "yeah, of course, Mi."

Silence falls over them, and Jungkook's eyes wander to look to the short boy that was still standing there beside Solar. Pursing his lips in thought, he finally looks to him and offers, "thanks for keeping her safe, Jinhwan."

The older boy is obviously surprised, but nods in acknowledgement, "I'd never let Soo get hurt, so there's no need to thank me."

You could already feel the tension building.

"Okay!" Solar clears her throat before they had another dominance battle she did not want to have to break apart.

"We should probably get out of here already," Hanbin is saying.

"This place is creeping me the fuck out now, no joke," Junhoe mumbles at he looks back to the building.

"Alright, we're heading to the van," Namjoon informs Solar and Jungkook. The members nod and follow him in the direction their vehicle was.

Jungkook watches them leave, pursing his lips as he was busy thinking about everything that happened that night. Finally, he turns to Soomi, grabbing one of her hands, "come on, Mi, we should head home, too."

"Yeah, we're staying there, too!" Chaerin hums brightly.

"I can't wait to see their fancy celebrity mansion," Juyeon is muttering to his other siblings.

His words caused the little sister's attitude to deflate, "you say that like we don't literally live in a mansion."

The boy only shrugs.

"Okay, well, I'll see you guys there," Solar chuckles to her siblings, about to follow Jungkook to the van the others were in.

When someone else grabbed her other hand, causing both her and Jungkook to stop.

"Wait, Soo," Jinhwan clears his throat, "are you sure you don't want to stay with us?"

A lump forms in her throat from the question she didn't expect to hear. The other iKON boys are either looking at her expectantly or facepalming due to the fact Jinhwan actually had the audacity to ask her that.

It clearly left her at a crossroad.

"Uh," she stutters, not knowing what the hell she should say. All she could do was glance back to Jungkook, who looked like he was seconds away from breaking all of Jinhwan's bones.

For the safety of Jinhwan, she shook her head, "I should be with my siblings and friends tonight, Nani, I'm sorry."

The boy only sends her a smile as if he was expecting that answer. There's a certain look in his eyes when he glances to Jungkook before back to her, waving a hand in dismissal, "no worries, Soo, just remember I'm here."

"Yeah," she breathes, unable to say anything else before Jungkook had dragged her away.

Kim Jinhwan, she could only think as she was climbing in the van with the other Bangtan boys seconds later, you're making this that much harder.

And that he was, completely on purpose.


139:00 | ramen

"I'll play the song for you sometime soon, Mi," Jungkook had been saying that night in the car on the way home, "not at a big party this time, just when it's you and me."

She beamed up at him, leaning her head on his shoulder as she hummed, "that sounds great, Kookie."


Though there was still something inside of her. She found herself wondering if she would ever love one boy and not the other some day, if she would ever be able to just have feelings for one person and not multiple. Her emotions never failed to confuse her, and all she wanted was to one day understand them. When she was with one, she missed the other. Why couldn't she just make up her mind? What did her heart truly want?

She missed Jungkook for so long, she swore she wouldn't be with Jinhwan again after what he did. Jinhwan and his psycho ex had seemed to make room in her heart for Jungkook, convincing her that Jungkook was really who she should be with.
