But just like when Jungkook told her the truth about the scandal, learning the truth about the incident with Jinhwan only resurfaced emotions she thought she'd put away for good. It wasn't his fault just like it wasn't Jungkook's fault. They both would have made better decisions, and they both caused permanent damage to her, one possibly worse than the other. 

Everyone made mistakes. 

So who was she to had thrown him away like that?

Torn, she was more torn than she had been in a long while. Just when she felt like everything was coming together, just when her and Jinhwan agreed that they would stay friends while Solar tried it out with Jungkook.

She heaved out a deep sigh as she sat there on the sofa in the living room. It was pitch dark, allowing for her to truly just sit and reflect on everything. She needed to talk to Sunwoo tomorrow about what happened regarding the displayed message, he was the only one who would know what to do about that.

But about her feelings, she was stranded on that one.

Everyone seemed to be asleep since they were all so tired from the long day. Jungkook had worked so hard on the party and getting everything together that he couldn't even keep his eyes open despite how hard he tried. Solar insisted for him to go to sleep, and he eventually had.

Even all of her siblings and friends were passed out upstairs in the guest rooms they were staying in. It left Solar completely alone to try to understand everything in her head.

That's when her phone buzzes beside her on the sofa.

nani: hey you

nani: come outside

She got lost staring down at the two text messages. At first, she wasn't sure if she was reading them right. Then, she got in a debate with if this was some sort of weird dream. Until finally, she exhaled, rising from the sofa and making her way to the front door.

Kim Jinhwan, his name repeats in her head like a broken record, the boy who saved her so many times, pulled her out of so many dark places. The boy she always found herself running back to.

Clicking the lock on the door, she pulls open one of the big double doors. Her green eyes squint in the night as she steps onto the porch, closing it behind her. The big street lights allowed for her to dimly see onto the front lawn.

And there he was.

The wind blowing through his dirty blonde hair, his hands shoved in the pockets of his skinny jeans. He'd been staring up at the stars in the sky, his brown eyes twinkling under the moon. Jinhwan was gorgeous, he was something that deserved to be looked at. She found herself standing there on the porch, staring at him like a complete weirdo.

Maybe she was just a weirdo when it came to him.

Stepping down the steps, she makes her way slowly through the grass. The sound of her steps in it makes him snap out of his trance, his eyes falling on her until she was right in front of him.

Solar is the first to speak tauntingly, raising a brow in question, "well, I didn't expect to see you here." Her words were light hearted, but Jinhwan looked rather serious for once as he met her eyes. There's a lot of emotion in those brown irises, too much emotion for Solar right now. She was going through a lot mentally after what happened at the party, even as much as she was trying to put it to the back of her head just like she always did.

Run from her problems and pretend they aren't there. No one did that like Solar Lee.

Jinhwan's hands are behind his back as if he was either holding something or nervous - or mayhaps both. He purses his lips as she hesitantly walks down the steps of the porch to stand only a few feet from him. It was so dark she wasn't fully able to see his facial expression, all she could see was the twinkle in his eyes when he met hers. 

"I wanted to make sure you're okay," he finally speaks, his words echoing through her heart as she can only peer at him, trying to hold in all the emotion that wanted to burst out, "I know that was a lot at once on a night that was supposed to be the opposite." Jinhwan breaks their eye contact to glance at the mansion they stood in front of, seeming to take note of the fact there weren't many lights on in the house. 

She knew he was going to comment something ill about Jungkook, probably something along the lines of he wasn't awake to comfort her. "He had a long day, too, everyone's asleep," she tells him, but her voice is barely audible.

Yet the caramel boy still heard her clear as day, his eyes fall back onto her like a wave of exhilaration. She couldn't lie, she was glad he was here. Her best friends and siblings were sleeping due to jet lag, and the other boys were just tired from the long day of preparing for her party. She wasn't the only one shaken up by the events, too, of course. Everyone was a mix of afraid, worried, and angry. Sometimes all you could do was try to sleep it off. 

But Solar couldn't do that. She was too concerned for what was coming.

"He should still be there for you, what happened tonight was bad," Jinhwan insists, shaking his head in disappointment, "I couldn't let you be alone right now, I'm so fucking sorry they ruined your birthday, Soo."

She sends him a small smile, "shush, what good does apologizing do?"

Her voice was still that of a mice. Jinhwan knew she needed someone right now, it was that gut feeling that made him come here in the first place - though it wasn't all he was there for.

They stood in silence for a while. The only sound was that of nature, the hoot of an owl and the slight gusts of wind blowing through their hair. Jinhwan could see she was having trouble holding in the stress she always seemed to be feeling. He wanted to hug her more than anything, but he found himself wondering if he was overstepping.

"I'm gonna find them, Soo," he informs sternly, tired of seeing her so down in the dirt, "remember, I saw their car and license plate? I can use that, I'll find them myself and they'll be the ones that pay, not us," he finally got the guts to step forward, but only placed his hands on her forearms. "It'll be okay, and I know you're tired of hearing it, but trust me this time, alright?"

Solar could only giggle - she wasn't even sure why she was laughing during a time like this. For some reason, Jinhwan was so cute that she found it funny. Though it was probably a good coping mechanism for all the bullshit. However, it sure caught him off guard.

"Yah, what's funny?" he's asking in bewilderment, almost offended at the fact she was laughing at him.

Shaking her head, she reaches a hand up to grab his own that was rubbing her forearm. Holding it in front of her face, she chuckles again, "you're cute, Nani, I don't know if you'd be able to fight anyone with these baby hands."

Now he was really offended.

"Yah!" he cries, snatching his hand out of her grip, "they're not that small."

Her giggles die down as she sighs, "I'm kidding, but really, it's unlikely you'll be able to find them with just that. I'm hoping Jaehyun can figure something out, if not I'll have to call Sunwoo."

Little did she know.

Jinhwan pouts a bit at her words. He wanted her to be safe more than anything, but he also wanted to be the one to make her so. And he sure didn't want Sunwoo to be involved, even if he was the easiest - and best - solution. 

Still, he knew that she was right. He talked big, but it was next to impossible for him to actually find them based off how little he knew. It wasn't like he was going to drive down every street in Seoul to find their car again. There was no telling what part they were located at and how often they were moving around from place to place.

All he was truly effective at was being there for her. It was what he'd always been good at from the very start. 

Looking back to her, Solar was staring at her sandals in the grass. He was worried about what might be going through her head. Hell, he was worried about his own safety, too. It wasn't like  the message they displayed was just for Solar, it was meant for all of them. Really, they were all guilty in different ways. It wasn't like he wasn't aware of what happened to his own manager, even with as shitty of a person she turned out to be, they still killed her. 

There was no telling what was coming. He just hoped they wouldn't turn to the police. 

"Hey," he clears his throat, nudging her shoulder with his to grab her attention. Her green eyes sparkle under the moonlight at 4:30 in the morning, shining despite how dull she was feeling, when Jinhwan offers the words cheerily, "come on, let's go to your favorite ramen place."

And somehow, she almost was cleansed of all worry.

"Really?" she hums in shock, "it's like 4 something."

"So what?" he rolls his eyes, "that never bothered us before."

"True!" she shrugs, "do I get $1000 worth of clothes this time, too?"

Jinhwan was caught in shock for a second as she was already jogging away to his car. He didn't think she remembered that. 

It wasn't like he was made of money, but he also couldn't say no to her. "If you want, Soo."



+


"Soo," Jinhwan whines as they're climbing back into his car at 6 AM, "I think my wallet is crying." 

Solar had closed the passenger door, setting the shopping bags on the floor next to her feet. To think hours ago she had been falling apart, but Jinhwan's distraction proved well. Her actions were full of giddiness, eyes shining with excitement as she finished fixating all of her shopping bags. Her eyes finally landed on the short boy in the driver's seat, who had been watching her with a smile on his face even though he was just complaining about how much money he'd spent on her. In reality, Jinhwan didn't mind it, it wasn't like he didn't have money to spare. He loved spoiling her. 

"Lucky for you," she hums, "I think I'm done with the shopping."

He raises a brow at this, not expecting such words, "really? Soomi is done shopping?"

The brunette nods in agreement, about to speak before a yawn catches her off guard. "See, it's because I'm tired," she explains, "but it was really fun being with you - thank you, Jinhwan, really."

"Don't thank me," he insists, "I have to make it up to you somehow, and even this isn't enough."

"Shut up," she reaches over the middle compartment to shove his arm, "you don't have to make anything up to me, it's passed and I forgave you, remember?"

But the caramel boy shakes his head. "I can't forgive myself though, Soo, I could've handled it so much better," he sighed, turning away from her to face the steering wheel so he could start the car. The engine roars, filling their ears for a split second. He waits until the car is silent to finish his statement, "it hurts me to think I hurt you like Jungkook did, I don't ever want to stoop as low as him."

Shots fired. 

"Hey, that's completely different," she says sternly, though not really taking sides for either of them, "look, it was just a big misunderstanding, and I'm at fault, too. We just have to grow from our mistakes, okay? That's what you would tell me."

"I know, you're right," he nods, and she smiles in response thinking he was done with feeling bad until he adds, "but I'm still going to make it up to you."

Solar exhales deeply, knowing his mind was set on whatever. But she knew this didn't just have to do with him "making it up to her" for what happened, it was much more than that. 

Like the fact she and Jungkook were getting back together.

"Before I take you back to that place," Jinhwan informs, "I have one more thing I want to show you."

The girl is confused yet intrigued as he drives out of the large parking lot they were in, seeming to have his mind harshly set on something in particular. She could only imagine what it might be - but there was no way she would've guessed what Kim Jinhwan had in store that night.


140:00 | embrace the change

"Nani," she's blinking in worry, fretting as her eyes scan the landscape out the window, "we are literally in the middle of nowhere."

Jinhwan only glanced at her for a mere second, a light chuckle leaving his lips before his face fell back to it's serious expression. One hand is gripping the steering wheel, while the other reaches for his boba to take a long sip, before he hums rather snarkily, "yes, Soo, I am aware we are in the middle of nowhere."

The girl pouts at his sarcasm she didn't ask for, heaving out a sigh as she leans back in the leather seat. "Okay, smart ass, I just don't know where we could possibly be going."

"Don't worry, we're not driving at random this time," he assures her, the words throwing her mind back in time as she recalls the first time they did that, "and I sure won't be drinking any suspicious coffee."

Solar giggles at his words, in a good enough mood to find it funny instead of grow concerned about the events. "Jesus, to think of everything we've gone through," she reminisces, her eyes staring to the morning sky outside the window. It had been a hard year nonetheless, but yet, she didn't regret it. She'd had some of the best times right here with the idiot she was sitting by, and she couldn't act like she didn't enjoy them.

Her eyes rake across the interior to fall back on Jinhwan, who was too focused on the road to notice her intense gaze. The soft silence became peaceful in that moment, nothing bothering them except the slight sound of the radio. Her mind seemed to go over many things in that time as she stared at the boy that still made her heart feel fuzzy.

Jungkook, was she really going to be with Jungkook? She knew that he knew that her feelings weren't completely gone for Jinhwan but yet he was fine with that because he just wanted to be with her. But could she really handle that? Could she really do that? Only a week ago, she was more sure than ever that she was going to give her and Jungkook another go.

But when she looked at the boy beside her, she wanted nothing more than to go back in time to relive the moments when he was hers.

"Take a picture, Soo, it'll last longer," his cocky words catch her off guard, and she was met with that same statement he had used too many times during the period she'd known him.

Resisting the urge to punch him in the face, only so he wouldn't crash, she rolled her eyes. "Close your mouth, Jinhwan."

He's only giggling in response, shaking his head in disbelief at her attitude, "don't worry, we're almost there, sassy."

She was about to go off again just for her own entertainment, but her phone buzzing in her back made her halt. Jinhwan looked to her for a second, eyes curious as to who was bothering her so early. He didn't think any of the guys, including Jungkook, had schedules today since they were planning to have gotten wasted at the party (even if it definitely didn't happen.) He figured everyone would be sleeping in, anyway.

Her eyes are also full of confusion as she turns on the screen. When she sees the number being not in her contacts, she almost had a heart attack, thinking it might've been from the stalker - that was, until she reads the text.

*random number*: hi noona!! it's haechan:) i got your number from your friend kevin, i wanted to just ask if you were okay after what happened?? i don't know for sure what was going on but there was no reason for someone to ruin your birthday :( i'm sorry!! the hyung's also wanted to wish you the best!! just let me know if there's anything i can do for you:)

She stares at the text for a long time, before a small smile spreads over her lips. That was really  nice of him, she thought to herself. He even called her noona, which she had never been called since she was usually younger than most of her friends. Now that she thought about it, though, Haechan probably was a bit younger than her. He was so sweet.

Adding his number to her contact, she put a sun by the name to represent what he seemed to be. It was rather funny that his name literally meant full sun and that he was such a bright person.

noona: hii haechan, i appreciate you checking on me! i'm honestly still shaken up. i know you're probably confused and kinda wondering what the heck was happening, it's been a lot:( i didn't think that it would happen on my bday though. it's okay tho!! tell them i said thank you, and thank you to you for caring!!

She presses send, and only then did she notice Jinhwan peering down at her phone screen - or more like trying to. "Yah," she calls to him, "don't be nosey."

He laughs lightly at the fact he was caught, looking back to the road. "I just wanted to make sure it wasn't Jungkook, he's not allowed to bother you during our time together."

The girl rolls her eyes because he sounded like a stubborn kid, but it was funny how much he really hated Jungkook. "Don't worry, he's definitely still sleeping, it was just my friend."

Jinhwan nods in understanding, a breathe of relief leaving his lips, "good."

Solar shakes her head at him before looking back down to her phone.

haechan: it seemed serious, if you're in danger you should really go to the police. i would probably tell them about what happened at your party just to be safe, even if you feel like you have it under control :( better safe than sorry!!

noona: i know it seems like that would be logical but trust me when i say i really cant do that

haechan: oh no.. just please stay safe, noona!

haechan: i'm here if you need a friend! :)

noona: ty! i know it sounds crazy when i say i can't go to the police, i just don't know how to really explain it over text

haechan: no worries, i know it's probably hard

haechan: well, we could meet up sometime for coffee if you'd like? :D

noona: oh, sure, that would be fine:)

haechan: yay

haechan: do you have any schedule this thursday?

noona: nope i doubt it

haechan: ahh okay, thursday it is then?

noona: sounds good haechannie~

haechan: i'll see you then, noona :D

"Made some new friends, huh?" Jinhwan raises a brow from the drivers seat, obviously having had been paying attention to her eyes that had been so intensely glued onto her phone screen. The girl had turned off her phone since the conversation was over, putting it back into her pocket as she met his glances.

He didn't look mad, and he hadn't said it in a picky way. Jinhwan knew it wasn't Jungkook, therefore he was happy.

"You could say that, remember one of the members of NCT was sitting with me at the party?"

He nods, clearly remembering since she was pretty much all he had looked at that entire night. "Oh, Haechan, right? He seems like a sweet kid."

Solar goggles at his words, shaking her head as she jokes, "saying it like that makes you sound old."

"Hey, isn't he a kid?!" the caramel boy tries to stick up for himself, "I swear the members of Dream are like ten."

She was laughing harder by now, unable to comprehend how much Jinhwan sounded like a fucking grandpa. "How should I know?" She finally shrugs, "he's probably like 20."

Exhaling, his eyes are back on the road as he hums, "jeez, don't get him involved in all of this garbage."

"Yeah," she mutters, the conversation taking a rather serious turn, "I don't think I have the heart to do that, but he is sort of on my tail after what happened."

"It'll be okay," Jinhwan sends her a reassuring smile, "you're good at playing things off."

Solar makes a sound of agreement, not wanting to talk more about it. Jinhwan was right, of course. She would easily be able to make up some sort of excuse — probably, just like the Bangtan boys were likely going to be doing in the morning so they could convince everyone that was there that everything was "fine." There was no telling how many calls and texts she would also be receiving in only a few hours from distant friends worried about her safety. Her party that was supposed to be a huge success really had taken a turn for the worst.

Yet here she was at 5 in the morning, on a rendezvous with her latest ex boyfriend she swore she wasn't going to get back with, instead of resting like she should be.

"We're here," Jinhwan finally cheers, the car coming to a halt on the side of a gravel road. Solar turns to peer out the window, but her eyes were only met with the same darkness that she'd seen the entire way here. It wasn't like there were any street lights in the mountains, after all.

That was, until she looked harder. Just behind some trees, you could see the slightest glint of light. She was intrigued, nonetheless, and could only stare in contemplation as to what this boy had planned — and when he'd even had the time to do so.

She wasn't able to finish trying to process what was happening when the door on her side is opened. Jinhwan is standing there, his face being lit up thanks to the car lights, with the biggest goofy smile on his face as he holds out a hand, "come on, Soo, you take too long."

She narrows her eyes at him in distaste, but nonetheless places her hand in his. Climbing out of the car, Jinhwan's hand fits perfectly inside her own like it'd always seemed to, and he uses the other one to close the door behind her.

With that, he leads the way through the darkness, branches and leaves crunching underneath their feet. Solar didn't say a word, she was too busy continuously trying to figure out what this all was.

"I did this all before your party," Jinhwan explains as the lights that she saw in the mix of trees from the car slowly became more evident, "I had to do something for the most special person in my life, too."

That's when he leads her through the trees, and her eyes are met with one of the prettiest sights she'd seen in a long time.

"Kim Jinhwan..." she's muttering is disbelief as her eyes take over the scene. Fairy lights were strung through out the branches, looking like diamonds hanging from the leaves. A picnic cloth was strung across the small place of grass that was in between all the nature, a few bags sitting in the middle of it. There was even a stereo sitting at the furthest corner of the mat, playing soft music that made her scoff in awe.

"It's nothing crazy, I didn't have a lot of time to prepare before I had to go to the party," Jinhwan rushes out, worried about what she might be thinking, "I know it's not a huge party like Jungkook did for you, but—"

He cuts himself off when Solar steps in front of him, leaning in and wrapping both her arms around his torso.

"I love this," she says, her cheek on his chest so she could continue to stare at the sight, "this is beautiful, Nani."

When she pulls away, he's grinning from ear to ear. "Here, come sit," he urges, leading her over to the middle of the mat where the bags were. The two of them sit across from each other, Solar leaning back on her hands so she could continue to look around. The light wasn't overbearing to the point where she could still look up at all the stars and the moon. It was something she would've traded a big party to look at — that's how pretty it was.

Jinhwan is busy unpacking the things he had brought in the bags, until he glances up and notices her watching the night sky. A giddy grin spreads over his lips, and he could feel his heart practically exploding out of his chest. There were no words to explain the feelings one person managed to give him, the feelings he knew he would cherish for the rest of his life.

The feelings he never wanted to let go of.

"I brought you soju!" He cheerily hums as he pulls a big bottle out of the bag. The sight caused Solar's eyes to bulge, and without a word, she snatches it from his hand. "Yah, don't drink it all, I don't want you to be drunk on this night."

She's giggling as she pops the cap off, already taking a sip. "I know~, I won't get drunk, you have my promise, Nani."

"Oh, and tell me if you're hungry, I brought snacks, too," he informs, taking out some pillows and spreading them around the edges.

Solar had watched him do so, his caramel locks falling in his face. The majority of her days recently had been spent with her thinking about Jungkook and Jinhwan, how she could never seem to fully grasp who her heart truly wanted. She had thought that maybe, getting back with Jungkook is what it would take for her to figure it out. That she needed to test both of the pools before she decided which temperature was fit for her.

But ever since the night of her party, she was beginning to think that nothing like that was necessary after all.

She would never know what was going on in her head. Solar wasn't smart enough to be able to understand her feelings. But right then, she had an urge to just be next to him, one she could actually grasp.

So without another thought, she climbs over the mat to fall directly next to Jinhwan. She was so close to him their legs were together, and the sudden feeling caused him to look up from something he was focused on in one of the bags.

"Soo," he chuckles at the expression she was giving him, "did the soju already get to your head?"

She gawks at his words for a second, before chuckling back, "no," she shakes her head, growing hesitant, "I just wanted to be by you."

There was that smile she could never get enough of. Setting the bag down, Jinhwan sits up straight, wrapping an arm around her shoulders so she could lay into him.

She takes this opportunity, snuggling into him without a second thought. Sure, maybe earlier she had been worried about getting too close with him because she didn't want to be unfair to Jungkook. But Soomi didn't care anymore, she couldn't deny the feeling she kept getting, she couldn't pretend like the cravings for him weren't there.

There's a comfortable silence that falls over them. One that she could easily fall asleep to, even despite the hard night she'd had, until Jinhwan whispers into her ear, "I love you, Soo."

Hearing the words so effortlessly fly from his tongue was an ecstasy. She wanted to hear it again, as if she had been starved. Jinhwan had a pretty tone to his voice, one that she never thought enough about. She wanted to hear it again and again.

But yet, she wasn't sure if she should say it back. Even though she had blurted out those same words to him only days ago.

Suddenly, she sits up, seeming to have an abrupt worry on her mind — something she needed to get off her chest.

"Nani," she clears her throat, "I really don't know what to do..." she had tried to sound strong, but by the end of her sentence her voice had faltered.

Jinhwan seemed to know what she was talking about without her saying so. He purses his lips as her eyes fall to her lap, trying to find the words to accurately convey what he wanted to.

He had told himself that he would have to accept her wishes if she was really going to get back with him. But Jinhwan had never really been able to accept it. The entire time at her party his blood had boiled — everytime he saw her next to him his blood boiled. How would he ever handle them truly being official again?

But he knew that if it was what she wanted, all he could do was support her. It wasn't like it meant she didn't love him anymore, she had said it herself.

Knowing she was in need of guidance and genuine advice rather than his usual insistence, the boy sighs, "you want me to be honest, Soo?"

At his sudden question, she looks up slowly from her lap. Her curious eyes are enough to answer his question.

"I think," he starts, choosing his words carefully as he thinks about everything that had happened in Soomi's life the last several years, "I think a part of you wants to go back to him because of how familiar it feels." The sentence he spoke made her knit her brows together, not quite grasping what he meant until he went on, "he takes you back to the days when you had nothing to worry about — no hurt, no drama, no stalkers, no love triangles — it was when life was easy. It makes you feel like that that's how it is again, that you can be that way again."

There's a bewildered look on her face by the time he finished. Solar wasn't sure what to say or if she could say anything. At first, it sounded insane, but the more he went on — the more she realized he wasn't wrong. She had never been able to grasp her emotions and what they meant, but Jinhwan seemed to be doing just that.

"It's not 2018 anymore, Soo," Jinhwan speaks up from beside her again, "the only way you can get past any of this is by embracing it," he pauses again, thinking hard. The next words he spoke struck a spot in her heart, "embrace the change, Soo. Who's your heart really calling? Not because they take you back to better times, but makes the present feel that way?"

There's a twinkle in his eyes when she meets them, "think of us, Soo, we got through everything even when it was the worst, that's what it means when it's love."

She was in awe, awe because Jinhwan was right. There wasn't a word that came out of his mouth that wasn't right. But at the same time, her and Jungkook had gotten through the same amount of things - they'd been through a lot, too. And he was still here.

Yet, there was truth in his words. For once, she saw that light again. A chuckle of amazement left her lips as she breaks their eye contact again, "that's probably the best speech you've ever given me, Jinhwan."

The boy scoffs at the fact that was all she had to say, "pabo... think about what I said."

"I am!" She insists, "I just — don't know what else to say."

Jinhwan smiles at her words, feeling accomplished at the fact he got through to her. At that time, he hoped he was actually able to help her. Not for his own benefit, but for hers.

The boy yawns, exhaustion seeming to take over him. Shifting the way he was sitting, he falls back until his head hits the soft pillow he'd laid on the edges of the mat.

Solar exhales as she watches him do so. His forearms flex as he puts his hands behind his head, that same twinkle in his brown irises as he looks to the sky.

The conversation seemed to be over, until he hums a moment later, "with all that aside, come lay with me, stupid."

She shakes her head as she looks to him. His playful smile was taunting, and far too tempting for her to turn down.

So she did, she spent the night that was supposed to be much different — the night that was supposed to be spent with Jungkook. The night they were supposed to get back together for what he deemed as forever had instead turned into the night she realized many things, the night she realized she wasn't getting back with Jungkook. It wasn't right for her.

Solar had been in the right place all along, all she had to do was just that.

Embrace the change.


141:00 | flower

"Thank you for tonight, Jinani," she's humming to him as they make their way down the sidewalk of the mansion, stopping midway to turn to face each other a last time. The old nickname was a drug coming from her lips, and it took him back to times he missed as his eyes flickered to her lips before meeting those green irises again. Solar didn't notice as she sighed, "you sure gave me a lot to think about."

Her words gave him purpose. Though he did want to get through to her about Jungkook and his view on it, he also hoped to give her a peaceful night to make up for the disaster her party had been. Solar obviously needed someone after that experience. He was even worried to leave her by that time, but he kept telling himself he didn't have a choice, especially since that morning sun was already showing. 

He'd avoided talking about the clear horrible event. The reason being, Jinhwan didn't know what to say about it. Nothing could bring the fear that was instilled in her to rest. The thing was, Jinhwan didn't know what was happening, either. Everyone had the same nervous pit in their stomachs, unsure how to fight what was happening, unsure what the next move was. If her brother had been right, were they truly all in danger?

All he could do, though, just like Solar said, was be there for her. It was his true best talent. And that night - or more like morning - he had one last thing in store for her.

While he'd been lost in momentary thought, Solar had been peering up at him in curiosity, waiting to see if he had to say anything before she went inside. After Jinhwan is quiet for a while, she was about to have turned and headed inside, figuring he just had too much on his mind. 

Jinhwan hadn't done what he was planning the entire night yet. For some reason, he was starting to feel bad about it. Should he really throw anymore emotions at her, anymore strong words after what she'd been through tonight? He didn't want to overwhelm her, but he also didn't want to miss such an opportunity. 

So just as she turns and takes the first step, her brown locks flying behind her back, eyes fluttering under the morning sun, his voice deadpans from the same stance, "don't get back with him, Soo."

The girl stops in her tracks, eyes widening a bit from his abrupt stern words. That conversation they had back in the woods had been more of Jinhwan explaining what her situation with Jungkook seemed like from an unbiased point of view. He surprisingly hadn't brought in any of his current emotions or said anything about how much he hated Jungkook - or anything else she would've expected to hear from him. 

Though she wasn't sure if she expected those exact words to leave his lips in that tone. He'd been so sweet tonight, so respectful of her space, they had barely touched more than her just leaning on him and them holding hands. It was the kind of date you'd see in a drama, not the kind that you would see from them.

She figured it would stay that way tonight. That they would leave off with her just thinking about what he'd said, wondering what her next move should be. 

When she finally meets his eyes, they seemed to be full of all the emotion he had masked for the entire night. It was all pouring out before him on a platter. 

"Think about what I said," he breathes, still holding something behind his back, though it was the last thing she was focused on at that time, "think about everything we went through and how we prevailed, think about who was there for you when you were at your worst, and think about who wasn't."

"Jinhwan-"

"-think about it," he insists, "I don't want to watch you make a decision you're not well enough to make. You've had so much going on for so long, and it's only getting worse. It's okay to take time away to focus on yourself and your safety, Soo, until you feel like you can think straight again. You know, it comes to you when you least expect it."

Her mouth is slightly agape from how hard she was thinking about the words he spoke. It was no lie that speech he gave her made her realize a lot of things. It could very well be the reason she was going back to Jungkook, it could very well explain why she always longed for him during the weirdest times, during times when it made no sense to be she wanted him. But had it been for the right reason? 

Could it really just be because of that feeling, that familiar sense that she could just go back? 

It was how she felt when she was with her old friends. That was exactly what they did, they brought her back to the old times, the times when everything was okay. It was true she had always known there was a clear difference in the ways she felt when she looked at Jungkook versus Jinhwan. There was always an evident brightness when she was around the caramel boy, a feeling that would resemble walking on clouds, a feeling of freedom, a feeling that made her feel like they could do whatever they wanted. But when she was with Jungkook, she had always thought how it just felt like she was safe, comforted - and as she had always said, like she'd gone back in time. That was why she was so comfortable around him. It could very well be the reason she was so tempted to get back with him, not because she genuinely wanted Jungkook in those same ways that she wanted Jinhwan. 

Could the stress from it all just be building up that a part of her thought it would be an escape? Did she think she could drown herself in that adrenaline rush, that comforted feeling, that sense as if she was back in 2018 and there were no worries? 

Was Jinhwan really right?

"I'm sorry," Jinhwan soothes, "I know you were already thinking hard and I just made it worse - but I had to tell you that. Take a break, Soo, focus on you."

A break, she thought, when was the last time she had a break?

She couldn't even remember.

"Here," he adds, clearing his throat so his voice wasn't so hoarse. Jinhwan pulls out a golden gift box, a pretty bow wrapped around it and tied perfectly on top. It shone under the sunlight, and he grins ear to ear as he holds it out to her, "this is my gift to you, and it just might help you figure it all out."


+

He left after that.

Solar was left to bask in her own thoughts. A heavy sigh left her lips as she had locked herself in one of the vacant guest rooms in the Bangtan mansion, throwing her bag on the made bed before she sat on the edge of it. 

Brown locks fall in her face as she was busy intently staring down at the golden gift box. The way it sparkled under the light was taunting her. Just thinking about everything he'd said made her wonder what he possibly could've gotten her. It clearly wasn't just any kind of gift, it had to be something he'd really thought on, something that was meaningful. That was the kind of thing Jinhwan always did, was things that were more meaningful rather than showy or impactful, which was Jungkook's thing. 

This entire night had showcased both of those things.

She wondered if she should go wake up Chanhee or Chaerin to open it with her, until she decided against that. This was something she should experience alone, along with all of the things she had to think about.

Now that he had put the thought in her head, she realized how bad she needed a break.

Exhaling, she pulls the box onto her lap, her fingers shaking ever so slightly as she undoes the fancy bow on the top. The fabric comes undone in an instant, falling to the side. The brunette purses her lips in contemplation as she hesitantly lifts the lid off the box. 

At the sight, she chokes on her own oxygen. Coughing a bit, a chuckle of disbelief leaves her lips, shaking her head at how weirdly creative Jinhwan was. Inside the box was multiple items, all placed carefully inside as if they were the most fragile objects anyone would ever touch.

The first one she took out was a set of polaroids, she didn't even need to see the pictures to know where they were from. She could never forget that roadtrip her and Jinhwan took at 4 AM because the other boys had been drunk and celebrating. Jinhwan saw that, at the time, she still struggled in the presence of alcohol, and especially when it was offered to her. He panicked and just offered for them to drive around, but their supposed short drive turned into a full on trip. 

It had been amazing at first, she would never forget how much fun they had at the beginning. Just blasting the radio, screaming lyrics together with the windows open to let the wind blow through their hair, watching the scenery fly by without a care in the world even though they had so many. Spending time with him was always like that. That trip was one of the best things they'd ever done together, and she found herself growing more sentimental than she planned as she looked through the different pictures they took before the accident had happened and it all went south.

Still in disbelief, she sets them aside to pull out the next item in her surprise gift. At the sight, she laughed probably a little louder than she planned. There was no way he included this, was all she could think as she peered down at the familiar poster.

Sure enough, it was the Bling Bling promotion posters from when iKON released it as a single. It was also when she was going through some of the hardest times in her life, when she spent her days drinking away the pain that the scandal had left her with. When all she had was Jinhwan there to comfort her and take care of her. She only knew it was the poster because of how Jinhwan had told her the story. How she'd walked in their dorm with him guiding her late at night, only to see the poster and start screaming.

She couldn't remember it personally, but it sure did sound like something she would do. 

Still giggling to herself, she sets it aside with the polaroids. 

"What else could be in here?" she's muttering, pulling out the last items together. The unfamiliar sight causes her to knit her brows together, holding the items up above her so she could see them clearly under the light. 

One was a CD, a normal CD that was no more than silver in color and glistened under the glare of the ceiling light. The other was a necklace, something she wouldn't of thought she'd received with a CD. She recognized the flower as that of a scorpion grass flower, being a baby blue in color. There was a shimmering golden outline, one that showed it wasn't anything he bought for a cheap price. It was real gold, plated in the middle with the engravement she didn't notice until she held it up directly in front of her face.

KJH+KSM

Their initials, she laughed out loud again at how cliche it was. Yet, she couldn't seem to grasp what the reason for this necklace was. Though it was beautiful and obviously expensive, there had to be more to it, there was always more meaning to something when it came to someone like Kim Jinhwan.

She realized then, that the meaning was likely in whatever was on this CD. Looking back to the CD that was carefully placed in a case, she squinted to notice the small scribbled writing on it. 

Happy birthday, Soo. This is something I wrote special for you, and the first song I can say I wrote all on my own. It's all thanks to you, my muse. 

I'm sorry I couldn't perform it for you in person.

I love you, Soo~

Jinani

A sigh leaves her lips as she reread his note, Jinhwan's weirdly messy but yet neat handwriting making her smile. Her eyes trail across the floor to where she'd put the rest of her things like her laptop and everything else that she'd brought. When Soomi traveled, she traveled big, and since she had been in New York, she'd brought a lot of things back. It meant she had multiple suitcases, and so she'd put them somewhere they wouldn't crowd up. 

Moments later, she was sitting criss crossed on the bed, her laptop in her lap. Those green eyes of hers are filled with curiosity as she slides the CD into the laptop. She could only watch as it took a second to process, before it pulled up the file. 

She figured it would be just an audio file, but before she knew it, a video had been pulled up on her screen.

And there he was, his caramel hair falling in his face, a loose t shirt hanging lightly off his shoulders. He looked to be sitting in the studio, his guitar on his lap and a nervous smile plastered on his face. He'd ran a hand through his messy hair, before clearing his throat and peering into the camera, "Soomi-ah, when you're watching this, it's probably your birthday, so happy birthday!" he cries cheerfully, speaking the last words in his broken English, before clapping a few times in excitement. 

Calming down again, Jinhwan sighs, and she could see that he didn't actually look too well in the video. "I've been working on this song for some time, since I'm new to writing music. I'm not sure what it's going to look like in the present for us," his eyes were dull, and it almost looked as if he'd been crying as he goes on, "you might be with Jungkook right now, but the fact that you're watching this in the first place is enough for me," he chuckles lightly, looking down at his guitar as he finishes, "so, I hope you like this song I wrote for you, Soo. It's special to me."

Her eyes were glued to the screen as he cleared his throat again, looking down to focus on the guitar before his fingers began to strum the chords. She'd never seen him play much of the guitar, but now that she had a chance to really focus on it, he wasn't bad at all. 

Though she didn't have long to focus on his skills, because with a deep breath, he'd began his song.

The moonlight is used as a spotlight as it shines over the faded memories,
Following the path of clouds, on the other side,
Will our old selves be there?
And I know I can't turn back time, or hold onto you and bring you back,
It's all because it's too hard for me to face you
even after saying "I'm sorry,"
I smiled as I let you go,
but yet I can't erase the image of you 

His eyes were sad when he would nervously glance up to the camera. She'd never seen Jinhwan so emotional yet anxious whilst singing. Usually, when he was on stage and performing, he was effortless and natural. The boy never got nervous about anything like that anymore because he was just too good at it.

Soomi knew what he looked like when he was actually nervous, though it wasn't very often. And this was a perfect example. She could just tell his palms were sweaty as he looked back down to the guitar, closing his eyes as if to calm himself and his rambling emotions.

All my words that I couldn't express, it was a farewell to them all
You still live on in my heart now
I went crazy over the thought of never being able to see you again,
so I tried to hold on for a short while
because I couldn't forget you
Walking away with a smile
I hope the path you choose brings no pain
I hope you continue smiling happily
I hope maybe
you'll return to me,
even in my dreams
I plant the flowers that you are, I follow the wind that you are
and I fly away,
I scatter away
The times without you, and your times with me,
I hope we can plant it in the scent of flowers
and keep it all here

By the time the song ended and the clip had been over, with him saying a farewell message, she could only sit there, staring at the ended video. There was so much flowing through her mind, so much she felt like she had ignored. She guessed she never really thought about how everything had affected Jinhwan, and probably Jungkook too. 

Just look at how hurt the poor boy looked in the video, yet he worked on that entire song for her, the song she now knew was called Flower. Her entire rollercoaster of never being able to truly know who she wanted had really messed with them both. It was true, they had all made mistakes before, but that was only because they were all human.

She pursed her lips as she thought back to everything Jinhwan had told her those hours ago. He'd given her a lot to think about. It was clear he didn't want her to be with Jungkook, not unless he knew she was sure about it. He wanted to make sure she didn't rush into something for all the wrong reasons, that she was doing this for herself, that she understood what her heart was telling her. 

And he was right, a lot of the stuff he said was right.

After hearing the song, she knew it well, too. As sad as it was, that was how her life was. It was reckless decisions, never knowing what path to take.

Maybe instead of getting back with Jungkook and possibly making all the same mistakes again, she needed something that was for her.

A break.


142:00 | emotional growth

"Mi, Mi, it's time to wake up~."

"Just leave her alone, Jungkook, she probably wants to sleep," Jimin is nagging the maknae, slapping his shoulder in disapproval. 

But at this point, Jimin knew Jungkook would never listen to him. He only continues to bother the poor sleeping beauty, who hadn't moved a muscle since he'd began. With a heaved sigh, Jimin turns to look to someone who had more power than he could ever wish for.

"How would you like it if I sat and screamed at you to wake up for hours every morning?!" Chanhee snaps in annoyance, "dear Solar was probably up all night while you slumbered like a pig, you have no right to rudely wake her up when she's finally resting."

Jungkook had stopped bothering her the second Chanhee opened his mouth. His throat looked dry as he cleared his throat, bowing to show his apologetic demeanor, "sorry, I guess I didn't think about that. I was just really tired last night when we got back, I think we were all shaken up."

Chanhee is burning holes into Jungkook, observing every inch of the boy as he seemed to be intensely processing something. Finally, his face returns to a less scary state, and he exhales deeply, "just give her some time today, alright? I can imagine she's stressed out, and more than anything, she needs her closest friends and family right now."

Jungkook nods in agreement, still looking apologetic for his seemingly wrong actions. "You're right, if she asks for me when she wakes up, just tell her I'm downstairs."

With that, the boy left the room, but both Chanhee and Jimin had noticed something off about his abrupt change in attitude. There was a weird shift in his facial expressions and tones, and they found themselves exchanging confused glances as he had shut the door quietly behind him, his footsteps echoing down the stairs.

"I assume you noticed that, too?" Chanhee raises a brow as he locks eyes with Jimin.

"Yeah," the boy nods, "you probably already knew, but that was not very like Jungkook. I figured he would probably listen to you since you are Solar's closest friend, but there was something really off there."

While Jimin had been saying this, Chanhee had made his way across the carpeted floor to pick up a weird looking box beside the bed. His eyes are weary as he peers inside of it, scanning over all the items. When he sees the polaroids, he already figured what the problem must be, but then he saw the note on the CD - and it all made sense.

Turning back to Jimin, he answers to his confused expression, "looks like Solar met up with an ex boyfriend last night and got a rather sentimental present."

Though he was curious, Jimin didn't step forward to see what he was talking about. Just seeing the box and the way Chanhee had even looked surprised when he read what was inside was enough. Chanhee had a right to snoop around in Solar's things because that was just how he was, they'd been friends practically since birth, so it wasn't abnormal. But Jimin felt weird doing something like that, so he stayed put, blinking as he thought about what he said, "you think? Is there a note or something?"

"Pictures, a CD with a note on it, and a necklace that probably costs as much as my car," he informs, looking in the box one last time before he sets it back down to it's original spot. 

"Oh," is all he says for a moment, "that sounds nice, I guess it's better that she wasn't alone since we all were asleep."

Chanhee sighed, feeling indifferent about the information. "I would've stayed up with her, but she insisted she just wanted to be alone," he shook his head, "she probably was hoping either him or Jungkook would be there for her."

Jimin was nodding in agreement, "I don't really think it was right for Jungkook to just pass out without checking on her, but like he said, he probably was really tired."

"Sure," Chanhee is mumbling, too deep in thought, "it's not really that," he pauses for a second as he thought a bit more, "at Solar's party, I could see how Jungkook loves her. I know there's a twinkle in his eyes when he looks at her, they clearly have something special."

His brows are knitted together from how hard he was thinking about his words, "yet, when she's with Jinhwan, she looks like she's known him all her life despite how it's been the shortest time... I've watched Solar grow up before my eyes, I knew her through out her relationship with Sunwoo - but even then, she doesn't shine the way she does when she's with him."

Jimin is taking in what he's saying, recalling how he'd seen Solar when she was with Jungkook versus Jinhwan. It was hard for him to think about, but he could understand where he was coming from. "I think you're right," he finally speaks, "I wasn't sure about Jinhwan after what happened, but he really felt sorry for it. Even when it wasn't completely his fault, he did everything he could to make it up to her, I can only imagine what happened last night."

"It's weird, you know? I never thought I'd see Solar with anyone that wasn't Sunwoo. I fucking hate the guy, but they were the perfect couple, the couple that everyone wanted to be, the couple that pained you to be around," he was rambling at that point, eyes focused on the ceiling, "back then, I thought that she was the happiest she would ever be. But then she shows up with this guy, and I realized that this life she made for herself, that was where she found her true happiness, and I think a lot of it had to do with him."

The two had chatted a bit more about Solar and what they thought, until Jimin had posed a rather odd question, "have you heard anything from Sunwoo? As much as they act like it, I don't think anyone from here is smart or strong enough to take on whoever it is that's doing this to her. I figured Sunwoo would be here trying to help her by now."

Chanhee pursed his lips from the question. Not that he didn't trust Jimin, but he couldn't just blurt out the fact that Sunwoo was indeed in South Korea when Solar was sleeping right beside him. As much as he wanted to tell her, this was the one exception he would actually do what Kim Sunwoo says. 

So, with a shrug, he responds, "no, I haven't. My conversations with Sunwoo are very rare, and usually consist of us insulting each other for about five minutes before walking away."

Jimin is staring at him a bit oddly, being able to sense that there was something off about his speech. Yet, he didn't question it.

Though he already had a bit of an idea.



+



"I know, Mi," Jungkook blurts, causing the nervous girl's gaze to whip up to him from having had been fiddling with her fingers in her lap, "I know that it's hard for you, I wanted to get back together with you because I thought that everything had slowed down for us. I thought that whoever this person is that's bothering you gave up, which was dumb of me. I guess I just thought we could go back in time and pretend like it was 2018 again."

She hadn't expected such a reaction from him, she thought he would be angry, confused, upset. Jungkook had been hoping to get back with her for a long time, she knew that much. But after those last words he spoke, she realized that maybe they both had the same idea. 

"After last night, when they did what they did, I knew that they weren't done. I knew I would have to give you some space instead of parading you about us becoming official again," he goes on, a dull look in his doe eyes yet he appeared so hopeful, "I knew how shaken up you must've been, but yet when you were next to Jinhwan it looked like you had no cares in the world."

"Jungkook, it's not-"

"-but I'm glad, because he's always helped you when I couldn't or didn't," he shakes his head sadly, breaking their eye contact to stare at his feet, "so, I guess what I'm saying is, it's okay. I think a break from both of us would be great for you."

She only gawked at him as she took in his words. Didn't Jungkook just technically say that he knew she wanted to be with Jinhwan? She wasn't even completely sure of it herself, but he basically just admitted it. It almost felt like deja vu all over again, except this time, she wasn't going to go run to Jinhwan. 

She was going to be alone. 

Or not alone, but with her family and closest friends. She was only romantically alone, which is what she had probably needed for a long time.

"Thank you, Kookie," she finally breathes, "I-It means a lot to me that you're so understanding, I think this is what I really need to figure this all out."

Jungkook nods with that familiar sad smile on his face, "of course, Mi, I love you, and I want what's best for you. You know that, we've been through this before."

She felt bad from how heartbroken he sounded, "yeah... but this isn't me making a decision this time, so wipe that look off your face," she tried to comfort him even if it wasn't exactly truthful, "we'll keep in touch, okay? I think I'm just going to focus on my music and spend some time with my siblings, I rented a new apartment in the other YG apartment building and had a big security system installed, so I should be okay for the time being - plus, you know, I'll have Jaehyun and everyone."

"That's great, Mi," Jungkook managed to smile a bit, feeling better from her kind words that reassured his worry, "even if they do track you to the new building, they probably wouldn't do much since you'll have a full house. I definitely wouldn't want to mess with your older brother."

She giggled with a nod, "yeah, I wouldn't either, though he can be a bit dense."

The boy shrugs, "we all can, right?"

"True!" she hums, and the two of them found themselves laughing together even though they were technically saying goodbye. Even though it wasn't like it was forever, it still was rather serious. In a way, it was like a breakup despite them not being officially together.

But it had felt like they were for a while, and Jungkook would cherish every moment of it. As hard as it was to accept it, he knew it would likely be the last time he would be that close with her. 

They spoke a bit more, until heavy footsteps were heard through the living room. Solar hadn't even glanced up when a whole crowd of boys came tumbling through the kitchen doorway. 

"Mi, you're leaving us!" Hoseok is crying out dramatically, holding a hand to his heart. 

"It's about time you stopped mooching off of us," Jin is saying in a rather serious tone, until a big smile spreads over his lips, "joking, I feel like a mother sending her kid off to college."

Solar was staring at their dramatic reactions to her leaving as if they were crazy, "guys, calm down, I wasn't even here that long. You're acting like I'm moving back to America - I'm literally gonna be like - twenty five minutes away."

"That's twenty five minutes too far!"

They all kept whining and whining about how sad they were until Chanhee had walked in the room, that motherly look on his face as he informed Solar, "Sol, I'm pretty sure the moving truck is there, we should probably head out."

"Really?" she raises a brow, "what time is it?"

"3 PM."

"Oh fuck!" she gasps, jumping to her feet, "I didn't even load my bags yet!" she's crying as she rushes into the living room with everyone following.

"Got you covered there!" Juyeon is calling from the front door that was wide open, "we're all ready to go, big sis."

She stops in her tracks, having about to have run up the stairs to go grab all her things. A breath of relief leaves her lips as she sends her little brother a thankful grin. 

Though, since they were ready to go, it meant it was time to say goodbye.

"Nobody cry," Namjoon is saying as they all huddle around her to give their hugs and best wishes.

"I can't wait for your new EP, Mi!" Taehyung is cheering her on, "we'll be streaming!"

"Text me if you need help with music," Yoongi had said, giving her a half assed hug until she forcefully pulled him in for a bigger one. The boy wasn't able to keep that sour look on his face as that gummy smile made an appearance, he couldn't help but hug her tightly.

When she finally finished her goodbyes and swore to Jimin that they would meet up every once in a while and keep in close contact, the only one that was left was Jungkook. 

She stood before him with bright eyes, feeling a lot better since the talk they had left them on pretty good terms. Though, a part of her was sad that they wouldn't be close like that anymore, she knew it was what she had to do. This break was going to help her a lot more than she knew.

Jungkook had peered down at her for a second, before that bunny smile spread over his face. "Good luck on your music, Mi," he tells her as he pulls her into his arms, "please don't ever be afraid to call if you need me, I'll be there in seconds."

"Of course, Kookie, I'll be okay," she insists, inhaling his scent one last time.

Jungkook sighed deeply as they pulled away. She sent him another small reassuring smile, taking her first steps towards the car that was waiting for her with all her friends and family tucked inside.

"And be careful, Mi," he called out when she was about to get in the car.

She turned back to him for the last time in God knows how long, nodding in acknowledgment as she returned, "same goes to you, Jungkook!"


143:00 | closer than you think

"Wow, look at this place," Jacob is humming as they're all standing there, hands on his hips with a proud look on his face, "I think it looks great."

"Woo~! Good work team!" Kevin is cheering, clapping his hands together as he jumps in the air like the idiot that he was. His actions caused everyone to give him a weird look, but no one said anything since they didn't want to burst his poor bubble.

Solar found herself feeling emotional as she watched her siblings and friends celebrate them finally finishing moving her furniture in all the right places. Though the decorating part of her new apartment was nowhere near done, the fact they managed to get the furniture all the way up here and put it in the right places was pretty impressive. 

Though she couldn't help but get a little sad, it was going to be weird not living in the same building as iKON anymore. Since this YG apartment building was much newer, it was obviously way nicer, but the other one would always have a special place in her heart. There was no telling how many memories she made in it, after all. 

In fact, since this was a YG building, she didn't even know who else lived in it despite it being under the company. She wasn't sure how reliable that security system would be when she was stuck here all alone with no one in the building that she knew, but she couldn't worry about that now. Plus, she didn't think her siblings seemed to be leaving anytime soon.

She'd be just fine.

"Well, little sis," Jaehyun hums, hitting her back in a friendly manner. The thing was, he was too strong, and it almost caused her to stumble over as she sent him an annoyed glare, which he only answered with a smile, "how do you feel?"

"Hmm," she ponders, "it's nice, just empty."

"What's it like being a genuine single woman?!" Chaerin chimes in with that bright laugh, being in a scarily good mood, "geesh, my big sister breaks hearts left and right."

"I do not," she gawks at her, slapping her shoulder even though she had been kidding, "I think all of this is just what I needed."

The two siblings smile warmly, nodding in agreement. "Mhm, now you can focus on what actually matters," Chaerin tells her, "finally taking a break from those idiots."

Solar rolls her eyes, before she thinks of something. "Wait, I don't think I got all my things out of the car," she announces, turning to Jae, "hey, can I have the keys?"

"Course, just don't get kidnapped or something," he warns her playfully as he throws them at her. 

She catches them with her hand, shaking her head, "no promises."

Informing the others where she was going, she made her way out the door, closing it behind her. The scent in the hallway seemed to tell her that it was a new building, it was so fresh compared to the old one. Despite how she felt a little emotional about the move, she was happy. Solar figured she would be so sad if she took a break from Jinhwan and Jungkook, but she felt great, a lot better than she had in a while.

All she had to do now was finish this for good. She was going to focus on her music, but also focus on figuring out who this person was. Jinhwan would also be on the lookout, as he wanted to try to do something with what he knew about their car and license plate. She wasn't too confident that that kind of information would actually help them, though.

Since they were both under the same company, she knew she would be seeing him pretty often despite their break. It wasn't the end of the world, really, they would just talk as if they were friends. How they always were, minus the romantic aspects. 

Though she was unsure if she wanted to face him yet, with that song still on her mind.

It was exactly what she forgot in the car, his gift. She wanted to put up those polaroids to remind her of the positive memories she'd made. Solar was pretty sure she had more photos packed up in the boxes from her old apartment that she needed to get out, too. 

That would make it feel like home.

Moments later, she had successfully gathered anything she left in the car. She held the golden box that glimmered under the lights close to her heart as she made her way down the hallway towards the elevator. She found herself thinking about that song, Flower, even though she knew she should have other things on her mind. 

Jinhwan and Jungkook shouldn't be what she was thinking about anymore. The thing was, she was too afraid to think about the real problem at hand. All Solar wanted to do was focus on her music, even though that wasn't the reason for her entire break. 

Thinking about the way her party ended caused a dull sigh to leave her lips. What the hell could they be planning?

Poor Solar was always too caught up in her head. She was so focused on her thoughts that she didn't hear nor notice the group of boys that were also in the lobby of the apartment building. 

"Yah, yah, is that really her?! Am I dreaming?!" one was frantically whispering to the other, running his hands through his hair, "someone pinch me, hurry!"

"Yes, hyung, I'm pretty sure that's her and not a Soomi look alike," the other had answered, "you know, she has ears. She can probably hear you from how bad you are at whispering."

The cutesy boy frowns at his friend's words, "that would be really embarrassing," he mutters, before piping up again out of nowhere as he glances to the girl that was nearing their spot by the elevator, "should I say hi? Do you think she even knows who we are?!" he's asking the other boy in a rush, jumping around out of anxiety, "Oh, why don't we just take the elevator with her?! Then she might notice us and say something!"

His friend was much calmer than he was, thankfully. The red haired boy chuckles at his hyung, shaking his head in dismay, "I'm pretty sure she's really nice, let's just say hi," he's shrugging, "don't think so hard about it, Junkyu-hyung, she probably won't know us since we're just trainees."

The boy is practically flying as he was still so jumpy, "it feels like there's so much I want to say, though! I don't know how to talk to her, I never thought I would meet her - at least not yet, and now I found out she moved into the same building as us-"

His rambling was cut off by a loud screech, one that they both knew too well. The two friends turn with worried eyes, knowing that it belonged to the very person they were just talking about. The red haired boy was ready to head towards the scene, eager to help her if she'd tripped or something, but when he saw what exactly went down - he was in too much shock to even make a move. 

"Ah, mianhae!" the boy is crying, eyes full of horror as he hurriedly picks the box up off the ground, bowing repeatedly as if it was all he knew to do, "I'm so sorry, that was so stupid of me! Please forgive me!"

"Oh no..." the boy is mumbling to the other, "please tell me he didn't just do that."

"He sure did."

Solar's mouth was slightly agape still, as she hadn't taken into account what just happened to her. Blinking a few times, she seemed to come back down to Earth as she had been standing there staring at the boy before her like she was frozen solid. Meanwhile, the poor boy had only continued apologizing and bowing, still holding out the box that had fallen to the ground to her for her to take. 

It was like she forgot how to talk as she only peered up at him since he was much taller than her. The weirdest twinkle was in those green irises as she finally found her words, clearing her throat and shaking her head as she took the box out of his hands, "no, no, stop apologizing, it's okay!" she insists with a soft smile on her lips. 

Yet, the boy still bowed a few more times, until Solar had harshly persisted, "really, it's okay, it's not that serious!" she cries, laughing a little at how kind this mystery boy seemed to be, "if you bow one more time I'm seriously going to lose it."

Finally, he stopped his apologetic actions. His worried expression suddenly turned into one of the brightest smiles she'd ever seen as he nodded, "ah, I don't want to know what that means."


Solar was in a trance again, until she shook her head vigorously, trying to snap herself out of whatever was happening to her. "You sure don't," she hums in response, yet she was still staring up at him with confused eyes. Unable to take it anymore, she blurts, "not to sound rude if you're an artist, but who are you?"

The boy was taken aback for a second, processing her words, until he shook his head quickly, "oh, no, no! I'm a trainee under YG, so it's understandable you probably don't know me!" he waves a hand in the air in dismissal, "Jihoon, my name's Park Jihoon," he bows again with another pretty smile, "it's a honor to meet you."

Solar couldn't lie, she was pretty impressed at his manners. She also couldn't seem to stop staring at him like a complete weirdo. Still, she offers cheerfully, "oh, okay, I see! Nice to meet you, I'm Soomi."

Jihoon chuckles, "don't worry, I definitely know that. We train to your songs all the time, you're basically YG's pride."

The brunette wrinkles her nose at his compliment, "nah, I wouldn't go that far," she shrugs it off, "so, are you debuting soon? Sorry, I'm real out of touch with what's happening with the company these days."

Shoving his hands in his pockets, she could tell he was really excited that she asked. A nervous smile hangs on his lips as he answers, "ah, yeah, I'm debuting in a group called Treasure in August!"

Solar pursed her lips in deep thought for a second, before a wave of realization washes over her face, "oh, I think I've heard word about you guys. August isn't so far, congratulations!"

He bows again with that same grin on his face, "thank you~!"

Pleased to have already met some kind people, the brunette boy seems to suddenly be focused on something behind them. Solar was about to turn to see what he was doing as he was waving his hand, presumably calling someone over, when her phone starts buzzing in her pocket. 

"Come over here, weirdos, or you'll miss your chance," Jihoon had been mouthing to the two boys, noticing that she wasn't paying attention currently. Doyoung had been trying to calm Junkyu down, who was frantically telling him he didn't have the guts to talk to her and that he would pass out. Doyoung had rolled his eyes, shaking his head in disappointment before grabbing the boy's arm and dragging him in their direction. 

Just as they're about to reach the two, Solar had sighed down at her phone screen. An apologetic look is on her face as she meets the eyes of Jihoon, "I'm sorry, I'm getting an important phone call, and I should probably get back up to my apartment anyway or my friends will get worried."

Jihoon shakes his head vigorously in dismissal, smiling sweetly as he insists, "oh no, no, no worries! I can't imagine how busy your schedule is, and moving is a pain as it is!"

"It definitely is," she hums in agreement, already a few steps away as she calls back to him, "I'll see you around, Jihoon! Maybe you can come by my studio sometime!"

Fighting the urge to scream at her words, he nods excitedly, "I'd love to!"

Solar sends him one last bright flash of her smile before turning her back and entering the elevator. As soon as the doors close, she swipes her finger across the screen, answering the FaceTime call.


+


"Huh? You're telling me that dumbass genuinely saw the model and license plate?"  Sunwoo asks in that monotone voice he always had. It was clear he didn't believe it, and even then, he still wasn't impressed. Though, he hadn't managed to catch the stalker himself even though he had been at the venue. It was bad timing, clearly, since he'd been on the opposite side of the building at the time they made their escape. If he'd been the one to catch them, they'd already be dead.

"Yeah, apparently, but it's likely it's not the guy we're even looking for," Jaehyun had reported on the other line, "no telling how many people are under them."

"Probably more than we know," Sunwoo sighs, "just give me the details, I've already got access to the police database, I can find them in no time."

"Already?" the eldest brother of the Lee family snorts, "I don't even want to know how you managed that so quickly."

"Nothing impressive," he mutters a few minutes later, already typing in the information he'd been given, "I'm getting rusty, otherwise I'd already have found them."

"Nah, man, they're just smarter than a lot of people."

Sunwoo didn't say anything in response. He really did feel like he should've already found them. Really, he had every opportunity to, he felt like he was slacking on what was supposed to be his talent. Things like this were what he was raised to do, and he was failing at it somehow, putting the love of his life in danger. He was done with it. Now that he had this piece, he was going to track them even if they were moving every single second. It was over.

Jaehyun had sat there, phone against his ear as he stared at himself in the mirror of Solar's new apartment. He knew he probably didn't have much time before Solar would be back from getting whatever was in her car, so he needed to hurry up before questions were asked that he couldn't answer right now. 

Figuring it would take Sunwoo a while to find it, Jaehyun cleared his throat, "hey, man, I'm gonna go ahead and get back to everyone, tell me if you find 'em."

A good minute passed of him waiting for a response, but Sunwoo was dead silent. All he could hear was the tapping of his keyboard. 

That was, until...

"Wait," his sharp voice cuts into the air.

"Find it?" Jaehyun asks immediately intrigued, "I'm ready to go whenever."

"The car belongs to YG Entertainment," Sunwoo deadpans, "it's a company vehicle."

They both knew well what that meant. "You've gotta be fucking kidding me."

"Do you think I would be joking?" Sunwoo scoffs, "It makes sense, look at who the partner was. It was clear they were likely under her company, but I think it's multiple people under her company."

"Holy fuck," the brother is mumbling, "well, it's not safe for her to go there, then! Hell, she's probably even unsafe in this apartment complex, they could literally be her fucking neighbor."

"Nowhere is safe for her," Sunwoo asserts, "don't tell her anything."

Jaehyun could tell by his tone he already had a plan, but he didn't have any idea what it was. "So, I'm just supposed to let her go there by herself knowing she's in danger?"

"She won't be by herself," Sunwoo corrects, "I have it under control. It's time to finally end this."

He had a feeling what Sunwoo meant by this and what his plan was, but he still didn't understand how he would pull it off. Did he not need any help sneaking into YG Entertainment out of all places? He knew this was Kim Sunwoo, but how was he even going to manage that?

But he didn't have a chance to even ask, because Sunwoo had ended the call, leaving him to stand there, basking in his own thoughts and worries.

He trusted Sunwoo, though. He had to. 


144:00 | a good day

"Sunwoo~!" Solar is singing at she peers down at the phone screen, seeing the boy's face just barely due to the dim lighting. "Never thought I'd get a call from you today," she's humming, "speaking of, I did need to talk to you!"

The boy stares at her for a moment, his brown eyes managing to sparkle under the dim light as they peer into her soul. His black hair was a mess in his face, and there remained a blank look on his face, as per usual, unless he was next to her. "What, you thought I wouldn't check on you?" he asks, his voice as monotone as ever, yet she knew he meant it as a joke. 

"Not that," she shakes her head, "I know no one cares about me as much as you do... probably."

Despite him trying to hide it, a small grin spreads across his usually sinister looking face. "Mm, and don't forget that. But what's the update, sweetheart?"

Before she could even begin to tell him about the party Jungkook threw for her, the elevator let out a ding, indicating she was at her floor. Clearing her throat, she steps out of it and begins her way to her new apartment. "I'll tell you when I'm somewhere safe," she informs him, "I finally got a new apartment! Great, right?"

"Sure, great if it was in New York," he retorts.

Solar rolls her eyes, "Sunwoo, we went over this-"

"-Princess, I'm aware, your little company won't let you."

"Exactly," she nods, "and it's not like I don't have a life here."

"Mhm, a life with no protection," he sighs.

By that time, Solar had reached the front door, stepping inside and locking it behind her. There was music playing, loud but not too loud, from the living room stereo. Lounging on the couches sat her siblings, followed by her best friends, talking about God knew what. They all look to her as she enters the room, and she waves to them, setting the things down that she brought up from the car. 

"I'm talking to Sunwoo," she explains, pointing to her phone, "I'll be out in a bit."

Some of them exchanged glances she was attentive enough to notice, before sending her a nod. 

With that, she heads down the hall to her new bedroom, closing the door behind her. 

"Anyway," she's muttering, falling onto her soft mattress and squinting at the phone screen, "where the fuck even are you?" It was about time she asked, Sunwoo definitely wasn't at the mansion. If he was, it wouldn't be that dark, and there would be booming music and screaming in the background. 

If he was anyone other than Kim Sunwoo, she would've been able to tell something was up. "Work," is all he says.

But Solar was still confused. "I thought you weren't working for your father at the moment after he literally shot me."

Sunwoo purses his lips, a glint in his irises, before a light chuckle leaves his lips, "not every job I do is for my father, Sol."

"Oh," she hums, deciding to drop the subject. Whatever he was up to, it was better if she didn't know. Plus, there were more important things she needed to tell him. "You know, you never told me happy birthday!" she suddenly gasps, never realizing such a thing until now.

"I was a little occupied," Sunwoo responds, "I think you've already gotten your present from me anyway, just a little early."

Knowing what he was talking about without a second thought, the brunette wrinkles her nose. "God, I hate you," she groans, "you know what, nevermind."

Sunwoo is snorting from amusement at that same reaction she always had, before getting back to the important matter at hand, "so, give me the update." He already knew what she was going to tell him. In fact, he knew more than she did about the situation, but he couldn't tell her that. 

"Oh! I had a party," she begins, before pausing in thought, "or, well, Jungkook threw me a party. It was at this huge venue and practically everyone I knew was there. It was going really well for a while, but you know, I'm sure you can guess what happened."

Sunwoo was listening closely, nodding to show his attentiveness. "So, what did they do this time?"

"Long story short, cut the power and displayed some sinister message on the screen," she goes on, "I guess it wasn't really that bad. I mean, that's all they seem to do, you know? Anyway, I was leaving with Jinhwan when we saw whoever was behind it escaping the venue. He tried to catch them but they sped off," she stops to exhale, "however, he did see the kind of car they had and the license plate. I was thinking that would be useful to you."

"Maybe," he nods, "but he didn't catch any physical traits about them? Height, weight, unique aspects?"
