"I don't guess," she shrugs, "it was nighttime."

"Hm, not impressed," he deadpans, "not that I would've expected more."

"Hey!" she scolds, "you should be glad he got something that could help us catch whoever the fuck this is, okay? God knows I am," her voice begins to falter as she lays down onto her bed, holding the phone with one hand, "I just want this to all be over."

Sunwoo could feel his blood boiling from seeing how much it was all hurting her. With each second that passed by, he felt like she was a sitting duck for these pieces of shit. 

He had to stop it.

"It'll be okay, sweetheart," he breaks into the silence in her head with that raspy voice of his, "it'll be over before you know it, alright? Just make sure you're aware of everything around you, beware of the shadows, beware of absolutely everyone. I'm going to find out who it is in a global database if possible, and we'll go from there. But in the meantime, I need you to be safe, alright?"

Feeling better already from his reassurance, she nods, "yeah, of course, Sunwoo."

The boy flashes a small smile her way, "and call me immediately if anything slightly suspicious happens."

"Will do!"


+


"Jae, I am not letting my sister go to work to be literally surrounded by people who want to kill her!" Chaerin is practically on fire from anger, her fists clenched at her sides, as she glares at the eldest brother. 

The boy had sighed, taking a lighter out of his leather jacket pocket and lighting the cigarette he had in his other hand. "Chae," he breathes, as calm as ever, "Solar will be fine. Do you think I would allow her to go there if it was any other way?"

"Well it sure fucking seems like you are!" she snaps, "Ugh, just wait till Chanhee and the others hear about this, they are not going to be fucking happy!"

"Chae," he says again in a much sterner tone, "calm down. I'm going to talk to them after this, but you need to understand that everything is fine. Sunwoo is going to be there with her."

Her once frustrated expression slowly fades into one of pure confusion. "Huh? How is he going to get into YG fucking Entertainment?" she scoffs, "what, did they hire him as a fucking personal bodyguard?!"

Wishing that his sister would stop yelling for two seconds, Jaehyun rubs his temples as he tries to figure out what to tell her. "Look, I don't know for sure how he's doing it. The point is, nobody doubts Sunwoo, and he's going to take care of it. We just have to trust him, you know he wouldn't let anyone lay a hand on Solar."

Her nerves were finally seeming to relax. With a deep breath, her hands fall back to her side. Shaking her head, she falls onto the sofa, "I guess you're right, I'd rather it be Sunwoo than anyone else. No one's as trained for this as he is."

Relieved at the fact she was finally calm, Jaehyun nods, "that is very correct, sister. This should be over with before we know it."

All they had to do was hope Sunwoo could keep her safe for whatever was coming.


+


There was a lot on Solar's mind that sunny morning. She'd been allowed to use the vehicle Jaehyun had rented in Korea to get to work. It was the first time she'd actually driven herself in a long time. It felt refreshing, was all she could think as she locked the vehicle behind her in the parking lot of YG Ent.

She's just reaching the entrance of the company when she notices a familiar face. Her eyes brighten with excitement, and she clears her throat, jogging a bit faster as she calls out, "hey, Jihoon-ah~!"

The tall boy appeared shocked as he turned to see who was calling his name. His eyes landed on the brunette, who had finally reached him just inside the lobby and he had to hold a hand to his heart for a mere moment to keep from passing out. Trying to keep his composure, he instead offers her a smile, "ah, hi noona! Nice to see you!"

There she was, caught off guard again by that weird name she wasn't used to hearing. She supposed since he was a trainee, he was likely younger than her, too. Fuck, was she getting old or something?

"You too!" she beams, "I couldn't help but come say hi, I really feel like today's gonna be a good day."

Jihoon is observing her for a moment, presumably trying to take the moment in. Finally, he hums in agreement, "you have a bright aura surrounding you today, noona, I think the sun might be jealous."

Solar wrinkles her nose, "don't tell me you're a corny one."

He only responds with a giggle, causing her to shake her head. She felt like she already had enough of those in her life. But Jihoon was a sweet kid, reminiscent of Haechan, really. Maybe the younger generation was just full of sweethearts. Or maybe they were obviously going to suck up to the biggest idol in Korea. Either way, she didn't mind it.

"Do you have a busy schedule today?" she's asking as they're making their way further into the lobby, "or, what does a schedule for a trainee even look like?" she's muttering, "sorry, I can't remember... it's been so long."

"No worries!" he shakes his head, "it's not too busy, just practice and evaluations."

"Ah~," she's in deep thought for a second, before she proclaims, "well, if you have time, feel free to come up to my studio!" She pauses her movement, Jihoon doing so as well, to dig in her bag. Within a second, she pulls out a pass with her name on it, smiling as she hands it to him, "here, this'll give you access to my floor."

His entire body was practically trembling as he took it from her hands. "Wow, that's so nice of you," he's gawking down at the pass like it was the most amazing thing he'd seen in his life, "thank you! I definitely will."

"No thanking needed," she shrugs it off, "but I need to get to my studio, lots of recording to do for my new EP!"

"Good luck, noona!"

Waving to Jihoon, she faces back towards the front with that same bright grin on her face. A good day, she told herself, I'm going to have a good day. She had a new apartment, new music coming out, she made a new friend. She couldn't let what happened at her party destroy her. At this point, if these people really wanted to do something to her, they would have already. The fact they were still sitting around proved they were bluffing. Quite frankly, she was tired of it.

And if Sunwoo was on the case, it would be better in no time. He was going to find who it was, and then send someone to take care of them. All would be well in the world, she knew that. It would only be a matter of time.

They wouldn't know what was coming to them. All she had to do was wait and be aware of her surroundings, just like he said.

Solar wasn't dumb. She knew that whoever it was likely had a connection or was a staff at YG. She also figured that there was literally no way someone would try to pull something on her in the company. There was way too much security, way too many cameras, and way too many witnesses. You would have to be an idiot.

Unless they had access to somewhere without those things, like her studio. But no one else did except her and her manager.

Everything was fine.

Moments later, Solar was walking down the hall of her floor. iKON's studio was also on this floor, and she passed it as she was going to her own. A part of her really wanted to go say hi, but she was already going to be late for her recording session. Though, she was going to have to talk to Jinhwan eventually. They couldn't just not speak after she got a gift like that.

The thing was, when they spoke, she knew he would be looking for an answer she couldn't give him. Jinhwan likely expected the gift to wake her up, to make her realize that she needed him. As much as it touched her heart, as much as it made her think about everything that happened, and as much as she knew a part of her loved him so deeply - she wouldn't be able to tell him that.

Plus, the boys were probably busy with their new song coming out in only a few days. 

So, she only peered at the door as she passed it, before continuing on.

"Sorry, I'm a little late!" she's calling as she enters her own studio, expecting her manager and producer to already be there. Solar was instead left in confusion as she turned the light on, seeing the studio as empty as ever. 

That's weird, he's usually never late, she thought, setting her bag down on the booth before walking over to the desk. Atop it sat a note, scribbled messily, that she figured must be from her missing manager. 

had to run to the store for some last minute things, be back soon! producer is currently working on another project with iKON, so might be a little late as well!

Blinking down at the note in confusion, she shrugs it off after a moment. He probably went to get some snacks for their session today or something. And it wasn't abnormal for the producer to be working with other artists. She thought nothing of it, instead she scooted out the chair at the front table, sitting down in front of the computer to mess with music until they all got back. 

At least she wasn't technically late now, right?!





145:00 | taught right

"I'm here to help with some final touch ups to the song," the producer had been explaining to the seven boys. They all peered up at him from their seats, nodding in understanding. 

That was, until Jinhwan cleared his throat, "weren't you supposed to be working with Soomi today?" 

The producer swallowed a lump in his throat, but shook it off quickly. "She's running late today," he explains, "so I have time to spare."

Some of them exchange glances, wondering what the hell that could mean. Soomi wasn't really one to be late, after all. Especially recently, she was taking her music more seriously than ever. 

There was a sour taste in Jinhwan's mouth. As the producer went on to talk about their new song coming out and what he wanted to change, Jinhwan slips his phone out of his back pocket.

nani: hey are u already at the company?

nani: the producer is with us for some reason, he said u were late 

nani: tell me when u get here, i want to see u

Pursing his lips, he scratches the back of his head as he holds his phone with the other. With a tendency to sweat when he got nervous, Jinhwan was beginning to feel his forehead heat up. He wanted to get up and check on her in the studio, but he knew it would be bad news to leave during a session. 

Still, he was tempted to do it anyway, otherwise it would drive him insane. He just wanted to know if she was okay. 

Just as he's rising to his feet to excuse himself, the producer had turned to him, "say, Jinhwan, could you re-record your part in the second verse?"

Fuck. 

"Sure, no problem," he nods, plastering a fake smile on his face. Heading into the recording booth, he exhales deeply as he puts the headphones on. Clearing his throat, he sang a few notes to make sure his voice was still warmed up.

He'd get this over with quickly, and then go check on her. Everything would be okay.

Yet, he felt the most sinister vibe surrounding him. His gut was trying to tell him something, and it was making him feel sick.

He finished before he knew it, taking off his headphones when the producer spoke from the microphone connected to the booth, "that was good, but can you do a little more vibrato on the last line?"

Trying not to show his annoyance, he nods in agreement, putting them back on and beginning to sing the part again.

And that went on for what felt like hours.

Every time he would finish, the producer would nitpick one tiny thing he did so he would have to do it over again. It was beginning to feel like some sort of sick game. The boys noticed something was off, too, as they were only watching in confusion at that point. Some of them were laughing about it, joking about how their poor eldest must be having an off day. They didn't seem to be noticing the same vibe that he was. He was clearly being kept here for a reason, and it couldn't be a good one.

This producer had to be in on it.

"Hmm, I think that first bit was a little hoarse, why don't you-" he began to speak into the mic again, when Jinhwan rips the headphones off his head and barges out of the booth without another word. The producer didn't falter, as he turned to him in confusion, "do you need a break? I understand it might feel like a lot, I just want the song to be as polished as possible."

"I think I need to pay a visit to YG-nim's office," Jinhwan informs, "there's a few things I need to discuss with him that have been bothering me, so it's hard to focus."

The producer stares at him for a moment, pursing his lips. Turning back to the computer, he informs blatantly, "It's his off day, I'm afraid, he's not here today."

"Are you sure?" he pushed even further, knowing even more that something was wrong. Something was more than wrong, and he needed to get to Solar before it was too late. "We usually know if he'll be off, and I never heard anything."

Acting as casual as possible, the guy was busy clicking away on the computer, "I'm guessing it was a last minute decision," is all he mutters.

The others, confused and growing nervous about the aura in the room, look to their eldest. "Hyung, why don't you just sit down?" Hanbin calls, "you look like you're going to faint."

Jinhwan only shook his head, "no, I need to go check on something. It's important," he insists sternly, unable to control his breathing. He was breathing heavily from the anxiety building inside of him. All he could think was Solar.

Turning towards the door, he hurriedly rushes towards it. His hand is just gripping the handle when a voice speaks from behind him. 

"I wouldn't do that if I were you."

"Huh?" 

"It's wired," the producer wasn't hiding it anymore. His tone no longer had any ounce of emotion in it, yet he sounded menacing almost as he chuckles, "open the door and you'll set off God knows how many dynamite around this building."

"Fucking what?!" Junhoe had blurted, and the others were in awe, mouths agape as they were beginning to realize what this meant.

Too bad it'd taken them far too long. 

Jinhwan's throat was dry, all he could do was let his hand fall back to his side. Trying to process everything, trying to grasp what this meant, he could feel his blood boiling. His entire body was losing control. "You fucking... how fucking dare you..." is all he could mutter, eyes narrowed at the producer that was only staring at him with a bored expression. 

Knowing he was about to lunge at him, the guy sighs, pulling something out of his pocket. 

"Don't even think about it," he snaps, "any of you."

And it was clear he wasn't afraid to use the weapon he was holding in front of them. 

"What the hell is wrong with you?!" Bobby had cried, eyes full of anger. He shot to his feet, fists balled at his side, "How could you people work against someone who's done nothing to you?!" Hanbin had quickly pulled him down onto the seat again before he could do something stupid. 

Junhoe had been biting down on his bottom lip, mind racing. He couldn't face the thought, the reality of what was happening. It had to be some kind of nightmare. 

They were helpless. They couldn't help Solar from here. All they could do was sit there, holding in their tears, and trying to contain their worry for the poor girl. Jinhwan slides against the wall onto the floor, burying his face in his hands as he curses to himself. Idiot, he was an idiot. He knew this was coming, but he didn't think it would happen this way, not like this. He didn't think it would be inside their company.

And now he had no idea what was possibly in store for Kim Soomi.


+


A yawn leaves Solar's lips as she peers at the computer screen. It seemed like it'd been a fairly long while that she'd been messing around with different melodies, not really working on anything in particular. At this point, she should've been working on a song with all the time that had passed unexpectedly. Her manager wasn't one to take this long, and it was causing her to worry more than she should.

Unsure of what more to do, she leans back in the chair, exhaling deeply. The brunette closes her eyes for a mere second, just relishing in the day and trying to think rationally about the current situation. 

There had been a weird aura in the company today. It felt like it was much less empty than usual, like a lot of staff had an off day for some reason. Perhaps it was a holiday she didn't know about, Solar wasn't very up on those kinds of things.

The most logical thing to do would try to call her manager and ask what was going on. Rising to her feet, she heads over to her bag to dig out her phone. 

And she invited Jihoon to her studio today, too! She was hoping to have gotten much more done before then. Oh well, it wasn't the end of the world. The majority of her EP was polished anyway, Solar was just a perfectionist. 

A look of confusion spreads over her face as she had sat there, digging around in her duffle bag. Where the hell - I swear it's usually right on top... her brain was telling her, but she didn't see her iPhone anywhere. 

As she stands back up straight, trying to gather her thoughts, a certain feeling in her gut settled in. 

Something wasn't right. But what was it? 

Wrong, something was indeed very wrong.

But what-

Though Solar once had impeccable reaction time, her guard wasn't fully up at that moment. A curse leaves her lips as she feels something ever so sharp graze her neck. It wasn't pushed into her skin, but there as a warning, and she knew what was happening. 

"Don't," a familiar voice hisses, "move."

But it wasn't a voice she expected to hear. 

"What the hell?" she's muttering, almost laughing at how lost she was, "you?" she's asking in bewilderment, "wait, it was you?!"

The girl heaves out a sigh, turning Solar around by gripping her shoulder before pushing her back so she falls onto the booth. Solar lets her do this, in too much shock from the fact it was her standing in front of her. The more she thought about it, though, the more it made sense.

"You're not as smart as he said you were," she's observing, twirling the knife in her fingers before pointing it back at her face, "I expected more from you, Solar."

The brunette is shaking her head in constant disbelief, unable to fathom the entire situation. "He?" she's blinking, "who's he? And why would you even be working with them?! We don't even know each other, is it just because of Jinhwan?!"

Jimin rolls her eyes, clearly not looking like she was in the mood to give her the whole rundown. Still, she groans, "maybe if you'd take your head out of your ass you'd figure it out," she snaps, wrinkling her nose in disgust towards the girl. "I have plenty of reasons to hate you, that's just one of the many. I don't know what he could possibly see in you, but I'm not letting you take him away from me any longer."

"Really?" she's looking to Jimin in disappointment, "you'll kill me all for a guy? You don't realize how fucking sad that is?"

Suddenly, she jerks the knife to her throat again. "Close your fucking mouth, bitch," she snaps, "you're a bold piece of fucking shit to talk to me like that when you're life is in my hands."

Solar felt sick to her stomach. She knew Jimin was crazy, but she didn't think she would ever care to go to such lengths. She didn't think she would work with whoever this person was. But the thing was, Solar didn't think a lot of things. Such as multiple people from her company working against her, working to kill her.

Did she really deserve that? Or was this karma catching up to her? Maybe all of her bad deeds were finally being paid off, she was getting what she deserved for all of the bad things she'd done. It wasn't like she could run away from them forever.

The past always follows you.

"Kill me then," she blurts, her expression growing blank as she meets Jimin's eyes again, "kill me for a guy that'll never love you. It'll only make him resent you even more."

Jimin narrows her eyes at her, irises flaming with fury. She grips the knife in her hand as it grazes Solar's neck, the temptation clear in her movement. 

"You're fucking lucky," she finally exhales, "I have to wait for the call."

Speaking of, it made no sense that she was the one who was getting to do the deed. All this work that the real culprit had done, just to not be the one who got to kill her? It seemed so pointless to her, where was the real satisfaction? Or did they just want her gone that bad that they didn't care who did it?

Still, why wouldn't they want to be there? Perhaps they were afraid of getting caught, the chances were still fairly high, even though the entire company had seemingly been cleared out. 

Except for the ones who were in on it, she presumed.

Jihoon, she hoped he and his friends were okay... and Jinhwan... he was in the studio.

That couldn't be good.

"What call?" she asks, even though she doubted she would get any kind of helpful answer, "why aren't they here to kill me themselves?"

Jimin was holding the phone in one hand and the knife against her neck in the other. Every few seconds, she would stare down at the phone before looking back to Solar. She was focused on the screen for a moment, before she sighs, "let's just say - he had a precaution to take care of first."

Huh?

Precaution.

The only one's that appeared in her brain was her siblings. They seemed to have been targeting her family, after all, they tried to kill Chaerin, right? It had to be her siblings. They were going after her siblings. 

But why?

Perhaps they were trying to save her, they must know what was going on. Would they be okay? Would they be outnumbered? 

Please be okay...

Solar could feel tears trying to fight their way to her eyes. Just the thought of losing any of her family members broke her heart. She spent years away from them, feeling like they all hated her, wanting nothing more than to see them again. Now that she was only beginning to rebuild their relationships, she couldn't handle losing any of them.

Especially not when it would be all her fault. If she never would've ran away, this would've all never had happened. They would all be safe and sound, living their reckless but fun lives. 

It was all her fault.

Solar squeezes her eyes shut, the feeling of the blade just gently piercing into her skin sending her back into a frenzy. Her mind is racing, her thoughts jumbled as they wind through the many years of her life. Why was she so afraid? She'd been through things like this before. Had her innocent life in Korea really washed her of those ways? 

She couldn't forget how she once lived. Solar had held a gun before, she'd seen people die before, she'd seen torture, agony. She'd killed someone before. For all those years, she was with Sunwoo, side by side - she saw those things happen every single day. That lifestyle, it was normal for her.

And Sunwoo, Sunwoo had taught her well. She would've never have been able to survive if she didn't know how to.

But she did.

Those ways, those instincts, they didn't just disappear. They were still in there. She forced herself to forget how it all was, how her life was, how she was. But it was still a part of her, deep down.

She never thought those things would come of use to her again.

"Hmm, seems like he might be having some trouble with that one," she's humming, sighing down at the phone screen, "he must be refusing to die, I know he's a tough one." She began to laugh to herself, maniacally. 

Jaehyun? her mind screams, her blood boiling as she heard those words fly off the girl's tongue. The fact anyone could say things like that, about her sibling.

"I'm getting impatient," she's mumbling, "I can't wait to see the life drain from you..."

What a fucking psychopath.

She had to do something before it was too late. 

It was now or never.

This couldn't be the end. Solar wasn't going to let her life end like this.

The millisecond Jimin had looked down at the phone again, her hand swings upward to grab onto her wrist. Twisting it the opposite way, the girl lets out a screech of surprise, immediately using her other hand to grab the knife as Solar had twisted her arm behind her back.

"Oh, you really are fucking bold!" she's crying in disbelief, laughing hysterically, "fine with me, Solar!" Using the other hand, she plunges the knife towards her chest.

Solar ducks, going to the side as quick as she could manage. Her breath hitches in her throat as Jimin turns, her eyes wide with hunger as she jumps towards her again. 

Just as the knife was coming towards her face, she reached out to grab her wrist.

But she missed her wrist.

The knife goes right into the middle of her hand, cutting clear through. A cry of agony leaves her lips as she cowers over for a moment, trying to regain her unsteady breathing. It hurt so fucking bad, but she couldn't let herself falter. 

She'd been literally shot before. This was okay, she was okay.

"Aw, that must've hurt."

Still bending over, she could feel Jimin preparing to probably try to knock her down. Pretending like she was in immense pain, she hurriedly uses her other hand to rip the knife out. 

Holy fucking...

Everything happened too quickly for her to comprehend. Solar wasn't thinking anymore. If you think, you lose sight, you lose focus. Don't think, only do. Follow your instincts, they're always right, only if you were taught right.

That was what Sunwoo always said.

So she didn't think. It happened so fast, she couldn't feel the pain anymore. Jimin tried to stop her by attempting to knock her down, but it didn't work. 

It didn't work because the knife went right into her chest, right were the heart was.

Because Solar didn't miss. 

She was taught right.

"Fuck!" she curses, holding a hand to her chest as she slips to the ground. As much as she was fighting, it was clear she wasn't going to make it any further. "You fucking..." she'd tried to say, but she was unable to even catch her breath.

Breathing heavily, Solar only watches her there on the ground. Coming back down to Earth, she winced as she felt the stinging in her hand. Looking down at it, the bleeding wasn't stopping. 

Making sure Jimin was really gone, she rushed to her duffle bag to pull out a change of clothes she'd brought for dance practice. She used a t-shirt to wrap it around her wound. 

As bad as it hurt, she needed to find - who she presumed it was - Jaehyun and her other siblings. 

Hopefully it wasn't too late.

Gripping the knife in her other hand, she rushed out of the studio. Though it might've been a smart idea to check on the iKON boys, she couldn't even think of them at the moment.

All she cared about was making sure her siblings were okay. 

She wasn't letting any of them die on her watch, especially not now that her adrenaline was pumping. She was ready to go, ready to fight. Just like the old days.

Solar would take on anything.



146:00 | how to kill a crime boss

(i rewrote this entire chapter so it might not fit in with the story line after, i still have to go through and edit it all to go together. for example, solar doesnt get shot in this new version but she did in the old one so its going to talk abt her getting shot)

The problem was, Solar wasn't sure where to go. 

If the staff in the building were working with them, they probably were on the lookout for her. If they saw her, there was no telling what would happen. And with one hand, she was probably an easy match for anymore than one. 

But her siblings, where would they be? If they know what's happening, they must have come to save her, which was why he was after them, right? 

So it had to be somewhere in the building, but there was no way they would've gotten in through the lobby. 

What other entrances were there?

It suddenly hit her, the only thing that would make sense. She breaks into a run towards the elevator, knowing she might not have much time. Jimin was probably supposed to send out some kind of message when she got rid of her. They were expecting it, and if they didn't get it after a long time, they would likely come to check if everything was okay. 

She couldn't get caught.

Her hand was stinging more than ever. She felt like it was bound to fall off. Solar definitely needed fucking stitches, but it would be a long while until she could get any.

Suck it up, she kept telling herself, it's not your first rodeo. 

And it sure wasn't, but pain was still pain.

She was practically smashing the elevator call button, desperate for it to get there. Holding the shirt over her wound, she pushes down on it so she bleeding would hopefully stop.

Hurry, hurry...

The elevator dings, and she jumps in practical excitement. 

That was when it opened to reveal a person.

She nearly stabbed them on sight, when their eyes widened at her. "Noona?!" the boy cries, and she'd lowered the knife back to her side, her anxiety that had built up instantly slowly fading away. "Oh my God," Jihoon gasps as he rushes out of the elevator, "are you okay?! There's so much blood, you need to get to a hospital, noona..."

He kept rambling and rambling, before she finally cut him off, "Jihoon, please, I'm okay."

"What happened?!" he's crying, eyes full of confusion and fear as he'd never seen anything like this, "did someone hurt you? The entire company seems to be in turmoil. It's like it's full of zombies."

"It's - a long story," is all she can say, "look, I need you and the other trainees to go home as fast as possible, okay? It's not safe here, please."

He only stares at her for a moment, trying to gather any kind of piece as to what was happening. "B-But you, what about you? Don't you need to get medical attention for your hand?! You can't stay here, either!"

"Jihoon," she cries loudly, placing a hand on his shoulder, "please go home. I promise I have this under control, okay? And don't tell anyone about anything you've seen today. I'll explain it to you later, but I can't right now. I really have to go."

Before he could try to stop her again, she rushes into the elevator, pressing the button to go up. The boy didn't try to, though, he let her go. Shaking his head, he was still trying to process any of what was happening. Until finally, he faces the fact he was going to have to be left in the dark. "Be safe!" he called to her as the elevator doors were closing.

Solar only sends him a small but thankful smile as the doors fully closed. 

She hoped they would get home safe, listening to the rushed footsteps of her new friend as he had ran back down the stairs to gather his friends. 

He would get home safe and uninjured. There was no reason for them to try to hurt them, after all. But Solar, Solar was another story.

As the elevator slowly goes up, she closes her eyes, leaning against the wall. 

Breathe, just breathe, she tells herself, it'll all be okay. Your siblings will be okay. 

She was trying her best to prepare herself for whatever it was to come. But no matter how much she tried and how much shit she'd been through, she couldn't feel prepared for a day like this. All Solar ever wanted was to live freely, to live without fearing being judged by her parents, to live like she'd always wanted. She wanted to make her dreams come true, and she did, by doing what she loved. And she'd met so many great people along the way, made so many amazing fans that she was so thankful for. She became the person she never thought she could be. 

Kim Soomi, was who she became. The sweet, empathetic, fearless girl who had taken over the scene of South Korea. She never expected the mistakes of Solar Lee to follow her around.

But they did, because no matter how hard she tried to make it disappear, that was who she was. That was her name, Solar.

All her life, she was always protected. Whether it was by her family and their power, or by Sunwoo, and then later by Jinhwan or Jungkook, she always had someone there in front of her to take the bullet. 

It was time for her to fight her own fight, and she already had. Whoever Jimin was working with, they had something coming for them that they wouldn't expect from her. She was human, no doubt, but that day, she felt no remorse when she'd killed that girl. 

Because she wasn't who they were making her out to be. They might've convinced her of it before, but she was tired of it. They were wrong. She wasn't guilty, she wasn't a villain, she wasn't evil. 

With that thought in mind, she grips the knife harder in her one hand. Her other hand was still stinging, the blood beginning to drip to the ground through the t-shirt she had tied around the wound. She could handle the pain, it was nothing to her, not at that point in time.

The elevator was almost to the top floor when she heard the commotion. It sounded like a bunch of things crashing and falling, likely furniture being knocked over. She tried to listen to make out any voices, but they were muffled through the walls.

"Fuck!" she's crying, banging on the elevator door, "hurry the fuck up!"

Seeming to listen to her annoyance, it had finally dung, the doors opening before her. The brunette rushes out, breathing heavily from fear as to what she was going to see. Gripping the knife, her head whips every direction in search for the incident, her senses tingling and ready to dodge any incoming attack. She could barely breathe in that moment, her entire body moving up and down rapidly. But she didn't have time to steady her breathing, she didn't even have time to think.

It wasn't like she could think straight anyway, as the sound of a gunshot rang through the floor walls. A lump forms in her throat as she could only see one thing in her head - the sight of her siblings flailing to the ground. She kept trying to figure out how it would make sense for them to even be here. Just because there was another entrance that wasn't the lobby didn't mean there wasn't some kind of security, right?

Then again, if there was good security, none of this would be happening. Plus, most of the staff were off today as part of this person's entire plan. 

She wondered just how long they had been planning it out, how deep it went. How long were they under her nose without her knowing? Was she really that stupid to not notice it? Jimin sure seemed to think so.

Jimin, she scoffed, she should've known better.

The sound of yells fill her ears as she rushes through the hall, knife held tightly in her one working hand as blood drips from the other. Her hand was still stinging like a bitch, but she had to do her best to ignore it. It was the last thing on her mind, anyway, as she turns a corner just as another bang sounds through the atmosphere.

Please don't be them, she's pleading, please don't be any of them. 

How would she ever handle losing a sibling? She wouldn't be able to, especially knowing it would be all her fault. She was the one who got them involved in all of this, she was the one who got everyone involved in all of this, starting from her beloved Jungkook, the boy she was supposed to marry. 

It needed to be put to an end. Her happiness had been prevented and destroyed for far too long, and she wasn't going to let it happen anymore. Solar was tired of always running, of always being afraid, of always cowering, Sunwoo taught her better than that. 

The yelling finally becomes more audible and closer, close enough to where she was able to pinpoint what room it was in. Her breathing becomes heavier and heavier, but she breaks into a run towards the room, knowing she might already be too late.

All she could do was pray that she wasn't.

Her sneakers skid and screech loudly against the floor, no doubt alerting the people who were there. Solar didn't care though, all she did was run faster.

"You can hide, but don't think I won't find you!" a voice she couldn't seem to recognize in that moment calls out angrily.

But when she got closer and closer, all the noise stopped. Her stomach dropped as she turned the corner, reaching the doorway, worried this meant that the deed was done and at least one side was dead. 

Stepping inside, she grips the knife as tightly as she could manage, head jerking each and everyway. There was no one there, at least that she could see. 

Surveying the tile floor of YGE, she didn't see a dead body, either.

Her siblings wouldn't hide, she was thinking in confusion. This made no sense.

Unable to put any of the pieces together, Solar is about to turn around from a sinister feeling that she feels creeping up her spine. It was dark, unlike anything she'd ever felt before. Like there was a sudden menacing aura about to engulf her.

Just as her body is beginning turn, knowing her gut was never wrong, she was too late.

A curse leaves her lips when not one, but two hands, wrap themselves around her neck, squeezing. They were squeezing and squeezing as if it was the last thing they were ever going to do. Solar chokes out, wailing as she reaches up with her one good hand, holding the knife. 

All she could think was this was it, this was them. She had no idea where her siblings had gone, but if they were possibly hurt, she hoped it was to safety. This was her own fight, after all. And she couldn't go down so easily.

This was them, the words repeat in her head, she could catch them, once and for all. It can all be over.

They didn't seem to realize she had the knife, which was lucky for her, as it gave her the chance to send it right into their hand, much like what happened to her earlier. 

"FUCK!" the person screams, very much belonging to a man. A loud wince fills the air as they had stumbled back, and she took that as the chance to turn around - or she thought, until she heard something else.

A gun cocking.

She forgot she should be cautious of that.

Still not facing them, all she could do was put her hands up. Blood is still dripping down her arm onto the ground from her one hand, and she squeezed her eyes shut as she realized her mistake. She should've tried to knee them.

Now it might be too late.

Idiot, she cursed at herself.

"You should be fucking ashamed to of just tried that," the voice hisses, "poor Solar, can't seem to do anything right." They were taunting, just like always, just like in every message they had ever sent. Always looking down on her, always making her feel like she was a joke.

She was quiet, not knowing what to say because she knew one wrong word could send a bullet through her head. 

"Like always," they snarl, "Solar Lee is worthless without any protection," they pause, before a dark chuckle leaves their lips, "well, I'd hate to tell you that your so-called protection left you here. Not that I blame them, they did take a bullet through the arm. Definitely not the fighter I thought they'd be."

He kept rambling and rambling, until it began to sink in that she knew this voice. She heard this voice before, many times every single day. She had been so shaken up and focused on her siblings that she hadn't realized who's voice it was.

"Wait..." her words are shaky as she stutters out, "wait... I-" she couldn't seem to get them out, unable to process the fact that it was really him. There was just no way, she was telling herself, how the hell would that work? She knew him well, and he wasn't that kind of person at all.

"Oh, dear," he'd snickered, enjoying her bewildered and horrified state, "finally putting the pieces together? Have to admit, I thought you were smarter than this, but what can I expect from an American?"

"W-Wait... b-but why?" she spouts, shaking her head as if she was trying to wake up from a bad dream, "that d-doesn't make any sense... I-I don't..."

She couldn't believe it was real.

For what?

What was the motive?

"It doesn't make any sense?" he repeats, voice booming. A humorous snort escapes, before he scowls, "you know what doesn't make any fucking sense, Solar?! You know what I'm never going to fucking recover from thanks to you?!"

"L-Look, I don't understand what..." she tried to say, before she was cut off by his screaming.

"THE FACT THAT MY WIFE IS DEAD!" it was so loud the entire room seemed to shake as if there was an earthquake, so loud she squeezed her eyes shut, and so loud she wasn't sure she heard him correctly.

"Wife?" she blinks, "W-What? Who's your-" she cut herself off that time.

Because it sunk in.

The woman, the woman who started it all. The one that she watched die right in front of her.

Still, Solar couldn't grasp this, and even then, it didn't make sense. "S-She was going to kill me!" she cries out, "It's not my fault she lost!"

Those were words she should have known better than to say.

"Oh, but it is your FUCKING FAULT!" he bellows, pushing the gun harder against her head, "I didn't previously care about you, Solar. I just did my job, I did what I had to. But I deemed I would help my wife because it was what she wanted," she could only stand there, hands in the air still, as she heard out his story, "I knew what you'd taken from her, but I thought what could I do about it? I promised my wife that I would help her ruin your career so she could have back what she deserved. Until she decided to take it into her own hands," he paused, exhaling deeply, before growling out, "and now she's gone, all thanks to you. I have no choice but to finish what she started."

The sound of the gun cocking sends her heart into overdrive. No... was all she could think, trying to come up with absolutely anything that she could do to get her out of this.

It was an absurd story he told, one she couldn't believe was true. But from the way that woman had acted, from the things she had said before she died, she found herself believing it. It made the puzzle pieces fit, it made everything fall into place. That woman was crazy, and she had gotten all the inside information from her own manager. He was planning to destroy her career from the inside out, which was the explanation for the scandal, the explanation for them having all of that information about her, the reason they were going to display it all at KCON on the big screen. When it all failed to take her career down, she had decided to do the next big thing - kill her.

But she'd failed at that, too.

So now her husband was going to finish the job for her, guided by rage and pain from losing his wife. He was never the kind manager she thought he was, it was all part of the plan. The plan that had now become as dark as ending her life.

And it was going to be completed, because there was nothing she could do. Like he'd said, her protection had left - as much as it seemed crazy for her siblings to leave her - she sure seemed to be alone, or they would have done something by now, right?

She'd lost.

"You're guilty, Solar Lee," he croaks, finger hovering above the trigger, "and it's your turn to pay."

Knowing there was nothing more she could do from here, all Solar did was close her eyes. She had to accept that this was it, this was how it had to be. At least now, everyone could live peacefully, right? This would complete his plan, his mission, and mean that Jungkook, Jinhwan, everyone could live without worrying about the next move, the next disaster. They could live normally, how they were supposed to.

Because all she had ever done for them was create trouble.

But no shot went off.

Solar's green eyes slowly flutter back open, and she dares herself to turn around to figure out what was happening. 

When suddenly, a clash sounds through the air as she gun had been knocked out of his hand, flying to the tiled floor. Confusion fills her brain like a virus, especially more so when an all-too-familiar mocking voice fills her ears, "oh wow, all this to your own artist for your own psychotic wife?" A dark chuckle leaves the new souls lips as they badger, "hmm, and you really thought I'd leave my precious Solar Lee? I thought you were the smart one, no?"

Kim Sunwoo's voice was like a symphony. A symphony she couldn't believe she was hearing as she lowered her hands, spinning around to face him and her manager. Sunwoo had a gun against his head now, and her manager was standing there with a rather pissed but defeated expression wiped on his face.

When Solar's shocked eyes meet his, Sunwoo only winks at her. "Surprise, princess, guess who decided to make a last minute visit?" he monotones, and she could only shake her head, still trying to process this all. Something was telling her it definitely wasn't a last minute visit.

Which meant, it hadn't been her siblings, either.

It was Sunwoo, this is confirmed when she sees the gunshot wound in his arm. Yet, he was still moving it around like nothing.

He was fucking insane.

"You're really going to save her?" her manager had scoffed, "after everything? She's practically seeing two other guys and you still care for her so much? Notice how they aren't here, it's sad you're allowing someone to use you so ruthlessly, don't act like you're much smarter, pal."

There's a small smirk on Sunwoo's lips, clearly amused by his comment and attempt at shaking his mental. "You're gonna have to try a lot harder than that if you want to get into my head," he's yawning, "and yes, they aren't here because they're both dimwit children, that's really not important. But what is, is the fact that you're going to come with me for some fun Q&A time."

She knew what that meant. Sunwoo probably wanted to turn him into the authorities, but first, he wanted more answers.

It was unlike him to not just kill on sight, but she didn't mind it. She would prefer for him to rot in jail, anyway.

"I'll be fucking damned if you think I'm going with you!" he snapped back angrily.

An impatient exhale leaves the lips of Kim Sunwoo, who still had the gun against his skull. "Look, we can do this the hard or the easy way," he deadpans, before turning back to Solar, "sweetheart, go to the car," he paused to reach into his pocket with his other hand, throwing her the keys. The thing was, this meant he was momentarily distracted. "It's a rental, you'll recognize it, wait there."

She catches the keys, glancing down at them in confusion. "Wait, but don't you want me to help?" she asks, "It's also dangerous-"

"-Princess," he breathes, his voice stern as his twinkling brown eyes pour into hers, "it's a bad time to ask me questions, I just need you to listen."

But Solar was a stubborn one.

"What? No, Sunwoo, I'm not leaving you."

The few seconds that Sunwoo had been distracted by Solar, by making sure she was safe, was enough for the man to grab his weapon. He tucks it inside the bottom of his shirt, just inside his pants, to where Sunwoo couldn't see his from his perspective. Then, he puts his hands back up as if he hadn't performed the action at all.

"Solar," he coaxes, trying his best not to get angry. But with a temper like Sunwoo, it was a struggle, "Go. Now. I can handle this on my own, I need to make sure you are safe."

Though just as Sunwoo's eyes had trailed away from her, it was too late. The man had whipped the weapon out of his pants, spinning away and out of Sunwoo's gun sight line, dipping down and holding up the weapon with both of his hands - pointing straight at the boy.

Solar's eyes bulged out of their sockets as she saw this happen. "SUNWOO!" she tries to yell, hurriedly rushing towards him from her stance across the room.

But it was too late. It'd all gone south. 

Because a bullet was sent flying straight into his chest.




147:00 | promise rings

"That's wrong," the brunette hums as she leans over to peer at the paper in the boy's lap, scooting over across the leather car seats so she could get a closer look. Her green eyes scan quickly over the rest of the answers, a sigh leaving her lips as she had finally sat back up to give the final verdict, "all but one are wrong," she announces, before a frown spreads over her lips, "Sunwoo, you have to at least try to get the work right or you're never going to graduate."

The boy had been looking out the window to peer at the highschool building. He bites his lip, pulling one of his lip rings in between his teeth for a brief moment before he lets it go. "Sol, graduating isn't important for me like it is for you," he finally says, "there's no college ahead of me, no running a business ethically. My life is only ever going to be what it is right now - killing, dirty work. I don't need to know how to do calculus."

The girl has a saddened look in her eyes as she stares at him. She knew he didn't mean any of that in a rude way, he was never rude to her. In fact, Sunwoo was depressed about it. Deep down, she knew he wanted to be able to be a normal student, probably. He craved the idea of normalcy, of being able to have no worries, of not having to do the kind of things he had to. But it wasn't his fault that he was this way. He only let those emotions show ever so slightly when he was with her, and even then, he was monotone and lifeless seeming. It was just because she knew him like the back of her hand.

"okay," she mutters, taking the paper off his lap and setting it on her own. Gripping the pencil in her hand, she squints as she hurriedly erases the wrong answers on the homework to correct them.

Sunwoo had finally looked away from the building to watch her. "Sweetheart," he breathes, starting to feel bad for the fact he was always making her do all of his schoolwork for her, "I can do it, you don't have to stress yourself." But the girl was already vigorously scribbling away at the paper, practically effortlessly. When she focused, she bit down on her lip and squinted her eyes. It was a cute gesture, and he knew she hadn't heard a word of anything he said. Knowing she was too far in her element, he only grins like an idiot as he looks away.


Never did he think he would love someone like he loved her. Never did he think he was even capable of having those kinds of feelings. With the way he was raised, he wasn't supposed to ever feel those kinds of things. But here he was, a dumb kid in high school who had already met what he knew was the love of his life. 

Someone like Solar Lee, who was known as the likely valedictorian of their graduating class, the girl who had never scored anything lower than a 98% on an exam, the girl who did every extra credit assignment even if she already had a 100 in the class. The daughter of Jaesang Lee, the owner of one of the most successful businesses in the United States, who's beloved daughter Solar was meant to take over one day as she proved to be the most "capable." 

And him, the son of Kim Sihyuk, one of the richest men in New York with a company that had suspiciously climbed to the top of the charts. Yet, no one could ever figure out why. He did all of his work dirtily, through hit men and a secret gang that was run by his son. Most of Sunwoo's time growing up was spent learning how to fight, how to not get caught, training someone like a top FBI agent would receive. But Sunwoo was only around 5 years old when he began his training. His father, an evil man who had pretended to be friend's with Solar's parents just to try to screw them over. 

It'd left the two of them banned from seeing each other. 

Yet, here they were, sitting in his brand new Audi together. Here Solar Lee was, doing things no one would ever believe her to be doing, like skipping class and helping him do his dirty work. 

All because she loved him.

"Here, done!" she cries, "hopefully this'll keep your grade at a steady B even though you flunked the exam." She was shoving the paper into his bag on the floor of the car, before sitting back up. Her eyes land on Sunwoo, who wasn't paying attention to her for once, and she notices he was intensely reading something on his phone. "What is it?" she asks in curiosity.

His eyes had narrowed at the screen, before a sigh left his lips. Cursing under his breath, he'd hurriedly started the car, foot slamming on the pedal. Solar's eyes were wide with confusion, wondering what the trouble could be this time. 

"An attack at the mansion," he deadpans, "probably the company we shut down last week, hired someone to try to get payback."

"Weird they would still have money for that," she's thinking out loud, sliding back to the passenger side of the car so she could open the glove box. She already knew how this went because she'd done it before. Sunwoo always kept his gun with him, but she only carried one when she knew she'd need it. 

Taking the weapon out of the compartment, she slides it in her bra since she didn't have pockets. Sunwoo was gripping the steering wheel with one hand, checking his phone for any updates. "Babe, can you light me a cigarette?" he asks in that sweet tone of his. He didn't usually ask her to do anything for him, but he couldn't help but do it now since he was preoccupied.

Solar nods, getting the lighter out of the middle compartment. Leaning over, her small hand reaches into the pocket of his leather jacket, pulling out the pack. She takes out not only one, but two cigarettes, lighting his first before putting it in his mouth for him. Setting his phone down on the middle seat, he takes it out with his hand, "thanks, sweetheart."

She didn't say anything since she was lighting her own. But before she took the first hit of it, she pulled something out of her bag. Popping off the top cap, a smile is on her face as she peers down at her guilty pleasure.

Pulling a few of the pills out, she dry swallows them before taking a whiff of her cigarette. Sunwoo had watched her do this, knowing well it was something she did all the time. It was nothing new for them.

This was nothing new for them at all.

sister: hey dnt let mom or dad see u when u go to sunwoos, they've been checking outside to see whats going on at his place

sister: looks pretty fkin serious too

sister: dont get shot 

sister: even tho we know sunwoo's simp ass would take the bullet before he'd ever let u die

sister: but still

She giggled down at the text, holding her phone with one hand and the cigarette in the other. Using her singular thumb, she slowly managed to send a reply, not thinking much about what her sister had said. 

sol: no worries they're both pretty blind

sol: ill b ok either way, i know how to handle myself

sol: sunwoos taught me well little sister :)

sister: yeah ok whateva u say twirp 

sister: be careful still idiot

sister: also u better have left some of that xanax for me 

sol: yeah dw

The sudden sound of gunshots and screaming had filled her ears. They had entered the Castle without a doubt, and she had hurriedly texted Chaerin that she had to go. Sunwoo had glared in the direction of the action, and she followed his glance. 

All of the commotion had to be inside the house. The attackers had likely tipped off the police to not come to the location if they were called. Multiple vans were parked outside the house and even down the street. There's a lump in her throat as Sunwoo had hurriedly parked in front of the house, taking his gun out of his pocket as he surveyed the area before getting out of the car.

"You don't have to come inside," he's calling to her, "I can tell it's bloody in there, I don't know if I'll be able to protect you, so it would be best if you stayed here."

She'd already had her gun in hand and had been opening the door with the other when he says this. A frown spreads over her lips as she watches her boyfriend already running across the yard, when he'd yelled towards her, "I love you!"

Before she could even yell back to him, he had ran inside the house to join the sounds of gunshots and screaming. 

Solar purses her lips as she stares at the front door that was now closed. Should she take the risk? There was no doubt a lot more people here trying to kill them than anything she had ever dealt with. Sunwoo had trained her to be prepared for a fight at any time and to have decent awareness to know what to watch out for, but did he train her well enough to face off against people like this? They likely had about as much training as he and the gang did.

But the big number increase in attackers meant the more likely it was for him to get killed. A life without Sunwoo was something she couldn't even imagine.

Though, it was Kim Sunwoo she was talking about. 

Solar still wanted to feel useful. She was dating a gang leader, a crime boss, she couldn't always sit around and do nothing like this. His lifestyle was a part of hers now, and she had to show that she was okay with that. 

Gripping her gun tighter, she finally prepared herself to open the door and head inside, when her phone buzzes in her pocket.

heehee: i KNOW you are not at sunwoos right now where there is a LITERAL gang fight i can see from my window

heehee: if i fkin see u SO HELP ME GOD SOLAR LEE

heehee: i will BE VERY ANGRY

heehee: u BETTER not be going in that house to meet ur literal death, i dont care if sunwoo is fucking jesus himself and will resurrect u

Heaving out a sigh, she rolls her eyes as she climbs out of the vehicle. As soon as she was walking into the front yard, her phone went off again.

heehee: SOLAR FUCKING LEE TURN THE FUCK AROUND RIGHT NOW AND GO HOME 

heehee: OHMYGOD i know you're dumb but ur NOT THIS DUMB 

heehee: LITERALLY no man is worth DYING OVER

He really was overdramatic, was all she was thinking. Turning towards Chanhee's home, she eyed his bedroom window that looked out towards the front. Knowing he could see her, she holds up a middle finger in that direction. With that, she tucks her phone in her bra, gripping the weapon in her hands as she opens the door.

Screams of agony fill her ears, with the spraying sound of guns in every which way. Though she wouldn't admit it, a part of her was nervous. From all the noise, it was clear how many attackers there were. At least a few of the gang members were likely injured or even killed. They were her friends, it would sadden her to see any of them die.

Looking around, the main room seemed to be empty as far as live suspects went. She steps over some dead bodies, making sure she didn't get any blood on her shoes, holding her gun in the air in case anyone popped out unexpectedly.

Most of the commotion was coming from the upstairs part, so she decided she was going to check the basement in hopes that was where she would find Sunwoo. He was likely doing surveillance of the whole house.

Exhaling deeply, she shuffles down the steps. 

Just as she figured, as soon as she reached the bottom of the stairs, the sounds of loud yells entered her ears. She could recognize one of them as Sunwoo, and knowing he had to be in there alone, she rushes down the hallway to the door. 

Placing her ear against it, she listens intently to see what was going on. Though it was hard to make out the voices, she was finally able to understand a few words.

"It's quite the honor to be able to kill someone who's ruined so many things I worked for..."

Her brows furrow, and she presses her ear harder against the door as if it would help anything. 

"You're a good fighter, but still no match... seems like your father didn't train you well enough, kid..."

It slowly began to sink in what was happening. Trying to keep her composure, she places her hand on the door knob, twisting it as slow as she could manage. When she could feel that the door was unlocked, she gradually pulls it open until she could squeeze her body through without making a peep. 

Thank God she was frail enough.

There, across the room, was Sunwoo in a position she'd never seen him before. He was always the one that towered over his victims, he was always the one who won. Yet, there he was, on the floor looking only slightly panicked. Her eyes scan the room, seeing the gun he had brought across the floor. Her eyes then landed on the state he was in, blood was dripping from his leg due to the fact there was a knife dug into it. 

She began to feel nauseous as the man raised the gun against his head. Still in momentary shock, she forced herself to come back to Earth.

Without another thought, she raises her own weapon, finger pressing down on the trigger. A loud shot went off as the bullet cut straight through his chest. He didn't even get a chance to turn and see she was the culprit before he fell to the ground, a pile of blood quickly gathering under his now dead body.

Sunwoo, who had previously had his eyes closed as he was angrily cursing out the man that was about to shoot him, hadn't known she was there. There's a hint of surprise on that usually emotionless face of his, before a cocky smirk slowly spread across his lips as the girl had rushed over to him, kneeling over to make sure he was okay.

"Well, look at you," he teased, "stepping up and doing the dirty work all by yourself."

The brunette rolls her eyes at the fact he could be so calm, "idiot, I just saved your life."

His blank expression didn't change. "Yeah, something like that," he suddenly winced, before motioning to his leg, "sweetheart, mind pulling that out for me?"

Her green eyes widen a bit as she realizes what he was asking of her. Looking to his poor leg, she winced at the horrible sight. Her hand hovers over it, gently grabbing onto the knife so she doesn't move it anymore and hurt him, nervously sputtering, "i-isn't this going to make it bleed worse? Shouldn't I go get Sangyeon first?"

"Princess," his voice was somehow calm, "pull it out."

She couldn't understand how he wasn't screaming at the top of his lungs. Solar couldn't imagine what it felt like to have a weapon lodged into your leg, yet here he was, as calm as ever as if he couldn't feel a thing. Sunwoo only showed anger when he was on the verge of death, but that was the only emotion. He was crazy.

"okay..." she's muttering, closing her eyes as she firmly grabs onto the knife, ripping it out of his leg as quick as she could. 

A curse leaves his lips as he definitely felt that intense pain, and he squeezed his eyes closed at how horrible it was. Opening her own eyes, she gasped at the sight of the blood pouring out even faster, just like she thought.

"Hey," he's motioning across the floor, "I need you to wrap that shirt around my leg, okay? As tight as you can, sweetheart, and tie it."

She nods, not questioning it this time. Getting up, she rushes across the room to grab the random shirt. Back by his side in a second, Solar slowly lifted his leg up so she could place the shirt under it, before wrapping it around the wound and tightening it as hard as she could.

He winced loudly as she finished, and her eyes looked at his face in worry. "Are you okay?" she's asking, holding onto his arm in concern, "do you feel lightheaded? You've lost a good amount of blood. Should I go get Sangyeon? I should, right? You need stitches... and it's probably infected..." She was rambling, so much that Sunwoo could only stare at her with a humorous look on his face. It was something she did a lot when she was nervous, and he was more than used to it.

Shifting on his side, he reaches an arm up to graze her face with his thumb. "Sol," he whispers sweetly to calm her nerves, "it's okay, stop talking."

The brunette pouts at his command, and is about to go on an entire nother rant, when she's cut off by a slight ping of something small hitting the ground beneath her. She looks down in confusion, not noticing the shocked look on Sunwoo's face as she notices something fell out of his pocket.

Before he could, she reaches out and grabs it. Solar's brows knit together as she holds it up in front of her face in the light, seemingly forgetting about everything that just happened. "What is - oh my God..." she cuts herself off, her eyes sparkling as she looks back to her boyfriend, "is this what I think it is?"

Sunwoo sighs at the fact he did not want that to happen, but shrugs, "yeah, will you marry me, Solar?" he hums sarcastically, causing the girl to shake her head in annoyance.

"I didn't think you would ever do anything like this," she's mumbling, staring down at the ring in her hand. It was gold, clearly real, with small diamonds surrounding a big diamond in the middle. A design was traced around it, placing the diamonds in a small cocoon of gold. "You're so sweet," she's gushing, "I love it."

The boy is cringing from her words, not wanting to be called anything like cute. He got them promise rings not to be cute, but to show how important their relationship was to him - to show he never wanted anyone else and he promised that.

Before anything more could be said, the basement door came bursting open. Sunwoo's reflexes kick in immediately, and he grabs the gun that was in Solar's lap, aiming it straight at the door. The sight of the other gang members calms his nerves, and he exhales deeply, setting the weapon back down.

Solar had slipped the ring on her finger before looking up to the boys that were rushing towards them. "Oh fuck, what the hell happened here?" Hyunjae is the first one to speak as they walk up to the two.

Peering up at the boys, only then did she notice the shape they were in. Not all of them were here, meaning that some of them were probably hurt and in recovery. You could tell the fight was over, due to the fact they were all covered in blood, bruises, clothes dirty, and exhausted expressions on their faces. Yet, there was a sense of peace on their faces, because they did this all the time.

"I never thought our big bold leader would lose in a fight," Eric is giggling, earning him a disapproving look from Younghoon.

"Here, his wound looks bad," Sangyeon is muttering, having already looked at the stab on his leg, "you need some serious stitches, Sunwoo."

"I realize," he's sighing, "let's just get it over with."

A couple hours later, after Sunwoo's leg wound had been cleaned and patched up, and Sunwoo had informed his members that she had killed the guy today, joking that she was really becoming "an official gang member," you'd find the two in his bedroom. It was around 10 PM, which was Solar's weekday curfew. Of course, she was clearly breaking it, which was something she didn't try to do - it just happened. 

Luckily, she had an easy plan that she used everytime this happened. The simple "I'm at Chanhee's" text, complete with a picture she'd ask him to send her that made it look like she was with him.

solsol: send me a pic that makes it look like im with u

heehee: bitch what the fuck??? u ghost me for all these hours after i WATCH ur ass ignore my texts and go into a LITERAL GANG FIGHT 

heehee: u are LUCKY UR EVEN ALIVE u stupid BITCH 

heehee: god i fucking hate u all u do is worry me 

heehee: if this is what having kids feels like i dont fucking want them

solsol: im sorry! i was worried about sunwoo

solsol: i couldnt let anything happen to him

heehee: solar he is A FUCKING GANG BOSS u dont have to worry about him he should worry about U

solsol: i know but it goes both ways! 

solsol: im perfectly fine, hee, i actually DID save sunwoos life

heehee: i dont even want to fucking know even tho i already do

heehee: have u even studied for midterms? and i know u skipped class today

solsol: YES i mean i dont need to study its all engraved in my brain, its fine

solsol: and i had to help sunwoo, the teacher doesnt care she loves me

heehee: solar... u cant be doing all this sht for sunwoo

heehee: srsly its not ok

heehee: the shit he does is not normal, u cant become accustomed to that

heehee: and u KNOW i love u but i cant keep covering for u and helping u lie to ur parents

solsol: okok i get it

solsol: im not used to the kind of life he lives or anything

solsol: but im not gonna break up with him and i know thats what u want

solsol: just dont worry abt it hee, everything is fine

solsol: ill stop staying over at his house tho, this is the last time i need u to cover for me, promiseee

heehee: ugh ok fine

heehee: ur still so fucking lucky

solsol: love u best friend

It indeed wouldn't be the last time, but she knew she could always convince Hee to help her out. He sends her the picture within a few minutes, and she hurriedly had sent it to her mother attached with a text explaining she was at his house for the night. 

A sigh leaves her lips as she was about to set her phone down when she saw a bunch of texts from her siblings. They all were aware of her relationship with Sunwoo since they were so close, and they were all obviously aware of the gang fight that was going on. She replied to them in their group chat, letting them know she was okay and that Sunwoo was hurt so she was going to take care of him. 

With that, she was finally able to set her phone down on the nightstand, allowing her to pay attention to Sunwoo. 

His eyes were already on her, piercing into her soul, which shocked her as soon as she looked to him. "Have you been staring at me the entire time?" she's asking, and as soon as she words leave her mouth, he'd sighed, shifting his eyes elsewhere. Solar is confused as she only scoots closer to him on the bed, about to speak again when she notices the happiness that was taking over his expression. There was a glint in his eyes, and a smile tinting the base of his lips. 

It was a weird thing to see from him. Even though she was used to every aspect of Kim Sunwoo, Solar could never be used to seeing him happy. Most of the time, they were always around other people, and Sunwoo hated to show emotion around anyone that wasn't her. 

"I'm thankful for you, princess," he finally breathes out, the rasp in his voice shaking her ears, "I never thought the beloved Solar Lee would be so accustomed to this life, but you did great today."

Even weirder, he was complimenting her on her actions. She didn't think he would say another thing about it. 

And the promise rings? Sunwoo was softer than usual.

She loved it.

"I wouldn't say I'm accustomed to it," she hesitates with her words, thinking carefully. She could feel his eyes burning holes into her again, "but it's what it takes to be with you, so of course I'll be willing to try."

Even though they'd known each other so long, even though they'd already been together for a long while, she still sounded as nervous as ever. Sunwoo chuckles at her, reaching out to grab her hand. "You're precious."

Wrinkling her nose, she reaches up a finger to poke his cheek, "well, I wasn't going to let you die. It's you and me forever, Sunwoo," she informs him matter-of-factly. Green eyes sparkling, she looks down at their hands, seeing the golden promise rings they wore glistening in the light. 

She knew it then, it was them forever. Solar didn't have a doubt in her mind, and she didn't care what happened. 

You and me forever.



148:00 | the harder they fall

Tense. 

That was the only thing anyone in the apartment living room could feel.

It was dead silent, everyone sitting or standing. No one spoke a word, they were too busy letting their minds go rampant. 

Until Chanhee had sighed, rising from his seat and immediately exclaiming, "have you heard from Sunwoo yet?! It's been quite a fucking while!" He was as stressed as he had ever been in his life, and that was saying something. Most of the stress he'd felt throughout his lifetime was thanks to Solar Lee. His best friend that he watched grow up, that he watched get involved in one of the most dangerous lifestyles anyone could have. He watched her risk her life every day for Sunwoo. And then one day, she disappeared. He swore he lost her forever, and it had stressed him out constantly for all of those years. 

He'd finally gotten his best friend back. His idiotic, stupid best friend that brought him nothing more than worry but yet he loved her dearly. The last thing he was going to do was let some idiot like Sunwoo, who had always ruined her life, fail to protect her. 

Jaehyun had been tapping his foot on the wood floor, his lips a straight line due to concentration as he narrowed his eyes at his phone. Finally, he sighs, "nope, nothing, not a word."

The others grew even more concerned at this. Chaerin jumps to her feet, too, picking up her bag and taking a few steps towards the front door. "That's enough waiting, we need to get the fuck over there before it's too late," she's snapping, "I'm not losing my sister again."

Realizing she was right, Jaehyun let out a deep breath as he shoved his phone in his pocket. Picking up his gun off the coffee table, he follows Chaerin to the door, announcing, "she's right, we need to get over there and figure out what's going on."

The others get up to follow them out the door. Kevin had spoken in confusion from the back, "how are we going to get in? Entertainment companies have a lot of security, they don't just let a bunch of random people inside."

No one really knew how to answer that. 

"Wait..." Changmin had muttered, standing in front of the TV he had just turned on. His eyes are wide with shock as he turns to the others, "uh, you might want to watch this."

Everyone crowded around the TV to see what he was talking about. There, on one of the biggest news channels, was YG Ent. It looked like it was in shambles, with people rushing out of the building, horrified expressions on their faces. The speaker was informing everyone to steer clear of the building, that it was being "robbed" by unknown suspects. 

"We're unaware of who might be trapped inside the building and what casualties have been caused. Police were killed trying to break past the thugs, and are currently working on a way to break through. Please stay tuned . . ." The news speaker's voice faded into the background as Changmin had turned the volume back down.

"Fuck!" Jaehyun curses, running towards the door, his keys already jingling in his hands, "we need to go, now!"


+


But they were too late to stop the chaos that had long ensued. 

"SUNWOO?!" Solar screams, her voice so loud the walls shook, piercing through the ears of anyone that heard it. She tried to comprehend what just happened as she saw his already weakened body flail to the ground, she tried to make it make sense in her mind. He was here, Sunwoo was here - the entire time? He saved her, he saved her life. It wasn't her siblings they had been after, it was Sunwoo.

And now Sunwoo is dead.

"NO!" she's yelling, her eyes glass as she peers up to the culprit. However, there was no one there anymore, at least not in the same place. They had either made a run for it or they were hiding, "you fucking piece of shit, show your fucking face!" she's crying, turning around the room in circles, body trembling with horror. 

She could barely think straight, so much so that she couldn't make out the person standing outside the broken door, down the hallway, with a gun aimed straight at her. Her eyesight was blurry from tears, her mind racing, she couldn't think, she couldn't do anything.

All she could do as the next gunshot went off - was take it. 

But before it could hit her straight in the heart, a hand reached out, grabbing her ankle and pulling her so she fell to the side. The bullet still hit her, but only in her forearm.

"Sunwoo..." She's slowly trying to regain her composure, realizing what just happened. A cry of pain leaves her lips as she holds her aim, squeezing her eyes shut to take it in as the familiar feeling of being shot settles in. She'd been through this before, and she wasn't going to pass out this time - it was only her arm. Just her arm, she was fine.

With every ounce of strength, she grabs the gun that was across the floor. Tears are streaming down her face, and she couldn't make out where they exactly even were. All Solar could do was shoot in the direction the shot came from, she shot around 5 bullets in the same area. The bullets hit furniture and glass, knocking things over and creating endless sounds of destruction. The shots were booming in the now quiet floor of the company, sounding as if they were bombs bouncing off the walls.

"LEAVE!" she's screaming, "YOU CAN LEAVE, BUT DON'T THINK THIS IS OVER!"

But wasn't it?

She didn't want to go any further without Sunwoo. She couldn't handle the thought of her being the reason he was gone.

When she was sure they had run for it, she hurriedly kneels on the ground. Her breathing is heavy and unsteady, hands shaking with fear as she slaps his cheek a few times, before trying to check his pulse. "Sunwoo, Sunwoo, hey, stay with me... please, can you hear me?" her voice breaks, and she chokes on her own sobs, "Sunwoo, hey, don't leave... you can't leave, you can't go because of me..." the only sounds in the air became her heartfelt sobs as she grabbed onto his arm, "hey! You weren't supposed to save me, I told you I didn't need you, Sunwoo!"

He couldn't leave... not now. It was all her fault. The words kept repeating in her head. Not knowing what else to do, she prepares to try CPR. Solar didn't even know if she felt a pulse, but she was going to do anything she could think of. 

When a weak voice spoke, cutting through the air and her heart, "you'll always need me, Solar Lee," he managed to taunt her even when he was dying. Sunwoo coughed a bit, struggling to breathe, but opened his eyes to peer into hers. They were the same as they'd always been when they looked at her, sparkling with love. He'd loved her since they were kids, and it had never once faltered. You could tell it was hard for him, but a small smile had managed to spread across his lips as he muttered out, "it's you and me forever, remember, sweetheart?"

The memory hits her like a truck. Squeezing her eyes shut, tears roll down her puffy face anyway, as vivid images of their lives together flash through her brain. Before she left New York, that was all her life ever was - was Sunwoo. Even though it could never have been the same as it was, she would always love him deep down. A part of her would always need him. He would always be separate from Jinhwan and Jungkook. Her and Sunwoo were different, they were special in a way no one else could ever compare.

"No..." she's crying, another throaty sob escaping her mouth, "there's no you and me if you're dead... stay with me, please..." she didn't know if she was begging him or whatever Gods might be watching over them. All she knew was she was desperate, unstable, horrified. She couldn't even begin to think straight. 

Solar grabs onto his hand, begging over and over for him to stay with her. She kept saying they would make it through, that it would all be okay, and it was more to herself than him.

Gripping onto his hand, she suddenly felt something on his finger. Her green eyes were still glass, her vision blurry as she glanced down, but she knew what it was just by the feeling. The promise ring.

It only made her cry harder, the fact he was still wearing it after all these years and she hadn't noticed until now.

"I love you, Solar Lee."

His voice had managed to whisper, causing her eyes to jolt back to his face. He was losing the fight, she realized as his eyes began to close again. Her heart was bleeding onto the floor as she sat up straight, already ready to give him CPR again. "Sunwoo, Sunwoo!" she's yelling out, shaking his arm as if it would help anything, "hey, Sunwoo, don't go, remember what I told you? I can't finish this by myself..." but his eyes just kept fluttering closed, "SUNWOO! SUNWOO, HEY!" she starts screaming at the top of her lungs, her voice breaking with each word, "SUNWOO, YOU CAN'T GO!"

The poor girl hadn't even heard the sound of a group of people entering the room. All she could hear was her own screams of agony as she repeatedly cried out to him, "SUNWOO, DON'T LEAVE, HEY - SUNWOO!"

It all felt so hopeless.

Suddenly, she was pulled back by two people. Solar immediately fought their strength, kicking and screaming like a psychopath, "NO, SUNWOO... SUNWOO!" She was crying out like a lost puppy.
