She didn't even care to look at who was pulling her back and away from him, she just kept screaming her head off even though it wasn't going to do anything.

"Solar... Solar, SOLAR!" The voice of Chanhee enters her ears, his hand slapping her cheek as he tries to pull her back into reality, "Solar, listen to me, you need to calm down. Come back to Earth."

"It'll be okay, Solar," Changmin, the other one who had grabbed her, was trying to assure her.

All she could do was sob. Chanhee had exhaled deeply, pulling his best friend into a tight hug as she cried her heart out into his shirt. The others were busy picking up Sunwoo and rushing him to the elevator to get him to the hospital as fast as possible.

"He's gone!" she's screeching, "I really lost him this time... he's fucking gone…"

"Solar, you need to calm down, okay? You've been through a lot in the last few hours," Chanhee is telling her sternly, pulling out of the hug to put his hands on her shoulders, forcing her to look at him, "we don't know if Sunwoo is really gone, alright? Your brothers are rushing him to the hospital as fast as they can, and Jacob is using what he's learned in medical school to try to help on the way."

She was still breathing heavily, her face dripping with sweat, her eyes puffy and red. Chanhee had rubbed her forearm, when he felt something in particular that he hadn't noticed before with all the commotion. She'd covered it up with a cloth, and before she could stop him, he removed it.

"Fucking hell, Solar," he curses, "I didn't know you got hit, we need to get you to the hospital, too."

But she only shakes her head. "I-I'm fine, it doesn't matter," she sighs, turning away from him so he would hopefully let it go. "All that matters is Sunwoo," she states, using the back of her hand to wipe her eyes so she could see better, when a certain thought rushes into her mind, "wait," she turns back to Chanhee with wide eyes, "Jinhwan... did you see Jinhwan on your way here?"

She couldn't text or call him thanks to the fact she had no idea where her phone had gone. Chanhee blinks a few times at her, opening his mouth to speak. Kevin, who was standing beside him with Changmin, clears his throat hesitantly, "Sol, I think we should really take you to the hospital so you can get your wound checked out."

But Solar hadn't even heard a word he said due to the fact she was already rushing down the stairs, ignoring the calls of her friends behind her.

"Solar!" Chanhee is yelling in annoyance, "I know you're all mixed up right now, but you need to please think rationally! Your siblings took care of pretty much everyone, but that doesn't mean there isn't still some left, and you're already hurt!"

His words only made her rush down the stairs faster.

The floor their studios on was a few floors down. It was the fastest she had ever gone down steps in her life. Her arm felt like it was going to fall off, but she was sure the bleeding had mostly calmed down. Even then, she was starting to feel lightheaded, but she didn't care. How much blood she lost was minimal compared to the fact her other friends could be in danger. She couldn't afford to lose anyone else.

So even though her legs felt like they were giving out, she kept running down the stairs.

Coughing from how out of breath she was, she finally managed to reach the right floor. Taking out the gun she tucked in her bra, she holds it out as she runs towards the iKON studio.

"Jinhwan-ah!" she's yelling, "June-ya?!"

She nearly passed out as soon as she reached the studio. The door was wide open, and inside, was no one - except for a dead body. Examining it, she thankfully saw it wasn't one of the boys, but a producer she recognized due to having worked with them many times.

At the realization as to why that producer was now dead, she felt sick to her stomach. Turning back towards the hallway, she could only wonder where the guys were now. Surely, they'd made it home safely, then, and out of the chaos.

Stepping out into the hallway, her vision began to become a random assortment of black spots. Solar groans in frustration, shaking her head vigorously to attempt to make it go away. Doing this only made it worse, though, and she could feel her body growing weaker.

She'd lost a lot of blood. There was no telling how long her wound had been bleeding out.

I hope they're okay, I can't lose anyone else, was all she could think. She wished she could go back, go back and do it all over again, prevent any of this from happening. Maybe then she could save Sunwoo, she could save everyone. 

Solar always ended up being at fault for the bad things in her life, and this was the one that would destroy her the most.

"Soo?" a familiar voice broke through her demonic thoughts, "Soomi!" She heard heavy footsteps behind her somewhere, but she didn't have the strength to turn around.

Instead, she fell to the ground, and was lucky that they'd gotten there quickly enough to catch her as she was doing so. "Soomi?!" he calls out to her gently, eyes wide with concern. The caramel boy was a sweaty mess, his own heart rate higher than it'd ever been after everything that had just happened. His eyes bolted around in search of anyone, but there was nothing, at least not on this floor. Carefully, he picks her up fully in his arms, preparing to find a safer place to be - or even get out of here. He had no idea what Soomi could have possibly been through or who else was involved, but the fact she wasn't conscious clearly meant something bad.

That was when he saw her arm. 

Before he could even process, heavy footsteps are heard rushing towards him. The boy looks up, afraid of the worst, but quickly calms his nerves when he sees Chanhee and the others.

"Jinhwan?!" Kevin is crying in surprise, "you're okay, Solar was worried about you."

He only nods, figuring there was not enough time to even begin to tell them what happened with him and the others. Instead, Chanhee and Chaerin had stepped forward to inspect Solar. A heavy sigh leaves Hee's mouth as he shakes his head in annoyance, "I told her not to go, but of course she didn't listen!" 

"Has she ever listened to you?" Chaerin retorts to him, rolling her eyes, before looking back to Jinhwan. She still wasn't a big fan of him, but she was glad that he had found her. "Can you help us bring her to the hospital?" She was more telling than asking, but Jinhwan nodded in eager agreement anyway.

Before they could all make their way to the elevator, screaming had filled their ears. It wasn't screams of agony or pain, but screams coming from none other than what they should've expected - police.

"Yeah, okay, I think it's time to go," Changmin is the first to say.

"Upstairs, I saw an emergency exit!" Chaerin tells them quickly, and Jinhwan speaks up to inform them that that is indeed correct.

Without another word, they run as fast as they can up the steps, hoping to not get caught by the police. Even though they didn't technically do anything wrong in their minds, all of them had participated in multiple crimes today, and none were about to get caught. It was a massive felony to even have a gun in Korea, after all.

Yet, the majority were used to this feeling. Even Hee and the other friends had been through it, they'd known Solar her entire life. This wasn't the first time they had seen her in this condition, nor saved her. (Though it was the first time they'd ever seen Sunwoo in such a dire condition.) And surely, it wouldn't be the last time they pulled Solar out of a tough spot.

Jinhwan, on the other hand, was not used to this. He wasn't sure how anyone could be. All he could do was grip onto the girl in his arms as tightly as possible, hoping they would find a way through this.

Their story only ever seemed to get harder, after all.



149:00 | waiting rooms

Jinhwan's head hurt. 

He felt nauseous as he sat there, foot tapping vigorously on the tiled floor of the hospital waiting room. Jacob, Changmin, Kevin, and Juyeon were sitting to his left. Junhoe was on his right, being the only one who had the mental energy to come to the hospital after what happened. The other guys were back at the apartment, despite being worried for their friend, they were all shaken up. The rest of the siblings and Chanhee were dealing with the doctors, trying to get an update on either Soomi or Sunwoo's conditions. They weren't allowed any visitors yet, which was why he was left to sit there hopelessly.

On the way to the hospital, he'd informed them all of what he and the guys had experienced - or at least the bits he could remember. It all felt rather blurry in his head, like he was remembering pieces of a weird twisted dream. That was what all of this felt like. It was becoming harder and harder for him to grasp that any of this was their reality.

With a sigh, he pulls his hat further over his eyes, probably looking like a complete weirdo. Both him and Junhoe were wearing hats and masks to cover the majority of their faces so no one would recognize them. The last thing they needed was any media attention. After all, YG Ent was getting the most attention in the entire country as of right now. On every media page, all you saw was 'YG Ent attacked, entire building in chaos. Police searching for culprits. At least 10 were found dead.' All the articles were raving about the dead bodies of the ones who were helping whoever had started this all, including the one he had killed. 

He hoped maybe the authorities could get to the bottom of this disaster now that the stalker had done serious damage to the company itself. Surely, he'd be arrested just for that, just for being the reason that all of these people were killed under him. 

But he could only hope.

"I'm going to go insane," Kevin mutters, burying his face in his hands, "this is all too crazy for me."

No one really said anything. Jinhwan could feel eyes peering into him, though he didn't bother to look, until Changmin had spoken up sweetly from beside him, "hey, Jinhwan, don't worry. Solar is going to be fine, as soon as her blood is back up and they remove the bullet, she'll be in peak condition. It's really not her first rodeo, you know that."

 A sigh leaves his lips as he nods, his voice a mumble when he responds, "yeah, I'm just concerned about what this means for the company, for me, for Soomi. . . like, where do we go from here?" he's shaking his head, "The company probably lost at least a quarter of staff that were all actually working under him, all planning to do this. Soomi was supposed to be dead, but he got Sunwoo instead. This can't be the last thing he's going to do. . ."

"We have to be more prepared," Junhoe speaks up from beside him, placing a hand on his shoulder and patting it a few times, "those staffs are gone now, meaning he's also all alone now, right?"

"Yeah, that's true," Jacob is trying to assure them, "he's definitely not going to try to do anything at the company after all of this media attention. I'm sure YG is going to hire a lot more security now, too."

"We'll all figure out a plan together," Changmin says, "as soon as we find out how Sunwoo and Solar are."

They all make sounds of agreement, trying to use that small amount of encouragement from each other as motivation. Everything would be okay, and they had to promise each other that. 

Despite the words of assurance, Jinhwan still felt sick to his stomach. He kept thinking about what he could have done to prevent this, if he didn't do enough, if something was his fault. What was the next move, did he already have it planned out? There was so much he wanted to know, so many things that were important. If this person had possibly killed one of the biggest crime bosses in the United States, what the hell did that mean for them?

His thoughts were cut off by a voice, and it wasn't one he wanted to hear today - or anytime soon.

"Is she okay?! Have you heard anything?!"

Junhoe had swallowed a lump in his throat when he saw the boy and some of the others enter. It wasn't because he himself disliked them, but he could already feel Jinhwan burning beside him. Not knowing what else to do, he grabbed the edge of Jinhwan's sleeve in case he tried to get up and cause a scene. 

"Oh, we didn't know you'd be here!" Changmin is the first to say, getting up with the other friends to greet him and the others, "We haven't heard anything yet, Chanhee and the others are still talking to the doctors. She'll be okay, though, she's a strong one."

The boy had grinned lightly from his words, "that she is," he agrees. Jimin, who was the other one with him, had waved and bowed to everyone, before the two of them had taken seats right across from everyone else. 

Jungkook's eyes had slowly fallen on Jinhwan, burning holes into him. As hard as he was trying to fight in, he'd looked up to meet his dark gaze, his own being just as hateful. 

"Aish," Jungkook had finally shook his head in disappointment when Jinhwan kept glaring at him, "at least let your grudge go for now, we're both here to make sure Soomi's okay, no?"

Everyone's eyes had widened at the sudden confrontation, no one knowing if they should say something or not. Jimin had leaned over in his seat to whisper something to Jungkook, likely telling him not to start anything at such a time. But the boy only shrugged it off, instead only raising a brow at the caramel boy.

Keeping his composure, Jinhwan rolls his eyes. "Don't act like you don't always have an ulterior motive."

Jungkook's expression remained unphased from his ridiculous comment. Instead, he retorts, "don't act like you're the only one allowed to care about her."

Yikes.

He was at a loss for words.

"Anyway, what do you guys think Sol's going to want to eat?!" Jacob had spoken up in that angelic, bright tone of his, trying his best to ease the tension in the area, "I know she'll be hungry!"

"Probably McDonalds," Changmin is the first to say.

"Oh yeah," Kevin nods in agreement, "without a doubt."

"Ugh, remember when she made us go there every morning before school?" Kevin goes on to reminisce, causing the other old friends to laugh as they cry out how they felt like it had been yesterday.

Their energy was so vibrant despite the horrible time that they were all in. Obviously, Jungkook and Jinhwan didn't know her old friends as much as they wished they did, but it was odd to them how positive they managed to be. Though they understood that they just had that much faith in her, they truly believed she would be perfectly fine, which was fair - Solar Lee wasn't going to go out thanks to one bullet shot in something as small as her arm. 

Though the thing none of them seemed to be talking about was Sunwoo. Would he be able to make it out? That was really the question at hand that not a single one of them had bothered to ask, either because they didn't care or they were too unsure of the answer.

None of them had noticed the new soul that had entered the hospital. With a bucket hat loosely on his head, his hair falling in his face, the boy's brown eyes scan the hospital waiting room, seemingly in search for someone in particular. He pursed his lips in wonder, looking down at his phone quickly before glancing up again. He was clearly feeling a little lost.

The entire world seemed to stop when all of the friends saw Chaerin walking towards them from the hall. Her dark hair was flying behind her from the speed, and most of them rise to their feet, eyes wide with hope as they await whatever news she must have.

As soon as she reaches them, a sigh leaves her lips. The poor girl looked exhausted from everything, and she finally clears her throat, crossing her arms over her chest as she informs, "well, she's fine, definitely awake - that's for sure," her eyes land on the boy she didn't know would be here, and she sends him a nod as a greeting, "oh, Jungkook, Jimin, I didn't know you'd be here, too. She'll be thrilled to see you, you know, if she wasn't having a mental breakdown."

Some of them exchange glances of worry. "Sunwoo?" Changmin asks knowingly, raising a brow.

The sister only nods, a groan leaving her lips, "I know it's hitting her hard. Hell, no offense to you two," she looks at Jinhwan and Jungkook, "but I never saw Solar with anyone that wasn't Sunwoo. It's like they were destined for each other or something," a dull sigh leaves her lips as she looks to the ceiling, "they were always all over each other ever since they met. I know she's in a lot of emotional pain - but that doesn't mean it doesn't make me angry," she finishes, her usual personality resurfacing. 

"It's a lot for her," Jacob is frowning, "the last thing she ever expected was to lose Sunwoo in all this. Think about it, Solar had no idea he was even here."

"Yeah..." Chae mutters, "she's allowed visitors, and she'll be discharged by morning. She definitely needs any support you guys can give her, she'll be happy to see all of you."

After telling everyone Solar's room number, some of them head to her room as fast as they could manage. The only one that had hung back was Jinhwan, who was still wondering something he figured she could answer. 

"Chaerin," he's calling to her just as she was about to turn and walk with everybody else to Solar's room. The girl turns to him, a look of annoyance and confusion written on her face, yet she raises a brow as if to ask what it was that he needed. Feeling rather awkward about what he was asking, he sputtered out the question, "uh, how is Sunwoo doing?"

She definitely didn't think he would ask that, it caught her off guard. Her mouth opens, before closing, as her brain tries to pull together the right words.

"Ah..." she hesitates, her lips forming a straight line of contemplation, "I don't. . . really know. He'd gotten a concussion before he got shot, they said he might have brain damage, but they can't do a screening for it yet because they have to keep him alive. He lost a lot of blood, so they're trying to get it back up, but he needs a lot of stitches, and you know - the bullet removed," she's explaining, "he's been in surgery for a while. They had to immediately perform it before getting his blood levels back up because if they didn't he'd be for sure dead."

Jinhwan was at a loss for words, all he could do was shake his head in awe. 

Seeing his expression, she nods in agreement at how he was feeling, "yeah, I know. I never thought I'd see the day karma caught up to Kim Sunwoo," she exhales, "yet, it's not as rewarding as I thought it would feel. It's not that it's affecting me, but the way it's tearing Solar apart is just…” There are tears in her eyes before she can stop them, and a few manage to roll down her cheek.

Jinhwan never thought he would see the hard headed Chaerin cry. But there she was, tears beginning to stream down her face as she heaved out a frustrated sigh. 

Not knowing what to do at that moment, he's about to pull her in for a small hug, when someone had awkwardly cleared their throat behind them. Chaerin had quickly regained her composure, wiping her tears away with the back of her hand before turning to the person. There was a harsh expression on her face, as always, and it almost worried the poor boy once she was facing him.

Despite her appearance, he bows, kindly offering, "Chaerin, right? I'm Jihoon, we spoke on the phone about Soomi-sunbaenim."

The sassy girl had been staring down at him, about to speak, when she realized what he had called her. One thing about their family was despite them being Korean, they knew little about Korean culture and formalities. The Lee family was about as Americanized as one could be, and she had no idea what she had just heard because of that, causing her to spurt out, "sorry, but what..." she cuts herself off, realizing it wasn't worth the time, "right, you can just call her Solar - or Sol, no need for the formalities, I promise she won't mind."

He's extremely surprised by her words, as he had never expected or heard of anything like this. Nonetheless, he nods in agreement, not wanting to argue with her because he could tell she already likely had a temper, "ah, no problem. How is she doing?"

"She's fine, very shaken up," she tells him, keeping it short. Chaerin had turned to Jinhwan, who had been pretty hidden behind her. Jihoon's eyes landed on him, not having noticed he was even there, and as soon as they made eye contact, his mouth fell open in surprise. 

He'd bowed as a polite greeting a few times, and Jinhwan finally had recognized him. He was a trainee at YG, he was sitting there the entire time wondering who he was and how Soomi would know him - she must have met him recently at the company.

"Nice to meet you, Jihoon, I'm Jinhwan," he offers to him, bowing in return.

The boy had nervously returned his greeting, introducing himself and saying he was a big fan, before Chaerin had gone back to updating him. She exchanged a look with Jinhwan, as if mentally asking him if he thought it would be safe for Jihoon to know anything. Neither of them really knew anything about Jihoon, or if he and Solar were even close. It would probably be pretty bad if they just spouted out every detail of everything that was happening, but at the same time, he had already witnessed a fairly good bit of it. 

"She's allowed to have visitors, I'm sure she would be glad to see you," she had told him instead, "beware, though, she's a bit out of it. But whenever she's feeling better, I'm sure she'll tell you more about what's going on, it's not really our place to.

Jihoon only nods in understanding, saying it was no big deal at all. He really just wanted to make sure she was okay. After everything he saw at the company, and seeing the state that Soomi had been in, he was concerned for her safety. He was honored to have become a friend of hers, and what kind of friend would he be if he didn't come to check on her?

Or maybe he was already overstepping his boundaries. They hadn't know each other very long, and he didn't want to seem like a weirdo or that he was some trainee obsessed with her or trying to use her to climb the ranks. It wasn't like that at all. He couldn't help but feel self conscious, along with the fact he was among Jinhwan of iKON, another celebrity. As they walk towards the hallway to her hospital room, he could only worry about what other people he might encounter. 

He felt so out of place already. Jihoon just hoped Solar wouldn't mind that he was here as he'd trailed behind Chaerin and Jinhwan, who were engaged in a conversation. He didn't want to be rude and eavesdrop, but he couldn't help it.

"What do you think will happen if Sunwoo doesn't make it?"

"I'm not sure I want to find that out, honestly," she'd sighed, "I don't think Solar could handle it, she's a mess - we're all a mess. We know damn well that we need Sunwoo, now more than ever, losing him would be really bad for all of us."

Sunwoo? He wondered who that could be. Whoever it was, it sounded like they must have been injured in the chaos, too. How many people were injured, exactly?

And what the hell did they mean they needed him?

There's an uneasy feeling in the pit of his stomach. 

It sounded like they all knew more about the attack at the company than they were letting on. It wasn't just an ambush, was it?


150:00 | in need of comfort

A tired sigh leaves the lips of Solar Lee. She was exhausted, more emotionally exhausted than anything, but she would never admit it. She was finally off of blood, with a cast around her arm and stitches where Jimin had managed to cut her. Despite it all, she wasn't really in pain anymore. She felt fine physically, she had been through things like this too many times to count. It wasn't long ago she got shot by Sunwoo's father, after all.

Chanhee was beside her on the bed, looking rather tired himself. He had been dealing with her persistence for quite a while, whilst Jaehyun had been talking to the doctors about Sunwoo and Chaerin went to get everybody in the waiting room.

It had been exactly three minutes of silence, which was now a record. Chanhee was starting to think that maybe she had calmed down for once, when the girl had blurted out in a sudden panic, "wait, but Sunwoo..." her voice falters when his name leaves her mouth. All he knew to do was hold his best friend's hand as tight as he could, repeatedly telling her it would be fine. The doctor's had said Solar was just having hallucinations and panic attacks from the PTSD. What had happened to her was something that would follow her around forever, especially now when she didn't know if Sunwoo would make it out alive. 

And as much as he hated Sunwoo's guts, he knew how special he was to his best friend. Because of this, it was hurting him, too. He couldn't really imagine his life without the nuisance of Kim Sunwoo. 

"It's all my fault, Hee..." she'd whispered a few seconds later, and his eyes bolted to hers. Her green irises were glassy, and she only stared down at her lap, holding a golden ring that was shimmering in the daylight. He knew exactly what it was, as the ring was tainted with the slightest amount of blood. "I didn't know he was gonna be there," she mutters on, "if I would have known, I..." she cuts herself off abruptly, because she didn't know. She didn't know what she would have done if she would've known.

Chanhee could feel the sharpest ping of guilt in his chest. He did know, but how was he going to tell her that? He knew that it would have been the right thing to tell her that Sunwoo was there the whole time, but he listened to Sunwoo's wishes like an idiot. 

That's exactly what Sunwoo was, too, a fucking dumbass. 

"Sol, hey, don't think about that," he tells her sternly, "Sunwoo is going to be fine, alright? Do you really think he would leave you to deal with this on your own?"

His question made her think hard. Her eyes only peer down at the ring, before she shakes her head in frustration, a sob leaving her lips as she sputters, "I don't know! How am I supposed to know when I can't see him?! I need to know how he's doing, Hee!" 

Knowing she was on the verge of another breakdown, the boy nods in agreement, rising to his feet. "Alright, calm down," he soothes her, "I'll go talk to Jaehyun and see if he's heard anything, I just need you to let yourself breathe, okay? I know you don't want to be stuck here another night."

He gives her a tight hug before making his way out of the room. Solar was left there in complete boredom again, heaving out a sigh as she leaned her head back to stare at the ceiling. She wondered what everyone else was up to, at that moment. If everybody was safe, if everything was okay. Her memory was rather hazy, but thanks to Chanhee telling her what apparently happened, she knew she ran into Jinhwan when she had gone looking for him. He was okay, and she was happy to hear that. She could only imagine what they'd gone through.

But she wasn't aware of the fact that all of her close friends were here. It was really the last thing she was thinking of.

Chaerin had peeked her head into the room, seeing Solar sitting in the same position, before opening the door fully. "Solar!" she calls out, "I've brought your therapists, so hopefully you'll cheer the fuck up!"

The brunette sits up in her bed from her sister's words, brows knitting together in complete confusion. All she could do was peer at her sister like she was a psychopath, until she saw the group she'd brought pour into her small hospital room. When she does, she coughs on air, having to hold a hand to her chest to ease her sudden anxiety.

"Oh my God..." she manages to say, her green eyes unsure where to look, "oh wow, you guys are all here..." She didn't think she would see them all together in the same room for a long time - especially not Jinhwan and Jungkook.

The two boys run to her in unison, each on a different side of the bed. "Mi, I'm so glad you're okay," Jungkook is the first to offer her with that bunny tooth smile, leaning forward to hug her and kiss her forehead, "I saw what happened on the news so I called your phone, Chaerin picked up and told me what happened so I came here as soon as I could."

"We were worried, Mi!" Jimin had called from behind him.

"Thank you both," she shakes her head in awe, "I-I didn't think I would see you again so soon."

Grinning down at her, he shrugs, "well, I guess the world had other plans for us."

Typical Jungkook.

Jinhwan had been glaring at him every second of their interaction, until it was his turn. Solar's eyes poured into his like a waterfall, and he found himself nearly melting. He'd nearly had a heart attack when he saw her like that, but here she was, perfectly okay. If something would have happened to her, he would've felt horrible. Even now, he felt like he should've been more aware, he could've done something more to prevent this.

When he doesn't speak for a moment and instead is sitting there in awe, gawking at her like an idiot, she whispers out with a small chuckle, "hi, Nani."



(hes cute i needed to include this)


He doesn't say anything. Jinhwan only shakes his head in disbelief, before he pulls her in for a huge hug. The familiar scent of his cologne fills her nose, and she found herself almost snuggling into him as she squeezes him tightly. 

The two of them could've sat there like that forever, until they could feel the burning gazes of everyone, telling them they should probably pull away. Jinhwan had cleared his throat as he did so, but stayed on the bed next to her, which she didn't really mind. 

The problem was, she could feel the tension. Solar swallows a lump in her throat as she glances from Jungkook to Jinhwan. Jungkook was still standing right beside her bed, already looking down at her when she makes eye contact. Despite witnessing the passionate interaction, when she meets his eyes he sends her a warm smile, as if to tell her it was okay.

She was thankful to him, to all of them.

"I'm going to pretend like I can't see how awkward you guys are," of course Chaerin couldn't help herself, before turning to face everyone else, "so, Solar is getting out of the hospital in a few hours. Right now, we're hoping to hear something about Sunwoo. From there, everyone needs to meet up at Solar's new apartment, and we'll figure out where to go from there. Sound good?"

Everyone nods in agreement or lets out a hum to say they understood. 

A stressed sigh leaves the sister's lips, and she turns to head towards the door, likely to find Chanhee and Jaehyun and see what was going on. In the meantime, Solar calls out to someone she had yet to talk to, "yah, Koo Junhoe!" she used the same annoying, insistent tone she always did when she was trying to bother him.

Junhoe had playfully rolled his eyes as he walked to stand on the side Jinhwan was on, reaching out a hand to gently hit her forehead, "ugh, still annoying even when you're on your deathbed. Where's the doctor? I think this abomination is still-"

"-Yah..." Jinhwan hits his arm, scolding him for going on too far, "close your mouth already."

Junhoe is unphased as he looks to the eldest, "I see Snow White's dwarf still has an attitude."

Jinhwan was even more annoyed now, as he could never handle Junhoe's ridiculous sense of humor. Not wanting to get up from the bed beside her, he swung a punch towards Junhoe, who simply stepped back slightly to avoid it. Solar was just sitting there, laughing at the two as Junhoe had mocked him for missing. 

"Hey, you're the one who brought him," she told Jinhwan when he was heaving out a sigh.

"He knows you prefer me," Junhoe hums, "dude is salty."

"One more word, I'm going to fucking lose it."

"This guy is so uptight."

In between their bickering, Solar suddenly notices someone else in the room. He'd been standing talking to Jimin, and the sight caught her off guard. She was surprised enough to see both Jinhwan and Jungkook, but he was here, too? 

She wasn't mad, really. More so, she was surprised and impressed he would care enough about someone he barely knew to come see them.

Though, she also realized that meant he probably heard and knew too much about the situation. Not to mention the fact he'd seen her interactions with Jinhwan and Jungkook. Both of those things were a problem, and she wasn't sure how she would solve that.

But so far, she didn't get any ill vibe from Jihoon. He seemed trustworthy enough, and it wasn't like she had much of a choice now.

And it was always good to have more allies.

Knowing he was probably feeling awkward and even afraid from being around people he looked up to, she tried to lighten the mood as she calls out to him, "don't worry, Jihoon-ah, I'll fill you in on everything when we're somewhere that isn't a hospital," she hums, "do you want to join us at my apartment when I'm discharged?"

Swallowing a lump in his throat, he tried not to let his nerves get the best of him. But Jihoon could feel the eyes of everyone burning holes into him. All he could do was nod, bowing apologetically, "Of course, I'm sorry to have intruded on everyone. I really just wanted to make sure you were okay, I didn't have any intention-"

"- Hey, don't worry about it. You're part of the club now," you would of thought Jihoon had just saw God himself with the way he was looking up at Jungkook, who was now standing right beside him. Jungkook was a sweetheart, he could tell that the boy was feeling bad and out of place, and he knew what that felt like. Clearing his throat, he holds out his hand, "by the way, I'm Jungkook. It's nice to meet you - you know, despite the chaos."

He was shaking when he accepted his handshake. "Jihoon," he bows again, "I-I'm a really big fan, you're so t-talented."

The bunny toothed boy only wrinkles his nose, but thanks him anyway, "don't mention it! Forget the formalities, we're all friends here."

Solar's heart was warmed just seeing how sweet he was being to the poor boy. She couldn't forget that deep down, Jungkook was one of the sweetest people she'd ever met. He just became competitive and therefore selfish when it came to her, he let his jealousy get the best of him. But he wasn't a bad person.

Jinhwan notices the way Solar was eyeing him, and feels himself grow frustrated. He didn't know how the boy could act so cool and nonchalant in times like this. Was he really more used to it than him? They'd been through about the same amount of unexpected events, hadn't they? Was Jinhwan just softer? 

Chill out, he tells himself harshly, it's not your place, anyway. 

He couldn't be mad. After all, at least Solar was distracted from the time being and wasn't mentioning Sunwoo over and over. That was a positive, right?

Though despite everyone trying to crack jokes and lighten the mood, there was still the problem at hand, and it wasn't just Sunwoo. Everyone knew that Solar had probably seen the culprit, the one who had done it all, the final piece to the puzzle. She had to know who it was, as long as her memory served her right. And everyone, deep down, was dying to know the answer to that life long question.

Who was it?

And where did they go from here?

The hospital door opening had cut everyone's deep thoughts off. An exhausted Chaerin and Jaehyun had poured inside, meeting the expected and worried gazes of all the friends.

Solar was the first to speak up, her mind being reminded of what was going on again, the distraction fading away as she cried out, "what happened? What did they say? Is he okay? Is the surgery over? Is he awake yet, is his blood rate okay?" Chanhee was back at her side, grabbing onto her hand to calm her down so she didn't start to panic again. The last thing they needed was for her to have another PTSD attack.

Jungkook and Jinhwan were both peering at her in concern. Jungkook was still by Jihoon, and there was no room for him to be beside her since Chanhee was in his original spot. This annoyed him, since Jinhwan was taking up the other side, but he let it be even though he wished he could be there to comfort her.

 Chaerin, Jaehyun, and Chanhee exchange a glance, as if asking each other who would be the one to explain. Finally, Jaehyun clears his throat, "his surgery was successful-" 

"-That's great, so he's okay?!" Solar practically yells, almost jumping out of her bed if Jinhwan hadn't been there to hold onto her other hand and keep her from doing so, "He's awake then, right? He's okay?! He's alive?!"

Worried about her current state, Chanhee hurriedly tells her soothingly, "yes, he's fine, Solar, remember? Sunwoo wasn't going to go down that easy."

Coming back down to Earth again, the girl closes her eyes, exhaling deeply as she takes in the information. 

"But," Jaehyun slowly goes on, "he's not awake yet. Apparently, he woke up again during the procedure, so they had to induce a coma with some medicine because he was - uh, let's just say, giving them quite the trouble."

Solar's face falls.

"He'll be awake soon enough, though," Chaerin pipes up, "his health is in fine condition, look on the bright side - I guess." She walks to her sister's side, bending over to grab Solar's bag, "and with that, Solar, you're also able to go home now."

Though this was all good news, she still felt hesitant. "I don't want to leave him here alone, it's dangerous," she shakes her head, "after everything that happened, they clearly wanted him dead. Now he's alive, but in the most vulnerable position he could ever be in."

Everyone is silent, realizing she had a good point. 

"We can stay here," Jaehyun assures her, looking to Juyeon, who nods vigorously in agreement.

"Yup, no worries, big sister!"

"Okay, I guess that works," she agrees after a moment of thought, "I'll be here to check on him tomorrow."

All the friends are busy gathering their things as Jinhwan helps Solar out of her bed, making sure she could walk okay and that she wasn't in any kind of pain. She insists that she's fine, because she was, and he finally lets her off the hook. 

Meanwhile, Jaehyun had pulled something out of his back pocket. It was an extra gun he had in case he needed it, but he figured he now had found a better use for it. The weapon glistens under the light as he hits Jungkook's shoulder to get his attention, holding it out to him. 

The boy looks down to it, and there's a brief moment of shock written on his face. After a second, he realized that it would be convenient for him to have it, and took it from Jaehyun's hand, "thanks."

Her brother only nods, "one of you needs some kind of protection, figured you were the best bet."

Jungkook sends him a smile, feeling quite happy about the compliment. That meant he thought he was more capable than Jinhwan.

Tucking the weapon in his pocket, Solar had been talking to Jinhwan, when she noticed the encounter between them. Unable to help herself, the brunette raises a brow as she walks over to Jungkook, crossing her arms over her chest, "well, well, do you even know how to shoot that?"

Opening his mouth and faking a gasp, he takes the gun out of his pocket again, pretending to load, cock, and shoot it to show that he knew what he was doing. Jungkook wasn't that stupid, of course he knew how to use a weapon, just because he had never actually used a gun didn't mean he wasn't educated on them.

The girl giggles, nodding in approval, "okay, okay, I see you, mister Kookie." 

Putting it back in his pocket, he claps a few times in exaggeration, "yes, I got approval from the crime boss queen!"

Solar makes a fake gagging sound, "whatever just came out of your mouth, never say it again."

"Hey, I'm just trying to be more in-tune with your past life, you know?"

"Jungkook, no," she shakes her head, and he only laughs at her. She found her cheeks burning pink from how precious that bunny smile of his was, and she had to admit, it cheered her up.

"LET'S GO HOME, SOO!" Junhoe is yelling to her, standing at the open door. It appeared pretty much everyone else was already heading down the hall, including Jinhwan. She figured he was giving her privacy and respecting her space - it was weird from him, but she appreciated it.

"Coming, loser!" she had called back, but surprisingly, Junhoe hadn't started a fight with her over her insult. Instead, he sends her a bright smile, complete with a wink, before he runs off down the hall towards Jinhwan, who was talking to her siblings and Chanhee.

As she walks further back with Jungkook at her side, she watches them for a moment. She could tell from the way Chaerin looked at Jinhwan that she still wasn't fond of him. It made her feel bad, as she knew he only meant good. What happened was over - way over.

The thought of what more had happened slapped her in the face, and she winced at the thought. She was going to have to tell him - and she had no idea how he was going to react. Solar had been so glad they had finally put all of the ex-psycho-girlfriend business behind them. As much as it had hurt her, she understood how much all of her past actions had hurt him. They had finally reached a mutual understanding, had forgave each other, they even had that heart-to-heart that night when he took her on a private picnic. She was glad they were finally back in a good place, a comfortable one, one were they weren't together but they were just normal. 

Now she was afraid of ruining it again.

Familiarity, change, 2018, she kept thinking of everything Jinhwan had preached to her that night. She had thought he was so right, that he made such a good point and that it meant she shouldn't get back with Jungkook. But of course that was what Jinhwan wanted, he had obviously been speaking with bias.

But maybe it was what she needed to hear.

Either way, Solar would never make up her mind as she looked up to the boy beside her. As she kept thinking about it, she realized that the only one who could really provide the kind of comfort she was craving was right there beside her. 

Jinhwan had glanced back to see what the two were doing. He knew that Soomi seemed to want to talk to Jungkook, which he understood, as it had probably been a long while since they'd seen each other since she decided on the "break." As much as he hated it, he had to be respectful of that, so he preoccupied himself with talking to her siblings about everything that was going on. But sometimes, his curiosity got the best of him.

And as soon as his eyes lay on the two, he felt a ping in his chest. The words of Park Jimin repeated in his head at that moment, it had been so long since they had that talk, yet it had been slowly becoming more evident to him as time passed.

Every time he saw them together, he realized how true his words had been.

"Take it from me, there's no breaking that bond those two have. It doesn't matter how much they bicker, fight, or get pissed at something the other does, they always find a way back to each other."

He puffs his cheeks up with air before deeply exhaling, trying to make himself look away from the way Solar's eyes brightened when Jungkook grinned down at her.

"And Jungkook, he's not any stranger to competition. He's always been protective over Soomi like some sort of prize, and with the way he's been recently, I don't think he's afraid to get his hands dirty."

His eyes had raked down to the weapon that was freshly tucked into Jungkook's pocket. At that moment, he wondered what Jimin had meant by those words. Get his hands dirty, but in what way? Jinhwan had been around Jungkook a lot. And while he was one of the biggest pretentious assholes he'd ever met in his life, he wasn't really the type to be violent, was he?

A replay of him punching Jungkook in the nose enters his brain. Jinhwan could vividly recall the flames in his eyes, the sweat dripping down his forehead from anger, and the way he looked like a bull just ready to be let out of a cage.

The memory seemed to prove him wrong, he realized.

Maybe he had a little bit more to worry about that wasn't just the current ensued chaos.


151:00 | her rock

"Hey, Kookie," she clears her throat, and there's a glimmering tint in his brown irises when he looks at her. Jungkook's beaming smile fades when he sees the dull expression on her face, and it forms into one full of concern.

"Are you feeling okay?" he immediately asks, and she knew he didn't mean physically. 

The brunette only shakes her head, pursing her lips as she directs her eye contact elsewhere, "no, not at all," she mutters, "c-can we talk?"

Jungkook nods in instant agreement, "yeah, of course, Mi. You can ride back with me, I'll tell Jimin to hitch a ride with your friends." Before she could even speak up to say he didn't have to tell Jimin that, he was already jogging off to catch up with everyone to relay the news.

She winced at the sight, already worried about the reactions this would gain both of them. Sure enough, Chanhee had turned towards her with a loud, dramatic gasp, his mouth hanging wide open as he snapped, "you are bold to think I'm going to let my best friend be with you after a night like this!"

Knowing she needed to do something now because Jungkook was probably going to falter under Chanhee, only because he was so dramatic and insistent, she jogs ahead to catch up with everyone. Trying to run had given her more pain than she was looking for, but she sucked it up as she forced a reassuring smile on her face, "Hee, it's fine, okay? I'm fine. Don't treat me like a child, I can go with Jungkook if I want to."

But the boy only rolls his eyes, scoffing, "excuse me, Solar Lee, I'm treating you like a child because you are a child. Your decisions in life up to this point aren't exactly saying otherwise."

The girl lets out a deep sigh. All Chanhee ever did was roast her - heartlessly.

"Hee," Jacob came to save her as he pats the boy's shoulder, "there's nothing wrong with her going with Jungkook, he's equipped to protect her. You're overreacting."

Her other friends pitch in to agree with him, causing Chanhee to let out a groan. "Okay, whatever, choose more people over me."

"Oh my god," she's gawking, "you're too dramatic for no reason."

They were out in the parking lot by now, and Chanhee had only held up his hand, "I don't want to hear it, Solar, talk to the hand."

Solar mutters something of annoyance under her breath. Either way, she knew he was kidding, so she rolled her eyes and walked to the others, who were standing in the lot by their vehicles. 

"You can ride with us, Jihoon," Jinhwan had been kindly offering, "we have space."

Jihoon nodded, offering a thank you as he followed them to climb into the car. Chaerin was the one driving, and Solar watched as all of them had gotten inside, except for her and Jungkook. Junhoe offers her a wave and a middle finger, to which she responds by sticking her tongue out. She also calls goodbye to Jimin and Jihoon, who returned with waves before closing their doors.

Now all that was left was Jinhwan.

The situation felt all too familiar to her, almost like she had experienced it recently.

"I'll see you at my apartment," she hums to him. Her mouth had hung open for a second longer, as if there was more she wanted to say, but Solar shook it off, deciding against it.

"Okay, Soo," he'd said. She thought that was it, until the boy stepped forward to pull her into a tight hug. She had to remind herself that this wasn't exactly like that experience at the airport when Jungkook picked her up or like that time at the funeral. They were on good terms, and she was the only one who was feeling awkward due to what she knew that he didn't. As far as Jinhwan knew, they were okay.

With this thought in mind, she accepts his hug, inhaling that scent of cologne that she always loved. Jinhwan pulls away after leaving a short kiss on her forehead, before he sends a small nod Jungkook's way, and heads off to climb in the vehicle with everyone else.

Jungkook heaved out a sigh as soon as he was gone, as if he had been holding it in for hours. "Finally," he breathes, "he's gone."

Rolling her eyes, she couldn't help but smile at him as they walked away together towards his car, "you're an idiot."

Jungkook only sends her that grin of his that made her knees weak, before opening the door with a dramatic motion, pretending to bow as if he just finished a theater show. Solar couldn't help but chuckle at him, glad to see he was in such a good mood - or just trying really hard to cheer her up. Either way, she was thankful for his actions, she was thankful for the fact things could never be awkward between them, not even after the last way they'd talked. 

That was how it always was with them. Just like Jimin had told Jinhwan that day. You couldn't truly break them apart, they had a weird bond, a weird connection, some kind of chemistry you only saw in the movies. Through every disagreement, every fight, they found a way back to each other.

Solar had winced as she got comfortable in the seat, her hand and arm aching with each movement she made. Everyone had kept asking her about it and making sure she wasn't in too much pain, and the brunette had kept angrily shrugging all the questions off as if it was nothing. But the truth was, it really fucking hurt. After all, she was literally shot again and a knife went straight through her hand. The stitches and pain medicine only did so much for something like that.

Though it was better than being dead.

A deep sigh leaves her lips as Jungkook had leaned over to put on her seatbelt for her since it was a bit hard for her to do it herself. Closing her eyes, she leans back into the leather seat of his Bugatti, wanting to just go to sleep and disappear into her own world. 

But every time she closed them, all she could see was Sunwoo, her manager's sinister expression, the life that drained from Jimin's face as she fell helplessly to the ground. 

Her brain raked through every single moment, every single moment she had hoped to forget. But after Chanhee had reminded her what apparently happened, she remembered it all. 

She thought it was Jaehyun. Sunwoo had been here the whole time when she thought he wasn't. Every time he called to check on her, it was because he was making sure she was, what she was doing, so he could be there. He was watching over her all without her knowing, keeping her safe. It was insane for her to realize he had been there, probably even at the party Jungkook had thrown. 

And he had been there, on the top floor, fighting off God knew how many people. There was no telling how many bodies were from Sunwoo, there was no telling what exactly went on up there. But she knew that Kim Sunwoo didn't go down easy, he didn't lose a fight easily, it wasn't in his blood. If he lost, it had to be because he was outnumbered to a scary amount, and even then, he would put up every ounce of energy to fight them. 

She knew that was exactly what he'd done. After all, he'd killed them all but one - all but the main culprit.

Solar had no idea she was crying until she jumped at the feeling of a hand on her thigh. When her green eyes open, tears pour out of them that had been eagerly waiting to do so. Jungkook's eyes are peering at her from the driver's seat while he focuses on the road every few seconds, worry dripping from his irises. "Hey, Mi, calm down, okay?" he soothes her, "I'm here, everything is okay. Sunwoo's going to make it through, alright? And we'll find them, okay?"

The girl only purses her lips because she wasn't sure if she could agree to anything he said. Would he be okay? Would they find her manager?

She realized then, she hadn't even got a chance to tell anyone about those details - or really anything at all. Steadying her breathing, Solar mentally curses at herself, she had to keep calm and handle this like Sunwoo would. She was better than this.

Knowing she had to start somewhere, she clears her throat, "Jungkook..." her voice trails off.

Still, he looked to her with concentration to show she had his full attention, "yeah, Mi?"

The more she thought about it, the more she realized Jungkook was the best person she could talk to about this. 

"Um, about everything," she breathes, and Jungkook had grown a bit nervous about what she was going to say, not wanting her to stress herself too hard and have another panic attack.

So, before she can say another word, he reaches out again to squeeze her thigh ever so slightly, assuring her sweetly, "I know it's hard to talk about, if you don't feel like you can do it yet, you don't have to force yourself, Mi."

His heartfelt words tickled her, but she shook her head, reminding herself she had to keep her head in the game. "No, I-I'm okay," she insists, clearing her throat again, "I think you're the best person for me to talk to about it."

The biggest giddy grin spreads across his lips from those words. Pushing his long, brown locks out of his eyes, he nods, eyes on the road again as he turns on a different street, still heading towards her apartment complex, "okay, of course, I'm here to listen."

Closing her eyes again, she takes a deep breath. "So... when I was waiting in the studio," she begins, staring out the window into the Seoul sky. It was around 6 AM, meaning the sun was finally starting to come up on that Thursday morning, and it was a calming sight that gave her the strength to keep going, "Jimin was the one who came up behind me, it was how my hand got hurt."

Jungkook looks to her, distracted from the road for a second again, his eyes widened slightly as he recalls who that was, "wait, Jimin?" he blinks, pushing his hyung to the back of his brain who had the same name, "oh... the psychotic ex girlfriend..." it sunk in his brain finally, causing him to feel a rapid mix of emotions, "Oh my God, what the fuck?"

Knowing he was likely going to get frustrated at this, she only nods. "Yeah, I guess she was working with them, who knows for how long," she goes on, and Jungkook had slowly begun to piece together the fact that Solar was talking in past tense, "she told me she had plenty of reasons to want me dead."

Gripping the steering wheel harder in anger, the boy scoffs, "yeah, with the main one being something as pathetic as some dwarf."

Solar doesn't even react to the insult anymore. "Anyway... she said she was waiting for a call to signal it was time, that the main culprit of it all was preoccupied or something... so I thought-I thought they must be fighting my siblings," she shakes her head, feeling the urge to cry again. Fighting it away, she exhales, "I knew I had to do something, so I killed her."

There's a brief silence in the car. All you could hear was the quiet hum of the radio and the sound of the engine as it drove along the road. 

Until Jungkook speaks in a calm tone, "that was what you had to do, Mi. Self defense, plus she was a literal psychopath."

Solar shrugs, sighing as she continues to stare out the window, "I know, I'm not really concerned about that - it's just - Jinhwan," she mutters, knowing Jungkook would already get the memo and she didn't have to elaborate more. 

The car had come to a stop, and only then did she look out the front window to see her apartment complex. For all she knew, everyone else was already in her apartment waiting for them since she gave Jaehyun a key. Jungkook purses his lips in thought as he comprehends what she was saying, turning the engine off before leaning back into the seat, "oh, you think he's going to be upset," he thinks out loud, before scoffing, "he has no room to be mad or upset at all. What, would he have preferred you got killed just so he could keep his side piece?" he angrily shakes his head, his long hair falling in his face again. Letting out a deep breathe so his annoyance at how much he genuinely hated Jinhwan didn't overcome him, he sighs, "if you really feel like he's going to react that way, Mi, then that's a problem in his own. You should be able to say confidently that he'll be understanding, right?"

A dull look is on her face as she nods, "I know he would be understanding if it was anybody else," she explains, "but - after that entire incident, after the fact that it's the girl he cheated on me with, his ex girlfriend. I can't help but feel like there's gonna be some kind of emotion there, whether it's anger or sadness."

Jungkook had heaved out a grumble, hating the thought of what happened because of how pissed off it made him feel. He never forgave that guy like Soomi did, why would he? The fact that she had forgave him even made his blood boil. He didn't deserve an ounce of anyone's forgiveness, he didn't care that Soomi had basically cheated on him, he didn't care if she had done the same sorts of things. He hated him.

And Jungkook knew all of her friends, her siblings, they all felt the same way, but for Soomi's sake they weren't going to start anything. Plus, they got along with the rest of the iKON members, it was just him that they had an issue with.

If it wasn't for the entire funeral incident and the investigation being announced in regards to the woman they'd killed, Solar would have never really made up with him. She understood his reasoning, and she understood she didn't have a lot of room to talk. But that didn't make it right.

At the end of the day, she would always have a soft spot for him, but she would never be able to forget something like that.

Jungkook seemed to know this, too.

"You haven't really forgiven him, have you, Mi?"

There's a second of silence, before she mumbles, "not really. His apology seemed so off, that's why I'm so worried about telling him about her being dead at my hands."

"It seemed off because it was," he sternly corrects, "he tried to play it off like he was going through so much and it was so hard on him, and then he claimed that he didn't actually cheat on you - and then he said that it was after you guys broke up so it was okay," he snorts in disgust, "he was just spinning webs of bullshit. He cheated while you were still together, and whether the girl was crazy and 'forced' him into it doesn't change it, Mi. He still did it, and he can't act like it was 'all her'."

Solar is dead silent. All she could do was sit there, staring at the apartment complex they were parked in front of, her mouth slightly agape as she tried to comprehend everything Jungkook just said. Through out the time she was taking a break from them, she had realized that she had to be careful when she spoke to Jinhwan or Jungkook about each other. They each were going to naturally speak from a bias, and she had to keep this in mind.

But at the same time, every point Jungkook made made complete sense. While she did believe Jinhwan wasn't some kind of monster, he didn't intentionally want to hurt her, he still did do what Jungkook said. He was completely right on that.

When she still was sitting there in deep thought, the boy speaks up again, "look, Mi. When the scandal happened, when all of that happened, I hurt you because I was scared, so I could protect you," he's muttering, "but Jinhwan... when he cheated on you, when he hurt you, it was out of spite. Don't think he didn't know what he was doing, Mi."

Briefly, memories of 2018 flash through her mind. Her and Jungkook's engagement, how he had always supported her, how he was always there when she needed him in their relationship, he was always her rock. Sure, he was competitive, selfish, and sometimes even a little bit of an asshole, but deep down, he was one of the sweetest, compassionate people she had ever had the pleasure of knowing.

And she knew he was speaking now from more than just a biased standpoint against Jinhwan.

"You're right," is all she knew to say, but her mind had ran back to that night after her party with Jinhwan. Everything he'd said to her, embracing the change, how they had gotten through everything because "it was love," but was that really true? Had they really gotten through everything?

Or had he just been trying to sway her?

She believed he was sorry, but she wasn't sure if she could accept that. She wasn't sure if anything else he had said that night really held true like she had thought it did. 

"I don't know though," she sputters, burying her face in her hands. Jungkook peers at her in worry, knowing he might have gotten a little carried away. He guessed that she hadn't thought too much about it since she was on her break, especially now, and he couldn't help but feel bad.

"I'm sorry, I got a little carried away," he frowns, reaching out to grab her hand comfortingly, "I've just been wanting to tell you that for a while. You're going to have to tell everyone about what happened eventually, and Jinhwan will likely find out either way. But if you aren't ready to do it tonight, you don't have to, and don't let anyone force you to talk about it."

With a sigh, she focused for a moment on the feeling of his warm hand in hers. Their fingers entwined together gave her a sense of comfort, a sense of familiarity just like Jinhwan had said. But what was wrong with her wanting to go back to that?

Because he was her rock.

"I can do it," she finally blurts, "I have to, we have to all talk about this, Kookie. There's no time for me to feel ready."

"Mi..." he sighs, a worried expression still on his face. But once she looked at him with that look, he knew there was no changing her mind. "Alright, whatever you think is best."


152:00 | tense get togethers

Chanhee had let out an impatient sigh, tapping his foot in annoyance against the tiled floor of the kitchen in Solar's apartment. Jaehyun, Chaerin, and the other childhood friends are in the same area, sitting on bar stools at the kitchen island. Juyeon was the only one absent, as he had stayed back at the hospital with Sunwoo to keep guard like they assured Solar they would do. 

They'd been there already waiting for quite a while. Everyone knew they kinda needed Solar in order to begin their entire talk about all of this. But she and Jungkook were taking quite the while.

"God, we don't have all day," the best friend is mumbling, distracting himself by wiping the counters clean (as if they were dirty at all,) "they better not be touching each other somewhere."

Some of them exchange glances, humorous looks on their faces, from his ridiculous words. Everyone knew that was probably the last thing they would be doing, but they found it hilarious that Chanhee out of all people would say such. 

Shaking her head as she takes a sip of the water in her hand, Chaerin is the first to say, "pretty sure that's not what they're doing, cut her some slack, so what if she wants to fuck him? It's stress relief."

Usually, the conversation would be flipped. But Chanhee was feeling extra concerned for Solar and her mental state. He hated to leave her alone without supervision, after all, he had watched her almost die last night, and then he sat with her in a hospital room for several hours as she had repeated panic attacks and PTSD episodes. It wasn't like she was going to immediately feel better after everything she just went through, and he felt like it was his duty to keep an eye on her. So much so, that he couldn't believe that he was the only one so worried out of her other supposed "best friends," who were casually lounging in the living room as if it was nothing.

(Which really wasn't true, they were just as worried.)

"I just," Chanhee exhales deeply, "God, I can't believe any of this happened."

They all nod in agreement to his statement. "Did she ever tell you who it was?" Jacob asks, "I know she was pretty out of it so she might've not remembered yet."

Pursing his lips, the pink haired boy leans over the counter in thought, "no, I don't think so. I know just as much as you all do."

"I hope she's well enough to talk about it," Chaerin mumbles, thinking seriously again, "we shouldn't force her, even though I really want to fucking know."

"If she's not, that's okay," Jaehyun finally speaks up after sitting in silence for a while, "we can't do anything anytime soon. Whoever it is, they're definitely not going to come out for a while - if ever, we'll have to find wherever they're hiding," he goes on, staring off at the wall, "but I'm not sure how possible that'll be without Sunwoo, let's hope he wakes up soon or it'll get a lot harder on us all. We need to stick together now more than ever."

A groan leaves Chae's lips at the last part of his speech, "I wish we could get rid of that asshole though."

Everyone knew who she was talking about. 

"He keeps trying to talk to me like we're buddies," she rolls her eyes, crossing her arms over her chest as she scoffs, "as if I'll just forget about what he did like Solar."

"Look, none of us like him," Chanhee reminds her, "trust me, but Jaehyun is sadly correct." All of them still clearly resented Jinhwan, who had seemed to be trying his best to make it right. But they couldn't just let go of that. Even though Hee was the only one who knew of the gift he had given Solar, he still didn't care for him. Even if it was so 'sentimental,' he didn't trust him.

"Let's focus on what's important for now," Jaehyun sighs, "so not Solar's love life, as much as the guy fucking pisses me off."

Meanwhile, they weren't aware of the boy that was casually standing outside, close enough to hear the conversation in the kitchen. Jihoon wasn't trying to eavesdrop, he just felt so awkward and didn't know where to stand. He had also wanted to go get a drink, but when he heard the serious conversation going on, he felt weird if he were to just intrude. 

Though he didn't know much about absolutely anything that was going on, he was slowly piecing the pieces together. He was aware that Chanhee and the other few boys were friends from Soomi's hometown, and that the three that looked weirdly alike were her siblings. Of course, he knew who everyone else was since they were his superiors. The part he was patching together was how they were all involved with each other in something that was more serious than he could ever imagine.

Some of it was easy to put together. Jihoon had just become a trainee when the scandal of 2018 happened, he read every bit of news about it, everyone had because it was everywhere. Even though the pictures had come out of them that proved the scandal to be true, along with the anonymous letter, he always wondered if it was all fake or some kind of stunt. Seeing them together at the hospital, though, had shown him the clear truth. You could just tell they had adoration for each other, from the way they looked at each other to how they spoke to one another.

It warmed his heart, he wished he had something like that. Did that mean they were still together, though? Jihoon had no idea, and it would be inappropriate for him to ask. He thought it might be the case, but then he saw the way her and Jinhwan were acting in the hospital room.

It was safe to say he was confused, especially after hearing the talk in the kitchen. Why did they hate Jinhwan, what could he have done to Soomi? Jihoon always thought Jinhwan seemed like a nice guy. 

But the biggest question was, what the hell were all of them involved in? And now that he was here, did that mean he was involved in it, too? Jungkook had sure said that when he welcomed him. What the hell did he get himself into?

"Hey, Jihoon, don't worry so much," a voice cuts through his thoughts, and his eyes jerk up to meet the eyes of Park Jimin. The boy sends him a kind smile, "I know it's probably all super confusing, but Soomi will be here soon, and everything will make sense."

He only nods in understanding, about to thank him for his words, when he speaks again in a rather apologetic tone, "I'm sorry you got caught up in all this, I hope you know it wasn't Mi's intention or anything."

Worried of him getting the wrong idea, he hurriedly shakes his head, "oh no, no! It's okay! I just don't know what all this is quite yet."

Jimin chuckles, "I understand, but keep in mind that we're all friends here. You'll be safe with us!"

Before the boy could say more to Jimin, the front door of the apartment had opened. There came pouring in Solar and Jungkook, hair messy from the morning wind. Jungkook had shut the door behind them, locking it before he catches up to Solar, who had collapsed onto the sofa in exhaustion. The bunny toothed boy takes a seat beside her, announcing loudly, "we're here, everyone~!"

Glad to see his best friend, Jimin sends a smile to Jihoon before making his way over to sit on the other side of Solar. "Hey, Mi," he offers her as she sits up from his presence, "are you feeling okay? How's your arm? How's your hand?"

"It's fine, Chimmie," she hums, poking his fluffy cheek playfully, "just like it was the last time you asked me."

"Sorry~," he giggles, "I needed to provide an update for the others back at home, they keep spamming my phone," he's explaining, already typing on his phone, "they wanted to come, too, but I told them we already have a bit of a full house."

From his words, she glances up to take a look around the room. Her siblings and childhood friends were pouring in from the kitchen now, making it even more crowded. He was definitely right there, she realized at the sight of everyone. Solar wasn't even sure if her apartment was big enough for this many people.

If she didn't have to say the things she was going to, and if everything was still perfect between her and Jinhwan, it would be an amazing sight to her. But instead, she couldn't help but feel sick to her stomach from anxiety.

"My God, finally," Chanhee is breathing out as he walks out to take a seat with everyone else, "it's about time!"

"Sorry, we took a bit of a detour," Jungkook apologizes with a bright tone, sending that smile to Chanhee, who only shakes his head in disappointment.

At least he didn't mean that.

It would be at least an hour until everyone was finally situated to the point that they were ready to talk about it. Chanhee and Jimin had repeatedly been asking her if she was okay as the caring best friends that they were, and Junhoe had been playfully bullying people to lighten the mood - since it was his known talent. The other iKON boys had come by to join the "get together," since they were fairly involved in it all, too, and needed to hear the entire talk they were bound to have.

Jungkook had stayed by Solar's side. He only left one time to get her some water and more pain meds. He was being the sweetheart she knew he was deep down when he wasn't busy making Jinhwan mad - however, she had a feeling he was still doing things just to make Jinhwan mad, but only partly. It wasn't like he didn't care about her, she knew he loved her still, just like she still loved him.

And Jinhwan, poor Jinhwan, was left to sit on the sofa with the other guys, trying his best not to stare. He forced himself to instead look around at the brief decor that Solar had set up already in her apartment, though he knew her well enough to know she was far from done. Her last apartment was decorated as if it was done by a professional, she was very serious about it.

It looked great. He wished he could have helped her with it. 

His eyes wander the walls, until they land on a particular shelf filled with pictures. The pictures ranged from her and her family, to her and her childhood friends, to photos of her and Jungkook. He could tell the pictures of her and Jungkook were from when they were together, with how young they both looked. The sight made him feel sick to his stomach, thinking about how he missed her and how much he hated to see them together.

Not to mention, Jinhwan felt so tense - like everyone was looking down on him.

Probably because they were. He realized now that no matter what he seemed to do to say sorry, he would never outlive what he'd done. Yet, Jungkook was off the hook after the entire scandal he caused? Jinhwan could only scoff at that thought, it was unfair.

Only because he refused to think of the differences between the two.

"Alright," Jaehyun clears his throat as he takes a seat, "looks like everyone is situated, we have a lot to figure out."

Everyone feels tense at his words, recalling the events of last night in their heads. No one, especially Solar, wanted to think about what happened - but they knew they had to, for the sake of each other and their safety, for the sake of getting to the bottom of this, she was going to have to tell them everything.

"Okay, we should probably start by hearing everyone's point of view," her older brother goes on, tapping his foot on the floor repeatedly out of force of habit. His eyes raked across the room, falling onto the boys of iKON, "you guys got trapped in your studio, right? Someone was working with them and was trying to keep you situated so you couldn't help Sol."

The boys exchange glances, as if asking which one was going to talk about the events that occurred. All of their eyes fell on the eldest, who was sitting in the middle of them all. Caramel hair falling in his eyes, he looked like he was going to puke as he recalled it in his head, his eyes staring at his feet. Solar swallowed a lump in her throat as she watched him, wondering if he was well enough to have this talk. She had a feeling of what happened, she knew because she had seen the dead body in their studio.

Bobby had patted his hyung's back comfortingly, and Hanbin leans over to whisper something in his ear. But Jinhwan shook them off, sitting up straight and clearing his throat hesitantly, "uh, the producer we were working with was one of them. He kept forcing me to re-do my part because I was going to go check on Soo, and when I finally realized it, he said the entire place was wired with dynamite..." everyone is staring at him intently as he goes on, "He had a gun pointed at us, but I figured he must have some kind of remote control to disable it so I-uh... when he was distracted with the others I..." his voice trails off as he pauses. 

Jinhwan appeared to be rather distressed, closing his eyes and trying to breathe normally. The memory flashes in his head on a loop, of him using every ounce of strength in his body to snap the producer's neck, watching as he had fallen to the ground. Then he'd took the weapon, closed his eyes, and put a bullet into his head.

He remembered every second, but he didn't have the strength to tell the rest. Everyone seemed to get the memo, and Jaehyun hurriedly spoke up so the boy didn't try to force himself, "alright, and from there, you disabled it and left. Everyone but you went home, and then you went looking for Solar?" he raises a brow to him, asking him to confirm it was true.

Jinhwan only nods as he regains his composure. His eyes wander to Solar, who was sitting there by Jungkook and Jimin. Those green irises were already peering at him, and his eyes were glassy when they met hers.

Solar feels her heart drop, she didn't realize how much something like that would affect Jinhwan. He wasn't used to things like that, nevertheless killing someone, even if they deserved it. Her killing Jimin hadn't been enjoyable, but she looked at it as a necessary measure. She grew up with Sunwoo, after all.

But him? This kind of life was far from anything he was prepared for, and she knew because of that, it was likely tearing him apart.

So despite her ill feelings towards him, despite the confusion, she mouths to him, "I'm sorry, are you okay?"

He only nods to her, a small smile forming on his face as he mouths back, "don't worry, Soo."

Only then did she notice her brother Jaehyun, who was leading the conversation like Sunwoo would typically be doing, had stopped talking. Chaerin was already staring at him in confusion, along with everyone else who wasn't distracted. The iKON boys were all trying to cheer up Jinhwan, who still appeared rather shaken up. Jungkook and Jimin were talking to Jihoon, who looked as confused as ever since he still didn't have much of an explanation. 

Before Solar could speak up to say something to her brother, Jungkook had gently touched her arm to grab her attention. Her eyes jerk to him, and he had hesitated, before asking, "Mi, do you want me to explain everything to Jihoon? You know, so you don't have to. Do you really think we can trust him?" he lowers his voice at the last bit so the boy didn't overhear.

Thinking for a moment, she supposed, "yeah," she finally nods, "yeah, we can trust him. Give him the gist of the stalker and all of that, but keep it short about - uh - my home life."

Understanding she meant to exclude certain parts that might be critical, Jungkook agrees with a bunny smile, "okay, Mi, got you!"

Returning his pretty grin, it suddenly faltered when she noticed Jaehyun had left the room. Telling Jungkook she would be back, she rises to her feet to make her way to Chaerin and Chanhee. 

"Are you going to actually sit with me now or are you gonna keep pretending I don't exist?" Chanhee hummed as soon as she stepped in front of them. 

His words cause a deep, long sigh to leave her lips. He really could not help himself. Rubbing her temples, she tried to think of a good response to that, before remembering nothing she said would be good enough for her dramatic best friend, "Hee, you know I love you, don't be mad I was sitting by Jimin and Jungkook."

The pink haired boy scoffs, sticking his nose to the air, "pfft, okay, don't be mad I'm getting endlessly getting replaced right in front of my own eyes, sure!"

Solar groans, not in the mood, "Hee, can you please give me a break - just this once?"

Looking at his best friend, his hard-headed expression remains for a good minute. But Solar just kept staring at him with those sad eyes, until he finally snapped, "I suppose, only because you're crippled."

Unable to hold in her slight laughter from him, she shakes her head as she looks to Chaerin, "Chae, where did Jae go?"

The girl shrugs, "said he needed a cig, didn't want to stink up your new apartment."

Only muttering an okay in response, she already knew where he was, then. Solar makes her way down the hallway of her apartment until she reaches the backdoor that leads onto the rather large balcony. Peering out the window, she saw him there, leaning forward against the railing that surrounded it, blowing smoke into the air around him. His brown eyes were peering up at the 8 AM sky, filled with an emotion she didn't see often out of her brother.

The cold air tickles her skin as she turns the door handle with her working hand, stepping out into the morning. Her brother didn't even turn at the sound of it opening, he only continued to stare out into the city of Seoul.

Before she could even open her mouth to ask what had him out here looking so sentimental, he remarks, "you know, it's weirdly peaceful here, I wonder why we never visited here as kids."

Solar didn't even bother to wonder how he knew it was her. She only walks to stand beside him on the balcony, her one hand grabbing onto the railing as she peers out to the gleaming city, thinking about his words for a second, before she vacillates, "who knows, mother and father rarely ever mentioned Korea. My Korean was so bad when I got here because we never spoke it, I basically had to learn the entire language from scratch."

Her older brother chuckles, shaking his head as he blows more smoke into the air. "Oh, come on, I'd say all of our Korean is pretty solid," his voice trails off into the wind as he had paused again. Solar could tell he was thinking real hard about something, part of that older sibling telepathy. She hadn't seen Jaehyun in such a state in a while. He was usually too busy looking out for everyone else, he never bothered to stop and show emotion. 

Finally getting tired of not knowing, she opens her mouth to ask, but he speaks first.
